english_verse: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
translation_verse: “Pɔ'asarisɔ nin ti gbaai puuwa n‑dɔ'ai bidibga, Ka ba ti booli u yuuri Imanuɛl.”

english_verse: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn NaaGyoosaaya biisi n daa nyɛ NaaGyɛkɔnaaya ni u tunzɔppa: lala saha ka ba daa gbaasi ba n‑kyaŋŋi Babɛlɔn.

english_verse: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Yaakubu maa bii n daa nyɛ Gyoosɛfi; ka Gyoosɛfi mun daa nyɛ Mariama sira - ŋɔn Mariama mun daa dɔ'ai Yisa, sɔ ban boonni ‘Kirisita, Masiiya’ la.

english_verse: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Eezɔr bii daa nyɛ Zeedɔkku, ka Zeedɔkku mun bii daa nyɛ Eekyim, ka Eekyim bii daa nyɛ Ilayuuda;

english_verse: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ban daa dɔ'ai Yisa Masiiya seem nŋɔa#:- Sahaseaa ban daa zaŋŋi u ma Mariama n‑ti Gyoosɛfi, ka na bu taasi u la, di saha ka ba daa yi nya ka Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko daa pun kyɛ ka pɔ'asariga maa mari puuwa.

english_verse: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
translation_verse: U sira Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu pam, ka dizugu u daa yɛli, ni, u ya sɔ'ai n‑za'asi u ka niriba bu ya baŋŋi di gbinni: ni, “M bu boori ni ba ti diisi u vi.”

english_verse: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa na kur teesiri lala yɛla maa, ka u daa zaasi zaasimni, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, “Nyini Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn nyɛ NaaDauda ya'aŋŋa wa, di zɔri dabeem ni i kyɛ ka ba taasi Mariama i yiri na, dama ŋɔn mari puseaa maa yi la Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko ni na.

english_verse: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
translation_verse: U nin ti dɔ'ai bidibga, ka i purim u yuuri Yisa, dama u nin ti tiligi u niriba ba tumbeeri taalli zaa ni.”

english_verse: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka sahaseaa Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn daa neaai goom ni la, u daa niŋi hoo ti Dugma malaaka maa ŋɔn sa yɛli u seem maa, ka kyɛ ka ba taasi u pɔ'a maa na n‑ti u.

english_verse: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa bu la'am taaba n‑dooni halli ka Mariama ti dɔ'ai u pɔ'asaridɔ'am, bidibga; ka Gyoosɛfi daa puri u yuuri Yisa.

english_verse: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
translation_verse: Ban daa ti dɔ'ai Yisa Bɛtlihɛm timpuuni n‑naai, Meegyai daa yi wuntampusiri tinwa'aligu ziiya n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na - ban n daa nyɛ ninvugseeba ban baŋŋi saŋmariwuusi yɛla, ni bamsim pam m‑paasi. Di saha maa Bɛtlihɛm daa bɛ la Gyuudiiya naam suulinsi ni, ka NaaHɛroodi mun daa di lala tingbaŋŋu naam maa.

english_verse: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
translation_verse: Ka Meegyai maa daa kur boosiri, ni, “Yɛni ka ba dɔ'ai Izraɛli dima naaba la? Ti daa nya u saŋmariwuu maa wuntampusiri lugri ni, dizugu ka ti kyɛ na ni ti gyeem u.”

english_verse: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa wum ba lala boosigu maa, di daa gyɛri u pampam - ŋɔn ni u niriba ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm zaa - ka ba sufu daa sa'am.

english_verse: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa kan bɛ Gyuudiiya suulinsi ni la ka ba nin dɔ'ai u. Ni, dama anabidima daa sɔbi n‑zaali, ni#:-

english_verse: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
translation_verse: “Nyin Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa wa, Nyin bɛ Gyuudiiya suulinsi ni la, I bu poora Gyuudiiya nabiisi sunsukkuni; Nasɔ m bɛ ni ŋɔn ti ya n‑guri ya - Ya Izraɛli dima maa - Hoo ban guri bunkɔbri seem la: Ŋɔnwa ti ya n‑yi nyin Bɛtlihɛm saani na.”

english_verse: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
translation_verse: Ka NaaHɛroodi daa booli Meegyai maa asiiri ni, m‑maali m‑boosi ba, ni, “Bɔn saha deede ka saŋmariwuu maa daa pusi saazugu na?” Ka ba daa wulisi u di saha ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛm Bɛtlihɛm nti bɔ bii maa lahabaari. Ka ya yi ti paai n‑nya u, ya tumma yooma na n‑yɛli ma, ka man gba nin kyaŋŋi nti gyeem u.”

english_verse: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa wum Naaba ŋɔn daa yɛli ba seem la, ba daa yi n‑lu Bɛtlihɛm soori yaasa, ka saŋmariwuu maa daa gaari ba tooni ka ba daa dɔla - ban daa pun nya seaa wuntampusiri zii la - halli nti paai n‑zaani saazugu n‑wulisiri bii maa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela.

english_verse: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
translation_verse: Ba sufu daa maaya pam, saŋmariwuu maa zugu.

english_verse: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba zaasi zaasimni m‑bugsi ba, ni, ba di naa n‑labi NaaHɛroodi saani, ka dizugu ba daa lɔkki sɔseaa n‑labi n‑kuli ba tiŋŋa.

english_verse: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
translation_verse: Ban daa ti gaari n‑kuli n‑naai, Gyoosɛfi daa zaasi zaasimni n‑nya ka ti Dugma malaaka daa kyaŋŋi u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, “Doaama n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Igyipti. See ka ya kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni halli ka man ti laa n‑ti ya kukɔri; dama NaaHɛroodi ya boori la bii maa ziiya ni u gbaai u n‑ku.”

english_verse: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
translation_verse: Ka u daa doaai yuŋŋu n‑zaŋŋi u pɔ'a ni bii maa n‑yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Igyipti.

english_verse: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
translation_verse: Ba daa kpalim la bɛni halli ka NaaHɛroodi ti kpi. Ka dinŋɔ n daa kyɛ ka di siri niŋi hoo ti Dugma ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ ka u anabisɔ ti tɔ'asi anabitiri ni, ni#:- “N daa booli m bii maa Ka yiisi u Igyipti tingbaŋŋu la ni na.”

english_verse: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
translation_verse: Ka NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Meegyai maa yɔmsi u ka sɔ'ai n‑kuli, u sufu daa yisigiya pam. Ka u daa zaali zaaligu, ni, ba kyaŋŋi Bɛtlihɛm, ni tinkpanseesi an bɛ gyilagyila la ni, nti ku biisi maa - bidibseeba ban dɔ'ai ka ba nyɛ dahinyinni halli nti paai yuuma ayi biisi la zaa. U lala zaaligu maa daa dɔlisi la sahaseaa Meegyai maa ŋɔn daa wulisi u la.

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
translation_verse: Di saha di siri daa niŋi hoo Anabi Gyɛrimaaya ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛliseelli, ni#:-

english_verse: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
translation_verse: “Raama ka kukɔri maa yi na, Kunkuma ni fabligu pam n nyɛ di. Reekyɛl n kumni u biisi zugu Halli ka sɔ ka ni n‑nin toaai n‑goaai u; Dama u biisi maa zaa na ka ni.”

english_verse: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka NaaHɛroodi kum nyaaŋŋa, Gyoosɛfi daa zaasi zaasimni, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa laa n‑kyɛ u saani na

english_verse: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
translation_verse: nti yɛli u, ni, “Doaama n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑labi Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu ni, dama ninvugseeba ban daa boori ba ku bii maa la kpiya.”

english_verse: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu u daa doaai n‑zaŋŋi bii maa ni u ma la n‑labi Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu ni.

english_verse: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, NaaHɛroodi bii Arikyileesi n diri u ba Hɛroodi naam n‑su Gyuudiiya maa, dabeem daa gbaai u ni u kyaŋŋi bɛni. Ka u daa zaasi zaasimni ka di wulisi u, ni, u kyɛm Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni nti zinni bɛni.

english_verse: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti zinni Nazarɛti tiŋŋa ni. Ka dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka di siri daa niŋi hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem n‑zaali la, ni#:- “Ba ti ya m‑boonni u la Nazarɛti nira.”

english_verse: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
translation_verse: n‑yɛtti, ni, “Ya kyɛli ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri zaa, dama Arizana naam bɛ la ya saani.”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Gyɔni daa yeeri la arakoomi kɔbri bunyeerigu, ka daa mari gbaŋŋu n‑loori u seaa, ka u bundirigu daa nyɛ toori, ni siiri.

english_verse: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
translation_verse: Ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑yi n‑kyɛ u saani na; ba daa yi la Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni na, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa ni, halli ni tinseesi zaa an bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari duuri m‑paasi.

english_verse: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa paai u na ka wulisiri ban tum tumbɛseesi maa, ka u daa su ba tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi kɔsuubu, Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la ni.

english_verse: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ka Naawunni nin namsi tumbeeri dima hoo nira ŋɔn maali u leefu siri ni u ŋma tiseaa kan bu wanni suŋŋasuŋŋa seem. U nin ŋma ka n‑lusi n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑lɔsi bugum ni.”

english_verse: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: U kyɛnni na; ka pun maali u neema siri ni u pii u kabuubu tampii maa ka ka niŋi kasikasi. Ka u nin yeeli u kyi maa n‑yiisi wuurigu la ka kyɛ kabeelim. Kasuma maa ka u nin la'asi n‑su kusɔkku maa ni, ka naayi zaŋŋi wuurigu maa zaa n‑vaai m‑basi bugunseebu bun ka kpiisigu puuni.”

english_verse: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
translation_verse: Amaa Gyɔni daa bu sakki, ka yɛli u, “Mani n da naa n‑kyɛ ka nyini su ma koom, ka nyin laa n‑kyɛnni n saani na, bee?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Kur kyɛ ka di niŋi lala pampaŋŋɔa. Dama di sumsi ni ti tumni bunseelli kam zaa la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali n‑ti ti seem maa.” Ka di saha maa ka Gyɔni daa sakki ka daa su u koom maa.

english_verse: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti deaai Naawunni kɔsuubu n‑naai ka daa ti yi koom maa ni na, di saha maa ka sangbana daa yoaai ka u daa nya ka Naawunni Sii daa sigri u zugu na; Sii maa mun daa ŋmani la hoo naŋmanni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa wum Naawunni kukɔri saazugu na, n‑yɛtti, ni, “M bii nŋɔa, ka n wuri u ni pam. N nini n tigi u.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, ba gbaai Gyɔni n‑kpari, u daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

english_verse: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
translation_verse: U daa bu kpalim Nazarɛti tiŋŋa, amaa u daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ Kapirinyum tiŋŋa ni, tinseaa kan zɛ mɔ'ari gbiriŋŋu ni, Zɛbulɔn ni Naftaali tingbana ziiya.

english_verse: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Ka Sintaani, yɔmsigu daana, daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, ni, “I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku kyɛ ka kuga wa leebi bundira?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “Ka di laa n‑sɔbi m‑paasi, ni, nyin di zamsi i Dugma Naawunni!”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sintaani daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑duusi n‑tɔm zɔwa'alli saazugu m‑bulisi u duniya puuni tinsi zaasa, ni a gyirima maa.

english_verse: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
translation_verse: Ka yɛli u, ni, “I yi ti gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeem ma Naawunni gyeeŋŋu, n nin zaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa n‑ti i.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “Kyɛma sa, Sintaani! Di pun sɔbi n‑dɔya, ni#:- ‘Naawunni yinni kɔkɔa, Ŋɔn nyɛ ya Dugma la, Ŋɔn sumsi gbaanigu ni gyeeŋŋu.’ ”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:-

english_verse: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
translation_verse: “Zɛbulɔn tingbaŋŋu, wumma! Naftaali tingbaŋŋu, wumma! Yan bɛ teeku la soori maa, Yan bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari sa: Yan bɛ Galilii, saamma tiŋŋa la ni, gba, wumma!#:-

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa basi ba laama maa yoomyooma ka dɔli u.

english_verse: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli la Galilii mɔ'ari duuri la nti nya niriba ayi, Saimoon ni u tunzɔ Anduru, ka ba daa lɔbri ba laama mɔ'ari maa ni, dama ba daa tumni la zingbaariba tuma.

english_verse: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tooni, u daa nya niriba ayi yaasa m‑paasi, Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii biiya, ni u tunzɔ Gyɔni; ka ban ni ba ba Zɛbidii daa bɛ nyariŋŋu ni m‑maanni ba laama. Ka Yisa daa booli ba ni ba dɔli u na.

english_verse: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba daa bakki ka kyɛ nyariŋŋu maa, halli n‑kyɛ ba ba Zɛbidii gba, ka kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gyiligi Galilii tinsi zaa n‑kpeeri ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑wulisiri ka moonni lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Arizana naam yɛla, ka tibri niriba ban daa beeri zaa bɛni ka dooru balimbalim m mari ba, bee, ban kɔŋŋni yaa ni alaafɛa.

english_verse: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u yɛla daa paai halli Siriiya tingbaŋŋu ni sa, ka niriba daa mari ba baaridima zaa n‑kyɛ u saani na, dooru ni beerim kam zaa ŋɔn mari seeba la, ni arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa kpɛ seeba, ni kpisinkpiiri ŋɔn daa gbaai seeba, ni ban nuusi bee ban gbaya n daa kpi la. Ka Yisa daa tibi ba zaa.

english_verse: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Ka Galilii dima daadam pam daa yi na n‑dɔli u, ni Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu dima, ni Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, ni Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu dima, ni tingbanseaa zaa an bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari wa sa.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
translation_verse: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa nya niriba maa, u daa kyaŋŋi nti du zoori la ni. U daa zinni bɛni ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na.

english_verse: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
translation_verse: Dama man ya n‑yɛli ya wa, ni, di yi ti pa la ya dɔli Naawunni soori suŋŋa n‑gaari zaaligu karimbadima ban dɔli seem maa, ni Farisidima ŋɔn dɔli seem maa, ya ku nya sɔseaa yan nin zaŋŋi n‑kpɛ Arizana naam ziiya.”

english_verse: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
translation_verse: Dizugu i yi ti mari i piini n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai maaligu ziiya, di yi pa seem ka bɛni ka i ti teaai, ni, i niŋi i mabisɔ taalli.

english_verse: and he taught them:
translation_verse: Ka u daa gbaai ba wulisigu n‑yɛli, ni#:-

english_verse: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: “Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban nyɛ faradima ba sufu ni, dama Arizana naam nyɛ la ba dinni.

english_verse: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban sufu n sa'amna, dama Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba sufu maai.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban sigsi ba mansi, dama ba nin di faari n‑su duniya wa.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban zɔri niriba namboo dama Naawunni nin zɔ ba gba namboo.

english_verse: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban mari sufu yinni, dama ba nin ti nya Naawunni.

english_verse: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
translation_verse: Yaasa, ya ŋmanni la neesim, ni duniya zaa ti neaai suŋŋa. Tinseaa yi mɛ n‑zɛ zoori zugu, ka ku toaai n‑laa n‑sɔ'ai.

english_verse: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
translation_verse: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di pɔ noori halli beela. Di pɔ arizana, dama Naawunni naam zizii nla.

english_verse: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
translation_verse: Mun di pɔ tingbaŋŋu, dama Naawunni napɔntɔmnigu nla. Di laa m‑pɔ Gyɛrusalɛm, dama Naaba Yiikodaana maa tiŋŋa nla.

english_verse: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ya ŋmanni la yaarim, ni duniya zaa ti nya'asa. Amaa yaarim nya'asim yi ti yi ka kyɛ di, di laa n‑ya n‑niŋi la wula ka di nya'asim maa labi na yaasa? Di saha maa di laa n‑ka anfaani, ka ba yi zaŋŋi di ni m‑basi samanni ni, ka niriba yi kyɛnni n‑noori di.

english_verse: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
translation_verse: Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑sɔ'ai luginseaa, bee n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka; see ka u na'a yi zaŋŋi ka ni, n‑tɔm bunseelli zugu ni ban kam zaa bɛ duu maa ni nin nya kan neaai seem maa.

english_verse: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya di teesi, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti sa'am Musa zaaligu ni anabidima la bulisigu yɛla. Ay! N bu kyɛ na ni n ti sa'am a ni, amaa ni n ti kyɛ ka a niŋi yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli ti yaadima, ni, ‘Di ku ninsaala.’, ka sɔ ŋɔn ku nin ti bɛ sariya karibu yɛttɔ'a ni.

english_verse: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
translation_verse: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn suuri yisigi n‑niŋi u mabiiya, u bɛ la sariya karibu ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa!’, ba nin boosi u boosa gyintooriba duu maa ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin gyɛrigu wa!’, u nin di wahala bugum ni.

english_verse: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
translation_verse: Di yi bɛ lala, see ka i kyɛ i piini maa n‑dooli maaligu zii la ni ka kyaŋŋi m‑maali yɛttɔ'a maa ka nyin ni i mabii maa nya nangbanyinni pɔi, ka naayi labi na n‑tisi.

english_verse: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
translation_verse: M‑paasi, nyin ni sɔ yi mari sariya karibu yɛttɔ'a ka nyin ni ŋɔn ti bɛ soori ni n‑kyɛnni sariya karibu ziiya, kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ ka ya maali yɛttɔ'a maa yooma, lala saha maa; di yi pa lala u nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi sarikatta nuu ni, ka ŋɔn mun nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi pirinsi nuu ni, ka ba nin kpari i dansarika duu ni.

english_verse: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, i ku laa n‑yi bɛni na see ka i ti yɔ i samni maa zaa n‑naai, halli ka kɔbɔ yinni bu kpalim gba.”

english_verse: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Ba daa yɛli, ni, ‘Dɔsɔ yi ya n‑kari u pɔ'a, see ka u sɔbi pɔ'akaribu gbaŋŋu n‑ti u.’

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “M‑paasi yaasa, ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli ti yaadima, ni, ‘Di pɔ noori ka yi di nyaaŋŋa; i yi ti pɔ, ni, i zaŋŋi bunseelli n‑ti Naawunni, see ka i siri zaŋŋi n‑tisi.’

english_verse: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
translation_verse: Kur tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti ‘Ii, ii!’, bee, ‘Ay, ay!’, dama din paasi lala zugu yi la Yɛlibeeri Daana la saani na.”

english_verse: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
translation_verse: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di zabi ni ya ta'ai ya taalli daana tuma; faasee ka sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u.

english_verse: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
translation_verse: Sɔ yi ti ya taasi i kootu ni n‑deaai i bunyeerigu, ka laa m‑boori i kyinkyinni m‑paasi, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.

english_verse: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
translation_verse: Ka sɔ yi ya mugsi i, ni, i zi u neema n‑kyaŋŋi maalli yinni, nyin ziima a n‑kyaŋŋi abayi n‑ti u.

english_verse: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ yi ti susi bunni i saani, zaŋŋma n‑ti u. Sɔ mun boori ni i pɛŋŋi u bunseelli, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.”

english_verse: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ; ya pun wum, ni, ba daa yɛli, ni, i wuri i zɔ ka yaai i dindaana.

english_verse: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
translation_verse: Amaa man yɛtti ya, ni, ya wura ya dindaandima, ka susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ban namsiri ya.

english_verse: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
translation_verse: Lala ka ya nin ti leebi bisuma n‑ti ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni. Ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka wuntaŋŋa pusiri n‑neaari ninvugbeeri m‑paasi ninvugsuma zugu; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka saa miiri n‑niŋni ban deaari u yɛla ni ban za'asiri u yɛla m‑paasi.

english_verse: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
translation_verse: Ya yi wuri la ban wuri ya, ya nin nya laara di zugu? Ay! Halli lampodeaariba gba niŋni la lala - bee, m ma'ari ni?

english_verse: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
translation_verse: Ka ya yi puusiri la ya mabiisi kɔkɔa, tunsuŋŋu bɔ ka ya tumna? Halli ban pa Izraɛli dima gba niŋni lala, bee, m ma'ari ni?

english_verse: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
translation_verse: I yi ya puusi Naawunni, di niŋi hoo birikɔnsi dima: ba boori ba zɛ saazugu m‑puusiri gyeeŋŋu duri ni, ni sɔkyira ni, ni niriba ti nya ba. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ba pun deaai ba laara zaa n‑naai.

english_verse: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
translation_verse: Nyin muna, i yi ya puusi Naawunni, nyin kpeem i duu puuni n‑yɔ kyɛburu la ka sɔ'ai m‑puusi n‑ti i Ba. Ka i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara zaa nin yɔ i.

english_verse: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ya yi ya m‑puusiri Naawunni, di mari yɛttɔ'ayɔna pam hoo ban pa Izraɛli dima ŋɔn niŋni seem. Dama ba teesi, ni, ba yi ti tɔ'asi n‑yuui, ni, Naawunni nin wum n‑ti ba dizugu.

english_verse: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
translation_verse: Yarima muna, yan di ŋmanni bana. Dama ya Ba pun mi yan boori seelli pɔi, ka ya naayi susi.

english_verse: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ ka ya nin puusi, ni#:- ‘Ti Ba, nyin bɛ la arizana ni, Kyɛ ka i yuuri du.

english_verse: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka i naam kyɛ na; Kyɛ ka ba tumni i yuubu yɛla N‑niŋi duniya wa ni, Hoo ban niŋni arizana ni seem.

english_verse: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
translation_verse: Dama ya yi kyɛ ya taaba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba, ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana la gba nin kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.

english_verse: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
translation_verse: Amaa ya yi bu kyɛ ya taaba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi, dinŋɔna ya Ba maa gba ku kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.”

english_verse: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: ka lala ka ninsaalidima ku baŋŋi i noori loobu yɛla, see ka i Ba ŋɔn bɛ la zisɔ'arli maa ni nin baŋŋi. Di saha ka i Ba ŋɔn nyari yɛlisɔ'ara nin yɔ i.”

english_verse: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
translation_verse: Ay, see ka ya daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi ya Ba naam yɛttɔ'a, ni u yɛla zaa, m‑mugsi ya mansi pɔi, ka ŋɔn nin naayi zaŋŋi bunseelli din kpalim zaasa m‑paasi ya.

english_verse: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
translation_verse: Di fabinni beoo yɛla zuna, see ka i kyɛ ka beoo maa sa ya fabli ka maŋtoori yɛla. Zuna mun mari la ka toori yɛlimugsa ka a sakki.”

english_verse: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
translation_verse: Amaa ya la'asim neenseesi an nin zaali arizana ni n‑niŋi ya arizikki maa; bɛni ka leelisi ku ŋɔbi a, bee, a ku nyaamna, ka na'ayigsi mun gba ku toaai n‑kabri n‑zuuri a.

english_verse: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
translation_verse: Dama i sufu kur ya bɛ la i arizikki ŋɔn bɛ seela.”

english_verse: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa i nini yi sa'amna, i ningbina zaa bɛ la zibsim ni. Tɔ - ka neesim din bɛ i ni maa yi nyɛ la zibsim, di nin zibgi halli nti laa n‑ya'ai.”

english_verse: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
translation_verse: Ni, “Ninvugyinni ku toaai n‑leebi daabri n‑ti dugmadima ayi: see ka u za'asi yinni yɛla ka wuri yinni la, bee u tibgi yinni la, ka laa n‑zeem ŋɔnla. Ya ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka Naawunni su ya yɛla ka kyɛ ka ligri gba su ya.”

english_verse: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
translation_verse: Dinzugu yan di fabinna, bee n‑teesiri, ni, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya di?’, bee, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya nyu?’, bee, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya yɛa?’

english_verse: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
translation_verse: Ni, “Dizugu ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, yan di zaŋŋi yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya nin niŋi seem n‑nya bundirigu n‑di, bee n‑nya situra n‑yɛa - yan di fabli anŋɔ zaa yɛla; dama nyɔvuri gaari bundirigu, ka ningbina gaari bunyeerigu, bee m ma'ari ni?

english_verse: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
translation_verse: Ya gɔsim niinsi ban bɛ saazugu la; ba bu butta, ba mun bu la'asiri bundirigu n‑niŋni napaari ni, amaa ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la diisiri ba ni: ka yan mun mari bukaata n‑gaari ba, bee m ma'ari ni?

english_verse: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
translation_verse: Ka wula zugu ka ya zaŋŋi bunyeera yɛla m‑mugsiri ya mansi? Ya baŋŋma pɔ'a-veelli-ku-siisi-koom la yɛla: gɔsim n‑nya kan zoaari seem moo ni, ka bu tumni n‑waanni gumni.

english_verse: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
translation_verse: Amaa n yɛtti ya wa, ni, halli NaaSulɛmaana ni u naam gyirima zaasa gba daa na zi m‑bɔbi suturiveela kaman mɔpuuma wa yinni veelim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni yi zaŋŋi veelim wa tataabu n‑niŋi mɔseesi an bɛ ni zuna ka beoo ka a sa nin nyɔ bugum m‑basi maa, wula zugu ka u ku yeeli yarima sutura? - Ya yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa!

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban bu mi Naawunni la bɛ la anŋɔ zaa nɔba karibu ni. Amaa yarima mari ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni maa, ka u mi halli, ni, ya boori la anŋɔ zaa.

english_verse: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
translation_verse: Ka yan ma'asiri n‑tiri ya taaba seem, lala ka Naawunni nin ti ma'asi n‑ti ya gba.”

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: U daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, “Ni wula ka i nya muŋŋa i mabii nimbiri ni ka zaantɔliga dee n‑sa i maŋtoori dinni ni ka i bu nya ka?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
translation_verse: Bee wula ka i nin toaai n‑yɛli i mabiiya, ni, u kyɛ ka i yiisi munseaa kan bɛ u nimbiri ni, ka zaantɔliga maa kpalim m‑bɛ i maŋtoori dinni ni?

english_verse: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
translation_verse: Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Nyin daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni!”

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
translation_verse: Tɔ! ya nyɛ la ninvugseeba bam mari teesibeeri ya beebu ni, ka halli yarima gba yi mi yan zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑tiri ya biisi; ka dinŋɔna ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ saazugu ku zaŋŋi u piinisuma n‑ti ban susiri u?”

english_verse: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
translation_verse: Sɔ m bɛ ya puuni ka u biiya nin susi paanu ka u mɔŋŋi, ka zaŋŋi kugri n‑ti u? - Ay!

english_verse: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya susima ka nin deaai yan susi seelli maa; ya booma ka nin nya yan boori seelli maa; ya kpaasima ka ba nin yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑ti ya.

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔnkam susira deaari ni; ŋɔnkam boora nyari ni; ka ŋɔnkam kpaasira, ba nin yoaai n‑ti u.

english_verse: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Bee, u yi ti susi ziŋŋa ka u mɔŋŋi ka zaŋŋi waafu n‑ti u? - Ay!

english_verse: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
translation_verse: Ni, “Dinŋɔna yan boori ni niriba niŋi seem n‑ti ya zaa, see ka ya niŋi lala n‑ti ba gba. Zaaligu yɛla maa zaa nla, ni anabidima maa bulisigu m‑paasi.”

english_verse: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
translation_verse: Ni, “Dizugu ŋɔnkam zaa wum n yɛligu ka dɔli ka, u ŋmanni la yam daansɔ ŋɔn daa mɛ u yiri n‑tɔm tampii zugu.

english_verse: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
translation_verse: Sɔseaa n bɛ ni, ka ka za'anɔri poora, ka soori maa bu yɛlima; ka mun wa la nyɔvuri ziiya, amaa niriba ban nya ka n‑dɔla bu zoaai.”

english_verse: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ya baŋŋma, ka ziri anabidima m bɛ ni. Ba ŋmani la kunduna ban yeeri peesi gbana, ni ba ti la'am ni peesi maa n‑gbaai ba.

english_verse: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Ba wala nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ba. Goaa bu wanni sisibi ka kpikpaliga mun ku toaai n‑wali kinkama.

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
translation_verse: Tiseaa kan mari alaafɛ wanni la walisuma. Tiseaa kan ka alaafɛ muna - walibeeri ka ka nin wali.

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Tiseaa kan mari alaafɛ ku toaai n‑wali walibeeri, halli ka kan ka alaafɛ ku toaai n‑wali bunsuma.

english_verse: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
translation_verse: Tiseelli kam kan bu wanni walisuma, ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi bugum ni.

english_verse: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka n yɛliya, ni, ba wala - di nyɛ la ba tuma maa - nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi ba.”

english_verse: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa mun wum n yɛligu ka bu dɔli ka, u ŋmanni la gyɛrigu sɔ ŋɔn daa mɛ u yiri n‑tɔm tambisigu ni.

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
translation_verse: Saa daa mi pam, ka mɔ'a daa paali n‑yakki m‑pɔbri na, ka pɛbsigu daa dam pam. Ka yiri maa daa lu zaa, ka ka luuwa daa bu veela gba.”

english_verse: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Pa seeba ban boonni ma, ni, ‘N Dugma, n Dugma’ zaa n nin kpɛ arizana naam ziiya, amaa seeba ban tumni m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la yuubu maa nin ti kpɛa.

english_verse: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
translation_verse: Dabsiri maa yi ti paai na, niriba pam nin ti yɛli ma, ni, ‘Ti Dugma, ti Dugma! I yuuri ni ka ti daa yɛli anabitiri yɛla; i yuuri ni ka ti daa kari arizimbeeri m‑basi; i yuuri ni ka ti daa tum alahazibu tuma pam! Bee, ti ma'ari ni?’

english_verse: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka n nin mooli n‑yɛli ba, ni, ‘Man zi ya kɔtaa! Ya zɔma ka kyɛ ma, yan tumbeeri dima wa!’ ”

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
translation_verse: Saa daa mi pam, ka mɔ'a daa paali n‑yakki m‑pɔbri na, ka pɛbsigu daa dam pam. Ka di zaa yɔri, yiri maa daa bu lu, ka nyeeba ŋɔn tɔm tampii maa ni zugu.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli anŋɔ zaa n‑naai maa, di daa niŋi daadam maa alahazibu pam u yɛttɔ'a maa zugu.

english_verse: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Dama u daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'amaŋni, ka u bu birisira halli beela; ni, di pa la kaman ba zaaligu baŋŋniba la ŋɔn yi wulisiri seem la.

english_verse: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
translation_verse: Halli ka seeba ban nyɛ naam zuliya dima maa, ba nin yiisi ba m‑basi, ni ba ti paai wa'aligu ya'aseelli ban ŋɔn bɛ zibsim ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.”

english_verse: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Ba yi ti mugsi ya tinseaa ni, ya kur yima n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa yaasa. Dama yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya na ku kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Izraɛli tingbaŋŋu tinsi zaa n‑naai ka Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na.

english_verse: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - karimbii bu gatti u karimba, ka daabri bu gatti u dugma.

english_verse: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
translation_verse: Karimbii yi ti paai u karimba tataabu, di nin sakki u ni. Ka daabri yi paai u dugma saani gba, di sakkiya. Ba yi zaŋŋi Arizimbeeri Naa yuuri m‑pa yiriseaa yiridaana, ni, Biyɛlizibuli n nyɛ u, ba bu ya booli u yiri maa dima lala? - halli ni yuseesi an bɛ n‑gaari lala.”

english_verse: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
translation_verse: Ni, “Yaasa, yan di zɔri ninvugseeba ban kuuri niriba ningbina, ka ku toaai n‑kuuri ba siisi m‑paasi. Amaa ya zɔm sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑sa'am ningbina ni siisi zaa ka zaŋŋi a n‑lɔsi bugum ni la.

english_verse: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
translation_verse: Ba bu zaŋŋi la'abila yinni n‑daari niimbisi ayi? Amaa din zaa yɔri, niimbila yinni ku toaai n‑lu tiŋŋa ka di pa la ya Ba maa yuubu ni.

english_verse: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
translation_verse: Dizugu yan di zɔri dabeem, dama yarim mari bukyi pam n‑gaari niinsi maa zaa.”

english_verse: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam zaani niriba tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la man dinni, man gba nin ti zaani m Ba tooni arizana ni, n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔn nyɛ la n dinni.

english_verse: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔnkam mun yi za'asi ma niriba tooni, man gba nin za'asi u m Ba tooni arizana ni.”

english_verse: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ya di teesi, ni, m mari la nangbanyinni beesigu n‑kyɛ duniya wa na. Ay! M bu kyɛ na ni m mari nangbanyinni yɛla na, see zaba.

english_verse: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
translation_verse: N kyɛ na ni n tugli la doowa ni u ba, ni bipuŋŋa ni u ma gba, ni bii pɔ'a ni u dinyaampɔ'a m‑paasi, ka ba zabi.

english_verse: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
translation_verse: Lala ka nira yi ti mari dindaana, u toori yiri puuni ka di daama nin bɛ ni.

english_verse: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn wuri u ba, bee, u ma, n‑gaari ma, di daama bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani; ka ŋɔn laa n‑wuri u biiya, doo bee pɔ'a, n‑gaari ma, ŋɔn gba bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani.

english_verse: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: Halli ŋɔn mun ku sakki n‑zaŋŋi u kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli n nyaaŋŋa, ŋɔn gba bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani.

english_verse: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.”

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa gyaari anabi ŋɔn nyɛ anabi maa zugu, ŋɔn gba nin deaai laara seaa kan nyɛ anabi dinni. Ka ŋɔnkam mun gyaari yɛlimaŋni daana ŋɔn nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana maa zugu, ŋɔn gba nin deaai laara seaa kan nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana dinni.

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam zaa gyaari ya, mani ka u gyaari. Ka ŋɔnkam gyaari ma, Naawunni ka u gyaatira, dama ŋɔn Naawunni n tum ma na.

english_verse: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
translation_verse: Halli ŋɔnkam zaa mun yi ti zaŋŋi kɔmaasim n‑ti n nyaandɔlisɔ ni u ti nyu, ŋɔn nyɛ n nyaandɔla maa zugu, yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, di daama gba ku toaai n‑kɔŋŋi u laara.”

english_verse: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Gyɔni nyaandɔliba maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi, ka Yisa mun daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑ti daadam maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɔni yɛla, ka boosiri ba, ni, “Bɔ ka ya daa kyaŋŋi mooni ni ya ti nya? Kyimnigu ka pɛbsigu zeera, bee?

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna Gyɔni daa kyɛ na ka u ba'a ka diibu ni nyuubu yɛla ni, ka ya yɛli, ni, arizimbeoo n zi u zugu ni.

english_verse: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kpaam u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi tinkpanseesi an daa tabi bɛni maa n‑wulisiri m‑moonni yɛligu maa n‑tiri niriba.

english_verse: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
translation_verse: Di saha ka Gyɔni daa bɛ dansarika ni - Gyɔni sɔ ŋɔn daa su niriba Naawunni koom la. U daa ti wum Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni seem, ka daa tum u nyaandɔliba Yisa saani na,

english_verse: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: ni, “Nyin n nyɛ ŋɔn ya kyɛ na la, bee ti na gɔsim di daama soori?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli Gyɔni niriba wa, ni, “Ya labma nti yɛli Gyɔni yan wumni ka nyari seela:

english_verse: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: ni, zoomma nini neaara, ka gbarisi kyɛnna, ka ban mari kɔmni nyari alaafɛa, ka tubkpira daandima wumna, halli ka ban kpi gba neaari kum ni; ni, ka lahabasuŋŋu ka ba moonni n‑tiri faradima.”

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam bu birisiri n yɛla zugu mari zugsuŋŋu!”

english_verse: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Bee bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi suŋŋa? Ya bu nya? - ninvugseeba ban yeeri bunveela n kur bɛ nadima yiya ni.”;

english_verse: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: ka laa m‑boosi ba n‑yɛli, “Ka bɔ zugu ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi? - ni ya ti nya anabisɔ, bee? Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, halli u gaari anabi gba.

english_verse: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
translation_verse: Amaa yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura gaari bidibseeba pɔ'aba ŋɔn pun dɔ'ai la zaa; amaa ka n ya yɛli ya yaasa, ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn poora zaa ka bɛ Naawunni naam ni, di daama yi gaari ŋɔna.”

english_verse: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Sahanseaa ka Gyɔni daa mooli u mooligu la, halli nti paai zuna, arizana naam yɛla ka ba moori n‑gbaari ni nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
translation_verse: ni, ‘Ti peebi yiiya n‑ti ya ka ya za'asi waa, ka ti laa m‑maanni la kuuri n‑ti ya ka ya bu sakki n‑kaasira!’

english_verse: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
translation_verse: Ka man mun Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na ka man ni niriba la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura, ka ya yɛli, ni, ‘Ya bu nya doo wa ŋɔn nyɛ beem daana ni danyuura seem? - ka u laa n‑lɔ zoori ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi daandima.’ Amaa Naawunni yam tumni n‑wulisiri kamaŋŋa yɛla.”

english_verse: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa gbaai tinsi maa galiŋŋu - tinseesi ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni n‑tumni u alahazibu tuma ba saani la; ka di zaa yɔri ba daa za'asi, ni, ba ku wum u yɛligu n‑kyɛ ba tumbeeri ni ba teesibeeri yɛla.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
translation_verse: Yarima muna, ya Kapirinyum dima wa, ya bu duusiri la ya mansi m‑paai arizana ni? Amaa ba nin sigsi ya kpiimma tiŋŋa ni sa. Dama alahazibu tunseesi an daa tum i saani maa yi daa tum la Sɔdoom tiŋŋa puuni, ka daa naa n‑kpalim m‑bɛ ni, halli ni zuna.

english_verse: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
translation_verse: Amaa n yɛtti ya, ni, Zaadaari halli Sɔdoom ninvugbeeri wahala gba ku paai ya dinni.”

english_verse: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
translation_verse: Di saha maa Yisa daa yɛliya, “M Ba Naawunni, nyin ŋɔn su arizana ni duniya wa, m pɛ'ari i ni, dama nyin zaŋŋi yɛla wa zaa n‑sɔ'ai ka kyɛ yam dima ni baŋŋniba, ka laa n‑kasigi a n‑wulisi n‑ti bileesi.

english_verse: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Ii, m Ba, yɛlimaŋni lala veela i saani.

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: Bunseelli kam zaa ka m pun deaai m Ba nuu ni. Sɔ ka ni m‑mi Bii maa na'ala u Ba maa m mi u; ka sɔ mun gba ka ni m‑mi m Ba maa na'ala mani u Bii maa m mi u; m‑paasi sɔkam man u Bii maa ŋɔn nin sakki n‑kasigi m Ba maa yɛla n‑wulisi n‑ti u la.”

english_verse: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ya ninvugseeba ban zi zikara n‑gyɛ zaa, ya kyɛma n saani na, ka n nin kyɛ ka ya vuusi.

english_verse: Take the yoke I give you. Put it on your shoulders and learn from me. I am gentle and humble, and you will find rest.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka man ni yarim la'am n‑tumna: di ŋmani la hoo nigi ayi ŋɔn darigri na'adoo seem, ya nin ti bamsi tuma man saani, dama ninvugmaŋni n nyɛ ma, ka laa m‑mari sufubaalim. Ya nin vuusi ni sufudoaa.

english_verse: This yoke is easy to bear, and this burden is light.
translation_verse: Dama n na'adoo wa ku ŋmaari ya, ka n ziya maa fa'asi ni.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees noticed this and said to Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do this!”
translation_verse: Ka Farisidima daa nya lala, ka yɛli u, “Nyama! I nyaandɔliba wa tumni la puri tuma. Ba galimni zaaligu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri wa.”

english_verse: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
translation_verse: Ka m mun zaŋŋi la Naawunni Siiya n‑katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni, dinŋɔna Naawunni naam daŋŋi m‑paai ya na.

english_verse: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Bee, ya na zi n‑karim Musa zaaligu yɛla? Ni, maalimaanniba tumni la ba tuma Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni Vuusim Dabsiri. Ban n tumni la Vuusim Dabsiri maa ka bu deaari taalli di zugu.

english_verse: He went into the house of God, and then they ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are supposed to eat.
translation_verse: ka u daa kpɛ Naawunni gyeeŋŋu zii maa, n‑zaŋŋi boroboro n‑ŋɔbi. Pa boroboro anŋɔ taabu ka ba dooli Naawunni tooni ka a kyisiri ni? Ii! A kyisiri n‑tiri u taaba - halli n‑tiri u mammaŋŋa gba - see maalimaanniba kɔkɔ n ŋɔbri a.

english_verse: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
translation_verse: Sɔppu la yɛliya, ni, ‘Man Naawunni bu boori ni ya maanni m‑maanni bunkɔbri wa, amaa m boori ya zɔri niriba namboo.’ Ya siri da mi dinŋɔ gbinni, ya naa n‑ku galim niriba ka ba bu niŋi taalli halli beela.

english_verse: Jesus left and went into one of their synagogues,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa bakki bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu duu ni.

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u yɛli nukpiiŋŋu daana maa, ni, “Teaam i nuu maa!” Ka u daa teaai ka u nuu maa daa nya tippu n‑leebi hoo kanla.

english_verse: When Jesus found out what was happening, he left there and large crowds followed him. He healed all of their sick,
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, u daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi mɔ'ari ni, ka niriba pam daa dɔli u. U daa tibi ba baaridima zaa,

english_verse: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: amaa ka daa kpamsi ba, ni, ba di yɛli daadam maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ daa niŋi ni di kyɛ ka seelikam siri ti niŋi hoo anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la: ni#:-

english_verse: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
translation_verse: “Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- ‘N bii nŋɔa, mani n yiisi u: N wuri u, ka n sufu laa m‑pɛligi u zugu. N nin zaŋŋi n Sii Kasi n‑niŋi u ni, U nin wulisi n sariya karibu yɛla N‑ti tinsi zaa niriba maa.

english_verse: If you are not on my side, you are against me. If you don't gather in the harvest with me, you scatter it.
translation_verse: Ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ku sakki ni u zɛ n nyaaŋŋa, ŋɔna n za'asi ma n‑yi n nyaaŋŋa; bee sɔ yi ku suŋŋi ma ni ti la'asi bunwurima, u wurimni a m‑basira.

english_verse: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ninvugseeba daa zi dɔsɔ ŋɔn nyɛ zooma ka bu toaai n‑tɔ'asiri n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na; doo maa daa bɛ lala dama arizimbeoo daa bɛ u ni. Ka Yisa daa tibi zɔligu wa ka kyɛ la u laa n‑tɔ'asiri ka nyari yaasa.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa mi ba n daa teesiri seem, ka di zugu u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Tinseelli kam zaa kan timbiisi yi purigi n‑zabri taaba la nin sa'am zaa. Bee, tinseaa, bee, yiriseaa, m bɛ ni kan niriba yi purigi n‑zabri taaba, lala tiŋŋa, bee, yiri, ku toaai n‑zaani.

english_verse: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Ni, n zaŋŋi la Biyɛlizibuli yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri maa, ka dinŋɔna ya karimbiseeba ban katti ba la - ba zaŋŋi ŋɔni dinni n‑katti ba? Dizugu ban nin ti di ya yɛttɔ'a wa sariya n‑lusi ya.

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when the demon doesn't find a place,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Tɔ; ka arizimbɛsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa kpɛ dɔsɔ puuni n‑su u. Ka ba daa yiisi u ŋɔn daa bɛ dɔsɔ puuni maa ni n‑kari m‑basi. Ka u daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gyinni yoo ni, m‑bɛ kukoo ni m‑boori vuusim ziiya, halli nti gyɛa.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and the person ends up in worse shape than before. That's how it will be with you evil people of today. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Mark
translation_verse: ka dizugu u daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi u arizintaaba ayɔpɔi seeba ban bɛ n‑gaari u mammaŋŋa, ni ba ti paasi u zugu, ka ba daa kpɛ nti bɛ bɛni. Di saha maa ka di daama beesigu daa bɛ pam n‑gaari din daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ seem. Ka di kur bɛ la lala zuna niriba saani.”

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit. You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛ ka tiseaa nyɛ tisuŋŋu, ka ka nin wanni walisuma. Di yi pa lala, tibeoo n nyɛ ka, ka ka nin wanni walibeeri. Ba gɔsiri la tii kam zaa wala ni ba ti baŋŋi tii maa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
translation_verse: Ya bunkyɔ'ama wa! Ya kur ŋmanni la wa'abeeri! Ya ya niŋi la wula n‑yɛtti din veela, ka ya mammansi n nyɛ ninvugbeeri? Dama dinkam bɛ ninsaala sufu ni nti paali ka, dina ka u yiisiri u noori ni na.

english_verse: I promise you on the day of judgment, everyone will have to account for every careless word they have spoken.
translation_verse: Man yɛtti ya wa, ni, sariya karibu Zaadaari ba nin boosi sɔkam boosa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'ayɔya kam zaa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi.

english_verse: On that day they will be told they are either innocent or guilty because of the things they have said. A Sign from Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Dama i noori yɛligu zugu ka ba nin yiisi i na, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana; ka i noori yɛligu zugu yaasa ka ba nin lusi i m‑basi.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka karimba dimseeba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u, ni Farisii dimseeba m‑paasi, n‑yɛli, ni, “Ti Karimba, ti boori la i tum alahazibu tunseaa n‑wulisi ti.”

english_verse: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Zuna niriba wa nyɛ la ninvugbeeri ban boori zina. Ba kur bɛ la alahazibu nyaabu ni, ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛla, amaa ba ku ti nya sɛseelli m‑paasi Anabi Gyoona alahazibu yɛla maa.

english_verse: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
translation_verse: Gyoona ŋɔn daa bɛ zintitaari maa puu ni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata maa, lala ka man Ninsaala Bii maa gba nin bɛ tingbaŋŋu puuni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata bɛni.

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Nineveh will stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah.
translation_verse: Niniva dima gba daa wum Gyoona wa'azu yiisigu la ka daa kyɛ ba tumbeeri m‑basi ka niŋi tuuba, ka dizugu ba nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari maa, dama ŋɔn gaari anabi Gyoona bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.

english_verse: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Pɔ'a-naaba sɔ ŋɔn daa su nudirigu luga tinseaa la daa yi wa'aligu n‑kyɛ na ni u ti wum NaaSulɛmaana yam yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu u nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari maa, dama ŋɔn gaari NaaSulɛmaana bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.”

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa na tɔ'asiri n‑tiri zama maa, u ma ni u tunzɔppa daa paai na n‑zaani samanni, ni, ba boori n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti u.

english_verse: Someone told Jesus, “Your mother and brothers are standing outside and want to talk with you.”
translation_verse: Ka ninvugsɔ daa yɛli u, ni, “I ma ni i tunzɔppa bɛ samanni m‑boori n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti i.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “M ma n nyɛ ŋɔni? ni, n tunzɔppa n nyɛ bandima?”,

english_verse: Then he pointed to his disciples and said, “These are my mother and my brothers!
translation_verse: ka daa teaai u nuu m‑bugsi u nyaandɔliba maa ka daa yɛli, “Nnya ba! M ma ni n tunzɔppa maa nŋɔa!

english_verse: Anyone who obeys my Father in heaven is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa tumni m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni la yuubu nyɛ la n tunzɔa, bee n tunzɔpɔ'a, bee m ma.”

english_verse: Such large crowds gathered around him that he had to sit in a boat, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Daadam pam daa la'am u saani na, ka dizugu u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni nti zinni. Nyariŋŋu maa daa bɛ la koom puuni ka daadam maa daa zɛ mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu.

english_verse: Then he taught them many things by using stories. He said: A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: Ka u daa tɔ'asi n‑tiri ba n‑nyɛsiri ba nyɛsa yɛla pam; ni, “Dɔsɔ n daa kyaŋŋi u puu, ni u ti waali u bumbura;

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Ka u bumburiseesi mun daa lu kuga ziiya ka bɛni tanni daa bu zoaai, ka a daa tɔ bula yoomyooma dama tanni maa daa pɔsigi a.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Ka seesi gba daa lu goosi ni, ka goosi maa daa zoaai na m‑mugsi n‑gabi kabula maa.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Why do you use stories to speak to the people?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na nti boosi u, ni, “Bɔ n kyɛ ka i tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'anyɛsa wa n‑tiri ba?”

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more. But people who don't have anything will lose even what little they have.
translation_verse: Sɔkam zaa ŋɔn mari seelli nin nya m‑paasi halli ka di ti zoaai, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli maa ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn mari seelli fiiŋ maa gba, ba nin ti fa a ka kyɛ u yɔri.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “These people will listen and listen, but never understand. They will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: Dizugu anabi Aizaaya yɛlisim maa daa yɛli n‑ti la bana, ni#:- ‘Ti Dugma yɛliya, ni#:- “Ya nin wum, m‑maali n‑wum, Amaa ya ku baŋŋi di gbinni. Ya nin gɔsi m‑maali n‑gɔsi, Amaa ya ku nya di.

english_verse: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
translation_verse: Dama n yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, sahaseaa din gaari ka anabidima ni ninvugsuma pam daa boori ba da nya yan nyari bunseesi wa, ka mun bu nya a; ka daa laa m‑boori ba wum yan wumni seelli wa, ka daa bu wum.”

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it at once.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ mun ŋɔn ŋmani la bumbura an lu kuga zugu la n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn yi wum yɛligu maa sahanseaa la, u yi deaai ka la ni sufupeelli;

english_verse: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message about the kingdom, but don't understand it. Then the evil one comes and snatches the message from their hearts.
translation_verse: Sɔkam ŋɔn wum Naawunni naam lahabaari maa ka bu baŋŋi di gbinni maa ka Yɛlibeeri Daana maa kyɛ na n‑fa yɛligu maa u sufu ni, ŋɔna n ŋmanni la bumburiseesi an lu sɔkpaŋŋa.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they start worrying about the needs of this life and are fooled by the desire to get rich. So the message gets choked out, and they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ŋmanni bumbura an lu goosi ni la n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn yi wum yɛligu maa pɔi, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi duniya wa yɛla m‑mugsi u maŋŋa, ni bundaantirigu yɛla gba; ka dizugu ka Naawunni yɛligu bu toaai m‑bunni u sufu ni nti zoaai na n‑wanni tunsuma wala.

english_verse: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and understand the message. They produce as much as
translation_verse: Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ŋmanni la bumbura an lu tam zii la n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn wum lahabaari maa ka baŋŋi di gbinni la, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin wanni tunsuma wala - halli kɔbkɔbga, bee pisiyooyoobu, bee pisitata.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a farmer scattered good seed in a field.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa m‑paasi, ni, “Arizana naam maa ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u daa buri bumburisuma u puu.

english_verse: But while everyone was sleeping, an enemy came and scattered weed seeds in the field and then left.
translation_verse: Ka yunseaa, sɔkam zaa ŋɔn daa ti gbisi, ka u dindaana daa kyaŋŋi nti buri niimbɛŋni n‑niŋi puu maa bumbula maa sunsukkuni, ka bakki.

english_verse: When the plants came up and began to mature, the farmer's servants could see the weeds.
translation_verse: Ka bumbula maa ŋɔn daa ti zoaai ka kyi maa daa ti ya niŋi n‑yirigi, di saha maa ka niimbɛŋni gba daa ti bunni na.

english_verse: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
translation_verse: Ka yiri maa daana tuntumniba daa kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, ‘Ti dugma, pa bumburisuma ka i daa buri i puu maa ni? Ka niimbɛŋni wa yi la yani?’

english_verse: “An enemy did this,” he replied. His servants then asked, “Do you want us to go out and pull up the weeds?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, ‘N dindaansɔ n niŋi lala maa.’ Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, ‘Dinŋɔna ti kyɛma n‑vɔ niimbɛŋni maa m‑basi?’

english_verse: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, it grows larger than any garden plant and becomes a tree. Birds even come and nest on its branches.
translation_verse: Ka nyɛ la bunseelli bumbura kam zaa ŋɔn galisi n‑gaari ka; amaa kan daa ti zoaai n‑niŋi buntitaari n‑gaari zeeri bumbula zaa, ka daa leebi hoo tiiya, halli ka niinsi daa tɛ ba teeri ka zugu ka nyari ka vaari maasim.”

english_verse: Jesus told them another story: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a farmer plants a mustard seed in a field.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa m‑paasi, ni, “Arizana naam ŋmanni la hoo zinzambeelli nira ŋɔn zaŋŋi m‑buri u puu ni.

english_verse: Jesus used stories when he spoke to the people. In fact, he did not tell them anything without using stories.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Anŋɔ zaa ka Yisa daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri niriba la, ka daa kur zaŋŋi ma'asa ni nyɛsa n‑tɔ'asira. U daa bu tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseelli ka ma'asa ka di ni.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I will use stories to speak my message and to explain things hidden since the creation of the world.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Weeds
translation_verse: Lala daa kyɛ ka anabi la yɛligu la ti niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:- “N nin tɔ'asi n yɛttɔ'a n‑tiri ba - Yɛttɔ'anyɛsa ka n nin tɔ'asira: N nin kasigi yɛlisɔ'ara n‑ti ba Ba na zi m‑baŋŋi yɛliseesi, Halli duniya naaŋŋu saha zaŋŋi m‑paai zuna.”

english_verse: After Jesus left the crowd and went inside, his disciples came to him and said, “Explain to us the story about the weeds in the wheat field.”
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti kyeoosi zama maa n‑ti ba soori ni ba bakki, u daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri maa ni. U daa bɛ bɛni, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, “Yɛlima ti niimbɛŋni an bɛ puu ni la nyɛsiri la gbinni!”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The one who scattered the good seed is the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ; dɔsɔ ŋɔn buri bumburisuma la n nyɛ man Ninsaala Biiya.

english_verse: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
translation_verse: Puu maa n nyɛ duniya wa, ka bumbulisuma maa n nyɛ ninvugseeba ban su naam maa beebu soori, niimbɛŋni bumbulibeeri mun n nyɛ ninvugseeba ban bɛ Yɛlibeeri Daana la suulinsi ni.

english_verse: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
translation_verse: Dindaansɔ ŋɔn daa buri bumbeeri la nyɛ la Sintaani; kaŋmaa saha n nyɛ duniya wa naari, ka ban ŋmaari maa n nyɛ malaakadima.

english_verse: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
translation_verse: Niimbɛŋni la ŋɔn la'asi n‑nyɔ seem la, lala ka di ti ya niŋi duniya naari Zaadaari maa.

english_verse: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
translation_verse: Lala saha ka Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti tum u malaakadima ka ba zaŋŋi bankam zaa daa kyɛ ka niriba tum tumbeeri, ni bankam zaa daa niŋi yɛlibeeri, n‑la'asi n‑yiisi u naam maa ni m‑basi.

english_verse: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Ka ba nin ti zaŋŋi ba n‑lɔsi bugum ni, ya'aseaa ban nin bɛ ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.

english_verse: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
translation_verse: Di saha ka Naawunni niriba nin nya'ara, hoo wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn nya'ari seem maa, m‑bɛ ba Ba naam maa ni. Ŋɔnkam mari tuba n nin wum, ŋɔn wumma!”

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
translation_verse: Ni, “Arizana naam ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- di daa niŋiya ni, ka dɔsɔ daa kyaŋŋi nti nya arizikki kan pi tiŋŋa, ka u gbi tanni n‑labsi m‑pi ka. Ka daa kuli nti koosi u marigu zaa n‑labi na nti da lala tingbanni maa.”

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
translation_verse: Ni, “Arizana naam laa n‑ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- walima daansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa goori m‑boori kugsuma, bumpeela nyain, an ligri daa zoaai pam.

english_verse: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti nya seaa ka ka veeli n‑gaari a zaa, u daa labi yiri ni nti koosi u marigu zaa n‑kyaŋŋi nti da ka.”

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
translation_verse: Ni, “Arizana naam laa n‑ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- zingbaariba daa lɔbi ba laama mɔ'ari ni n‑gba zaasim balibu kam zaa.

english_verse: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
translation_verse: Laaŋŋu maa daa paali la ni zaasim. Tɔ, ka ba darigi ka n‑du mɔ'ari gbiriŋŋu nti yiisi a n‑gamsi. Ba daa zaŋŋi bunsuma la n‑niŋi ba peeri ni; ka daa zaŋŋi bunseesi an bu veeli la n‑lɔbi m‑basi.

english_verse: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
translation_verse: Lala ti yɛ nyɛ di duniya naari daari; malaakadima la nin gamsi ninvugbeeri la ninvugsuma la puuni

english_verse: Then those evil people will be thrown into a flaming furnace, where they will cry and grit their teeth in pain. New and Old Treasures
translation_verse: n‑zaŋŋi ba n‑lɔsi bugum ni, ya'aseelli ban nin kumni ka ŋɔbri ba nyina la.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, “Ammi, ya baŋŋi anŋɔ zaa gbinni, bee?” Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ii.”

english_verse: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Dinŋɔ wulisiri, ni, zaaligu karimbasɔ ŋɔn labgi Arizana naam yɛla nyaandɔla ŋmanni la yiridaana ŋɔn kpeeri u neema duu ni n‑yiisiri bumpaala na, ka yiisi bunkura na m‑paasi.”

english_verse: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli ba nyɛsa wa n‑naai, u daa bakki bɛni

english_verse: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: n‑labi u maŋtoori tiŋŋa. U daa bɛ la ba la'aŋŋu duu maa ni nti wulisi ba. Ka alahazibu daa gbaai ba, ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, “Yani ka doo wa lee n bamsi u yɛla wa zaa, ka laa n‑nya yiiko n‑tumni tunkara wa?

english_verse: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
translation_verse: Pa kampinta bii maa nŋɔa? Pa u ma n nyɛ Mariama? - ka u tunzɔppa n nyɛ Gyeemsi, ni Gyoosɛfi, ni Saimoon, ni Gyuudasi la?

english_verse: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
translation_verse: U tunzɔpɔ'aba zaa gba bu bɛ la ti saani? Ka yani ka u lee n‑nya anŋɔ zaa?”

english_verse: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
translation_verse: Lala zugu ba daa bu boori u yɛla halli beela. Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Anabi kam nyari gyirima luginseelli kam zaa ni, na'ala u mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa niriba ni u yiri dima saani.”

english_verse: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
translation_verse: Ka u daa bu tum tunkara pam bɛni, ban daa bu niŋi Naawunni yɛdda maa zugu.

english_verse: When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi nyariŋŋu maa puuni na, u daa laa n‑nya daadam maa, ka u sufu daa zɔ ba namboo, ka u daa tibi ba baaridima maa.

english_verse: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Tɔ, kyɛm na!” Ka Piita daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni n‑tɔm koom maa zugu ka gbaai kyɛnni ni u ti paai Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela.

english_verse: so much he swore to give her whatever she wanted.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, ni, u nin ti u ŋɔn boori seelli kam zaa.

english_verse: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
translation_verse: ka tɔ'asi n‑ti u kpambaya, ni, “Gyɔni sɔ ŋɔn daa suuri niriba koom la nŋɔa, ŋɔna n doaai kum ni; lala zugu n kyɛ ka u mari yiiko n‑tumni alahazibu tuma wa.”

english_verse: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
translation_verse: Ka di gbinni n‑daa nŋɔa: Pɔ'asɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Hɛroodiyasi. Ŋɔn Hɛroodiyasi daa nyɛ la NaaHɛroodi beeri, Filipi, pɔ'a, ka Naaba daa fa u n‑di u. Dizugu ka Gyɔni daa yɛli Naa maa, ni, “Di ka soori ni i di pɔ'a maa!” Ka u pɔ'a Hɛroodiyasi zugu daa kyɛ ka NaaHɛroodi daa tum n‑gbaai Gyɔni m‑ba u kutti ka kpeesi u dansarika.

english_verse: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Ka bii maa ma daa wulisi u ŋɔn ya n‑yɛli seem, ka u daa susi Naaba, ni, u boori la Gyɔni Kɔsuura zugu, ka ba fikki ka n‑niŋi laa ni m‑mari na n‑ti u.

english_verse: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi laa ni m‑mari na nti ti bipuŋŋa maa, ka u daa mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti u ma la.

english_verse: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ na nti zaŋŋi u ningbiŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai, ka naayi kyaŋŋi n‑yɛli Yisa.

english_verse: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
translation_verse: Amaa Piita ŋɔn daa ti nya pɛsigu ŋɔn zeeri seem la, dabeem daa gbaai u ka u daa piili n‑simni koom maa ni. U daa kaasi n‑yɛli, “Faam ma, n dugma!”

english_verse: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, “Ti ka diibu kpa, m‑paasi paanu bunkpila anu, ni zaasim ayi kɔkɔa.”

english_verse: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
