translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum Gyɔni yɛla maa, u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba kɔkɔa. Ka niriba maa daa wum lala ka daa yi tinsi zaa ni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli mɔ'aduuri maa ka kari u nɔba.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ba ku kyaŋŋi seela. Ya tim ba seelli ka ba di.”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, ba mara a na n‑ti u.

english_verse: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa yɛli niriba maa, ni, ba zinma moori la zugu. Ka u daa zaŋŋi paanu anu ni zaasim ayi maa n‑gɔsi saazugu m‑puusi Naawunni ka ŋmaai paanudima maa n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba deaai n‑ti zama maa.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ba zaa daa di n‑tigi, ka ba daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri, ka ŋɔn mammaŋŋa daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa.

english_verse: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu, ni u ti bɛ bɛni u kɔkɔ m‑puusi Naawunni. Yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa ti ya zibi, ka Yisa daa kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni, u kɔkɔa.

english_verse: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
translation_verse: Ka nyariŋŋu maa daa pun kyaŋŋi tooni wa'aligu, m‑bɛ la mɔ'ari sunsukkuni. Ka pɛsigu daa zeeri ba tooni n‑nyaŋŋi nyariŋŋu maa koom maa ni, ka di daa mugsi ba pam.

english_verse: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
translation_verse: Ka din daa pɔri bɛkyikyeoo, ka Yisa daa kyɛ ba saani maa na n‑kyɛnni koom maa zugu.

english_verse: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
translation_verse: Amaa u nyaandɔliba daa nya u ka u kyɛnni koom zugu, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba yɛli, ni, kpiinyisigi n nyɛ u, ka daa ŋmɛ kuŋŋa dabeem zugu.

english_verse: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, n‑yɛli ba, “Ya mari sufukpeemni ka di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya! Ka mani nla!”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
translation_verse: Dinsaha ka Piita daa yɛli, “N dugma, di yi siri nyɛ la nyini, nyin kyɛ ka n kyaŋŋi koom wa zugu n‑kyɛ i saani na.”

english_verse: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa teaai u nuu yooma n‑gbaai Piita, ka yɛli u, ni, “Nyin yɛddakɔŋŋna wa, wula zugu ka i birisira?”

english_verse: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni ka pɛsigu maa daa kyɛ zeebu.

english_verse: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba ban daa bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni daa gyeemni Yisa n‑yɛtti, ni, Naawunni bii n siri nyɛ u.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban daa lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari maa ba daa ti paai Gyɛnɛsarɛti tiŋŋa.

english_verse: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka bɛni niriba maa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u, ba daa tum nti yɛli tinkpansi zaa an daa gyiligi bɛni maa, ka ba daa mari ba baaridima zaa n‑kyɛ u saani na.

english_verse: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa susi u, ni, u kur kyɛ ka ba siisi u kparigu nangbanni, ka ban daa siisi zaa daa nya alaafɛa.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, “M Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni wa yi bu sa tiseaa, ba nin vukki lala tii maa n‑lusi.

english_verse: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
translation_verse: ni, “Bɔ n niŋi ka i nyaandɔliba maa sa'amni la ti yaadima kaari? Dama ba bu peaari ba nuusi ka diri bundirigu.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Why do you disobey God and follow your own teaching?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ka bɔ n niŋi ka yarima za'asiri Naawunni zaaligu, ya yaadima kaari dɔligu zugu?

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Ya mun yɛtti, ni, sɔ yi ti yɛli u ba, bee, u ma, ni, ‘Yan da naa n‑nya seelli n saani ka n pun zaŋŋi n‑ti Naawunni.’

english_verse: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
translation_verse: Ni, lala zugu di bu laa n‑kpa ni di daama gyeemni u ba ni u ma yaasa. Di zugu ka ya niŋi ka ya ba'a laa n‑ka Naawunni zaaligu ni, ya yaadima kaari dɔligu zugu.”

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
translation_verse: Ba gyeemni ma la yɔri: Ban wulisiri la zaaliseaa maa pa la bunseelli zaa Na'ala ninsaala yɛligu kɔkɔa.’ ”

english_verse: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
translation_verse: Ya kyɛli ba! Dama ba nyɛ la zoomma, ka zooma yi ya n‑dari u zoontaraana, ba zaa ya n‑lu la bɔ'ari ni.”

english_verse: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa bu labsi u seela. Ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na nti susi u, ni, “I bu ya kari u? - Dama u kur katti ti nɔba n‑fabinni pampam.”

english_verse: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
translation_verse: Di pa la din kpeeri ninsaala noori ni n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira noori ni na n kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”

english_verse: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na nti boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i mi, ni, nyin yɛli seem la bu niŋi Farisidima maa nya'asim?”

english_verse: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Piita daa yɛli u, “Kur wulisim ti nyɛsiri wa gbinni.”

english_verse: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli, ni, “Ya gba yam na bu gaari ban dinni maa?

english_verse: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
translation_verse: Ya bu mi di gbinni? - ni, bunseela an kpeeri ninsaala noori, u puuri ka a kpeera, ka gatti n‑yi bangyira zii la ni.

english_verse: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
translation_verse: Amaa din yiiri ninsaala noori ni na, u sufu ni na ka di yiira, ka din mun nin toaai n‑sa'am ninvugkasi.

english_verse: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
translation_verse: Dama teesibeeri zaa yiiri ninsaala puuni, ni u sufu ni na; an kyɛ ka u tumni tumbeeri - n‑kuuri niriba, n‑tumni la zina, m‑boori seeba pɔ'aba, n‑zuura, m‑ma'ari ziri n‑lusiri niriba kootu ni; ka tuuri ninsaala ni Naawunni.

english_verse: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
translation_verse: Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa n kyɛ ka ninsaala leebi da'ari daana, amaa nira yi bu peaai u nuusi ka di bundirigu, lala ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ba daa bu tum ma na faasee ni n kyɛ Izraɛli dima ban bɔrigri hoo peesi la saani na.”

english_verse: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
translation_verse: Ka niriba pam daa mari ba baaridima n‑kyɛnni u saani na, gbarisi ni wɔbsi n daa bɛ ni, ni zoomma ni tubkpira dima, ni banŋɔ taabu pam. Ba da zaŋŋi ba m‑basi Yisa nɔba ziiya, ka u daa tibi ba zaa.

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
translation_verse: Yisa daa yi bɛni m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya ni Saidoon tinsi ni.

english_verse: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
translation_verse: Ka Kyɛna'ampɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa yi lala tinsi maa na daa fabinni pampam, ni, “N daana, nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ma namboo! Arizimbeoo n kur mugsiri m bipumbila ka di bu veeli gba!”

english_verse: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
translation_verse: Pɔ'a maa mun daa kyɛ u saani na n‑gbaantiri n‑ti u n‑yɛtta, “N daana, kur suŋŋma ma!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Kyɛ ka ti daŋŋi n‑diisi biisi la. Di bu tu ni ti zaŋŋi biisi bundirigu maa m‑basi babisi la.”

english_verse: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛliya, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni, n Daana, amaa babisi la gba ku kɔŋŋi ba piisi ban su ba la sa'akyɛba an luri tiŋŋa la.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑ti u, ni, “Mboo! Pɔ'a, i mari la yɛdda pam gba! Di nin niŋi nyin boori seem n‑ti i.” Ka u bipuŋŋa maa daa nya tippu lala saha maa.

english_verse: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Galilii mɔ'ari kpaŋŋa, nti du zoori zugu n‑zinni bɛni.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi daadam maa alahazibu, dama ba daa nya ka mugsi tɔ'asira, ka wɔbsi nyari tippu, halli ka gbarisi ti doaari n‑kyɛnni ka zoomma gɔsiri n‑nyari gba. Ka ba daa pɛ'ai Izraɛli dima Naawunni di zugu.

english_verse: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa booli u nyaandɔliba maa u saani na nti yɛli, “N sufu bu maai niriba wa yɛla zugu, dama ba bɛ n saani ka di paai dabsa ata ka ka seelli ni ba di. M bu boori n kyɛ ka ba kuli yɔri, baseeba nin ligi soori ni.”

english_verse: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa yɛli u, ni, “Yani ka ti nin nya bundirigu n‑diisi niriba wa zaa moo wa ni?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Boroboro abala ka ya mara?” Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Abayɔpɔi ka ti mara, ni zaasim beela.”

english_verse: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka niriba maa zini tiŋŋa maa,

english_verse: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
translation_verse: u daa zaŋŋi boroboro ayɔpɔi ni zaasim maa n‑tisi Naawunni pɛ'abu ka ŋmaasi n‑tisi u nyaandɔliba maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑tɔri daadam maa.

english_verse: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba ka ba kyaŋŋi maa, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi Magyada tiŋŋa ziiya.

english_verse: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna wula zugu ka ya bu mi, ni, pa boroboro yɛla ka n tɔ'asi ka yɛli, ni, ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina la, ni Saddusii dima dinni gba?”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti paai tinkpanseesi an pɔri Filipi Sizarii la ni. Ka u daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Niriba maa yɛtti, ni, man Ninsaala Bii maa nyɛ la ŋɔni?”

english_verse: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
translation_verse: Ka lala saha maa ka ba daa baŋŋi, ni, pa boroboro dabina yɛla ka u tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ba, amaa wulisigu yɛla, Farisii dima ni Saddusii dima ban daa wulisiri seem la, ka u tɔ'asira.

english_verse: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya bu nya? - zaanoori saha ka ya gɔsiri saazugu n‑yɛtti, ni, sangbana moaaya, saa bu ya mi:

english_verse: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
translation_verse: asusuba muna ka ya yɛtti, ni, saa ku n‑sɔbgi vilim, saa nin mi. Tɔ - sangbana gɔsibu ŋɔn nyɛ seem ka ya mi suŋŋa, ka mun na zi pampaŋŋɔ saha ŋɔn wulisiri makkri yɛla seem. Ka wula nla?

english_verse: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛli u, ni, “Baseeba ban yɛtti, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura n nyɛ i; seeba mun yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya; ka seeba mun yɛtti, ni, Gyɛrimaaya, bee, kurimbuni sa anabisɔa.”

english_verse: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Ka lala saha maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ka Yisa Kirisita Masiiya daa piili n‑wulisiri u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, u nin namsi pam kpamma maa nuusi ni, ban ni zaaligu karimbadima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima zaa. Ni, ba nin ku u, amaa dabsa ata daari u nin yisigi n‑labi u nyɔvuri ni.

english_verse: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina la, ni Saddusii dima dinni gba.”

english_verse: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri ni taaba, ni, “U yɛtti la lala tin ka boroboro la zugu!”

english_verse: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Ninsaala Bii maa siri nin zaŋŋi u Ba Naawunni gyirima ka kyɛ ka Naawunni malaakadima dɔli u ka u kyɛ na: lala saha maa ka u nin yɔ sɔkam samni hoo di daama tuma ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.”

english_verse: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
translation_verse: Man mun yɛtti i wa, ni, i yuuri boonni la Piita, ka di gbinni nyɛ la ‘tampiiya’: tɔ, n niriba nin la'am n‑tɔm tampii wa zugu, ka Sintaani suulinsi ku toaai n‑zabi ba n‑nyaŋŋi.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ka yarima muna? Ya yɛtti la wula?”

english_verse: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon Piita daa labsiya, ni, “I nyɛ la Masiiya Kirisita, sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tumni na nti tiligi ti: Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ Nyɔvuri Daana Bii n nyɛ i.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Mboo! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti nyini, Saimoon, nyin Gyoona bii wa! Dama pa ninsaala n wulisi i lala maa, amaa m Ba ŋɔn bɛ la arizana ni maa - ŋɔna n wulisi i.

english_verse: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: N nin zaŋŋi Arizana naam saafibiisi n‑ti i, ka nyin kpari bunseelli duniya wa ni, lala bunni nin ti kpari arizana ni gba, halli ka nyin kparigi bunseelli duniya wa ni, lala bunni nin ti kparigi arizana ni gba.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka u daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di yɛli sɔa, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya maa.

english_verse: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa mari u n‑labi kpaŋŋa m‑piili n‑kpaamni u, ni, “Ay, n dugma! Di ku toaai n‑niŋi i lala!”

english_verse: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli Piita, ni, “Bakkma ka kyɛ ma, nyin Sintaani wa! I ta'ari la n soori.”, ni, “I teesa wa bu yi Naawunni saani na, amaa a kur nyɛ la ninsaala teesa.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa zaa, ni, “Ŋɔnkam boori u kyɛ n saani na, see ka u za'asi u mammaŋŋa, ka kpikki u kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli ma.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ yi ti deaai duniya wa zaasa ka kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, nyɔbɔ ka u nya? Seelli mun ka ni ka ninsaala nin zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri n‑takki ka.

english_verse: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
translation_verse: Ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ninvugseeba m bɛ kpa, ban ku kpi halli ka ti nya ka Ninsaala Bii maa kyɛ na ni u naam yiiko.”

english_verse: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
translation_verse: Ban daa gɔsiri maa, ba daa ti nya ka Yisa takki ni, ka u nindaa daa ti nya'asiri hoo wuntaŋŋa la, ka u neema daa pɛligi nyain!

english_verse: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura maa yɛla ka u tɔ'asiri ba maa.

english_verse: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Dabsa ayoobu ŋɔn daa ti gaari, Yisa daa zaŋŋi Piita ni Gyeemsi ni u tunzɔ Gyɔni n‑zi ba n‑gaari tooni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑du zɔtitaari zugu ka ba daa ti bɛ ba kɔkɔa.

english_verse: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Dinsaha u nyaandɔliba ata maa daa nya ka Musa ni anabi Ilaygya daa bɛ ni ka ban ni ŋɔn daa sɔsira.

english_verse: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa yɛli Yisa, ni, “Ti Dugma, ti kpa wa beesigu veela! I yi boori lala, n nin ti suga ata, i dinni yinni, ni Musa dinni, ni Ilaygya mun dinni!”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Ka Piita noori ŋɔn na bu lu tiŋŋa, sangbampeelli daa ligi ba na, ka kukɔri daa tɔ'asiri sangbaŋŋu maa ni n‑yɛtti, ni, “M bii maa nŋɔa, ka n wuri u: n nini tigi u pam: ya wumma u yɛligu!”

english_verse: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
translation_verse: Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, dabeem daa gbaai ba. Ba daa sunni tiŋŋa dabeem maa zugu.

english_verse: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kyɛ na nti siisi ba ka yɛli ba, “Ya yisigma! Yan di zɔri dabeem.”

english_verse: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba daa wukki ba zugri saazugu, amaa ba daa bu nya sɔa; kulla Yisa kɔkɔ n daa zɛya.

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa boosi Yisa, ni, “Wula zugu ka ti karimbadima yɛtti, ni, Ilaygya nin daŋŋi n‑kyɛ na pɔi, ka Masiiya naayi paai na?”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ilaygya n siri ya n‑kyɛ na n‑sɛ'asi buna kam zaa.

english_verse: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
translation_verse: Man mun yɛtti ya wa, ni, Ilaygya pun kyɛ na ka ba daa bu baŋŋi u, ka daa niŋi u ban daa boori seem. Lala ka man Ninsaala Bii gba ya n‑di wahala ba nuu ni.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
translation_verse: Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba ata wa daa sigi na nti paai daadam saani na. Ka zama maa puuni dɔsɔ daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti sɔmni u tooni

english_verse: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
translation_verse: n‑yɛli, ni, “N daana, zɔm n bidibga wa namboo! Kpisinkpiiri ŋɔn mari u n‑namsiri u pam. Di kur lusiri u bugum ni, bee, koom ni sahakam.

english_verse: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
translation_verse: Ka n mari u n‑kyɛ i nyaandɔliba wa saani na, ka ba bu nyaŋŋi n‑tibi u.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, “Ah! Ya zuna niriba wa n kur nyɛ bungɔlima ka ku sakki yɛdda niŋbu! N ya yuui la wula ya saani? Bee man nin di sugru ya saani ka di yuui wula? Tɔ, yan mari bii maa n‑kyɛ kpa na!”

english_verse: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa tasi arizimbeoo maa, ka u zɔ ka kyɛ bii maa, ka bii maa di kpaŋŋi zaa.

english_verse: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ u saani na nti sɔ'ai n‑yɛli u, ni, “Bɔ n niŋi ka tirima guui arizimbeoo maa karibu?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: It is because you don't have enough faith! But I can promise you this. If you had faith no larger than a mustard seed, you could tell this mountain to move from here to there. And it would. Everything would be possible for you. Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, “Di nyɛ la ya yɛdda poorim zugu. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi da mari yɛdda ka di sa'ai zinzam beelli, ya naa n‑toaai n‑yɛli zoori wa, ni, ka doaai m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni sa, ka ka niŋi lala. Halli sɛseelli ku kɔŋŋi niŋbu ya saani.”

english_verse: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
translation_verse: Ban daa goori n‑gyinni Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni sahanseaa, Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii n‑ti ninvugseeba nuusi ni,

english_verse: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
translation_verse: ka ba nin ku ma. Ka Ninsaala Bii maa kum maa nyaaŋŋa di nin paai dabsa ata ka u nin laa n‑doaai.” Ka ba sufu daa sa'am pam di zugu.

english_verse: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
translation_verse: Ban daa paai Kapirinyum tiŋŋa sahanseaa, ninvugseeba daa kyɛ ba saani na ni ba ti toosi Gyɛrusalɛm Gyeeŋŋu Yiri lampoo ligri maa. Ba daa yɛli Piita, ni, “Ammi, ya karimba bu yoori ti lampoo la?”

english_verse: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
translation_verse: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ay! U yoori, walla!” Ka ban daa ti paai yiri maa ni, Yisa daa yɛli Piita, ni, “N zɔ Saimoon, i teesi la wula? - Duniya wa nadima maa, bandima saani ka ba deaari lampoo ni piini? Ba timbiisi saani, bee, tinzundima saani?”

english_verse: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, tinzundima maa saani, Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, dizugu timbiisi ka samni.

english_verse: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Amaa ti bu boori ni ti daamni ba sufu, ka dizugu kur kyɛma mɔ'ari ni n‑lɔbi zingoaa; i yi daŋŋi n‑gbaai zinseaa, zaŋŋma u n‑yaam u noori ka i nin nya lampoo buyi la'afu: kana ka i nin zaŋŋi n‑yɔ man ni nyin samni.”

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
translation_verse: I nimbiri yi yɔmsiri i ka kyɛ ka i kɔŋŋi yɛdda, nyin lukkma ka n‑lɔbi. I yi mari nimbiri yinni n‑kpɛ nyɔvuri ni, di sɔ i mari nini ayi ka kpɛ bugum wahala ni.”

english_verse: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
translation_verse: Ka dizugu di bu sumsi ni i zɔ i taraana namboo? - kaman man zɔ i namboo seem maa.’

english_verse: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translation_verse: Sɔ ŋɔn sigsi u maŋŋa ka ŋmanni bibila wa - ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn galisi n‑gaari sɔkam zaa Arizana naam ni.

english_verse: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
translation_verse: Sɔ yi gyaari bibila wa taabu man yuuri zugu, mani ka di daama n gyaari.”

english_verse: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
translation_verse: Ka u yi ku sakki n‑wum n‑ti ya yaasa, ya kyɛ ka ya mabiisi zaa la'am n‑wumna. U yi ku sakki n‑wum la'aŋŋu maa noori, ya gɔsima u hoo ŋɔn pa la Naawunni nira, bee, ŋɔn nyɛ lampodeaara.”

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
translation_verse: Ni, “Amaa sɔ yi yɔmsi bibisi wa yinni ka kyɛ ka u bu laa n‑niŋi ma yɛdda ka za'asi ma, lala nira nin ti nya ka ba yi da zaŋŋi nɛtitaari n‑lɔ u nangoori ni ka zaŋŋi u n‑lɔsi mɔ'azuliŋŋu ni, di naa n‑sɔ u beesigu.

english_verse: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
translation_verse: Dizugu i nuu, bee, i gbari, yi yɔmsiri i ka kyɛ ka i kɔŋŋi yɛdda, nyin ŋmaam ka m‑basi. I yi mari nuu yinni, bee, gbari yinni, ka mari nyɔvuri, di sɔ i mari nuusi ayi, bee, gbaya ayi, ka kpɛ bugum bun bu kpiiri halli sahakam maa ni.

english_verse: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli, “I mabii yi tum i taalli, kur kyɛma nti nya u, nyin ni ŋɔn kɔkɔa, n‑wulisi u u taalli ŋɔn nyɛ seem. U yi ti wum n‑ti i, i labsi u n‑lɔ mabiitiri nangbanyinni yaasa.

english_verse: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Nyama! Yan di zeem bibisi wa yinni yɛla. N yɛtti ya, ni, ba malaakadima m bɛ arizana ni n‑gyeemni m Ba Naawunni.”

english_verse: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
translation_verse: Ka u yi ti nya u, n yɛtti ya wa, ni, u sufu nin maai peoo yinni maa zugu n‑gaari peesi kɔbga‑yinni‑ka ban bu bɔrigi la.

english_verse: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
translation_verse: Lala n nyɛ di ya Ba saani arizana ni: u gba bu boori u bibisi wa yinni basi yɔri.”

english_verse: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
translation_verse: Amaa u yi ku sakki n‑wum n‑ti i, zaŋŋma ninvugyinni, bee, babayi m‑paasi i maŋŋa zugu ka ya kyaŋŋi nti tɔ'asi n‑ti u. Lala ka ya nin kyɛ ka ‘sɛhara dima ayi-ata noori ni ka yɛttɔ'a yɛlimaŋni nin yi paalu’.

english_verse: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: Ni, “N yɛtti ya ni, ni, yan lɔ bunseelli duniya wa ni, dina n lɔ Arizana ni gba; yan mun lɔrigi bunseelli duniya wa ni, dina n lɔrigi Arizana ni gba.

english_verse: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
translation_verse: Ka n yɛtti ya yaasa, ni, niriba ayi yi gbaai seelli duniya wa ni ka susi Naawunni di zugu, dina ka m Ba sɔ ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni nin ti niŋi n‑ti ba.

english_verse: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
translation_verse: Niriba ayi-ata yi ti la'am taaba man yuuri maa zugu, n nin bɛ ba saani.”

english_verse: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti boosi u, ni, “N dugma, m mabii yi ti tum ma taalli, napɔmbula ka n nin kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u? Napɔmbuyɔpɔi, bee?”

english_verse: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, ni, “Dama arizana naam yɛla ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Nasɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa yɛ gɔsi u kpamma ŋɔn mari u ligri n‑tumni seem.

english_verse: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
translation_verse: U daa piili lala laasaabu, ka ba daa zi sɔ n‑kpɛ na, ka u daa di Naa maa samni salima ligri tusatusa.

english_verse: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑yɔ lala samni maa, ka Naaba daa yɛli, ni, ba koosima u ni u pɔ'a ni u biisi ni u marigu zaa n‑yɔa.

english_verse: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa gbaanni Naa maa tooni ka bɛlimni u, ni, u dim sugru ka u nin yɔ samni maa zaa.

english_verse: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
translation_verse: Ka Naaba maa sufu daa sa'am pam ka u daa zɔ u namboo ka kyɛ u samni zaa m‑pɛŋŋi u.

english_verse: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa gbaanni u tooni ka bɛlimni u, ni, u dim sugru ka u nin yɔ samni maa zaa.

english_verse: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
translation_verse: Amaa u daa za'asi ka zaŋŋi u n‑taasi dansarika ni, halli ka u ti yɔ samni maa.

english_verse: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
translation_verse: Ka ba taaba maa ŋɔn daa nya ka u niŋi u seem maa, di daa dam ba sufu ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti pooti n‑ti Naaba ŋɔn tum seem maa.

english_verse: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
translation_verse: Ka Naa maa daa booli u na n‑yɛli u, ‘Nyini ninvugyɔri wa! N da kyɛ i samni zaa m‑pɛŋŋi i, nyin da susi ma zugu.

english_verse: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
translation_verse: Ka Naaba maa sufu daa yisigi m‑basi doo maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑kpari dansarika duu ni halli ni u ti yɔ u samni maa.

english_verse: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka lala ka m Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni gba nin niŋi ya, ya sɔkam yi ku siri zaŋŋi u sufu n‑kyɛ u mabii taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi u.”

english_verse: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa naai yɛttɔ'a wa, u daa yi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ya'ari, halli nti lɔŋŋi Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la.

english_verse: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
translation_verse: Dizugu yɛla pam m bɛ ni n‑kyɛ ka doo ku toaai n‑di pɔ'a#:- baseeba m bɛ ni ka ba madima daa dɔ'ai ba lala, ka baseeba ninsaala daa niŋi ba lala, seeba mun niŋi la lala ba mammansi, Arizana naam maa zugu. Kyɛ ka sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑deaai wulisigu wa deaai di.”

english_verse: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
translation_verse: Ka zama pam daa dɔli u, ka u daa tibri ba bɛni.

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
translation_verse: Ka Farisii dimseeba daa kyɛ u saani na ni ba da u gbinni. Ba daa boosi u n‑yɛli, ni, “Yɛlim ti: ammi, doo mari soori ka u nin toaai n‑yiisi u pɔ'a daliiri kam zaa zugu?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, “Ya bu karim Litaafi la? Ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa piili duniya naaŋŋu la, u daa naam ba doowa ni pɔ'a,

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
translation_verse: ka daa yɛli ba, ni, dinŋɔ zugu doo nin kyɛ u ba ni u ma ka ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a la'am n‑tabli taaba. Ni, babayi maa nin la'am m‑bɛ ni kaman bunyinni.

english_verse: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Dizugu ba laa m‑pa niriba ayi amaa bunyinni. Dinŋɔna Naawunni ŋɔn la'am bunseesi ni a leebi bunyinni maa, ninsaala di wɔligi a.”

english_verse: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
translation_verse: Ka Farisii dima la daa boosiya, ni, “Ka di niŋi la wula ka Musa daa zaali zaaligu la, ni, sɔ nin toaai n‑za'asi u pɔ'a? - ni, u zaŋŋma pɔ'aza'asigu gbaŋŋu n‑ti u pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka u bakki.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya tuppuwumni la zugu ka Musa daa ti soori, ni, ba niŋma lala. Amaa di daa pa lala piiligu saha la.

english_verse: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
translation_verse: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, doowa yi kari u pɔ'a ka pɔ'a maa bu tum la zina, ka doo maa ti laa n‑di pɔ'asɔ, u niŋi zina.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Wulisigu wa muna - pa sɔkam zaa nin toaai n‑dɔli dinŋɔ maa, na'ala seeba Naawunni ŋɔn pun zaŋŋi n‑ti ba la.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka ba daa mari bibisi n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ni u zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa ba zugri n‑susi Naawunni n‑ti ba. Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa nyikki ba di zugu.

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Ka dɔsɔ daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, ni, “N Karimba, tunsuŋŋu bɔ ka n nin tum ka deaai nyɔvuri kan ka naari?”

english_verse: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Zaaligu bɔ n nyɛ a?” Ka Yisa daa yɛli, “Ni, ‘Di ku ninsaala.’, ‘Di tum zina.’, ‘Di zu.’, ‘Di di ziri sɛhara n‑lusi i kpeaa u yɛlli ni.’,

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Ka u daa zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa biisi maa zugri, ka daa doaai m‑bakki bɛni.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli u, “Wula ka i boosi ma bunsuma yɛla? Naawunni kɔkɔ n nyɛ bunsuŋŋu. I yi boori i kpɛ nyɔvuri, see ka i dɔli zaaligudima la.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛli bibisi maa, yan di ta'ari ba ni ba di kyɛ n saani na! Dama banŋɔ taabu n su Naawunni naam la.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Amaa ban daŋŋi tooni nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa, ka ban kpalimni nyaaŋŋa mun nin ti daŋŋi tooni.”

english_verse: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: ‘Tim i ba ni i ma gyirima.’, ni, ‘Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.’ ”

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Ka na'akyimbila maa yɛli, “Anŋɔ zaa ka n daa pun dɔla. Bumbɔ m pɔsigi ma yaasa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “I yi boori n‑niŋi seelikam suŋŋa deede, kyɛma nti koosi i marigu zaa n‑zaŋŋi n‑tɔri faradima, ka i arizikki maali n‑zoaai Naawunni saani. Ka kyɛ na n‑dɔli ma.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka na'akyimbila maa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala u daa bakki ni sufusa'aŋŋu, dama u daa nyɛ la bundaana ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, di tɔ pam ni bundaana kpɛ Arizana naam ni.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ka n yɛtti ya yaasa, ni, halli arakoomi nin ti kpɛ seeriga voori ni m‑puligi n‑yi ka bundaana na ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni u kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba ŋɔn wum lala di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba boosi, ni, “Ka ŋɔni n lee n‑nin tiligi?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa gɔsi ba ka yɛli, “Dinŋɔ tɔ pam ninsaalidima saani, amaa Naawunni ku kɔŋŋi bunseelikam niŋbu.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
translation_verse: Dinsaha ka Piita daa yɛli u, “Nyama, tirima n nyɛ seeba ban kyɛ yɛlli kam m‑basi ka dɔli i! Tɔ - bɔ ka ti nin mari di zugu?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛli, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, yaseeba ban pun dɔli man nyaaŋŋa la, ni, Ninsaala Bii maa yi ti zi u naam zizii maa ni, ka di bɛ la duniya paalli din kyɛ na la puuni - lala saha maa ka ya niriba pii ni ayi wa nin ti zini naam gbana zugu n‑katti Izraɛli za'anɔya pii ni ayi niriba sariya.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Arizana naam yɛla ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Yiridaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa daŋŋi asuba n‑doaai n‑yi na, ni u ti bɔ tuntumniba ni ba tum u puu tuma n‑ti u.

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: U daa nya seeba, ka daa sakki ni u nin yɔ ba sɔkam dabsimunni tuma ligri, ka daa kyɛ ka ba kyaŋŋi u puu ma ni.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yi na kurigu awai saha nti kpɛ daa ni n‑nya ninvugseeba ka ba kpalim n‑zɛya n‑ka tuma.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, ba gba kyɛma nti tum u tuma maa, ka u ti yɔ ba din tuusi.

english_verse: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa sakki ka kyaŋŋi. Ka u daa laa n‑yi na agbaa saha, ni kurigu ata saha yaasa, n‑niŋi lala.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: Di daa ti paai kurigu anu saha, ka u daa laa n‑yi na n‑kpɛ daa ni ka seeba kpalim n‑zɛya ka ka tuma. Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, ‘Bɔ n niŋi ka ya kpalim n‑zɛ kpa yɔriyɔri, halli ni zaanoori saha wa?’

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, ‘Sɔ na bu zaŋŋi ti ni ti tum.’ Ka u yɛli ba, ‘Tɔ! Ya gba kyɛma m puu ni nti tum ka n nin yɔ ya.’

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: Ka zaanɔyuŋŋu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai, puu maa daana daa yɛli u tuma zuguraana, ni, u kyɛma nti booli u tuntumniba na ka u ti yɔ ba ba ligri. Ni, u daŋŋma m‑piili ban kpalim nyaaŋŋa la halli nti paai asuba dima maa.

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu baseeba ban daa kyaŋŋi kurigu anu saha la daa kyɛ na nti deaai dabsimunni tuma ligri.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa deaai lala maa, ba daa nyurimni ba kparima n‑niŋi puu maa daana,

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: n‑yɛtti, ni, ‘Ban kpalim nyaaŋŋa wa bu tum hoo tirima ŋɔn tum seem la. Saha beela kɔkɔ ka ba tum, ka tirima mun tum asuba halli ni yuŋŋu saha wa ka di wum ti pam; ka ti zaa deaai yoori yummu!’

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba ninvugsɔ, ni, ‘N zɔa, wumma! M bu yɔmsiri i ni. I da bu sakki, ni, i ya deaai dabsimunni tuma ligri?

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: Zaŋŋma i ligri n‑kyaŋŋi! M boori n zaŋŋi n‑tisi ŋɔn kpalim nyaaŋŋa wa hoo man zaŋŋi n‑ti i.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: Mani n su ligri maa. I bu sakki ni n zaŋŋi m mammantoori bunni n‑niŋi man boori seem? Bee, i zabri ma nyuuri n nirilim tuma zugu?’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, “Dizugu ban daŋŋi tooni nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa, ka ban kpalimni nyaaŋŋa mun nin ti daŋŋi tooni.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa zaani, ka booli ba na, nti boosi ba, ni, “Bɔ ka ya boori ni n niŋi n‑ti ya?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa siisi ba nini maa, ka di saha ka a daa neaai ka ba nyara, ka ba daa dɔli Yisa di daari maa.

english_verse: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Ka daadam maa daa nyikki ba n‑yɛli, ni, ba sina ba vuuri. Amaa ba daa laa n‑tasi pampam m‑paasi, ni, “Nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ti namboo!”

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wa Gyɛrusalɛm, u daa zaŋŋi u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n‑yi kpaŋŋa n‑yɛli ba,

english_verse: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
translation_verse: ka naayi zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ban pa ti butiri niriba la nuu ni, ni ba maali ansarisi, ka feebi ma ka ku ma. Amaa daba ata daari n nin yisigi kum ni.”

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
translation_verse: Ka Zɛbidii pɔ'a daa mari u biisi, Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni, n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na nti gbaani n‑ti u ka susiri bunseelli u saani.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa boosi u, ni, “Bumbɔ n nyɛ di?” Ka pɔ'a maa yɛli u, “Ni, i kyɛ ka m biisi ayi wa ti zinni n‑tabli i tooni, i nudirigu ni i nuzaa ziiya, i naam zɔŋŋu puuni.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Ya bu mi yan susiri seelli maa. Ya nin toaai n‑nyu wahala kɔbɛseaa ka man ya n‑nyu wa?” Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, ii, ba nin nyaŋŋi.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, “Ya siri nin ti nyu n wahala kɔbeaa la, amaa mani bu su noori ni n wulisi ban nin zinni n nudirigu bee n nuzaa zii maa. Di nyɛ la : m Ba Naawunni n ya n‑yiisi niriba maa, ka u pun maali ba ziziisi siri n‑zaali ba.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka u nyaandɔliba ban kpalim piiya la ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, ba suuri daa yisigi n‑niŋi mabiisi ayi wa, Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
translation_verse: Dizugu Yisa daa booli ba ni ba la'amma taaba na, ka yɛli ba, “Ya mi ninvugseeba ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la yɛla, ni, ba saani ka ban su ba fukumsi mari la yiiko ka niŋni putoo n‑tiri ba, ka ba kpamma su ba yɛla ni kpeeŋŋu.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
translation_verse: Amaa di pa lala ya saani. Ya sɔ yi boori tibsim, see ka u tumni n‑tiri ya daadam wa zaa.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
translation_verse: Ya sɔ yi boori ni u leebi toondaana, see ka u leebi ya zaa daabri.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
translation_verse: Kaman man Ninsaala Bii bu kyɛ na ni ba tumni n‑suŋŋni ma, amaa n kyɛ na ni n ti tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba, ka zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri m‑pa talima ka niriba pam nya tiliginsim.”

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
translation_verse: Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa ti ya yi Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa maa, daadam pam daa dɔli ba.

english_verse: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka zoomma ayi daa zi soori maa gbiriŋŋu, ka daa ti wum, ni, Yisa n gatta, ka ba daa tasi n‑yɛli, ni, “Nyin NaaDauda bii wa, zɔm ti namboo!”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Tɔ; ka ya teesiri la wula? - Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni m‑mari u bidibsi ayi. Ka u daa kyaŋŋi u bii yinni saani nti yɛli u, ni, ‘Kyɛma nti tum tiisi puu maa tuma zuna.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Amaa tuntumniba maa ŋɔn daa nya puu maa daana bii la, ba daa yɛli n‑ti taaba, ni, ‘Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u, ka mari u faari maa.’

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Ka sɔ yi ya boosi ya seelli, yan yɛlim u, ni, ya Dugma m boori ba ni, ka mun nin labsi ba na yooma.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ daa niŋiya, ni anabi maa yɛlisim niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:-

english_verse: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
translation_verse: “Ya tɔ'asima n‑ti Zayɔn tiŋŋa wa, Hoo yan nin tɔ'asi n‑ti pɔ'asariga, Ni, ‘Nyama, i Naaba n kyɛnni i saani na wa! U sigsi u maŋŋa baalimm Ka baari buŋŋa, Halli bumbila ka u baara.’ ”

english_verse: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka nyaandɔliba ayi maa daa kyaŋŋi nti tum Yisa ŋɔn daa wulisi ba seem la.

english_verse: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Ban daa mari buŋŋa ni u bii maa na, ba daa zaŋŋi ba kyinkyina m‑pa bunsi maa nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa m‑ba n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni la, tiŋŋa maa zaa daa damni n‑vuura, ka ba boosiri taaba, ni, “Ŋɔni n‑lee n‑nyɛ u?”

english_verse: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
translation_verse: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima, ni karimbadima maa sufu daa yisigi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi alahazibu tunseesi Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni la; ni biisi ban daa bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑tasiri n‑yɛtta, “Hoosana! Tiliginsim daana! NaaDauda Biiya!”

english_verse: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
translation_verse: Di saha ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni Farisii dima maa daa wum lala ka daa baŋŋi, ni, nyɛsiri maa galimni la bana.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
translation_verse: Ka yɛli ba, ni, “Di sɔbi n‑zaali, ni, ‘Ba nin booli n Yiri wa, ni, Wunsusigu Yiri.’, amaa ya zaŋŋi ka n‑labgi la ‘ta'ayigsi ŋɔn sɔ'ari seela.’ ”

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
translation_verse: Ya yi susi Naawunni bunseelli kam zaa, ka niŋi yɛdda, ya nin nya.”

english_verse: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, “I bu wum ban yɛtti seem wa?” Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ii, n wumiya. Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu maa yɛla? - ni, ba pɛ'ari Naawunni, ni, ‘Nyin, Naawunni, bamsi la biisi - halli bileesi gba - ni ba tiri pɛ'abu.’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa kyɛ ba m‑bakki n‑yi Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa n‑gbɛa.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “N gba ya boosi ya la boosigu yinni. Ya yi yɛli ma pɔi, ka man gba nin naayi labsi ya ya boosigu wa.

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
translation_verse: Beoo ŋɔn daa neaai asusuba, ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni n‑labri timpuuni na, kɔm daa ti mari u.

english_verse: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi mari yɛdda, ka bu birisira, ya nin toaai n‑niŋi man niŋi kinkaŋŋa wa seem. Ka pa dinŋɔ kɔkɔa, amaa halli ya yi ti yɛli zoori wa, ni, ka doaama n‑lɔbi ka maŋŋa m‑basi teeku puuni, di nin niŋi.

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa labsi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, “Ti bu mi.” Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Dinŋɔna n gba ku yɛli ya ŋɔn ti ma soori ni n tumni anŋɔ zaa.”

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba nyɛsiseaa yaasa, ni, “Yiri daansɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑daa sa u tiwanna puu. U daa mɛ la goomni n‑gyili puu maa, n‑gbi bɔkku ba n soorigri tiwala maa seela, ka laa n‑ti sugri puu maa puuni. U daa maali di zaa ka zaŋŋi puu maa n‑niŋi ninvugseeba nuu ni ni ba tumma puu maa tuma n‑tiri u: ka u daa naayi kyaŋŋi n‑gɔa.

english_verse: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa, ni u ti wulisiri niriba la. Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima daa kyɛ u saani na, ban ni tiŋŋa maa kpamma la, m‑boosi u, ni, “Ŋɔni n ti i soori ni i tum i lala tuma maa, bee, yiiko bɔ ka i mara?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ŋɔni n daa ti Gyɔni soori ni u suuri niriba Naawunni koom la, bee, u yiiko yi la yani? Di daa yi la Naawunni saani, bee, ninsaalidima saani?” Ka ba daa piili n‑ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑tiri taaba, ni, “Ti ya yɛli la wula? Ti yi yɛli, ni, Naawunni saani, u nin boosi ti, ni, bɔzugu ka ti daa bu niŋi Gyɔni yɛdda?

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Ka ti yi sakki n‑yɛli, ni, ninsaalidima saani...? - Ay! ti zɔri la niriba, dama sɔkam sakkiya, ni, Gyɔni Kɔsuura daa nyɛ la anabi.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: Ka bii maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, ‘Ay! - N ku kyaŋŋi.’, amaa di nyaaŋŋa u daa laa n‑takki u teesa ka kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa paai u bii ŋɔnla, ka yɛli ŋɔn gba, ni, u kyɛma nti tum, ka ŋɔn mun sakki n‑yɛli, ni, ‘Tɔ, n wumiya.’, amaa u daa bu kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka babayi wa saani, ŋɔni n daa tum u ba ŋɔn boori seem?” Ka ba labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni la.” Ka u daa yɛli ba, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ban deaari lampoo ni ban goori gyɛ'amiru la nin gaari yarima tooni n‑kpɛ Arizana naam ni.

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Dama Gyɔni daa kyɛ na ni u ti wulisiri ya yɛlimaŋni soori, ka yarima daa bu niŋi u yɛdda, amaa ka lampodeaariba ni gyɛ'amirudima daa niŋi u yɛdda. Ka yarima muna, halli ni yan daa nya lala zaa, ya daa bu takki ya teesa n‑naayi niŋi u yɛdda.”

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka tiisi maa wala saha ŋɔn daa ti pɔri, u daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani nti deaai u puu maa diibu na n‑ti u.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: Amaa tuntumniba maa daa gbaai ba yinni m‑bu u, ka sɔ mun ka ba daa ku, ka daabsɔ yaasa ka ba daa lɔbi kuga.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Ka puu maa daana daa laa n‑tum seeba, daaba pam gba n‑gaari banla, ka tuntumniba maa daa niŋi ba zaa lala.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Ka u daa naayi tum u toori biiya ba saani, dama u daa teesiya, ni, ‘Ba nin ti m maŋtoori bii maa gyirima.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Ka lala zugu ba daa gbaai u n‑zaŋŋi u m‑basi puu maa kpaŋŋa n‑ku u.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka puu maa daana yi ti paai na, u nin niŋi tuntumniba wa wula?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “U nin ku tuntumniba wa zaa ka ba kum nin ti bɛ halli. Ka u nin zaŋŋi puu maa n‑ti tuntunseeba ban nin sakki n‑zaŋŋi u wala maa n‑ti u, a walibu saha yi ti paai.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ammi, ya na bu karim Sɔppu la? - ni#:- ‘Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu; Ti Dugma tuma maa nla, Ka di nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ti.’ - ya bu baŋŋi lala sɔppu maa?”

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: Ni, “Dinŋɔna man yɛtti ya ni, ni, ba nin fa Naawunni naam yɛla ya saani nti zaŋŋi n‑ti tingbanseaa niriba, ban tuma sumsi ni ba deaai.” [[

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Ŋɔnkam zaa lu kugri maa zugu nin kabi m‑basi, amaa kugri maa ŋɔn lu sɔ zugu ka nin ŋmɛrigi u zaa.”]]

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boori soori ni ba gbaai u; amaa ba daa zɔri la daadam la dabeem, dama zama maa daa yɛnni, ni, anabi n nyɛ u.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Niriba pam ka ba booli, amaa ban gamsi ninvugseeba la bu zoaai.”

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Farisiseeba daa la'am taaba n‑gbaai gbaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba nin niŋi seem n‑gbaai Yisa u yɛttɔ'a puuni.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: Ba daa yɛli u, ni, “Ti Karimba, Musa daa yɛli, ni, dɔsɔ beeri yi ti kpi ka u ka biiya, ŋɔn dim u beeri pukoori maa n‑dɔ'ai biisi n‑ti u beeri maa.

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑tɔ'asi daadam maa, nyɛsiri puuni, ni,

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: “Arizana naam yɛla ŋmanni la nŋɔa#:- Nasɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa sɛ'asi pɔ'adiri kyuu n‑ti u biiya.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: U daa tum u daaba ni ba kyaŋŋi nti booli ŋɔn pun zuusi seeba maa, ni, ba kyɛm na. Amaa ba daa bu sakki n‑kyɛ na.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: Dizugu u daa laa n‑tum seeba yaasa ni ba ti yɛli ba, ni, ‘Kyuu maa saha paaya. N maali kyuu maa yɛla zaa siri n‑naai. M pun kɔri nigi ni bunkɔbkara la, ka di zaa niŋi la siri. Ya kyɛm pɔ'adiri kyuu maa na!’

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.

translation_verse: Musa zaaligu la zaa, ni anabidima wulisigu zaa gba, bɛ la abayi wa puuni.”

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka lala saha maa ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa.

translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya burigi soori ya ziilinsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Sɔppu maa zugu, ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn mari yiiko seem la gba.

translation_verse: Zaadaari kum yisigri saha ba ku ti laa n‑diri pɔ'aba bee n‑kunni dɔppa, ba ti ya n‑ŋmanni la hoo arizana malaakadima la.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,

translation_verse: ‘Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba.’ - ya bu wum lala? U siri pa la ninvugkpiimma Naawunni amaa ninvugvuya Naawunni.”

translation_verse: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn daa ti wum dinŋɔa, di daa gyɛri ba, u wulisigu wa zugu.

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Farisii dima maa ŋɔn daa ti wum Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka Saddusidima sini ba noori seem la, ba daa la'am n‑kyɛ u saani na.

translation_verse: Kan paasi buyi daana ŋmanni la kana: ka nyɛ la, ni, ‘Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.’

translation_verse: ‘Ti Dugma daa yɛli n‑ti n Dugma maa, Ni, “Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni, Halli ka n zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni.” ’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: ni, “Bɔ ka ya ya n‑yɛli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Masiiya maa yɛla? - Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum sɔ na ni u ti di naam n‑fa u niriba m‑basi. Ni, naam za'anɔri kani ka u ti ya n‑yi na?” Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “NaaDauda dinni.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Ka di yi nyɛ la dina, ka NaaDauda booli ŋɔn Masiiya maa, ni, u Dugma, ka wula ka Masiiya maa nin toaai m‑bɛ NaaDauda za'anɔri ni?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka di saha sɔ daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑labsi Yisa seela, halli sɔ daa bu laa m‑boosi u boosa yaasa, lala dabsiri maa zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn duusi u maŋŋa, ba nin sigsi u; amaa ŋɔn sigsi u maŋŋa muna, ba nin duusi lala nira maa pam.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: Ya gɔsima! Ya Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa yi kpalim la daboo.

translation_verse: Yarim saani, ya kyɛ ka ya ninvugtibsiri leebi sɔkam daabri.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gbaai kunkuma Gyɛrusalɛm zugu, ni, “Gyɛrusalɛm, Gyɛrusalɛm! Waay, waay, waay! Nyin ŋɔn kuuri anabidima ka zaŋŋni tuumuseeba ban daa tum i saani maa n‑lɔbri ba kuga wa! N da kur boori la n la'asi i niriba luginyinni kaman nɔnyaaŋŋa ŋɔn la'asi u biisi ka yirigri u kpunkpama n‑ligri ba seem la; amaa ya bu sakki n‑ti ma.

english_verse: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pɔ maaligu bimbinni maa, bimbinni maa ka u pɔa, ni bunseesi zaa an pa ka zugu gba.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri daadam maa, ni u nyaandɔliba gba,

translation_verse: ka yɛli ba, ni, “Musa zizii maa ni ka Farisii dima zintira, ban ni karimbadima maa.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Dizugu ban yɛtti ya maa zaa, ya tumma a ni, ka ya dɔli ba wulisigu la suŋŋa; amaa ba tuma muna, yan di dɔli anŋɔ, halli beela! Dama ba tɔ'asira, ka bu tumna.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: Ba loori la zitibsa n‑ziinni niriba, ka ba sɔ mun pun ku wukki ziiri maa halli beela n‑suŋŋi ba.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: Ba tumni la ba tuma zaa ni ba ti kyɛ ka niriba gɔsiri a; ba mari la sabkara m‑pitta, ni saariveela ka ba paari ka puusira.

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Deema ziiya ka ba boori la ba bɛ tooni, halli la'aŋŋu duri puuni gba ba zi la sɔkam zaa tooni.

translation_verse: Ba boori ni sɔkam zaa puusiri ba daasi puuni, ka gyeemni ba m‑boonni ba, ni ‘Rabbi!’, ni, ‘N Karimba!’

translation_verse: Yarim muna - yan di kyɛ ka ba boonni ya, ni, ‘Rabbi’. Karimba yinni m bɛ ni n‑ti ya zaa, ka ya mun zaa bɛ ni hoo mabiisi n‑ti taaba.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Di laa m‑boonni sɔ ya Ba, duniya wa puuni: ya Ba yinni m bɛ ni n‑ti ya, ka ŋɔn mun bɛ la saazugu.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya kyɛnni n‑gyinni teeku ni tingbaŋŋu ziisi zaa ni ya ti nya ya nyaandɔla yinni; yan mun yi ti nya sɔa, ya yi kyɛ ka u leebi Sintaani dinni n‑gaari ya mammansi ŋɔn nyɛ seem napɔmbuyi.

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ la yarima zugu, yan wulisiri niriba soori ka ya mun nyɛ zoomma! Dama ya yɛtti, ni, ninvugsɔ yi ti pɔ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri, di pa seelli; amaa ŋɔn mun yi pɔ Yiri maa salima maa - ɔhoo! di nin dɔli u!

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Yan gyɛra wa! Ni yan zoomma wa! Abayi wa puuni, kani n tibsa n‑gaari? - Salima la n gaari? - bee, Gyeeŋŋu Yiri kan kyɛ ka salima maa leebi Naawunni dinni kasi?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Ni, ninvugsɔ yi ti pɔ sara maaligu bimbinni la, di pa seela, amaa ŋɔn mun yi pɔ piiniseaa kan pa bimbinni maa zugu - ɔhoo! di nin dɔli u!

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Yan zoomma wa! Abayi wa puuni, kani n tibsa n‑gaari? - Piini la gaari? - bee, maaligu bimbinni kan kyɛ ka piini maa leebi Naawunni dinni kasi?

translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima karimbadima ni Farisidima zugu! Yan birikɔnsi dima wa! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, ka kur kyɛ ka ninvugsuma dabɔya veela,

translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba kum taalli bɛ ya zuna niriba wa zugu ni.”

translation_verse: U mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya bu nya anŋɔ zaa? Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, a seaa ku kpalim n‑tɔm ka kugkpeaa zugu, amaa a zaa nin ti ŋmɛ n‑wurim n‑lu tiŋŋa na.”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.

translation_verse: Ka di saha maa, sɔ yi ti yɛli ya, ni, ‘Nyama, Masiiya maa bɛ la kpa.’ bee ‘Kpa sa ka u bɛ ni.’ yan di niŋi ba yɛdda.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya susim Naawunni ka yan zɔri sahanseaa wa bu kyɛ na waari saha, bee, Vuusim Dabsiri maa ni.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: Ya nin ti nya bumbɛseelli kan kyisiri la - anabi Daniyɛli ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seaa yɛla nla - ka ba zaŋŋi n‑zaali luginseaa kan nyɛ Naawunni bunkasi la ni, n‑sa'amna [kyɛ ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn karimni anŋɔ baŋŋi suŋŋasuŋŋa].

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: ka seeba ban bɛ puri ni di labi nyaaŋŋa nti zaŋŋi ba kpari.

translation_verse: Ka ba yi daa bu ŋmaai dabsa maa n‑gyiŋŋi, saalibii n daa ka ni n‑da naa n‑nya tiliginsim, amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi seeba la zugu ka ba siri daa ŋmaai dabsa maa.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Ka di bɛ la lala: ya yi ti nya yɛla wa zaa, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, saha maa pɔriya.

translation_verse: Hoo din daa kur niŋni seem Nuhu saha la, lala ka di ti ya n‑niŋi Ninsaala Bii gba kyɛnni na saha maa.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: Ni, “Amaa di daari maa, bee di saha, ŋɔn ya m‑paai sahanseaa la ka sɔsɔ bu mi deede, halli malaakadima ban bɛ arizana ni la bu mi, Naawunni Bii maa gba bu mi, na'ala ti Ba maa kɔkɔ m mi.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Koom ŋɔn na bu kyɛ na n‑di ba sahanseaa la ka niriba daa diri ni ka nyuura, ka diri pɔ'aba ka zaŋŋni ba bipunsi n‑tiri dɔppa, halli nti paai Nuhu ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ nyarintitaari maa ni dahinseaa maa.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Amaa ni dinŋɔ zaasa, ba daa bu mi din ya n‑niŋi, halli ka koom maa daa ti di ba zaa n‑kyaŋŋi. Tɔ: di nin ti nyɛ la lala Ninsaala Bii maa ŋɔn yɛ n‑ti kyɛ na sahanseaa la.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.

translation_verse: Ka pɔ'aba ayi nin ti la'am n‑neemni ba kyi neeri zugu, ka Naawunni kpikki yinni ka kyɛ yinni.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.

translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ya gba niŋma siri, dama sahanseaa yan bu teesiri u yɛla maa, lala saha ka Ninsaala Bii maa ti ya kyɛ na.”

translation_verse: Amaa daabsɔ yi bɛ ni n‑nyɛ ninvugbeoo, ka ti yɛli u sufu ni, ni, u dugma maa yuuya pam ni u kulibu na,

translation_verse: Di saha ka pɔ'asara la daa doaai m‑maali m‑maali ba firiladima maa.

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Di ŋmanni la nŋɔ : dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u boori ni u kyaŋŋi soori, ka u daa booli u toori daaba u saani na n‑zaŋŋi u marigu n‑niŋi ba nuusi ni.

english_verse: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
translation_verse: ka ŋɔn daa deaai la'aboorisi anu la daa zaŋŋi a n‑walim, ka daa nya abanu m‑paasi a zugu.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Ka u dugma daa yɛli u, ni, ‘Mboo! Daabsuŋŋu ni yɛdda daana n nyɛ i gba! Nyin ŋɔn su yɛttɔ'abisi fukumsi n‑zɛ yimmu maa, n nin zaŋŋi bunkara fukumsi n‑ti i ka i mara. Dizugu kyɛm na ti di n nya'asim maa.’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”

translation_verse: - Ka daa yɛli u niriba maa, ni, ‘Ya deaam ligri maa u saani n‑zaŋŋi m‑paasi sɔ ŋɔn mari la'aboorisi piiya maa.
