english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Ni, ‘Ka bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa muna - ya gbaama u ka zaŋŋi u n‑yi m‑basi zibsim ni sa. Bɛni ka ba kur kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina.’ ”

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.

translation_verse: Dama kɔm ŋɔn daa mari ma sahanseaa la, ya daa zaŋŋi diibu n‑diisi ma; kɔnyuuri gba ŋɔn daa mari ma, ya daa zaŋŋi koom n‑ti ma ni n nyu: n daa yi tinseaa ni n‑goora, ka ya daa deaai ma hoo ya saana n‑kpeesi ya yiya ni.

translation_verse: Bɔn daari ka ti daa nya i ka i goora ka daa deaai i, bee, i daa kyɛnni yɔri ka ti yeeli i kparigu?

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Dama kɔm ŋɔn daa mari ma sahanseaa la, ya daa bu zaŋŋi diibu n‑diisi ma; kɔnyuuri gba ŋɔn daa mari ma, ya daa bu zaŋŋi koom n‑ti ma ni n nyu:

translation_verse: Di saha ka u nin labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, ‘Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan daa bu niŋi lala n‑ti banŋɔ yinni, halli ŋɔn poora zaa, mani ka ya daa mɔŋŋi suŋŋsim maa.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”

translation_verse: Ni, “Ti di niŋi lala kyuu maa zama sunsukkuni, mira ka niriba maa nin zabi di zugu.”

translation_verse: Ka ba daa yuum yuuma m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Ban daa yuum n‑naai ba daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Ɔlivii Zoori lugri ni.

translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Zuna yuŋŋu wa ya zaa nin wurim ka kyɛ ma, vi zugu. Di bu sɔbi n‑dooli lala? - ni, ‘N nin ŋmɛ pɛgura maa ka u peesi maa wurim.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, zuna yuŋŋu wa ka nyin mammaŋŋa nin kyiisi, ni, i zi ma, m‑maali n‑yɛli lala napɔŋŋu buta pɔi, ka nɔroo naayi kum.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa, nti paai ya'aseelli ban boonni Gyɛtisamanni. Ka Yisa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, “Ya zinma kpa ka man kyaŋŋi sa n‑susi wunsusigu ka kyɛ na.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”

translation_verse: Ka Piita labsi n‑yɛli ni nimmoaa, ni, “Halli di yi zaan kum ni, n ku kyiisi i.” Ka u nyaandɔlitaaba zaa gba daa yɛli lala.

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa m‑bakki yaasa n‑kyaŋŋi ka laa n‑susiri, ni, “M Ba, soori yi ka ni ka namsigu kɔbeaa wa nin gaari ka kyɛ ma see ka n nyu di, tɔ! di pa la man ŋɔn boori seem la amaa nyin ŋɔn boori seem, di niŋma.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: U daa kyaŋŋi tooni beela m‑paasi nti zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑gbaani tiŋŋa ka susi, ni, “M Ba, di yi ti nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi, n susiri i, ni, nyin kyɛ ka namsigu kɔbeaa wa gaari ka kyɛ ma: di kyɛ ka n nyu di! Amaa di pa la man ŋɔn boori seem la amaa nyin ŋɔn boori seem, di niŋma.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Ya doaama ka ti kyaŋŋi! Minaafikka maa n kyɛnni na wa.”

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,

translation_verse: U daa labi nyaandɔliba la saani na ka nya, ka babata maa zaa daa gbisiri ni; ka u booli Piita, ni, “Saimoon, ni wula? I naa n‑ku toaai n‑gali goom halli hawa yinni n‑ti ma?”

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba zaa, ni, “Ya guusima ka susi Naawunni ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba di zamsi ya halli ka ya ti lu. Ya sufu boori sakkri amaa ya ningbina ka yaa ka za'asiri ni.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ ba, din daa paasiri buta. U daa laa n‑susiri n‑yɛtti lala.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “N zɔa, niŋma nyin ya niŋi seem la!” Tɔ, ka ba daa paai na n‑gbaai Yisa ba nuusi m‑bibgi u kirikiri.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri maa, nnya Gyuudasi ŋɔn paari na, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi la ni yinni. Niriba pam daa dɔli u: ba daa yi la gyeeŋŋu kpambaya ni Izraɛli kpamma saani na, ka ba daa mari ba takoobidima ni dɔya na.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: Minaafikka maa daa pun wulisi u nyaaŋŋa ŋɔn ya n‑niŋi seem n‑kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi Yisa, ni, “N yi ti puusi sɔ suŋŋasuŋŋa, ŋɔna nŋɔa. Yan gbaam u!”

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu Gyuudasi daa kyaŋŋi yooma m‑paai Yisa saani, ka yɛli u, “N Karimba, aniŋwula!” ka daa puusi u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

translation_verse: Ka ban daa dɔli Yisa maa sɔ daa foaai u takoobi n‑kyɛ kpambaya maa bisɔ n‑fikki u tubri m‑basi.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, u zaŋŋma u takoobi maa n‑labsi n‑su ka teoo la ni, ni, “Bankam zaa zaŋŋi takoobi n‑zabra, ban ka ba nin zaŋŋi takoobi n‑zabi n‑ku ba.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Ka Toondaana maa suuri daa yisigi, halli ka u daa toaai u maŋtoori sutura dizugu ka yɛli, “Ah! U tuuri la Naawunni! Ti bu laa m‑boori sɛharadima yaasa, bee bɔa? Ya pun wum ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi an kyisira.

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa sina, Toondaana maa daa laa m‑boosi u n‑yɛli, “Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana zugu n nin zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi nangbampɔri ni - yɛlima ti#:- nyin nyɛ Masiiya ŋɔn ya fa ti m‑basi ka leebi naaba? Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni bii la?”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa bu nya lala sɛhara, halli ni niriba pam daa yi na ni ba ma'ai ziri n‑galim u. Ka niriba ayi daa naayi doaai n‑zaani na,

translation_verse: ni, “Doo wa yɛliya, ni, u nin sa'am Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu ka zaŋŋi daba ata m‑mɛ seaa.”

translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑ti u, ni, “I yɛliya; amaa n yɛtti ya, ni, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna#:- ‘Ya nin nya Ninsaala Bii maa’ ‘Ka u zi Naawunni Yaadaana maa nudirigu ya'ari’ ‘Ka kyɛnni na ka saazugu sangbana gyiligi u.’ ”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Baseeba daa piili n‑tusiri nantoori n‑niŋi Yisa nini, ka daa lɔbri u tampɛ'asi ka daa buuri u

translation_verse: ka boosiri u, ni, “Nyin Masiiya, nyin anabi wa, bugma m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn ŋmɛ i wa!”

translation_verse: Ka Piita daa kyiisi yaasa, ni, “M pɔ la noori, ni, m bu mi doo wa!”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Piita daa na bɛ la tiŋni dundɔŋŋu maa puuni, ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana yiri bipunsi sɔ daa kpɛ na nti yɛli u, ni, “Nyin gba yi bɛ la Yisa, Galilii nira wa, saani!”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa kyiisi ba zaa tooni, ni, “M bu baŋŋi nyin tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi wa!”

translation_verse: Din daa paasi beela, ban daa zɛ bɛni maa daa laa n‑yɛli Piita, ni, “I ku toaai n‑kyiisi, ni, i pa ba nyaandɔla, dama i gba tɔ'asiri la Galiliitiri ka ti baŋŋi i yɛla di zugu.”

english_verse: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.

translation_verse: - dama u daa mi halli, ni, nyuuri zugu ka ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ti u la.

english_verse: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.

english_verse: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.

translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa bu gyɛrigi, halli yɛlli yinni gba, ka di daa gyɛri Gɔmna maa pam.

translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa yiisi Barabasi m‑basi n‑ti ba, ka daa feebi Yisa muna, ka zaŋŋi u n‑tisi ni ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
translation_verse: Niriba ban daa gatti daa lɔbri ba zugu ka tuuri Yisa,

translation_verse: Di saha ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni kpamma maa daa kur tɔ'asiri n‑tiri zama maa n‑kpaasiri ba zugri, ka naayi kyɛ ka ba sakki ni ba ti susi Gɔmna maa, ni, u yiisima Barabasi n‑ti ba na, ka kyɛ ka Yisa baasi yɔri.

english_verse: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
translation_verse: Ka Pailiti ŋɔn daa ti nya, ni, u yɛttɔ'a sɛseelli ku toaai m‑maali yɛlli maa, halli u tuma maa zaa yɔri ka niriba maa maali n‑vuuri nti yakki, u daa zaŋŋi koom m‑peaai u nuusi daadam maa tooni. Ni, “Man nuu ka doo wa kuubu ni! Ya tuma nla.”

english_verse: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka zama maa zaa daa la'am n‑yɛli, “Tɔ! Kyɛ ka u ziim taalli kpalim n‑nyɛ tirima dinni, ni ti biisi la dinni gba!”

english_verse: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”

english_verse: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa laa n‑yɛtti, ni, “U niŋi la Naawunni yɛdda, ka yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la Naawunni biiya: tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ŋɔn Naawunni yi siri boori u, ŋɔn faam u m‑basi ka ti nya!”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Ban daa deem u lala n‑naai ba daa yeaai ba kparigu maa ka labsi u maŋtoori neema n‑yeeli u. Ka daa mari u n‑yi ni ba kyaŋŋi nti kpaasi u.

english_verse: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.

english_verse: Then they sat down to guard him.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa zinni bɛni ka gɔsiri u ni.

english_verse: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Di saha maa Yisa daa kaasiya pam, ni, “iIlayi, Ilayi, lima sabakyitani?” - di gbinni n nyɛ ‘N Naawunni, n Naawunni, wula ka i kyɛ ma m‑basi?’

english_verse: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.

english_verse: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.

english_verse: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
translation_verse: Nti yɛli u, ni, “Ti wumiya, ziridaana wa ŋɔn daa na bɛ ni maa, ni, u yɛtti, ni, u nin niŋi dabsa ata pɔi ka naayi doaai.

english_verse: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna kyɛ ka soogyadima kyaŋŋi nti guri u tampifeoo gballi maa n‑niŋi dabsa ata la. Dama di pa seem u nyaandɔliba nin zu u n‑yiisi na, ka yɛli niriba, ni, u doaai la kum ni. Di yi bɛ lala, di naari nin niŋi yɔmsigu n‑gaari daŋŋni dinni ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.”

english_verse: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”

english_verse: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.

english_verse: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.

translation_verse: Ka Toondaandima la daa la'asi kpamma zaa na nti zinni n‑ŋmɛ taaba saawara ka gbaai gbaari. Ba daa zaŋŋi la ligri pam n‑da tɔbbiisi maa noori

english_verse: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
translation_verse: Ya wulisima ba, ni, ba dɔla man zaali ya noori seem maa zaa. Ka ya baŋŋma, ni, n nin bɛ ya saani, halli ni duniya wa saha maa naari.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya di zɔri dabeem. Ya kyɛma nti yɛli m mabiisi la, ni, ba kyɛm Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni - bɛni ka ba nin nya ma.”

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
translation_verse: Di daa bu yuui ka tingbaŋŋu daa vibgi pam, ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yi arizana ni n‑sigi na n‑vuui gballi dunoori kugbiliŋŋu maa m‑biligi ka n‑yi kpaŋŋa n‑zinni ka zugu.

english_verse: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
translation_verse: Ka u nindaa nya'ari ni n‑ŋmanni hoo saa ŋɔn yi nya'asi seem la, ka u neema pɛligi hoo gumni.

english_verse: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
translation_verse: Dabeem daa gbaai gbaliguriba maa, halli ka ba ningbina kpi m‑maai hoo kum la.

english_verse: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa tɔ'asi n‑yɛli pɔ'aba maa, ni, “Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya. M mi halli, ni, ya boori la Yisa ziiya, Nazarɛti dɔsɔ ban daa kpaasi la.

english_verse: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
translation_verse: Amaa ya kyɛm yooma n‑yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, u neaai kum ni ka u nin daŋŋi ya tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Galilii. Bɛni ka ya nin ti nya u. Ya nyama, m pun yɛli ya ni.”

english_verse: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yisa daa tuusi ba soori ni m‑puusi ba, ni, alaafɛa? Ba daa pɔri u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni m‑bɔbli u nɔba ka gyeemni u.

english_verse: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka pɔ'aba maa ŋɔn daa ti yi soori, tɔbbiisi la seeba ban daa guri gballi maa daa labi timpuuni nti yɛli Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima la seelli zaa din niŋi.

english_verse: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
translation_verse: ka yɛli, ni, sɔ yi ti boosi ba Yisa yɛla, ban yɛlima, ni, ba daa gbisiri ni yuŋŋu ka Yisa nyaandɔliba la kyɛ na n‑zu Yisa ningbiŋŋu la u tampifeoo gballi maa ni.

english_verse: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
translation_verse: Ka tɔbbiisi maa daa deaai ba ligri ka daa tum ban daa bulisi ba seem la. Di zugu lahabaari kanŋɔ na bɛ Yɛhudiya dima maa saani halli ni zuna.

english_verse: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
translation_verse: Yisa daa paai ba na ka yɛli ba, ni, “Ba ti ma la yiiko zaa duniya wa ni, ni arizana ni gba.

english_verse: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
translation_verse: Dizugu ya kyɛma n‑yi m‑paai duniya wa lugri kam zaa n‑wulisiri tinsi zaa niriba. Ya deaama ba n‑su Naawunni koom ya Ba Naawunni yuuri zugu, ni u Biiya, ni u Siiya Vuusim Kasi yuuri zugu gba.

english_verse: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
translation_verse: ka bɛni ka ba daa nya u. Di saha ba daa gyeemni u ni, amaa baseeba daa birisiri ni.

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Yoomyooma ka ba daa basi ba laama maa ka dɔli u.

english_verse: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi tooni beela maa, u daa nya Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii biiya, ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni, ka ba daa bɛ nyariŋŋu ni m‑maanni ba laama.

english_verse: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka lahabaari wa daa gyiligi tiŋŋa maa yoomyooma, ka u yɛttɔ'a n daa bɛ Galilii tinsi zaa ni.

english_verse: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna Naawunni daa pun kyɛ ka Anabi Aizaaya sɔbi, ni: “Nnya n tuumu maa nŋɔ, N tumni u ni u ti daŋŋi i tooni: Ŋɔna nin maali soori maa n‑ti i.”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Gyɔni mun daa yɛ la arakoomi kɔbri kparigu, ka daa mari gbaŋŋu n‑loori u seaa, ka u diibu daa kur nyɛ la toori, ni siiri.

english_verse: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Gyɔni daa moonni, ni, “Sɔ nin kyɛ n nyaaŋŋa na n‑kpeem n‑gaari ma, halli ka mani bu sumsi ni n kuntiri m‑pirigi u namna n‑tiri u.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti paai Kapirinyum. M‑paaya, ka Vuusim dabsa maa ni ka Yisa daa la'amni ni Kapirinyum dima n‑kpeeri wumpuusigu la'aŋŋu duu maa ni nti wulisiri ba.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'amaŋni zugu, ka u bu birisira halli beela; ni, di pa la kaman zaaligu baŋŋniba la ŋɔn yi wulisiri seem la.

english_verse: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋiya ka Yisa daa yi Nazarɛti, tinseaa kan bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni la, ka daa kyɛ na nti kpɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari maa ni n‑su koom Gyɔni nuu ni.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti yi koom maa ni na, u daa nya ka sangbana n bɔkki ka Sii maa daa sigri u zugu na. U daa gɔsi ka Sii maa gɔsibu daa ŋmani hoo naŋmanni ŋɔn bɛ seem la.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka kɔnsɔ daa ti kyɛ u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni n‑susiri u; u daa yɛli, ni, “I yi da sakki, i naa n‑kyɛ ka n kɔmni wa naai ka n kpɛ n‑taabi ni.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: Ni, “Saha maa paaya; Naawunni naam la bɛ la ya saani. Ya takkma ya beebu, ni ya teesibeeri, ka niŋni lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛdda.”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Ka u daa booli ba, ni, ba dɔli u na, ka ba daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa, ka bakki ka kyɛ ba ba Zɛbidii, ni u tuntumniba, ka ba daa kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Ka u gaari tooni n‑gbaai pɔ'a maa nuu n‑suŋŋi u ka u doaai n‑zaani, ka u dooru daa basi u, ka u daa yi na n‑tum n‑ti ba.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: nti nya u, n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ba zaasa boori la i ziiya.”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni, ka arizimbeoo daa kur bɛ u ni; di daama daa kaasi

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: ka yɛli, “Yisa nyin Nazarɛti doo wa! Wula ka i ya mukki ti? I kyɛnni na ni i ti sa'am ti, bee? M mi nyin ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa: ni, nyin nyɛ la ŋɔn nyɛ bunkasikasi ka yi Naawunni saani na!”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa nyikki u, ka tasi u n‑yɛli, ni, “Sina i vuuri ka yi na ka kyɛ u!”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Ka arizimbeoo maa daa dam doo maa n‑lusi kyirigiligi, ka kaasi pam m‑bakki ka kyɛ doo maa.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Ka di daa gyɛri ba zaasa, ka ba yi yɛtti taaba, “Bɔ nŋɔa? Wulisigu paalli nŋɔa! U tɔ'asiri la ni yiiko, halli u yɛtti arizimbeeri gba ka ba sakkri u noori.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛ ka ti gaari n‑kyaŋŋi tingbaŋŋu wa tinkpanseesi ni, bɛni gba ka n ya mooli lahabaari maa: dama dinŋɔ zugu ka n yi na.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa zɔ u namboo ka daa teaai u nuu n‑siisi u, ka yɛli, “N sakkiya, di naama!”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa yi la'aŋŋu duu maa, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑ti kpɛ Saimoon ni Anduru yiri ni, ŋɔna ni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Di saha ka waari n daa mari Saimoon deempɔ'a ka u ningbina daa bii, ka u dɔya. Ka ba daa yɛli Yisa u yɛla.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Di daari zaanɔyuŋŋu, wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti luri maa, ba daa mari seeba ban beera, ni arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa bɛ seeba zugri la, n‑kyɛ u saani na;

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: ka tiŋŋa maa niriba zaa daa la'asi na nti mugi yiri maa za'anɔri.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
translation_verse: Ka u daa tibi ba dooribeeri maa zaa ka kari arizimbeeri pam n‑yiisi niriba saani. U daa bu sakki ni arizimbeeri maa tɔ'asira, dama ba daa mi ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa.

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Ka din daa kaai beoo asuba pinipini, u daa yisigi n‑yi. U daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ moo ni nti bɛ u kɔkɔ m‑puusiri Naawunni.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Amaa Saimoon ni u taaba maa daa bɔ m‑bɔ

english_verse: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Lala ka u daa yi n‑gyiligi Galilii tinsi zaa n‑yiisiri wa'azu ba la'aŋŋu duri ni, ka katti arizimbeeri daadam saani.

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Ka di saha maa ka u kɔmni maa daa basi u ka u daa niŋi kasi.

english_verse: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kpaamni u ni nimmoaa n‑kyeoosi u:

english_verse: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
translation_verse: ka yɛli, ni, “Di yɛli seelli n‑ti sɔ zaa. Kur kyɛm nti zaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑wulisi maalimaanna ka maali sariseesi Musa ŋɔn daa sɔbi n‑zaali zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kɔmni tippu zii la; dina n nin kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, i siri nya tippu.”

english_verse: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
translation_verse: Amaa doo maa daa yi n‑kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa wum lahabaari maa, halli ka di ti niŋi ka Yisa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa kam puuni ka ba nya u, amaa u daa kpalim m‑bɛ la moo ni. Amaa di zaa yɔri, niriba daa yiiri la lugri kam n‑kyɛnni u saani na.

english_verse: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
translation_verse: ka u daa kpɛ la Naawunni gyeeŋŋu zii maa n‑zaŋŋi boroboro n‑ŋɔbi. Pa boroboro anŋɔ taabu ka ba dooli Naawunni tooni ka a kyisiri ni? - na'ala maalimaanniba kɔkɔ n ŋɔbri a ni. Halli ka u daa laa n‑tisi u taaba maa ka ba daa ŋɔbi.”

english_verse: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
translation_verse: Ka niriba pam daa la'am halli ka paalu beela daa ka duu maa ni; yiri maa samanni gba daa ka paalu halli beela. Ka Yisa daa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a maa n‑tiri ba.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
translation_verse: Di saha ka niriba anaasi daa zi gbariga m‑paai na.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
translation_verse: Ka daadam ŋɔn daa zoaai seem maa zugu ba daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑mari ba baara maa n‑kpɛ Yisa saani na. Lala maa zugu ba daa du pii maa zugu nti kyiibi voori n‑niŋi sunsukku, Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ seela maa deede. Ban daa kyiibi n‑naai la, ba daa kyɛ ka gbariga ni u sɔŋŋu maa zaa sigi na nti doonni Yisa tooni.

english_verse: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: “Bɔ ka doo wa tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti wa? - lala ka u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑zaantiri Naawunni zaaziiya! Ŋɔni n‑lee n‑toaai n‑kyɛ niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba? Ka pa ni Naawunni kɔkɔa?”

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Amaa m boori la ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba duniya wa ni.” Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa,

english_verse: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: ni, “N ya yɛli i ni, ni, i doaama ka kpikki i sɔŋŋu wa n‑kuli!”

english_verse: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑labi n‑dɔli mɔ'aduuri maa ni. Ka niriba zaa daa kyɛ u saani na ka u daa piili n‑wulisiri ba.

english_verse: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa naa n‑yɛli ba, “Vuusim dabsiri ka ba daa zaali ninsaala zugu, pa la Vuusim Dabsiri zugu ka ba daa maali ninsaala.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ ŋɔn Liivai yiri maa, ban ni Yisa nyaandɔliba. Ka lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima pam daa dɔli ba n‑kpɛa, ka ba zaa daa la'am nti di bundirigu, dama lala niriba daa zoaaya, ka daa dɔli Yisa.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
translation_verse: Ka karimbadima maa, baseeba ban daa dɔli Farisii dima maa soori la, daa nya ka Yisa la'amni ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima n‑dira, ka ba daa boosi u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Wula ka ŋɔn ni ninvugbanŋɔ taabu n la'amni n‑dira?”

english_verse: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Gyɔni Kɔsuura nyaandɔliba ni Farisii dima daa ti loori noori, ka ninvugseeba daa kyɛ Yisa saani na m‑boosi u, ni, “Bɔ n niŋi ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba maa loori noori, ban ni Farisii dima maa, ka nyin mun nyaandɔliba bu loora?”

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Amaa sahanseaa kyɛnni na ka ba nin ti yiisi amriya doo maa ba saani - di dabsa maa puuni ba nin ti lɔa.”

english_verse: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa m‑paasi ba nyɛsiri n‑yɛli, “Sɔ na zi n‑zaŋŋi kyinkyimpaalli tanni n‑sɛ n‑ligi bunyeerikurigu. U yi niŋi lala, tampaalli maa yi yiisi kan ligi seela maa ka kyeaai bunkurigu la, ka ka maali n‑sa'am m‑paasi.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
translation_verse: Bee, sɔ mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akura ni. U yi niŋi lala, daam maa nin ŋma ga'akura maa ka sa'am m‑basi, ka ga'a maa gba sa'am. Ay! ga'apaala n zaŋŋni dapaalli.”

english_verse: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi ka Vuusim dindahinseaa ka Yisa daa dɔli puri ni n‑gatta. U nyaandɔliba n daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli u, ka ba daa kabri kasana maa.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Ka Farisii dima daa yɛli u, “Nyama! Wula ka ba tumni puri tuma wa? Ba galimni zaaligu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri wa.”

english_verse: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Dizugu man Ninsaala Bii n su Vuusim Dabsiri fukumsi gba.”

english_verse: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
translation_verse: Lala zama maa zugu ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba boom nyariŋŋu m‑maali siri ni ba deaai u, ni, niriba maa ŋɔn kur gyiligri m‑mugri u pam.

english_verse: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba n daa bɛ bɛni m‑paasi, ka ba daa boori daliiri ni ba galim Yisa. Ka dizugu ba daa gɔsiri u ni, ni ba ti nya u nin tibi sɔ Vuusim Dabsiri daari, bee?

english_verse: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli doo maa, “Doaam n‑zaani sunsukkuni!”,

english_verse: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gɔsi n‑gyiligi ba zaa ka u suuri daa yisigi ka u sufu sa'am ba zugkpeeŋŋu ŋɔn zoaai maa zugu, ka naayi yɛli nukpiiŋŋu daana maa, ni, “Teaam i nuu maa!” Ka u daa teaai, ka u nuu maa daa nya tippu.

english_verse: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
translation_verse: Piiya ni babayi ka u daa yiisi maa, ka ba yuya nŋɔa#:- u daa pa Saimoon yupaa, ni ‘Piita’ ,

english_verse: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa du zoori zugu, ka booli ŋɔn boori seeba ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti paai u saani na.

english_verse: and to force out demons.
translation_verse: ka mari yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri m‑basira.

english_verse: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
translation_verse: ni Gyeemsi, Zɛbidii bii la, n daa bɛ ni, ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni - ban ka u daa pa ba yupaa, ni ‘Bɔ'anɛrigyeesi’ ,

english_verse: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: ni Anduru ni Filipi, ni Baatɔlɔmiyu, ni Matiyu ni Tɔmaasi ni Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Taddiyusi ni Saimoon Kyɛna'an doowa,

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka karimbaseeba daa pun yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑sigi na, ka ba daa bɛ bɛni n‑yɛtti, ni, “Biyɛlizibuli m mari u. Ii! - ArizinNaaba yiiko ka u zaŋŋni n‑katti arizimbeeri maa.”

english_verse: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
translation_verse: Daadam mun n daa zi n‑gyiligi u, ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, “I ma ni i sunzɔppa bɛ samanni m‑boori i ziiya.”;

english_verse: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa booli ba na ka zaŋŋi nyɛsa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, ni, “Sintaani ya n‑niŋi la wula ni u yiisi Sintaani m‑basi?

english_verse: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri ni, ka daadam daa laa n‑la'amni na halli ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑di bundirigu gba.

english_verse: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
translation_verse: Tinseaa yi bɛ ni ka ka timbiisi yi purigi n‑zabri taaba, lala tiŋŋa ku toaai n‑zaani.

english_verse: And a family that fights won't last long either.
translation_verse: Bee yiriseaa yi bɛ ni ka ka yiri dima yi purigi n‑zabri taaba, lala yiri ku toaai n‑zaani.

english_verse: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
translation_verse: Dizugu Sintaani muna, u yi ti za'asi u mammaŋŋa n‑zabra, ŋɔn gba ku toaai n‑zaani, u yɛla zaa naari nla.

english_verse: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, sɔ ku toaai n‑kpɛ yaa daana yiri n‑zu u neema maa ka di pa la u daŋŋi n‑lɔ lala yaa daana maa ka naayi nyaŋŋi n‑fa u yiri ni buna zaasa.

english_verse: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya maa, Naawunni nin kyɛ ninsaalidima ala'akyi taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ba; halli nira yi tum tumbeeri, bee u yi sa'am Naawunni yuuri gba:

english_verse: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
translation_verse: amaa ŋɔn sa'am Sii Kasi yuuri maa, u ku nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi halli saha kan ka naari, ka u nin di la saha din ka tariga taalli.”

english_verse: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
translation_verse: - Ban daa yɛtti, ni, u mari la arizimbeoo, dizugu ka u daa yɛtti anŋɔ maa.

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ma, ni u sunzɔppa, daa paai na n‑zaani samanni ka tum sɔ ni u kpɛ m‑booli u na.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “M ma ni n sunzɔppa n nyɛ bandima?”,

english_verse: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
translation_verse: ka daa gɔsi ban zi n‑gyiligi u maa ka daa yɛtti, “N‑nya ba! M ma ni n sunzɔppa maa nŋɔa!

english_verse: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam tumni Naawunni yuubu tuma nyɛ la n sunzɔ, bee n sunzɔpɔ'a, bee m ma.”

english_verse: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa laa n‑gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑wulisiri niriba, Galilii mɔ'ari kpaŋŋa. Niriba ban daa la'am u saani maa daa zoaaya pam, dizugu u daa kpɛ la nyariŋŋu puuni nti zinni. Nyariŋŋu maa daa bɛ la koom puuni ka daadam maa daa kpalim mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu n‑wumna.

english_verse: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
translation_verse: ka ŋɔn daa waanni maa, a seesi daa lu la sɔkpaŋŋa, ka niinsi daa kyɛ na n‑di a zaa.

english_verse: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
translation_verse: Ka u daa nyɛsi ba nyɛsa ka a wulisi ba yɛla pam;

english_verse: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: ka daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ya wumma! Dɔsɔ n daa kyaŋŋi puu, ni u ti waali u bumbura;

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Tɔ, di saha maa ka u bumburiseesi daa lu kuga ziiya ka tanni ka ni see beela fiiŋ, ka daa tɔ bula yooma dama tanni maa daa bu zoaai;

english_verse: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
translation_verse: amaa wuntaŋŋa daa bii ka ka bula maa daa gyɛkki n‑kpi, dama a nya'a daa bu sigi wa'aligu.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
translation_verse: Ka seesi gba daa lu la goosi ni, ka goosi maa daa zoaai na n‑gabi kabula maa ka a daa kɔŋŋi dɔ'abu.

english_verse: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, “Ti nin yɛli, ni, Naawunni naam ŋmani la bɔa? Ma'asiri bɔ ka ti nin zaŋŋi n‑wulisi di gbinni? Tɔ: di ŋmani la nŋɔa#:-

english_verse: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
translation_verse: amaa di bu kpɛ la ba sufu ni ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ba nya'a kaalinsi zugu ba bu yuura: dizugu yɛlibeoo bee vɔlisigu yi ti kyɛ na Naawunni yɛligu maa zugu, ba yi labi la nyaaŋŋa yooma.

english_verse: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Ni#:- ‘Ba nin gɔsi m‑maali n‑gɔsi, Amaa ba ku nya seela: Ba mun nin wum m‑maali n‑wum, Amaa ba ku baŋŋi di gbinni. Di yi pa lala, ba da naa n‑labi Naawunni saani na, Ka u da kyɛ ba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba.’ ”

english_verse: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban ŋmani la bumbura an lu soori ni la n nyɛ seeba ban yi wum yɛligu maa pɔi, ka di bu yuui ka Sintaani naayi kyɛ na n‑fa yɛligu maa ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ya wumma: ŋɔn butti kyi la n nyɛ hoo ŋɔn moonni Naawunni yɛligu.

english_verse: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and welcome the message. They produce
translation_verse: Amaa ninvugseeba ban ŋmani la bumbura an lu tam ziiya la n nyɛ baseeba ban wumni Naawunni yɛligu maa ka deaari ka, ka laa n‑wanna: baseeba pisitata, seeba pisiyooyoobu, seeba mun kɔbkɔbga.”

english_verse: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka, bee u zaŋŋi ka n‑sɔ'ai gadu lɔŋni. Ay! U yi zaŋŋni ka n‑tɔmni la saazugu.

english_verse: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
translation_verse: U daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya kpasim suŋŋa ya wumpu ziiya! Yan ma'asiri seem n‑tiri niriba, lala ka ba nin ma'asi n‑ti ya gba, halli ba nin paasi ya lala.

english_verse: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
translation_verse: Ni, “Naawunni naam ŋmani la nŋɔa#:- Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑daa yaam bumbura u paalu ni n‑naai;

english_verse: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
translation_verse: Ay! - Tanni maa daa tumni ka maŋtoori tuma n‑kyɛ ka kyi maa ti niŋi: a daa daŋŋi m‑buli m‑pusi n‑dɔ'ai, halli nti piri.

english_verse: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka kyi maa yi ti kuui, doo maa nin zaŋŋi u suuwa n‑yi na n‑ŋma a ni, dama kyi ŋmaabu saha paaya.”

english_verse: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
translation_verse: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni n‑zaŋŋi zinzambeelli, m‑buri ka tiŋŋa ni. Tɔ, bumbilifiiŋ nla, ka bumbura kam zaa n galisi n‑gaari ka.

english_verse: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka ka daa buli, n‑zoaai n‑niŋi buntitaari n‑gaari zeeri bumbula zaasa, ka daa tɔ wula, halli ka niinsi daa tɛ ba teeri a zugu ka nyari ka vaari maasim.”

english_verse: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
translation_verse: U daa bu tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseelli ka ma'asa ka di ni, amaa ŋɔn daa ti bɛ ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba kɔkɔa, sahanseaa la u daa yi wulisiri ba la yɛttɔ'anyɛsa maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
translation_verse: Di daari zaanoori maa ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba la, ni, “Ya kyɛ ka ti lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari wa ya'ari sa.”

english_verse: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri u yɛligu wa n‑tiri niriba la, u daa kur zaŋŋi ma'asa ni nyɛsa anŋɔ taabu n‑tɔ'asira. Lala ka di daa paai ba zaa yinniyinni bamsim ŋɔn sa'ai seem.

english_verse: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi u ŋɔn daa pun bɛ nyariŋŋu seaa la ni, ka bakki n‑kyɛ zama maa mɔ'aduuri ni. Ka nyarinseesi gba daa dɔli u m‑paasi.

english_verse: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
translation_verse: Di daa bu yuui ka pɛbsititaari daa ti yisigi ka daa mari nyariŋŋu maa n‑laantiri ni, halli ka koom daa ti kpɛ ka ni.

english_verse: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
translation_verse: Yisa daa bɛ la nyariŋŋu maa nyaaŋŋa ziiya n‑kukki dafeeru n‑gbisira. Ka ba daa neaai u ka yɛli u, “Ti Karimba, di bu mugsi i tin ya kpi wa zugu?”

english_verse: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Ka u daa neaai ka tasi pɛbsigu ni koom maa, ni, “Ya sina! Kur kyɛla!”, ka a daa siri kyɛya, ka di lu n‑fɔ surimm!

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
translation_verse: Din saha ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Bɔzugu ka dabeem gbaai ya lala? Ammi, ya na bu niŋi yɛdda, bee?”

english_verse: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
translation_verse: Ka dabeem pam daa maali n‑gbaai ba, ka ba daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba, ni, “Bɔ nira nŋɔa? - halli ka u mari yiiko n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asiri pɛbsigu ni mɔ'ari gba ka a wumna!”

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: dama u daa yɛtti, ni, “N yi siisi u bunyeerigu n nin nya alaafɛa.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “M biiya, kyɛma ni alaafɛa. I yɛdda zugu ka i nya tippu.”

english_verse: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
translation_verse: U daa pun zɔ n‑gyili tiima dima saani halli nti gyɛa, ka di u marigu zaa ka bu nya tippu. Di zaa puuni u dooru maa daa bu baligra, amaa ka lee n daa paasiri ni.

english_verse: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa kpɛ nti yɛli, “Wula ka di nyɛ basaa ni kunkuma? Bii wa bu kpi, amaa u gbisiri ni.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa du mɔ'ari maa duuri m‑paai Gyɛrasa dima tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
translation_verse: Gbala zii wa ni ka u daa kur bɛ ni; ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑gbaai u m‑ba, halli kyɔrimi gba.

english_verse: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
translation_verse: Halli ba daa pun zaŋŋi kutti m‑ba u nuusi ni u nɔba, amaa u daa kur kyisiri a ni - sɔsɔ daa ka yaa n nin toaai n‑gbaai u suŋŋa.

english_verse: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
translation_verse: Lala zugu u daa kpalim m‑bɛ gbala maa ni, ni zɔya zugu, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa. U daa kaasira, ka mari kuga n‑ŋmaari u maŋŋa.

english_verse: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
translation_verse: Tɔ; doo wa daa pun bɛ wa'aligu n‑nya Yisa,

english_verse: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
translation_verse: ka daa zɔ na n‑tuusi u soori nti gbaanni u tooni ka tasi n‑yɛli kpeeŋŋa, ni, “I ya niŋi ma la wula, Yisa nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa biiya? Poom noori n‑ti ma, ni, i ku namsi ma!”

english_verse: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
translation_verse: Ka u yi susiri u, ni, u di kari ba n‑yiisi tiŋŋa maa ni.

english_verse: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di saha maa kurikyuudima pam gba daa bɛ la kunkunni lugri ni n‑dira.

english_verse: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
translation_verse: Ka arizimbeeri maa daa susi Yisa n‑yɛli, “Kyɛ ka ti kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ kurikyuudima wa ni.”

english_verse: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
translation_verse: Ka u daa ti ba soori, ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni - kurikyuudima maa mun daa paai tusa ayi - ka ba daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom daa di ba.

english_verse: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa guri kurikyuudima maa daa zɔ la yoomyooma n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai timpuuni niriba, ni ban tumni puri ni la, nti tisi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nya seem maa.

english_verse: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Ka niriba daa yi na ni ba ti nya yɛttɔ'aseaa kan niŋi maa. Ban daa paai na maa, ba daa nya arizindima ŋɔn yi ka kyɛ dɔsɔ la, ka u zi la suŋŋa ka yɛ u sutura ka yɛttɔ'aseaa ka u.

english_verse: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
translation_verse: Di daa gbaai ba dabeem pam. Ka baseeba ban nini nya maa daa yɛli daadam maa Yisa ŋɔn kari arizimbeeri n‑tibi doo maa seem, ni kurikyuudima yɛla gba.

english_verse: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑susi u, ni, u kyɛma ka kyɛ ba tiŋŋa maa.

english_verse: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa bu sakki lala, amaa ka yɛli u, ni, “Kulima i toori niriba saani n‑yɛli ba ti Dugma ŋɔn zɔ i namboo ka tum seem n‑ti i.”

english_verse: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi nti piili n‑gyinni Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu maa ni n‑yɛtti Yisa ŋɔn niŋi seem n‑ti u maa, ka alahazibu daa gbaai ba zaa.

english_verse: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
translation_verse: U daa tɔ'asiya lala ka di ku ba la laari; amaa u daa kari niriba maa zaa n‑yiisi, ka zaŋŋi la bii maa ba ni u ma m‑paasi u nyaandɔliba ata la zugu ka ba kpɛ bii maa ŋɔn dɔ seela la.

english_verse: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa n‑du mɔ'ari maa duuri sa maa, daadam pam n daa la'am n‑kyɛ u saani na. Ka ŋɔn daa na bɛ mɔ'ari maa gbiriŋŋu la ni,

english_verse: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
translation_verse: ba la'aŋŋu duu kpeensɔ daa kyɛ na ka nya u - doo maa yuuri daa boonni Gyayiruusi.

english_verse: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔna n daa zɔ na n‑gbaani u tooni n‑susi u n‑yɛli, “M bipumbila n ya n‑kpi,” ni, “Kur kyɛm na n‑zaŋŋi i nuusi m‑pa u zugu, ka u nin nya nyɔvuri ni alaafɛa.”

english_verse: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka lala zugu ka Yisa daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi, ka niriba pam gba daa dɔla m‑mugri u soori ni.

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
translation_verse: Di saha maa gba ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ka u daa soori halli yuuma piiya ni ayi ka ziim bu goaara.

english_verse: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a wa daa wum niriba ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri Yisa yɛla seem maa, ka lala zugu u daa la'am ni zama maa n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa nti tabli n‑siisi u bunyeerigu,

english_verse: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
translation_verse: Ka di saha maa u soobu maa siri daa ŋmaai, ka u mammaŋŋa daa baŋŋi, ni, u dooru basiya ka u nya alaafɛa.

english_verse: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa gba daa baŋŋi, ni, yiiko yi la u saani n‑kpɛ sɔa, ka u ŋmaligi daadam maa ni m‑boosi, ni, “Ŋɔni n siisi n neema?”

english_verse: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa yɛli u, “Nyam daadam ŋɔn daari taaba seem, ka i laa m‑boosi, ni, ‘Ŋɔni n siisi ma?’ fa?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn mun daa na gɔsiri n‑gyinna, ni u ti nya ŋɔn siisi u.

english_verse: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
translation_verse: Di saha ka pɔ'a maa daa gyigri ni dabeem ka mun mi din niŋi u seem, ka u daa kyɛ na nti gbaani Yisa tooni ka yɛli u din niŋi u maa zaa kasi.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa na bɛ la yɛttɔ'a ni, ka ba daa yi kpeema maa yiri ni na nti yɛli kpeema maa, ni, “I bii maa kpiya; wula zugu ka i laa m‑mukkri ti karimba maa yaasa?”

english_verse: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
translation_verse: Yisa daa wum ba lahabaari maa, ka yɛli kpeema la, ni, “Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i, kur niŋma yɛdda!”

english_verse: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
translation_verse: U daa bu sakki ni sɔsɔ dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi na'ala Piita, ni Gyeemsi ni u sunzɔ Gyɔni.

english_verse: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
translation_verse: Ba daa paai kpeema maa yiri ka di kur nyɛ la basaa ni, ni kunkuma ni fabla.

english_verse: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
translation_verse: Yoomyooma ka bipumbila maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna: - u daa nyɛ la yuuma piiya ni ayi biiya. Nŋɔ maa daa niŋi ba alahazibu ni sufupeelli pam.

english_verse: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
translation_verse: U daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ wum din niŋi maa, ka laa n‑yɛli ba ni ba zaŋŋma diibu n‑ti bii maa ka u di.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban daa lɔŋŋi mɔ'ari maa, ba daa ti paai Gyɛnɛsarɛti tiŋŋa na, ka daa nya nyarima loobu ziiya bɛni, n‑lɔ ka yi na.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Anabi kam nyari gyirima luginseelli kam zaa ni, na'ala u mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa niriba, ni u dɔ'ariba, ni u yiri dima maa saani ka u bu nyara.”

english_verse: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
translation_verse: Amaa NaaHɛroodi ŋɔn daa ti wum di, u daa yɛliya, “Gyɔni Kɔsuura n nyɛ u, mani n daa fikki u zugu, amaa u laa n‑doaai kum ni na!”

english_verse: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari daa ti paai, ka u kyaŋŋi la'aŋŋu duu maa ni nti gbaai wulisigu. Niriba pam daa bɛ bɛni: ban daa wum u yɛla maa, di daa kyɛ ka alahazibu gbaai ba ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, “Yani ka doo wa lee m‑bamsi u yɛla wa zaa? Yam bɔ ka ba zaŋŋi n‑ti u? - halli n‑ti u yiiko ka u tumni tunkara wa?

english_verse: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
translation_verse: Pa kampinta maa nŋɔa? - Mariama bii la? - Ka u sunzɔppa n nyɛ Gyeemsi, ni Gyoosɛfi, ni Gyuudasi, ni Saimoon la - halli u sunzɔpɔ'aba gba bɛ la ti saani!” Lala zugu ba daa bu boori u yɛla halli beela.

english_verse: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
translation_verse: Ka yɛli ba, ni, ba di zaŋŋi seelli m‑mari m‑paasi ba kyɛnni maa ni, na'ala zangbeaa: ni, ba di zaŋŋi bundirigu, bee booriga, bee n‑suuri ligri gyɛfa ni.

english_verse: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
translation_verse: Ya yi ti paai tinseaa ka ba ku sakki n‑deaai ya, ka za'asi ya yɛttɔ'a wumpu, ya yim tiŋŋa maa ni ka kpaasi ya napɔna tankpoo m‑basi n‑niŋi tiŋŋa wa ni, ka lala nin leebi din wulisiri ba halibeoo ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”

english_verse: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
translation_verse: Di daa nyɛ la lala zaa zugu ka Hɛroodiyasi daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi Gyɔni, ka daa boori ni u ku u; amaa ka u daa bu nyaŋŋi, NaaHɛroodi zugu.

english_verse: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
translation_verse: Dama Naaba maa daa zɔri Gyɔni pam ka guri u suŋŋa, ŋɔn daa mi u yɛla zugu, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu ka zɔri Naawunni maa. Gyɔni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi la daa kyɛ ka NaaHɛroodi birisi pam u sufu ni, amaa di zaa yɔri u daa wumni u yɛla maa ni sufupeelli.

english_verse: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa pɔ noori ka yɛli u, “N nin ti i nyin boori seelli kam zaa halli di yi nyɛ n naam tingbaŋŋu maa purigri.”

english_verse: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Ka bipuŋŋa maa daa yi m‑boosi u ma, ni, “N susim bumbɔa?”, ka u ma labsi n‑yɛli u, “Susim Gyɔni Kɔsuura zugu!”

english_verse: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Ka bipuŋŋa maa daa labi yooma n‑kpɛ Naaba saani nti susi n‑yɛli, “Zaŋŋma Gyɔni zugu n‑niŋi laa ni n‑ti ma na ya'abi!”

english_verse: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi Naa maa sufusa'aŋŋu, amaa u daa bu boori ni u mɔŋŋi bii la, ŋɔn daa pun pɔ noori n‑ti u u saamma tooni zugu.

english_verse: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi laa ni m‑mari na. Ka Naaba daa deaai ka n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti bii maa, ka bii maa daa mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti u ma la.

english_verse: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, ba daa kyɛ na nti zaŋŋi kum maa m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai.

english_verse: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
translation_verse: lala maa zugu Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa bu nya saha halli n‑di bundirigu. Dinŋɔna u daa yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛ ka ti bakki n‑yi n‑kyaŋŋi luginseelli sɔsɔ ŋɔn ka ni, m‑bɛ ti kɔkɔa, ka ya vuusi.”

english_verse: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa doaai n‑kpɛ ba nyariŋŋu la n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ba kɔkɔa.

english_verse: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
translation_verse: Ka saha ŋɔn daa pun yuui ka yuŋŋu zibri maa, u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ na nti yɛli u, “Saha pun gaari, ka kpa wa laa n‑nyɛ la luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya tim ba bunseelli ka ba di.” Ka ba labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i boori la ti kyaŋŋi nti da ligri pam paanu na n‑diisi ba, bee? Ay! - ti ligri ku paai lala!”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, “Paanukpila ala ka ya mara? Ya kyɛm nti nya!” Ban daa gɔsi n‑nya maa, ba daa labi u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, “Paanukpila anu ka ti mara; ni zaasim ayi.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, ba kyɛ ka niriba maa puri ba mansi n‑zinni mɔmaasa la zugu.

english_verse: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Ban daa nya u ka di gyɛri ba maa, Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, n‑yɛli ba, “Ya mari sufukpeemni ka di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya! Ka mani nla!”

english_verse: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi paanu anu ni zaasim ayi maa n‑gɔsi saazugu m‑puusi Naawunni, ka purigi paanudima maa, ni zaasim maa m‑paasi, ka yi zaŋŋi di n‑ti u nyaandɔliba maa ni ba tɔri n‑ti ba.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
translation_verse: Di saha maa ba zaa daa di n‑tigi,

english_verse: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
translation_verse: ka nyaandɔliba maa daa la'asi dikyɛba an kpalim la, ni zaasikyɛra la, m‑paali peeri piiya ni ayi.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa, sahanseaa u mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa, Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni la n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri sa n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.

english_verse: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti puusi Naawunni.

english_verse: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
translation_verse: Yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa ti ya n‑zibi la, nyariŋŋu maa daa bɛ la mɔ'ari maa sunsukkuni, ka Yisa daa kpalim m‑bɛ mɔ'aduuri ni u kɔkɔa.

english_verse: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
translation_verse: Di saha ka pɛbsigu daa zeeri ba tooni n‑nyaŋŋi nyariŋŋu maa koom maa ni, n‑ta'ai ba kyɛnni, ka di daa mugsi ba pam; ka Yisa daa nya lala. Di daa paari bɛkyikyeoo saha, ka u daa kyɛ ba saani na n‑kyɛnni koom maa zugu, ka daa ya yakki ba n‑gaari.

english_verse: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa nya u ka u kyɛnni koom zugu, ka ba zaa daa yɛli, ni, kpiinyisigi n nyɛ u, ka daa ŋmɛ kuŋŋa.

english_verse: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
translation_verse: Ka u kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni ka pɛbsigu maa daa kyɛ zeebu. Ka di laa n‑gyɛri ba, halli nti yakki.

english_verse: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba daa na bu baŋŋi ŋɔn diisi niriba tusa anu maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem: ka di zugu ba sufu maa daa bu zuligi na.

english_verse: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
translation_verse: Ban daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni la, bɛni niriba maa daa baŋŋi ya'abi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u,

english_verse: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
translation_verse: ka ba daa zɔ n‑gyili tingbaŋŋu maa zaa ka gbaai baaridima ziibu na. Ba daa doonni ba bundoosi zugu n‑ziiri n‑kyɛnni ban daa wum, ni, u bɛ seela zaa.

english_verse: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ tinkpanseaa, bee tinseaa puuni, halli m‑bɛ puri ni gba, ba daa zaŋŋi ban daa ka alaafɛ la n‑dooli daasi ni nti susi u, ni, u kur kyɛ ka ba siisi u kparigu nangbanni, ka ban daa siisi zaa daa nya alaafɛa.

english_verse: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Farisiisi maa seeba daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm na, ban ni zaaligu karimbaseeba.

english_verse: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
translation_verse: “Yarima muna - ya za'asiri Naawunni soori, ni ya ti dɔli ya mammaŋŋa kaari maa: yam nla, bee?

english_verse: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
translation_verse: Ban mun yi bu dɔli ba soori m‑peaai ba nuusi ni nimmoaa, ba ku sakki n‑di bundirigu;

english_verse: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
translation_verse: ka ba yi yi daa na see ka ba peaai ba mansi lala pɔi ka naayi di: ka ba yaadima kaari laa m‑paasi pam, hoo ban peaari kɔbeesi ni dugri ni tasilaasi seem.

english_verse: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka lala niriba daa boosi Yisa, ni, “Wula ka i nyaandɔliba maa bu dɔli ti yaadima kaari la, amaa ka diri ni nuda'ari?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Anabi Aizaaya daa pun mari yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn daa bugi n‑yɛli ya birikɔ'anu dima yɛla seem la; ka daa sɔbi, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, ni#:- ‘Niriba wa gyeemni ma la ni ba nangbana. Ba sufu mun yi n saani Ka kpalim n‑wɔ'a;

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
translation_verse: Ban wulisiri la zaaligu wa, Ba gyeemni ma la yɔri; Di pa la bunseelli zaa Na'ala ninsaala yɛligu kɔkɔa.’ - Dama ya kyɛ Naawunni zaaligu ka dɔli saalidima kaari.”

english_verse: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni,

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Amaa yarima wulisiri, ni, nira ŋɔn laa n‑zaŋŋi bunseelli n‑suŋŋi u ba ni u ma, u yi ti yɛli ba, ni, ‘Bunni maa nyɛ la “Kɔribaani” ’, bee ‘Naawunni dinni’,

english_verse: You won't let those people help their parents.
translation_verse: ka mɔŋŋi u ba ni u ma dizugu, ni, u ka taalli.

english_verse: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
translation_verse: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni, “Din yiiri ninsaala puuni na muna, dina n sa'amni ninvugkasi maa.

english_verse: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa m‑booli niriba maa u saani na nti yɛli ba, ni, “Ya wumma! Ya zaa baŋŋma!

english_verse: The food that you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
translation_verse: Seelli ka ni din nin kpɛ ninsaala puuni ka kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira puuni na n kyɛ ka u leebri da'ari daana.”

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa bakki ka kyɛ daadam maa n‑kpɛ yiri maa ni maa, u nyaandɔliba daa susi u, ni, u wulisim ba yɛttɔ'a maa gbinni.

english_verse: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya gba yam bu gaari niriba wa dinni maa zugu, fa? Ya bu baŋŋi di gbinni? Ni, bunseelli din kpɛ ninsaala puuni ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka ninvugkasi leebi la da'ari daana.

english_verse: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
translation_verse: Dama lala buna bu kpeeri u sufu ni; ay! - u puuri ni ka a kpeera, ka gatti n‑yi u ningbina maa, bangyira ziiya.” Dinŋɔ ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisi, ni, bundirigu zaa sumni diibu ka bu kyisira.

english_verse: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
translation_verse: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”

english_verse: The woman replied, “Lord, even puppies eat the crumbs that children drop from the table.”
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni, n Daana, amaa babisi ban dɔya ku kɔŋŋi ba piisi biisi sa'akyɛba an luri tiŋŋa la.”

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
translation_verse: Yisa daa yisigi n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya tiŋŋa la. U daa kpɛ yiri ni ka bu boori ni sɔsɔ baŋŋi, ni, u bɛ bɛni. Amaa u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa yɛli u, “Kyɛ ka ti daŋŋi n‑diisi biisi la. Di bu tu ni ti zaŋŋi biisi bundirigu maa m‑basi babisi la.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Mboo! Labsigu seaa nyin labsi wa zugu, kur labma n‑kuli; i nin paai n‑nya ka arizinni maa yiya, ka kyɛ i bipuŋŋa maa!”

english_verse: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
translation_verse: Ka dizugu u daa kuli nti nya u bii maa ka u dɔ la gadu zugu; ka arizimbeoo maa siri pun yi ni ka kyɛ u.

english_verse: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa yi Taaya n‑dɔli Saidoon tiŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi, ka daa kyɛnni n‑dɔli Tinsi Piiya tingbaŋŋu maa puuni soori ni u ti paai Galilii mɔ'ari zii maa na.

english_verse: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
translation_verse: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u tuba daa bu wumna, ka u bu laa n‑nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri suŋŋa: ba daa mari lala doo maa n‑zi u m‑paai na nti susi Yisa, ni, u zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa u zugu.

english_verse: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa u kɔkɔ ka kyɛ zama maa, ka zaŋŋi u nubisi n‑tum doo maa tuba ni; ka tusi nantoori n‑zaŋŋi n‑siisi doo maa zulimni.

english_verse: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
translation_verse: Ka daa gɔsi saazugu n‑nyurim u lɔŋŋu ni, ka yɛli doo maa, ni, “Efatta'a!” - dinŋɔ gbinni nyɛ la “Yoaama!”

english_verse: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa piili n‑wumna; ka din daa ta'ari u yɛttɔ'a maa gba daa lakki, ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a kasikasi.

english_verse: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, ba di yɛli lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din niŋi maa n‑ti sɔa; amaa ŋɔn daa kur yɛtti ba nimmoaa seem maa zaa yɔri, ba daa yɛtti niriba m‑paasira.

english_verse: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa wum lala lahabaari maa muna, di daa niŋi ba zaa alahazibu ka ba daa yɛtti, ni, “U tumni seelli kam zaa la suŋŋa.”, ni, “U toaai n‑kyɛ ka tubkpiradima wumna, ka mugsi ni zɔlisi gba tɔ'asira!”

english_verse: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kpaamni ba n‑yɛli, “Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina la, ni NaaHɛroodi dinni gba.”

english_verse: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
translation_verse: Ka daa tɔ'asi lala kasikasi ka di ka sɔ'abu. Lala zugu ka Piita daa mari u n‑labi kpaŋŋa m‑piili n‑kpaamni u, ni, u di tɔ'asiri lala.

english_verse: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
translation_verse: ni, “N sufu bu maai niriba wa zugu, dama ba bɛ n saani ka di paai dabsa ata ka ka seelli din di.

english_verse: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: N yi kyɛ ka ba kuli yɔri, baseeba nin ligi soori ni, dama baseeba yi la wa'aligu na.”
