translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yɛli lala maa, doo maa daa miisi u nindaa, ka daa bakki ni sufusa'aŋŋu, dama u daa nyɛ la bundaana ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi u nyaandɔliba maa ka yɛli ba, “Di siri nin tɔ pam ni bundaana kpɛ Naawunni naam ni!”

english_verse: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Ka yɛttɔ'a anŋɔ daa kpeesi u nyaandɔliba maa alahazibu, amaa Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “M biisi, di tɔ pam ni nira ti kpɛ Naawunni naam maasim ni!

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Halli arakoomi nin ti kpɛ seeriga vɔbila ni m‑puli n‑yi ka bundaana na ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni u kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.”

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were even more amazed. They asked each other, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Di daa gyɛri u nyaandɔliba maa pam yɛlli wa zugu, ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, “Ka ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑tuligi?”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa gɔsi ba ka yɛli, “Dinŋɔ tɔ pam ninsaalidima saani, amaa Naawunni saani di bu tɔa. Dama Naawunni ku kɔŋŋi bunseelikam niŋbu.”

english_verse: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
translation_verse: Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa bɛ la soori ni n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm. Yisa daa gaari tooni, u nyaandɔliba mun daa dɔli u nyaaŋŋa ka di daa gyɛri ba pam; halli dabeem daa gbaai ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli ba zaa. Yisa daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n yi kpaŋŋa ba kɔkɔ nti yɛli ba yɛliseesi an ti ya paai u.

english_verse: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
translation_verse: U daa yɛli ba, “Ya wumma: ti wa la Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, bɛni ka ba nin zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii n‑ti gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni karimbadima nuusi ni. Ban nin ti kari n sariya n‑zaŋŋi ma taalli, ni, n sumni kum, ka naayi zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ban pa ti butiri niriba la nuu ni,

english_verse: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “I naam gyirima saha yi ti paai na, ni, ti boori ni ti zini n‑tabli i, yinni i nudirigu, ni yinni i nuzaa ziiya.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli, “Ya bu mi yan susiri seelli maa. Ammi, ya nin toaai n‑nyu wahala kɔbɛseaa man ya n‑nyu wa? Bee man ya n‑su tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi koom ka ya gba nin su lala maa?”

english_verse: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
translation_verse: Ni, “Ii! Ti nin nyaŋŋi.” Ka Yisa mun yɛli ba, “Ya siri nin ti nyu n wahala kɔbeaa la, ka su n lala koom maa,

english_verse: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
translation_verse: amaa man bu su soori ni n wulisi ban nin zinni n nudirigu bee n nuzaa zii maa. Naawunni pun maali ziziisi wa siri n‑zaali n‑ti ŋɔn gaŋŋi ninvugseeba la.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with James and John.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka u nyaandɔliba piiya ban kpalim la ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, ba suuri daa yisigi n‑niŋi Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
translation_verse: Dizugu Yisa daa booli ba, ni ba la'amma taaba na, ka yɛli ba, “Ya mi ninvugseeba ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la yɛla, ni, ba yɛtti, ni, ninvugseeba ban su ba fukumsi mari la yiiko ka tumni ba putoo, ka ba kpamma su ba yɛla ni kpeeŋŋu.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
translation_verse: Ka sɔ yi boori ni u leebi toondaana, see ka u leebi ya zaa daabri.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
translation_verse: Dama man Ninsaala Bii bu kyɛ na ni ba tumni n‑suŋŋni ma; ay! - n kyɛ na ni n tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba, ka zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri m‑pa talima ka niriba pam nya tiliginsim.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ba daa paai Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa n‑kpɛ bɛni. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa naayi yi tiŋŋa maa ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba ni zama pam, ka zoonsɔ daa zi soori kpaŋŋa m‑maanni bara, doo mun yuuri daa boonni Baatimeeyusi, ni, Timeeyusi biiya.

english_verse: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Ŋɔna daa ti wum, ni, di nyɛ la Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, n‑gatti maa, ka u daa tasi, n‑yɛli, “Yisa, NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo!”

english_verse: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Ka niriba pam daa nyikki u n‑yɛli, ni, u sina u vuuri. Amaa u daa laa n‑tasi pampam, ni, “Yisa, NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa zaani, ka yɛli, “Ya boolim u na!”, ka ba daa booli zooma maa na, ka yɛli u, “Mari sufupeelli, doaam na! Ka u boonni i maa.”

english_verse: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka zooma maa daa yisigi n‑zaani, ka yeaai u waari kparigu m‑basi, ka vaai n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa saani.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi u, ni, “Bɔ ka i boori ni n niŋi n‑ti i?” Ni, “N Karimba, m boori ni n nyari yaasa.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Kyɛma! - i yɛdda maa taasi la sookyi na n‑ti i.” Ka di saha maa u nini maa daa neaai, ka u daa doaai n‑dɔli Yisa ka ba kyɛnna.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɔ; lala ka Yisa daa kpɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni m‑paai Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri. U daa gɔsi n‑gyiligi zii kam, amaa yuŋŋu ŋɔn daa pun ya zibi maa zugu u daa yi n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Bɛtani tinkpaŋŋa maa ni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi la.

english_verse: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka zaanoori ŋɔn daa ti paai, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa yi tiŋŋa maa ni.

english_verse: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: m‑boosi u, ni, “Ŋɔni n ti i soori ni i tum i lala tuma maa? Bee, yiiko bɔ ka i mara?”

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
translation_verse: Sɔ mun yi boosi ya, ni, ni wula ka ya lɔrigri u? yan yɛlim u, ni, ya Dugma m boori u, ka mun nin labsi u na yooma.”

english_verse: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti nya bumbila maa ka u lɔ n‑zɛ samanni ni n‑tabi yiri la za'anɔri kpaŋŋa. Ban daa yi ya lɔrigi u maa,

english_verse: some of the people standing there asked, “Why are you untying the donkey?”
translation_verse: ninvugseeba ban zɛ n‑tabi bɛni daa nya ba ka boosi ba, ni, “Bɔ ka ya niŋni maa, bumbila maa ka ya lɔrigri wa?”

english_verse: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli ba Yisa ŋɔn pun yɛli ba seem maa, ka dɔppa maa daa kyɛ ka ba lɔrigi u n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Ban daa zaŋŋi buŋŋa maa n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na maa, ba daa zaŋŋi ba kyinkyina m‑pa buŋŋa maa nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa ba n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: In front of Jesus and behind him, people went along shouting, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ tooni, ni seeba gba ban daa bɛ nyaaŋŋa n‑dɔli maa, daa piili n‑kpalimna n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Hoosana! Tiliginsim Daana! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ n‑ti ŋɔn mari ti Dugma yuuri na!

english_verse: God bless the coming kingdom of our ancestor David. Hooray for God in heaven above!”
translation_verse: Ti yaaba NaaDauda naam labri n‑kyɛnni na: tabarikala! Tiliginsim Daana gyirima bɛ la saazugu! Hoosana!”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
translation_verse: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni tiŋŋa maa karimbadima daa wum lala lahabaari, ka di saha ka ba daa piili m‑boori sɔseaa ban nin nya n‑ku Yisa. Dabeem daa gbaai ba, dama di daa gyɛri daadam maa zaa Yisa wulisigu maa zugu.

english_verse: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli kinkaŋŋa maa, ni, “Sɔ ku laa n‑di i wala yaasa, halli walli yinni gba!” Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa wum ŋɔn yɛli seem wa.

english_verse: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa wulisi niriba maa, ni, “Di pun sɔbi n‑zaali Naawunni Litaafi maa ni, ni, ‘N Yiri wa ka ba nin booli, ni, “Wunsusigu Yiri” n‑ti duniya dima zaa.’ Amaa ya zaŋŋi ka n‑leebi “ta'ayigsi ŋɔn sɔ'ari seela”.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya niŋma yɛdda n‑ti Naawunni.”

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples reached Jerusalem, he went into the temple and began chasing out everyone who was selling and buying. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and the benches of those who were selling doves.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti paai Gyɛrusalɛm la, Yisa daa kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, n‑daa ti piili n‑katti ban daa daari ka koosiri bɛni maa. U daa labgi n‑labgi ninvugseeba ban daa takkri ligri teebulidima, ni ŋmankoosiriba kugsi,

english_verse: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
translation_verse: ka u daa bu sakki ni ba ziiri ziya ni neema n‑dɔli Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa n‑gatta.

english_verse: Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins. A Question about Jesus' Authority (Matthew
translation_verse: Ya mun yi zaani m‑puusiri Naawunni, see ka ya teaai ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tum tumbeoo zaa n‑ti ya la yɛla, ka kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u; ka ti Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni gba nin kyɛ ya mammaŋŋa tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ya.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba maa daa laa m‑paai Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni na. Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑gyinni bɛni, ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni tiŋŋa maa karimbadima ni kpamma maa daa kyɛ u saani na

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “N nin daŋŋi m‑boosi ya gba boosigu yinni kɔkɔ pɔi; ya yi ti labsi ma ka n nin naayi labsi n‑ti ya yan boosi seem la.

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ŋɔni n daa ti Gyɔni soori ni u suuri niriba Naawunni koom la? Di daa yi la Naawunni saani bee ninsaalidima saani? - Ya labsim n‑yɛli ma!”

english_verse: They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa piili n‑ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑tiri taaba, ni, “Ti ya yɛli la wula? Ti yi yɛli, ni, Naawunni saani, u nin boosi ti, ni, bɔzugu ka ti daa bu niŋi Gyɔni yɛdda?;

english_verse: and told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.”
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa labsi n‑ti Yisa, ni, “Ti bu mi.” Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ka dinŋɔna man gba ku yɛli ya ŋɔn ti ma soori ni n tumni anŋɔ zaa.”

english_verse: It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.”
translation_verse: ka wunsɔ laa n‑ka ni m‑paasi yaasa na'ala u kɔkɔa. Ni, i yi zaŋŋi i sufu zaa n‑wuri u, ni i teesa, ni i yaa zaa, ka laa n‑wuri i taraana hoo i mammaŋŋa - lala mari anfaani n‑gaari sara maaligu zaa, bee, i kuuri bunkɔbri n‑tiri Naawunni.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka wula ka puu maa daana nin niŋi? U bu ya n‑kyɛ na nti ku tuntumniba maa zaa? Ka u nin zaŋŋi puu maa n‑ti tuntunseeba.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: ni, “Ti Karimba, Musa daa sɔbi n‑ti ti n‑wulisi, ni, dɔsɔ beeri yi ti kpi ka kyɛ u pɔ'a, ka u na bu dɔ'ai biiya n‑ti u, see ka u di u beeri pukoori maa n‑dɔ'ai biisi n‑ti u beeri maa.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, he sent a servant to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka tiisi maa wala saha ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa tum u daabsɔ ni u kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani, nti deaai puu maa diibu na.

english_verse: The renters grabbed the servant, beat him up, and sent him away without a thing.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa gbaai daabri maa m‑bu u ka kari u n‑labsi yɔri na.

english_verse: Then the man sent another servant, and they killed him. He kept sending servant after servant. They beat some of them and killed some.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑tum sɔ, amaa ba daa ku ŋɔna. U daa tum niriba pam ka ba daa bu baseeba, ka ku seeba.

english_verse: The owner had a son he loved very much. Finally, he sent his son to the renters because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Ninvugyinni n daa kpalim ni u tum, u toori biiya ŋɔn daa wuri sɔ pam la. Ŋɔna ka u daa naayi tum, dama u daa teesi, ni, ‘Ba nin ti m maŋtoori bii maa gyirima.’

english_verse: But they said to themselves, “Someday he will own this vineyard. Let's kill him! That way we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Amaa tuntumniba maa daa yɛli n‑ti taaba, ni, ‘Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u ka faari maa nin kpalim ti dinni.’

english_verse: So they grabbed the owner's son, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Ka lala zugu ba daa gbaai u n‑ku n‑zaŋŋi u n‑lɔbi puu maa kpaŋŋa.

english_verse: This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’ ”
translation_verse: Ti Dugma tuma maa nla, Ka di nyɛ la alahazibu n‑ti ti.’ - ya bu baŋŋi lala sɔppu maa?”

english_verse: The leaders knew that Jesus was really talking about them, and they wanted to arrest him. But because they were afraid of the crowd, they let him alone and left. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Di saha ka kpamma maa daa boori la soori ni ba gbaai u, ban daa mi, ni, nyɛsiri maa galimni la ban zugu; amaa ba daa zɔri la daadam maa dabeem, dizugu ka ba daa kyɛ u ka bakki.

english_verse: You surely know about people being raised to life. You know that in the story about Moses and the burning bush, God said, “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.”
translation_verse: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kum yisigri la yɛla, ya bu karim Musa bukku la? Ŋɔn daa sɔbi bugum ni tutuuri yɛla Suuriseaa ni ka u daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni daa yɛli, ‘Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba.’

english_verse: The Pharisees got together with Herod's followers. Then they sent some men to trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa tum Farisiseeba ni NaaHɛroodi niriba ni ba ti kyɛ Yisa saani na n‑gbaai u u yɛttɔ'a puuni.

english_verse: They went to him and said, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are. And you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. Tell us, should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Ban daa ti paai na maa, ba daa yɛli u, “Ti Karimba, ti mi halli, ni, i nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu; i ba'a ka niriba yɛla ni, ka bu zɔri ninsaala nini, amaa ka kur wulisiri Naawunni soori ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: They brought him a silver coin, and he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi la'afu n‑ti u na, ka u yi boosi ba, ni, “Ŋɔni fɔtoo m bɛ la'afu maa ni wa, ni ŋɔni yuuri n sɔbi m‑bɛ ka ni?” Ka ba yɛli, “NaaTitaari dinni.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.” The men were amazed at Jesus. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa laa n‑labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Dizugu din nyɛ Naaba dinni yan zaŋŋma n‑ti Naaba maa, ka zaŋŋi din nyɛ Naawunni dinni n‑ti Naawunni.” Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam ŋɔn yɛli seem maa.

english_verse: He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living. You Sadducees are all wrong. The Most Important Commandment (Matthew
translation_verse: U siri pa la ninvugkpiimma Naawunni amaa ninvugvuya Naawunni. Ya burigi pam di zugu!”

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and pray long prayers just to show off. They will be punished most of all. A Widow's Offering (Luke
translation_verse: Ba faari la pukɔya yiya pɔi, ka naayi wulisiri ba maŋŋa Naawunni puusigu maa ni, dama ba yuui puusigu ni. Lala niriba maa nin ti nya galiŋŋu pam.”

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa. Ba daa yɛli u,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Lala n nyɛ di! Ya burigi soori ya ziilinsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Sɔppu maa zugu, ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn mari yiiko seem la gba.

english_verse: When Jesus saw that the man had given a sensible answer, he told him, “You are not far from God's kingdom.” After this, no one dared ask Jesus any more questions. About David's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ka doo maa labsigu maa nyɛ labsisuŋŋu, u daa yɛli u, ni, “I bu wɔ'a ni Naawunni naam maa ni.” Di nyaaŋŋa vi daa gbaai ba, ka sɔ zaa daa bu laa m‑boosi u boosiseaa yaasa.

english_verse: One of the teachers of the Law of Moses came up while Jesus and the Sadducees were arguing. When he heard Jesus give a good answer, he asked him, “What is the most important commandment?”
translation_verse: Ka karimbasɔ daa kyɛ na nti wum ba nangbankpeemni ŋmeebu maa. Ŋɔn daa baŋŋi, ni, Yisa daa labsi ba suŋŋa maa, u daa boosi u, ni, “Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaali zaaligudima maa zaa puuni, kani n lee n‑daŋŋi m‑mugsi ka kpa n‑gaari zaa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The most important one says: ‘People of Israel, you have only one Lord and God.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi u, ni, “Kan mugsi ka kpa pam nyɛ la, ni, ‘Ya Izraɛli dima, ya wumma! - Ti Dugma Naawunni nyɛ la Naawunni yinni;

english_verse: You must love him with all your heart, soul, mind, and strength.’

translation_verse: Ka na'akyimbilisɔ n daa bɛ bɛni, u mun daa gɔbi kyinkyinni kɔkɔ n‑dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa.

translation_verse: Ka ba daa gbaai bii wa, amaa u daa zɔ m‑bakki yɔri ka u kyinkyinni maa kpalim ba nuu ni.

english_verse: This time Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't even know the man you're talking about!”

english_verse: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
translation_verse: Ni di zaa ba sɛhara daa bu dɔli taaba deede.

english_verse: But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa sina ka daa bu labsi n‑yɛli u seelli maa, Toondaana maa daa laa m‑boosi u n‑yɛli, “Nyini n nyɛ Masiiya ŋɔn ya fa ti m‑basi ka di naam la? Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni bii la?”

english_verse: You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?” They all agreed he should be put to death.
translation_verse: Ya bu wum ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseesi an kyisiri wa? Ya teesi la wula?” Ka ba zaa daa yi Yisa nyaaŋŋa, ni, u niŋi taalli - taalli kan sumni la kuubu.

english_verse: While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up
translation_verse: Tɔ; di saha ka Piita daa na kpalim m‑bɛ tiŋni dundɔŋŋu maa puuni. Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana yiri maa pɔ'asarisɔ daa kpɛ na

translation_verse: Kyɛ ka ti nya Masiiya maa, Izraɛli Naa maa, ka u yi dɔpulli maa zugu n‑sigi na ka ti niŋi u yɛdda!” Ka niriba ayi seeba ban daa kpaasi n‑sa Yisa ya'a ayi maa gba daa tuuri u.

translation_verse: Lala zugu man gba daa teesi, ni, di sumni ni n sɔbi yɛla maa deede n‑ti i. Man pun dɔli bunseelli kam zaasa balabala halli nti kyɛ ka i baŋŋi din siri nyɛ yɛlimaŋni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛliseesi ban daa wulisi i la zaa.

english_verse: Both of them were good people and pleased the Lord God by obeying all that he had commanded.

english_verse: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
translation_verse: Ka Zakariiya ŋɔn daa nya malaaka maa, u daa yirigi ni, ka dabeem gbaai u.

english_verse: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa yɛli u, “Zakariiya, di zɔri dabeem, dama Naawunni daa pun wum i susigu, ka i pɔ'a Ilizabɛti nin ti dɔ'ai bidibga n‑ti i, ka i nin ti puri u yuuri Gyɔni.

english_verse: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
translation_verse: I nin ti mari sufupeelli ni sufumaasim pam i bii wa zugu, ka niriba pam gba nin ti mari sufupeelli u dɔ'am maa zugu.

english_verse: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
translation_verse: Dama u nin ti niŋi ninvugtitaari ti Dugma Naawunni soori ni. Ka u ku ti nyu dazeoo bee dapeelli, ka Naawunni Vuusim Sii Kasi nin ti kyɛnni u zugu ni na halli u dɔ'am saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna.

english_verse: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
translation_verse: nti zaŋŋi anabi Ilaygya sii maa ni u yiiko gba n‑daŋŋi Naawunni tooni na. U nin kyɛ ka yaseeba ban nyɛ yaadima la sufu ti labi m‑pɛligi ba ya'ansi zugu, ka kyɛ ka tuppuwumni dima ti takki n‑nya yɛm hoo ninvugseeba ban wumni Naawunni noori ŋɔn mari seem la, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka niriba maali siri n‑guri ti Dugma.”

english_verse: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
translation_verse: Ka Zakariiya daa yɛli malaaka maa, “N kurigiya ni, ka m pɔ'a gba kurigi. Ka wula ka n nin toaai n‑niŋi dinŋɔ yɛdda?”

english_verse: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
translation_verse: ka dizugu i ya leebi la zɔligu zuna wa, ka ku laa n‑tɔ'asi halli ka di saha maa ti paai.”

english_verse: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
translation_verse: Ka niriba maa daa guusiri Zakariiya, ka di ti gyɛri ba ŋɔn yuui Naawunni Duu maa ni zugu.

english_verse: When he did come out, he could not speak, and they knew he had seen a vision. He motioned to them with his hands, but did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti yi na, u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ba. Ka ba baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔn bɛ Duu maa ni maa, u nya la zii. Ka u yi mari la u nuusi n‑tɔ'asiri m‑bugsiri ba, ka yi kpalim n‑kɔŋŋi yɛttɔ'a.

english_verse: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi dabsiseesi ka u maaligu tuma dabsa daa ti naai, ka u daa kuli u yiri.

english_verse: Soon after this, his wife was expecting a baby, and for five months she did not leave the house. She said to herself,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka u pɔ'a Ilizabɛti daa gbaai puuwa, n‑sɔ'ari u maŋŋa niriba saani halli nti paai u gɔya anu daari,

english_verse: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
translation_verse: n‑yɛtti, ni, “Pampaŋŋɔ ti Dugma yi teaai n yɛla, ka niŋi ma nŋɔa, ni u yiisi ma vi ni niriba saani.”

english_verse: One month later God sent the angel Gabriel to the town of Nazareth in Galilee
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Ilizabɛti puu maa gɔya ayoobu saha, Naawunni daa tum u malaaka Gyeebriyɛli maa ka u kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni, ka ka yuuri boonni Nazarɛti la.

english_verse: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa ti paai Mariama saani na, nti yɛli u, ni, “M puusiri i ni, nyin pɔ'asɔ Naawunni ŋɔn suŋŋi pam wa! Ti Dugma Naawunni bɛ i saani.”

english_verse: Mary was confused by the angel's words and wondered what they meant.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa puusi u seem maa daa gyɛri u pam, ka u birisiri di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seela.

english_verse: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa yɛli u, “Mariama, di zɔri dabeem, ka i nya zugsuŋŋu Naawunni saani.

english_verse: and you will have a son. His name will be Jesus.
translation_verse: Wumma! I nin gbaai puuwa n‑dɔ'ai bidibga, ka i nin ti puri u yuuri Yisa.

english_verse: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
translation_verse: U nin ti mari tibsim, ka ba nin ti boonni u la Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa biiya. Ka ti Dugma Naawunni nin ti zaŋŋi naam n‑diisi u, u yaaba NaaDauda ŋɔn daa di naseebu la.

english_verse: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
translation_verse: Ka u nin ti di naam n‑su ya yaaba Yaakubu butiri, halli ni saha din ka tɔriga, ka u naam maa ku ti mari naari.”

english_verse: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
translation_verse: Ka Mariama daa yɛli malaaka maa, “N na zi n‑dooni ni doowa. Ka wula ka dinŋɔ nin toaai n‑niŋi?”

english_verse: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come down to you, and God's power will come over you. So your child will be called the holy Son of God.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Naawunni Sii Kasi Vuusim nin sigi i zugu na, m‑bɛ i ni, ka Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa wa yiiko nin ti bɛ i saani. Dizugu nyin ti ya dɔ'ai bikasi sɔ maa, ba nin ti boonni u la, ni, ‘Naawunni Biiya’.

english_verse: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
translation_verse: Baŋŋma i dɔ'ara Ilizabɛti yɛla: ammi, ba daa bu boonni u, ni, kundɔ'ara? Tɔ: ŋɔn mun mari bidibga puuwa, ni ŋɔn kurigi maa zaa yɔri, ka ka gɔya ayoobu nŋɔa.

english_verse: Nothing is impossible for God!”
translation_verse: Dama sɛseelli bu tɔ n‑nyaŋŋi Naawunni yiiko.”

english_verse: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
translation_verse: Ka Mariama daa yɛli u, “Ti Dugma n su ma. Kyɛ ka di niŋi ma hoo nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem maa.” Ka malaaka maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ u.

english_verse: A short time later Mary hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Mariama daa yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi yoomayooma n‑kyaŋŋi zɔya ni, nti paai tinseaa kan bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni la.

english_verse: She went into Zechariah's home, where she greeted Elizabeth.
translation_verse: M‑paai bɛni nti kpɛ Zakariiya yiri m‑puusi Ilizabɛti.

english_verse: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby moved within her. The Holy Spirit came upon Elizabeth.
translation_verse: Ilizabɛti ŋɔn daa wum Mariama puusigu maa, bii maa daa damni u puuri ni, ka Ilizabɛti paali ni Naawunni Sii Kasi.

english_verse: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
translation_verse: Ka u daa tasi n‑yɛli pampam, ni, “Nyini m mari zugsuŋŋu n‑gaari pɔ'aba zaa, ka bisɔ ŋɔn bɛ i puuri ni maa gba mari la zugsuŋŋu!

english_verse: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
translation_verse: M mun nyɛ la ŋɔni, halli ka n Dugma maa ma kyɛ n saani na wa?

english_verse: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
translation_verse: Man ŋɔn kur wum i puusigu maa, bisɔ ŋɔn bɛ m puuri ni wa damni ni sufupeelli zugu.

english_verse: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
translation_verse: I niŋi yɛdda, ni, ti Dugma ŋɔn tɔ'asi seelli n‑ti i la siri nin niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ka di zugu nyini n nyɛ sufumaasim daana.”

english_verse: Mary said: With all my heart I praise the Lord,
translation_verse: Ka Mariama yɛli#:- “N tibgri ti Dugma n sufu ni,

english_verse: and I am glad because of God my Savior.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn tuligi ma la zugu ka n sufu pɛligi pam.

english_verse: God cares for me, his humble servant. From now on, all people will say God has blessed me.
translation_verse: Dama u teaai u daabri tarintiri yɛla. Ka zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, Zamaana zaa nin ti boonni ma sufumaasim daana;

english_verse: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
translation_verse: Dama Yiiko Zaa Daana tum tunkara n‑ti ma, Ka u yuuri n nyɛ kasi.

english_verse: He always shows mercy to everyone who worships him.
translation_verse: Ka ban zɔri u la muna - Ban ka ŋɔn mun zɔri ba namboo saha kam.

english_verse: The Lord has used his powerful arm to scatter those who are proud.
translation_verse: U zaŋŋi u nuu ni yiiko n‑ŋmɛ karimbaani dima maa - Ba teesibeeri nin wurim m‑basi.

english_verse: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
translation_verse: U yiisi la nadima ba naama ni, Ka tibgri tarimma.

english_verse: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
translation_verse: U zaŋŋi bunsuma n‑diisi kɔm ŋɔn mari seeba maa, Ka kari bundaandima ka ba bakki ni nuzaala.

english_verse: God helps his servant Israel and is always merciful to his people.
translation_verse: U suŋŋi u daaba Izraɛli dima, Ka teaai ti yɛla n‑zɔ ti namboo;

english_verse: The Lord made this promise to our ancestors, to Abraham and his family forever!
translation_verse: Hoo ŋɔn daa yɛli ti yaadima seem la. U zɔ Ibrahim ni u nyaaŋŋa dima namboo Halli ni saha din ka naari.”

english_verse: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
translation_verse: Ka Mariama daa kpalim m‑bɛ Ilizabɛti saani n‑niŋi kaman gɔya ata, ka laa n‑labi n‑kuli u yiri.

english_verse: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
translation_verse: Ka u ziziitaaba ni u dɔ'ariba daa wum, ni, ti Dugma zɔ u namboo pam, ka ŋɔn ni ban zaa daa mari sufupeelli.

english_verse: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
translation_verse: Bii maa dabsa anii daari ka ba daa la'am na ni ba gunni u ka puri u yuuri. Ba daa ya zaŋŋi la u ba Zakariiya yuuri m‑puri u,

english_verse: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
translation_verse: ka u ma Ilizabɛti mun daa za'asi ka yɛli, ni, “U yuuri nin boonni Gyɔni.”

english_verse: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ay! - I dɔ'arisɔsɔ ka ni ka u yuuri boonni Gyɔni.”

english_verse: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
translation_verse: Ka daa mari ba nuusi m‑bugsiri m‑boosiri u ba, ni, u boori bii maa yuuri boonni la wula?

english_verse: Zechariah asked for a writing tablet. Then he wrote, “His name is John.” Everyone was amazed.
translation_verse: Ka u daa susi bunni n‑sɔbi, ni, “Bii maa yuuri nyɛ la Gyɔni.”

english_verse: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
translation_verse: Ka dabeem daa gbaai ba ziziitaaba maa zaa, ka niriba zaa daa kur diri taaba azama n‑tɔ'asiri yɛla wa Gyuudii zɔya tinsi zaasa ni.

english_verse: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa daa wum yɛla maa daa zaŋŋi a n‑niŋi u sufu ni ka teesiri, ni, “Bii wa lee n ti ya nyɛ la ninvugbɔa?” Dama Naawunni yaa n daa bɛ u ni.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit came upon Zechariah, and he began to speak:
translation_verse: Ka u ba Zakariiya daa paali ni Vuusim Kasi maa, ka tɔ'asi anabitiri ni, n‑yɛtta#:-

english_verse: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
translation_verse: “Ti pɛ'ari ti Dugma, Izraɛli Naawunni, Dama u suŋŋi ti n‑yiisi ti wahala ni.

english_verse: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
translation_verse: Ka u daa bɔ tuliginsim daana n‑ti ti na, Dauda za'anɔri biiya - Naawunni ŋɔn daa su NaaDauda maa.

english_verse: to save us from our enemies and from everyone who hates us.
translation_verse: Ni, u nin tuligi ti ti dindima ni, Ka yiisi ti ban yaai ti zaa nuu ni.

english_verse: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
translation_verse: M biiya, ba nin ti boonni i la Anabi - Anabi n‑ti Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa. Dama i nin ti daŋŋi ti Dugma Naawunni tooni M‑maali u sɔya n‑ti u.

english_verse: You will tell his people that they can be saved when their sins are forgiven.
translation_verse: I nin wulisi u niriba yɛm, Ni ba nin baŋŋi tuliginsim ŋɔn nyɛ seem: Ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba,

english_verse: God's love and kindness will shine upon us like the sun that rises in the sky.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni zɔ ti namboo pam. Ka u kyɛ ka neesim yi saazugu n‑kyɛ ti saani na,

english_verse: On us who live in the dark shadow of death this light will shine to guide us into a life of peace.
translation_verse: N‑neaai tirim ban bɛ zibsim ni, ni kum puuni, Ka zaŋŋi nangbanyinni ni sufudoaa soori N‑wulisi ti nɔba.”

english_verse: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka bii maa daa zoaai na, m‑mari sufukpeemni, m‑bɛ moo ni halli ni dahinseaa ŋɔn daa ti zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi n‑ti Izraɛli dima la.

english_verse: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Di daa niŋi lala, ka saha kanŋɔna ka Room NaaTitaari Agasitisi daa tisi noori ni ba ti sɔbri u suulinsi ni niriba zaa yuya.

english_verse: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
translation_verse: Tɔ; dɔsɔ n daa bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm, ka u yuuri boonni Simiyɔn, ka doo wa daa zɔri Naawunni ka dɔli u zaaligu suŋŋa: ka guusiri Izraɛli dima tuligra soori; ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi daa bɛ u ni.

english_verse: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
translation_verse: Ka sɔkam zaa daa kunni u toori tiŋŋa ni ba ti sɔbi u yuuri.

english_verse: So Joseph had to leave Nazareth in Galilee and go to Bethlehem in Judea. Long ago Bethlehem had been King David's hometown, and Joseph went there because he was from David's family.
translation_verse: Ka Gyoosɛfi gba daa yi Nazarɛti, Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni, nti sigi n‑kyaŋŋi Bɛtlihɛm, Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni. Ban daa dɔ'ai NaaDauda tinseaa nla; Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ la NaaDauda za'anɔri nira, dizugu ka u daa kyaŋŋi bɛni ni u ti sɔbi u yuuri.

english_verse: Mary was engaged to Joseph and traveled with him to Bethlehem. She was soon going to have a baby,
translation_verse: Ŋɔn ni Mariama n daa la'am n‑kyaŋŋi, dama Mariama daa pun basi nangbambasiri, ni, u kuli u. Di saha maa ka Mariama daa mari pukarigu.

english_verse: she gave birth to her first-born son. She dressed him in baby clothes and laid him on a bed of hay, because there was no room for them in the inn. The Shepherds
translation_verse: Ka u daa dɔ'ai u pɔ'asaridɔ'am bidibga, ka zaŋŋi tana n‑vili u, n‑dooli bunkɔbri diibu bunni ni, dama ba daa bu nya doaa ziiya saamma yiri maa ni.

english_verse: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka pɛguriba daa bɛ tiŋŋa maa moo ni yuŋŋu, n‑guri ba peesi.

english_verse: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yi ba saani na, ka ti Dugma gyirima neesim daa neaai n‑gyiligi ba, ka dabeem daa kpɛ ba pam.

english_verse: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa yɛli ba, “Ya di zɔri dabeem! Ka n zi la lahabasuŋŋu na ni n ti ya, ka ka mari sufupeelli pam, ka nyɛ sɔkam zaa dinni.

english_verse: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
translation_verse: Amaa dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi, ni, n yɛligu wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni: ya nin ti nya bileaa maa ka ba vili u tana n‑dooli bunkɔbri diibu bunni puuni.”

english_verse: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka malaaka bɔbgu daa yi arizana n‑kyɛ na, m‑paasi malaaka yinni maa zugu, ka ba yuumni m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta#:-

english_verse: “Praise God in heaven! Peace on earth to everyone who pleases God.”
translation_verse: “Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ saazugu mari gyirima pam, Ka daadam ban bɛ duniya wa ni nya sufudoaa ni nangbanyinni, Naawunni ŋɔn wuri ba zugu!”

english_verse: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
translation_verse: Malaakadima maa ŋɔn daa basi ba ka labi arizana ni maa, pɛguriba maa daa yɛtti la taaba, ni, “Dinŋɔna, ti kyɛm Bɛtlihɛm nti nya bunseelli din niŋi maa, ti Dugma ŋɔn wulisi ti seelli la.”

english_verse: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi yooma, m‑paai m‑bɔ n‑nya Mariama ni Gyoosɛfi, ni bileaa maa, ka u dɔ bunkɔbri diibu bunni maa puuni.

english_verse: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
translation_verse: Ban daa nya lala maa, ba daa yɛli malaaka ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi bii maa yɛla seem la n‑ti ba.

english_verse: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
translation_verse: Mariama mun daa zaŋŋi yɛla maa zaa n‑niŋi u sufu ni, n‑kur teesiri a yɛla.

english_verse: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
translation_verse: Ka pɛguriba maa daa labi n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima ni pɛ'abu ban daa wum ka nya seelli maa zugu. Ka di siri daa nyɛ la hoo malaaka maa ŋɔn daa yɛli ba seem la.

english_verse: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka bii maa dabsa anii daari ŋɔn daa ti paai, ba daa gunni u ka puri u yuuri Yisa, dama ban daa na bu gbaai u puu saha seaa la, malaaka maa daa pun yɛli, ni, ba boolim u yuuri lala.

english_verse: The time came for Mary and Joseph to do what the Law of Moses says a mother is supposed to do after her baby is born. They took Jesus to the temple in Jerusalem and presented him to the Lord,
translation_verse: Tɔ. Di nyaaŋŋa ka saha daa ti paai ni Gyoosɛfi ni Mariama kyaŋŋi nti tum kaman Musa zaaligu ŋɔn yɛli seem, ni, ba maalim Mariama dɔ'am kasitiri maaligu. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi bii maa n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm, ni ba ti zaŋŋi u n‑ti ti Dugma.

english_verse: just as the Law of the Lord says, “Each first-born baby boy belongs to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Ba daa niŋi kaman din sɔbi n‑yɛli ti Dugma zaaligu ni seem, ni, ba zaŋŋma ba bikpamma, bidibsi la, n‑tisi ti Dugma.

english_verse: The Law of the Lord also says parents have to offer a sacrifice, giving at least a pair of doves or two young pigeons. So that is what Mary and Joseph did.
translation_verse: Ka daa maali maaligu maa kaman ti Dugma zaaligu ŋɔn yɛli seem la, ni, ba kɔrim ŋmanzeesi ayi bee ŋmambisi ayi m‑maali maaligu.

english_verse: and told him that he would not die until he had seen Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: Sii Kasi daa pun kasigi n‑yɛli u, ni, faasee ka u ti nya ti Dugma Masiiya pɔi ka naayi kpi.

english_verse: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni Sii maa daa kyɛ ka u kyaŋŋi Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Di saha maa ka Gyoosɛfi ni Mariama daa mari Yisa n‑kpeeri na ni ba ti niŋi u hoo zaaligu kaari ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa.

english_verse: Simeon took the baby Jesus in his arms and praised God,
translation_verse: Ka Simiyɔn daa deaai bii maa n‑zali, ka pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta#:-

english_verse: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
translation_verse: “Dizugu, n Dugma, zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, I nin kyɛ ka m bakki duniya wa ka kyaŋŋi ni sufudoaa; Hoo nyin ŋɔn daa basi nangbambasiri n‑ti ma seem la.

english_verse: and foreign nations will also see this.
translation_verse: nyin daa zaŋŋi sɔ n‑ti butiri kam zaa wa.

english_verse: Your mighty power is a light for all nations, and it will bring honor to your people Israel.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔna n nyɛ neesim din ya neaai tinzundima M‑bugsi ba i soori; Ka i niriba muna, Izraɛli dima - I nin taasi gyirima na n‑ti ba.”

english_verse: Then he blessed them and told Mary, “This child of yours will cause many people in Israel to fall and others to stand. The child will be like a warning sign. Many people will reject him,
translation_verse: Ka Simiyɔn daa susi Naawunni susisuma n‑niŋi ba zugu, ka yɛli u ma Mariama, ni, “Dinŋɔ ka Naawunni zaali, ni bii wa nin kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima pam ti sigsi ba maŋŋa ka laa n‑du yaasa. U nin ti laa n‑leebi ŋɔn ya wulisi niriba tuliginsim soori, ka baseeba nin za'asi u yɛla.

english_verse: and you, Mary, will suffer as though you had been stabbed by a dagger. But all this will show what people are really thinking.” Anna Speaks about the Child Jesus
translation_verse: Ka i gba sufu nin sa'am di zugu, kaman takoobi ŋɔn kusi nira seem la. Ka yɛlisɔ'ara nin ti yi niriba pam sufu ni na.”

english_verse: The prophet Anna was also there in the temple. She was the daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, and she was very old. In her youth she had been married for seven years, but her husband died.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'asɔ gba daa bɛ ni, n‑nyɛ anabi, ka u yuuri boonni Anna. Ka nyɛ Fanuwɛli biiya, Aseeri za'anɔri ni nira. U daa kurigi pam,

english_verse: At this time Anna came in and praised God. She spoke about the child Jesus to everyone who hoped for Jerusalem to be set free. The Return to Nazareth
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a wa daa kyɛ na di saha maa, m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni, ka tɔ'asiri bii maa yɛla n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban daa guusiri Gyɛrusalɛm tuliginsim dabsiri la zaa.

english_verse: After Joseph and Mary had done everything that the Law of the Lord commands, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban daa tum ti Dugma zaaligu ŋɔn nyɛ seem zaa n‑naai, ba daa labi n‑kuli Galilii, m‑paai ba toori tiŋŋa Nazarɛti.

english_verse: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
translation_verse: Ka bii maa daa zoaai na, m‑mari yɛm ni yaa, ka Naawunni wurilim bɛ u ni pam.

english_verse: Every year Jesus' parents went to Jerusalem for Passover.
translation_verse: Yuumni kam Yisa ba ni u ma daa yi kyɛnni la Gyɛrusalɛm nti diri Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu.

english_verse: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
translation_verse: Yisa daa kpalim la Gyɛrusalɛm. Ka u ba ni u ma bu mi, dama ba daa teesi, ni, u bɛ la daadam maa ni.

english_verse: They thought he was traveling with some other people, and they went a whole day before they started looking for him.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu daa kyɛ ka ba kyaŋŋi la dabsimunni kyɛnni; di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa naayi boori u ziiya ba dɔ'ariba ni ba zɔdima saani.

english_verse: When they could not find him with their relatives and friends, they went back to Jerusalem and started looking for him there.
translation_verse: N‑daa bɔ u n‑kɔŋŋi bɛni, ka labi Gyɛrusalɛm nti boori u ziiya.

english_verse: Three days later they found Jesus sitting in the temple, listening to the teachers and asking them questions.
translation_verse: Di dabsa ata daari ka ba daa nya u Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni, ka u zi karimbadima saani, n‑wumsiri ba yɛttɔ'a, ka boosiri ba boosa.

english_verse: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa daa wum u yɛttɔ'a maa, di daa niŋi ba la alahazibu u yɛm gyɛrigri zugu.

english_verse: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
translation_verse: Ban daa nya u maa di daa gyɛri ba ni, ka u ma boosi u, ni, “M biiya, wula ka i niŋi ti seem wa? Man ni i ba yi boori la i ziiya ni sufusa'aŋŋu.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Bɔ n niŋi ka ya boori n ziiya? Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, di kpa la talasi ni m bɛ m Ba tuma maa ni?”

english_verse: But they did not understand what he meant.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa bu mi ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa gbinni.

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
translation_verse: Ka u daa dɔli ba n‑labi n‑kuli Nazarɛti, ka deaari ba noori. Ka u ma daa zaŋŋi yɛla maa zaa n‑niŋi u sufu ni.

english_verse: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa bamsim ni u yɛm daa paasira, ka u mari zugu pam Naawunni ni ninsaalidima saani.

english_verse: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka lampodeaariba gba daa kyɛ Gyɔni saanni na, ni u su ba Naawunni koom maa. Ba daa boosi u, ni, “Ti Karimba, ka wula ka tirima ya niŋi?”

english_verse: Do something to show that you really have given up your sins. Don't start saying you belong to Abraham's family. God can turn these stones into children for Abraham.
translation_verse: See ka ya tum tunseesi an wulisi, ni, ya pun takki ya halibeoo ni ya teesibeeri zaa, kaman tisuŋŋu ŋɔn wanni walisuma seem la. Yan di yɛtti taaba, ni ‘Ibrahim ya'ansi n nyɛ ti!’; ya bu baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi halli kuga wa gba m‑maali ya'ansi n‑ti Ibrahim?

english_verse: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka NaaHɛroodi daa pun fa u maŋtoori beeri pɔ'a la - pɔ'a maa mun yuuri daa boonni Hɛroodiyasi - ka anŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ka Gyɔni daa galim Naaba maa dizugu, ni, Naaba galimiya pam, m‑paasi ŋɔn daa pun tum tumbɛseesi la zugu.

english_verse: Annas and Caiaphas were the Jewish high priests. At that time God spoke to Zechariah's son John, who was living in the desert.
translation_verse: Saha kanŋɔ yaasa ka Annasi ni Kayafasi daa di Izraɛli dima gyeeŋŋu suulinsi. Di daa nyɛ la saha kanŋɔ ka Naawunni daa tɔ'asi n‑ti Gyɔni, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Zakariiya bii la, yoo ni sa.

english_verse: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
translation_verse: Di zaa daa nyɛ hoo Anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn pun sɔbi u bukku maa puuni n‑yɛli seem la. U daa yɛnni, ni#:- “Ya wumma! Sɔ m bɛ yoo ni n‑tasira, Ni, ‘Ya maalim ti Dugma palli n‑ti u, Ya sɛ'asim u sɔya zaa ka a tuusi kyappi;

english_verse: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
translation_verse: Baa kam zaa nin ti nyɛri, Ka zɔya ni kunkuna zaa nin birigi n‑sigi; An gɔlim zaa nin teaai, Ka sɔgbiragbira la mun leebi bɔlibɔli;

english_verse: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
translation_verse: Ka ninsaala zaa nin ti nya M‑baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn nin tuligi u niriba seem la.’ ”

english_verse: Crowds of people came out to be baptized, but John said to them, “You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka daadam daa la'am Gyɔni saanni na pam, ni u su ba Naawunni koom. Ka u daa yɛli zama maa, ni “Ya bunkyɔ'ama wa! Ya kur ŋmani la hoo wa'abeeri! Ŋɔni n wulisi ya Naawunni ŋɔn ya namsi ya seem ka ya yɛnni, ni, ya nin zɔ n‑yi namsigu maa ni?

english_verse: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
translation_verse: Soogyadima gba daa boosi u m‑paasi, ni, “Ka tirima ŋɔna?” Ka u daa yɛli ba, “Ya di mugsiri sɔ n‑deaari ligri bee n‑galimni sɔkam yɔriyɔri; ka ya kyɛ ka ya nini tigi ya yoori ŋɔn n‑nyɛ seem maa.”

english_verse: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
translation_verse: Amaa Gyɔni daa tasi n‑yɛli ba zaa, ni, “Koom ka mani zaŋŋi n‑suuri ya wa, amaa sɔ kyɛnni na ŋɔn nin zaŋŋi Sii Kasi ni bugum n‑suuri ya. Ŋɔna n kpeem n‑gaari ma halli ka mani bu sum ni m pirigi u namna n‑ti u.

english_verse: In many different ways John preached the good news to the people.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Gyɔni daa kpamsiri niriba lala, ka paasiri ba yɛligu pam zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: Finally, Herod put John in jail, and this was the worst thing he had done. The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Dinzugu NaaHɛroodi daa laa n‑tum tumbɛseaa kan gaari ŋɔn pun tum tunseesi la zaa ka kyɛ ka ba gbaai Gyɔni n‑kpari dansarika ni.

english_verse: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa yi na n‑sigi u saanni na: Sii maa mun daa ŋmani la hoo naŋmanni. Ka ba daa wum Naawunni kukɔri saazugu na ka yɛnni, ni, “M bii n nyɛ i, ka n wuri i pam; n nini n tigi i.”

english_verse: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
translation_verse: Kyeenan Inɔsi biiya, Inɔsi Sɛtti biiya, Sɛtti Adam biiya; ŋɔn Adam muna, Naawunni bii n daa nyɛ u.

english_verse: The Scriptures say: ‘God will tell his angels to take care of you. They will catch you in their arms, and you will not even hurt your feet on the stones.’ ”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑sɔbi, ni, ‘I yi ya lu, ba nin teebi i saazugu ba nuusi ni ka i napɔŋŋu ku tuui kugri.’ ”

english_verse: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti lura, ka niriba pam daa zi ba baaridima n‑kyɛ u saani na, ka u zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa sɔkam zaa zugu, ka tibi ba.

english_verse: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Ka Sintaani daa yɛli u, ni, “I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku kyɛ ka kugri wa leebi bundirigu?”

english_verse: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sintaani daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi n‑duusi saazugu n‑wulisi u duniya puuni tinsi zaasa yooma.

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
translation_verse: Ka Sintaani daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni ka zaŋŋi u n‑du n‑tɔm Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri saazugu sa, ka Sintaani yɛli, “I yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, i ku zaŋŋi i maŋŋa m‑basi tiŋŋa?

english_verse: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: U daa kpeeri ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑wulisiri ba, ka ba zaa daa tiri u gyirima u yɛttɔ'a zugu.

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa paai Nazarɛti - ban daa wubsi u tinseaa la nla - ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari daa ti paai, ka u daa kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu duu ni hoo u soori ŋɔn daa kur nyɛ seem.

english_verse: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
translation_verse: “Ti Dugma Sii maa bɛ man saani, Dama Ŋɔna n yiisi ma ni m mooli N‑wulisi u lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti faradima. Ka laa n‑tum ma ban gbaasi seeba saani Ni n ti yɛli ba, ni, ban nin yiisi ba m‑basi. Ni n kyɛm zoomma gba saani N‑yɛli ba, ni, ba nini nin neaai. Ni, ban mugsiri seeba yaasa nin nya soori Ni ba ti su ba mansi.

english_verse: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
translation_verse: Lala maa n ya wulisi, Ni, ti Dugma sahasuŋŋu maa paaya.”

english_verse: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn karim lala n‑naai maa, u daa labsi n‑kpabi bukku maa ka zaŋŋi ka n‑tisi ka laa n‑zini. Ka ban daa zi duu maa ni maa zaa kur bɛ la u gɔsibu ni.

english_verse: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
translation_verse: Ka sɔkam zaa daa pɛ'ari u, ka ŋɔn zaŋŋi yɛm ni nya'asim n‑tɔ'asiri u yɛttɔ'a maa seem n daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam. Baseeba boosiri taaba, ni, “Pa Gyoosɛfi bii n‑kur ŋɔa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Man kur mi halli, ni, ya nin teaai ma nyɛsiseaa yɛla - kan yɛtti, ni, ‘Nyin tiimaraana wa, kur tibma i maŋŋa!’, ni, ‘Tin daa wum ka i tum seem Kapirinyum tiŋŋa ni la, see ka i tum lala i mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa wa puuni!’ ”

english_verse: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, anabi zaa maŋtoori timbiisi n nyɛ ban bu sakki n‑deaari u.

english_verse: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
translation_verse: Amaa yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, Izraɛli pukɔya daa zoaai pam Anabi Ilaygya saha la, wari ŋɔn daa zaani yuuma ata ni gɔya ayoobu ka kɔm lu.

english_verse: but Elijah was sent only to a widow in the town of Zarephath near the city of Sidon.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni daa bu tum Ilaygya ni u kyaŋŋi pukɔya wa sɔsɔ saani, amaa pukɔsɔ ŋɔn daa bɛ Zarifaati tiŋŋa saani ka u daa tum u - tiŋŋa wa n kur bɛ Saidoon tingbaŋŋu ni.

english_verse: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
translation_verse: Yaasa m‑paasi: Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ka kɔnsi daa zoaai pam Anabi Ilayseaa saha la, amaa ba sɔsɔ daa bu nya tippu, amaa Na'aman sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Siriiya nira la ka ba daa tibi.”

english_verse: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
translation_verse: amaa u daa dɔli ba sunsukkuni n‑yi n‑kyaŋŋi ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: Jesus went to the town of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi n‑sigi m‑paai Kapirinyum - Galilii tingbaŋŋu tinseaa n nyɛ ka. U daa paai m‑bɛ ni, ka daa la'amni ni Kapirinyum dima n‑wulisiri ba, Vuusim Dabsiri kam zaa.

english_verse: His teaching amazed them because he spoke with power.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'amaŋa la zugu, ka u bu birisiri halli beela.

english_verse: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
translation_verse: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni ka arizimbeoo daa kur bɛ u ni. Ka u daa kaasi pampam, ni,

english_verse: Jesus ordered the evil spirit to be quiet and come out. The demon threw the man to the ground in front of everyone and left without harming him.
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa nyikki u ka yɛli, “Sina i vuuri ka yi na ka kyɛ u!” Ka arizimbeoo maa daa tɔ doo maa n‑lusi u tiŋŋa, ba zaa sunsukkuni, ka yi na ka kyɛ u, ka sɛseelli bu niŋi doo maa.

english_verse: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
translation_verse: Ka di daa yi gyɛri ba zaasa ka ba yi yɛtti taaba, ni, “Ka yɛligu bɔ nŋɔa? U kur zaŋŋi yaa n‑tɔ'asiri u yɛttɔ'amaŋni n‑yɛtti arizimbeeri, ni, ba yim na, ka ba siri yiiri na, fimm!”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa daa mooli n‑gyili bɛni tinsi maa ni, ni a tinkpansi zaa.

english_verse: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yi la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑kpɛ Saimoon yiri. Di saha maa Saimoon deemma ka waari daa mara, ka u dɔya n‑gyigra, ka ba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti Yisa m‑boosi u zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi pɔ'a maa yɛla.

english_verse: So Jesus went over to her and ordered the fever to go away. Right then she was able to get up and serve them a meal.
translation_verse: Ka u daa kpɛ n‑zaani u saani ka tasi dooru maa ka di yi ka kyɛ pɔ'a maa ka u daa doaai yooma n‑yi na n‑tum n‑ti ba.

english_verse: Demons went out of many people and shouted, “You are the Son of God!” But Jesus ordered the demons not to speak because they knew he was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Ka arizimbeeri daa yi na ka kyɛ niriba pam, ka kur tasiri n‑yɛtti, ni, “Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni bii la!”, amaa u daa nyikki ba ni, ka bu sakki ni ba tɔ'asiri lala, dama ba daa baŋŋiya, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya, Naawunni ŋɔn tum sɔ la.

english_verse: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, u daa yi timpuuni n‑kpɛ moo ni. Ka ba zaa daa bɔ m‑bɔ nti nya u, ka susiri u n‑yɛnni, ni, u di laa n‑kyaŋŋi seela ka kyɛ ba;

english_verse: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
translation_verse: amaa u daa yɛli ba, “Di nyɛ la talasi ni m mooli Naawunni naam lahabasuŋŋu n‑gyili, ka tinseesi an kpalim la zaa gba wum: dizugu ka Naawunni tum ma na.”

english_verse: So he kept on preaching in the synagogues in Judea.
translation_verse: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni nti kpɛ ba la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑yiisiri wa'azu.

english_verse: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa mun daa yisigri n‑kyɛnni moo ni, ni u ti bɛ u kɔkɔ m‑puusiri ka susiri Naawunni.

english_verse: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ban niŋi u yɛdda seem maa zugu, u daa yɛli baara maa dizugu, ni, “N zɔsuŋŋu, n kyɛ i taalli m‑pɛŋŋi i.”

english_verse: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kpɛ nyarinseaa ni, nyariŋŋu maa mun daa nyɛ la Saimoon dinni, ka dinŋɔna u daa bɛlim Saimoon, ni, u tugsim ka n‑kpɛ koom maa ni n‑zaali tooni beela. Ka Yisa daa zini nyariŋŋu maa puuni n‑wulisiri niriba maa.

english_verse: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon labsi n‑yɛli u, “N Daana, ti sa pun lɔbi a yuŋŋu la n‑neaai beoo, ka mun bu gbaai halli yinni gba; amaa nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem wa zugu, n nin lɔbi.”

english_verse: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa siri niŋi lala ba daa gbaai zaasim pam, halli ka ba laama maa ti boori a kyeesi.

english_verse: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa booli ba zingbaaritaaba ni ba zaŋŋma nyarinseaa la n‑kyɛ na n‑suŋŋi ba. Ka ba daa kyɛ na nti suŋŋi ba ka yiisi zaasim maa m‑paali nyarima ayi maa zaa, halli ka a ti ya sim koom maa ni.

english_verse: His partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were surprised too. Jesus told Simon, “Don't be afraid! From now on you will bring in people instead of fish.”
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi Zɛbidii biisi Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni ban daa nyɛ Saimoon tuntuntaaba la gba alahazibu. Ka Yisa daa yɛli Saimoon “Di zɔri dabeem! I yi gbaari la zaasim halli ni zuna; amaa zuna zaŋŋi kyaŋŋi i ya leebi la ŋɔn gbaari ninsaalidima n‑tiri Naawunni.”

english_verse: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Ban daa ti zaŋŋi ba nyarima maa na nti dooli duuri ni, ba daa kyɛ ba bunseelli kam zaa m‑basi ka yisigi n‑dɔli Yisa.

english_verse: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa yuuri daa maali n‑du pam n‑kyaŋŋi halli, ka daadam pam daa la'am na ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka u tibri ba dooru balibu kam la.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they went up on the roof, where they removed some tiles and let the mat down in the middle of the room.
translation_verse: ka kɔŋŋi, daadam maa ŋɔn daa zoaai m‑mugsiri u maa zugu. Dinŋɔ zugu ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑du la yiri maa pii saazugu nti kyiibi voori n‑yoaai n‑sigsi u, ŋɔn ni u sɔŋŋu maa zaa, voori maa ni, halli nti paai tiŋŋa; ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑dooli Yisa tooni lala.

english_verse: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
translation_verse: Yɛla ayi wa sunsukkuni, kani m bu tɔa - i yi yɛli sɔ, ni, ba kyɛ u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, bee i yi yɛli sɔ, ni, u doaama n‑kyaŋŋi?”

english_verse: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, “Amaa m boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni ba, duniya wa puuni.” Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa, “N ya n‑yɛli i, ni, doaama n‑zaŋŋi i bundoaa wa n‑kuli!”

english_verse: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa siri doaai n‑zaŋŋi u bundoaa maa ba zaa nini ni m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni ka va n‑kuli.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka di daa gyɛri ba zaa pam; ka ba pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtta, “Ti siri nya alahazibu tuma zuna!”

english_verse: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa yi nti nya lampodeaarisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Liivai. Ŋɔnŋɔ mun daa zi ba lampoo tuma ziiya n‑tumna, ka Yisa yɛli u, “Doaama n‑dɔli ma!”

english_verse: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka u daa doaai n‑kyɛ u bunseelli kam zaa ka dɔli u.

english_verse: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn Liivai daa dugi diibu u yiri maa ni, ka daa booli Yisa n‑zuusi u ni u kyɛm na n‑di. Ka lampodeaariba ni daadam pam daa paasi u zugu n‑la'am na nti dira.

english_verse: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka Farisii dima ni karimbadima ban daa bɛ bɛni maa daa galim Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ka boosiri ba, ni, “Wula ka yarima ni lampodeaariba ni tumbeeri dima la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ban mari alaafɛ bu boori tiimaraana, na'ala ban beeri m‑boori u.

english_verse: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
translation_verse: Pa tunsuma dima zugu ka n kyɛ na, ni m boonni, amaa tumbeeri dima ka m boonni ni ba kyɛm na n‑takki ba beebu, ni ba sufu ni teesibeeri zaa.”

english_verse: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
translation_verse: Ba mun daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Seeba ban dɔli Gyɔni maa loori la noori sahakam ka puusiri aduuwa, ka Farisii dima nyaandɔliba gba n niŋni lala, amaa nyini mun nyaandɔliba wa n kur diri ni ka nyuura.”

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.”
translation_verse: Amaa sahanseaa kyɛnni na ka ba nin ti yiisi amriya doo maa ba saani - di dabsa maa puuni ka ba nin lɔ noori.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa m‑paasi ba nyɛsiri n‑yɛli, “Sɔ na zi n‑toaai bunyeeripaalli tanni n‑ligi bunyeerikurigu. U yi niŋi lala u nin sa'am bumpaalli maa, ka tampaalli maa ku ŋmani bunkurigu la.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
translation_verse: Sɔ mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akurigu ni. U yi niŋi lala, daam maa nin ŋma ga'akurigu maa ka yuuli m‑basi, ka ga'ari maa gba sa'am.

english_verse: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
translation_verse: Ay! Ba zaŋŋma ga'apaala n‑leeri dapaalli n‑niŋi a puuni.

english_verse: No one wants new wine after drinking old wine. They say, “The old wine is better.”
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyu daam m‑miili mun bu boori dapaalli; amaa u yɛli, ni, dakurigu kɔkɔ n nya'asa u saani.”

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa mi ba sufu ni teesa ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka u daa yɛli nukpiiŋŋu daana maa, ni, “Doaama n‑zaani sunsukkuni!”, ka u doaai n‑zaani.

english_verse: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you picking grain on the Sabbath? You're not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Ka Farisiseeba n daa boosi ba, ni, “Wula ka ya tumni puri tuma wa? Ya galimni zaaligu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri wa!”

english_verse: Long ago your own people did these same things to the prophets. So when this happens to you, be happy and jump for joy! You will have a great reward in heaven.
translation_verse: Di nin wulisi, ni, ya laara zoaaya pam arizana ni; ka dizugu ya kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi dindaari maa, ka ya waam sufumaasim waa. Ya bu nya? - lala ka ba badima n daa niŋi anabidima la.”

english_verse: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
translation_verse: Ya susim bunsuma n‑niŋi ban yɛtti ya zulima, ka laa n‑susi Naawunni n‑tiri ban tuuri ya gba.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “On the Sabbath should we do good deeds or evil deeds? Should we save someone's life or destroy it?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, “M boosiri ya ni: Vuusim Dabsiri muna, soori bɛ ni ni nira tum tunsuma di dabsiri maa, bee tumbeeri? M‑paasi yaasa, u tuligma sɔ nyɔvuri, bee u sa'am ka?”

english_verse: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
translation_verse: Ba yuya nŋɔa#:- Saimoon , ni u tinzɔ Anduru; Gyeemsi, Gyɔni, Filipi, Baatɔlɔmiyu,

english_verse: On another Sabbath Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, and a man with a paralyzed right hand was there.
translation_verse: Ka Vuusim dahinseaa yaasa ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu ni n‑wulisiri ba; ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka u nudirigu daa pun kpi ni.

english_verse: The teachers and the Pharisees were furious and started saying to one another, “What can we do about Jesus?” Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa ba sufu daa yisigi pam ka ba tɔ'asiri ni taaba ba nin niŋi Yisa seem.

english_verse: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
translation_verse: Beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, u daa booli u nyaandɔliba zaa ni ba la'am na, ka u daa gaŋŋi ba puuni ninvugpiiya ni bayi, ka daa laa m‑booli ba, ni, ‘Tumudima’ la.

english_verse: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
translation_verse: ka niriba maa zaa daa kpaŋŋi ba mansi ni ba paai Yisa saani n‑siisi u, dama yiiko n daa yiiri u ni na n‑tibri ba zaa.

english_verse: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban diri ya yuubu pampaŋŋɔa, dama kɔm nin ti gbaai ya. Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban laari pampaŋŋɔa, dama ya nin ti kaasiri ka kumna.

english_verse: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba kɔm ŋɔn mari pampaŋŋɔa, dama ya nin ti di n‑tigi. Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti yaseeba ban kumni pampaŋŋɔa, dama ya nin ti laara.

english_verse: God will bless you when others hate you and won't have anything to do with you. God will bless you when people insult you and say cruel things about you, all because you are a follower of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ya, sahanseaa niriba yi yaai ya, ka kari ya m‑basi, ka tuuri ya ka za'asiri ya yuya, ni, a nyɛ la yubeeri, ka ba niŋi lala man Ninsaala Bii zugu.

english_verse: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ ni n‑ti ya, sahanseaa niriba zaasa yi tiri ya yusuma; ba badima daa pun niŋi lala n‑ti ziri anabidima la - Bee, m ma'ai ni?”

english_verse: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
translation_verse: Ya zaŋŋma piini n‑ti niriba, ka Naawunni ti ti ya gba, ka yan ti ya deaai seelli maa nin ti paali ya nuusi halli nti yaamna; dama yan ma'asiri n‑tiri ya taaba seem la, lala ka Naawunni nin ti ma'asi n‑ti ya gba.”

english_verse: is like someone who dug down deep and built a house on solid rock. When a flood came and the river rushed against the house, it was built so well that it didn't even shake.
translation_verse: u ŋmani la hoo tammeerisɔ ŋɔn daa gbi tiŋŋa n‑nyeebi u yiri n‑tɔm tampii zugu; ka koom daa ti kpɛ n‑gyiligi yiri maa ka bu nyaŋŋi n‑dam ka, dama ka daa mɛ suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
translation_verse: Sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u. Sɔ mun yi fa i bunyeerigu ka boori i kyinkyinni m‑paasi, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.

english_verse: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam susi i bunseelli, nyin zaŋŋma n‑ti u, ka sɔ mun yi kpikki i neema, nyin di laa m‑boosi u a yɛla.

english_verse: Treat others just as you want to be treated.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna ya yi boori ni niriba niŋi seelli n‑ti ya, ya niŋma lala n‑ti ba gba.”

english_verse: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ya yi wuri seeba ban wuri ya, ba nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu? Ay! Halli ala'akyi dima gba niŋni lala.

english_verse: If you are kind only to someone who is kind to you, will God be pleased with you for that? Even sinners are kind to people who are kind to them.
translation_verse: Ka ya yi niŋni din veela n‑tiri ban niŋi ya suŋŋa maa, ba nin pɛ'ai ya di zugu? Ay! Halli ala'akyi dima gba niŋni lala.

english_verse: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
translation_verse: Dinzugu ya zɔm taaba namboo, hoo ya Ba Naawunni ŋɔn zɔri namboo seem la.”

english_verse: Jesus said: Don't judge others, and God won't judge you. Don't be hard on others, and God won't be hard on you. Forgive others, and God will forgive you.
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ya di teesiri taaba yɛla, ka Naawunni ku ti teesi ya gba yɛlibeeri; yan di zaŋŋni taaba taalli, ka Naawunni ku ti zaŋŋi ya gba taalli. Ya kyɛri ya taaba taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba, Ka Naawunni nin ti kyɛ ya taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ya gba.

english_verse: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑nyɛsi ba nyɛsiri wa, ni: “Zooma nin toaai n‑dari u zoontaraana, bee? Ba bayi maa zaa ku lu bɔ'ari ni?

english_verse: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
translation_verse: Karimbiiya bu gatti u karimba, amaa karimbii ŋɔn nya wulisigu suŋŋa, u bamsim ni u karimba bamsim nin ti sa'ai taaba.”

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: U daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, “Ni wula ka i nya muŋŋa i mabii nimbiri ni, ka zaantɔliga deen sa i maŋtoori dinni ni ka i bu nya ka?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
translation_verse: Wula ka i nin toaai n‑yɛli i mabiiya, ni, u kyɛ ka i yiisi munseaa kan bɛ u nimbiri ni, ka deen bu nyara zaantɔliseaa kan bɛ i maŋtoori dinni ni? Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka yi nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni!”

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Yisa daa naa n‑yɛli ba, “Tisuŋŋu bu wanni walibeeri, ka tibeoo mun bu wanni walisuma.

english_verse: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Ba gɔsiri tikam zaa maŋtoori wala maa nti baŋŋi bumbɔ tii n nyɛ ka. Dama nira zi n‑tɔsi kinkama goaa zugu, ka sɔ mun zi n‑tɔsi sisibi purinkpaŋŋu zugu.

english_verse: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
translation_verse: Bunsuma yi bɛ ninvugsuŋŋu sufu ni m‑paali ka, u nin yiisiri bunsuma u sufu maa ni na; ka ninvugbeoo ŋɔn mun sufu paali ni bumbeeri yiisiri la bumbeeri na, dama dinkam bɛ ninsaala sufu ni m‑paali ka, dina ka u yiisiri u noori ni na.”

english_verse: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
