translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, “Wula ka ya boonni ma, ni, ‘N Daana, N Daana’ ka lee m‑bu tumni tunseesi man yɛtti ya, ni, ya tumma maa?

english_verse: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔnkam mun wumni n yɛligu maa ka bu dɔli di, di nira ŋmani la hoo tammeerisɔ ŋɔn daa bu gbi tiŋŋa n‑nyeebi, amaa u kur mɛ n‑tɔm la tambisigu zugu, ka koom daa ti kpɛ n‑gyiligi yiri maa, ka ka daa di lu zaa, ka ka luubu maa daa bɛ pam!”

english_verse: I have officers who give orders to me, and I have soldiers who take orders from me. I can say to one of them, ‘Go!’ and he goes. I can say to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes. I can say to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he will do it.”
translation_verse: Ni, dama ŋɔn gba nyɛ la ban su ninvugsɔa, ka u mun gba su soogyaseeba fukumsi ka nin toaai n‑yɛli ba yinni, ni, u kyɛm luginseaa ka u kyaŋŋi, bee n‑yɛli sɔ, ni, u kyɛm na ka u kyɛ na, ka nin toaai n‑yɛli u daabri, ni, u tumma nŋɔ ka u tum di.

english_verse: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
translation_verse: dama u wuri ti zuliya wa halli, ka u mɛ la'aŋŋu duu n‑ti ti ni ti kpeeri m‑puusiri Naawunni gba.”

english_verse: When the Lord saw the woman, he felt sorry for her and said, “Don't cry!”
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma daa nya pukoori maa ka di niŋi u namboo, ka u yɛli u, “Di kumna!”

english_verse: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
translation_verse: Ka ba daa paai u saani na nti bɛlim u n‑yɛtta, “Soogyadima toondaana maa sum ni i suŋŋi u,

english_verse: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon yɛli, ni, “N teesiya, ni, ŋɔn samni daa galisi pam la.” Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “I siri baŋŋiya!”,

english_verse: In this town an army officer's servant was sick and about to die. The officer liked his servant very much.
translation_verse: Ka soogyadima toondaansɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka u daabwuttigu daa beera, halli ka dooru maa ti wum u halli ka u ti pɔri kum.

english_verse: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish leaders to ask him to come and heal the servant.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka toondaana wa daa wum Yisa yɛla ka daa tum Izraɛli dima kpamseeba ni ba ti susi u ka u kyɛ na nti tibi u baara maa n‑ti u.

english_verse: So Jesus went with them. When Jesus wasn't far from the house, the officer sent some friends to tell him, “Lord, don't go to any trouble for me! I am not good enough for you to come into my house.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti ya labi n‑kunni na ka Yisa daa dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi. Ŋɔn daa ti kyaŋŋi m‑pɔri toondaana maa yiri, ŋɔnwa daa laa n‑tum u zɔdima Yisa saani na, ni, u di mugsi u maŋŋa; ni, ŋɔn toondaana maa poora ni Yisa kyɛ u yiri ni na;

english_verse: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
translation_verse: ni, ka mun bu laa n‑sum ni u gba kyɛ Yisa saani na. Amaa u kur yɛlim yɛttɔ'aseesi ŋɔn ya yɛli, ka u baara maa kpaŋŋi.

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “In all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa wum soogyadima toondaana maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa, di daa gyɛri u pam: ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli daadam ban daa dɔli u maa, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, halli Izraɛli dima ni gba n na zi n‑nya sɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda hoo doo wa ŋɔn niŋi seem!”

english_verse: The officer's friends returned and found the servant well. A Widow's Son
translation_verse: Ka toondaana maa ŋɔn daa tum ninvugseeba maa daa labi yiri maa ni na nti nya ka daabri maa pun kpaŋŋi zaa.

english_verse: As they came near the gate of the town, they saw people carrying out the body of a widow's only son. Many people from the town were walking along with her.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti pɔri tiŋŋa maa kpeebu, tiŋŋa maa ni na'akyimbilisɔ kpi ka ba ziiri u n‑yiiri na, ka tiŋŋa maa niriba kabri n‑dɔla. Ŋɔn daa kpi maa ma daa nyɛ la pukoori, ka daa ka bisɔ m‑paasi ŋɔn kpi maa.

english_verse: Jesus went over and touched the stretcher on which the people were carrying the dead boy. They stopped, and Jesus said, “Young man, get up!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kyaŋŋi nti siisi ban daa mari bunseaa n‑ziiri kum maa, ka ban daa ziiri u maa kpalim n‑zaani, ka u tasi m‑booli kum maa, ni, “Na'akyimbila, n ya yɛtti i ni, ni, i doaama!”

english_verse: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
translation_verse: Ka kum maa daa siri neaai u kum ni n‑doaai n‑zini m‑piili yɛttɔ'a, ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑ti u ma maa.

english_verse: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
translation_verse: Ka dabeem daa gbaai sɔkam ka ba pɛ'ari Naawunni n‑yɛtti, ni, “Anabititaari kyɛ duniya wa ni na, ni u ti bɛ ti saani!”, ka laa n‑yɛli, ni “Naawunni gɔsi u niriba yɛla maa.”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa zugu, lahabaari maa daa mooli n‑gyili Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni tinseesi an gyiligi bɛni maa zaa.

english_verse: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa yiisi ba niriba ayi n‑tum ba ti Dugma Yisa saani ni ba ti boosi u, ni, “Nyin n nyɛ ŋɔn ya kyɛ na la, bee ti na gɔsim di daama soori?”

english_verse: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
translation_verse: Ka tuumudima babayi maa daa ti paai Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, “Gyɔni ŋɔn suuri niriba Naawunni koom la n tum ti i saani na ni ti boosi i, ni, nyini n nyɛ ŋɔn ya kyɛ na la, bee, ti na gɔsim di daama soori?”

english_verse: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa tibri baaridima pam, ni ninvugseeba dooribeeri ŋɔn daa mari zaa, ka katti arizimbeeri niriba zugu m‑basira, ka neaari zoomma pam nini n‑tiri ba.

english_verse: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli Gyɔni niriba, ni, “Ya labma nti yɛli Gyɔni yan nyari ka wumni seela: ni, zoomma nini neaara, ka wɔbsi kyɛnna, ka ban mari kɔmni nyari alaafɛa, ka tubkpiradima wumna, halli ka ban kpi gba neaari kum ni; ni, ka lahabasuŋŋu maa moonni n‑tiri fara dima.”

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam bu birisiri n yɛla zugu mari la zugsuŋŋu!”

english_verse: After John's messengers had gone, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: What kind of person did you go out to the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna Gyɔni tuumudima daa doaai n‑kuli, ka Yisa mun daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri daadam maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɔni yɛla, ka boosiri ba, ni, “Bɔ ka ya daa kyaŋŋi mooni ni ya ti nya? Kyimnigu pɛbsigu ŋɔn zeera, bee?

english_verse: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Bee bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi neensuma? Ay! - ya bu nya? - ninvugseeba ban yeeri bunveela ka diri bunnya'asa n kur bɛ nadima yiya ni.”;

english_verse: What then did you go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was! I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: ka laa m‑boosi ba n‑yɛli, “Ka bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ya ya n‑nya la anabisɔ, bee? Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, ni, halli u gaari anabi gba.

english_verse: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔna n nyɛ ban daa sɔbi sɔ yɛla, ni, Naawunni yɛliya: ‘Ya nyama! - N tuumu nŋɔa; N tumni u ni u ti daŋŋi i tooni: Ŋɔna nin maali i kyɛnni maa soori n‑ti i.’

english_verse: No one ever born on this earth is greater than John. But whoever is least important in God's kingdom is greater than John.
translation_verse: Amaa n ya n‑yɛli ya, ni, Gyɔni gaari pɔ'aba ŋɔn pun dɔ'ai bidibseeba zaa; amaa ka n ya yɛli ya yaasa, ni, halli ŋɔn poora zaa m‑bɛ Naawunni naam ni gba yi gaari ŋɔna.”

english_verse: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka zama kam ban daa bɛ bɛni n‑wumni Yisa ŋɔn yɛli seem maa - halli lampodeaariba gba - ba zaa daa pɛ'ari Naawunni, ni, u soori n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni soori, dama ba zaa daa pun deaai Naawunni kɔsuubu Gyɔni saani.

english_verse: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Amaa Farisii dima ni karimbadima mun daa bu deaai kɔsuubu maa, ka dizugu ba daa za'asi Naawunni ŋɔn zaali ba seem.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Ka bumbɔ ka n ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑ŋmansi zuna niriba wa? Ba lee n‑ŋmanni la bɔa?

english_verse: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
translation_verse: Ba ŋmani la hoo biisi ban zi daa ni m‑boonni taaba n‑yɛtti, ni, ‘Ti peebi yiiya n‑ti ya ka ya za'asi waa, ka ti laa m‑maanni kuuri n‑ti ya ka ya bu sakki n‑kumna!’

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Dama Gyɔni Kɔsuura la daa kyɛ na ka u ba'a ka bundira diibu, bee bunnyuura nyuubu, yɛla ni, ka ya yɛli, ni, arizimbeoo n zi u zugu ni.

english_verse: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
translation_verse: Ka man mun Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na ka man ni niriba la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura, ka ya yɛli, ni, ‘Ya bu nya doo wa ŋɔn nyɛ beem daana ni danyuura seem? - ka u laa n‑dɔli zoori ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi dima.’

english_verse: Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what its followers do. Simon the Pharisee
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni yɛm wulisi kamaŋŋa la ban deaai ka zaa saani.”

english_verse: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka Farisii dima dɔsɔ daa booli Yisa ni u kyɛ u yiri na ka ba la'am n‑zini bundirigu zii ni ba di.

english_verse: When a sinful woman in that town found out that Jesus was there, she bought an expensive bottle of perfume.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ na n‑zini sahaseaa la, tiŋŋa maa ni pɔ'a tumbeeri daansɔ daa ti wum, ni, Yisa bɛ la Farisii maa yiri n‑diri bundirigu. Pɔ'a maa daa zaŋŋi kɔlɔbaveelli kan paali turaali kpaam

english_verse: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
translation_verse: n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ n‑zɛ Yisa nyaaŋŋa ka gbaai kunkuma, ka u nintam tɔ'ari n‑luri Yisa nɔba ni m‑peaari a, ka u mari u zɔbwɔ'ala n‑nyeesiri a, ka bɔbli a, ka zaŋŋi turaali maa n‑soosi Yisa nɔba.

english_verse: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
translation_verse: Ka Farisisɔ ŋɔn daa booli Yisa na maa daa nya lala; ka yɛli u sufu ni, ni, doo wa yi da siri nyɛ la anabi, u naa m‑baŋŋi pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn siisiri u wa ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka mi, ni, tumbeeri daana n nyɛ u.

english_verse: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u “Saimoon! M mari yɛttɔ'aseaa ni n yɛli i.”; ka Saimoon labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ndana, yɛlim ma.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Two people were in debt to a moneylender. One of them owed him
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Dɔppa ayi n daa bɛ ni, n‑daa pɛŋŋi dɔsɔ ligri. Ka ba yinni maa samni daa gaari u taraana samni maa kaman napɔŋŋu piiya.

english_verse: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Di saha la ka Yisa laa n‑yɛli pɔ'a maa “Kyɛma ni sufudoaa; i yɛdda niŋbu n tuligi i.”

english_verse: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
translation_verse: Ka babayi maa zaa daa ka ligri n‑nin yɔ n‑labsi sandaana wa, ka u daa kyɛ u sama maa n‑ti niriba ayi maa zaa. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna niriba ayi wa sunsukkuni, ba ŋɔni nin lee n‑wuri ligri maa daana n‑gaari u kpeaa?”

english_verse: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
translation_verse: ka ŋmaligi n‑gɔsi pɔ'a maa ka yɛli Saimoon “I nya pɔ'a wa? Man da kpɛ i yiri wa ni na, i da bu ti ma koom ka m peaai n nɔba: pɔ'a wa mun zaŋŋi u nintam m‑peaari n nɔba ka zaŋŋi u zɔbri n‑nyeesi a.

english_verse: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
translation_verse: Ka nyin da bu puusi ma ka mɔ'asi n kparima.” - ba tiŋŋa puusigu kaari nla - “Amaa man kpɛ i yiri wa na halli wayoo ka pɔ'a wa na bu kyɛ n nɔba bɔbligu.

english_verse: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
translation_verse: Din paasi yaasa, i bu niŋi kpaam n‑soosi n zugu, amaa pɔ'a wa ŋɔn zaŋŋi turaali kpaam n‑soosi n nɔba.

english_verse: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
translation_verse: Dizugu n ya yɛli i, ni, ba kyɛ pɔ'a wa taalli din galisi pam m‑pɛŋŋi u, u wurilim ŋɔn galisi pam zugu; amaa ninvugsɔ taalli din bu galisi ka ba kyɛ di m‑pɛŋŋi u, di daama wurilim gba bu ya galisi.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Ka ŋmaligi n‑yɛli pɔ'a maa, “N kyɛ i taalli maa m‑pɛŋŋi i.”

english_verse: Some other guests started saying to one another, “Who is this who dares to forgive sins?”
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni ni Yisa n‑diri maa daa nyurimni ba kparima ni n‑yɛtti taaba, ni, “Ŋɔni mun n lee nŋɔa? - halli nti kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni ba!”,

english_verse: and so were some women who had been healed of evil spirits and all sorts of diseases. One of the women was Mary Magdalene, who once had seven demons in her.
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi la daa dɔli u m‑bɛ u saani. Pɔ'aseeba gba daa dɔla, baseeba Yisa ŋɔn daa pun tibi ba dooribeeri ka kari arizimbeeri ni ba yi ka kyɛ ba. Pɔ'aba maa yinni n daa nyɛ Mariama ŋɔn daa yi Magdala tiŋŋa ni na, ka ŋɔna ka arizimbeeri ayɔpɔi daa yi u ni n‑kyɛ u la:

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka kuga zugu dina la mun nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa, ka deaai ka ni sufumaasim; amaa ban ka nya'a zugu di bu yuura, dama sahasuŋŋu ka ba niŋi Naawunni yɛdda; amaa di yi tɔ sahanseaa, ba nin labi la nyaaŋŋa m‑basi.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
translation_verse: Tɔ, di saha maa bumburiseesi daa lu la kuga ziiya; ka an daa ti buli sahanseaa la, kuga maa daa kyɛ ka a bu nyaŋŋi n‑zoaai ka a gba daa gyɛkki n‑kpi, an daa kɔŋŋi tammaasiri maa zugu.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked the plants.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka a seesi gba daa lu goosi ni, ka goosi maa daa doaai n‑gabi a ka a kpi.

english_verse: So he answered: I have explained the secrets about God's kingdom to you. But for others I use stories, so they will look, but not see, and they will hear, but not understand. Jesus Explains the Story about a Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: ka u yɛli ba, “Yarima ka ŋɔn Naawunni pun ti ya soori ni ya baŋŋi u naam yɛlisɔ'ara an nyɛ seem; amaa seeba saani di tɔ pam ni ba baŋŋi nyɛsiri gbinni, halli ba gɔsiri n‑gɔsiri gba ka ku nya, ka ba wumni gba ka bu ya m‑baŋŋi di gbinni.

english_verse: There is nothing hidden that will not be found. There is no secret that will not be well known.
translation_verse: Bunsɔ'ariseaa ka ni ka ba ku baŋŋi kan bɛ seem zaa.

english_verse: and while they were sailing across, he went to sleep. Suddenly a storm struck the lake, and the boat started sinking. They were in danger.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ba daa piili n‑kyɛnni koom maa zugu halli ka Yisa ti gbisi. Di saha ka pɛsititaari daa doaai mɔ'ari maa ni n‑zeera. Ka daa zɛ ni halli ka nyariŋŋu maa ti ya paali ni koom.

english_verse: This is what the story means: The seed is God's message,
translation_verse: Tɔ; nyɛsiri maa gbinni nŋɔa#:- Bumbura maa nyɛ la Naawunni yɛligu.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they are so eager for riches and pleasures that they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Goosi ni dina la mun nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa ka ba teesa na kur bɛ duniya wa yɛla ni la, ni ka neema ni ka bunnya'asa ni la m‑paasi. Lala zugu ka yɛligu maa bu mari nyoori ba sufu ni.

english_verse: Those seeds that fell on good ground are the people who listen to the message and keep it in good and honest hearts. They last and produce a harvest. Light (Mark
translation_verse: Amaa tansuŋŋu dina la nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban wum yɛligu maa ka deaai ka ni yɛlimaŋni n‑niŋi ba sufu ni; ka ba mari nyoori pam di zugu - ba sufu puu antaam nla.”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the disciples, “Don't you have any faith?” But they were frightened and amazed. They said to each other, “Who is this? He can give orders to the wind and the waves, and they obey him!” A Man with Demons in Him (Matthew
translation_verse: Di saha ka u yi boosi ba, ni, “Ya yɛdda la lee?”, ka dabeem maali n‑gbaai ba. Di daa gyɛri ba pam, ka ba yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri taaba ka boosiri, ni, “Bɔ nira n nyɛ ŋɔnŋɔa? - halli ka u mari yiiko n‑toaai n‑tasiri pɛsigu ni kooma gba ka a wumna!”

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl or under a bed. A lamp is always put on a lampstand, so people who come into a house will see the light.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Sɔ bu nyoaari firila ka laa m‑mari laa m‑pinni ka, bee n‑zaŋŋi n‑zaali gadu lɔŋni; ay! - ba tɔmni ka la saazugu ka ban kpeeri na maa nyari kasi.

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “Someone touched me, because I felt power going out from me.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa bu sakki, ka yɛli “Ay, amaa sɔ n siisi ma, dama m baŋŋiya, ni, yiiko yi n saani n‑kpɛ sɔ ni.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, “Don't worry! Have faith, and your daughter will get well.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa labsi n‑yɛli doo maa, ni, “Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i, kur niŋma yɛdda ka u nya alaafɛa.”

english_verse: They begged Jesus not to send them to the deep pit, where they would be punished.
translation_verse: Arizimbeeri maa yi susiri u ni, ni, u di katti ba n‑yiisiri n‑taasiri ba bɔ'azuliŋŋu ni.

english_verse: The people went out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they also found the man. The demons had gone out of him, and he was sitting there at the feet of Jesus. He had clothes on and was in his right mind. But the people were terrified.
translation_verse: nti tisi lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban nya seem maa. Ka niriba pam daa siri yi na ni ba nya yɛttɔ'aseaa kan niŋi maa. Ban daa paai na, ba daa nya doo maa, ka arizimbeeri yi ka kyɛ u, ka u zi la Yisa tooni ka yɛ u sutura ka yɛttɔ'aseaa ka u. Di daa gbaai ba dabeem pam.

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers went to see him, but because of the crowd they could not get near him.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa ma, ni u tinzɔppa, daa kyɛ u saani na ka bu toaai n‑nya u, daadam ŋɔn daa zoaai la zugu.

english_verse: As Jesus was getting out of the boat, he was met by a man from this town. The man had demons in him. He had gone naked for a long time and no longer lived in a house, but in the graveyard.
translation_verse: Tiŋŋa maa ni ka arizimbeeri daa daamni dɔsɔa, ka lala doo maa daa bu yeeri kparigu ka laa m‑bu soori kuriga, ka u daa pun bɛ lala n‑yuui. U daa bu gbeeri yiri ni, amaa u daa bɛ la gbala zii ni n‑gyinna. Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni sahaseaa la, arizinni daana wa daa yi u zugu na.

english_verse: Just then the man in charge of the synagogue came and knelt down in front of Jesus. His name was Jairus, and he begged Jesus to come to his home
translation_verse: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u yuuri daa boonni Gyayiruusi; ba tiŋŋa wunsusigu la'aŋŋu duu kpeema n daa nyɛ u. Tɔ, ka ban daa kur gyaari u n‑naara, Gyayiruusi gba yi na n‑gbaani u tooni,

english_verse: One day, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat, and he said, “Let's cross the lake.” They started out,
translation_verse: Dahinseaa ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa paai mɔ'ari ni nti kpɛ nyariŋŋu ni. Ka u yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛ ka ti kyaŋŋi nti du mɔ'ari wa duuri sa.”

english_verse: So they went to Jesus and woke him up, “Master, Master! We are about to drown!” Jesus got up and ordered the wind and waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Ka dabeem yi gbaai u nyaandɔliba maa ka ba daa neaai Yisa u goom ni ka yɛli, “Eeh, Ti daana! Ti zaa kpiiri ni, pɛsigu wa zugu!” Ka ŋɔn daa neaai, u daa tasi pɛsigu maa ni kɔmiisi la. Ka di lu n‑fɔ surimm.

english_verse: The man saw Jesus and screamed. He knelt down in front of him and shouted, “Jesus, Son of God Most High, what do you want with me? I beg you not to torture me!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ŋɔn daa ti nya Yisa, u daa gbaani tiŋŋa ka tasi pampam, ni, “I ya niŋi ma la wula? - Yisa nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa wa biiya! N susiri i ni, ni, nyin di namsi ma!”

english_verse: When the men taking care of the pigs saw this, they ran to spread the news in the town and on the farms.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa guri kurikyuudima maa ŋɔn daa nya lala, ba daa zɔ yoomyooma n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai timpuuni niriba, ni seeba ban daa tumni puri ni gba,

english_verse: He said this because Jesus had already told the evil spirit to go out of him. The man had often been attacked by the demon. And even though he had been bound with chains and leg irons and kept under guard, he smashed whatever bound him. Then the demon would force him out into lonely places.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Yisa daa pun tasi arizimbeoo maa, ni, u yim ka kyɛ doo maa, ka dizugu ka arizinni maa daa tɔ'asi lala. Arizimbeoo maa daa yi ti lusi doo maa la sahaseaa, ba daa yi zaŋŋi kutti m‑ba u nuusi ni u nɔba n‑dooli u ka u kur kyisiri a ka arizinni maa kyɛ ka u zɔ n‑kpɛ yoo ni; ka di daa pun yuui lala.

english_verse: Jesus asked the man, “What is your name?” He answered, “My name is Lots.” He said this because there were “lots” of demons in him.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa yi boosi u yuuri ka u yɛli, ni, u yuuri boonni la ‘Zama’, dama arizimbeeri pam n daa la'asi m‑bɛ u ni.

english_verse: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. So the demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and Jesus let them go.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka di saha maa kurikyuu bɔbgu gba daa bɛ la kunkunni lugri ni n‑dira, ka arizimbeeri maa daa susi soori, ni, Yisa sakkma ka ba yi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni.

english_verse: Then the demons left the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Ka u daa ti ba lala soori. Ka arizindima maa daa yi doo maa ni n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni, ka ba vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom di ba.

english_verse: Then all who had seen the man healed told about it.
translation_verse: Ka ban nini daa nya maa daa yɛli daadam maa Yisa ŋɔn tibi arizimbeeri daana maa seem.

english_verse: Everyone from around Gerasa begged Jesus to leave, because they were so frightened. When Jesus got into the boat to start back,
translation_verse: Niriba ban daa yi Gyɛrasa tinkpansi zaa maa daa yɛli u, ni, u kyɛma ka kyɛ ba, dama di kpeesi ba dabeem pam. Ka Yisa daa siri laa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ni u labi.

english_verse: the man who had been healed begged to go with him. But Jesus sent him off and said,
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yisa ŋɔn tibi dɔsɔ maa daa susi soori ni u dɔli u n‑kpalim m‑bɛ u saani; amaa Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, u kulima u tiŋŋa

english_verse: “Go back home and tell everyone how much God has done for you.” The man then went all over town, telling everything that Jesus had done for him. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: nti wulisi Naawunni ŋɔn tum seem n‑ti u. Ka u daa siri kyaŋŋi n‑gyili tiŋŋa maa zaasa nti mooli Yisa ŋɔn tibi u seem.

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but none of them could make her well.
translation_verse: Di saha maa gba ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ka u daa soori halli yuuma piiya ni ayi ka tiimadima sɔsɔ daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tibi u - u ziim daa bu ŋmaara:

english_verse: Everyone had been waiting for Jesus, and when he came back, a crowd was there to welcome him.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa labi la, niriba pam pun guri u, ni ba gyaari u.

english_verse: As soon as she came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes, her bleeding stopped.
translation_verse: ka pɔ'a wa daa tabli na n‑kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑siisi Yisa bunyeerigu, ka ziim maa daa di ŋmaai kyiribbi!

english_verse: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. While everyone was denying it, Peter said, “Master, people are crowding all around and pushing you from every side.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; din daa niŋi maa zugu Yisa daa boosi, ni, “Ŋɔni n da siisi ma la?” Ka niriba maa zaa kyiisi n‑naai, ka Piita gba yɛli, ni, “N Daana, daadam maa ŋɔn zoaai m‑mugri i seem wa, ka sɔ siisi i?”

english_verse: The woman knew that she could not hide, so she came trembling and knelt down in front of Jesus. She told everyone why she had touched him and that she had been healed at once.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa nya ka di ka sɔ'abu, ka dizugu u daa kyɛ Yisa saani na, halli ka dabeem daa gbaai u gba, ka gbaani u tooni ka yɛli ba daliiriseaa kan kyɛ ka u siisi u, ni u siisigu maa ŋɔn tibi u dooru yoomyooma seem; ka ba zaa daa wumna.

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yisa yi yɛli u, “M biiya, kyɛma ni alaafɛa. I niŋi la yɛdda, di zugu ka i nya tippu.”

english_verse: While Jesus was speaking, someone came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa kur bɛ la yɛttɔ'a ni, ka dɔsɔ yi kpeema maa yiri ni na nti yɛli u, ni, u bii maa kpiya, ni, dizugu u di laa m‑mukkri karimba maa u bii maa zugu.

english_verse: Jesus went into the house, but he did not let anyone else go with him, except Peter, John, James, and the girl's father and mother.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ŋɔn daa mari nti paai yiri maa ni, u daa bu sakki ni sɔsɔ kpɛ duu maa ni, na'ala Piita ni Gyɔni ni Gyeemsi, m‑paasi bii maa ba ni u ma zugu.

english_verse: Everyone was crying and weeping for the girl. But Jesus said, “The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka yiri maa ni niriba zaa daa kur bɛ la kunkuma ni, ka kaasiri bii maa zugu; amaa Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Ya di kumna; u bu kpi, amaa u gbisiri ni.”

english_verse: The people laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
translation_verse: U daa tɔ'asi lala, ka di ku ba la laari, dama ba daa pun koaai bii maa tama zaa.

english_verse: Jesus took hold of the girl's hand and said, “Child, get up!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; di saha ka u gyi bii maa nuu ka yɛli, “Biiya, doaama!”

english_verse: Her parents were surprised, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
translation_verse: amaa u daa kpaam ba, ni, ba di yɛli n‑ti sɔsɔ din niŋi seem.

english_verse: The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Tuumudima maa ŋɔn daa ti labi na, ba daa yɛli Yisa ban daa niŋi seem zaa. Ka u daa zaŋŋi ba ka ba foaai ba mammansi n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.

english_verse: They did this, and all the people sat down.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa niŋi lala, ka sɔkam zaa zinni tiŋŋa.

english_verse: He told them, “Don't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya di zaŋŋi bunseelli ya sɔkyɛnni maa ni: yan di zaŋŋi zangbeaa bee booriga, bee bundirigu, bee ligri, bee bunyeera ayiyi.

english_verse: When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town.
translation_verse: Ya yi kur ti paai yiri seaa, ya kpeema ka bɛ bɛni halli nti yi tiŋŋa maa ni.

english_verse: Late in the afternoon the twelve apostles came to Jesus and said, “Send the crowd to the villages and farms around here. They need to find a place to stay and something to eat. There is nothing in this place. It's like a desert!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti kabgi u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi maa daa kyɛ u saani na n‑yɛli u, “Tim niriba maa soori ka kyɛ ka ba wurim n‑kpɛ tinkpanseesi an gyiligi kpa maa ni, ni tiŋŋa maa zaa ni, nti bɔ bundirigu n‑di, ka nya goom ziiya, dama moo ni ka ti bɛ ni maa.”

english_verse: While he was praying, his face changed, and his clothes became shining white.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti susiri maa, u nindaa daa takkiya, ka u sutura neaai, nyain, kaman saa nya'asigu.

english_verse: Jesus strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone about this. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa kpamsi ba pam ni ba di yɛli sɔ, ni, u nyɛ la Masiiya maa.

english_verse: But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Amaa zama maa daa baŋŋi ŋɔn bɛ luginseaa ka dɔlisi u nɔba. Ka Yisa daa deaai ba ka tɔ'asi n‑ti ba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam maa yɛla. U daa laa n‑tibi bankam zaa daa boori tippu.

english_verse: “Don't stop him!” Jesus said. “Anyone who isn't against you is for you.” A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
translation_verse: Amaa ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “Di za'asiri u, dama ŋɔnkam bu zabri ya n nyɛ ŋɔn zɛ ya nyaaŋŋa.”

english_verse: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi lala nyaaŋŋa, di daa niŋi kaman daba anii ka u daa zaŋŋi Piita, ni Gyɔni, ni Gyeemsi m‑paasi u zugu, ka ba daa du zoori maa zugu ni ba ti susi Naawunni.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔnkam zaa yi boori u nyɔvuri maa nin kɔŋŋi ka; ŋɔn mun yi sakki ni u kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, mani zugu, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin nya nyɔvuri.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life?
translation_verse: Nira yi di duniya zaa ka kɔŋŋi u nyɔvuri, bee sa'amni ka, di suŋŋi u la wula?

english_verse: If you are ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in his glory and in the glory of his Father and the holy angels.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ yi zɔri vi man ni n yɛligu maa zugu, ŋɔna zugu Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti zɔ vi, sahanseaa u yi mari u gyirima n‑kyɛ na, ni u Ba gyirima, ni malaakadima zaa m‑paasi u zugu.

english_verse: They appeared in heavenly glory and talked about all that Jesus' death in Jerusalem would mean.
translation_verse: Ba daa neaari ni veelim ni gyirima pam, ka ba yɛttɔ'a daa nyɛ la zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn nin paai Gyɛrusalɛm nti ka ni seem yɛla.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them.
translation_verse: Ka Piita ŋɔn daa kur kpalim n‑tɔ'asiri lala maa sangbana di sigi na n‑lim ba, ka dabeem daa gbaai ba pam ban daa kpeeri sangbana maa ni zugu.

english_verse: From the cloud a voice spoke, “This is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Ka kukɔri daa yi sangbana puuni na n‑yɛtti, ni, “M bii nŋɔ, ŋɔna ka n gaŋŋi: ya wumma u noori!”

english_verse: After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka kukɔri maa ŋɔn daa ti tɔ'asi n‑naai, ba nya ka Yisa kɔkɔ n kpalim n‑zɛya. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi yɛlli maa n‑sina, ka daa bu yɛli ban daa nya seelli maa yɛla n‑ti sɔa.

english_verse: A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out.
translation_verse: I bu nya arizimbeoo ŋɔn zi u zugu n‑yisigri u seem? U yi ti bɛ m‑bɛ n‑kaasi pam, ka arizinni maa darigi u halli ka nantoori fufukku ti yiiri u noori ni na, n‑kur mugsiri u ka ku sakki ni u basi u.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?” Then Jesus said to the man, “Bring your son to me.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, “Ah! Ya zuna niriba wa n kur nyɛ bungɔlima, ka ku sakki yɛdda niŋbu! Daba ala ka mani ni yarima nin la'am m‑bɛ ni, bee daba ala ka n ya toaai n‑di ya sugru? Mari i bii maa n‑kyɛ kpa na!”

english_verse: While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa mari bii maa n‑kyɛnni Yisa saani na, arizimbeoo maa daa lusi u tiŋŋa ka darigi u. Ka Yisa daa tasi arizinni maa zugu ka kyɛ ka bii maa kpaŋŋi, ka u zaŋŋi u n‑labsi n‑ti u ba maa.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at God's great power. Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi niriba maa alahazibu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn galisi seem.

english_verse: But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa gbinni, ka di daa sɔ'ai ni ba saani, ka ba daa kɔŋŋi di baŋŋni zaa. Ka ba mun daa bu boosi u m‑baŋŋi, dabeem ŋɔn daa mari ba maa zugu.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest.
translation_verse: Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa ti ŋmeeri la taaba nangbankpeemni ni ba baŋŋi ŋɔn gaari u kpeaa ba puuni.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ba sufu ni teesa, ka u booli bibila na n‑zaŋŋi u n‑zaali n‑tabli u mammaŋŋa, ka yɛli ba, ni,

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.” For or against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: “Ŋɔnkam deaari bibila wa tataabu man yuuri zugu deaari la mani, ka ŋɔnkam mun deaari ma deaari la Ŋɔn tum ma na maa. Dinŋɔna ŋɔn poora ya zaasa ni, di daama gaari ya zaa.”

english_verse: John said, “Master, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.”
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa yɛli Yisa, “N Daana, ti da nya sɔ ka u mari i yuuri n‑katti arizimbeeri, ka ti bu sakki ni u tum lala, ŋɔn bu tabli ti la'aŋŋu n‑dɔla zugu.”

english_verse: Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Yisa saazugu lappu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai na, u daa gbaai la Gyɛrusalɛm kyɛnni ni nimbiri moaa.

english_verse: He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him.
translation_verse: Ka u daa tum tuumudima ni ba daŋŋma u tooni. Ka dizugu u tuumudima maa daa paai Samɛrii tinkpanseaa puuni nti maali siri zaa n‑guri u.

english_verse: But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him.
translation_verse: Ka tinkpaŋŋa maa dima daa ti za'asi, ni, ba ku deaai u, ŋɔn bɛ soori n‑kyɛnni Gyɛrusalɛm maa zugu.

english_verse: When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba wa, ban yuya boonni Gyeemsi ni Gyɔni la, ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, “Ti Dugma, i boori ni ti kyɛ ka bugum yi saazugu na nti di ba, bee?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned and corrected them for what they had said.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑tasi ba zugu;

english_verse: Then they all went on to another village. Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers (Matthew
translation_verse: ka ba daa doaai n‑gaari m‑paai tinkpanseaa nti gbɛ bɛni.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Foxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Yɔbaasi mari la vɔya n‑kpeera, ka niinsi mun mari la teeri n‑tɔmna, amaa man Ninsaala Biiya ka luginseaa man nin zaŋŋi n zugu n‑dooli gba.”

english_verse: Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, “Lord, let me wait until I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli dɔsɔ, ni, “Dɔli ma!”, ka di daama yɛli u, ni, “Kur kyɛ ka n kuli ka m ba yi ti kpi ka n sɔ'ai u pɔi ka naayi kyɛ na n dɔli i.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Let the dead take care of the dead, while you go and tell about God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Amaa ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “Kyɛ ka kpiimma sɔ'ari ba kpiintaaba: nyin muna - kyɛma nti moonni Naawunni naam mooligu.”

english_verse: Then someone said to Jesus, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.”
translation_verse: Ka sɔ mun daa ti laa n‑kyɛ na nti yɛli Yisa, ni, “N Daana, n nin dɔli i, amaa kyɛ ka n kuli n‑kyeoosi n yiri dima pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ na n‑dɔli i.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi u nuu n‑gbaai kuuri ka laa n‑gɔsiri u nyaaŋŋa bu sumni Naawunni naam beebu ni.”

english_verse: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
translation_verse: Ka yɛli ba, ni, “Ya kyi ŋmaabu tuma wa zoaaya pam, amaa ka ya tuntumniba maa mun bu zoaai; dizugu ya susim ti Dugma ŋɔn nyɛ puu wa daana, ka u tum niriba na m‑paasi ba ŋma.

english_verse: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
translation_verse: N tumni ya ni ya yim soori n‑kyaŋŋi, amaa ya baŋŋma, ni, ya nyɛ la kaman pɛbiseeba ban bɛ kunduna sunsukkuni.

english_verse: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
translation_verse: Yan di mari baagyi bee booriga bee namna n‑kyaŋŋi; ya mun di puusiri sɔ soori ni.

english_verse: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”
translation_verse: Ka ya yi ti paai n‑kpɛ yiri seaa zaa ya yɛlima, ni, Naawunni nin taasi u sufudoaa n‑niŋi yiri wa ni na:

english_verse: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
translation_verse: ka sufudoaa daana yi siri bɛ bɛni, dinŋɔna ya gba sufudoaa nin bɛ u saani. Amaa di yi pa lala, di nin labi ya saani na.

english_verse: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
translation_verse: Ya kpalimma yiri maa ni n‑diri ka nyuuri bunseelli kam zaa ba yi ti nyaŋŋi n‑ti ya, dama di sumsi ni tuntumna deaari u yoori. Yan di laa n‑yiiri yiri seaa n‑lɔŋŋni yiri seaa.

english_verse: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
translation_verse: Ya mun yi paai tinseelli kam ka ba sakki n‑deaai ya, ya dim bundiriseelli zaa ban nin zaŋŋi n‑ti ya,

english_verse: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
translation_verse: ka tibi baariseeba ban bɛ tiŋŋa maa ni ka ba kpaŋŋi, ka yɛli daadam maa, ni, Naawunni naam paari ba na.

english_verse: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
translation_verse: Amaa ya yi ti kpɛ tinseaa ka ba bu sakki ni ba deaai ya, ya dɔli lala tiŋŋa maa sɔya n‑yɛtta,

english_verse: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka n yɛli ya, ni, Zaadaari yi ti paai, di nin tɔ n‑tiri lala tiŋŋa dima n‑gaari din ti ya n‑tɔ n‑tiri ninvugbɛseeba ban daa bɛ Sɔdoom tiŋŋa ni seem la gba.”

english_verse: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi ni yan nyaŋŋi arizimbeeri maa zugu, amaa ya kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi ban pun sɔbi ya yuya arizana ni maa zugu.”

english_verse: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
translation_verse: Dama n yɛtti ya, ni, sahaseaa din gaari, anabidima ni nadima pam daa boori la ba da nya yan nyari bunseesi wa ka mun bu nya a; ka daa laa m‑boori la ba wum yan wumni seelli wa ka daa bu wum.”

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
translation_verse: Amaa Zaadaari halli Taaya ni Saidoon ninvugbeeri wahala gba ku paai ya dinni.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
translation_verse: Yarima muna, ya Kapirinyum dima wa, ya bu duusiri la ya mansi m‑paai arizana ni? Amaa ba nin sigsi ya kpiimma tiŋŋa ni sa.

english_verse: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, “N gɔsi n‑nya ka Sintaani mammaŋŋa yi saazugu n‑lu na kaman saa ŋɔn yi nyakki seem la!”, ni,

english_verse: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
translation_verse: “Ya nyama, n ti ya soori ni ya nin toaai n‑nɔ wugi ni nansi yɔriyɔri, ka nyaŋŋi ti Dindaana maa kpeeŋŋu zaa, ka bunseelli kam ku toaai n‑niŋi ya seela.

english_verse: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Naawunni zaaligu baŋŋnisɔ daa ti boori ni u zamsi Yisa yɛm, ka doaai n‑zaani m‑boosi u, ni, “N Karimba, wula ka n ya n‑niŋi n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari?”

english_verse: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli, “Ni, ‘I zaŋŋma i sufu zaa n‑wuri i Dugma Naawunni, ni i siiya zaa, ni i kpeeŋŋu ni i teesa zaa.’, ni, ‘Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.’ ”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa kyɛ ka Yisa sufu pɛligi pam, ka u yɛli, “M Ba Naawunni, nyin ŋɔn su arizana ni duniya wa, m pɛ'ari i ni, dama nyin zaŋŋi yɛla wa zaa n‑sɔ'ai ka kyɛ yɛm dima ni baŋŋniba ka laa n‑kasigi a n‑wulisi bileesi. Ii, m Ba, yɛlimaŋni lala veela i saani.

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: Bunseelli kam zaa ka m pun deaai m Ba nuu ni. Sɔ ka ni m‑mi Bii maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ, na'ala u Ba maa m mi u; ka sɔ mun gba ka ni m‑mi m Ba maa ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ, na'ala mani u Bii maa m mi u; m‑paasi sɔkam man, u Bii maa, n nin sakki ni n kasigi m Ba maa yɛla n‑wulisi u.”

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa u daa ŋmaligi kpaŋŋa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba kɔkɔ n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ninvugseeba ban nya yan nyari bunseesi wa mari la zugsuŋŋu!

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Wula ka di sɔbi Naawunni zaaligu ni? I Litaafi kariŋŋu wulisiri i la wula?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Mboo! I labsi suŋŋa pam: dizugu niŋma lala ka i nin nya nyɔvuri maa.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Ka u mun daa bu boori ba galim u u boosigu maa zugu, ka dizugu u daa laa m‑boosi, ni, “Ka ŋɔni n nyɛ n lala ‘taraana’?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑kyɛnni ni u ti paai Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa. Ka u na kyɛnni n‑sigri zoori, ka sɔguriba daa luri u soori maa zugu m‑pirigi u sutura zaa, ka bu u halli ka u ti gyɛa, ka kyɛ u n‑dooli sɔkpaŋŋa ka u daa pɔtti la kum.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Ka di daa bu yuui ka maalimaannisɔ ti dɔli soori maa na nti nya u, ka lɔkki n‑gaari ka kyɛ u.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Lala gba ka Liivai za'anɔri dɔsɔ gba daa ti dɔli soori maa na nti nya u, ka ŋɔn gba daa lɔkki n‑gaari ka kyɛ u.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Amaa ka Samɛrii dɔsɔ gba daa ti dɔli soori maa na nti paai bɛni n‑nya doo maa, ka di daa niŋi u namboo pam.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Ka u daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai doo maa saani n‑zaŋŋi daam m‑peaai u dansi maa, ka boaai kpaam n‑niŋi, ka zaŋŋi tana n‑lɔ u yuuma maa. U daa zaŋŋi u n‑ziili u mammaŋŋa bumbaarigu zugu n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ saamma kpeebu yiri m‑mari u suŋŋa.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai ka Samɛrii doo wa yi ya kyaŋŋi u kyɛnni la, u daa yiisi ligri n‑ti u yikpɛdaana maa ka yɛli u, ni, u gɔsim u baara wa suŋŋa halli ka u ti labi na. Ni, ligri maa yi ti pɔsigi ka u labi na, u nin yɔ ka samni maa an kpalim n‑ti u dirindaari maa.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ba niriba ata maa sunsukkuni, ba ŋɔni n zaŋŋi doo maa n‑leebi u taraana?” Ka u labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa zɔ u namboo la.”

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa ti paai tinkpanseaa, ka pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Maata zaŋŋi u nti kpeesi u yiri.

english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Ka Maata tinzɔ, ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Mariama la, daa kyaŋŋi nti zinni Yisa tooni n‑wumni u yɛttɔ'a,

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: amaa Maata ŋɔn daa kur bɛ la bundirigu dugbu ni. Ka Maata daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti boosi u, ni, “N Daana, i ba'a na'a ka ni n tinzɔ wa yɛla ni? - dama u kyɛ tuma maa zaa n‑gyeeli ma. Yɛlim u, ni, u suŋŋma ma!”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Ɛɛh, Maata! I zaŋŋi la yɛla pam m‑mugsi i mammaŋŋa nimmoosi,

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: amaa bunyinni kɔkɔ n kpa talasi, dizugu Mariama n yiisi bunsuŋŋu, sɔ mun ku toaai n‑deaai di u saani.”

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: Dinzugu ka n yɛtti ya, ni, ya susima ka ya nin deaai yan susi seelli maa; ya booma ka ya nin nya yan boori seelli maa; ya boolima ka ba nin yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑ti ya.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɔ! ya nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban mari teesibeeri ya beebu ni, ka ni di zaa yɔri halli yarima gba mi yan zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑tiri ya biisi: dinŋɔna ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni bu ya zaŋŋi u Vuusim Kasi maa n‑ti ban susiri u?”

english_verse: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
translation_verse: Ka nyini mun kpalim m‑bɛ duu puuni n‑gyɛrigi u, ni, ‘Di daamni ma! Man ni m biisi pun kpɛ la doaa ziiya, n ku toaai n‑doaai n‑ti i bunseelli zaa!’

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Yaseeba ban mari ya biisi wa, n ya boosi ya, ni, i bii yi ti susi ziŋŋa i saani, i nin zaŋŋi waafu n‑ti u, bee?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya yi ti susi Naawunni, ya susim nŋɔa#:- ‘Ti Ba, kyɛ ka i yuuri du, Ka i naam kyɛ na.

english_verse: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
translation_verse: Ka kyɛri ti tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ti, Dama ti gba kyɛri ban tum ti taalli tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ba: Ka di kyɛ ka ti nya zamsigu nti lu.’ ”

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔnkam susira deaari ni, ŋɔnkam boora nyari ni, ka ŋɔnkam boonna ba nin yoaai n‑ti u.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Ya nyama: Niniva dima ŋɔn daa nya Anabi Gyoona yɛla ka di daa niŋi ba alahazibu seem la, lala ka zuna niriba nin nya man Ninsaala Bii yɛla ka di nin niŋi ba alahazibu.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa kur yɛtti anŋɔ maa, ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ zama maa puuni n‑tasi m‑booli, ni, “I ma sɔ ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai i, ka daa mɔ'asi i maa - ni u zugsuŋŋu!”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa ti katti arizimbeoo sɔ saani ka arizinni wa daa kyɛ ka doo maa kpalim n‑nyɛ zɔligu. Ka arizinni maa ŋɔn daa yi na ka kyɛ u maa, zɔligu maa daa tɔ'asiri ni, ka di daa niŋi zama maa alahazibu.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Baseeba mun daa zamsi u n‑susiri u, ni, u kyɛ ka alahazibu tunseelli yi saazugu na ka ba nya.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Yisa daa mi ba sufu ni teesa ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka dizugu u daa yɛli ba, “Tinseelli kam zaa kan timbiisi ŋɔn yi purigi n‑zabri ni taaba nin sa'am zaa, bee yiri dima yi zabri ni taaba, lala yiri maa nin ti lu.

english_verse: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
translation_verse: Dizugu ŋɔn Biyɛlizibuli muna, u yi ti za'asi u mammaŋŋa, ŋɔn gba ku toaai n‑zaani. Dama ya yɛliya, ni, Biyɛlizibuli yiiko ka n zaŋŋi n‑katti arizimbeeri.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna n yi zaŋŋi la Biyɛlizibuli yiiko n‑katti arizimbeeri maa, ka ya karimbiseeba ban katti ba la zaŋŋi ŋɔni dinni n‑katta? Dizugu ban nin ti kari ya yɛttɔ'a wa sariya n‑lusi ya.

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: Yaa daana yi ti mari u tɔppu neema n‑guri u yiri, sɔsɔ ku niŋi u marigu seela.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima Farisii dima zugu! Dama ya boori la tooni ziziisi ya yi ti la'am Naawunni la'aŋŋu duri maa ni, ka na boori ni sɔkam zaa puusiri ya daasi puuni.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di saha maa ka Naawunni zaaligu baŋŋnisɔ daa labsi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, “N Karimba! i yi tɔ'asiri nŋɔa, i tuuri la tirim gba m‑paasira.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: “Tɔ; ka arizimbɛsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u daa kpɛ dɔsɔ puuni n‑su u. Ka ba daa yiisi u ŋɔn daa bɛ dɔsɔ puuni maa n‑kari u m‑basi. Ka u daa yi n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinni yoo ni, tinkuuma maa ni, m‑boori vuusim ziiya, halli nti gyɛa. Ka u daa yɛli, ni, ‘See ka n labi n doo maa saani - yiri seaa man daa yi ka kyɛ la ni!’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Ka u daa ti labi na n‑nya ka ba pun pii yiri maa kasikasi n‑sɛ'asi ka suŋŋa.

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: Dizugu u daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi u arizintaaba ayɔpɔi seeba ban bɛ n‑gaari u mammaŋŋa, ka ba daa kpɛ nti bɛ bɛni. Di saha maa ka di daama beesigu daa bɛ pam n‑gaari din daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ seem maa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli u, “Di pa lala. Zugsuŋŋu dima n nyɛ baseeba ban wum Naawunni yɛligu ka sakkri n‑dɔla.”

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Ka di gyɛri Farisii maa ŋɔn daa nya ka Yisa bu daŋŋi m‑peaai u nuusi ka zinni ni u di maa zugu.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ yarima Farisii dima zugu! Dama ya yiisiri la naanzuuwa ni kpaligu ni zɛvaari zaa zakka, amaa ka lee n‑kyɛri yɛlimaŋni tumpu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi niriba, ni wurilim zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni, m‑basira. Ya yi ti teaari anŋɔ ka bu laa n‑tam seesi la, di yi sɔa.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Sahanseaa ka zama pam daa la'am na m‑maali n‑zoaari pam, ka Yisa daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Zuna niriba wa n nyɛ ninvugbeeri. Ba ba'a kur bɛ la alahazibu nyaabu ni, ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛla, amaa ba ku nya sɛseelli m‑paasi la Gyoona alahazibu yɛla zugu.

english_verse: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a-naaba sɔ ŋɔn daa su nudirigu lugri tinseaa la daa yi wa'aligu n‑kyɛ na ni u ti wum NaaSulɛmaana yɛm yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu u nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari, dama ŋɔn gaari NaaSulɛmaana bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.

english_verse: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Niniva dima gba daa wum Gyoona wa'azu yiisigu la ka daa kyɛ ba tumbeeri m‑basi ka niŋi tuuba, ka dizugu ba nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari, dama ŋɔn gaari anabi Gyoona bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.”

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Sɔ na zi n‑nyoaai firila n‑zaŋŋi kuriba m‑pili ka, amaa u zaŋŋi ka n‑tɔm la bunseelli zugu ni ban kam kpeeri na zaa nin nya neesim maa.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Nira nini n nyɛ hoo u ningbina firila: i nini yi neaai suŋŋa, i ningbina zaa mari la neesim, nyain! Amaa a yi ti sa'amna, i ningbina zaa bɛ la zibsim ni.

english_verse: So be sure your light isn't darkness.
translation_verse: Dizugu di kyɛ ka neesim din bɛ i ni maa ti leebi zibsim.

english_verse: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
translation_verse: Yarim gyɛra wa! Pa Naawunni ŋɔn daa maali nyaaŋŋa la n daa maali puuni maa gba?

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu! Dama ya maanni la gbala n‑tiri anabidima, amaa ya yaadima n daa nyɛ ban daa ku ba.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Tɔ; Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ yɛttɔ'a maa ni ka Farisisɔ daa zuusi u, ni u kyɛ u yiri maa na ka ba ti la'am n‑di bundirigu: ka u daa kyaŋŋi ka ba daa ti zinni ni ba di.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma daa yɛli u, “Yarima Farisii dima peaari la kɔbeesi ni laasi nyaaŋŋa, amaa ka ya puuni na paali ninvugfaabu ni yɛlibeeri.

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya zaŋŋma din bɛ laasi maa puuni n‑tiri faradima, ka bunseelli kam zaa nin leebi kasi n‑ti ya.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu! Dama ya ŋmani la hoo gbala seesi niriba ŋɔn noori n‑gatta ka bu mi, ni, gballi nla.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa gyɛrigi u, ni, “Yarima gba ban nyɛ zaaligu baŋŋniba, zugbeoo bɛ ya zugu! Dama ya zaŋŋi la zitibsa n‑ziinni niriba, ziseesi ban ku toaai n‑zi, ka ya sɔ mun pun ku wukki ziiri maa halli beela n‑suŋŋi ba.

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ya wulisiri, ni, ya nini tigi ya yaadima ŋɔn daa niŋi seem la - ni, ban daa ku ba, ka yarima mun n maanni ba gbala.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Naawunni yɛm yɛla puuni ba daa pun yɛli, ni, Naawunni nin tum anabidima ni tuumudima na, ka ya nin ku baseeba ka kari baseeba nti namsi ba pam.

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Dizugu zuna niriba nin ti zi taalli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban daa ku anabidima zaa yɛla maa, duniya piiligu ni la sa,

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: n‑yi Eebli kuubu ni, halli nti paai Zakarii ban daa ku sɔ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, Dutitaari ni saramaaligu zii maa sunsukkuni la. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, Naawunni siri nin zaŋŋi ba taalli n‑ziili zuna niriba wa.

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: Zugbeoo bɛ ya zaaligu baŋŋniba wa zugu! Dama ya zaŋŋi la bamsim bamsigu za'anɔri saafibii m‑basi. Yarima mammansi ku sakki n‑kpɛa, ka ya ta'ari ban kpeeri soori.”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi bɛni, Farisii dima ni zaaligu karimbadima daa piili m‑boosiri u boosa ni ba lugsi u noori ni.

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Ba daa guusiri ni ba gbaai u, u yɛttɔ'a puuni.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: Ka niriba tusa tusa ŋɔn daa kur la'amni Yisa saani na halli nti mugi taaba - halli nti noori taaba gba - Yisa daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba ni ba gbaam ba maŋŋa suŋŋa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Farisii dima dabina yɛla - di nyɛ la ba birikɔnsi.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni yi zaŋŋi veelim wa tataabu n‑niŋi mɔseesi an bɛ moo ni zuna ka beoo ka a sa nin nyɔ bugum m‑basi, wula ka u ku yeeli yarima sutura? Yarim yɛdda kɔŋŋniba wa!

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Ay, see ka ya zaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi, ka u nin zaŋŋi bunseelli din kpalim zaa m‑paasi ya.”

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: “N ya yɛli ya ni, yarim ban nyɛ n zɔdima la, ni, yan di zɔri ninvugseeba ban kuuri niriba ningbina, ka ku laa n‑toaai n‑niŋi ba seelli m‑paasi.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Halli ya zɔbri muna, Naawunni mi a gba yɛla, ka baŋŋi a zaa yinni yinni kaaligu ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Yan di zɔri dabeem, dama ya mari bukyi pam n‑gaari niinsi maa zaa.”

english_verse: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
translation_verse: Ni, “Yarima n bibisi wa, yan di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya, dama ti Dugma Naawunni yuubu nyɛ la ni u zaŋŋi u naam maa beebu n‑ti ya.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Ya koosim ya neema zaa ka zaŋŋi lala ligri maa n‑ti faradima. Ya maalim la'abooriseesi an bu kpiiri n‑ti ya mansi, ka zaŋŋi a n‑su ya arizana ni arizikki n‑dooli: bɛni ka a ku fiilim, dama na'ayigsi ku toaai m‑paai a, ka leelisi gba ku toaai m‑paai a n‑ŋɔbi.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: dama ba boosa maa saha ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi nin ti wulisi ya yan ya labsi seem.”

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: Amaa ka Naawunni yɛli u, ‘Nyin gyɛrigu wa! Yuŋŋu wa zaazaa ka n nin deaai i nyɔvuri, ka bunseelli zaa nyin mari n‑kuunni maa nin leebi ŋɔni dinni?’

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya, ni, ŋɔnkam zaani niriba tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la n dinni, man Ninsaala Bii gba nin ti zaani Naawunni malaakadima tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la man gba dinni.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔnkam mun yi za'asi ma niriba tooni, man gba nin za'asi u Naawunni malaakadima tooni.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: Ba yi ti zaŋŋi ya n‑taasi la'aŋŋu duri ni, bee nadima saani, yan di boosiri ya sufu ni yan ya labsi seem bee yan ya gyɛrigi seem,

english_verse: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dɔsɔ daa zɛ zama maa sunsukkuni n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, “N Karimba, yɛlim m beeri wa ka u purigi ti ba faari n‑ti ma, ti ba ŋɔn daa kpi ka kyɛ u arizikki la tɔrli kan nyɛ n dinni!”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli doo maa, “N zɔa, ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ya sarikatta bee ya faariputta?”

english_verse: Then he said to the crowd, “Don't be greedy! Owning a lot of things won't make your life safe.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, ba gbaam ba mansi ka di boori ligri nti yakki, dama ninsaala nyɔvuri bu bɛ la u marigu ŋɔn nyɛ seem ni kɔkɔa.

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: ka daa yɛli u sufu ni, ni, ‘Wula ka n ya n‑niŋi, dama n ka luginseelli man ya n‑zaŋŋi m bundirigu wa n‑kpeesi?’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: Ka yɛli, ‘Man nin niŋi seem nŋɔa: n nin wurim n napaari wa ni m buya zaa ka takki m‑mɛ bunkara ka a gaari a, n‑zaŋŋi m bundira wa zaa n‑niŋi.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, “Dinzugu n ya yɛli ya, ni, yan di zaŋŋi yɛla m‑mugsi ya mansi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya nin niŋi seem n‑nya bundirigu n‑di, bee n‑nya sutura n‑yɛa, - yan di fabli anŋɔ zaa yɛla;

english_verse: Life is more than food or clothing.
translation_verse: dama nyɔvuri gaari bundirigu, ka ningbina gaari bunyeerigu.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: Ya teesima pɔ'a-veelli-ku-siisi-koom yɛla: ya gɔsim n‑nya kan zoaari seem, ka bu tumna, ka mun bu waanni gumni, bee, n‑wugri tana n‑seera, amaa n yɛtti ya ni, ni, halli NaaSulɛmaana ni u naam gyirima zaasa gba daa na zi m‑bɔbi suturiveela kaman mɔpuuma wa yinni veelim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
translation_verse: Dinzugu yan di fabinna, bee n‑teesiri yan ya di seelli yɛla, bee yan ya n‑nyu seelli.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Duniya wa ninvugseeba ban bu mi Naawunni la bɛ la anŋɔ zaa nɔba karibu ni - bee, m ma'ai ni? Amaa yarima mari ya Ba Naawunni, ka u mi halli, ni, ya boori anŋɔ zaa.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Amaa daabsɔ nin ti teesi u sufu ni, ni, u dugma maa yuuya pam ni kulibu na, ka dizugu u piili m‑buuri u taaba, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba, ka dira, ka nyuuri daam m‑bugra.

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Ka boosi ba, ni, “Ammi, ya teesiya, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti zaŋŋi nangbanyinni n‑ti duniya wa dima? Ay! - n yɛtti ya, ni, nangbankpeemni ka n taasiri na.

english_verse: Be ready and keep your lamps burning
translation_verse: Ni, “Dinŋɔna ya barim n‑zaani: ya bɔbma ka nyoaai ya firiladima m‑maali siri,

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Daabseeba ban bu ti gbisira, ka ziya n‑guri ba dugma halli ka u ti paai na - ay! ba nin ti mari sufupeelli pam.” Ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya wa, ni, u nin yeaai u kparigu maa ka kyɛ ka ba zinni ka u gyeemni ba.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: U yi ti yuui halli ni yuntasuusi bee bɛkyikyeoo saha ka kuli na ka ba niŋi siri n‑guri u lala, ba siri nin nya sufupeelli pam.

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Ya baŋŋma, ni, yiddaana yi da mi sahanseaa na'ayiga ŋɔn ya kyɛ u yiri ma na, u naa n‑guri ka ku kyɛ ka u kabi n‑kpɛ u yiri maa ni na.

english_verse: So always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya gba niŋma siri, dama Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti kyɛ na sahanseaa yan bu teesiri u yɛla.”

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Piita daa boosi Yisa, ni, “N Dugma, tirim ka i nyɛsi nyɛsiri wa n‑tiri ti, bee sɔkam zaa?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli “Ninvugŋɔni n lee n‑nyɛ daabsɔ ŋɔn mari yɛm ni yɛdda? - ka u dugma zaŋŋi u yiri maa fukumsi n‑niŋi u nuu ni, ni u basiri mɔnni n‑tiri ba, mɔnni basibu saha.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Ŋɔna n nyɛ daabsɔ ŋɔn nin nya sufupeelli, dama u dugma kuli na m‑paai u u lala tuma maa ni: bee, pa ŋɔna?

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya, ni, u nin ti zaŋŋi u bunseelli kam fukumsi n‑niŋi lala daabri nuu ni.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: Di daama dugma maa nin ti paai na dahinseaa daabri maa ŋɔn bu teesiri u yɛla, bee sahanseaa ŋɔn bu mi u yɛla. Di saha ka u dugma maa nin ti darigi u tubri ka ŋmaasi u bisibisi, ka nin ti zaŋŋi u m‑paasi ninvugseeba ban ka yɛdda maa zugu.”

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: Ni, “Amaa daabsɔ ŋɔn mi u dugma yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem ka ku sakki ni u maali siri n‑niŋi di, ŋɔn nin ti nya buuri pam:

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: ka daabsɔ ŋɔn bu mi u dugma yuubu ka tum din sumsi la buuri, ŋɔn mun buuri maa nin baala. Ban ti tisi ninvugsɔ pam, ŋɔn saani ka ba nin ti laa m‑boosi pam yɛla. Ka ninvugsɔ ban zaŋŋi tuma pam fukumsi n‑niŋi u nuu ni, ŋɔn gba saani ka ba nin ti boori ni ba wum u tuma pam tuma lahabaari.”

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Bugum zugu ka n kyɛ na, ni n zaŋŋi di n‑tɔ n‑niŋi duniya wa puuni, ka n sufu boori di da pun nyoaai.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: N yɛla laa n‑ŋmani hoo kɔsuubu, ka a na zɛya n‑guri ma, ka di nyɛ la din kyɛ ka n sufu bu dɔya see ka a ti niŋi n‑naai.”

english_verse: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna, yiri puuni niriba anu nɔya nin ti wɔligi taaba: ba niriba ata nin naai noori n‑za'asi babayi la, ka babayi gba nin ti za'asi babata maa.

english_verse: Fathers and sons will turn against one another, and mothers and daughters will do the same. Mothers-in-law and daughters-in-law will also turn against each other. Knowing What To Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Doo ni u bii nɔya nin ti wɔligi taaba, ka laa n‑za'asi taaba; ka pɔ'a ni u bipuŋŋa nin ti za'asi taaba, halli pɔ'a ni u bii pɔ'a nin ti za'asi taaba gba.”

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa ti yɛli zama maa, ni, “Ya yi ti nya ka sangbana doaari n‑yiiri gbinni ya'ari na, ya yɛtti, ni, saa nin mi, ka ka siri nin mi.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Ka ya yi ti nya ka pɛbsigu zeeri n‑yiiri nuzaa ya'ari na, ya nin yɛli, ni, wɔligu nin niŋi, ka di siri nin niŋi.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Yarima birikɔ'anu dima wa! Di kyaŋŋi la wula ka ya baŋŋi duniya wa ni sangbana an wulisiri seem, ka ku laa n‑toaai m‑baŋŋi zuna saha wa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem?”

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Ni, “Bɔ n lee n‑niŋi ka ya mammaŋŋa bu baŋŋni din tuusi? Sɔ yi ya zaŋŋi i n‑taasiri sarikatta saani, ka nyin ni ŋɔn na bɛ soori zugu n‑kyɛnna, see ka i kyɛ ka ya naai noori m‑maali yɛttɔ'a maa; ka di yi pa lala u nin zaŋŋi i n‑kyaŋŋi sarikatta maa saani, ka sarikatta maa ti zaŋŋi i n‑niŋi dansarika gura nuu ni, ka gura maa mun naayi zaŋŋi i n‑kpeesi dansarika duu.

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Din saha maa ka ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ bɛni daa tɔ'asiri Galilii dimseeba yɛla: ba daa yɛli Yisa, ni, Gɔmna Pailiti daa ku ba, ban daa maanni ba sara sahanseaa la, ka ba ziim ni bunkɔbri ziim maa daa gyɛtti taaba.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi m‑boosi ba, ni, “Kala, ya teesiya, ni, Galilii dima wa n daa nyɛ ninvugbeeri ni ala'akyi dima n‑gaari ba Galilii taaba zugu ka ba daa namsi ba dinŋɔ maa?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: N yɛtti ya, ni, Ay! Amaa faasee ka ya zaa takki ya teesibeeri ka kyɛ ya tunkyɔ'ama zaa: di yi pa lala ya gba nin baasi yɔri hoo ban daa baasi seem maa.

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Ka Naawunni naam maa yɛla muna - a ŋmani la bɔa?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: N yɛtti ya, ni, Ay! Faasee ka ya takki ya teesibeeri ka kyɛ ya tunkyɔ'ama maa: di yi pa lala ya gba nin baasi yɔri hoo ban daa baasi seem maa.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka u yi yɛli ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri, ni, “Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, m‑mari u tiwanni u gaadi puuni. U daa ti kyaŋŋi ni u tɔsi tii maa wala ka bu nya halli tiwalli yinni.
