english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ‘M Bapira, kyɛli ka yuumni yinni m‑paasi yaasa, ka n nin gbi n‑gyiligi ka, n‑niŋi tam n‑niŋi ka, ka yuundaari ka ka ti wali wala, tɔ!

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Ka mun yi na bu ti wali, dinŋɔna nyin ŋmaama ka m‑basi.’ ”

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka Vuusim Dabsiri dahinseaa Yisa daa ti bɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑wulisiri ba,

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa nya pɔ'a maa, u daa booli u na n‑yɛli u, “M ma, i dooru laa n‑ka i ni!”,

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma labsi u, ni, “Yarima birikɔ'anu dima wa! Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa ni ka ya nira zaa bu lɔrigri ya naafu bee ya buŋŋa n‑kyɛnni n‑nyuusiri ba koom?

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa yɛli dinŋɔ n‑naai, vi daa gbaai ban za'asiri u yɛla maa zaa, amaa zama maa daa mari la sufupeelli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa ŋɔn daa tum tunveela maa.

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Tɔ. Di saha maa ka Farisiseeba daa kyɛ Yisa saani na nti yɛli u, “I bu ya yi kpa na n‑gaari n‑kyaŋŋi luginseaa? Dama NaaHɛroodi boori ni u ku i.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Amaa di kpa ma la talasi ni n siri yi soori zugu zuna ni beoo ni daari, dama di ku toaai n‑niŋi ni ba ku anabisɔ kam zaa tinseelli ni, ka di pa la Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni.”

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Ni, “Yɛlimaŋni, ninvugseeba m bɛ ni n‑kpalim nyaaŋŋa ka nin ti leebi ban daŋŋni tooni, ka ban mun daŋŋi tooni la nin ti kpalim nyaaŋŋa!”

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑ti ba, ni, “Yɔbaa n kur nyɛ u! Ya kyɛma nti yɛli u, ni, zuna ni beoo n na kur nin katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni, ka tibri baaridima, ka daba ata daari n nin paai n tuma zaa tɔriga.

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: - A ŋmani la hoo zinzambeelli nira ŋɔn zaŋŋi m‑buri u puu ni; ka ka buli na n‑leebi hoo tiiya, ka niinsi tɔmni ka wula maa zugu.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Ka daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, “Bɔ yaasa ka n nin zaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla maa m‑ma'asi?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Tɔ: Yisa daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli tinsi ni tinkpansi n‑wulisira; ka gatti n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: “Ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑kpɛ za'anɔmugriga maa ni, dama niriba pam nin boori ni ba kpɛ, ka ku nyaŋŋi.”

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Ni, “Yiri maa daana yi sarigi n‑doaai n‑zaani ka kpari za'anɔri maa, ya nin zaani za'anɔri tooni sa m‑boonna ka kpaasira, ka nin susiri, ni, ‘Ti daana, yoaam n‑ti ti!’; amaa u nin labsi n‑yɛli ya, ni, ‘M bu mi yan yi seela na!’

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: Ka ya nin yɛli u, ni, ‘Tirima ni nyini n daa la'am n‑dira, ka nyuura, halli ka i daa dɔli ti tiŋŋa sɔya n‑wulisira.’

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: Ka u nin labsi n‑yɛli ya, ‘M bu mi yan yi seela na. Ya kyɛma sa, yarim tumbeeri dima wa!’

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Ka ya nin paai ya'aseelli niriba ŋɔn bɛ ni n‑kumna ka ŋɔbri ba nyina la. Ka ya nin ti nya Ibrahim ni Yisahaaku ni Yaakubu, m‑paasi anabidima maa zaa, ka ba ti bɛ Naawunni naam ziiya, ka ba zaŋŋi yarima n‑yiisi m‑basi sa.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Niriba nin ti yi wuntampusiri n‑kyɛ na, ni wuntanluri, ni nudirigu ni nuzaa luga zaa, nti zinni ba ziziisi ni, Naawunni naam kyuu diibu maa ni.”

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gbaai kunkuma Gyɛrusalɛm zugu, ni, “Gyɛrusalɛm, Gyɛrusalɛm! Waay, waay, waay! Nyin ŋɔn kuuri anabidima ka zaŋŋni ban daa tum tuumudimseeba i saani maa n‑lɔbri ba kuga wa! N da kur boori la n la'asi i niriba lugri yinni ni, kaman nɔnyaaŋŋa ŋɔn la'asi u biisi ka yirigri u kpunkpama n‑ligri ba seem la; amaa ya bu sakki n‑ti ma.

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Ya gɔsima! Ya Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa yi kpalim la daboo. Dinzugu n yɛtti ya ni, ni, ya ku laa n‑nya ma yaasa halli ka ya ti yɛli ‘Zugsuŋŋu bɛ n‑ti ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na!’ ”

english_verse: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
translation_verse: Ka ba nya ka baarisɔ daa bɛ Yisa tooni, ka u ningbina zaa daa kur fuusi u dooru maa zugu.

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi zaaligu baŋŋniba ni Farisii dima, ni, “Di tuusiya ni nira kyɛ ka baara kpaŋŋi Vuusim Dabsiri daari, bee di bu tuusi?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Ka ba zaa daa sini u. Ka Yisa daa tibi doo maa, ka ti u soori ni u labi n‑kuli.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kɔŋŋi u labsigu.

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa gɔsi n‑nya ban daa booli ninvugseeba ni ba la'am na nti di maa, ka ba kur boori gyirima ziziisi n‑zintira. Ka daa yɛli ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri, ni,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: “Ba yi ti booli i ni i kyɛm amriya kyuu diibu ni na, di daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑zinni gyirima zizii ni, dama di pa seem ŋɔn mari gyirima pam n‑gaari i nin ti kpɛ na,

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn duusi u maŋŋa, ba nin sigsi u; amaa ŋɔn sigsi u maŋŋa muna, ba nin duusi lala nira pam.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: U daa laa n‑yɛli dɔsɔ ŋɔn booli ba maa, ni, “I yi ti boonni niriba ni ba kyɛ i yiri na n‑di kyuu bundirigu, di booli i zɔdima, bee i dɔ'ariba, bee i zizii kpɛseeba ban nyɛ bundaandima. Lala niriba nin ti booli i gba ba maŋtoori kyuusi diibu ni, ka lala ka ba nin yɔ i samni maa.

english_verse: They cannot pay you back. But God will bless you and reward you when his people rise from death. The Great Banquet (Matthew
translation_verse: I yi niŋi lala i nin nya laara. Lala niriba ku nyaŋŋi n‑labsi n‑yɔ i. I nin nya zugsuŋŋu, dama Naawunni nin ti labsi n‑yɔ i, ninvugsuma yi ti neaai kum ni sahanseaa maa.”

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: Ka daabri maa daa kyaŋŋi ka labi na n‑yɛli u, ‘N dugma, n niŋi nyin yɛli seem maa, amaa paalu na kur kpalim ni.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ninvugsɔ n daa bɛ ban la'amni n‑zintiri maa sunsukkuni, ka daa wum lala, ka u daa yɛli u, “Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn la'am n‑diri kyuu maa diibu, Naawunni naam kyɛnni saha na!”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Amaa ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “Wumma! Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, n‑ya di u kyutitaari, ka tum m‑booli niriba pam.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka kyuu maa diibu daari ŋɔn daa ti paai, u daa tum u daabsɔ ni u kyaŋŋi m‑booli ŋɔn pun zuusi ninvugseeba la, ni, ba kyɛm na, ni, di zaa siri biya.

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.

translation_verse: N ya n‑yɛli ya ni, ni, ninvugseeba ban daa daŋŋi n‑zuusi maa zaa - ba sɔsɔ ku lam n kyuu bundirigu maa, halli beela!’ ”

translation_verse: Ni, “Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ku sakki n‑kpikki u toori kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli n nyaaŋŋa ku toaai n‑niŋi n nyaandɔla.

translation_verse: Dizugu ninvugsɔ zaa ŋɔn bu za'asi u seelikam zaasa yɛla ku toaai n‑niŋi man nyaandɔla.”

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba m‑paasi, ni, “Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni m‑mari u bidibsi ayi.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: Ni, “Ŋɔnkam kari u pɔ'a ka laa n‑di pɔ'asɔ tumni la zina, ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn di lala pɔ'a maa gba tumni la zina.”

translation_verse: dama m mabiisi anu na bɛ ni, ka u kyaŋŋi nti sɛ'asi ba ni ba di kyɛ na n‑kpɛ man ŋɔn bɛ wahala seaa wa ni.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: Ka bundaana maa laa n‑yɛli, ‘N Yaaba Ibrahim, pa lala. Amaa sɔ mun yi da neaai kum ni n‑kyaŋŋi ba saani nti yɛli ba, ba da naa n‑takki ba beesigu n‑niŋi tuuba.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Ka niriba awai la kpalim m‑bɛ la yani? Bee sɔsɔ bu labi na ni u ti pɛ'ai Naawunni na'ala tinzuŋŋu nira wa kɔkɔa?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya yi mari yɛdda ka di sa'ai hoo zinzam beelli, ya nin toaai n‑yɛli kinkaŋŋa wa, ni, ka voaama ka zaazii wa ni n‑kyaŋŋi nti sa mɔ'ari puuni, ka di nin niŋi lala.”

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba, ni, “Ya puuni ŋɔni n lee m‑mari u daabri ka u koora, bee, ka u guri la bunkɔbri, ka u ti yi moo na ka u di yɛli u, ‘Kyɛm na nti zinni n‑di bundirigu.’

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Bee, sɔ di yɛli, ‘Nyama, di bɛ kpa wa!’, bee, ‘Di bɛ kpa sa!’ Dama Naawunni naam pun bɛ la ya saani.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Saha kyɛnni na ka ya nin ti boori ni ya nya man Ninsaala Bii saha wa dabsiri yinni gba, ka ku nya ka.

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Ka daabri maa ŋɔn tum lala maa, i nin puusi u dizugu? Ay! ban zaali u tunseesi maa nla.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Di ku toaai n‑niŋi ka yɛttɔ'abeeri bu paari ya na, yɛttɔ'aseesi an lusiri niriba n‑kpeesiri tumbeeri ni. Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn taasi a na muna - waay, waay, waay!

english_verse: would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their neck.
translation_verse: Ba yi da zaŋŋi neeri n‑lɔ di daama nangoori ni ka zaŋŋi u n‑lɔsi mɔ'azuliŋŋu ni, di da sɔ u zugu n‑gaari u kyɛ ka bibisi wa yinni lu n‑kpɛ tumbeeri ni.”

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ya gbaam ya mansi suŋŋa! I mabiiya yi tum i taalli, nyin sɛ'asim u; ka u yi deaai u galinsi maa, nyin kyɛri u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Halli u yi ti tum i taalli napɔmbuyɔpɔi dahinyinni ka ŋmaligi buyɔpɔi n‑yɛli i, ni, ‘N galimiya.’ nyin kyɛri u taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u.”

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka tuumudima maa daa yɛli ba dugma, ni, “Paasim ti yɛdda!”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Kala, i ku yɛli u, ni, u kyɛm na n‑dugi bundirigu na nti zaali ka i di ka nyu pɔi, ka u mun naayi kyaŋŋi nti maali u siri n‑zinni n‑di ka nyu?

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Dizugu lala n nyɛ di#:- ya gba yi niŋi ban zaali ya ni ya niŋi seem maa zaa, ya yɛlima, ni, ‘Ti bu sumni pɛ'abu, dama ti tum la ti tuma ŋɔn nyɛ seelli kɔkɔa.’ ”

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti yi soori zugu n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm, u daa dɔli Samɛrii ni Galilii tingbana sunsukkuni n‑gatta.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: N‑kpeeri tinkpanseaa ni, ka kɔnsi piiya daa ti kyirigi u.

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nya ba lala maa, u daa yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛma nti zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑wulisi maalimaanniba.” Ban daa ŋmaligi n‑kyɛnni maa, ba daa nya alaafɛ zaa.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Ka ba ninvugyinni ŋɔn daa ti nya ka u kpaŋŋi maa n‑naai,

translation_verse: Ka ŋmaligi n‑yɛli u, “Doaama! Ka i yɛdda niŋbu zugu ka i kpaŋŋi.”

english_verse: The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky.
translation_verse: Dama Ninsaala Bii dabsiri maa yi ti paai, u nin ŋmani hoo saa ŋɔn yi nya'asiri la, n‑neaai sangbana gbinni n‑yi ya'ari wa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya'ari wa sa.

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Niriba nin ti yɛli ya, ni, ‘Nnya kpa sa!’, bee, ‘Nnya kpa wa!’ - yan di kyaŋŋi, ya mun di dɔli ba.

translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa boosi u, “Ti dugma, yani zii nla?” Ka u yɛli ba “Bunkpiiŋŋu ŋɔn bɛ luginseaa, bɛni ka zuusi la'amni taaba m‑bɛ ni.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus when God's kingdom would come. He answered, “God's kingdom isn't something you can see.
translation_verse: Farisiseeba daa ti boosi Yisa, ni, “Bɔ saha ka Naawunni naam maa ti ya m‑piili?” Ka u gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, “Naawunni naam piiligu pa la niriba ŋɔn gɔsiri n‑nyari bunseelli.

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Amaa faasee ka u daŋŋi n‑di wahala pam pɔi, ka zuna niriba ti za'asi u yɛla ka zeem u.

translation_verse: Ka din daa kur niŋi seem Nuhu saha la, lala ka di ti ya niŋi Ninsaala Bii gba dabsiri maa daari.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Niriba daa diri ni ka nyuura di dabsiri maa, ka diri pɔ'aba ka zaŋŋni ba bipunsi n‑tiri dɔppa, halli nti paai Nuhu ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ nyarintitaari maa ni dahinseaa maa. Di nyaaŋŋa ka koom daa kyɛ na nti di ba zaa.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: halli nti paai Lɔti ŋɔn daa yi Sɔdoom tiŋŋa na dahinseaa maa, ka bugum daa yi saazugu na, ni farinwuta bugum, n‑laŋŋi n‑di ba zaa.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Lala ka di nin ti niŋi Ninsaala Bii gba dabsiri maa yi ti yi paalu.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: Dindaari maa ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ti bɛ piiya duu zugu ka u neema kpalim m‑bɛ duu puuni, u di yɛli, ni, u sigi na n‑kpɛ n‑zaŋŋi a. Ka ŋɔn mun bɛ puu ni, ŋɔn di labi u nyaaŋŋa n‑kuli.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Ya teaama din daa niŋi Lɔti pɔ'a seem la.

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam boori ni u tuligi u nyɔvuri nin ti sa'am ka m‑basi, ka ŋɔnkam sakki ni u nyɔvuri sa'amna, ŋɔn mun nin ti nya nyɔvuri ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: N yɛtti ya wa, ni, di daari maa ka niriba ayi nin ti la'am n‑dɔ bundoaa yinni zugu yuŋŋu, ka Naawunni nin ti kpikki ninvugyinni ka kyɛ ŋɔnla.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'aba ayi nin ti la'am n‑neemni ba kyi neeri zugu, ka Naawunni kpikki yinni ka kyɛ ŋɔnla.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa tɔ'asi ba yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri din wulisiri, ni, di sumni niriba susiri Naawunni saha kam, ka ba di gyɛ ka kyɛ susigu.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: U daa yɛli ba, ni, “Sarikattisɔ daa bɛ tinseaa. U daa bu zɔri Naawunni ka mun bu laa n‑kyɛ ka u ba'a bɛ ninsaalisɔ kam yɛlli ni.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka pukɔsɔ daa bɛ lala tiŋŋa maa ni, ka u kur zɛya n‑kyɛnni doo maa saani na, ni, u tɔ'asim u yɛttɔ'a maa n‑fa u u dindaana nuu ni.

english_verse: For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, “Even though I don't fear God or care about people,
translation_verse: Ka sarikatta maa daa yuui beela ka bu sakki ni u fa u m‑basi. Ka di daa niŋi lala ka u daa yɛli u sufu ni, ni, ‘Halli ni m bu zɔri Naawunni ka m ba'a ka ninsaala yɛla ni la zaa yɔri,

english_verse: I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.”
translation_verse: amaa pukoori wa ŋɔn kur mugsiri ma maa zugu, n nin kari u sariya ka fa u m‑basi, ka di yi pa lala u sahakam kyɛnni na wa nin ti ŋmɛ n nyɔya.’ ”

english_verse: The Lord said: Think about what that crooked judge said.
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya wumma sarikatta tumbeeri daana ŋɔn yɛli seem la.

english_verse: Won't God protect his chosen ones who pray to him day and night? Won't he be concerned for them?
translation_verse: Kala, ya teesiya, ni, Naawunni ku ti wum ninvugseeba ban kumni m‑paari u wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu, ni, u suŋŋma ba maa ka zɔ ba namboo, ka fa ba m‑basi?

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: N ya yɛli ya, ni, u nin fa ba m‑basi yoomyooma. Ka di zaa yɔri, man Ninsaala Bii yi ti labi duniya wa ni na, n nin nya ban mari yɛdda bɛni, bee? - Ay, m bu ya nya!”

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Tɔ. Yisa daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri n‑ti ninvugseeba ban gɔsi ba mansi, ni, ninvugsuma, ka mun gɔsiri ba taaba n‑zeemni ba. Ka nyɛsiri daa bɛ ni, ni,

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: Ka Farisii maa ŋɔn daa zɛ saazugu m‑puusi u wumpuusigu n‑ti u maŋŋa, ni, ‘Naawunni, m pɛ'ari i pam man pa la hoo niriba zaa m bɛ seem maa, dama ba zuura ka tumni taalli, ka faari taaba pɔ'aba - halli m pa la hoo lampodeaara wa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
translation_verse: Dama n loori noori dabsa ayi bɛkwai kam, ka yiisiri man nyari bunseelli kam zaa zakka.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli ba, “N ya yɛli ya ni, ni, di daa nyɛ la lampodeaara maa ŋɔn daa yi n‑kuli ka yi taalli ni, pa ŋɔnla. Dinzugu ŋɔnkam tibgri u maŋŋa nin fiilim, ka ŋɔnkam mun sigsi u maŋŋa nin nya tibsim.”

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Ba daa mari bileesi n‑kyɛ Yisa saani na ni u zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑siisi ba, ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa nya ban niŋni seem ka daa nyikki ba di zugu.

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: di daama nin ti nya laara n‑gaari anŋɔ zaasa pampaŋŋɔ; ka saha din kyɛnni na u nin ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari.”

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑zaŋŋi u nyaandɔliba pii ni ayi maa n‑yi kpaŋŋa nti yɛli ba, “Ya wumma, ti wa la Gyɛrusalɛm; ka anabidima ŋɔn daa sɔbi seelikam zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii yɛla la nin ti paai a naari.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka toondaansɔ daa boosi u, ni, “N Karimbasuŋŋu! N ya niŋi la wula n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa yɛli, “Anŋɔ zaa ka n daa pun dɔli ka na nyɛ na'akyimbila.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa wum lala ka yɛli u, “Yɛlli yinni n kpalim m‑pɔsigi i; see ka i koosi i marigu zaa n‑zaŋŋi n‑tɔri faradima, ka i arizikki maali n‑zoaai Naawunni saani. Ka kyɛ na n‑dɔli ma.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka doo maa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala u sufu daa sa'am pam, dama u daa nyɛ la bundaana ni pam.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa gɔsi u ka yɛli, “Yɛlimaŋni, di nin tɔ pam ni bundaana kpɛ Naawunni naam ni!

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Halli arakoomi nin ti kpɛ kparipiim voori ni m‑puli n‑yi, ka bundaana ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni u kpɛ Naawunni naam ni.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa wum ŋɔn yɛli seem maa daa boosi u, ni, “Ka ŋɔni n nin tuligi?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, “Yɛttɔ'aseesi an tɔ pam ninsaalidima saani maa pa la an tɔ Naawunni saani.”

english_verse: Peter said, “Remember, we left everything to be your followers!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Piita daa yɛli Yisa, ni, “Nyama, tirima kyɛ ti bunseelli kam ka dɔli i maa!”

english_verse: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
translation_verse: Ba nin zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ban pa ti butiri niriba la nuu ni, ni ba maali ansarisi n‑zeem ma,

translation_verse: ka tusi nantoori n‑niŋi ma, ka naayi feebi ma ka ku ma. Amaa daba ata daari n nin yisigi kum ni.”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni n‑ti paai Gyɛrikoo tiŋŋa, zoonsɔ zi soori gbiriŋŋu m‑maanni bara;

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: ka ti wum daadam ŋɔn daa gatti la damni ka boosi ni u baŋŋi din niŋna.

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛli u, ni, di nyɛ la Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, n‑gatti maa.

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Amaa u daa n‑tasi pampam n‑yɛli, “NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo!”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa bɛ tooni maa daa nyikki u n‑yɛli, ni, u fooma u vuuri. Amaa u daa laa n‑tasi pampam, ni, “NaaDauda biiya, zɔm ma namboo!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa kpalim n‑zaani ka yɛli, ni, ba zaŋŋma u na. Ka ŋɔn daa ti pɔri ba saani na, Yisa boosi u, ni,

english_verse: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
translation_verse: “Bɔ ka i boora ni n niŋi n‑ti i?” Ni, “M Bapira, m boori n laa n‑nyari yaasa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Neaama ka nyari yaasa! I yɛdda maa kyɛ ka i nini neaaya.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Ka u nini maa daa neaai ya'abi ka u gyeemni Naawunni ka dɔli Yisa ka ba kyɛnna. Ka zama maa zaa daa nya din niŋi maa ka daa pɛ'ari Naawunni.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Lala zugu u daa zɔ n‑gaari ba tooni nti du kinkaŋŋa ni u ti nya u, dama Yisa daa ya dɔli la bɛni soori maa n‑gaari.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti paai tii maa gbinni na, u kpalim n‑zaani ka gɔsi tii maa saazugu ka yɛli, “Zakyeoosi, sigma na yoomyooma, dama di kpa ma talasi ni n gbɛ i yiri ni zuna!”

english_verse: Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka Zakyeoosi daa sigi na yoomyooma n‑gyaari Yisa ni sufupeelli.

english_verse: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka niriba maa zaa daa nya Yisa ŋɔn kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ Zakyeoosi yiri maa, ka nyurimni ba kparima ni, n‑yɛtta, “U kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ tumbeeri daana wa yiri!”

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Amaa ka Zakyeoosi daa zɛ ti Dugma tooni n‑yɛli u, ni, “Nyama, pampaŋŋɔ n nin zaŋŋi n neema purigri n‑ti faradima, ka n yi daa fa sɔ buna yɔri, n nin laa n‑labsi di daama buna zii bunaasi zugu n‑ti u.”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “Zuna dabsiri wa tuliginsim paai i yiri wa ni na, dama nyin gba nyɛ la Ibrahim biiya.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Dama man Ninsaala Bii daa kyɛ na ni m bɔ n‑nya ban bɔrigi m‑basi la ka tuligi ba.”

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛli u, ‘Ti daana, u ligri napɔmpiiya pun bɛ la u saani!’

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka u daa kyaŋŋi u kyɛnni; amaa u tiŋŋa maa niriba daa bu boori u, ka ba daa tum niriba nti yɛli, ni, ba di diisi nira wa naam maa. Di zaa yɔri, u daa di naam maa.

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: “Ya kyɛm ti tooni nti kpɛ tinkpanseaa kan zɛ ya tooni sa la. Ya yi ti paai bɛni, ya nin nya buŋŋa ka u lɔ n‑zɛya, bumbilisɔ nira ŋɔn na zi m‑ba; ya lɔrigma u m‑mari na.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni maa daa kpɛ na nti yɛli, ‘N daana i ligri la nɔbi ni m‑paasi a taaba napɔmpiiya!’

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ŋɔn pa u zugu la kpɛ na nti yɛli u dugma maa, ni, ‘N daana, i ligri la nɔbi ni m‑paasi napɔmbunu.’

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka sɔ kyɛ na yaasa, ni, ‘N daana, nnya i ligri la nŋɔa. N daa zaŋŋi bɔbga m‑pɔbi a n‑dooli, dabeem ŋɔn daa mari ma la zugu.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: Tɔ! Ka bɔ n daa lee n‑niŋi i ka i daa bu zaŋŋi n ligri maa n‑dooli bankyi ni? - ka man da kuli na wa, n naa n‑kur nya nyoori m‑paasi n la'adaya maa zugu!’

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: - Ka daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli ban zɛ bɛni maa, ni, ‘Ya deaam ligri maa u saani n‑zaŋŋi m‑paasi sɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni na maa.’

english_verse: The king replied, “Those who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, ni, ‘N yɛtti ya ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari beela ya n‑nya m‑paasi, amaa ŋɔn ka seelli ŋɔna, halli ŋɔn mari seelli fiiŋ maa ka ba nin ti ya fa ka kyɛ u yɔri.’

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Ni, ‘Amaa n dindaandimseeba ban daa bu boori ni n di naam n su ba la, ya zaŋŋma ba zaa na n‑ku ba ka n nini nya.’ ”

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛsi ba nyɛsiri wa n‑naai, u daa doaai n‑gaari ba tooni n‑kpa Gyɛrusalɛm soori.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti pɔtti tinkpanseesi an boonni Bɛtfeegyi ni Bɛtani la, seesi an zɛ Ɔlivii Zoori la lugri na, u daa tum u nyaandɔliba ayi, ni,

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
translation_verse: Sɔ mun yi ya yɛli ya, ni, wula zugu ka ya lɔrigri u? yan yɛlim u, ni, ya Dugma m boori u.”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa tum ninvugseeba maa daa kyaŋŋi nti nya ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa lɔrigri buŋŋa maa sahanseaa ka ninvugseeba ban daa su u la daa boosi ba, ni, “Wula zugu ka ya lɔrigri u ni?”

english_verse: They answered, “The Lord needs it.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ti Dugma boori u ni.”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa mari u m‑paai Yisa saani na, nti yeaai ba kyinkyina m‑pa buŋŋa nyaaŋŋa maa, ka daa kyɛ ka Yisa ba u.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ba u n‑kyɛnna, niriba maa daa na zaŋŋi la ba kyinkyina n‑yɛligri soori maa zugu.

english_verse: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai luginseelli soori maa ŋɔn daa zuligri n‑sigri Ɔlivii Zoori la, u nyaandɔliba maa zaa daa piili n‑tasira pampam m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Sufupeelli pɛ'abu ka ba daa moonna, ban daa nya alahazibu tunkariseesi ŋɔn tum la zugu.

english_verse: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
translation_verse: Ka yɛtta#:- “Zugsuŋŋu m bɛ n‑ti nasɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri na! Sufudoaa m bɛ arizana ni! Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ saazugu mari gyirima pam!”

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Farisiseeba ban daa bɛ zama maa ni daa yɛli Yisa, ni,

english_verse: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti pɔtti Gyɛrusalɛm n‑gɔsi n‑nya tiŋŋa maa, u daa kum ka zugu, ni,

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: “Yarima Gyɛrusalɛm dima, zuna ya yi da kur baŋŋi din nin ti ya sufudoaa, di naa n‑sɔa amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya bɛ la zibsim ni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi di zii ni!”

english_verse: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
translation_verse: Ni, “Dama saha kyɛnni na ka ya dindaandima nin ti gbi tanni n‑dooli n‑gyiligi ya tiŋŋa maa zaa, ka ta'ai ka sɔya zaa, ka di mugsi ya pam.

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba nin ti daai ya gooma n‑lusi yarima ni ya biisi zugu, halli ni kugri yinni gba ku laa n‑kpalim m‑pa ka kpeaa zugu, dama ya daa bu baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn kyɛ na nti fa ya m‑basi sahanseaa maa.”

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa paai Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni nti kpɛ bɛni m‑piili n‑katti ban daa koosiri neema Yiri maa puuni n‑yiisiri ba.

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Ka yɛli ba, ni, “Di sɔbi n‑zaali, ni, ‘N Yiri wa nyɛ la Wunsusigu Yiri.’, amaa ya zaŋŋi ka n‑labgi la ‘ta'ayigsi ŋɔn sɔ'ari seela.’ ”

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Beoo kam zaa ka Yisa daa kpeeri Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisiri niriba, ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima ni karimbadima maa daa boori sɔseaa ba nin nya n‑ku u, ban ni tiŋŋa maa ni kpamma maa.

english_verse: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa bu mi ban ya niŋi seem, dama sɔkam zaa ba'a daa kur bɛ la u wulisigu maa ni.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa boori ni ba zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi Gɔmna nuu ni. Ba daa boosi u, ni, “Ti Karimba, ti mi, ni, i yɛttɔ'a ni i wulisigu kur dɔli la soori deede; i ba'a ka niriba yɛla ni, ka i bu zɔri ninsaala nini, amaa ka i kur wulisiri la Naawunni soori ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Saddusidima - ban yɛtti, ni, kum yisigri ka ni la - ba gba daa kyɛ na nti boosi u boosa.

english_verse: When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? All seven brothers had married her.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna, Zaadaari, dɔppa wa zaa yi ti yisigi kum ni, ba ŋɔni n nin nyɛ pɔ'a maa sira? - dama babayɔpɔi zaa daa di u.”

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: “Ŋɔni n ti i soori ni i tum lala tuma maa? Yɛlim ti! Bee, yiiko bɔ ka i mara?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I want to ask you a question.
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “N gba ya boosi ya la boosigu yinni. Ya yɛlim ma,

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: ŋɔni n daa ti Gyɔni soori ni u suuri niriba Naawunni koom? Di daa yi la Naawunni saani bee ninsaalidima saani?”

english_verse: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri taaba, ni, “Ti ya yɛli la wula? Ti yi yɛli, ni, Naawunni saani, u nin boosi ti, ni, bɔzugu ka ti daa bu niŋi Gyɔni yɛdda?

english_verse: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
translation_verse: Amaa ka ti yi sakki n‑yɛli, ni, ninsaalidima saani, niriba maa nin ti lɔbi ti kuga, dama sɔkam sakkri ni Gyɔni daa nyɛ la anabimaŋni.”

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa labsi n‑yɛli Yisa, ni, ba bu mi.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Dinŋɔna n gba ku yɛli ya ŋɔn ti ma soori ni n tumni anŋɔ zaa.”

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Ya baŋŋma seeba ban wulisiri zaaligu maa yɛla! Ba boori ba yeeri kparikara n‑gyinna, ka sɔkam zaa puusiri ba daasi ni. La'aŋŋu duri puuni ka ba boori la ba bɛ tooni.

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story: A man once planted a vineyard and rented it out. Then he left the country for a long time.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba nyɛsiri, ni, “Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, n‑daa sa u tiwanna puu, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi ninvugseeba nuu ni, ni ba tumni puu maa tuma n‑ti u, ka u daa gɔ ni n‑yuui beela.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka sahasuŋŋu maa u daa tum u daabsɔ ni u kyaŋŋi tuntumniba maa saani nti deaai puu maa diibu na n‑ti u. Amaa tuntumniba maa daa gbaai daabri maa m‑bu u ka kari u n‑labsi yɔri.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Amaa tuntumniba maa ŋɔn daa nya ka u kyɛnni na, ba ŋmɛ taaba saawara n‑yɛli, ni, ‘Ŋɔn nin di puu maa daana faari la nŋɔa. Ya kyɛ ka ti ku u ka faari maa nin leebi ti dinni!’

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna wula ka puu maa daana nin niŋi? U bu ya n‑kyɛ na nti ku tuntumniba wa zaa? Ka zaŋŋi ka n‑ti tuntunseeba?” Ka daadam maa ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, ba daa yɛli, ni, “Naawunni tuligma ti ni dinŋɔ taabu!”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Di saha ka karimbadima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa baŋŋi, ni, Yisa nyɛsiri galimni la bana, ka dizugu ba daa boori la ba gbaai u, amaa ba daa zɔri la daadam maa dabeem.

english_verse: Jesus' enemies kept watching him closely, because they wanted to hand him over to the Roman governor. So they sent some men who pretended to be good. But they were really spies trying to catch Jesus saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa gɔsiri u yɛla, ni ba pigsi n‑gbaai u, u yɛttɔ'a puuni. Ba daa tum ninvugseeba na ni ba yɔmsiri u, ni, ba nyɛ la yɛdda dima.

english_verse: “Show me a coin.” Then he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: “Ya tim ma la'afu na ka n nya. Tɔ, ka ŋɔni makkri m bɛ la'afu wa ni wa, bee ŋɔni yuuri n sɔbi m‑bɛ ka ni?” Ka ba yɛli, “NaaTitaari dinni.”

english_verse: Then he told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa laa n‑labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Dizugu ya zaŋŋma din nyɛ Naaba dinni n‑ti Naa maa, ka zaŋŋma din nyɛ Naawunni dinni n‑ti Naawunni.”

english_verse: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kɔŋŋi u yɛttɔ'a maa gbaari, ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem zama maa ni maa, ka u labsigu maa daa gyɛri ba pam ka ba daa bu laa n‑yɛli seelli.

english_verse: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: Ba daa yɛli u, ni, “Ti Karimba, anabi Musa daa sɔbi n‑ti ti n‑wulisi, ni, dɔsɔ beeri yi ti kpi ka kyɛ u pɔ'a ka u na bu dɔ'ai bii n‑ti u, see ka u di u beeri pukoori maa n‑dɔ'ai biisi n‑ti u beeri maa.

english_verse: There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children.
translation_verse: Tɔ; mabiisi ayɔpɔi n daa bɛ ni, ka ba kpeema daa di pɔ'a ka kpi ka ka biiya.

english_verse: The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn pa buyi, ni buta daana gba, daa di u,

english_verse: The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children.
translation_verse: halli ka babayɔpɔi maa zaa daa di u ka kpi ka bu dɔ'ai biiya.

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka pɔ'a maa gba kpi.

english_verse: Jesus answered: The people in this world get married.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Zuna niriba wa diri la pɔ'aba ka kunni dɔppa;

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, you have given a good answer!”
translation_verse: Ka karimbaseeba daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, “Ti Karimba, i yɛli suŋŋa!”

english_verse: In the book of Psalms, David himself says, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side
translation_verse: NaaDauda mun daa tɔ'asi Masiiya maa yɛla n‑sɔbi Yuuma bukku maa ni, ni#:- ‘Ti Dugma daa yɛli n Dugma maa, ni, “Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni,

english_verse: until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: Halli ka n nin zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni.” ’

english_verse: David spoke of the Messiah as his Lord, so how can the Messiah be his son?” Jesus and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka NaaDauda yi boonni ŋɔn Masiiya maa, ni, u Dugma, ka wula ka u nin toaai n‑nyɛ u ya'aŋŋa?”

english_verse: While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
translation_verse: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn na kur wumni u wulisigu maa, Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni,

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and then pray long prayers just to show off. These teachers will be punished most of all.
translation_verse: Ba faari la pukɔya yiya pɔi, ka naayi wulisiri ba maŋŋa m‑puusiri Naawunni n‑yuuri puusigu maa ni. Lala niriba maa nin ti nya galiŋŋu pam.”

english_verse: Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors.
translation_verse: Amaa ba nin daŋŋi n‑kari ya nti gbaai ya n‑taasi ba la'aŋŋu duri ni, ka kpari ya duu. Ba nin zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi nadima nuusi ni, ni gɔmnadima nuusi ni: man yuuri zugu ka ba nin niŋi ya lala.

english_verse: Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had.” The Temple Will Be Destroyed (Matthew
translation_verse: dama ba sɔkam yiisi la u marigu pam puuni n‑tisi, amaa pukoori wa ŋɔna - u zaŋŋi la ŋɔn mari seelli zaasa n‑tisi ka kpalim yɔri.”

english_verse: Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said,
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba daa tɔ'asiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa meebu ŋɔn veela seem, ni, kugveela m bɛ ni, ni niriba ŋɔn tisi bunsuma zaa m‑paasi.

english_verse: “Do you see these stones? The time is coming when not one of them will be left in place. They will all be knocked down.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, “Yan gɔsiri bunseesi wa zaa maa muna - dahinseaa kyɛnni na ka a zaa nin ti wurim m‑basi halli ka kugseaa ku kpalim n‑tɔm ka kpeaa zugu.”

english_verse: Some people asked, “Teacher, when will all this happen? How can we know when these things are about to take place?”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, “Ti Karimba, wula saha ka dinŋɔ nin niŋi? Bee bumbɔ nin ti niŋi n‑wulisi, ni, anŋɔ zaa ya m‑paai ti na?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Don't be fooled by those who will come and claim to be me. They will say, “I am Christ!” and “Now is the time!” But don't follow them.
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, “Ya baŋŋma suŋŋa, ka di kyɛ ka ba yɔmsi ya. Niriba pam nin zaŋŋi n yuuri n‑kyɛ na, ni, ‘Mani n nyɛ u!’ bee ‘Saha maa paaya!’ Yan di dɔli ba nyaaŋŋa!

english_verse: When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
translation_verse: Ya yi ti wumni tɔppu damni, bee zaba yɛla, yan di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya, dama see ka anŋɔ taabu daŋŋi pɔi, amaa dinŋɔ na bu wulisi, ni, naari paaya.”

english_verse: Nations will go to war against one another, and kingdoms will attack each other.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Tinsi dima nin yisigi n‑tuusi taaba tɔppu, ka nadima gba nin tuusi taaba.

english_verse: There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
translation_verse: Tingbaŋŋu maa nin damni kpa, ka ka nin damni kpa; ka kɔm nin ti lu, ni dooru m‑paasi; ka ba nin nya bunnyaara saazugu sangbana ni, ka a nin ti kpeesiri dabeem pampam.

english_verse: You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed.
translation_verse: Ya badima ni ya madima nin ti zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi ya dindaandima nuu ni, ka ya mabiisi gba nin ti niŋi lala, ni ya dɔ'ariba ni ya zɔdima. Ba nin ti ku yaseeba.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be hated by everyone.
translation_verse: Ba zaa nin ti yaai ya, man yuuri zugu:

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ya kyɛ ka ban bɛ Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu la ni maa ti zɔ n‑du zɔya ni; ka ban ti bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm puuni, ban zɔma n‑yi ka ni, halli tinkpansi dima mun di kpɛ ka puuni na.

english_verse: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed.
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya yi ti nya ka tɔbbiisi ziintiri n gyiligri Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa, yan baŋŋma, ni, tiŋŋa maa sa'aŋŋu saha paaya.

english_verse: This time of punishment is what is written about in the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Dama ‘tubdarigri saha’ n nyɛ di, hoo ban daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem, sahaseaa kan gaari la.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished.
translation_verse: Di saha maa nin ti kpeem pam n‑tisi pɔ'apuusi dima ni pɔ'aseeba ban mari biisi ka ba na mɔ'ara. Dama namsigu pam nin niŋi duniya wa ni, ka Naawunni sufu nin ti yisigi m‑basi tiŋŋa wa dima zugu.

english_verse: Then Jesus told them a story: When you see a fig tree or any other tree
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa nyɛsi yɛttɔ'anyɛsiri n‑ti ba, ni, “Ammi, ya bu baŋŋi kinkaŋŋa ni tiisi zaa yɛla?

english_verse: So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here.

english_verse: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?

english_verse: Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Agbaa saha daa pun paai, ka tingbaŋŋu maa zaa daa niŋi zimm, ka zibsim daa ligi zaa n‑yuui m‑paai zaanoori hoo kurigu ata saha,

english_verse: Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa mari niriba ayi ban nyɛ tumbɛtumniba ni ba ti ku ba, m‑paasi Yisa zugu.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
translation_verse: Ka daadam daa zɛ kpaŋŋa ka gɔsiri u. Amaa ka tiŋŋa maa ni kpamma daa maanni u ansarisi, n‑yɛtta, “Ni, u faari la niriba m‑basira; tɔ, u faam u maŋŋa gba m‑basi - u yi siri nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn gamsi sɔ n‑diisi Masiiya maa!”

english_verse: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
translation_verse: Ka soogyadima maa gba daa maanni u ansarisi, ka zaŋŋi damiisiri n‑ti u na ni u nyu;

english_verse: They said, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
translation_verse: ka yɛtti, ni, “I yi siri nyɛ la Izraɛli dima Naaba, nyin faam i maŋŋa m‑basi!”

english_verse: But the other criminal told the first one off, “Don't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man?
translation_verse: Amaa ka u taraana daa nyikki u, ka boosi u, ni, “I bu zɔri Naawunni? Kala, i bu nya ka ba kuuri tin ni ŋɔna zaa?

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise that today you will be with me in paradise.” The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, zuna dabsiri wa i nin ti bɛ n saani zisuŋŋu ziiya.”

english_verse: The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple split down the middle.
translation_verse: ka wuntaŋŋa daa bu neaai. Ka pateesa kan daa ya'ai Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Dutitaari puuni maa daa toaai m‑purigi buyi.

english_verse: Then they went to prepare some sweet-smelling spices for his burial. But on the Sabbath they rested, as the Law of Moses commands.
translation_verse: ka daa labi n‑kuli nti bɔ turaali ni kpanyuuma n‑zaali. Ba daa vuusi Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa kaman zaaligu ŋɔn daa wulisi seem.

english_verse: A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home.
translation_verse: Ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑zɛ bɛni ni ba gɔsi din ya n‑niŋi seem: ba zaa daa nya din daa baasi seem maa, ka daa labi n‑kuli ni sufusa'aŋŋu pam.

english_verse: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa mi seeba daa zɛ kpaŋŋa sa, ban ni pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli u n‑yi Galilii na. Ba gba daa nya din daa niŋi maa zaa.

english_verse: There was a man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea in Judea. Joseph was a good and honest man, and he was eager for God's kingdom to come. He was also a member of the council, but he did not agree with what they had decided.
translation_verse: Tɔ: dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Gyoosɛfi. U daa yi la Izraɛli dima tinseaa kan yuuri boonni Arimatiiya ni na. U daa guri Naawunni naam kyɛnni na. Tinkpamma maa yinni n daa nyɛ u, amaa u daa nyɛ la dɔsuŋŋu, ni yɛlimaŋni daana, ka daa bu la'am ni u taaba ban daa lɔ ba nin niŋi Yisa seem sahanseaa la.

english_verse: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.
translation_verse: Ka Gyoosɛfi daa sigsi Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa na n‑vili kasiŋŋa ka zaŋŋi u n‑dooli gballi ni - gbaliseaa ban daa pun kyiibi ka tampii ni, ka ba na zi n‑sɔ'ai sɔ bɛni.

english_verse: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli Yisa n‑yi Galilii na maa daa dɔli n‑kyaŋŋi nti nya m‑baŋŋi tampifeoo maa, ni ba n ya n‑sɔ'ai u seem la;

english_verse: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
translation_verse: Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, n‑daa la, ni Gyeoowana, ni Mariama Gyeemsi ma la, ni pɔ'aseeba m‑paasi ba zugu. Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi nti mooli yɛla wa zaa lahabaari n‑ti Yisa tuumudima maa.

english_verse: they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus,
translation_verse: Ka ban daa kpɛ gballi duu maa puuni, ba daa bu nya ti Dugma Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa.

english_verse: and they did not know what to think. Suddenly two men in shining white clothes stood beside them.
translation_verse: Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam. Di saha ka dɔppa ayi di yi na n‑zaani n‑tabli ba, ka ba sutura nya'ari nyain.

english_verse: The women were afraid and bowed to the ground. But the men said, “Why are you looking in the place of the dead for someone who is alive?
translation_verse: Ka dabeem daa gbaai pɔ'aba maa pam, ka ba sunni ba zugri tiŋŋa. Ka dɔppa maa daa boosi ba, ni, “Wula ka ya boori nyɔvuri daana kpiimma ziiya?

english_verse: ‘The Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.’ ”
translation_verse: U daa yɛli ya, ni, di kpa la talasi ni ba zaŋŋi Ninsaala Bii maa n‑niŋi ala'akyi dima nuusi ni, ka ba kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, amaa ka u nin ti neaai kum ni daba ata daari.”

english_verse: That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Di daari maa ka ba niriba ayi daa kyaŋŋi tinkpanseaa. Tiŋŋa maa yuuri daa boonni la Imeeyusi: ka nyɛ la maala ayɔpɔi zaŋŋi m‑paai Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What were you talking about as you walked along?” The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy.
translation_verse: Ka u boosi ba, ni, “Azambɔ ka ya diri ni taaba ya soori wa ni?” Ka ba kpalim n‑zaani, ka ba sufu na kur sa'am ni.

english_verse: We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
translation_verse: Amaa tirim daa mari la tama, ni, ŋɔna n ya fa Izraɛli dima m‑basi. Ka din paasi dinŋɔ zugu, zuna nyɛ la daba ata daari yɛla wa ŋɔn niŋi maa.

english_verse: Some women in our group surprised us. They had gone to the tomb early in the morning,
translation_verse: Yaasa, pɔ'aseeba ban bɛ ti saani n kur kyɛ ka di gyɛri ti, dama ba da daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gballi maa ni zuna asuba wa, ka bu nya u ningbiŋŋu maa;

english_verse: Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
translation_verse: Ka ti ninvugseeba gba doaai n‑kyaŋŋi gballi maa ni, ni ba nya; ka di nyɛ la hoo pɔ'aba maa ŋɔn da kur yɛli seem. Amaa ba gba da bu nya Yisa.”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the two disciples, “Why can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said?
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔna n daa yɛli ba, ni, “Yarima yamkɔŋŋniba wa! Ya bu wumni anabidima ŋɔn daa pun tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi la, ka bu sakki n‑niŋi a yɛdda yooma!

english_verse: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”
translation_verse: Ammi, di daa bu kpa talasi ni Masiiya maa nya lala namsigu maa pɔi ka naayi kpɛ u naam gyirima ziiya?”

english_verse: Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a m‑piili n‑wulisiri ba u mammaŋŋa yɛla Naawunni Litaafi ni. U daa piili la Musa sɔppu ni, halli nti paai anabidima ban daa dɔli u nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
translation_verse: Amaa ka ba goaai u n‑yɛli, “Kpalimma ti saani, dama wuntaŋŋa luya ka yuŋŋu zibra.” Ka u daa kpɛ, ni u ti gbɛ ba saani.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them.
translation_verse: Ka bundirigu saha ŋɔn daa ti paai maa, ba daa la'am taaba ni ba di, ka u daa zaŋŋi la diibu n‑susi Naawunni n‑niŋi ka, ka ŋmaasi n‑ti ba.

english_verse: At once they knew who he was, but he disappeared.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba nini di neaai ka ba baŋŋi u, amaa ŋɔn mun lili ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: They said to each other, “When he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?”
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛli taaba, “Ti sufu da bu pɛligi ŋɔn da tɔ'asiri n‑ti ti soori ni la? - ŋɔn da wulisi Litaafi maa gbinni la.”

english_verse: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ba yisigi di saha maa n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm. Ba daa paaya, ka nyaandɔliba piiya ni yinni maa, ni ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ ba saani la, la'am n‑ziya.

english_verse: And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter.
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛli ba, ni, “Ti Dugma siri neaaya ka Saimoon nya u!”

english_verse: Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Imeeyusi dima maa daa yɛli ba din niŋi ba soori ni. Ka laa n‑yɛli ba ŋɔn ŋmaasi diibu n‑ti ba ka ba baŋŋi u seem.

english_verse: While Jesus' disciples were talking about what had happened, Jesus appeared and greeted them.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa kur tɔ'asiri maa, Yisa mammaŋŋa di zaani ba sunsukkuni ka puusi ba.

english_verse: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
translation_verse: Amaa dabeem daa gbaai ba pam. Ba daa teesi, ni, ba nya la kpiinyisigi.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Why are you so frightened? Why do you doubt?
translation_verse: Ka u boosi ba, ni, “Bɔ n niŋi ka di mugsi ya, ka ya birisiri ya teesa ni maa?

english_verse: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
translation_verse: Ya nyam n nuusi, ni n nɔba wa; mani nla! Ya siisim ma n‑nya! Siiya ka numni ni kɔba hoo man mari seem wa.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli nŋɔ n‑naai la, u daa wulisi ba u nuusi ni u nɔba.

english_verse: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
translation_verse: Amaa ba sufu ŋɔn daa pɛligi seem maa zugu ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi lala yɛdda, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam. Ka u boosi ba, ni, “Ya mari diseebu kpa?”

english_verse: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi zinseerigu beela n‑ti u

english_verse: He took it and ate it as they watched.
translation_verse: ka u deaai ka n‑ŋɔbi ba tooni.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka u yɛli ba, “Yɛttɔ'a wa ka n daa pun tɔ'asi ya, man ŋɔn daa na bɛ ya saani sahanseaa la. N daa yɛli ya, ni, yɛttɔ'a pam pun sɔbi n‑dooli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n yɛla Musa zaaligu maa ni, ni anabidima yɛla ni, halli Yuuma sɔppu maa ni gba. Di kpa la talasi ni anŋɔ zaasa ŋɔn wulisi seem la nin baasi n‑niŋi.”

english_verse: Then he helped them understand the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Ka u neaai ba yɛm ni ba ti baŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu maa gbinni,

english_verse: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
translation_verse: ka yɛli ba, “Di sɔbiya, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tum Masisɔ na maa nin nya namsigu lala pɔi, ka naayi neaai kum ni daba ata daari.

english_verse: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
translation_verse: Ni, ba nin zaŋŋi Masiiya yuuri m‑piili Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni m‑moonni lahabaari maa n‑ti duniya wa zaa niriba, ni, ba ŋmaligma ka kyɛ ba tumbeeri tuma, ni ba ti nya ba taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

english_verse: you must tell everything that has happened.
translation_verse: Yarima n nyɛ ban nyɛ buna wa zaa sɛhara dima maa.

english_verse: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Ya nyama, n nin tum sɔ na hoo m Ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun basi nangbambasiri n‑ti ya seem maa. Yarima muna - ya kpalimma Gyɛrusalɛm halli ka ya ti deaai yiiko din yi saazugu ni na.”

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
translation_verse: U daa zaŋŋi ba n‑yi halli nti paai Bɛtani; ka daa duusi u nuusi saazugu n‑susi Naawunni alibarika yɛlisuma n‑niŋi ba.

english_verse: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa na kur susiri n‑niŋni ba sahanseaa maa, u du n‑kyaŋŋi saazugu ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
translation_verse: Ka ba mun daa gyeem u ka ŋmaligi n‑labi Gyɛrusalɛm ni sufupeelli pam.

english_verse: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
translation_verse: Ka kur bɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni m‑puusiri Naawunni.

english_verse: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
translation_verse: Di piiligu saha ka ŋɔn Yɛligu maa daa bɛ la Naawunni saani.

english_verse: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
translation_verse: Yɛligu maa ni ka nyɔvuri daa yi na, ka lala nyɔvuri daa taasi neesim na nti neaai n‑ti ninsaala.

english_verse: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
translation_verse: Neesim maa muna - di neaari la zibsim ni, ka zibsim maa mun na zi n‑nyaŋŋi di, bee, m‑baŋŋi di gbinni gba.

english_verse: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
translation_verse: U daa kyɛ na ni u ti moonni neesim maa yɛla n‑tiri niriba, ka ba zaa ti wum, ka laa n‑niŋi yɛdda u mooligu zugu.

english_verse: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
translation_verse: Neesimaŋni n‑daa la; din neaari n‑niŋni ninsaalidima zaa. Lala saha maa ka neesim maa daa kyɛnni duniya wa na.

english_verse: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
translation_verse: U daa bɛ la duniya wa puuni. Amaa halli ni u saani ka Naawunni daa naam duniya maa gba, di zaa yɔri duniya dima daa bu baŋŋi u.

english_verse: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
translation_verse: U daa kyɛ u mammantoori tingbaŋŋu na, amaa u mammantoori niriba maa daa bu deaai u.

english_verse: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
translation_verse: Di pa la kaman ninsaala ŋɔn dɔ'ari biisi seem, pa ningbiŋŋu kɔrisi zugu, bee, dɔsɔ yuubu zugu, amaa ba dɔ'am yi la Naawunni mammaŋŋa saani na.

english_verse: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Yɛligu maa daa ti labgi la ninsaala. U daa bɛ ti saani, ka veelim ni yɛlimaŋni daa bɛ u ni n‑gaari sɔkam. Tirima n daa nya u gyirima ŋɔn bɛ seem maa; u Ba Naawunni ka biiya m‑paasi u zugu, ka bigaŋŋa gyirima n nyɛ u dinni.

english_verse: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
translation_verse: Veelim ni yɛlimaŋni an bɛ u ni maa, ana ka ti deaara. U veelim zugu ka ti siri nya veelim suŋŋa.

english_verse: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like. John the Baptist Tells about Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Sɔsɔ na zi n‑nya Naawunni. Amaa Naawunsɔ ŋɔn bɛ u Ba Naawunni saani u kɔkɔa, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka ti zaa baŋŋi u.

english_verse: The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, “I am not the Messiah.”
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa bu kyiisi, ka daa labsi yɛlimaŋni yɛttɔ'a paalu ni n‑yɛli ba, ni, u pa la Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tum sɔ ni u ti su ba naam fukumsi la, ni, “Man pa la Masiiya maa.”

english_verse: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa maali m‑boosi u, ni, “Ka dinŋɔna, i nyɛ la ŋɔni? Ilaygya bee?”; Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ay, pa mani nla.”: ni, “Anabi maa n nyɛ i, fa?”, ka u labsi n‑yɛli “Ay!”

english_verse: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
translation_verse: Ka ba naayi boosi u, ni, “Ka dinŋɔna yɛlima ti nyin ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa. Ka ti kyaŋŋi nti yɛli ban tum ti na maa. I mammaŋŋa yɛtti, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔni?”

english_verse: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa labsi n‑yɛli ba hoo anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la, ni, “Mani n nyɛ#:- ‘Sɔ m bɛ yoo ni n‑tasira, ni, “Ya sɛ'asima ti Dugma sɔya zaa Ka a tuusi kyippi!” ’ ”

english_verse: Some Pharisees had also been sent to John.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Farisii dimseeba gba ka ba daa tum na,

english_verse: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
translation_verse: ka ban n daa boosi Gyɔni, ni, “I yi pa la Masiiya maa, i pa Ilaygya, i mun pa anabi maa, wula zugu ka i suuri niriba Naawunni koom wa?”

english_verse: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Man n suuri niriba Naawunni koom wa, amaa ninvugsɔ n zɛ ya sunsukkuni, halli ya na bu baŋŋi u gba;

english_verse: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn pa man nyaaŋŋa n‑kyɛnni na, amaa man bu sumsi ni n tum u daabri tuma ka lɔrigi u namtiri n‑ti u.”

english_verse: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
translation_verse: Tɔ; anŋɔ zaa daa niŋi la Bɛtani tiŋŋa maa ziiya, tinseaa kan bɛ Gyɔdin mɔ'ari ya'ari sa; bɛni ka Gyɔni daa bɛ ni lala saha maa, ka daa suuri niriba Naawunni koom.

english_verse: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
translation_verse: Beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Gyɔni daa nya Yisa ka u kyɛnni u saani na, ka yɛli, “Ya nyama! Naawunni Pɛbila maa nŋɔa! Ŋɔn zaŋŋni duniya tumbeeri taalli m‑basira.

english_verse: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔnŋɔ ka n daa tɔ'asi u yɛla, ni, ‘U paari la n nyaaŋŋa, amaa u pun gaari n tooni, dama u daa pun daŋŋi m‑bɛ ni ka ba naayi dɔ'ai ma.’

english_verse: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
translation_verse: Halli man daa bu mi ŋɔn nyɛ ninvugsɔa, amaa ŋɔn zugu ka n kyɛ na ni n ti suuri niriba koom - ni n ti kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima ti baŋŋi u.”

english_verse: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa yɛli ŋɔn daa pun nya bunseesi yɛla, ni “N daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka Sii maa daa yi arizana ni n‑sigri na hoo naŋmanni nti tɔm u zugu n‑kpalim m‑bɛ u ni.

english_verse: Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, “You will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa man daa na bu baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔna nla. Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa tum ma ni n ti kyɛ na n‑suuri niriba koom, ŋɔna n daa laa n‑yɛli ma, ni, ‘I yi ti nya ka Sii maa sigi na n‑tɔm dɔsɔ zugu n‑kpalim, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin mari Sii Kasi maa n‑suuri niriba koom’.”

english_verse: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔna n nya u, ni, “Mani n yɛtti ya wa, ni, u siri nyɛ la Naawunni Biiya.”

english_verse: The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him.
translation_verse: Beoo ŋɔn daa neaai yaasa, ka Gyɔni daa laa n‑zɛya ni - ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba ayi.

english_verse: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Here is the Lamb of God!”
translation_verse: N‑daa nya, ka Yisa kyɛnni la u kyɛnni n‑gatta; ka u yɛli, “Ya nyama! Naawunni Pɛbila maa nŋɔa!”

english_verse: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba ayi maa daa wum u yɛttɔ'a maa ka daa doaai n‑kari Yisa nɔba.

english_verse: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa ŋmaligi n‑nya ka ba katti la u nɔba, ka u daa boosi ba, ni, “Bɔ ka ya boora?”; ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Rabbi, ” , “yani ka i kpeera?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ! Ya kyɛm na nti nya!”; ka ba daa dɔli u nti baŋŋi u yirikpeetiri ŋɔn bɛ seela. Ka ba daa kpɛ n‑gbɛ u saani, dama zaanoori saha daa pun paai.

english_verse: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka niriba ayi wa daa wum Yisa yɛla Gyɔni saani, ka laa n‑dɔli u nyaaŋŋa. Ba yinni n daa nyɛ Anduru: Saimoon Piita sunzɔ maa nla.

english_verse: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
translation_verse: Ka dizugu u daa daŋŋi m‑bɔ u beeri Saimoon ziiya nti yɛli u, ni, “Ti nya la Masiiya maa!” ,

english_verse: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
translation_verse: ka daa mari u n‑taasi Yisa saani na. Ka Yisa daa gɔsi u ka yɛli, “Saimoon, Gyɔni biiya, n nyɛ i: tɔ! ba nin booli i ‘Siifasi’ ” .

english_verse: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Ka Filipi mun daa nya Nataniyɛli n‑yɛli u, “Ti nya la Musa ŋɔn daa sɔbi sɔ lahabaari n‑dooli zaaligu gbaŋŋu ni la, ka anabidima gba daa sɔbi u yɛla. Yisa nla, Gyoosɛfi biiya, ka u yi Nazarɛti tiŋŋa ni na!”

english_verse: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
translation_verse: Ka Nataniyɛli daa yɛli u, ni, “Nazarɛti fa! Bunsuŋŋu nin toaai n‑yi tinkyɔ'aŋŋu wa na, bee?” Ka Filipi daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Kyɛm na n‑nya!”

english_verse: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti nya Nataniyɛli ka u kyɛnni u saani na, ka u daa tɔ'asi u yɛla, ni, “Ya nyama! Izraɛli nira ni yɛlimaŋni nŋɔa. Ziri halli beela ka u ni!”

english_verse: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
translation_verse: Dizugu ka Nataniyɛli daa boosi u, ni, “I mi ma la yani?”; ka Yisa labsi u, ni, “N daa nya i ka i zi la kinkaŋŋa la gbinni pɔi, ka Filipi naayi booli i.”

english_verse: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Nataniyɛli daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, “Rabbi, i siri nyɛ la Naawunni biiya! N‑laa n‑nyɛ Izraɛli dima naaba!”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “I kur niŋi ma la yɛdda man yɛli i lala zugu, bee? Ni, n daa nya i kinkaŋŋa maa gbinni? I siri nin nya yɛliseesi an gaari lala pam!”

english_verse: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli u, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ya nin nya ka arizana za'anɔya yi yoaai ka Naawunni malaakadima duuri ka sigri man zugu, man ŋɔn nyɛ Ninsaala Bii maa.”

english_verse: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi daba ata ka ba daa ti ya puusi amriya kyuu Galilii tingbaŋŋu tinkpanseaa kan yuuri boonni Kanna la. Yisa ma gba daa bɛ la amriya maa ni.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ba daa booli Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba gba ni ba bɛ amriya maa ni.

english_verse: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
translation_verse: Ka amriya yiri dima bunnyuuriseelli ban daa nyuuri maa daa ti pɔsigi. Ka Yisa ma daa yɛli u, ni, ba laa n‑ka daam.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “M ma, n nuu bɛ la i yɛttɔ'a wa ni? Ay! - n saha na bu paai.”

english_verse: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
translation_verse: U ma mun daa yɛli yiri maa biisi, ni, “U yi ti yɛli ya, ni, ya niŋi seem, ya niŋma lala.”

english_verse: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Yɛhudiya dima zaaligu daa wulisi, ni, ba mari koom n‑zaali ba wungyeeŋŋu kaari ningbimpeaari zugu; ka lala zugu kɔsinsi ayoobu daa zɛ bɛni, bunkara.

english_verse: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa ti yɛli yiri maa biisi, ni, ba gbeaama koom na m‑paali sinsi maa. Ka ba daa gbeaai na m‑paali a zaa.

english_verse: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ, pampaŋŋɔ ya gbeaam m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti ŋɔn su kyuu yɛla maa.” Ka ba daa niŋi lala.

english_verse: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
translation_verse: Ka kyuu maa daana daa deaai n‑nyu beela, ka di daa pun labgi daam; doo maa daa bu baŋŋi daam maa ŋɔn yi seelli na, amaa biseeba ban daa gbeaai bu na la daa mi. Di saha ka kyudaana maa daa booli pɔ'apaalli sira maa na

english_verse: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
