translation_verse: nti yɛli u, ni, “I niŋi la wula? Dama niriba yi daŋŋi n‑yiisiri daseebu bun nya'asa n‑tisiri zama maa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi bumpɛtti n‑ti ba, ban pun nyu n‑tigi sahanseaa la. Nyin mun na bu yiisi bunsuŋŋu wa na halli ni pampaŋŋɔa.”

english_verse: But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple.
translation_verse: Amaa u ningbina yɛla ka u daa yɛtti maa, n‑ŋmansi hoo Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la.

english_verse: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Sahanseaa ka di daa ti pɔtti Yɛhudiyadima maa Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu puusigu la saha, ka Yisa daa kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na

english_verse: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa zaŋŋi miisi m‑mili a taaba ka mari n‑feebri ba zaa n‑katti n‑yiisiri Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Ka laa n‑kari ba bunkɔbri, ka daai la'atakkriba teebulidima ka kyɛ ka ba ligri maa zaa kpaai m‑basi.

english_verse: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba daa teaaya din daa sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni, “Nimbirimoaa i Yiri maa zugu mari ma n‑daamna.”

english_verse: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ, ya wurimma Yiri wa m‑basi, ka n nin laa n‑labi m‑mɛ ka n‑zaali, daba ata saha.”

english_verse: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
translation_verse: nti kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑nya ban daa diri daa bɛni. Baseeba daa koosiri nigi ni peesi ni ŋmana, maaligu zugu, ka seeba mun daa zintiri n‑takkri ligri, lampoo maa zugu.

english_verse: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
translation_verse: Ka daa yɛli ŋmankoosiriba, ni, ba mari anŋɔ zaa n‑kyaŋŋi, ni, “Ya di kyɛ ka m Ba Naawunni Yiri leebi daa!”

english_verse: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yɛhudiya dima maa daa yɛli u, ni, “Alahazibu tumni bɔ ka i nin tum n‑wulisiri ti, ni, soori bɛ ni, ni i niŋni anŋɔ zaa?”

english_verse: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti neaai kum ni la nyaaŋŋa sahanseelli la ka u nyaandɔliba daa naayi teaai, ni, u daa pun yɛli ba yɛlli wa n‑zaali. Ka dizugu ba daa ti niŋi yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni ŋɔn Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la.

english_verse: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu puusigu saha la, niriba pam daa niŋi u yɛdda, ban daa nya alahazibu tunseesi ŋɔn daa wulisiri la zugu.

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa bu niŋi ba yɛdda, dama u daa baŋŋi ba zaa yɛla.

english_verse: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
translation_verse: U daa bu sakki m‑basi u maŋŋa n‑ti ba, dama u daa pun mi din bɛ ninsaala kam sufu ni, ka pa ni sɔ yɛli u.

english_verse: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya dima toondaansɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Nikɔdiimusi; u daa dɔli la Farisidima maa soori la.

english_verse: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ŋɔnwa daa kyɛ Yisa saani na yuŋŋu, nti yɛli u, “M Ba Rabbi, ti mi, ni, i nyɛ la karimba, ka Naawunni tum i na; dama sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑niŋi alahazibu tunseesi nyin niŋni maa, di yi pa la Naawunni m bɛ u saani.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, sɔ ku toaai n‑nya Naawunni naam maa, di yi pa la ba maali n‑dɔ'ai u yaasa.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i ni, ni, sɔ ku toaai n‑kpɛ Naawunni naam ni, ba yi bu dɔli koom suubu soori n‑dɔ'ai u, ni Naawunni Sii Vuusim soori gba.

english_verse: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
translation_verse: Ninsaala ningbiŋŋu ŋɔn dɔ'ai seelli nyɛ la ninsaala, ka Naawunni Siiya mun ŋɔn dɔ'ai seelli nyɛ la siiya.

english_verse: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
translation_verse: Dizugu di kyɛ ka di niŋi i alahazibu, man ŋɔn yɛli i seem, ni, see ka ba maali n‑dɔ'ai sɔ yaasa.

english_verse: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
translation_verse: I bu nya? - Pɛsigu maa yi zeeri n‑zeera, i mun wumni ka vuuri maa, amaa i ku baŋŋi kan yiiri ya'aseaa na, bee, kan kyɛnni ya'aseaa. Tɔ, ka Naawunni Vuusim ŋɔn dɔ'ai seeba saani di bɛ la lala.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “Nyini n nyɛ karimba n‑tiri Izraɛli dima, ka i na zi anŋɔa, fa?

english_verse: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, tirima n tɔ'asiri tin mi seelli, ka tisiri lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tin na mi n‑nya; ka di zaa yɔri yarima sɔsɔ ku sakki n‑deaai ti lahabaari maa.

english_verse: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
translation_verse: N yɛtti ya duniya wa yɛla ka ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda; ka dinŋɔna ya ya niŋi la wula n‑niŋi ma yɛdda, man yi ti yɛli arizana yɛla n‑ti ya?”

english_verse: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ka sɔ mun na zi n‑du m‑paai arizana ni, na'ala ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pun yi arizana ni n‑sigi na - Ninsaala Bii maa nla.

english_verse: And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert.
translation_verse: Kaman hoo Musa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi tasiri kutti m‑maali waafu maa n‑kpaasi n‑tabli doo zugu seem maa, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑sa yoo puuni la, lala ka di niŋi talasi ni ba zaŋŋi Ninsaala Bii maa gba n‑sa saazugu,

english_verse: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
translation_verse: ka sɔkam ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda la, di daama nin mari la nyɔvuri kan ka tariga.

english_verse: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
translation_verse: Naawunni wurilim daa bɛ la duniya wa dima maa pam, halli nti zaŋŋi u bigaŋŋa n‑tisi, ni sɔkam yi niŋi u yɛdda u ku baasi yɔri amaa u nin mari nyɔvuri kan ka tariga.

english_verse: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni daa bu tum u Bii maa ni u kyɛ duniya wa ni na nti kari duniya wa dima sariya, amaa u tum u na ni duniya dima maa ti nya tiliginsim u zugu.”

english_verse: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
translation_verse: Sɔ ŋɔn niŋi Naawunni Bii maa yɛdda zaa, u ku mari sariya; ŋɔn mun bu niŋi yɛdda zaa ka ba pun kari u sariya n‑lusi u, ŋɔn bu niŋi Naawunni Bigaŋŋa maa yɛdda zugu.

english_verse: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
translation_verse: Sariya karibu gbinni nŋɔa: neesim maa daa pun kyɛ duniya wa ni na, amaa ninsaalidima maa daa boori zibsim maa ka kyɛ neesim, ba tuma ŋɔn bɛ pam zugu.

english_verse: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
translation_verse: Dama sɔ yi niŋni tumbeeri, u nin gyɛ neesim maa ka ku sakki ni u kyɛ neesim maa saani na, dama u tumbeeri nin yi paalu na.

english_verse: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
translation_verse: Amaa sɔ ŋɔn niŋni yɛlimaŋni tuma, ŋɔna nin kyɛ neesim maa saani na, ni u tuma ti yi paalu na ka ba baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni zugu ka u daa niŋi a.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba daa kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu la ni, ka bɛni ka ŋɔn ni ban daa yuui beela n‑suuri niriba Naawunni koom.

english_verse: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni nyaandɔliba daa piili n‑ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni ni Yɛhudiya ninvugsɔ zaŋŋi n‑gbaai suubu ni peaari kaari yɛla.

english_verse: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kyɛ Gyɔni saani na nti yɛli u, ni, “Ti Ba Rabbi, ti ya boosi i zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa bɛ i saani Gyɔdin mɔ'ari zii la - nyin daa tɔ'asi sɔ lahabaari n‑ti ti la. Tɔ, ŋɔna n suuri niriba Naawunni koom, ka sɔkam zaa kyɛnni u saani.”

english_verse: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
translation_verse: Ka Gyɔni daa labsi, ni, “Sɔ ku toaai m‑mari seelli, di yi pa la Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi bunseelli maa n‑ti u.

english_verse: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
translation_verse: Ya mammansi daa pun wum man daa yɛli seem la, ni, man pa Masiiya maa, amaa Naawunni n tum ma ni n ti daŋŋi Masiiya maa tooni na.

english_verse: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Ba yi maali amriya kyuu sahanseaa la, dɔsɔ ŋɔn mari pɔ'apaalli maa nyɛ la amriya doo maa; u zɔ ŋɔn zɛ u saani n‑wumni muna, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn sufu yi maai amriya doo maa kukɔri wumpu zugu. Lala ka man gba sufu maai pam maa.

english_verse: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
translation_verse: Di sumni, ni, ŋɔna m paasira, ka man mun fiilimna.”

english_verse: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn yi saazugu ni na gaari sɔkam zaa. Ŋɔn mun yi duniya wa ni na nyɛ la duniya wa nira, ka tɔ'asiri la duniya wa yɛla; amaa ŋɔn yi arizana ni na gaari sɔkam zaa.

english_verse: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnwa wulisiri ŋɔn pun nya ka wum yɛla maa lahabaari; amaa di zaa yɔri, sɔ ku sakki n‑deaari u yɛligu maa.

english_verse: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔnkam yi sakki n‑deaai u yɛligu maa, di daama nin diisi n‑tabli, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.

english_verse: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Naawunni ŋɔn tum dɔsɔ maa tɔ'asiri la Naawunni yɛligu, dama Naawunni bu ma'asiri u Sii maa beelabeela n‑tiri u.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
translation_verse: Naawunni Bii maa muna - u Ba siri wuri u, ka zaŋŋi bunseelikam n‑niŋi u nuu ni.

english_verse: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam yi niŋni Naawunni Bii maa yɛdda nin nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga. Ŋɔnkam mun bu sakki n‑niŋi u yɛdda ku nya nyɔvuri, halli Naawunni sufugyeaa kpalim m‑bɛ u zugu.

english_verse: This time he had to go through Samaria,
translation_verse: Ka u soori maa ni u daa kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli n‑yi la Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu ni.

english_verse: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “I nyɛ la Yɛhudiya nira ka man mun nyɛ Samɛrii pɔ'a, dizugu wula ka i nin toaai n‑susi koom man saani?”

english_verse: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “M Bapira, i ka guuga ni i zaŋŋi n‑gbeaai koom, ka buliga wa mun zulim pam. Ka, i ya niŋi la wula n‑nya kɔsuŋŋu wa?”

english_verse: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
translation_verse: Ni, “Ti yaadoo Yaakubu daa ti ti buliga wa; ŋɔn ni u biisi ni ba bunkɔbri zaa daa nyuuri ka koom. Nyini n‑gaari Yaakubu, bee?”

english_verse: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
translation_verse: Amaa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyu kɔseebu man yɛ n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u maa muna - kɔnyuuri ku laa n‑gbaai di daama yaasa. Lala koom maa yɛ n‑leebi la din bunni u puuni, halli nti kyɛ ka u nya nyɔvurisuŋŋu din ka tariga.”

english_verse: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛli, “Tɔ, m Bapira, kur zaŋŋma lala koom n‑ti ma na, ka kɔnyuuri ku laa n‑gbaai ma yaasa halli ka n ku laa n‑sigri kpa na n‑gbeaari koom.”

english_verse: The woman answered, “I don't have a husband.” “That's right,” Jesus replied, “you're telling the truth. You don't have a husband. You have already been married five times, and the man you are now living with isn't your husband.”
translation_verse: I na mun kuli dɔppa anu, halli nyin ni dɔsɔ ŋɔn na bɛ ni maa gba pa la i sira: i yɛli ma la yɛlimaŋni.”

english_verse: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛli, “Mma, m Bapira! N baŋŋiya, ni, anabi n nyɛ i.

english_verse: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
translation_verse: Tɔ: kyɛ ka n boosi i bunseelli - ti Samɛrii yaadima daa gyeemni Naawunni zoori wa zugu, amaa ya Yɛhudiya dima maa yɛnni, ni, Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa kɔkɔ n nyɛ Naawunni gyeeŋŋu ziiya ni yɛlimaŋni.”

english_verse: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Pɔ'a, kur niŋni ma yɛdda; saha kyɛnni na ka ya yi ya gyeemni ti Ba Naawunni, di ku nyɛ la zoori wa zugu, bee, Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni yɛttɔ'a.

english_verse: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
translation_verse: Yarima Samɛrii dima kur bu baŋŋi yan gyeemni sɔa, ti mun Yɛhudiya dima mi tin gyeemni sɔa, dama tiliginsim yi la Yɛhudiya dima maa saani na.

english_verse: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
translation_verse: Amaa saha kyɛnni na, halli ka pun paai na gba, ka ban gyeemni ti Ba Naawunni ni yɛlimaŋni la nin dɔli Sii Kasi soori n‑gyeemni u, ni yɛlimaŋni soori gba. Lala soori ka Naawunni boori ni ba dɔli n‑gyeemni u.

english_verse: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
translation_verse: Naawunni nyɛ la siiya, ka dizugu see ka ban ya gyeemni u dɔli la siiya soori maa n‑gyeemna, ni yɛlimaŋni soori gba.”

english_verse: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “M mi halli, ni, ba n boonni sɔ Masiiya, bee Kirisita, la nin kyɛ na, ka u yi ti kyɛ na u nin wulisi ti bunseelli kam zaa.”

english_verse: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli, “Mani nla - man ŋɔn tɔ'asiri n‑tiri i wa.”

english_verse: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba la daa labi na, ka ban daa nya u ka ŋɔn ni pɔ'a maa diri azama maa, di daa gyɛri ba pam. Amaa ba sɔ daa bu boosi pɔ'a maa, ni, “I boori la bɔa?”, bee, m‑boosi Yisa, ni, “Bɔ n kyɛ ka nyin ni pɔ'a wa diri azama?”

english_verse: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa kyɛ u kɔyuuri maa ka labi n‑kyaŋŋi timpuuni nti yɛli niriba maa,

english_verse: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
translation_verse: ni, “Ya kyɛm na nti nya dɔsɔ ŋɔn yɛli ma man daa pun tum tunseesi zaa. Pa ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya maa?”

english_verse: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka niriba maa daa yi tiŋŋa maa ni na, ni ba kyɛ Yisa saani na.

english_verse: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba daa bɛlim u, ni, “Ti Ba Rabbi, deaama diibu n‑di!”

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “M mari la bundirigu ni n di, ka ya ka di lahabaari maa.”

english_verse: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa ŋmaligi m‑boosi taaba, ni, “Ka sɔ n zi bundirigu na n‑ti u, bee?”

english_verse: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli, “Man bundirigu maa nyɛ la nŋɔa#:- n niŋni Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu yɛla, ka laa n‑tumni u tuma zaa nti naai.

english_verse: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - ya yɛtti, ni, gɔya anaasi na kpalim ni kyi kyeebu saha, bee, m ma'ari ni? Amaa n ya yɛli ya ni, ni, ya wukkma ya nini n‑gɔsi puri maa suŋŋa - kyi maa pun bi n‑sakki kyeebu. Ni, pampaŋŋɔ

english_verse: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
translation_verse: ka ŋɔn kyeeri kyi maa deaari la u tuma ligri, ka la'asiri la kyi maa n‑doonni n‑guri nyɔvuri kan ka tariga; lala ka ŋɔn daa buri a maa, ni ŋɔn kyeeri maa gba, nin la'am taaba n‑di nya'asim.

english_verse: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
translation_verse: Lala ka yɛligu maa niŋni yɛlimaŋni, ni, ‘Ninvugsɔ n butta, ka sɔ mun n kyeera.’

english_verse: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka tiŋŋa wa Samɛrii niriba pampam daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda pɔ'a maa lahabaari zugu, dama u daa yɛli ba, ni, “U yɛli ma man daa pun tum tunseesi zaa.”

english_verse: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
translation_verse: Dizugu Samɛrii dima maa ŋɔn daa kyɛ u saani na maa, ba daa susi u, ni, u kpalimma m‑bɛ ba saani. Ka Yisa siri daa kpalim bɛni n‑niŋi daba ayi.

english_verse: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
translation_verse: Ka ba daadam pam m‑paasi niriba wa zugu daa wum u toori yɛligu ka niŋi u yɛdda di zugu.

english_verse: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka daa yɛli pɔ'a maa, ni, “Pampaŋŋɔ di pa la nyin ŋɔn yɛli seem maa zugu ka ti niŋni yɛdda, amaa ti mammaŋŋa n wum u noori nti baŋŋi, ni, u siri nyɛ la duniya dima Tiligra maa.”

english_verse: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
translation_verse: Yisa mammaŋŋa daa pun yɛli, ni, anabi ka gyirima u mammaŋŋa tiŋŋa.

english_verse: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai Galilii la, niriba maa daa deaai u ni, dama ba gba daa kyaŋŋi la Gyɛrusalɛm nti puusi kyuu maa ka nya ŋɔn tum seem zaa kyuu maa ni.

english_verse: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa naa n‑labi Galilii Kanna tinkpaŋŋa ni, bɛni ka u daa pun kyɛ ka koom la labgi daam. M‑paai bɛni ka Naaba kpambari sɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u bii daa bɛ la Kapirinyum m‑beera.

english_verse: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Yisa yi Gyuudiiya m‑paai Galilii na, u daa doaai n‑kyɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, u kyɛma Kapirinyum na nti tibi bii maa, ni, di kur kpalim la beela ni u kpi.

english_verse: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa yɛli u, ni, “Ya yi bu nya alahazibu tunkara, ya sɔ ku sakki n‑niŋi yɛdda.”

english_verse: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
translation_verse: Ka kpambari maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ay, n daana! Kur kyɛm na ka m bibila maa di kpi.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “Kyɛma! I bii maa ku kpi.” Ka doo maa daa niŋi Yisa yɛligu maa yɛdda ka daa labi n‑kuli.

english_verse: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa labri n‑kunni maa, u daa tuusi la u daabseeba soori ni, ka ba daa ti u lahabaari, ni, u bii maa mari alaafɛa.

english_verse: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa boosi ba m‑baŋŋi, ni, saha kani ka bii maa nya sookyi? - ka ba daa labsi, ni, “Soosa la wuntaŋŋa, kurigu yinni saha, ka u ningbimbiisim maa sa basi u.”

english_verse: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa teaai, ni, lala saha deede ka Yisa sa yɛli u, ni, “I bii maa ku kpi.” Di zugu ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda.

english_verse: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Makkri tunkanŋɔ daa paasi la buyi ka Yisa daa tum, ŋɔn daa yi Gyuudiiya n‑kyɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu na maa.

english_verse: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa Yɛhudiya dima kyuseaa n daa bɛ ni, ka Yisa daa ti kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, za'anɔtitaari m bɛ ni, ka ba boonni ka ‘Peesi Za'anɔri’ la: ka za'anɔri wa kpaŋŋa ka beeŋŋu bɛ ni, ka Hibruu dima boonni ka ‘Bɛtzaata’, ka ka mari luffadima abanu.

english_verse: Many sick, blind, lame, and paralyzed people were lying close to the pool.
translation_verse: A puuni ka zoomma, ni gbarisi, ni nukpiima dima, ni gbarikpiima dima, ni dooru baligu kam dima zaa dɔya.

english_verse: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ bɛni ka dooru daa mari u halli di yuumpisita ni anii.

english_verse: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
translation_verse: Ka u yɛligu maa mun bu laa n‑kpalim ya saani, dama ya bu niŋi ŋɔn tum sɔ na maa yɛdda.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
translation_verse: Yisa daa nya u ka u dɔ bɛni ka daa baŋŋi, ni, dooru maa daa pun mari doo wa ka di yuui; ka u daa boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i boori i nya tippu?”

english_verse: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
translation_verse: Ka dooru daana maa labsi n‑yɛli, “M Ba, man ka sɔsɔ kpa wa ka u nin zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi beeŋŋu wa puuni. Koom maa yi ti damni sahanseaa la; n yi ti kpaŋŋni la n maŋŋa ni n sigi di ni, sɔ n yi pun ti daŋŋi ma n‑sigi di puuni.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Doaama n‑zaani saazugu, ka kpikki i sɔŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi.”

english_verse: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Lala saha maa ka doo maa da nya tippu; u daa kpikki u sɔŋŋu ka piili n‑kyɛnna. Dindahinseaa lala ŋɔn daa niŋi maa daa nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri daari,

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: ka di zugu Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa daa yɛli doo ŋɔn nya tippu la, ni, “Zuna nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri, nyin nya'ai i sɔŋŋu wa di galim la ti zaaligu.”

english_verse: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
translation_verse: U daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Dɔsɔ ŋɔn tibi ma maa n yɛli ma, ni, n zaŋŋma n sɔŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi.”

english_verse: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
translation_verse: Ka ba boosi u, ni, “Ŋɔni n nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn yɛli i, ni, i niŋma nŋɔ maa?”

english_verse: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
translation_verse: Amaa dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nya tippu wa daa bu mi Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛ sɔa, dama daadam n daa bɛ bɛni maa pam, ka Yisa daa sɔ'ai baalimm m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi kpaŋŋa.

english_verse: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa daa ti nya doo maa Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni ka daa yɛli u, ni, “Wumma, i yi kpaŋŋiya pampaŋŋɔa, dinzugu kyɛli tumbeeri tumpu: ka di yi pa lala, bunseela din bɛ n‑gaari zaa nin ti niŋi i yaasa.”

english_verse: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa di bakki n‑kyaŋŋi nti yɛli Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa, ni, Yisa n nyɛ ŋɔn daa tibi u maa.

english_verse: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa piili m‑mugsiri Yisa, ŋɔn daa tibri niriba Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa zugu.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “M Ba kur tumni ni saha kam zaa, ka man gba tumna.”

english_verse: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
translation_verse: Yɛlli wa daa kyɛ ka Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa daa di maali n‑lɔ la niya ni ba ku u; di mun pa la ŋɔn daa burigi Vuusim Dabsiri daari zaaligu zugu kɔkɔa, amaa ŋɔn daa yɛli, ni, Naawunni nyɛ la u mammantoori ba zugu m‑paasi; ni, u zaŋŋni u maŋŋa m‑ma'asiri la Naawunni.

english_verse: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya: Bii maa ku toaai n‑niŋi bunseela u mammantoori; u niŋni la u yi nya u Ba ŋɔn niŋni seelli la kɔkɔa. U Ba maa ŋɔn tumni seem, lala ka Bii maa gba tumna.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
translation_verse: Dama u Ba maa wuri la Bii maa ka wulisiri u ŋɔn niŋni seelikam zaasa. Halli gba u nin ti wulisi u tunkara n‑gaari nŋɔa, ka di nin ti niŋi ya zaasa alahazibu.

english_verse: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Kaman u Ba maa ŋɔn kur neaari kpiimma ka tiri ba nyɔvuya seem la, lala taabu ka Bii maa gba zaŋŋni nyɔvuri n‑tiri ŋɔn boori seeba.

english_verse: The Father doesn't judge anyone, but he has made his Son the judge of everyone.
translation_verse: M‑paasi lala, u Ba maa mun bu katti sariya u mammaŋŋa. U tisi u Bii maa la soori ni yɛlimaŋni ni u kari sariya,

english_verse: The Father wants all people to honor the Son as much as they honor him. When anyone refuses to honor the Son, this is the same as refusing to honor the Father who sent him.
translation_verse: din nin kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa tibgi Bii maa kaman ban tibgri u Ba maa seem. Ŋɔn kam zaa bu tibgi Bii maa bu tibgi u Ba sɔ ŋɔn tum u na maa gba.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain that everyone who hears my message and has faith in the one who sent me has eternal life and will never be condemned. They have already gone from death to life.
translation_verse: Ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa wum n yɛttɔ'a wa ka niŋi ŋɔn tum ma na maa yɛdda, di daama mari nyɔvuriseaa kan ka naari. Ba bu ti yɛ kari u sariya, amaa u pun yi kum ni n‑kpɛ nyɔvuri ni.

english_verse: I tell you for certain the time will come, and it is already here, when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God. And those who listen to it will live!
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka man yɛtti ya, ni, saha kyɛnni na, saha maa pun paai na gba, kpiimma ŋɔn ti yɛ wumni Naawunni Bii maa kukɔri, ka ninvugseeba ban ti wum maa nin ti bɛ nyɔvuri ni.

english_verse: The Father has the power to give life, and he has given that same power to the Son.
translation_verse: Ti Ba Naawunni mammaŋŋa ŋɔn mari nyɔvuri u saani seem, lala ka u zaŋŋi n‑ti u toori Biiya ni u mari nyɔvuri u saani.

english_verse: And he has given his Son the right to judge everyone, because he is the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑ti u yiiko ni u kari sariya m‑paasi, ŋɔn nyɛ Ninsaala Bii maa zugu.

english_verse: Don't be surprised! The time will come when all of the dead will hear the voice of the Son of Man,
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka dinŋɔ zugu gyɛri ya yam, dama saha kyɛnni na ka ninvugseeba ban bɛ ba dabɔya ni zaasa nin ti wum Ninsaala Bii maa kukɔri.

english_verse: and they will come out of their graves. Everyone who has done good things will rise to life, but everyone who has done evil things will rise and be condemned.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba nin yi na: ka ban daa tum tunsuma la nin ti doaai kum ni n‑nya nyɔvuri, ka ban mun daa tum tumbeeri la nin ti doaai nti nya tubdarigri.

english_verse: I cannot do anything on my own. The Father sent me, and he is the one who told me how to judge. I judge with fairness, because I obey him, and I don't just try to please myself. Witnesses to Jesus
translation_verse: Man ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli m mammaŋŋa yiiko ni, see ka man wum pɔi, ka naayi dɔli man wum seem n‑kari sariya maa. Ka man sariya karibu wa nyɛ la din tuusi, dama m bu boori la m mammantoori yuubu, amaa ŋɔn tum ma na yuubu maa ka n boora.”

english_verse: If I speak for myself, there is no way to prove I am telling the truth.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “N yi di sɛhara n‑ti m mammaŋŋa yɛttɔ'a, lala sɛhara maa ku niŋi yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: But there is someone else who speaks for me, and I know what he says is true.
translation_verse: Ay! - Sɔ m bɛ ni n‑diri sɛhara n‑tiri n yɛttɔ'a maa; ka man gba baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔn niŋi ma sɛharaseelli maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: You sent messengers to John, and he told them the truth.
translation_verse: Ya daa tum Gyɔni saani ni ya ti baŋŋi, ka ŋɔn Gyɔni daa di sɛhara n‑ti yɛlimaŋni yɛla.

english_verse: I don't depend on what people say about me, but I tell you these things so that you may be saved.
translation_verse: Halli m bu deaari ninsaala sɛhara gba, n yɛtti Gyɔni yɛla dama m boori la ya wum ka nya tiliginsim.

english_verse: John was a lamp that gave a lot of light, and you were glad to enjoy his light for a while.
translation_verse: Gyɔni muna - u daa nyɛ la bugum firila n‑nya'asira, ka ya daa sakki ni ya paai u neesim maa na, ka di daa niŋi ya nya'asim ni sufupeelli ka daa yuui beela.

english_verse: But something more important than John speaks for me. I mean the things that the Father has given me to do! All of these speak for me and prove that the Father sent me.
translation_verse: Amaa m mari sɛhara seaa din gaari Gyɔni - tunseesi ka m Ba ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti ma ni n ti tum n‑naai maa nla: lala tuma maa siri diri la sɛhara n‑ti ma, ni, m Ba maa daa tum ma na.

english_verse: The Father who sent me also speaks for me, but you have never heard his voice or seen him face to face.
translation_verse: M‑paasi lala, m Ba sɔ ŋɔn tum ma na maa n nyɛ n sɛhara daana. Yarima mun na zi n‑wum u kukɔri, bee, ya baŋŋi ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: You search the Scriptures, because you think you will find eternal life in them. The Scriptures tell about me,
translation_verse: Ya karimni Naawunni Litaafi la n‑viisira, ni, ka puuni ka ya nin nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga; tɔ - ana n nyɛ an diri sɛhara n‑tiri man yɛla.

english_verse: but you refuse to come to me for eternal life.
translation_verse: Di zaa yɔri, ya mun ku sakki ni ya kyɛ n saani na n‑deaai nyɔvuri.”

english_verse: but I do know that none of you love God.
translation_verse: Amaa m mi ya yɛla, ni, ya ka Naawunni wurilim ya sufu ni.

english_verse: I have come with my Father's authority, and you have not welcomed me. But you will welcome people who come on their own.
translation_verse: Tɔ: m Ba yuuri zugu ka n kyɛ kpa na, ka ya ku sakki n‑deaai ma; amaa sɔ yi ti kyɛ na u mammaŋŋa yuuri zugu, ŋɔna ka ya nin deaai.

english_verse: How could you possibly believe? You like to have your friends praise you, and you don't care about praise that the only God can give!
translation_verse: Ya nin niŋi wula n‑niŋi yɛdda, sahanseaa ka ya na boori gyirima ya taaba saani ka ku sakki ni ya boori gyirima Naawunni ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa saani.

english_verse: Don't think that I will be the one to accuse you to the Father. You have put your hope in Moses, yet he is the very one who will accuse you.
translation_verse: Tɔ: yan di teesiri, ni, man nin zaŋŋi ya taalli n‑ti m Ba maa; sɔ bɛ ni n nin zaŋŋi ya taalli n‑ti u na - halli Musa, yan gɔsi sɔ n‑yɛtti, ni, ya tama kur bɛ u ni la.

english_verse: Moses wrote about me, and if you had believed Moses, you would have believed me.
translation_verse: Amaa ya yi daa siri niŋi ŋɔn Musa yɛdda, ya da naa n‑niŋi man gba yɛdda, dama Musa daa sɔbi man yɛla n‑ti ya.

english_verse: But if you don't believe what Moses wrote, how can you believe what I say?
translation_verse: Amaa yan ŋɔn bu niŋi Musa sɔppu yɛla la yɛdda, ya ya niŋi la wula n‑niŋi man yɛtti ya yɛliseesi wa yɛdda?”

english_verse: The disciples gathered them up and filled twelve large baskets with what was left over from the five barley loaves.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa la'asi bunkyɛba seesi m‑paali peeri pii ni ayi - ban di boroboro kpila abanu maa ni ka kyɛ dikyɛba maa.

english_verse: The disciples wanted to take him into the boat, but suddenly the boat reached the shore where they were headed. The Bread That Gives Life
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa sakki n‑zaŋŋi u n‑kpeesi nyariŋŋu maa puuni; ka nyariŋŋu maa ŋɔn kur ti siisi tingbaŋŋu, ba daa di paai la ban daa wa ya'aseelli maa ni.

english_verse: They replied, “What miracle will you work, so that we can have faith in you? What will you do?
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli u, “Alahazibu bɔ ka i ya tum ka ti nya ka niŋi i yɛdda? Bɔ ka i ya tum?

english_verse: No one can come to me, unless the Father who sent me makes them want to come. But if they do come, I will raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Sɔ ku toaai n‑kyɛ n saani na, di yi pa la m Ba ŋɔn tum ma na maa ti booli u n‑ti ma na, ka n nin kyɛ ka u yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

english_verse: “There is a boy here who has five small loaves of barley bread and two fish. But what good is that with all these people?”
translation_verse: “Bidibbila m bɛ kpa m‑mari boroboro kpila abanu ni zimi abayi, amaa a mun pun ku sakki niriba wa zaa.”

english_verse: The ground was covered with grass, and Jesus told his disciples to tell everyone to sit down. About
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, “Ya kyɛ ka niriba maa zinni tiŋŋa.” . Ka daadam maa zaa daa zinni tiŋŋa; ba daa nyɛ la kaman ninvugtusa anu laasaabu.

english_verse: They got into a boat and started across for Capernaum. Later that evening Jesus had still not come to them,
translation_verse: n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu, n‑yakkri mɔ'ari maa n‑kpa Kapirinyum ya'ari sa. Ka yuŋŋu mari nti zibi ka Yisa na bu kyɛ ba saani na.

english_verse: Jesus crossed Lake Galilee, which was also known as Lake Tiberias.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa, Yisa daa kyaŋŋi n‑yakki Galilii mɔ'ari la, seaa kan yuuri laa m‑boonni Taibiriyasi mɔ'ari la.

english_verse: A large crowd had seen him work miracles to heal the sick, and those people went with him.
translation_verse: Ka daadam pam daa dɔli u, dama ba daa nya ka u tum alahazibu tuntumsa n‑tibri baaridima la.

english_verse: It was almost time for the Jewish festival of Passover, and Jesus went up on a mountain with his disciples and sat down.
translation_verse: Di daa bu yuui ka Yɛhudiya dima maa ya puusi ba Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la.

english_verse: Philip answered, “Don't you know that it would take almost a year's wages just to buy only a little bread for each of these people?”
translation_verse: Ka Filipi daa yɛli u, ni, “Halli ti yi yɛ da bundirigu ka sɔkam zaa deaai beelabeela gba, di nin gaari anzulifa la'akɔba kɔbsiyi.”

english_verse: Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of the disciples. He spoke up and said,
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliba maa ni sɔa, Anduru, ŋɔn nyɛ Saimoon Piita sunzɔ la, daa yɛli u, ni,

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he passed the bread to the people, and he did the same with the fish, until everyone had plenty to eat.
translation_verse: Yisa daa zaŋŋi boroboro maa, ka puusi Naawunni, ka yi puri a n‑tɔri n‑tɔri daadam seeba ban daa zi tiŋŋa maa. Lala ka u daa niŋi zaasim maa gba, ka ba zaa daa di nti sakki ban boori seem.

english_verse: The people ate all they wanted, and Jesus told his disciples to gather up the leftovers, so that nothing would be wasted.
translation_verse: Ba zaasa ŋɔn daa ti di n‑tigi, Yisa daa yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, ba la'asim dikyɛba an kpalim zaa, ni ba di kyɛ ka halli beela gba basi.

english_verse: Jesus realized that they would try to force him to be their king. So he went up on a mountain, where he could be alone. Jesus Walks on the Water (Matthew
translation_verse: Yisa daa pun mi, ni, ba nin kyɛ na m‑mugsi u n‑diisi u naam; dinzugu u daa bakki ka laa n‑kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori maa u kɔkɔa.

english_verse: The people who had stayed on the east side of the lake knew that only one boat had been there. They also knew that Jesus had not left in it with his disciples. But the next day
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa neaai, daadam ban daa kpalim mɔ'aduuri la sa daa nya ka nyariŋŋu yinni kɔkɔ n sa bɛ ni. Ba daa mi suŋŋa, ni, Yisa sa bu la'am n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa u nyaandɔliba saani, kulla nyaandɔliba maa n sa kpɛ m‑bakki.

english_verse: Don't work for food that spoils. Work for food that gives eternal life. The Son of Man will give you this food, because God the Father has given him the right to do so.”
translation_verse: Yan di tumni m‑boori bundiriseelli din bu yuura, amaa ya tumma m‑bɔ bundiriseelli din nin kpalim m‑paai nyɔvuri kan ka tariga. Lala bundirigu maa ka Ninsaala Bii maa nin zaŋŋi n‑tisira. Dama ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn pun daalim n‑wulisi, ni, u nyɛ la u dinni.”

english_verse: One of the prophets wrote, “God will teach all of them.” And so everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him will come to me.
translation_verse: Anabidima maa sɔppu ni di daa sɔbiya, ni, ‘Naawunni n yɛ wulisi sɔkam zaa.’ Ŋɔn kam zaa wum n‑tisi u Ba Naawunni ka baŋŋi u wulisigu wa gbinni nin kyɛ n saani na.

english_verse: They started arguing with each other and asked, “How can he give us his flesh to eat?”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ n daa kyɛ ka Yɛhudiyadima maa piili la sugyeaa nangbankpeemni ba sunsukkuni. Ka ba boosiri taaba, ni, “U ya niŋi la wula n‑zaŋŋi u ningbina n‑ti ti ka ti ŋɔbi?”

english_verse: That evening, Jesus' disciples went down to the lake.
translation_verse: Zaanoori ŋɔn daa ti paai, Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑sigi mɔ'ari la ni,

english_verse: and a strong wind was making the water rough.
translation_verse: Lala saha maa, pɛsigu n daa zeera n‑damni koom maa pam.

english_verse: When the disciples had rowed for five or six kilometers, they saw Jesus walking on the water. He kept coming closer to the boat, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliba maa daa duusi nyariŋŋu maa n‑kyaŋŋi la kaman maala ata‑anaasi laasaabu, ka yi ti nya ka Yisa tɔm koom maa zugu n‑kyɛnni m‑pɔtti nyariŋŋu maa na, ka dabeem daa gbaai ba.

english_verse: But he said, “I am Jesus! Don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya, ka mani nla!”

english_verse: some boats from Tiberias sailed near the place where the crowd had eaten the bread for which the Lord had given thanks.
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ nyarinseesi n daa yi Taibiriyasi tiŋŋa n‑kyɛ na, halli nti paai hoo ban sa bɛ ya'aseaa la ni, sahanseaa ka Yisa sa puusi Naawunni boroboro maa zugu n‑ti ba ka ba ŋɔbi la.

english_verse: They saw that Jesus and his disciples had left. Then they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka zama maa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, Yisa ka bɛni, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba gba, ba daa kpɛ la lala nyarima maa n‑kyaŋŋi Kapirinyum m‑boori Yisa ziiya.

english_verse: They found him on the west side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa ti paai u ka u bɛ mɔ'ari ya'ari sa maa, ba daa boosi u n‑yɛli, ni, “Ti Ba Rabbi, bɔn saha ka i paai kpa na?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that you are not looking for me because you saw the miracles, but because you ate all the food you wanted.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, di pa la yan sa nya makkri tunkarigu m‑baŋŋi ka gbinni maa zugu ka ya boori n zii maa, amaa yan sa ŋɔbi paanu maa n‑tigi la zugu ka ya boori ma.

english_verse: “What exactly does God want us to do?” the people asked.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, “Ka ti ya niŋi la wula n‑tum Naawunni ŋɔn boori ni ti tum seem?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “God wants you to have faith in the one he sent.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Naawunni ŋɔn boori yan tum seem nŋɔa#:- ya niŋma yɛdda n‑niŋi ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa tum na maa.”

english_verse: For example, when our ancestors were in the desert, they were given manna to eat. It happened just as the Scriptures say, ‘God gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
translation_verse: Ti yaadima ban daa bɛ yoo ni sahanseaa la, ba daa diri la manna - kaman Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli seem, ni, ‘U daa kyɛ ka paanu yi arizana ni na ka ba di.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then told them, “I tell you for certain that Moses wasn't the one who gave you bread from heaven. My Father is the one who gives you the true bread from heaven.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, di pa la Musa n daa ti ya arizana ni paanu maa, amaa di nyɛ la m Ba Naawunni n tiri ya arizana ni paanu ni yɛlimaŋni maa.

english_verse: And the bread that God gives is the one who came down from heaven to give life to the world.”
translation_verse: Naawunni ŋɔn tisiri paanuseelli maa nyɛ la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yi arizana ni na n‑zaŋŋi nyɔvuri n‑tiri duniya dima maa.”

english_verse: The people said, “Sir, give us this bread and don't ever stop!”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa susi u, ni, “Ka dinŋɔna, ti Bapira, kur zaŋŋma paanu wa n‑ti ti sahakam!”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I am the bread that gives life! No one who comes to me will ever be hungry. No one who has faith in me will ever be thirsty.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli, “Mani n nyɛ paanu wa kan tiri nyɔvuri la. Ŋɔnkam kyɛ n saani na - kɔm ku laa n‑gbaai u; ka ŋɔnkam mun niŋi ma yɛdda - kɔnyuuri ku laa n‑gbaai u yaasa.

english_verse: I have told you already that you have seen me and still do not have faith in me.
translation_verse: Amaa m pun yɛli ya, ni, ya pun nya ma amaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda.

english_verse: Everything and everyone that the Father has given me will come to me, and I won't turn any of them away.
translation_verse: M Ba ŋɔn zaŋŋi sɔ zaa n‑ti ma la nin kyɛ n saani na. Man mun ku kari sɔ zaa ŋɔn kyɛ n saani na;

english_verse: I didn't come from heaven to do what I want! I came to do what the Father wants me to do. He sent me,
translation_verse: dama man bu yi arizana ni n‑sigi na ni n tum la m maŋtoori yuubu, amaa Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu ka n tumna.

english_verse: and he wants to make certain that none of the ones he has given me will be lost. Instead, he wants me to raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Ka Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu maa nŋɔa#:- ni, m mari Ŋɔn ti ma bunseelikam; ni, n di kyɛ ka sɔ zaa baasi yɔri, amaa ka n kyɛ ka sɔkam yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

english_verse: My Father wants everyone who sees the Son to have faith in him and to have eternal life. Then I will raise them to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Yaasa, Ŋɔn tum ma na la yuubu maa nŋɔa#:- ni, ŋɔnkam nya Naawunni Bii maa ka niŋi u yɛdda la nin nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga, ka n nin kyɛ ka u yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.”

english_verse: The people started grumbling because Jesus had said he was the bread that had come down from heaven.
translation_verse: Ka Yɛhudiya dima wa daa piili n‑nyurimni ba kparima n‑niŋni u, u yɛligu zugu, ŋɔn yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la paanuseelli din yi arizana ni n‑sigi na maa.

english_verse: They were asking each other, “Isn't he Jesus, the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father and mother? How can he say that he has come down from heaven?”
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛtti taaba, ni, “Pa Gyoosɛfi bii Yisa n nyɛ u? Ti mi u ba ni u ma - ka wula ka u yɛtti, ni, u yi la arizana ni na?”

english_verse: The only one who has seen the Father is the one who has come from him. No one else has ever seen the Father.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ bu wulisi, ni, sɔ na mi n‑nya Naawunni ti Ba maa; ŋɔn bɛ Naawunni saani la kɔkɔ n na mi n‑nya u Ba maa.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that everyone who has faith in me has eternal life.
translation_verse: N yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni: ŋɔn kam zaa niŋi yɛdda mari nyɔvuriseaa kan ka tariga.

english_verse: I am the bread that gives life!
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ nyɔvuri boroboro.

english_verse: Your ancestors ate manna in the desert, and later they died.
translation_verse: Ya yaadima daa di la manna maa yoo la ni, amaa ka daa kpiya.

english_verse: But the bread from heaven has come down, so that no one who eats it will ever die.
translation_verse: Amaa boroboroseaa kan yi arizana ni n‑sigi na maa nŋɔa; ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi ka ku kpi yaasa.

english_verse: I am that bread from heaven! Everyone who eats it will live forever. My flesh is the life-giving bread I give to the people of this world.
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ nyɔvuri boroboro kan yi arizana ni na maa. Ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi lala boroboro maa, u nin bɛ ni halli saha kan ka naari. Boroboroseaa man ŋɔn tisiri maa nyɛ la n ningbiŋŋu, ka n zaŋŋni n‑tisira, din nin kyɛ ka duniya wa dima ti nya nyɔvuri.”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that you won't live unless you eat the flesh and drink the blood of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, “Man yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni: ya yi bu ŋɔbi Ninsaala Bii ningbiŋŋu numni ka nyu u ziim la, ya ku nya nyɔvuri ya mammansi ni.

english_verse: But if you do eat my flesh and drink my blood, you will have eternal life, and I will raise you to life on the last day.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi n ningbiŋŋu numni ka nyu n ziim maa mari nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka n nin kyɛ ka u yisigi kum ni na Zaadaari maa.

english_verse: My flesh is the true food, and my blood is the true drink.
translation_verse: Dama n ningbina n nyɛ bundirimaŋni; ka n ziim maa nyɛ bunnyuurimaŋni.

english_verse: If you eat my flesh and drink my blood, you are one with me, and I am one with you.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn kam zaa ŋɔbi n ningbiŋŋu wa ka nyu n ziim wa, di daama bɛ la ma ni ka man gba bɛ u ni.

english_verse: The living Father sent me, and I have life because of him. Now everyone who eats my flesh will live because of me.
translation_verse: M Ba sɔ ŋɔn vu maa n tum ma na, u zugu ka n bɛ n nyɔvuri ni. Ka lala n nyɛ di n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ŋɔbi n ningbiŋŋu maa - ŋɔn gba nin bɛ u nyɔvuri ni man zugu.

english_verse: The bread that comes down from heaven isn't like what your ancestors ate. They died, but whoever eats this bread will live forever.
translation_verse: Tɔ, kanŋɔ nyɛ la boroboroseaa kan yi arizana ni n‑sigi na; di pa la ya yaadima ŋɔn daa ŋɔbi boroboro ka daa kpi la taabu. Ŋɔn kam ŋɔbi boroboro wa nin bɛ ni halli saha kan ka naari.”

english_verse: Jesus was teaching in a synagogue in Capernaum when he said these things. The Words of Eternal Life
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wulisiri Kapirinyum la'aŋŋu duu la ni, ka u daa tɔ'asi dinŋɔa.

english_verse: Many of Jesus' disciples heard him and said, “This is too hard for anyone to understand.”
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliba maa pam daa wum dinŋɔ ka yɛli, ni, “Wulisigu wa kpeema pam nti ya'ai. Ŋɔni n yɛ n‑toaai n‑wum n‑ti ka?”

english_verse: What if you should see the Son of Man go up to heaven where he came from?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ya yi da nya ka Ninsaala Bii labri ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ya'aseaa la, fa?

english_verse: The Spirit is the one who gives life! Human strength can do nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are from that life-giving Spirit.
translation_verse: Bunseelli din tisiri nyɔvuri nyɛ la Naawunni Siiya Kasi: ninsaala yiiko ka tunsɛseaa halli beela. Yɛttɔ'aseesi man tɔ'asi n‑ti ya maa nyɛ la Sii maa yɛla n‑tiri nyɔvuri na.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “You cannot come to me, unless the Father makes you want to come. That is why I have told these things to all of you.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli m‑paasi, ni, “Nŋɔ zugu ka n yɛli ya, ni, sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑kyɛ n saani na ka di pa ni m Ba maa n tisi soori ka u niŋi lala.”

english_verse: Because of what Jesus said, many of his disciples turned their backs on him and stopped following him.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka u nyaandɔliba maa pam daa ŋmaligi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑labi ka bu laa n‑dɔli u yaasa.

english_verse: Jesus then asked his twelve disciples if they also were going to leave him.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi u nyaandɔliba piiya ni ayi la, ni, “Ka yarima muna, ya nin boori ya gba bakki?”

english_verse: Simon Peter answered, “Lord, there is no one else that we can go to! Your words give eternal life.
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon Piita yɛli, ni, “N dugma, ŋɔni saani ka ti ya kyaŋŋi? Nyini m mari yɛliseesi an tisiri nyɔvuri kan bu naara.

english_verse: We have faith in you, and we are sure that you are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: Ka tirim mun yi niŋi yɛdda m‑baŋŋi, ni, nyini n nyɛ ninvugkasisɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “I chose all twelve of you, but one of you is a demon!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli, ni, “Mani n gamsi yarima ninvugpiiya ni ayi wa, bee m ma'ai ni? Di zaa yɔri, ya yinni nyɛ la Sintaani nira!”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot. He would later betray Jesus, even though he was one of the twelve disciples.
translation_verse: U daa tɔ'asiri la Saimoon Isikariyɔti bii Gyuudasi yɛla. Dama, halli ni ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ni yinni maa zaa yɔri, ŋɔna n daa nyɛ ŋɔn ti ya figsi u maa.

english_verse: Jesus decided to leave Judea and to start going through Galilee because the leaders of the people wanted to kill him.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa Yisa daa gɔ n‑gyiligi Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni. U daa bu boori u gɔ n‑gyiligi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni, dama Yɛhudiya dima maa daa boori la ba ku u.

english_verse: It was almost time for the Festival of Shelters,
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya dima Suga Kyuu saha daa ti pɔtta,

english_verse: and Jesus' brothers said to him, “Why don't you go to Judea? Then your disciples can see what you are doing.
translation_verse: ka Yisa sunzɔppa maa daa yɛli u, ni, “Kur kyɛli nyin bɛ ya'aseaa wa ka kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni; lala ka i nyaandɔliba maa nin nya tunseesi nyin yi niŋni maa.

english_verse: No one does anything in secret, if they want others to know about them. So let the world know what you are doing!”
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ yi boori ni niriba baŋŋi u yɛla, u bu sɔ'ari n‑tumna. I yi ya tum anŋɔ taabu, kur kyɛ ka duniya zaa nya.”

english_verse: Even Jesus' own brothers had not yet become his followers.
translation_verse: Halli u sunzɔppa maa sɔsɔ daa bu niŋi u yɛdda gba.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “My time hasn't yet come, but your time is always here.
translation_verse: Dizugu ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “N saha maa na bu paai. Yarima muna - sahaseaa zaa niŋi la deede n‑ti ya.

english_verse: The people of this world cannot hate you. They hate me, because I tell them that they do evil things.
translation_verse: Duniya maa ku toaai n‑yaai ya; amaa man mun ka duniya maa yaai, dama man yi yɛtti ka, ni, ka sɔya bu veeli maa.

english_verse: Go on to the festival. My time hasn't yet come, and I am not going.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya kyɛma Kyuu maa ni. Man bu wa Kyuu wa ni, dama saha maa bu paai ma na.”

english_verse: Jesus said this and stayed on in Galilee. Jesus at the Festival of Shelters
translation_verse: U daa yɛli nŋɔa, ka daa kpalim m‑bɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni.

english_verse: After Jesus' brothers had gone to the festival, he went secretly, without telling anyone.
translation_verse: Amaa u sunzɔppa maa ŋɔn daa ti gaari Kyuu maa ni, Yisa gba daa kyaŋŋiya. U daa bu yi paalu ni, amaa ka sɔ'ai ni.

english_verse: During the festival the leaders of the people looked for Jesus and asked, “Where is he?”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yɛhudiya dima maa daa boori u ziiya Kyuu maa ni, ka daa boosiri taaba, ni, “U lee?”

english_verse: The crowds even got into an argument about him. Some were saying, “Jesus is a good man,” while others were saying, “He is lying to everyone.”
translation_verse: Ka daadam maa daa sɔ'ai n‑tɔ'asiri taaba n‑yɛtti u yɛla halli n‑ŋmeeri taaba nangbankpeemni, dama baseeba daa yɛtti, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu, ka seeba mun yɛtti, ni, “Ay! Di pa lala - u yɔmsiri la niriba.”

english_verse: But the people were afraid of their leaders, and none of them talked in public about him.
translation_verse: Amaa sɔsɔ daa bu yi paalu na n‑tɔ'asi u yɛla, dama ba daa zɔri la Yɛhudiya dima maa dabeem.

english_verse: When the festival was about half over, Jesus went into the temple and started teaching.
translation_verse: Kyuu maa dabsa purigri daa na paai na pɔi, ka Yisa naayi kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni nti piili bulisigu.

english_verse: The leaders were surprised and said, “How does this man know so much? He has never been taught!”
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi Yɛhudiya dima maa alahazibu, ka ba yɛli, “Di niŋi la wula ka doo wa mi bukku pam lala, ka sɔ mun na zi n‑wulisi u?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I am not teaching something I thought up. What I teach comes from the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Man n bulisiri seela pa la m mammaŋŋa bulisigu, amaa di yi la Ŋɔn tum ma na la saani na.

english_verse: If you really want to obey God, you will know if what I teach comes from God or from me.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn boori ni u niŋi Naawunni ŋɔn boori seela maa, ŋɔna nin baŋŋi man bulisiri seelli maa yɛla, ni, di yi la ŋɔn Naawunni saani na, bee, n tɔ'asiri la m maŋtoori yiiko.

english_verse: If I wanted to bring honor to myself, I would speak for myself. But I want to honor the one who sent me. This is why I tell the truth and not a lie.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asiri ni u toori yiiko nyɛ la ŋɔn boori pɛ'abu ni gyirima n‑tiri u maŋŋa. Amaa ŋɔn boori pɛ'abu ni gyirima n‑tiri ŋɔn tum u maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka galinsi ka u saani.

english_verse: Didn't Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you obey it! So why do you want to kill me?
translation_verse: Ammi, Musa daa ti ya zaaligu maa, bee, u daa bu tisi? Amaa ya sɔsɔ bu sakki n‑dɔli zaaligu maa: wula zugu ka ya boori ni ya ku ma?”

english_verse: The crowd replied, “You're crazy! What makes you think someone wants to kill you?”
translation_verse: Ka zama maa daa labsi, ni, “I mari la arizimbeoo i ni! Ŋɔni n boori ni u ku i?”

english_verse: Moses commanded you to circumcise your sons. But it wasn't really Moses who gave you this command. It was your ancestors, and even on the Sabbath you circumcise your sons
translation_verse: Amaa Musa daa ti ya bidibsi gunibu kaari - halli di daa pa la Musa n daa piili di, amaa ya yaadima maa - ka di zugu ya zaŋŋni biisi n‑guntira, halli Vuusim Dabsiri daari gba.

english_verse: in order to obey the Law of Moses. Why are you angry with me for making someone completely well on the Sabbath?
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ba yi guni biiya Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa, ni, ba di burigri Musa zaaligu yɛla maa, wula ka ya suuri yisigri n‑niŋni mani, man ŋɔn kyɛ ka nira nya alaafɛ zaa Vuusim Dabsiri daari ni?

english_verse: Don't judge by appearances. Judge by what is right.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛli birikɔnsi sariya karibu din dɔli ya nini gɔsibu ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ka katti sariyamaŋni.”

english_verse: Some of the people from Jerusalem were saying, “Isn't this the man they want to kill?
translation_verse: Ka Gyɛrusalɛm niriba seeba daa yɛli, ni, “Pa doo wa ka kpambaya boori ni ba ku la?

english_verse: Yet here he is, speaking for everyone to hear. And no one is arguing with him. Do you suppose the authorities know he is the Messiah?
translation_verse: Nyama, u bɛ la zama ni n‑tɔ'asira ka ba bu yɛli sɛseela n‑galim u! Kala ba siri mi, ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya maa?

english_verse: But how could that be? No one knows where the Messiah will come from, but we know where this man comes from.”
translation_verse: Amaa Masiiya maa yi ti kyɛ na, sɔsɔ n bu ti yɛ baŋŋi ŋɔn yi ya'aseaa na. Tirima mun zaasa mi doo wa ŋɔn yi ya'aseelli ni na.”

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he shouted, “Do you really think you know me and where I came from? I didn't come on my own! The one who sent me is truthful, and you don't know him.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wulisiri Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni la, u daa tɔ'asi ni kukɔtitaari n‑yɛli, ni, “Kala ya siri mi ma ka baŋŋi man yi ya'aseelli na? N bu kyɛ na m mammantoori yiiko puuni. Ŋɔn tum ma na maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana. Yarima bu mi u,

english_verse: But I know the one who sent me, because I came from him.”
translation_verse: amaa man mi u, dama n yi la u saani na, ŋɔna mun daa tum ma na.”

english_verse: Some of the people wanted to arrest Jesus right then. But no one even laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
translation_verse: Ba daa boori la ba gbaai u, amaa sɔsɔ daa bu zaŋŋi u nuu n‑siisi u, dama u saha daa na bu paai.

english_verse: A lot of people in the crowd put their faith in him and said, “When the Messiah comes, he surely won't perform more miracles than this man has done!” Officers Sent To Arrest Jesus
translation_verse: Amaa daadam maa puuni niriba pampam daa niŋi u yɛdda ka daa yɛli, ni, “Kala Masiiya maa yi ti kyɛ na, u nin ti toaai n‑tum alahazibu tuma n‑gaari doo wa ŋɔn tum seem wa?”

english_verse: When the Pharisees heard the crowd arguing about Jesus, they got together with the chief priests and sent some temple police to arrest him.
translation_verse: Farisii dima maa daa wum daadam maa ŋɔn daa waasiri n‑tɔ'asiri Yisa yɛla seem maa. Dinzugu Farisii dima maa ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa tum ba Pirinsi ni ba ti gbaai Yisa.

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I will be with you a little while longer, and then I will return to the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛliya, ni, “N yɛ bɛ ya saani la saha beela kɔkɔa, ka n nin ti laa m‑bakki n‑labi Ŋɔn daa tum ma na maa saani.

english_verse: You will look for me, but you won't find me. You cannot go where I am going.”
translation_verse: Ya nin ti bɔ n ziiya, amaa ya ku ti nya ma, dama ya ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi man ti yɛ m‑bɛ seela maa.”

english_verse: The people asked each other, “Where can he go to keep us from finding him? Is he going to some foreign country where our people live? Is he going there to teach the Greeks?
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya dima maa daa yɛli taaba, ni, “Ya'akani ka u wa ka ti bu yɛ nya u? U ti yɛ n‑kyaŋŋi la ti niriba ŋɔn bɛ Griika tinseesi la ni n‑wulisiri ban, bee?

english_verse: What did he mean by saying that we will look for him, but won't find him? Why can't we go where he is going?” Streams of Life-Giving Water
translation_verse: U yɛliya, ni, ti nin ti bɔ u ziiya ka ku nya u; ni, ti mun ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn ti yɛ bɛ seela maa. Di wulisi la wula?”

english_verse: On the last and most important day of the festival, Jesus stood up and shouted, “If you are thirsty, come to me and drink!
translation_verse: Kyuu maa naari ni - ka dabsitibsiri maa daari nla - Yisa daa doaai saazugu n‑tɔ'asi ni kukɔtitaari n‑yɛli, ni, “Kɔnyuuri yi mari sɔ zaa, u kyɛm n saani na nti nyu.

english_verse: Have faith in me, and you will have life-giving water flowing from deep inside you, just as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Kaman Litaafi ŋɔn pun yɛli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔnkam niŋi ma yɛdda, ni, ‘nyɔvuri kɔpɔbrim nin buli n‑yi u sufu ni na.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about the Holy Spirit, who would be given to everyone that had faith in him. The Spirit had not yet been given to anyone, since Jesus had not yet been given his full glory. The People Take Sides
translation_verse: Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑kpa Sii Kasi yɛla, ni, ban niŋi u yɛdda la nin ti yɛ deaai Sii maa. Lala saha maa Sii Kasi maa daa na bu kyɛ na, dama Yisa daa na bu paai naam gyirima neesim maa ni.

english_verse: When the crowd heard Jesus say this, some of them said, “He must be the Prophet!”
translation_verse: Daadam maa ni ninvugseeba daa wum ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi seem maa ka daa yɛli, ni, “Doo wa siri nyɛ la anabi maa!”

english_verse: Others said, “He is the Messiah!” Others even said, “Can the Messiah come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Ka seeba daa yɛli, ni, “Ŋɔna n nyɛ Masiiya la!” Amaa ka seeba mun yɛli, ni, “Masiiya maa bu yɛ yi Galilii na.

english_verse: The Scriptures say that the Messiah will come from the family of King David. Doesn't this mean that he will be born in David's hometown of Bethlehem?”
translation_verse: Litaafi yɛliya, ni, Masiiya maa yɛ yi la NaaDauda za'anɔri ni na, ka u ti yi Bɛtlihɛm tiŋŋa ni na, tinseaa NaaDauda ŋɔn daa bɛ ni la.”

english_verse: The people started taking sides against each other because of Jesus.
translation_verse: Dinzugu nangbanwala daa bɛ daadam maa puuni Yisa zugu.

english_verse: Some of them wanted to arrest him, but no one laid a hand on him. The Leaders Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus
translation_verse: Ba seeba daa boori la ba gbaai u, amaa sɔsɔ daa bu zaŋŋi u nuu n‑siisi u.

english_verse: When the temple police returned to the chief priests and Pharisees, they were asked, “Why didn't you bring Jesus here?”
translation_verse: Pirinsi ŋɔn daa ti labi gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa ni Farisii dima maa saani na, ba daa boosi ba, ni, “Wula ka ya bu mari u na?”

english_verse: They answered, “No one has ever spoken like this man!”
translation_verse: Ka Pirinsi maa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Sɔ na zi n‑tɔ'asi hoo doo maa yɛttɔ'a wa taabu!”

english_verse: The Pharisees said to them, “Have you also been fooled?
translation_verse: Ka Farisii dima maa boosi ba, ni, “U na'a yɔmsi ya gba ni?

english_verse: Not one of the chief priests or the Pharisees has faith in him.
translation_verse: Ya na mi n‑nya ka kpambaya, bee, Farisii dima ninvugyinni n niŋi u yɛdda?

english_verse: And these people who don't know the Law are under God's curse anyway.”
translation_verse: Ay! - Amaa daadam wa zaa bu mi Musa zaalikpana la ŋɔn nyɛ seem, dinzugu ba bɛ la mbusu ni!”

english_verse: Nicodemus was there at the time. He was a member of the council, and was the same one who had earlier come to see Jesus. He said,
translation_verse: Ninvugtibsa wa yinni ŋɔn daa bɛ bɛni daa nyɛ la Nikɔdiimusi - dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa pun kyaŋŋi nti nya Yisa la. U daa yɛli u taaba maa, ni,

english_verse: “Our Law doesn't let us condemn people before we hear what they have to say. We cannot judge them before we know what they have done.”
translation_verse: “Ti zaaligu soori puuni, ti ku toaai n‑tu sɔ pɔi ka naayi yi wum u yɛttɔ'a, bee, ka naayi boosi m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn niŋi seela maa.”

english_verse: Then they said, “Nicodemus, you must be from Galilee! Read the Scriptures, and you will find that no prophet is to come from Galilee.” A Woman Caught in Sin
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, “Eeh! - i gba yi la Galilii na, bee? Kur gɔsim Litaafi maa suŋŋa: i nin baŋŋi, ni, anabisɔ bu yɛ yi Galilii na.” [[

english_verse: but Jesus walked out to the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi nti paai Ɔlivii Zoori luga ni.

english_verse: Then early the next morning he went to the temple. The people came to him, and he sat down and started teaching them.
translation_verse: Asuba bɛkyikyeoo ka Yisa daa laa n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Ka niriba maa zaa daa la'asi n‑gyiligi u na, ka u daa zinni tiŋŋa m‑piili n‑wulisiri ba.

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses brought in a woman who had been caught in bed with a man who wasn't her husband. They made her stand in the middle of the crowd.
translation_verse: Di saha ka zaaligu karimbadima ni Farisii dima daa pun gbaai pɔ'asɔ ka u bɛ zina tumpu ni. Ka ba daa mari pɔ'a maa n‑kyɛ na, ka zaali u ba zaasa tooni.

english_verse: Then they said, “Teacher, this woman was caught sleeping with a man who isn't her husband.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli Yisa, ni, “Ti Karimba, nyama! Pɔ'a wa ka ba gbaai ka u bɛ zina tumpu ni.

english_verse: The Law of Moses teaches that a woman like this should be stoned to death! What do you say?”
translation_verse: Ti zaaligu la puuni, Musa yɛliya lala taabu pɔ'a yɛla, ni, ba zaŋŋma kuga n‑lɔbi u nti ku u. Ka nyin mun yɛli la wula?”

english_verse: They asked Jesus this question, because they wanted to test him and bring some charge against him. But Jesus simply bent over and started writing on the ground with his finger.
translation_verse: Ba daa yɛli lala ni ba zamsi Yisa n‑nya yɛttɔ'aseaa din nin kyɛ ka ba galim u. Amaa ka Yisa daa sunni u zugu ka mari u nubila n‑sɔbri n‑niŋni tiŋŋa.

english_verse: They kept on asking Jesus about the woman. Finally, he stood up and said, “If any of you have never sinned, then go ahead and throw the first stone at her!”
translation_verse: Ban daa zɛ bɛni ka boosiri u boosa maa, u daa wukki u zugu ka yɛli ba, ni, “Ya ninvugsɔ ŋɔn na zi n‑tum ala'akyi zaa, di daama ŋɔn daŋŋma tooni n‑zaŋŋi kugri n‑lɔbi u.”

english_verse: Once again he bent over and began writing on the ground.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka u daa laa n‑sunni u zugu yaasa ka sɔbri n‑niŋni tiŋŋa.

english_verse: The people left one by one, beginning with the oldest. Finally, Jesus and the woman were there alone.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti wum dinŋɔa, ba zaa daa bakkiya n‑kyaŋŋi kpaŋŋa, halli ka ba kpamma maa n daa daŋŋi bakkri. Di daa kpalim la Yisa kɔkɔa, ka pɔ'a maa na kur kpalim n‑zɛ bɛni.

english_verse: Jesus stood up and asked her, “Where is everyone? Isn't there anyone left to accuse you?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa wukki u zugu ka boosi pɔ'a maa, ni, “Ba lee? Sɔsɔ bu laa n‑kpalim ni u galim i?”

english_verse: “No sir,” the woman answered. Then Jesus told her, “I am not going to accuse you either. You may go now, but don't sin anymore.” Jesus Is the Light for the World
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa yɛli, ni, “M Ba, sɔ bu kpalim.” Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “Man gba bu ya n‑galim i. Kyɛma, amaa di laa n‑tumni tumbeeri yaasa.”]]

english_verse: Once again Jesus spoke to the people. This time he said, “I am the light for the world! Follow me, and you won't be walking in the dark. You will have the light that gives life.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti niriba maa yaasa, ni, “Mani n nyɛ duniya wa neesim. Ŋɔn kam zaa dɔli ma nin ti nya nyɔvuri neesim, ka ku laa m‑bɛ zibsim puuni n‑kyɛnna.”

english_verse: The Pharisees objected, “You are the only one speaking for yourself, and what you say isn't true!”
translation_verse: Farisii dima maa daa yɛli u, ni, “Ay! Nyin mammantoori sɛhara ka i dira; nyin ŋɔn tɔ'asi seem wa ka gbinni.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Even if I do speak for myself, what I say is true! I know where I came from and where I am going. But you don't know where I am from or where I am going.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, “Ay! Halli man yi pun diri m mammantoori sɛhara maa zaa yɔri, man yɛtti seem maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, dama m mi man yi ya'aseelli na, ni man wa ya'aseela. Yarima mun bu mi man yi seelli na, bee, man wa seela.

english_verse: You judge in the same way that everyone else does, but I don't judge anyone.
translation_verse: Ya katti la sariya n‑dɔli la ya ninsaala soori; man mun bu katti sɔ sariya.

english_verse: If I did judge, I would judge fairly, because I would not be doing it alone. The Father who sent me is here with me.
translation_verse: Amaa n yi da ya n‑kari sariya, n sariya karibu maa da yɛ nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, dama pa la n kɔkɔ m‑bɛ kpa wa; m Ba daa tum ma na, ka ŋɔna n bɛ n saani.

english_verse: I am one of my witnesses, and the Father who sent me is the other one.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka mani mammaŋŋa n nyɛ n sɛhara daana, ka M Ba sɔ ŋɔn tum ma na maa gba nyɛ n sɛhara daana yinni.”

english_verse: “Where is your Father?” they asked. “You don't know me or my Father!” Jesus answered. “If you knew me, you would know my Father.”
translation_verse: Ka ba boosi u, ni, “I ba maa lee?” Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya bu mi ma, ya mun bu mi m Ba maa. Ya yi da mi ma, ya da naa m‑baŋŋi m Ba maa gba.”

english_verse: Jesus said this while he was still teaching in the place where the temple treasures were stored. But no one arrested him, because his time had not yet come. You Cannot Go Where I Am Going
translation_verse: Yisa daa tɔ'asi dinŋɔ maa zaa Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni, duseaa piini adakka la ŋɔn daa zɛya la ni. Sɔsɔ mun daa bu gbaai u, dama u saha maa daa na bu paai.

english_verse: Jesus also told them, “I am going away, and you will look for me. But you cannot go where I am going, and you will die with your sins unforgiven.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba yaasa, ni, “N nin ti bakki kpaŋŋa ka kyɛ ya; ya nin boori n ziiya, amaa ya nin ti kpi ya mammantoori tumbeeri tumpu puuni. Ya ku nyaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man wa ya'aseaa maa.”

english_verse: The people asked, “Does he intend to kill himself? Is that what he means by saying we cannot go where he is going?”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Yɛhudiya dima maa daa yɛli, ni, “Ni, ti ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn wa ya'aseelli maa. Dinŋɔ wulisiya, ni, u yɛ n‑ku la u mammaŋŋa, bee?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “You are from below, but I am from above. You belong to this world, but I don't.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya nyɛ la tiŋni wa dima, amaa man yi la saazugu sa na. Yarima yi la duniya wa ni na, amaa man mun bu yi duniya wa ni na.

english_verse: This is why I said you will die with your sins unforgiven. If you don't have faith in me for who I am, you will die, and your sins will not be forgiven.”
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka n pun yɛli ya, ni, ya ti ya n‑kpi la ya tumbeeri tumpu puuni. Ya yi bu ti niŋi yɛdda, ni, ‘Mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn m Bɛ Ni maa’, ya siri nin ti kpi ya tumbeeri puuni.”

english_verse: “Who are you?” they asked Jesus. Jesus answered, “I am exactly who I told you at the beginning.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boosi u, ni, “Nyini n nyɛ ŋɔni?” Ka Yisa yɛli ba, ni, “Man n daa yɛli ya seem maa n‑yi piiligu la ni na.

english_verse: There is a lot more I could say to condemn you. But the one who sent me is truthful, and I tell the people of this world only what I have heard from him.”
translation_verse: Ya zugu m mari yɛla pampam n ya n‑tɔ'asi, m mari la yɛla pam ni n galim ya. Ŋɔn tum ma na maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka n yɛtti la man wum seem u saani maa n‑tiri duniya wa dima.”

english_verse: No one understood that Jesus was talking to them about the Father.
translation_verse: Ba daa bu baŋŋi, ni, Yisa yɛtti la u Ba yɛla.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, you will know who I am. You will also know that I don't do anything on my own. I say only what my Father taught me.
translation_verse: Dinzugu u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya yi zaŋŋi man Ninsaala Bii maa n‑duusi saazugu, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, n nyɛ la ‘Mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn m Bɛ Ni maa’; di saha maa ka ya nin ti baŋŋi, ni, n bu niŋni bunsɛseela m mammantoori yiiko ni, amaa n yɛtti la yɛttɔ'aseesi m Ba maa ŋɔn wulisi ma ni n yɛlim seem maa.

english_verse: The one who sent me is with me. I always do what pleases him, and he will never leave me.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔn tum ma na la bɛ la n saani; u bu kyɛ ma m‑basi n kɔkɔa, dama n tumni n‑tigri la u nini saha kam zaa.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, many of the people put their faith in him. The Truth Will Set You Free
translation_verse: Niriba pam ban daa wum Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem wa daa niŋi u yɛdda.

english_verse: Jesus told the people who had faith in him, “If you keep on obeying what I have said, you truly are my disciples.
translation_verse: Yisa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ban daa niŋi u yɛdda maa n‑yɛli, ni, “Ya yi sakki n‑deaai n wulisigu maa, ya siri nyɛ la n nyaandɔliba.

english_verse: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
translation_verse: Ya nin baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni maa, ka yɛlimaŋni maa nin ti kyɛ ka ya ti su ya mansi.”

english_verse: They answered, “We are Abraham's children! We have never been anyone's slaves. How can you say we will be set free?”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, “Ti nyɛ la Ibrahim butiri dima; ti na zi n‑niŋi daaba n‑ti sɔsɔa. I wulisi la bɔa? - nyin ŋɔn yɛtti, ni, ‘Ya nin ti su ya mammansi.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus replied: I tell you for certain that anyone who sins is a slave of sin!
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “N yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni: ŋɔn kam zaa tumni tumbeeri nyɛ la daabri n‑ti tumbeeri maa. Ala'akyi n su u.

english_verse: And slaves don't stay in the family forever, though the Son will always remain in the family.
translation_verse: Daabri pa la ŋɔn ya n‑kpalim n‑kur bɛ yiri maa ni, amaa yiri biiya mun nyɛ la ŋɔn ya n‑kpalim n‑kur bɛ yiri maa ni halli sahakam.

english_verse: I know that you are from Abraham's family. Yet you want to kill me, because my message isn't really in your hearts.
translation_verse: M mi halli, ni, ya nyɛ la Ibrahim butiri ni dima. Amaa di zaa yɔri ya boori la ya ku ma, dama ya bu boori ya sakki n‑deaai n yɛligu.

english_verse: I am telling you what my Father has shown me, just as you are doing what your father has taught you. Your Father Is the Devil
translation_verse: N tɔ'asiri la man daa nya m Ba saani yɛliseesi maa, ni ŋɔn wulisi ma seem; amaa ya mun tumni la yan ŋɔn daa wum seem ya toori ba la saani.”

english_verse: The people said to Jesus, “Abraham is our father!” Jesus replied, “If you were Abraham's children, you would do what Abraham did.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ti ba n nyɛ Ibrahim.” Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya yi da siri nyɛ Ibrahim biisi, ya naa n‑niŋi ŋɔn daa niŋni seem maa.

english_verse: Instead, you want to kill me for telling you the truth that God gave me. Abraham never did anything like that.
translation_verse: Man yɛtti ya seem la zaa kur nyɛ la yɛlimanseaa man wum Naawunni saani la, amaa di zaa yɔri, ya boori la ya ku ma. Ibrahim daa bu niŋi nŋɔ taabu!

english_verse: But you are doing exactly what your father does.” “Don't accuse us of having someone else as our father!” they said. “We just have one father, and he is God.”
translation_verse: Ay! - Ya niŋni la ya ba ŋɔn niŋni seem la.” Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, “Naawunni mammaŋŋa kɔkɔ n nyɛ ti Ba, ka ti gba siri nyɛ u biisi - ti pa la ban ka daandima!”

english_verse: Jesus answered: If God were your Father, you would love me, because I came from God and now I am here. He sent me. I did not come on my own.
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Naawunni yi da siri nyɛ ya Ba, ya naa n‑wuri ma, dama n yi la Naawunni saani na, ka yi bɛ kpa wa pampaŋŋɔ maa. N daa bu kyɛ na la m mammantoori yiiko puuni, amaa ŋɔna n daa tum ma na.

english_verse: Why can't you understand what I am talking about? Can't you stand to hear what I am saying?
translation_verse: Bɔ n taasi ka ya bu baŋŋi man yɛtti seem maa gbinni? Di nyɛ la yan bu boori ni ya wum n lahabaari maa zugu.

english_verse: Your father is the devil, and you do exactly what he wants. He has always been a murderer and a liar. There is nothing truthful about him. He speaks on his own, and everything he says is a lie. Not only is he a liar himself, but he is also the father of all lies.
translation_verse: Ya nyɛ la ya ba Sintaani biisi, ka ya dɔli la ya ba maa sufu yuubu ŋɔn boori seem zaasa. Piiligu ni sa u daa pun nyɛ la ninvugkuura, ka daa na zi n‑labi yɛlimaŋni ya'ari na, dama yɛlimaŋni ka u ni. U naaŋŋu pun nyɛ u ma'ai ziri, dama ŋɔna n‑nyɛ ziridima zaa ba.

english_verse: Everything I have told you is true, and you still refuse to have faith in me.
translation_verse: Amaa man tɔ'asiri la yɛlimaŋa, dinzugu ka ya bu niŋi ma yɛdda maa.
