english_verse: Can any of you accuse me of sin? If you cannot, why won't you have faith in me? After all, I am telling you the truth.
translation_verse: Ya ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑wulisi n ala'akyi galinsi ŋɔn nyɛ seem? Ka n yi tɔ'asi yɛlimaŋni, wula zugu ka ya bu niŋni ma yɛdda?

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to God will listen to his message. But you refuse to listen, because you don't belong to God. Jesus and Abraham
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na la - di daama n wumni Naawunni yɛla. Amaa yarima mun bu yi u saani na, ka lala maa zugu kyɛ ka ya za'asi wumpu.”

english_verse: The people told Jesus, “We were right to say that you are a Samaritan and that you have a demon in you!”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boosi Yisa, ni, “Kala di daa bu dɔli soori tin daa yɛli, ni, i nyɛ la Samɛrii nira ka arizimbeoo bɛ i ni la?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I don't have a demon in me. I honor my Father, and you refuse to honor me.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Arizimbeoo ka ma ni. N tibgri la m Ba maa, amaa yarima n bu tibgri ma.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that if you obey my words, you will never die.”
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa sakki n‑deaai n wulisigu maa ku kpi halli saha kan ka naari.”

english_verse: Then the people said, “Now we are sure that you have a demon. Abraham is dead, and so are the prophets. How can you say that no one who obeys your words will ever die?
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, “Pampaŋŋɔ ti yi baŋŋiya, ni, i mari la arizimbeoo i ni! Ibrahim daa kpiya, ka anabidima gba daa kpi; ka nyin yɛtti, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa sakki n‑deaai i wulisigu maa kur ku kpi halli saha kan ka naari, fa!

english_verse: Are you greater than our father Abraham? He died, and so did the prophets. Who do you think you are?”
translation_verse: Ti ba Ibrahim daa kpiya, ka anabidima gba daa kpi. Ni, nyini n gaari Ibrahim, bee? Nyin teesiya, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔni?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If I honored myself, it would mean nothing. My Father is the one who honors me. You claim that he is your God,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “N yi da yɛ tibgi m maŋŋa, lala tibsim maa da yɛ nyɛ la yɔri. Ŋɔn kur tiri ma tibsim nyɛ la m Ba maa, ninvugsɔ yan yɛli, ni, u nyɛ ya Naawunni la.

english_verse: even though you don't really know him. If I said I didn't know him, I would be a liar, just like all of you. But I know him, and I do what he says.
translation_verse: Ya na zi m‑baŋŋi u, amaa man ŋɔn mi u. N yi yɛli, ni, n zi u, dinŋɔna n leebi la ziri daana kaman ya taabu. Amaa m mi u, ka sakkri n‑deaari u yɛla.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I tell you for certain that even before Abraham was, I was, and I am.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “N siri yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, pɔi ka ba daa dɔ'ai Ibrahim, ni, ‘Mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn m Bɛ Ni maa.’ ”

english_verse: The people picked up stones to kill Jesus, but he hid and left the temple.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ba daa di piisi kuga ni ba lɔbi Yisa, amaa ka Yisa daa sɔ'ai u mammaŋŋa m‑bakki n‑yi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni.

english_verse: As Jesus walked along, he saw a man who had been blind since birth.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa kyɛnni maa, u daa ti nya dɔsɔ ban daa dɔ'ai ka u pun nyɛ zooma.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples asked, “Teacher, why was this man born blind? Was it because he or his parents sinned?”
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliba daa boosi u n‑yɛli, ni, “Ti Karimba, ŋɔni tumbeeri n‑kyɛ ka ba dɔ'ai u zooma wa? Di daa nyɛ la u mammantoori tumbeeri, bee, u ba ni u ma tumbeeri zugu?”

english_verse: As long as it is day, we must do what the one who sent me wants me to do. When night comes, no one can work.
translation_verse: Din na nyɛ wuntaŋŋa saha wa, faasee ka ti tum n‑tisi Ŋɔn tum ma na maa; yuŋŋu yi ti zibi sɔsɔ ku ti laa n‑toaai n‑tum.

english_verse: While I am in the world, I am the light for the world.”
translation_verse: Man ŋɔn na bɛ duniya wa ni maa, mani n nyɛ duniya neesim.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he spit on the ground. He made some mud and smeared it on the man's eyes.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti yɛli nŋɔ maa nyaaŋŋa, Yisa daa tusi nantoori m‑basi tiŋŋa ka gyɛri n‑gyɛri tanni ni nantoori maa. U daa zaŋŋi tanni maa n‑tari n‑tari doo maa nini

english_verse: Then he said, “Go wash off the mud in Siloam Pool.” The man went and washed in Siloam, which means “One Who Is Sent.” When he had washed off the mud, he could see.
translation_verse: ka yɛli u, “Kyɛma nti peaai i nindaa Sailɔ'am beeŋŋu la ni.” : ka doo maa daa kyaŋŋi nti peaai u nindaa maa ka labi na ka u nini nyara.

english_verse: Some of them said he was the same beggar, while others said he only looked like him. But he told them, “I am that man.”
translation_verse: Seeba daa yɛliya, ni, “Ŋɔna nla, ” amaa ka seeba mun daa yɛli, ni, “Ay, pa ŋɔna nla; u kur ŋmani u ni.” Ka doo maa mammaŋŋa daa ti yɛli, ni, “Ay! - Mani nla.”

english_verse: He answered, “Someone named Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. He told me to go and wash it off in Siloam Pool. When I did, I could see.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Dɔsɔ ban boonni Yisa la n daa niŋi tanni n‑tari n‑tari n nimbiya ka yɛli ma, ni, n kyɛma nti peaai n nindaa Sailɔ'am beeŋŋu la ni. Ka n daa kyaŋŋi, ka man daa ti peaai n nindaa maa n‑naai, n di neaai ni.”

english_verse: “Where is he now?” they asked. “I don't know,” he answered. The Pharisees Try To Find Out What Happened
translation_verse: Ka ba boosi u, ni, “U bɛ la yani?” Ka u labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “N bu mi.”

english_verse: The day when Jesus made the mud and healed the man was a Sabbath. So the people took the man to the Pharisees.
translation_verse: Dindahinseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi tanni n‑tibi doo la zoonsi maa daa nyɛ la Vuusim Dabsiri daari.

english_verse: They asked him how he was able to see, and he answered, “Jesus made some mud and smeared it on my eyes. Then after I washed it off, I could see.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Farisii dima daa laa m‑boosi u ŋɔn niŋi seem ka u nini maa ti nyara maa yaasa. U yɛli ba, ni, “U daa niŋi la tanni n‑niŋi n nimbiya ni; ka n peaai n nindaa maa, ka yi di nyara pampaŋŋɔa.”

english_verse: Some of the Pharisees said, “This man Jesus doesn't come from God. If he did, he would not break the law of the Sabbath.” Others asked, “How could someone who is a sinner work such a miracle?” Since the Pharisees could not agree among themselves,
translation_verse: Farisii dima seeba daa yɛliya, ni, “Dɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi nŋɔ maa ku toaai n‑yi Naawunni saani na, dama u bu dɔli la Vuusim Dabsiri zaaligu.” Ka seeba daa yɛli, ni, “Bɔ zugu ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ala'akyi daana ti tum alahazibu nŋɔ taabu?” Ka ba nɔya daa di wali.

english_verse: they asked the man, “What do you say about this one who healed your eyes?” “He is a prophet!” the man told them.
translation_verse: Ka Farisii dima daa laa m‑boosi doo maa yaasa, ni, “I yɛliya, ni, u tibi la i zoonsi maa: tɔ, ka nyin teesiya, ni, u nyɛ la ŋɔni?” Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Anabi n nyɛ u.”

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders would not believe that the man had once been blind. They sent for his parents
translation_verse: Halli gba Yɛhudiya kpambaya maa daa na bu boori ni ba niŋi yɛdda, ni, u daa nyɛ la zooma ka pampaŋŋɔ n yi nyara, halli ka daa ti booli u ba ni u ma na

english_verse: and asked them, “Is this the son that you said was born blind? How can he now see?”
translation_verse: nti boosi ba, ni, “Ya bii nŋɔa? Ya yɛliya, ni, ya daa pun dɔ'ai u la zooma; tɔ, ka pampaŋŋɔ di kyaŋŋi la wula ka u nini yi nyara?”

english_verse: The man's parents answered, “We are certain that he is our son, and we know that he was born blind.
translation_verse: U ba ni u ma maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Ti mi, ni, u nyɛ la ti biiya, ka ti laa m‑mi, ni, ti daa pun dɔ'ai u la zooma.

english_verse: But we don't know how he got his sight or who gave it to him. Ask him! He is old enough to speak for himself.”
translation_verse: Amaa ti bu baŋŋi pampaŋŋɔ ŋɔn yi niŋi seem nti nyaŋŋi n‑yi nyara maa, ti mun bu mi ŋɔn tibi u zoonsi maa. Boosim u; u pa bibila, u mammaŋŋa nin toaai n‑tɔ'asi n‑labsi!”

english_verse: The man's parents said this because they were afraid of their leaders. The leaders had already agreed that no one was to have anything to do with anyone who said Jesus was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Lala maa zugu ka u ba ni u ma maa daa yɛli, ni, u pa bibila; ni, ba boosim u maa.

english_verse: The man replied, “I don't know if he is a sinner or not. All I know is that I used to be blind, but now I can see!”
translation_verse: Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Man bu mi, ni, u nyɛ la ala'akyi daana, bee, u pa ala'akyi daana. Yɛlli yinni ka man kur mi: n daa zoomna, ka pampaŋŋɔ n mun nini yi nyara.”

english_verse: “What did he do to you?” they asked. “How did he heal your eyes?”
translation_verse: Ba daa boosiya, ni, “Bɔ ka u daa dee n‑niŋi i? U daa dee n‑tibi i zoonsi maa la wula?”

english_verse: The man answered, “I have already told you once, and you refused to listen. Why do you want me to tell you again? Do you also want to become his disciples?”
translation_verse: U daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “N daa pun yɛli ya, ka ya daa za'asi wumpu. Wula zugu ka ya laa m‑boori ya wum ka yaasa? Bee, ya gba boori la ya niŋi u nyaandɔliba?”

english_verse: The leaders insulted the man and said, “You are his follower! We are followers of Moses.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa di gbaai u tuuri ka yɛli u, “Nyini n nyɛ lala doo maa nyaandɔla; amaa tirima ŋɔn mun nyɛ la Musa nyaandɔliba.

english_verse: We are sure God spoke to Moses, but we don't even know where Jesus comes from.”
translation_verse: Ti mun mi, ni, Naawunni daa tɔ'asi n‑ti Musa; amaa lala doo maa ŋɔna, halli ti bu mi ŋɔn yi ya'aseaa na.”

english_verse: “How strange!” the man replied. “He healed my eyes, and yet you don't know where he comes from.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Mma! - alahazibu nŋɔa! Ya bu mi ŋɔn yi ya'aseaa na, amaa u mun tibi ma n zoonsi ni!

english_verse: We know that God listens only to people who love and obey him. God doesn't listen to sinners.
translation_verse: Ti mi, ni, Naawunni bu wumni n‑tiri ala'akyi dima, u wumni n‑tiri la ninvugseeba ban tiri u gyirima ka niŋni ŋɔn boori ba tum seem.

english_verse: And this is the first time in history anyone has ever given sight to someone born blind.
translation_verse: Ti na zi n‑wum, ni, sɔsɔ neaai ban pun dɔ'ai sɔ zooma nini duniya piiligu ni sa.

english_verse: Jesus could not do anything unless he came from God.”
translation_verse: Di yi da pa ni doo maa yi la Naawunni saani na, u naa n‑ku nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi nŋɔ taataabu wa.”

english_verse: The leaders told the man, “You have been a sinner since the day you were born! Do you think you can teach us anything?” Then they said, “You can never come back into any of our synagogues!”
translation_verse: Ka ba labsi, ni, “Ba daa dɔ'ai i ka i zoaai la ala'akyi ni, ka i mun ti laa m‑boori i wulisi tirima?” Ka ba daa kari u n‑yiisi la'aŋŋu duu maa ni.

english_verse: When Jesus heard what had happened, he went and found the man. Then Jesus asked, “Do you have faith in the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum bunseela din daa niŋi maa, u daa bɔ doo maa ziiya nti boosi u, ni, “Kala i niŋi Ninsaala Bii maa yɛdda?”

english_verse: He replied, “Sir, if you will tell me who he is, I will put my faith in him.”
translation_verse: Ka doo maa labsi n‑yɛli, “M Bapira, yɛlim ma ŋɔn nyɛ ninvugsɔa, ka n nin niŋi u yɛdda!”

english_verse: “You have already seen him,” Jesus answered, “and right now he is talking with you.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “I daa pun nya doo maa, halli ka mani, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asiri n‑tiri i wa pampaŋŋɔ, nla.”

english_verse: The man said, “Lord, I put my faith in you!” Then he worshiped Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa di gbaani tiŋŋa Yisa tooni ka yɛli, ni, “N niŋi yɛdda, n dugma!”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “I came to judge the people of this world. I am here to give sight to the blind and to make blind everyone who can see.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛliya, ni, “N kyɛ duniya wa na ni n ti di la sariya, ka zoomma nini neaai ka ban nini nyari mun leebi zoomma.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “If you were blind, you would not be guilty. But now that you claim to see, you will keep on being guilty.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya yi da nyɛ la zoomma dinŋɔna ya da bu tum taalli; amaa yan yɛtti, ni, ya nini nɛ wa ŋɔna, di wulisiya, ni, ya na bɛ taalli ni.”

english_verse: Jesus said: I tell you for certain only thieves and robbers climb over the fence instead of going in through the gate to the sheep pen.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bu dɔli zampakka maa za'anɔri ni n‑kpɛa, amaa u ba ya'aseaa zaa n‑kpɛa, lala doo maa nyɛ la na'ayiga ni fafaara.

english_verse: But the gatekeeper opens the gate for the shepherd, and he goes in through it. The sheep know their shepherd's voice. He calls each of them by name and leads them out.
translation_verse: Za'anɔgura maa nin yoaai za'anɔri n‑ti u, ka bunkɔbri maa nin wum u kukɔri maa. U nin booli u maŋtoori peesi ba yuya ni, ka ba nin dɔli u ka yi na.

english_verse: When he has led out all of his sheep, he walks in front of them, and they follow, because they know his voice.
translation_verse: Ba yi dɔli u n‑yi na n‑naai, u nin bɛ ba tooni, ka bunkɔbri maa dɔli u, ba m mi u kukɔri la zugu.

english_verse: The sheep will not follow strangers. They don't recognize a stranger's voice, and they run away.
translation_verse: Ba ku dɔli sɔ m‑paasi u zugu: ban na zi dɔsɔ yi ti kpɛ na, ba nin zɔ ka kyɛ u, dama ba bu mi u kukɔri.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story. But they did not understand what he was talking about. Jesus Is the Good Shepherd
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛli yɛttɔ'a wa n‑ti ba, amaa ba daa bu wum di gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: Jesus said: I tell you for certain that I am the gate for the sheep.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, man nyɛ la za'anɔta'ariga n‑ti peesi maa.

english_verse: Everyone who came before me was a thief or a robber, and the sheep did not listen to any of them.
translation_verse: Sɔkam zaa ŋɔn pun daŋŋi ma na muna - na'ayigsi n daa nyɛ ba, ni fafaariba, amaa peesi maa daa bu wum n‑ti ba.

english_verse: I am the gate. All who come in through me will be saved. Through me they will come and go and find pasture.
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ za'anɔri maa. Sɔkam yi dɔli man saani na n‑kpɛ nin nya tiliginsim. U nin kpeeri ka laa n‑yiiri na n‑nya moori n‑ŋɔbra.

english_verse: A thief comes only to rob, kill, and destroy. I came so everyone would have life, and have it fully.
translation_verse: Na'ayiga maa muna - ŋɔn yi kpeeri ni na ni u ti zu peesi maa, ka kɔri ba ka sa'am. Man mun kyɛnni na ni ya ti nya nyɔvuri - nyɔvuri din gaari zaa.

english_verse: I am the good shepherd, and the good shepherd gives up his life for his sheep.
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ pɛgurisuŋŋu maa, ka nin zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri n‑takki m peesi maa dina.

english_verse: Hired workers are not like the shepherd. They don't own the sheep, and when they see a wolf coming, they run off and leave the sheep. Then the wolf attacks and scatters the flock.
translation_verse: Ba yi ti yɔ tuntumnisɔ ni u tum pɛgura tuma, amaa u pa la pɛgurisɔ ŋɔn su peesi maa la, di daama yi nya ka kunduŋŋu kyɛnni na, u nin kyɛ peesi maa ka zɔa. Ka kunduŋŋu maa nin gbaai bunkɔbsɔ, ka kyɛ ka ban kpalim maa zɔ n‑wurigi.

english_verse: I have other sheep that are not in this sheep pen. I must also bring them together, when they hear my voice. Then there will be one flock of sheep and one shepherd.
translation_verse: Halli pɛseeba m bɛ ni n‑nyɛ n dina, amaa ka ba ka zampakka wa puuni. Faasee ka m mari ba na m‑paasi, ka ba nin ti wumni n kukɔri maa, ka ba zaa la'am m‑bɛ luginyinni ka ba pɛgura yinni kɔkɔ n su ba.

english_verse: The Father loves me, because I give up my life, so I may receive it back again.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka m Ba wuri ma, dama n sakki ni n zaŋŋi n nyɔvuri n‑dooli, ni n ti deaai di yaasa.

english_verse: No one takes my life from me. I give it up willingly! I have the power to give it up and the power to receive it back again, just as my Father commanded me to do.
translation_verse: Sɔ bu faari n nyɔvuri n saani: man zaŋŋi ka n‑tisi, ka man nin laa n‑toaai n‑deaai ka yaasa. Nŋɔ ka m Ba pun zaali ma ni n niŋi.”

english_verse: The people took sides because of what Jesus had told them.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka nangbanwala daa laa m‑bɛ niriba maa sunsukkuni yaasa, Yisa yɛligu wa zugu.

english_verse: Many of them said, “He has a demon in him! He is crazy! Why listen to him?”
translation_verse: Baseeba n daa yɛtti, ni, u mari la arizimbeoo ka yinyaari gbaai u, ni, “Wula zugu ka ya laa n‑wumni u yɛligu?”

english_verse: But others said, “How could anyone with a demon in him say these things? No one like this could give sight to a blind person!” Jesus Is Rejected
translation_verse: Baseeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, “Arizimbeoo daana ku toaai n‑tɔ'asiri nŋɔa! Arizimbeoo ya niŋi la wula n‑kyɛ ka zoomma nini laa n‑neaara?”

english_verse: That winter, Jesus was in Jerusalem for the Temple Festival.
translation_verse: Di daa nyɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Piiligu Kyuu, ka ba puusi ka waari saha maa.

english_verse: One day he was walking in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch,
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa bɛ la Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la ni, n‑gyinni NaaSulɛmaana Leeŋŋa la puuni.

english_verse: and the people gathered all around him. They said, “How long are you going to keep us guessing? If you are the Messiah, tell us plainly!”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka niriba maa daa gyiligi u ka boosi u, ni, “Bɔn saha ka i nin yiisi ti birisigu ni? Kur yɛlim ti paalu ni, nyin nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum sɔ na ni u ti di Masiiya naam ka fa ti m‑basi, bee?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I have told you, and you refused to believe me. The things I do by my Father's authority show who I am.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “M pun yɛli ya, amaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda. Halli tunseesi man zaŋŋni m Ba yuuri n‑tumni maa, ana n wulisiri n yɛla.

english_verse: But since you are not my sheep, you don't believe me.
translation_verse: Amaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ma yɛdda, dama ya pa la n peesi maa.

english_verse: My sheep know my voice, and I know them. They follow me,
translation_verse: M peesi la n wumni n kukɔri maa; m mi ba, ka ba yi dɔli ma.

english_verse: and I give them eternal life, so that they will never be lost. No one can snatch them out of my hand.
translation_verse: N tiri ba nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka ba ku baasi yɔri halli saha kan ka tariga, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑fa ba man saani.

english_verse: My Father gave them to me, and he is greater than all others. No one can snatch them from his hands,
translation_verse: M Ba maa pun zaŋŋi ba n‑ti ma, ka ŋɔna n galisi n‑gaari seelikam, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑fa ba m Ba saani.

english_verse: and I am one with the Father.
translation_verse: Man ni m Ba maa - ti nyɛ la yimmu.”

english_verse: Once again the people picked up stones in order to kill Jesus.
translation_verse: Lala saha ka niriba maa daa laa m‑piisi kuga ni ba lɔbi Yisa.

english_verse: But he said, “I have shown you many good things my Father sent me to do. Which one are you going to stone me for?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “N daa niŋi alahazibu tunsuma pam, ka ya nini nya, tunseesi ka m Ba Naawunni daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ma ni n tumni a ni. Tunsuma wa muna - kani zugu ka ya boori ni ya lɔbi ma kuga?”

english_verse: They answered, “We are not stoning you because of any good thing you did. We are stoning you because you did a terrible thing. You are just a man, and here you are claiming to be God!”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Pa bunsunseaa zugu ka ti ya lɔbi i, amaa nyin ŋɔn tu Naawunni zugu. Ninsaala n kur nyɛ i, ka i boori la i zaŋŋi i maŋŋa n‑labgi Naawunni!”

english_verse: So why do you accuse me of a terrible sin for saying that I am the Son of God? After all, it is the Father who prepared me for this work. He is also the one who sent me into the world.
translation_verse: Ka man ŋɔna - m Ba maa n yiisi ma ka tum ma ni n kyɛ duniya wa ni na: ka man ŋɔn yɛtti, ni, Naawunni Bii n nyɛ ma, wula ka ya nin toaai n‑yɛli, ni, n tuuri la Naawunni?

english_verse: If I don't do as my Father does, you should not believe me.
translation_verse: N yi bu tumni tunseesi m Ba ŋɔn boori ni n tumna - tɔ, dinŋɔna yan di niŋi ma yɛdda.

english_verse: But if I do what my Father does, you should believe because of that, even if you don't have faith in me. Then you will know for certain that the Father is one with me, and I am one with the Father.
translation_verse: Amaa n yi tumni a ni, halli ya bu niŋni ma yɛdda, ya nin toaai n‑niŋi tuma maa yɛdda, ka lala zugu ka ya nin mi ka baŋŋi di gbinni, ni, m Ba maa bɛ la n saani, ka man gba bɛ u saani.”

english_verse: and crossed the Jordan to the place where John had earlier been baptizing. While Jesus was there,
translation_verse: Yisa daa laa n‑labi n‑yakki Gyɔdin mɔ'ari la yaasa, nti paai ya'aseaa Gyɔni ŋɔn daa yi daŋŋi m‑bɛ ni n‑suuri niriba Naawunni koom la. Bɛni ka Yisa daa kpalim n‑gbɛa.

english_verse: many people came to him. They were saying, “John didn't work any miracles, but everything he said about Jesus is true.”
translation_verse: Ka niriba pam daa kyɛ u saani na ka yɛli, ni, “Gyɔni daa bu tum alahazibu tunseelli, amaa ŋɔn daa pun yɛli yɛliseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi doo wa yɛla daa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni!”

english_verse: A lot of those people also put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka niriba pam daa niŋi u yɛdda bɛni.

english_verse: The sisters sent a message to the Lord and told him that his good friend Lazarus was sick.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka pɔ'aba ayi wa n daa tum Yisa saani na, nti yɛli u, ni, “Ti dugma, i zɔ maa beeri ni.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “His sickness won't end in death. It will bring glory to God and his Son.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala, u daa yɛli, “Ay! Dooru wa ku toaai n‑nyɛ u kum dooru; dinŋɔ niŋiya ni di kyɛ ka Naawunni yuuri ti du, dama Naawunni Bii maa nin nya gyirima di zugu.”

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “Now we will go back to Judea.”
translation_verse: Ka daa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba, ni, “Ya kyɛ ka ti labi Gyuudiiya.”

english_verse: “Teacher,” they said, “the people there want to stone you to death! Why do you want to go back?”
translation_verse: Ka u nyaandɔliba maa daa labsi u, ni, “Ti Karimba, saha beela din daa gaari la ka bɛni niriba maa daa ya lɔbi i la kuga. Kala! I na boori la i labi bɛni?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Aren't there twelve hours in each day? If you walk during the day, you will have light from the sun, and you won't stumble.
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa yɛli ba, ni, “Wuntaŋŋa ni ka neesim bɛ ni n‑niŋi hawa pii ni ayi, bee wula? Dizugu ŋɔnkam kyɛnni wuntaŋŋa ni bu tuura, dama u nini n nya duniya wa neesim maa.

english_verse: But if you walk during the night, you will stumble, because you don't have any light.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mun kyɛnni yuŋŋu saha, u tuuri ni maa, dama neesim ka u saani.”

english_verse: Then he told them, “Our friend Lazarus is asleep, and I am going there to wake him up.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ti zɔ gbisiri ni, see ka ti kyaŋŋi ni ti neaai u.”

english_verse: They replied, “Lord, if he is asleep, he will get better.”
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliba mun daa yɛli u, “Ti dugma, u yi gbisira, u nin nya alaafɛa, bee bɔa?”

english_verse: Jesus really meant that Lazarus was dead, but they thought he was talking only about sleep.
translation_verse: Tɔ: Yisa daa wulisiya, ni, Lazarusi kpi ni; ba mun daa mi, ni, u tɔ'asiri la hoo sɔ n gbisiri gɔmaŋni la.

english_verse: I am glad I wasn't there, because now you will have a chance to put your faith in me. Let's go to him.”
translation_verse: ni, “Amaa yarima zugu ka n sufu maai pam, tin daa ka u saani maa zugu, dama lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ya niŋi yɛdda. Tɔ - ti doaama n‑kyaŋŋi u saani!”

english_verse: Thomas, whose nickname was “Twin,” said to the other disciples, “Come on. Let's go, so we can die with him.” Jesus Brings Lazarus to Life
translation_verse: Ka Tɔmaasi, ban daa boonni sɔ ‘Gyeaa la’, daa yɛli u nyaandɔlitaaba maa, ni, “Tɔ! - ka dinŋɔna ti dɔla n‑kyaŋŋi, ka tin ni ŋɔna nin la'am n‑kpi!”

english_verse: When Jesus got to Bethany, he found that Lazarus had already been in the tomb four days.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn ti paai na maa, ba daa yɛli u, ni, ba pun sɔ'ai Lazarusi di daba anaasi.

english_verse: Bethany was less than three kilometers from Jerusalem,
translation_verse: Tɔ: Gyɛrusalɛm zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Bɛtani bu paai maala ayi,

english_verse: and many people had come from the city to comfort Martha and Mary because their brother had died.
translation_verse: ka Yɛhudiya dima pam daa kyɛ na ni ba ti kaai Maata ni Mariama ka kpamsi ba sufu zaŋŋi n‑kpa ba sunzɔdoo maa kum zugu.

english_verse: When Martha heard that Jesus had arrived, she went out to meet him, but Mary stayed in the house.
translation_verse: Maata ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Yisa wa ni na, u daa doaaya n‑yi n‑tuusi u soori, amaa Mariama ŋɔn daa kpalim m‑bɛ la yiri maa ni.

english_verse: Martha said to Jesus, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
translation_verse: Maata daa yɛli Yisa, ni, “N dugma, i yi daa da bɛ kpa, n sunzɔdoo maa naa n‑ku kpi!

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Your brother will live again!”
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “I sunzɔdoo maa nin yisigi kum ni n‑labi nyɔvuri ni yaasa.”

english_verse: Martha answered, “I know he will be raised to life on the last day, when all the dead are raised.”
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli, ni, “M pun mi, ni, Zaadaari ka u nin ti yisigi u kum ni.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “I am the one who raises the dead to life! Everyone who has faith in me will live, even if they die.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “Mani n‑nyɛ ŋɔn neaari kum; nyɔvuri gba n nyɛ ma. Ŋɔn kam zaa niŋi ma yɛdda na nin bɛ ni, halli u yi pun kpi gba;

english_verse: “Yes, Lord!” she replied. “I believe you are the Christ, the Son of God. You are the one we hoped would come into the world.”
translation_verse: Ka u yɛli u, ni, “Ii, n dugma! N niŋi yɛdda, ni, nyini n‑nyɛ Masiiya - Naawunni bisɔ ŋɔn daa yɛ kyɛ duniya wa ni na nla.”

english_verse: After Martha said this, she went and privately said to her sister Mary, “The Teacher is here, and he wants to see you.”
translation_verse: Maata ŋɔn daa yɛli wa n‑naai, u daa labi nti booli u sunzɔpɔ'a Mariama kpaŋŋa n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ti Karimba la kyɛnni na, ka boosiri i yɛla.”

english_verse: As soon as Mary heard this, she got up and went out to Jesus.
translation_verse: Mariama ŋɔn daa ti wum di, u daa doaai saazugu n‑kyaŋŋi yoomayooma n‑yi ni u ti tuusi u soori.

english_verse: Many people had come to comfort Mary, and when they saw her quickly leave the house, they thought she was going out to the tomb to cry. So they followed her.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka niriba pam daa bɛ yiri maa ni n‑kpamsiri Mariama sufu maa, ka ba daa ti nya ka u doaai yooma n‑yi, ka ba gba daa doaai n‑dɔli u n‑yi na. Ba daa teesiya, ni, u wa la gballi maa ni, ni u ti kum.

english_verse: Mary went to where Jesus was. Then as soon as she saw him, she knelt at his feet and said, “Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.”
translation_verse: Mariama daa paai Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ ya'aseaa maa, ka ŋɔn daa ti nya u, u daa gbaani tiŋŋa Yisa nɔba ni ka yɛli, ni, “N dugma, i yi daa da bɛ kpa, n sunzɔdoo maa naa n‑ku kpi!”

english_verse: When Jesus saw that Mary and the people with her were crying, he was terribly upset
translation_verse: Yisa daa nya Mariama ka u kumna, ka niriba ban paasi u zugu maa gba daa kumna; ka di daa niŋi Yisa yɔri ka u sufu sa'am.

english_verse: and asked, “Where have you put his body?” They replied, “Lord, come and you will see.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa boosi ba, “Ya'akani ka ya sɔ'ai u?” Ka ba yɛli Yisa, ni, “Ti Bapira, kyɛm na nti nya!”

english_verse: Jesus started crying,
translation_verse: Yisa daa kumiya.

english_verse: and the people said, “See how much he loved Lazarus.”
translation_verse: Ka niriba maa yɛli, ni, “Nyama ŋɔn wuri Lazarusi seem!”

english_verse: Some of them said, “He gives sight to the blind. Why couldn't he have kept Lazarus from dying?”
translation_verse: Amaa ka ba seeba daa yɛli, ni, “U daa neaai zoomma nini, bee, u daa bu niŋi lala? Kala u naa n‑ku toaai n‑tuligi Lazarusi kum maa ni?”

english_verse: Jesus was still terribly upset. So he went to the tomb, which was a cave with a stone rolled against the entrance.
translation_verse: U sufusa'aŋŋu maa puuni, Yisa daa kyaŋŋi gballi maa ni, ka ka daa nyɛ la tampifeoo ka ba zaŋŋi kugri n‑ligi ka noori.

english_verse: Then he told the people to roll the stone away. But Martha said, “Lord, you know that Lazarus has been dead four days, and there will be a bad smell.”
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛliya, ni, “Ya yiisima kugri maa kpaŋŋa!” Ka Maata, dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa kpi maa sunzɔpɔ'a la, daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “N dugma, poosigu nin bɛ ni, ban sɔ'ai u di daba anaasi nŋɔa!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Didn't I tell you that if you had faith, you would see the glory of God?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “N da pun bu yɛli i, ni, i yi niŋi yɛdda i nin nya Naawunni naam gyirima ŋɔn nyɛ seem?”

english_verse: After the stone had been rolled aside, Jesus looked up toward heaven and prayed, “Father, I thank you for answering my prayer.
translation_verse: Ba daa biligi kugri maa n‑tiili kpaŋŋa. Ka Yisa gɔsi saazugu ka yɛli, ni, “M Ba, n puusiri i, nyin pun wum n‑ti ma zugu.

english_verse: I know that you always answer my prayers. But I said this, so the people here would believe you sent me.”
translation_verse: M mi, ni, i wumni n‑tiri ma saha kam, amaa n tɔ'asi dinŋɔ la niriba ban zɛ kpa wa zugu, din yɛ kyɛ ka ba niŋi yɛdda, ni, nyini n siri tum ma na.”

english_verse: When Jesus had finished praying, he shouted, “Lazarus, come out!”
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi wa n‑naai maa, u daa tasiya ni kukɔtitaari m‑booli, ni, “Lazarusi, yim na!”

english_verse: The man who had been dead came out. His hands and feet were wrapped with strips of burial cloth, and a cloth covered his face. Jesus then told the people, “Untie him and let him go.” The Plot To Kill Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka u daa yi na, ka kasiŋŋa vili n‑vili u nuusi ni u gbaya, ka tanseaa mun vili u nindaa ni. Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Lɔrigma u ka kyɛ ka u kyaŋŋi!”

english_verse: Many of the people who had come to visit Mary saw the things Jesus did, and they put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Niriba pam ban daa kyɛ na ni ba ti kaai Mariama maa daa nya Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa, ka ba daa niŋi u yɛdda.

english_verse: Others went to the Pharisees and told what Jesus had done.
translation_verse: Amaa ba seeba daa labi nti yɛli Farisii dima Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem.

english_verse: Then the chief priests and the Pharisees called the council together and said, “What should we do? This man is working a lot of miracles.
translation_verse: Dizugu Farisii dima ni gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa daa zini n‑tɔ gyina ka yɛli, ni, “Ti yɛ niŋi la wula? Nyama alahazibu tuntunseesi doo wa ŋɔn tumna!

english_verse: If we don't stop him now, everyone will put their faith in him. Then the Romans will come and destroy our temple and our nation.”
translation_verse: Ti yi kyɛ ka u na kpalim n‑tumni lala, sɔkam zaa nin ti niŋi u yɛdda, ka Room dima nin ti sa'am ti Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa, ni ti tiŋŋa wa zaa, m‑basi!”

english_verse: One of the council members was Caiaphas, who was also high priest that year. He spoke up and said, “You people don't have any sense at all!
translation_verse: Ba puuni, dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri n daa boonni Kayafasi. Ŋɔna n daa nyɛ Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana lala yuumni maa ni. Ŋɔnŋɔ daa doaai n‑yɛli, ni, “Yan gyɛra wa, wumma!

english_verse: Don't you know it is better for one person to die for the people than for the whole nation to be destroyed?”
translation_verse: Ya bu mi di n bɛ seem maa? - ni, ya yi kyɛ ka ninvugyinni kpi n‑ti niriba di sɔ ya kyɛ ka ti butiri zaa sa'am m‑basi.”

english_verse: Caiaphas did not say this on his own. As high priest that year, he was prophesying that Jesus would die for the nation.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, u daa bu tɔ'asi di la u mammantoori; amaa ŋɔn daa nyɛ Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana di yuumni maa zugu, u daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛla, ka di gbinni nyɛ la : ni, see ka Yisa kpi n‑ti Yɛhudiya niriba;

english_verse: Yet Jesus would not die just for the Jewish nation. He would die to bring together all of God's scattered people.
translation_verse: ka di pa la ba butiri maa kɔkɔ zugu, amaa ka di la'am Naawunni biisi ban wurigi m‑bɛ tinsi tinsi zaa ni la m‑paasi.

english_verse: From that day on, the council started making plans to put Jesus to death.
translation_verse: Dirindaari maa ka Yɛhudiya kpambaya daa di lɔ niya ni ba ku Yisa.

english_verse: Because of this plot against him, Jesus stopped going around in public. He went to the town of Ephraim, which was near the desert, and he stayed there with his disciples.
translation_verse: Dinzugu Yisa daa bu laa n‑goori paalu ni Gyuudiiya tingbaŋŋu ni, amaa ka daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi m‑pɔtti ziseaa niriba ŋɔn daa ka ni la, m‑paai tinseaa kan yuuri boonni Ɛfirayim la ni. Bɛni ka ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔliba daa bɛ ni.

english_verse: It was almost time for Passover. Many of the Jewish people who lived out in the country had come to Jerusalem to get themselves ready for the festival.
translation_verse: Kyuu la n daa ti pɔtta, ka niriba pampam daa yi tinkpansi maa ni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm ni ba ti maali ba mansi siri kasikasi pɔi ka Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu maa naayi paai.

english_verse: They looked around for Jesus. Then when they were in the temple, they asked each other, “You don't think he will come here for Passover, do you?”
translation_verse: Ba daa boori la Yisa ziiya, ka ban daa ti la'asi m‑bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni la, ba daa boosiri la taaba, ni, “Ya teesi la wula? Ni, u nin kyɛ kyuu wa ni na, bee, u ku kyɛ na?”

english_verse: The chief priests and the Pharisees told the people to let them know if any of them saw Jesus. This is how they hoped to arrest him.
translation_verse: Ka gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa ni Farisii dima daa yɛli, ni, ŋɔn kam zaa mi Yisa ŋɔn bɛ ya'aseaa, ŋɔn tisim ba lahabaari, din nin kyɛ ka ba gbaai u.

english_verse: If you serve me, you must go with me. My servants will be with me wherever I am. If you serve me, my Father will honor you.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn boori u tumni n‑tiri ma, kyɛ ka u dɔli n nyaaŋŋa: ka man ŋɔn ya bɛ seela maa, bɛni ka n tuntumna wa nin ti bɛ ni. Ka ŋɔn tumni n‑tiri ma la, m Ba Naawunni nin ti di daama gyirima.”

english_verse: Judas did not really care about the poor. He asked this because he carried the moneybag and sometimes would steal from it.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa gbinni daa pa la u ba'a m bɛ faradima maa yɛla ni, amaa ligri yɛla ni, dama u daa guri la ba la'abooriga, ka mun daa zuuri ka puuni la'aseesi.

english_verse: they took palm branches and went out to greet him. They shouted, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord! God bless the King of Israel!”
translation_verse: ka daa zaŋŋi tiisi wula n‑yi na ni ba ti tuusi u soori. Ka ba daa ŋmeeri kpaliŋŋa n‑tasiri n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Hoosana! Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti u - N‑ti sɔ ŋɔn zaŋŋi ti Dugma yuuri n‑kyɛnni na! Zugsuŋŋu n‑ti Izraɛli Naaba!”

english_verse: Jesus found a donkey and rode on it, just as the Scriptures say,
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa nya buŋŋa m‑ba, hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:-

english_verse: Mary took a very expensive bottle of perfume and poured it on Jesus' feet. She wiped them with her hair, and the sweet smell of the perfume filled the house.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Lazarusi sunzɔpɔ'a Mariama la daa zaŋŋi ga'ari ni turaali kpaam, bun mari nyunya'asiri ka bu ligri zoaai pam, m‑mari na nti soosi Yisa nɔba, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u zɔbwɔ'ala n‑nyeesiri a. Ka yiri maa ni sɔkam daa ti wumni turaali maa nyunya'asiri maa.

english_verse: A lot of people came when they heard that Jesus was there. They also wanted to see Lazarus, because Jesus had raised him from death.
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya dima daadam pam daa ti wum, ni, Yisa daa bɛ bɛni. Dizugu ba daa la'asi na ni ba ti nya u, ka di pa lala zugu kɔkɔa, amaa ni ba ti nya Lazarusi gba, Yisa ŋɔn daa yisigi sɔ kum ni la.

english_verse: A meal had been prepared for Jesus. Martha was doing the serving, and Lazarus himself was there.
translation_verse: Di zugu ba daa dugi bundira n‑ti Yisa, ka ŋɔn ni Lazarusi daa la'am n‑dira, ban ni daadam pam: ka Maata daa gyeemna.

english_verse: A disciple named Judas Iscariot was there. He was the one who was going to betray Jesus, and he asked,
translation_verse: Gyuudasi Isikariyɔti muna, Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa ya figsi u la, daa yɛli,

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Leave her alone! She has kept this perfume for the day of my burial.
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli, “Ay, kyɛl u! U zaŋŋi turaali kpaam wa n‑zaali, see ka n sɔ'abu daari ti paai.

english_verse: You will always have the poor with you, but you won't always have me.” A Plot To Kill Lazarus
translation_verse: Ya kur nin mari faradima ya saani sahakam. Man mun ku kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani sahakam.”

english_verse: Philip from Bethsaida in Galilee was there too. So they went to him and said, “Sir, we would like to meet Jesus.”
translation_verse: Ka Griika dima wa seeba daa kyɛ Filipi saani na, nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa yi Bɛtsaida na - tinkpanseaa kan zɛ Galilii tingbaŋŋu ni. Ba daa yɛli u, ni, “Ti Bapira, ti boori ti nya Yisa.”

english_verse: But the Pharisees said to each other, “There is nothing we can do! Everyone in the world is following Jesus.” Some Greeks Want To Meet Jesus
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna Farisii dima maa daa fabinni n‑tiri taaba, ni “Ya bu nya? - Ti zabri u n‑gyɛa! Duniya wa dima maa zaa yi na ni ba ti dɔli u nyaaŋŋa!”

english_verse: So Father, bring glory to yourself. A voice from heaven then said, “I have already brought glory to myself, and I will do it again!”
translation_verse: N nin yɛli, ni, ‘M Ba, kyɛ ka i yuuri nya pɛ'abu!’, bee?” Ka di saha ka kukɔri n daa tɔ'asi saazugu na, ni, “N siri pun kyɛ ka n yuuri nya pɛ'abu, ka ka nin laa n‑nya pɛ'abu yaasa.”

english_verse: “The Lord has blinded the eyes of the people, and he has made the people stubborn. He did this so that they could not see or understand, and so that they would not turn to the Lord and be healed.”
translation_verse: “U kyɛ ka ba nini n zoomna, Ka ba sufu ku sakki n‑wumna. Di yi pa lala ba nini nin neaai n‑nyara, Ka ba sufu nin wumni n‑takkra, Nti nya tippu n saani.”

english_verse: The next day a large crowd was in Jerusalem for Passover. When they heard that Jesus was coming for the festival,
translation_verse: N‑neaai beoo ka Yisa daa lu soori, ni u kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm. Ka tiŋŋa maa puuni zama pam daa ti wum lala,

english_verse: “People of Jerusalem, don't be afraid! Your King is now coming, and he is riding on a donkey.”
translation_verse: “Di zɔri dabeem, nyin pɔ'asariga wa, Nyin Zayɔn tiŋŋa wa! Nyama, i Naaba n kyɛnni i saani na wa! U sigsi u maŋŋa baalimm, Ka baari bumbila n‑kyɛnni na.”

english_verse: At first, Jesus' disciples did not understand. But after he had been given his glory, they remembered all this. Everything had happened exactly as the Scriptures said it would.
translation_verse: Lala saha maa ka u nyaandɔliba daa bu baŋŋi anŋɔ gbinni; amaa Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ u gyirima zii la n‑naai maa ba daa naayi teaai yɛla wa zaa m‑baŋŋi, ni, ba daa daŋŋi n‑sɔbi Yisa yɛla lala.

english_verse: A crowd had come to meet Jesus because they had seen him call Lazarus out of the tomb. They kept talking about him and this miracle.
translation_verse: Yɛttɔ'a wa zugu gba ka niriba pam daa yi na ni ba ti tuusi u, ŋɔn daa tum makkri tumni wa zugu.

english_verse: Some Greeks had gone to Jerusalem to worship during Passover.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba daa bɛ la timpuuni, ka ba daa yi Griika dima tinsi na ni ba ti di Kyuu maa.

english_verse: Philip told Andrew. Then the two of them went to Jesus and told him. The Son of Man Must Be Lifted Up
translation_verse: Filipi mun daa paai Anduru saani na ka ba daa la'am m‑bɔ Yisa ziiya nti yɛli u.

english_verse: Jesus said: The time has come for the Son of Man to be given his glory.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ! Saha maa paai na: sahanseaa ba nin zaŋŋi gyirima n‑ti Ninsaala Bii maa.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that a grain of wheat that falls on the ground will never be more than one grain unless it dies. But if it dies, it will produce lots of wheat.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, kaburli yi bu buri tiŋŋa n‑kpi, ka kpalim la ka kɔkɔa; amaa ka yi ti kpi muna, ka nin buli n‑niŋi suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: If you love your life, you will lose it. If you give it up in this world, you will be given eternal life.
translation_verse: Di wulisiya, ni, sɔ yi ti boori u nyɔvuri pam, u nin kɔŋŋi ka; sɔ mun yi bu boori u nyɔvuri lala, duniya wa puuni, di daama nin guri di m‑mari halli saha kan ka naari.

english_verse: Now I am deeply troubled, and I don't know what to say. But I must not ask my Father to keep me from this time of suffering. In fact, I came into the world to suffer.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Pampaŋŋɔ ka n sufu sa'am: n ya n‑yɛli la wula? - N nin yɛli, ni, ‘M Ba, fa ma m‑basi saha wa yɛlimugsa wa puuni.’, bee? Ay! dama yɛlimugsa wa zugu ka n daa kyɛ na nti paai saha wa na.

english_verse: When the crowd heard the voice, some of them thought it was thunder. Others thought an angel had spoken to Jesus.
translation_verse: Zama maa ni ninvugseeba daa ti wum lala ka yɛtti taaba ni, “Saa tasiya!” Ka seeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, “Ay! Amaa malaakasɔ tɔ'asi n‑ti u!”

english_verse: Then Jesus told the crowd, “That voice spoke to help you, not me.
translation_verse: Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Pa man zugu ka kukɔri wa tɔ'asi n‑yi na, amaa yarima zugu.

english_verse: This world's people are now being judged, and the ruler of this world is already being thrown out!
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ka ba katti duniya wa sariya. Ka ŋɔn su duniya wa fukumsi la muna - ba nin lusi u n‑kari m‑basi kpaŋŋa sa.

english_verse: If I am lifted up above the earth, I will make everyone want to come to me.”
translation_verse: Man muna, ba yi ti zaŋŋi ma n‑sa n‑ya'ali saazugu maa n nin kyɛ ka sɔkam kyɛnni n saani na.”

english_verse: Jesus was talking about the way he would be put to death.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa, u daa wulisiri ŋɔn ya n‑kpi seem maa.

english_verse: The crowd said to Jesus, “The Scriptures teach that the Messiah will live forever. How can you say that the Son of Man must be lifted up? Who is this Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Ka daadam daa yɛli, ni, “Ba daa pun yɛli ti ti zaaligu ni, ni, Naawunni yi ti kyɛ ka Masiiya maa kyɛ na n‑di naam n‑tuligi ti, u nin kpalim m‑bɛ ni sahakam; tɔ, ka nyin mun yɛtti, ni, Ninsaala Bii maa nin ti ‘sa n‑ya'ali saazugu’. Dinŋɔna, ŋɔni n lee n‑nyɛ ‘Ninsaala Bii’ wa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The light will be with you for only a little longer. Walk in the light while you can. Then you won't be caught walking blindly in the dark.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Saha beela yaasa ka neesim maa nin kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani. Tɔ; yan mari neesim sahanseaa la, see ka ya kyɛnni suŋŋa ka zibsim di gbaai ya. Sɔ yi ti kyɛnni zibsim ni, di daama bu mi ŋɔn kyɛnni seela.

english_verse: Have faith in the light while it is with you, and you will be children of the light.” The People Refuse To Have Faith in Jesus After Jesus had said these things, he left and went into hiding.
translation_verse: Yan mari neesim maa sahanseaa la, see ka ya niŋi neesim maa yɛdda, ka ya nin leebi neesim maa niriba.” Yisa daa yɛli lala n‑naai, ka daa kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: He had worked a lot of miracles among the people, but they were still not willing to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Halli ŋɔn daa pun tum alahazibu tuma ni makkri tuma ka ba nini nya maa zaa yɔri, ba daa bu sakki n‑niŋi u yɛdda.

english_verse: This happened so that what the prophet Isaiah had said would come true, “Lord, who has believed our message? And who has seen your mighty strength?”
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi lala ka anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem siri daa ti niŋi lala, ni#:- “Ti Dugma, ŋɔni n daa niŋi tin moonni seem la yɛdda? Naawunni ŋɔn wulisiri u yiiko maa, ŋɔni mun n nyara? Ay! - sɔ ka ni.”

english_verse: The people could not have faith in Jesus, because Isaiah had also said,
translation_verse: Di wulisiya, ni, ba ku toaai n‑niŋi yɛdda. Aizaaya mun daa maali n‑yɛli, ni#:-

english_verse: Isaiah said this, because he saw the glory of Jesus and spoke about him.
translation_verse: Aizaaya ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi lala, u na pun nya Naawunni gyirima di saha maa, ka daa tɔ'asi u yɛla maa.

english_verse: Even then, many of the leaders put their faith in Jesus, but they did not tell anyone about it. The Pharisees had already given orders for the people not to have anything to do with anyone who had faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Halli kpamma maa pam daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda, amaa ba daa bu kyɛ ka sɔ baŋŋi, dama ba daa zɔri Farisii dima maa dabeem, ni, di pa seem ba nin kari ba n‑yiisi ba la'aŋŋu duri ni.

english_verse: And besides, the leaders liked praise from others more than they liked praise from God. Jesus Came To Save the World
translation_verse: Di wulisiya, ni, ba daa boori ni ninsaadima ti pɛ'ai ba, pa ni Naawunni pɛ'ai ba.

english_verse: In a loud voice Jesus said: Everyone who has faith in me also has faith in the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, ni, “Sɔkam n niŋni ma yɛdda, pa mani ka u niŋi yɛdda maa, amaa Sɔ ŋɔn daa tum ma na la ka u niŋna.

english_verse: And everyone who has seen me has seen the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nyari ma la gba nyari la Sɔ ŋɔn daa tum ma na maa.

english_verse: I am the light that has come into the world. No one who has faith in me will stay in the dark.
translation_verse: Man kyɛnni na hoo neesim, ni ŋɔnkam niŋi ma yɛdda la ku ti kpalim zibsim ni.

english_verse: I am not the one who will judge those who refuse to obey my teachings. I came to save the people of this world, not to be their judge.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ mun ŋɔn wumni n yɛla wa n‑tumni lala maa, n ku kari u sariya n‑lusi u: dama n daa bu kyɛ duniya wa na ni n ti kari ka sariya, amaa n kyɛ na ni n ti tiligi duniya maa.

english_verse: But everyone who rejects me and my teachings will be judged on the last day by what I have said.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ mun yi zeemni ma ka bu sakkri n‑tiri n yɛligu maa, di daama sarikatta m bɛ ni: man ŋɔn yɛli yɛliseelli - kana n ya kari u sariya n‑lusi u, Zaadaari maa.

english_verse: I don't speak on my own. I say only what the Father who sent me has told me to say.
translation_verse: Pa m mammantoori yɛla ka n yɛtta. Ay! - m Ba daa tum ma na ka daa zaali ma man ya yɛli yɛliseesi, ni man ya tɔ'asi seem.

english_verse: I know that his commands will bring eternal life. This is why I tell you exactly what the Father has told me.
translation_verse: M mi halli, ni, ŋɔn zaaligu maa nyɛ la nyɔvuri kan ka tariga. Man ŋɔn yɛtti yɛliseesi muna, hoo m Ba ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ma seem la, lala ka man gba tɔ'asira.”

english_verse: It was before Passover, and Jesus knew that the time had come for him to leave this world and to return to the Father. He had always loved his followers in this world, and he loved them to the very end.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti piili m‑puusi Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu maa sahanseaa la, Yisa ni u nyaandɔliba n daa bɛ ni: u daa baŋŋiya, ni, saha maa paaya ni u bakki ka kyɛ duniya wa n‑kyaŋŋi u Ba saani. Ka ŋɔn daa pun wuri u niriba wa seem la - baseeba ban ya kpalim m‑bɛ duniya wa puuni maa - u daa na wuri ba halli ni u yaa tariga.

english_verse: Jesus knew he had come from God and would go back to God. He also knew that the Father had given him complete power.
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa mi halli, ni, u Ba Naawunni pun zaŋŋi bunseelikam zaa n‑tisi u nuu ni; ka laa m‑mi, ni, u daa yi ŋɔn Naawunni saani na ka ya labi n‑kyaŋŋi u saani yaasa.

english_verse: So during the meal Jesus got up, removed his outer garment, and wrapped a towel around his waist.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna u daa doaai bundira diibu zii la ni n‑zaani nti yeaai u kparigu ka zaŋŋi bɔduuwa n‑lɔ u seaa ni.

english_verse: He put some water into a large bowl. Then he began washing his disciples' feet and drying them with the towel he was wearing.
translation_verse: Ka naayi zaŋŋi koom n‑niŋi tasilaa la ni n‑gbaai u nyaandɔliba nɔba peaari; ka u daa zaŋŋi bɔduuwa la n‑nyeesi n‑nyeesi a n‑ti ba.

english_verse: But when he came to Simon Peter, this disciple asked, “Lord, are you going to wash my feet?”
translation_verse: U daa niŋi lala nti paai Saimoon Piita saani na: ka ŋɔn Piita daa yɛli u, ni, “Kala, n dugma, i ya peaai la man nɔba bee?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “You don't really know what I am doing, but later you will understand.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Man ŋɔn niŋni seem maa muna, i ku toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni pampaŋŋɔa; di nyaaŋŋa ka i nin ti baŋŋi.”

english_verse: “You will never wash my feet!” Peter replied. “If I don't wash you,” Jesus told him, “you don't really belong to me.”
translation_verse: Ka Piita yɛli, “Ay, n ku sakki ni i peaai n nɔba la, halli sahakam!” Ka Yisa yɛli u, ni, “N yi bu peaai i, i nuu laa n‑ka n yɛla ni.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “People who have bathed and are clean all over need to wash just their feet. And you, my disciples, are clean, except for one of you.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli, ni, “Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pun su koom, di daama bu laa m‑boori suubu yaasa, amaa u peaai u nɔba kɔkɔa. Lala nin kyɛ ka u leebi bunveelli kasi zaa.” Ni, “Ii, ya siri nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban peaai kasikasi. Amaa pa ya zaa.”

english_verse: After Jesus had washed his disciples' feet and had put his outer garment back on, he sat down again. Then he said: Do you understand what I have done?
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ŋɔn daa peaai ba zaa n‑naai maa, u daa maali n‑yɛ u kparigu n‑labi bundira diibu ziiya. Di saha ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ammi, ya baŋŋi man ŋɔn niŋi ya seem maa?

english_verse: I have set the example, and you should do for each other exactly what I have done for you.
translation_verse: N tum tuma wa n‑ti ya ni n ti wulisi ya ya tunsuŋŋu soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ka ya gba ti tumni lala.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that servants are not greater than their master, and messengers are not greater than the one who sent them.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, daabri bu kpeem u dugma, bee, tuumu n‑kpeem ŋɔn tum u.

english_verse: I am not talking about all of you. I know the ones I have chosen. But what the Scriptures say must come true. And they say, “The man who ate with me has turned against me!”
translation_verse: Pa ya zaa yɛla ka n yɛtti maa: man mi man gamsi ninvugseeba la. See ka din daa sɔbi n‑dooli maa siri nin ti niŋi lala, ni: ‘Man ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn la'amni n‑diri diibu ni taaba, di daama yi la n nyaaŋŋa nti sa'am n yɛla.’ ”

english_verse: I am telling you this before it all happens. Then when it does happen, you will believe who I am.
translation_verse: Ni, “Pampaŋŋɔ n daŋŋi n‑wulisiri anŋɔ zaa n‑tiri ya pɔi, ka a ti naayi niŋi. Ka dizugu a yi ti siri niŋi lala, ya nin baŋŋi n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, ‘mani n nyɛ Man Ŋɔn Bɛ ni maa’.

english_verse: So the disciple leaned toward Jesus and asked, “Lord, which one of us are you talking about?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka nyaandɔla maa daa vuui m‑paai Yisa n‑sɔ'ai m‑boosi u, ni, “Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?”

english_verse: After Jesus had said these things, he was deeply troubled and told his disciples, “I tell you for certain that one of you will betray me.”
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli dinŋɔ n‑naai maa, sufusa'aŋŋu daa ti gbaai u, ka u daa yɛli u noori yɛlimaŋni zaa, ni, “Ya sɔ n yɛ koosi ma n‑figsi ma.”

english_verse: Jesus' favorite disciple was sitting next to him at the meal,
translation_verse: Ka Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ daa pɔri n‑tabli u saani; ŋɔn daa wuri pam sɔ n‑daa la.

english_verse: and Simon motioned for this disciple to find out which one Jesus meant.
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon Piita daa bugsi n‑yɛli doo wa, ni, u boosima Yisa m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn tɔ'asiri sɔ yɛla maa.

english_verse: No one at the meal understood what Jesus meant.
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliba maa mun daa bu mi u yɛligu wa gbinni.

english_verse: But because Judas was in charge of the money, some of them thought that Jesus had told him to buy something they needed for the festival. Others thought that Jesus had told him to give some money to the poor.
translation_verse: Halli Gyuudasi daa su ba la'abooriga yɛla, ka baseeba daa yɛtti, ni, Yisa tɔ'asiri ligri yɛttɔ'a n‑ti u, ni, u kyɛma nti da ba kyuu neema an kpalim na, bee, n‑yiisi zakka n‑ti faradima.

english_verse: Judas took the piece of bread and went out. It was already night. The New Command
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Gyuudasi daa deaai diibu la ka doaai yooma n‑yi. Di saha ka yuŋŋu daa zibi zimm.

english_verse: Then, after God is given glory because of him, God will bring glory to him, and God will do it very soon.
translation_verse: Ka din bɛ la lala zugu, dinŋɔna Naawunni nin kasigi ka u gyirima ti yi paalu Ninsaala Bii la saani, halli yoomyooma ka u nin niŋi lala.”

english_verse: My children, I will be with you for only a little while longer. Then you will look for me, but you won't find me. I tell you just as I told the people, “You cannot go where I am going.”
translation_verse: Ni, “M biisi, n ku laa n‑yuui ya saani: di kpalim la beela ka ya nin ti bɔ m‑bɔ n ziiya n‑gyɛa. Man daa pun yɛli lala n‑ti Yɛhudiya dima maa, ni, man wa seelli maa, ya ku toaai m‑paai bɛni: tɔ, ka pampaŋŋɔ n yɛtti lala n‑tiri ya gba.

english_verse: Simon Peter asked, “Lord, where are you going?” Jesus answered, “You can't go with me now, but later on you will.”
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon Piita daa boosi u n‑yɛli, “Yani ka i wa, n dugma?” Ka Yisa daa gyɛrigi u, ni, “Man n wa seelli maa, i ku toaai n‑dɔli ma m‑paai bɛni pampaŋŋɔa: amaa i nin ti naayi dɔli n nyaaŋŋa m‑paai sahanseaa.”

english_verse: Peter asked, “Lord, why can't I go with you now? I would die for you!”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli u, ni, “N dugma, bɔ n lee n‑kyɛ ka n ku toaai n‑dɔli i pampaŋŋɔa? N sɛ'asiya ni n kpi n‑ti i.”

english_verse: “Would you really die for me?” Jesus asked. “I tell you for certain before a rooster crows, you will say three times that you don't even know me.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ni, i sɛ'asiya ni i kpi n‑ti ma, bee? Ay! - yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, nɔroo sa ku ti kum ka di pa la ka nyin sa nin pun kyiisi napɔmbuta, ni, i bu mi ma.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “Don't be worried! Have faith in God and have faith in me.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya di kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am. Ya niŋma Naawunni yɛdda, tɔ - ka niŋi man gba yɛdda.

english_verse: There are many rooms in my Father's house. I wouldn't tell you this, unless it was true. I am going there to prepare a place for each of you.
translation_verse: Duri pam m bɛ m Ba yiri maa ni, ka n wa ni n ti maali beebu ziiya siri n‑zaali ya. Di yi da pa lala, n naa n‑ku yɛtti ya, ni, lala n nyɛ di. M bu ma'ari ya la ziri.

english_verse: After I have done this, I will come back and take you with me. Then we will be together.
translation_verse: Ka man kyaŋŋi m‑maali ya beebu zii n‑naai, n nin labi na nti zaŋŋi ya n‑kyaŋŋi, ka man ni yarima la'am m‑bɛ ni.

english_verse: You know the way to where I am going.”
translation_verse: Ya mi man wa luginseaa maa soori.”

english_verse: Thomas said, “Lord, we don't even know where you are going! How can we know the way?”
translation_verse: Ka Tɔmaasi daa yɛli u, “Ay, n dugma, ti bu mi nyin wa luginseaa maa; ti ya niŋi la wula m‑baŋŋi soori m‑paai bɛni sa?”

english_verse: “I am the way, the truth, and the life!” Jesus answered. “Without me, no one can go to the Father.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Mani n nyɛ soori maa; mani n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni gba, ka mani n nyɛ nyɔvuri m‑paasi. Sɔ ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai ti Ba Naawunni saani faasee ka u dɔli ma n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: If you had really known me, you would have known the Father. But from now on, you do know him, and you have seen him.”
translation_verse: Yan yi baŋŋi ma wa, ya nin baŋŋi m Ba gba; ka pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ya yi baŋŋi u, ka laa n‑nya u.”

english_verse: Philip said, “Lord, show us the Father. That is all we need.”
translation_verse: Ka Filipi daa yɛli u, “N dugma, wulisim ti i Ba maa - lala yi sakki ti!”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Philip, I have been with you for a long time. Don't you know who I am? If you have seen me, you have seen the Father. How can you ask me to show you the Father?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “N zɔ Filipi, man ni yan zaa kpɛya n‑yuui pam, ka i na bu mi ma, fa? Ŋɔnkam pun nya ma pun nya m Ba maa gba. Ka wula ka i yɛli lala? - ni, ‘Wulisim ti i Ba maa.’

english_verse: Don't you believe that I am one with the Father and that the Father is one with me? What I say isn't said on my own. The Father who lives in me does these things.
translation_verse: I bu niŋi yɛdda, ni, m bɛ la m Ba saani, ka m Ba mun bɛ la man saani? Yɛttɔ'aseesi man tɔ'asi n‑ti ya maa pa la seesi an yi m mammaŋŋa saani na. Ay - m Ba ŋɔn bɛ n saani maa - ŋɔna n tumni tuma maa.

english_verse: Have faith in me when I say that the Father is one with me and that I am one with the Father. Or else have faith in me simply because of the things I do.
translation_verse: Ya niŋma ma yɛdda, ni, m bɛ la m Ba maa saani ka m Ba gba bɛ man saani. Di yi pa lala, ya niŋma ma yɛdda n tuma maa zugu.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that if you have faith in me, you will do the same things I am doing. You will do even greater things, now that I am going back to the Father.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔnkam niŋi ma yɛdda nin tum kaman man tumni seem maa. U nin tum halli tunkpeema n‑gaari lala gba, dama man wa la m Ba maa saani.

english_verse: Ask me, and I will do whatever you ask. This way the Son will bring honor to the Father.
translation_verse: Ka ya yi ti susi bunseelikam zaa n yuuri zugu, n nin tum lala, ka lala yi kyɛ ka m Ba gyirima ti yi paalu, man u Bii maa zugu.

english_verse: I will do whatever you ask me to do. The Holy Spirit Is Promised
translation_verse: Ya yi susi ma bunseelikam n yuuri zugu, n nin niŋi di.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: If you love me, you will do as I command.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya yi wuri ma, ya nin sakki n noori zaaligu maa.

english_verse: Then I will ask the Father to send you the Holy Spirit who will help you and always be with you.
translation_verse: Ka n nin susi m Ba Naawunni, ka u nin ti ya suŋŋnisɔ yaasa, ka ŋɔn Suŋŋna maa nin bɛ ya saani halli saha din ka tariga.

english_verse: The Spirit will show you what is true. The people of this world cannot accept the Spirit, because they don't see or know him. But you know the Spirit, who is with you and will keep on living in you.
translation_verse: Naawunni Sii nla, yɛlimaŋni daana. Duniya wa dima ku toaai n‑deaai u, dama ba bu nya u, bee, m‑baŋŋi u. Yarima mun mi u, dama u kpalim la ya saani ka u nin ti bɛ ya ni.

english_verse: I won't leave you like orphans. I will come back to you.
translation_verse: N yi ti kyaŋŋi, ya ku kpalim ya kɔkɔa, hoo kpibisi ŋɔn kpalim seem. Ay! - N nin labi ya saani na.

english_verse: In a little while the people of this world won't be able to see me, but you will see me. And because I live, you will live.
translation_verse: Di kpalim la beela ka duniya dima ku laa n‑nya ma, amaa yarima nin nya ma; ka man ŋɔn vu maa zugu, ya gba nin vu.

english_verse: Then you will know I am one with the Father. You will know you are one with me, and I am one with you.
translation_verse: Dabsiri maa yi ti kyɛ na, ya nin baŋŋi, ni, m bɛ la m Ba maa saani, ka ya mun bɛ n saani, halli ka man gba bɛ ya saani.

english_verse: If you love me, you will do what I have said, and my Father will love you. I will also love you and show you what I am like.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam deaai la n noori zaaligu ka sakkri n‑tumni lala, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn wuri ma. Ka ŋɔnkam wuri ma muna - m Ba nin wuri di daama, ka man gba nin wuri u, ka nin kasigi m maŋŋa n‑wulisi u.”

english_verse: The other Judas, not Judas Iscariot, then spoke up and asked, “Lord, what do you mean by saying that you will show us what you are like, but you will not show the people of this world?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Gyuudasi bila daa yɛli , ni, “N dugma, i ya niŋi la wula n‑kasigi i maŋŋa n‑wulisi ti, ka duniya dima maa ku nya i?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If anyone loves me, they will obey me. Then my Father will love them, and we will come to them and live in them.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ŋɔnkam wuri ma nin deaai n yɛligu, ka m Ba nin wuri u, ka man ni m Ba nin kyɛ u saani na ka ti la'am m‑bɛ ni.

english_verse: But anyone who doesn't love me, won't obey me. What they have heard me say doesn't really come from me, but from the Father who sent me.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam mun bu wuri ma ku deaai n yɛligu maa, halli ka yɛligu maa pa la n dinni, yɛliseaa yan pun wum la, amaa m Ba ŋɔn daa tum ma na yɛligu n nyɛ ka.”

english_verse: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
translation_verse: Ni, “Dinŋɔ ka m pun yɛli ya ka man kur bɛ la ya saani maa.

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come and help you, because the Father will send the Spirit to take my place. The Spirit will teach you everything and will remind you of what I said while I was with you.
translation_verse: Suŋŋna maa muna, u yi ti paai na, ŋɔn nin wulisi ya bunseelikam, ka teaari ya man ŋɔn pun yɛli ya seem maa zaa. Ŋɔna n nyɛ Sii Kasi maa, ka m Ba Naawunni nin tum u na man yuuri zugu.

english_verse: I give you peace, the kind of peace only I can give. It isn't like the peace this world can give. So don't be worried or afraid.
translation_verse: Sufudoaa ka n tiri ya; di nyɛ la m mammaŋŋa sufudoaa ka n zaŋŋi n‑tiri ya. Pa duniya dima maa ŋɔn mari sufudoaa seem maa tataabu ka n zaŋŋni n‑tiri ya. Yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am, ya mun di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya.

english_verse: You have already heard me say I am going and I will also come back to you. If you really love me, you should be glad I am going back to the Father, because he is greater than I am.
translation_verse: Ya pun wum man yɛli ya seem, ni, n wa ni maa, ka n nin laa n‑labi ya saani na. Tɔ, ya yi siri wuri ma, ya sufu nin maai dizugu, dama n wa la m Ba saani maa, dama ŋɔna n gaari ma.

english_verse: I am telling you this before I leave, so when it does happen, you will have faith in me.
translation_verse: N ya n‑yɛli ya ni pɔi, ka di zaa naayi niŋi; ka di yi ti niŋi, ya nin mari yɛdda di zugu.

english_verse: I cannot speak with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. But he has no power over me.
translation_verse: N ku laa n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya, bee, n‑diri azama, ka di ti yuui, dama ŋɔn su duniya wa fukumsi la kyɛnni na. U bu su la mani,

english_verse: I obey my Father, so everyone in the world might know that I love him. It is time for us to go now.
translation_verse: amaa di niŋni lala nti kyɛ ka duniya dima nin baŋŋi, ni, n wuri la m Ba maa, ka lala zugu n tumni la bunseelikam zaa ŋɔn zaali ma seem.” “Tɔ: ya doaama ka ti kyaŋŋi!”

english_verse: He cuts away every branch of mine that doesn't produce fruit. But he trims clean every branch that does produce fruit, so that it will produce even more fruit.
translation_verse: Wuliseaa kan bɛ ma ni ka bu wanni wala, m Ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi; amaa wuliseaa kan wanni walisuma, u nin gɔsi ka suŋŋa n‑waasi, ka ka niŋi kasi, ni ka ti maali n‑wanni pam.

english_verse: Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. Just as a branch cannot produce fruit unless it stays joined to the vine, you cannot produce fruit unless you stay joined to me.
translation_verse: Ya kpalimma m‑bɛ man saani, ka n gba kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni. Tiwulli ku toaai n‑wali wala ka toori yiiko ni, faasee ka ka kpalim m‑bɛ tii maa yiiko ni: lala ka ya gba ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli, see ka ya kpalim man saani ka man ti ya yiiko.

english_verse: I am the vine, and you are the branches. If you stay joined to me, and I stay joined to you, then you will produce lots of fruit. But you cannot do anything without me.
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ tii maa, ka ya mun n nyɛ ka wula. Ya sɔ ŋɔn kpalim m‑bɛ ma ni ka n gba kpalim m‑bɛ u ni, di daama nin toaai n‑wanni wala pam, amaa man yi ka ya saani ya ku toaai n‑niŋi seelli.

english_verse: If you don't stay joined to me, you will be thrown away. You will be like dry branches that are gathered up and burned in a fire.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam zaa bu kpalim m‑bɛ ma ni, ba nin ŋmaai di daama m‑basi, hoo wuliseaa kan bu wanna, ka ba basi ka, ka ka kuui; ka ba la'asi anŋɔ taabu nti nyɔa, ka a di bugum.

english_verse: Stay joined to me and let my teachings become part of you. Then you can pray for whatever you want, and your prayer will be answered.
translation_verse: Ya yi kpalim m‑bɛ ma ni, ka n yɛligu maa kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni, ya yi ti susi bunseelli kam zaa, ya nin nya di.

english_verse: When you become fruitful disciples of mine, my Father will be honored.
translation_verse: M Ba yuuri nin nya pɛ'abu dinŋɔ zugu, ni, ya wanni la wala pam, ka ya leebi n nyaandɔliba.

english_verse: I have loved you, just as my Father has loved me. So remain faithful to my love for you.
translation_verse: M Ba ŋɔn wuri ma seem, lala ka n gba wuri ya; ya kpalimma m‑bɛ n wurilim maa ni.

english_verse: If you obey me, I will keep loving you, just as my Father keeps loving me, because I have obeyed him.
translation_verse: Ya yi deaai n zaaligu la ya nin kpalim m‑bɛ n wurilim maa ni; hoo man daa deaai m Ba zaaligu la ka kpalim m‑bɛ u wurilim maa ni la.”

english_verse: I have told you this to make you as completely happy as I am.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “N tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a wa n‑ti ya, ni n sufupeelli la ti kpalim ya saani ka ya sufupeelli zoaai.

english_verse: Now I tell you to love each other, as I have loved you.
translation_verse: Man zaaligu nŋɔa#:- ni, ya wuri taaba hoo man wuri ya seem wa.

english_verse: The greatest way to show love for friends is to die for them.
translation_verse: Ninsaala ku toaai m‑mari wurilim n‑gaari nŋɔa, ni, sɔ n zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri m‑pa talima u zɔdima zugu.

english_verse: And you are my friends, if you obey me.
translation_verse: Yarima mun n nyɛ n zɔdima. Ya yi sakki n‑tumni man boori seem, n zɔdima n nyɛ ya.

english_verse: Servants don't know what their master is doing, and so I don't speak to you as my servants. I speak to you as my friends, and I have told you everything my Father has told me.
translation_verse: N ku laa m‑booli ya n daaba yaasa, dama daabri zaa bu mi u dugma ŋɔn tumni seela. Ay! M boonni ya la n zɔdima, dama n zaŋŋi man wum yɛliseesi m Ba saani zaa n‑wulisi ya.

english_verse: You did not choose me. I chose you and sent you out to produce fruit, the kind of fruit that will last. Then my Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
translation_verse: Pa yarima n daa yiisi ma, amaa mani n daa yiisi ya, ka tum ya ni ya yima n‑wanni wala pam ka ya wala nin ti yuui. Ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ya yi boori seelli m Ba saani, man yuuri zugu, ŋɔn nin zaŋŋi n‑ti ya.

english_verse: So I command you to love each other. The World's Hatred
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka n yɛtti ya, ni, see ka ya wuri taaba.”

english_verse: If the people of this world hate you, just remember that they hated me first.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Duniya dima yi yaai ya, yan baŋŋma ka ba pun yaai mani pɔi, ka naayi yaai ya.

english_verse: If you belonged to the world, its people would love you. But you don't belong to the world. I have chosen you to leave the world behind, and this is why its people hate you.
translation_verse: Ya yi da nyɛ la duniya wa maŋtoori niriba, duniya wa da naa m‑boori ya yɛla; amaa yan pa duniya wa niriba zugu, man ŋɔn gamsi ya n‑yiisi duniya wa yɛla ni zugu, lala ka duniya wa yaai ya.

english_verse: Remember how I told you that servants are not greater than their master. So if people mistreat me, they will mistreat you. If they do what I say, they will do what you say.
translation_verse: Ya teaam man ŋɔn daa yɛli ya seem la, ni, daabri bu gaari u dugma. Ba yi da tum ma putoo, ya gba ka ba nin niŋi ya putoo. Ban mun yi deaai n noori, ba nin deaai ya gba noori.

english_verse: People will do to you exactly what they did to me. They will do it because you belong to me, and they don't know the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Anŋɔ zaa ka ba nin niŋi ya man zugu, dama ba bu mi m Ba ŋɔn daa tum ma na maa.

english_verse: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
translation_verse: N yi daa bu kyɛ na n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, ba daa naa n‑ku deaai taalli; amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba ku laa n‑toaai n‑kyiisi n‑yi ba taalli maa ni.

english_verse: Everyone who hates me also hates my Father.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn yaai ma zaa, m Ba gba ka u yaai maa m‑paasi.

english_verse: I have done things no one else has ever done. If they had not seen me do these things, they would not be guilty. But they did see me do these things, and they still hate me and my Father too.
translation_verse: N yi daa bu wulisi ba tunseesi an na zi n‑tum, ba daa naa n‑ku deaai taalli; amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba pun nya ma ka laa n‑yaai ma, n‑tabi m Ba gba.

english_verse: This is why the Scriptures are true when they say, “People hated me for no reason.”
translation_verse: Di bɛ la lala ni di kyɛ ka ba zaaligu yɛligu la niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni, ‘M bu niŋi ba seela ka ba yaai ma yɔri.’

english_verse: Then you will also tell others about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.
translation_verse: Ka di sumsi ni ya gba di n sɛhara n‑wulisiri niriba, dama ya daa bɛ n saani, halli piiligu ni sa.”

english_verse: I am telling you these things, so that you will not turn away.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Dinŋɔ ka n yɛli ya, ni, yan di kyɛ ka ya yɛdda maa nyɛŋŋi ya.

english_verse: You will be chased out of the synagogues. And the time will come when people will kill you and think they are doing God a favor.
translation_verse: Ba nin ti kari ya n‑yiisi ba la'aŋŋu duri maa ni, ka saha nin ti kyɛ na ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ti ku ya nin ti teesi, ni, u gyeemni la Naawunni di zugu.

english_verse: They will do these things because they don't know either the Father or me.
translation_verse: Niriba nin ti niŋni anŋɔ n‑tiri ya, dama ba bu mi m Ba maa, bee, mani.

english_verse: I am saying this to you now, so that when the time comes, you will remember what I have said. The Work of the Holy Spirit I was with you at the first, and so I didn't tell you these things.
translation_verse: Amaa n yɛli ya dinŋɔa ni di kyɛ ka saha maa yi ti paai na, ya nin teaai man daa yɛli ya seem nŋɔa.” Ka u laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “N daa bu daŋŋi n‑yɛli ya dinŋɔ piiligu ni, man daa na bɛ la ya saani maa zugu.

english_verse: But now I am going back to the Father who sent me, and none of you asks me where I am going.
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni la Ŋɔn tum ma na la saani, ka din zaasa yɔri ya sɔ bu boosi ma man ya n‑kyaŋŋi seela.

english_verse: You are very sad from hearing all of this.
translation_verse: Amaa man ŋɔn yɛli ya lala zugu ka sufusa'aŋŋu yi mari ya zaa.

english_verse: But I tell you I am going to do what is best for you. This is why I am going away. The Holy Spirit cannot come to help you until I leave. But after I am gone, I will send the Spirit to you.
translation_verse: Amaa n yɛtti ya la yɛlimaŋni, ni, di kpa ni n nin bakki ka kyɛ ya, dama n yi bu kyaŋŋi, Suŋŋna maa ku kyɛ na. Man mun yi ti kyaŋŋi, n nin tum u ya saani na.

english_verse: The Spirit will come and show the people of this world the truth about sin and God's justice and the judgment.
translation_verse: Ka u yi ti kyɛ na, u nin kasigi n‑ti duniya wa niriba ba tumbeeri taalli yɛla, ni ba yɛlimaŋni kaalinsi yɛla, ni Naawunni sariya karibu yɛla.

english_verse: The Spirit will show them that they are wrong about sin, because they didn't have faith in me.
translation_verse: U nin wulisi ban tum taalli seem yɛla, dama ba bu niŋi ma yɛdda;

english_verse: They are wrong about God's justice, because I am going to the Father, and you won't see me again.
translation_verse: u nin wulisi ban ka yɛlimaŋni seem yɛla, dama n kyɛnni la m Ba saani ka ya ku laa n‑nya ma yaasa;

english_verse: And they are wrong about the judgment, because God has already judged the ruler of this world.
translation_verse: u nin laa n‑wulisi, ni, ba nin ti nya sariya karibu ni yɛlimaŋni, dama ba pun kari duniya wa Daana maa sariya n‑lusi u.

english_verse: I have much more to say to you, but right now it would be more than you could understand.
translation_verse: Tɔ. N na mari yɛla pam ni n yɛli ya, amaa di nin zoaai pam n‑gaari ya.

english_verse: The Spirit shows what is true and will come and guide you into the full truth. The Spirit doesn't speak on his own. He will tell you only what he has heard from me, and he will let you know what is going to happen.
translation_verse: Amaa Sii Kasi maa nin ti kyɛ na n‑kasigi Naawunni yɛlimaŋni maa zaa n‑ti ya. U bu ti ya zaŋŋi u toori yiiko n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ya, amaa u ya tɔ'asi la ŋɔn wum seelli, ni bunseesi an nin ti kyɛ na yɛla m‑paasi.

english_verse: The Spirit will bring glory to me by taking my message and telling it to you.
translation_verse: U nin ti ti ma gyirima, dama u ti ya deaai la man ŋɔn yɛli u seem la n‑yɛli ya.

english_verse: Everything the Father has is mine. This is why I have said that the Spirit takes my message and tells it to you. Sorrow Will Turn into Joy
translation_verse: M Ba ŋɔn mari seesi - a seelikam zaa nyɛ la n dinni, lala zugu ka n yɛli, ni, Sii Kasi maa nin ti zaŋŋi n seelikam zaa n‑yɛli ya.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “For a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Di nin ti niŋi saha beela ka ya ku laa n‑nya ma, ka saha beela yaasa ya nin nya ma.”

english_verse: They said to each other, “What does Jesus mean by saying that for a little while we won't see him, but after a while we will see him? What does he mean by saying he is going to the Father?
translation_verse: U nyaandɔliseeba daa yɛli taaba, “Bumbɔ ka u yɛtti ti wa? - Ni, ‘Saha beela ya ku nya ma, ka saha beela ka ya nin nya ma yaasa.’, ni, ‘Di nyɛ la n kyɛnni m Ba saani.’ ”

english_verse: What is this ‘little while’ that he is talking about? We don't know what he means.”
translation_verse: Ka ba yɛli, “Bɔ ka u wulisiri, ni, ‘saha beela’? - Ay! ti ku toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni!”

english_verse: Jesus knew they had some questions, so he said: You are wondering what I meant when I said that for a little while you won't see me, but after a while you will see me.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa mi, ni, ba boori ba boosi u, ka u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Dinŋɔ ka ya boosiri taaba wa, bee bɔa? - ni n wulisiri, ni, saha beela ka ya ku laa n‑nya ma, ka saha beela ya nin nya ma yaasa?

english_verse: I tell you for certain that you will cry and be sad, but the world will be happy. You will be sad, but later you will be happy.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya nin ti kum ka fabli, amaa ka duniya wa dima nin ti di nya'asim; ya sufu nin sa'am, amaa ya sufusa'aŋŋu maa nin ti takki sufudoaa.

english_verse: When a woman is about to give birth, she is in great pain. But after it is all over, she forgets the pain and is happy, because she has brought a child into the world.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a pudaana yi ya dɔ'ai, u mari sufusa'aŋŋu, dama u beerim saha paai u lala; amaa u yi ti dɔ'ai n‑naai sahaseaa maa, u ku laa n‑teaari beerim maa yaasa: ay! - u mari sufudoaa, dama u baŋŋiya, ni, u dɔ'ai bii la ka u bɛ ni.

english_verse: You are now very sad. But later I will see you, and you will be so happy that no one will be able to change the way you feel.
translation_verse: Ka sufusa'aŋŋu mari ya pampaŋŋɔa, amaa ya nin ti nya ma yaasa ka ya mari sufumaasim ni sufudoaa, ka sɔ ku laa n‑fa ya sufudoaa wa ya saani.

english_verse: When that time comes, you won't have to ask me about anything. I tell you for certain the Father will give you whatever you ask for in my name.
translation_verse: Dirindaari maa di pa la ya nin ti susi bunseelli man saani: yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ni, ya yi susi seelli zaa m Ba saani u nin ti ya, man yuuri zugu.

english_verse: You have not asked for anything in this way before, but now you must ask in my name. Then it will be given to you, so you will be completely happy.
translation_verse: Halli ni pampaŋŋɔ ya na bu susi seela n yuuri zugu: susima ka ya nin nya, ka ya nin nya sufudoaa pam.

english_verse: I have used examples to explain to you what I have been talking about. But the time will come when I will speak to you plainly about the Father and will no longer use examples like these.
translation_verse: N daa zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'anyɛsa n‑yɛtti ya dinŋɔa, amaa saha kyɛnni na ka n ku ti tɔ'asi ya nyɛsa ni, amaa n nin kasigi n‑yɛli ya m Ba yɛla.

english_verse: You will ask the Father in my name, and I won't have to ask him for you.
translation_verse: Di daari ya nin ti susi n yuuri zugu, ka di pa la n yɛtti ya, ni, man nin susi m Ba n‑ti ya;

english_verse: God the Father loves you because you love me, and you believe I have come from him.
translation_verse: ay! - m Ba maa mammaŋŋa wuri ya, dama ya gba wuri ma ka niŋi yɛdda, ni, n yi la ŋɔn Naawunni saani na.

english_verse: I came from the Father into the world, but I am leaving the world and returning to the Father.
translation_verse: N siri yi la m Ba saani n‑kyɛ duniya wa na; ka n nin laa n‑yi duniya wa ni n‑labi m Ba saani yaasa.”

english_verse: The disciples said, “Now you are speaking plainly to us! You are not using examples.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba daa yɛli u, ni, “Tɔ! Pampaŋŋɔ i kasigi n‑yɛli ti, pa nyɛsa ni yaasa.

english_verse: At last we know that you understand everything, and we don't have any more questions. Now we believe you truly have come from God.”
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ti yi mi, ni, i mi seelli kam; ka i karimba sɔ ku laa m‑boosi i boosa n‑wulisiri i maa. Dinŋɔ zugu ti baŋŋiya, ni, i siri yi la Naawunni saani na.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you really believe me?
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, ni, “Ya siri niŋi yɛdda, fa?

english_verse: The time will come and is already here when all of you will be scattered. Each of you will go back home and leave me by myself. But the Father will be with me, and I won't be alone.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - saha kyɛnni na, halli m‑pun paai na gba, ka ya nin ti wurim; ya sɔkam nin zɔ n‑kuli u yiri ka kyɛ ma n kɔkɔa - amaa n siri pa n kɔkɔa, dama m Ba maa bɛ la n saani.

english_verse: I have told you this, so that you might have peace in your hearts because of me. While you are in the world, you will have to suffer. But cheer up! I have defeated the world.
