translation_verse: N yɛtti ya anŋɔa, ni di ti kyɛ ka ya mari sufudoaa n saani. Duniya wa ni ya mari yɛla, amaa ya mari sufukpeemni dama m pun nyaŋŋi duniya maa.”

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking to his disciples, he looked up toward heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come for you to bring glory to your Son, in order that he may bring glory to you.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli anŋɔ n‑naai, u daa wukki u nini n‑gɔsi saazugu m‑puusi Naawunni n‑susi, ni, “M Ba, saha maa paaya. Tima i Bii gyirima, ni i Bii maa gba ti zaŋŋi gyirima n‑ti i.

english_verse: And you gave him power over all people, so he would give eternal life to everyone you give him.
translation_verse: Dama i pun zaŋŋi yiiko n‑ti u, ni u ti su sɔkam yɛla ka zaŋŋi nyɔvuri kan ka tariga n‑ti baseeba nyini, m Ba, ŋɔn daa ti u la zaa.

english_verse: Eternal life is to know you, the only true God, and to know Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
translation_verse: Nyɔvuri kan ka tariga wa nŋɔa: ni, ba ti baŋŋi nyini, m Ba, ni, nyin kɔkɔ n nyɛ Naawunni yinni ni yɛlimaŋni; ka laa m‑baŋŋi Yisa Kirisita, nyin ŋɔn tum sɔ la na.

english_verse: I have brought glory to you here on earth by doing everything you gave me to do.
translation_verse: Tɔ: n tum tunseesi nyin ŋɔn daa ti ma ni n ti tum la, halli nti naai: ka lala ka n zaŋŋi gyirima n‑ti i, duniya wa puuni.

english_verse: Now, Father, give me back the glory I had with you before the world was created.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna pampaŋŋɔa, m Ba, tim ma gyirima i saani hoo man daa nya gyirima seem pɔi ka duniya wa naayi piili la.

english_verse: You have given me some followers from this world, and I have shown them what you are like. They were yours, but you gave them to me, and they have obeyed you.
translation_verse: N yiisi i yɛla n‑wulisi seeba nyin ŋɔn daa yiisi duniya wa ni n‑ti ma la. I maŋtoori niriba n nyɛ ba, ka i daa zaŋŋi ba n‑ti ma ka ba deaai i noori.

english_verse: They know that you gave me everything I have.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ba baŋŋiya, ni, seelikam nyin ti ma la yi la i saani na.

english_verse: I told my followers what you told me, and they accepted it. They know I came from you, and they believe you are the one who sent me.
translation_verse: Dama n daa zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi nyin daa ti ma la n‑ti ba, ka ba deaai a. Ba daa mi suŋŋasuŋŋa, ni, n yi la i saani na, ka laa n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, nyini n daa tum ma na.

english_verse: I am praying for them, but not for those who belong to this world. My followers belong to you, and I am praying for them.
translation_verse: Ban ka n susiri i zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba zugu. M bu susiri n‑tiri la duniya wa dima, amaa seeba nyin ŋɔn daa ti ma la zugu ka n susira, dama ban n nyɛ i niriba.

english_verse: All I have is yours, and all you have is mine, and they will bring glory to me.
translation_verse: Halli man mari seela zaa nyɛ la i dinni, ka nyin ŋɔn mari seela n nyɛ n dinni - ka niriba wa zugu ka n nya gyirima.

english_verse: Holy Father, I am no longer in the world. I am coming to you, but my followers are still in the world. So keep them safe by the power of the name you have given me. Then they will be one with each other, just as you and I are one.
translation_verse: Man ku laa m‑bɛ duniya wa ni, amaa ban na kpalim m‑bɛ la duniya ni, ka man mun kyɛnni i saani na. M Ba Kasi, guri ba suŋŋa i yuuri yiiko la zugu - yuseaa ka i daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la - ni ba ti bɛ yimmu hoo man ni nyin nyɛ yimmu seem la.

english_verse: While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power you have given me. I guarded them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who had to be lost. This happened so that what the Scriptures say would come true.
translation_verse: Man ŋɔn daa bɛ ba saani sahanseaa la, n daa zaŋŋi la yuseaa nyin ŋɔn daa ti ma la n‑guri ba. Ka ba bɛ ni ka ba sɔsɔ bu baasi yɔri, ka di yi pa la baasiyɔri daana la kɔkɔa, ni din daa sɔbi n‑dooli la siri ti niŋi lala.

english_verse: I am on my way to you. But I say these things while I am still in the world, so my followers will have the same complete joy that I do.
translation_verse: N kyɛnni la i saani na pampaŋŋɔa, amaa anŋɔ ka n yɛtta, man na bɛ duniya wa puuni maa, ni di kyɛ ka ba ti nya n sufupeelim zaa ka bu pɔsigi seela.

english_verse: I have told them your message. But the people of this world hate them, because they don't belong to this world, just as I don't.
translation_verse: N zaŋŋi la i yɛligu n‑ti ba, ka duniya dima kur yaai ba di zugu, dama ba pa la duniya wa niriba, hoo man gba ŋɔn pa duniya wa nira seem.

english_verse: Father, I don't ask you to take my followers out of the world, but keep them safe from the evil one.
translation_verse: N susigu pa la, ni, i yiisi ba duniya wa ni, amaa, ni, i ta'ai Sintaani Yɛlibeeri Daana ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: They don't belong to this world, and neither do I.
translation_verse: Pa duniya wa niriba n nyɛ ba, hoo man gba ŋɔn pa duniya wa nira seem.

english_verse: Your word is the truth. So let this truth make them completely yours.
translation_verse: I yɛligu nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni; dinŋɔna kur kyɛ ka yɛlimaŋni wa mari ba, ni ba ti leebi i niriba kasikasi.

english_verse: I am sending them into the world, just as you sent me.
translation_verse: Hoo nyin daa tum ma duniya wa ni na seem la, lala ka n gba tumni ba duniya ni.

english_verse: I have given myself completely for their sake, so they may belong completely to the truth.
translation_verse: Ban zugu ka n zaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑ti i; ni ba gba nin ti zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑ti i, yɛlimaŋni maa zugu.

english_verse: I am not praying just for these followers. I am also praying for everyone else who will have faith because of what my followers will say about me.
translation_verse: Pa niriba wa kɔkɔ zugu ka n susiri n wunsusigu wa: n susigu laa m‑bɛ ni n‑tiri ninvugseeba zaa ban nin niŋi ma yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑yi ba lahabaari mooligu maa.

english_verse: I want all of them to be one with each other, just as I am one with you and you are one with me. I also want them to be one with us. Then the people of this world will believe that you sent me.
translation_verse: Ii, m Ba, lala nin kyɛ ka ba zaa leebi yimmu kaman nyini m bɛ ma ni la, ka man gba bɛ i ni: lala ka ba gba nin bɛ ti ni, ka di nin kyɛ ka duniya wa dima baŋŋi, ni, nyin daa tum ma na, ka ba niŋi lala yɛdda.

english_verse: I have honored my followers in the same way you honored me, in order that they may be one with each other, just as we are one.
translation_verse: Man mun deaai la gyirimaseaa nyin ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la ka laa n‑ti n niriba wa. Di nin kyɛ ka ba bɛ yimmu, kaman man ni nyini, m Ba, ŋɔn bɛ la yimmu seem la.

english_verse: I am one with them, and you are one with me, so they may become completely one. Then this world's people will know that you sent me. They will know that you love my followers as much as you love me.
translation_verse: Ka n nin bɛ ba ni, ka nyin mun bɛ ma ni. Kyɛ ka ba bɛ ni ni nangbanyinni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka duniya wa dima baŋŋi, ni, nyin daa tum ma na ka man mun wuri niriba wa kaman nyin ŋɔn wuri ma seem.

english_verse: Father, I want everyone you have given me to be with me, wherever I am. Then they will see the glory you have given me, because you loved me before the world was created.
translation_verse: M Ba, ninvugseeba nyin zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la - m boori ba bɛ n saani man ŋɔn bɛ seela zaa, ka ba nin nya n gyirima - nyin ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi gyirimaseaa n‑ti ma la. Ka di nin wulisiri, ni, i daa wuri ma pɔi ka daa naayi naam duniya wa.

english_verse: Good Father, the people of this world don't know you. But I know you, and my followers know that you sent me.
translation_verse: M Ba yɛlimaŋni daana, halli ni duniya wa dima na zi i yɛla, man mun mi i, ka n niriba wa gba mi, ni, i tum ma na.

english_verse: I told them what you are like, and I will tell them even more. Then the love you have for me will become part of them, and I will be one with them.
translation_verse: N gba pun kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi i, ka kur kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi nyin ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa. Di nin kyɛ ka wurilim bɛ ba saani, ka di nyɛ wurilim seaa nyin mari zaŋŋi n‑ti ma; ka man gba nin bɛ ba saani.”

english_verse: One of the high priest's servants was there. He was a relative of the servant whose ear Peter had cut off, and he asked, “Didn't I see you in the garden with that man?”
translation_verse: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana daabsɔ gba daa yɛli u, ni, “N da bu nya i tiisi puu maa ni, ŋɔn Yisa saani?” - daabri wa daa nyɛ la dɔ'ara n‑ti Piita ŋɔn fikki dɔsɔ tubri la.

english_verse: Jesus again asked, “Who are you looking for?” “We are looking for Jesus from Nazareth,” they answered.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa maali m‑boosi ba yaasa, ni, “Ŋɔni ka ya boora?”; ka ba yɛli u, ni, Yisa zii ka ba boora, ni, Nazarɛti doo la.

english_verse: This time Jesus replied, “I have already told you that I am Jesus. If I am the one you are looking for, let these others go.
translation_verse: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “M pun yɛli ya, ni, mani nla; dinŋɔna di yi nyɛ la mani ka ya boora, ya kur kyɛ ka niriba wa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.” (

english_verse: Then everything will happen, just as I said, ‘I did not lose anyone you gave me.’ ”
translation_verse: Lala daa kyɛ ka ŋɔn pun yɛli seem maa siri ti niŋi, ni, “M Ba, ninvugseeba ka i zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la, m bu kyɛ ka ba sɔsɔ ti baasi yɔri.”)

english_verse: Simon Peter had brought along a sword. He pulled it out and struck at Malchus, the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear.
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon Piita daa mari takoobi, ka u daa foaai ka ka kyɛ dɔsɔ nudirigu tubri n‑fikki m‑basi. Doo maa daa nyɛ la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la daabsɔa, ka u yuuri boonni Malikyiisi.

english_verse: The Roman officer and his men, together with the temple police, arrested Jesus and tied him up.
translation_verse: Di saha ka soogyadima maa, ni ba zuguraana, ni Yɛhudiya dima maa Pirinsi la m‑paasi, daa gbaai Yisa n‑lɔ u.

english_verse: Peter stayed outside near the gate. But the other disciple came back out and spoke to the girl at the gate. She let Peter go in,
translation_verse: Piita mun daa kpalim n‑zɛ samanni maa ni, amaa u taraana la daa labi na n‑yɛli pɔ'asariga la, za'anɔgura maa, ka mari u n‑kpɛa.

english_verse: but asked him, “Aren't you one of that man's followers?” “No, I am not!” Peter answered.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'asariga la daa yɛli Piita, ni, “Ammi, nyin gba pa la doo wa nyaandɔla?” Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ay! Pa mani.”

english_verse: It was cold, and the servants and temple police had made a charcoal fire. They were warming themselves around it, when Peter went over and stood near the fire to warm himself. Jesus Is Questioned by the High Priest (Matthew
translation_verse: Waari saha n‑daa la, ka dundɔŋŋu ni dima maa - daaba ni Pirinsi la - daa pun la'asi saala n‑nyoaai bugum, ka ba daa zɛ ni n‑wuusira. Ka Piita gba daa la'am ba saani n‑wuusiri bugum maa.

english_verse: The high priest questioned Jesus about his followers and his teaching.
translation_verse: Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa boosi Yisa u nyaandɔliba yɛla ni u wulisigu.

english_verse: Why are you questioning me? Why don't you ask the people who heard me? They know what I have said.”
translation_verse: Bɔ zugu ka i boosiri la mani? See ka i boosi baseeba ban daa wum maa m‑baŋŋi man daa yɛli ba seem. Ban m mi man ŋɔn daa yɛli seem n‑ti ba.”

english_verse: Jesus was still tied up, and Annas sent him to Caiaphas the high priest. Peter Again Denies that He Knows Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Di saha ka Annasi daa kyɛ ka ba lɔ u n‑zi n‑taasi Kayafasi saani, Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa n daa nyɛ u gba.

english_verse: It was early in the morning when Jesus was taken from Caiaphas to the building where the Roman governor stayed. But the crowd waited outside. Any of them who had gone inside would have become unclean and would not be allowed to eat the Passover meal.
translation_verse: Bɛkyikyeoo saha ka ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑yi Kayafasi yiri ni n‑kyaŋŋi Baarikyi yiri ni. Ba mammansi daa bu kpɛ Baarikyi puuni, ni ba di di kyisigu taalli ka kɔŋŋi Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu maa diibu di zugu.

english_verse: Pilate came out and asked, “What charges are you bringing against this man?”
translation_verse: Dinzugu Gɔmna Pailiti daa yi ba saani na, m‑paai ba nti yɛli, ni, “Taalli bɔ ka ya zaŋŋi n‑galim doo wa?”

english_verse: They answered, “He is a criminal! That's why we brought him to you.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Doo wa yi da pa la tumbeeri daana, ti naa n‑ku zaŋŋi u n‑tisi.”

english_verse: Pilate then went back inside. He called Jesus over and asked, “Are you the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Pailiti daa laa n‑kpɛ Baarikyi puuni ka booli Yisa na nti yɛli u, ni, “Nyini n nyɛ Izraɛli dima Naaba la?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Are you asking this on your own or did someone tell you about me?”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “I yɛtti la lala i maŋtoori noori ni, bee, seeba n yɛli lala n‑ti i?”

english_verse: “You know I'm not a Jew!” Pilate said. “Your own people and the chief priests brought you to me. What have you done?”
translation_verse: Ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa yɛli u, “Yɛhudiyadoo n nyɛ ma? - Ay! i maŋtoori butiri niriba maa zaŋŋi i n‑ti ma na - halli ya Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana gba. Tumbɛbɔ ka i daa tum?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “My kingdom doesn't belong to this world. If it did, my followers would have fought to keep me from being handed over to our leaders. No, my kingdom doesn't belong to this world.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “N naam pa la duniya wa puuni naam. Di yi pa lala, n nyaandɔliba da naa n‑zabi n‑ta'ai ma ka sɔ ku zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi Yɛhudiya dima maa nuu ni. Man mun naam suulinsi bu yi la duniya wa ni na.”

english_verse: “So you are a king,” Pilate replied. “You are saying that I am a king,” Jesus told him. “I was born into this world to tell about the truth. And everyone who belongs to the truth knows my voice.”
translation_verse: Ka Pailiti yɛli, “Asee i nyɛ la naaba, fa?” Ka Yisa daa labsi, ni, “Nyini n nyɛ ŋɔn yɛli, ni, naaba n nyɛ ma. Dinŋɔ zugu ka ba daa dɔ'ai ma, ni n di sɛhara n‑tiri yɛlimaŋni yɛla. Ŋɔnkam nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana wumni n kukɔri maa.”

english_verse: And since I usually set a prisoner free for you at Passover, would you like for me to set free the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Amaa ya kaari m bɛ ni, ni, n zaŋŋi dansarika daana sɔ n‑yiisi n‑ti ya na, ya Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu wa zugu. Tɔ, ya boori n yiisi Yɛhudiya dima Naaba wa n‑ti ya na, bee?”

english_verse: They shouted, “No, not him! We want Barabbas.” Now Barabbas was a terrorist.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa laa n‑kaasi pampam n‑yɛtti, ni, “Pa doo wa, see Barabasi!”

english_verse: Pilate gave orders for Jesus to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Gɔmna Pailiti daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑tisi ka ba zaŋŋi daari n‑feebi u.

english_verse: The soldiers made a crown out of thorn branches and put it on Jesus. Then they put a purple robe on him.
translation_verse: Ka soogyadima maa daa zaŋŋi goosi m‑paam, ka yɛtti, ni, naam zupiliga n nyɛ ka. Ba daa zaŋŋi ka m‑pili u zugu, ka zaŋŋi naam kparizeoo n‑yeeli u.

english_verse: They came up to him and said, “Hey, you king of the Jews!” They also hit him with their fists.
translation_verse: Ba daa kyɛnni u saani na nti maanni u la ansarisi, ni, “Ndana, m Ba Izraɛli Naaba!”, ka lɔbri u pɛ'asi.

english_verse: Once again Pilate went out. This time he said, “I will have Jesus brought out to you again. Then you can see for yourselves that I have not found him guilty.”
translation_verse: Pailiti mun daa labi n‑yi n‑yɛli daadam maa, ni, “Tɔ! m mari u na nti ti ya, ni ya baŋŋi, ni, man bu nya u taaliseaa.”

english_verse: Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe. Pilate said, “Here is the man!”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yi na, ka u na pinni la goosi zupiliga maa, ka yɛ kparizeoo la. Ka Pailiti daa yɛli ba, ni, “Nnya doo maa!”

english_verse: When the chief priests and the temple police saw him, they yelled, “Nail him to a cross! Nail him to a cross!” Pilate told them, “You take him and nail him to a cross! I don't find him guilty of anything.”
translation_verse: Gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa mun daa nya u, ban ni ba Pirinsi dima maa, ka daa gbaai tasibu, ni, “Ya kpaasim u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu! Kpaasim u!” Pailiti mun daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, ni, “Tɔ: ya deaam u n‑kpaasi ya mammansi! Man mun bu nya u taaliseaa ni n galim u.”

english_verse: The crowd replied, “He claimed to be the Son of God! Our law says that he must be put to death.”
translation_verse: Yɛhudiyadima maa daa labsi n‑yɛli “Ti mari la ti zaaligu ka di wulisi, ni, di sum ka u kpi, dama u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑yɛtti, ni, Naawunni bii n nyɛ u.”

english_verse: When Pilate heard this, he was terrified.
translation_verse: Ka Gɔmna Pailiti ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, dabeem daa kpa u m‑paasi.

english_verse: He went back inside and asked Jesus, “Where are you from?” But Jesus did not answer.
translation_verse: Ka u daa mari Yisa n‑labi n‑kpɛ Baarikyi zɔŋŋu maa puuni m‑boosi u, ni, “Yɛni ka i yi na?”; ka Yisa daa bu labsi u seela.

english_verse: “Why won't you answer my question?” Pilate asked. “Don't you know I have the power to let you go free or to nail you to a cross?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli u, “I ku tɔ'asi n‑ti ma? I bu mi, ni, m mari yiiko ni n kyɛ ka ba basi i, bee, ba kpaasi i n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu n‑ku i?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If God had not given you the power, you couldn't do anything at all to me. But the one who handed me over to you did something even worse.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa yɛli u, “I ka yiiko n nin toaai, di yi pa la yiiko maa yi Naawunni saani na. Dinzugu ninvugsɔ ŋɔn figsi ma n‑ti i la nin deaai taalli n‑gaari i.”

english_verse: Then Pilate wanted to set Jesus free. But the crowd again yelled, “If you set this man free, you are no friend of the Emperor! Anyone who claims to be a king is an enemy of the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Di saha zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna ka Pailiti yi boori sɔseaa ŋɔn nin dɔli m‑basi Yisa. Amaa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa tasiri n‑yɛtta, ni, “I yi basi doo wa di wulisiya, ni, i pa la Room NaaTitaari maa zɔa! ninvugsɔ ŋɔn yɛnni, ni, u nyɛ la naaba - tɔ! di daama za'asiri NaaTitaari la naam maa!”

english_verse: When Pilate heard this, he brought Jesus out. Then he sat down on the judge's bench at the place known as “The Stone Pavement.” In Aramaic this pavement is called “Gabbatha.”
translation_verse: Gɔmna Pailiti ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa mari Yisa n‑yi na, ka daa zini u sariya karibu bimbinni maa zugu. Bɛni ka ba daa boonni, ni, ‘Litɔsitɔroota’ la, ka ka yuuri Hibruuli ni daa nyɛ la ‘Gabbata’ - ka di gbinni “Kuga Ziiya” maa.

english_verse: It was about noon on the day before Passover, and Pilate said to the crowd, “Look at your king!”
translation_verse: Di saha maa n daa nyɛ Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu siri maaligu daari, ka di daa paai agbaa saha ka Pailiti daa yɛli Yɛhudiyadima maa, ni, “Ya nyama, ya Naa maa nŋɔa!”

english_verse: Then Pilate handed Jesus over to be nailed to a cross. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Di saha ka Pailiti daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑tisi ni ba ti ku u. Dinzugu ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa

english_verse: The soldiers first broke the legs of the other two men who were nailed there.
translation_verse: Di zugu ka soogyadima maa daa paai ba saani n‑kabsi doo yinni gbaya, ni ŋɔnla gba gbaya - dɔseeba ban daa la'am n‑kpaasi Yisa saani la.

english_verse: So the chief priests went to Pilate and said, “Why did you write that he is King of the Jews? You should have written, ‘He claimed to be King of the Jews.’ ”
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya gyeeŋŋu toondaandima maa mun daa yɛli Pailiti ni, “Ay! di sɔbi, ni, ‘Yɛhudiyadima Naaba’ amaa, ni ‘U yɛli u mammaŋŋa yɛla, ni, “Yɛhudiyadima Naaba n nyɛ ma.” ’ ”

english_verse: When Jesus saw his mother and his favorite disciple with her, he said to his mother, “This man is now your son.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa nya ka u ma n zɛya, ŋɔn ni u nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa wuri pam la gba. Ka u yɛli u ma maa, ni, “Nyama, m ma, i bii nŋɔa.”

english_verse: and he carried his cross to a place known as “The Skull.” In Aramaic this place is called “Golgotha.”
translation_verse: n‑kyɛ ka u bugi u maŋtoori kum dɔpulli daari n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai ya'aseaa ban boonni “Zuguŋmaaŋŋu Ziiya”, di n nyɛ, Hibruuli ni, ‘Gɔligɔtta’ la.

english_verse: Pilate ordered the charge against Jesus to be written on a board and put above the cross. It read, “Jesus of Nazareth, King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Gɔmna Pailiti daa pun sɔbi bunni n‑tabli dɔpulli maa saazugu, ni, “Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, nŋɔa: Yɛhudiyadima Naaba.”

english_verse: The words were written in Hebrew, Latin, and Greek. The place where Jesus was taken wasn't far from the city, and many of the people read the charge against him.
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya niriba pam daa karim lala sɔppu maa, dama ban daa kpaasi Yisa seela ni Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni daa bu wɔ'a; halli ba daa sɔbi ka Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a, ni Room yɛttɔ'a ni Griiktiri gba.

english_verse: But Pilate told them, “What is written will not be changed!”
translation_verse: Ka Pailiti daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Man ŋɔn sɔbi seem la, di sɔbiya!”

english_verse: The soldiers said to each other, “Let's not rip it apart. We will gamble to see who gets it.” This happened so the Scriptures would come true, which say, “They divided up my clothes and gambled for my garments.” The soldiers then did what they had decided.
translation_verse: ka ba daa yɛli taaba, ni, “Ya di kyɛ ka ti toaai ka n‑tɔri see ka ti tɔ tɛtɛ m‑baŋŋi ŋɔn ya deaai!” Lala ka din daa sɔbi n‑dooli siri daa niŋi, ni#:- “Ba zaŋŋi n situra n‑tɔri n‑ti taaba Ka tɔ la tɛtɛ n kyinkyinni zugu.” Tɔ: soogyadima maa ŋɔn daa tum seem nla.

english_verse: Jesus' mother stood beside his cross with her sister and Mary the wife of Clopas. Mary Magdalene was standing there too.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka pɔ'aba daa zɛ n‑tabi Yisa dɔpulli ziiya: u ma Mariama n daa bɛ ni, ni ŋɔn Mariama sunzɔ la; ni u tɔ'ama, Klɔpaasi pɔ'a; ni Mariama Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la gba.

english_verse: But when they came to Jesus, they saw he was already dead, and they did not break his legs.
translation_verse: Amaa ban daa paai Yisa saani na nti nya ka u pun kpi ni maa, ba daa bu kabsi u gbaya.

english_verse: In the place where Jesus had been nailed to a cross, there was a garden with a tomb that had never been used.
translation_verse: Ka gaadi daa bɛ bɛni, ziseaa ban daa ku Yisa la ni, ka gaadi maa puuni ka ba daa pun kyiibi tampifeoo gballi duu, amaa ka na bu zaŋŋi sɔ n‑sɔ'ai ka puuni.

english_verse: Jesus knew that he had now finished his work. And in order to make the Scriptures come true, he said, “I am thirsty!”
translation_verse: Tɔ: di daa niŋi lala, ka Yisa daa naayi ti baŋŋi, ni, u tum bunseelikam zaa n‑naai. Di saha ka u daa yɛli, “Kɔnyuuri m mari ma!” - ka yɛligu wa gba dɔli la din daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli Litaafi maa ni seem la.

english_verse: A jar of cheap wine was there. Someone then soaked a sponge with the wine and held it up to Jesus' mouth on the stem of a hyssop plant.
translation_verse: Di saha ka damiisiri n daa bɛ dukku ni n‑zɛ bɛni. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi sɔ'asu n‑niŋi daam maa ni n‑kusi ka n‑tabli bunni zugu n‑duusi m‑paai u noori, ni, u nyuuma.

english_verse: kilograms of spices made from myrrh and aloes. This was the same Nicodemus who had visited Jesus one night.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa nyu n‑naai maa, u daa yɛli, ni, “Di naaya!”, ka daa sunni u zugu n‑leebi bunni din ka ni.

english_verse: The next day would be both a Sabbath and the Passover. It was a special day for the Jewish people, and they did not want the bodies to stay on the crosses during this day. So they asked Pilate to break the men's legs and take their bodies down.
translation_verse: Dirindaari maa daa nyɛ Kyuu maa siri maaligu dabsiri, ka lala zugu ka Yɛhudiyadima maa daa bu boori la niriba n kpalim n‑tabli dɔpula maa ni ba ti kpi ka di bɛ la Kyuu la Vuusim Dabsiri maa ni, dama dabsitibsiri daari n‑daa la. Ka ba daa susi Gɔmna Pailiti, ni, u kyɛ ka ba kabsi niriba maa gbaya ka ba kpi yooma ka ba sigsi ba na.

english_verse: One of the soldiers stuck his spear into Jesus' side, and blood and water came out.
translation_verse: Amaa ba ninvugsɔ daa zaŋŋi u kpanni n‑kusi Yisa pulugri ka ziim daa toosi n‑yi na, ni koom m‑paasi.

english_verse: We know this is true, because it was told by someone who saw it happen. Now you can have faith too.
translation_verse: [Tɔ, ya bu nya? - ŋɔn yɛtti lahabaari wa n daa bɛ bɛni ka u nini nya. U lahabaari yɛligu wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni: u mammaŋŋa mi halli, ni, yɛlimaŋni n nyɛ di, ni ya gba ti niŋi yɛdda di zugu.]

english_verse: All this happened so that the Scriptures would come true, which say, “No bone of his body will be broken”
translation_verse: Anŋɔ zaa daa niŋi lala ni di kyɛ ka din daa sɔbi n‑dooli siri niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni, “U kɔba muna - aseaa zaa ku kabsi.”

english_verse: and “They will see the one in whose side they stuck a spear.” Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi yaasa, ni, “Ba nin gɔsi n‑nya ban daa kusi sɔ la.”

english_verse: The tomb was nearby, and since it was the time to prepare for the Sabbath, they were in a hurry to put Jesus' body there.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑dooli, dama ka daa bu wɔ'a: ba daa na kur teesiri la Yɛhudiyadima Kyuu maa siri maaligu dabsiri yɛla.

english_verse: Joseph from Arimathea was one of Jesus' disciples. He had kept it secret though, because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. But now he asked Pilate to let him have Jesus' body. Pilate gave him permission, and Joseph took it down from the cross.
translation_verse: Tɔ: dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni ka u yuuri boonni Gyoosɛfi, ŋɔn daa yi Arimatii tiŋŋa la na. Ka ŋɔn Gyoosɛfi daa nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa dɔli la Yisa, amaa u daa sɔ'ai n‑dɔli ni, Yɛhudiyadima maa dabeem zugu. Ka Yisa kum nyaaŋŋa u daa kyɛ Pailiti saani na nti susi u, ni, u kyɛ ka u deaai Yisa ningbiŋŋu la n‑sɔ'ai. Ka Pailiti daa sakki n‑ti u. Ka Gyoosɛfi daa kyaŋŋi nti deaai ningbiŋŋu maa m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: The two men wrapped the body in a linen cloth, together with the spices, which was how the Jewish people buried their dead.
translation_verse: Ba daa zaŋŋi Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa n‑vili n‑vili tampeela n‑niŋni turaali dima wa, hoo ba Yɛhudiyadima kum sɔ'abu kaari ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem.

english_verse: Thomas replied, “You are my Lord and my God!”
translation_verse: Ka Tɔmaasi daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Nyin n nyɛ Naawunni n‑ti ma: n Dugma n nyɛ i!”

english_verse: On Sunday morning while it was still dark, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb and saw that the stone had been rolled away from the entrance.
translation_verse: Din daari asuba pinipini - Alahadaari n‑daa la - halli beoo ŋɔn daa na bu neaai la, Mariama, Magdala tiŋŋa pɔ'a la, daa kyaŋŋi dabɔri maa ni. Ka u daa ti nya ka kugri maa laa n‑ka dabɔri maa noori ni.

english_verse: The disciple who got there first then went into the tomb, and when he saw it, he believed.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka nyaandɔla la gba daa kpɛa - sɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi tooni m‑paai bɛni la. Ŋɔn mun daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka daa niŋi yɛdda.

english_verse: Although Thomas the Twin was one of the twelve disciples, he wasn't with the others when Jesus appeared to them.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Tɔmaasi daa ka bɛni, ban daa pa sɔ yupaa, ni, ‘Gyeaa la’: Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ ba saani na sahanseaa la u daa ka ba saani.

english_verse: and saw two angels inside. They were dressed in white and were sitting where Jesus' body had been. One was at the head and the other was at the foot.
translation_verse: nti nya malaakadima ayi ka ba yɛ neenpeela n‑zi n‑tabi Yisa ningbiŋŋu maa ŋɔn daa dɔ seela. Ba yinni n daa zi Yisa zugu ya'ari ni, ka ŋɔnla mun daa zi u gbaya ya'ari.

english_verse: She ran to Simon Peter and to Jesus' favorite disciple and said, “They have taken the Lord from the tomb! We don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa zɔ n‑labi Saimoon Piita saani na, ŋɔn ni Yisa nyaandɔla sɔa, dɔsɔ Yisa ŋɔn daa wuri pam la. M‑paai ba na nti yɛli ba, ni, “Ba zaŋŋi ti Dugma n‑yiisi u dabɔri maa ni na, ka ti bu mi ba n zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi seela!”

english_verse: Peter and the other disciple started for the tomb.
translation_verse: Ka Piita ni nyaandɔla wa daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi dabɔri maa ni.

english_verse: They ran side by side, until the other disciple ran faster than Peter and got there first.
translation_verse: Ba bayi maa daa zɔra, ka ŋɔnla daa zɔ n‑gaari Piita n‑daŋŋi u tooni m‑paai dabɔri maa na.

english_verse: He bent over and saw the strips of linen cloth lying inside the tomb, but he did not go in.
translation_verse: U daa kunni n‑nya tampeela maa ŋɔn dɔya, amaa u daa bu kpɛ dabɔri duu maa ni.

english_verse: When Simon Peter got there, he went into the tomb and saw the strips of cloth.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Piita daa kari u nɔba ka naayi paai na nti kpɛ dabɔri duu maa ni. Ŋɔn gba daa gɔsi n‑nya tampeela ka a dɔya,

english_verse: He also saw the piece of cloth that had been used to cover Jesus' face. It was rolled up and in a place by itself.
translation_verse: ka daa laa n‑nya kyinkyinseaa ba n daa zaŋŋi m‑pili Yisa zugu la - kana mun daa kpabi ni n‑dɔ kpaŋŋa sa, ka daa ka tampeela maa ziiya.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene stood crying outside the tomb. She was still weeping, when she stooped down
translation_verse: Mariama mun daa kpalim n‑zɛ la dabɔri maa zii n‑kumna. U daa kur kpalim n‑kumni maa, ka kunni n‑gɔsi dabɔri duu maa puuni

english_verse: The angels asked Mary, “Why are you crying?” She answered, “They have taken away my Lord's body! I don't know where they have put him.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boosi Mariama n‑yɛli, “Pɔ'a, bɔ n niŋi ka i kumna?”; ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ba zaŋŋi la n Dugma, ka m bu mi ba n zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi seela.”

english_verse: Jesus asked her, “Why are you crying? Who are you looking for?” She thought he was the gardener and said, “Sir, if you have taken his body away, please tell me, so I can go and get him.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa boosi u, ni, “M ma, bɔ n niŋi ka i kumna? I boori la sɔ ziiya, bee?”: ŋɔn mun daa teesi, ni, gaadi maa daana n nyɛ u, ka dizugu u daa yɛli u, ni, “M Bapira, nyin yi zaŋŋi u m‑bakki, nyin wulisi ma ka n kyaŋŋi nti zaŋŋi u.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Don't hold on to me! I have not yet gone to the Father. But tell my disciples I am going to the one who is my Father and my God, as well as your Father and your God.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, ni, “Di siisiri ma, dama n na bu labi m Ba maa saani. See ka i kyaŋŋi nti yɛli m mabiisi maa, ni, n doaaya ka n ya n‑labi m Ba Naawunni saani, ka u nyɛ la ba gba Ba Naawunni.”

english_verse: and showed them his hands and his side. When the disciples saw the Lord, they became very happy.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa puusi ba n‑naai maa, u daa zaŋŋi u nuusi ni u pulugri n‑wulisi ba. Di saha ka u nyaandɔliba maa sufu daa maai pam, ni, ba nyaya, ni, ba Dugma nla.

english_verse: The disciples were afraid of the Jewish leaders, and on the evening of that same Sunday they locked themselves in a room. Suddenly, Jesus appeared in the middle of the group. He greeted them
translation_verse: Di dabsiri maa, Alahadaari maa, zaanoori saha, ka Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa la'am taaba n‑kpɛ duu ni nti kpari dunoori maa, dama ba daa zɔri la Yɛhudiya dima maa dabeem. Di saha ka Yisa daa kpɛ na nti zaani ba sunsukkuni m‑puusi ba.

english_verse: After Jesus had greeted them again, he said, “I am sending you, just as the Father has sent me.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa laa m‑puusi ba yaasa, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, “M Ba ŋɔn daa tum ma na seem la, lala ka n ya n‑tum yarima gba.”

english_verse: Then he breathed on them and said, “Receive the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka u daa vuusi vuusim n‑ti ba ka yɛli, “Ya deaam Sii Kasi la.

english_verse: If you forgive anyone's sins, they will be forgiven. But if you don't forgive their sins, they will not be forgiven.” Jesus and Thomas
translation_verse: Ya yi ti kyɛ sɔ tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi u, di daama siri nin nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi: ka yan mun yi kyɛ ka sɔ taalli ti kpalim n‑dɔli u, di daama nin kpalim m‑mari taalli.”

english_verse: So they told him, “We have seen the Lord!” But Thomas said, “First, I must see the nail scars in his hands and touch them with my finger. I must put my hand where the spear went into his side. I won't believe unless I do this!”
translation_verse: Sahanseaa ka u nyaandɔlitaaba daa ti nya u nti yɛli u, ni, “Ti nya ti Dugma!”, ŋɔn Tɔmaasi daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, “M mammaŋŋa yi bu nya u nuusi ka kpaasi daalima m bɛ a ni, halli n‑laa n‑zaŋŋi n nubila wa n‑tum u nuusi dansi wa ni, ka zaŋŋi n nuu n‑kpeesi u pulugri la daŋŋa ni gba - ay! di yi pa lala n ku niŋi yɛttɔ'a maa yɛdda.”

english_verse: A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi dabsa anii ka ba daa maali n‑la'am n‑kpɛ duu ni, ka Tɔmaasi mun gba daa bɛ ba saani. Ba daa laa n‑kpari dunoori, ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ na nti zaani ba sunsukkuni m‑puusi ba.

english_verse: and said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa yɛli Tɔmaasi, ni, “Tɔ! zaŋŋi i nubila n‑tum na n‑nya n nuusi, ka zaŋŋi i nuu n‑kpeesi n pulugri wa daŋŋa wa ni! Ka di laa n‑za'asiri yɛdda niŋbu, amaa ka niŋi yɛdda.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Thomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!” Why John Wrote His Book
translation_verse: Yisa mun daa yɛli u, “I nini n nya maa, ka di zugu ka i niŋni yɛdda, bee? Zugsuŋŋu siri nin bɛ ni n‑tiri seeba ban bu nya amaa ka niŋni yɛdda!”

english_verse: Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book.
translation_verse: Tɔ: di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni - Yisa siri daa tum tunkara ni makkri yɛla pampam m‑paasi anŋɔ zugu, ka daa wulisi u nyaandɔliba maa. Pa anŋɔ yɛla zaa n sɔbi n‑niŋi bukku wa puuni.

english_verse: But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
translation_verse: Aseesi mun sɔbi n‑dooli kpa, ni ya ti niŋi yɛdda ka baŋŋi, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Masiiya maa, Naawunni ŋɔn tum sɔ na ka diisi u naam la - halli Naawunni Bii n nyɛ u gba. Ka anŋɔ sɔbiya, ni ya ti niŋi lala yɛdda ka lala zugu ya nin ti nya nyɔvuri ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu.

english_verse: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Saimoon Piita daa yɛli, ni, “N wa la zaasim gbaabu tuma ni.” Ka ba zaa yɛli u, ni, “Tɔ! Ti gba ya dɔli i n‑kyaŋŋi.” Ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi mɔ'ari la ziiya n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑du. Ka ba daa tum halli ka beoo neaai, n‑wɔlim n‑gyɛa. Ba daa bu gbaai halli ziŋŋa yinni.

english_verse: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
translation_verse: N‑neaai beoo asuba pinipini ka Yisa daa zɛ mɔ'agbiriŋŋu ni. Amaa u nyaandɔliba maa daa bu baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔna nla.

english_verse: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
translation_verse: Ni, “Tɔ: see ka ya zaŋŋi laaŋŋu maa n‑lɔbi nyariŋŋu maa nudirigu lugri ziiya! Lala ka ya nin ti nya.” Ka ba daa niŋi lala. Ka ba daa yɛli, ni, ba ya voaai laaŋŋu maa n‑niŋi nyariŋŋu puuni, ka vaai n‑kɔŋŋi, dama zimi pampam daa bɛ ka ni, ka ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑voaara.

english_verse: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
translation_verse: Di saha ka nyaandɔlisɔ Yisa ŋɔn daa wuri pam la daa ti yɛli Piita, ni, “Ti Dugma nla!” Ka Saimoon Piita ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, u daa yɛ u kparigu - dama u daa pun yeaai ka, ni u tumna - ka yikki n‑lu mɔ'ari koom maa ni.

english_verse: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
translation_verse: Ba daa paaya, nti nya bugunsaala, ka zaasim beela ni paanu daa tɔm ka zugu n‑seera.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Ya zaŋŋi yan na pun gbaai zinseesi la na!”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, ni, “N zuusi ya! Ya kyɛm na n‑su noori!” Ka vi daa mari ba ka ba daa bu boosi u, ni, “Nyin n nyɛ ŋɔni?”, dama ba daa mi, ni, ti Dugma n‑daa la.

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi paanu maa n‑ti ba na, ni zinseera gba.

english_verse: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
translation_verse: Tɔ: Yisa ŋɔn daa wulisi u maŋŋa n‑ti u nyaandɔliba, ni, u yisigi kum ni na, zii napɔmbuta n‑daa la.

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa di n‑naai maa, Yisa daa yɛli Saimoon Piita, ni, “Saimoon, nyin Gyɔni bii wa, i wuri ma n‑gaari niriba wa, bee?” Ka u daa yɛli, “Ii, n Dugma, i mi, ni, n nyɛ la i zɔa.” Ka u yɛli u, ni, “Wubsima n pɛbisi wa.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
translation_verse: Ka u daa maali n‑yɛli u yaasa, ni, “Saimoon, nyin Gyɔni bii wa, i wuri ma, bee?” Ka u daa yɛli, “Ii, n Dugma; i mi, ni, n nyɛ la i zɔa.” Ka u yɛli u, “Kur guri n peesi.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
translation_verse: Ka u maali n‑yɛli u napɔmbuta daana, ni, “Saimoon, nyin Gyɔni bii wa, ammi, i siri nyɛ la n zɔa?” Di saha ka Piita sufu daa bu maai, Yisa ŋɔn boosi u di napɔmbuta wa, ni, u zɔ n nyɛ u, bee? Di zugu u daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “I mi n yɛla halli zaa, n Dugma; ka baŋŋi, ni, n nyɛ la i zɔa.” Ka Yisa yɛli u, “Kur wubsiri n peesi maa.”

english_verse: I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti i wa, ni, nyin ŋɔn daa nyɛ na'akyimbila, i daa bɔbi i maŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi i sufu yuubu kyɛnni, amaa nyin yi ti kurigi maa i nin teaai i nuusi ka ba nin yeeli i n‑taasi i ya'aseaa i sufu ŋɔn bu boora.”

english_verse: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa yɛli u lala, u daa wulisiri la u kum soori yɛla n‑tiri u, ni, lala ka u nin ti ti Naawunni gyirima. Ka naayi yɛli u, ni, “Kur dɔli n nyaaŋŋa.”

english_verse: When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
translation_verse: Ka Piiya daa nya u ka daa boosi Yisa n‑yɛli, ni, “N Dugma, ka ŋɔnŋɔ fa?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.”
translation_verse: Ka Yisa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Halli n yi ti boori ni u kpalim m‑bɛ ni halli ni n ti labi na, pa i yɛlli nla: nyin mun ka n yɛtta - kur dɔli ma!”

english_verse: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
translation_verse: Lala maa zugu ka ninvugseeba daa yɛtti taaba, ni, nyaandɔla wa ku kpi: amaa Yisa daa bu yɛli, ni, u ku kpi; u daa yɛli Piita, ni, “Halli n yi ti boori ni doo wa kpalim m‑bɛ ni halli ni n ti labi na, pa i yɛlli nla.”

english_verse: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
translation_verse: [Ŋɔna n nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ ŋɔn daa nya anŋɔ zaa n‑sɔbi n‑ti ti la; ka tin mun mi, ni, ŋɔn wulisi seem wa zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.]

english_verse: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa daa tum tuntumsa pam m‑paasi anŋɔ zugu. A zaa yi ti sɔbi n‑dooli yinniyinni, n bu teesi, ni, paalu naa m‑bɛ duniya wa zaa puuni ni ba ti mari lala litaafidima maa n‑su.

english_verse: Theophilus, I first wrote to you about all that Jesus did and taught from the very first
translation_verse: M Ba Tiyɔfiluusi: N piiligu gbaŋŋu maa ni, n daa sɔbi bunseelli kam zaa Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi ka wulisi, sahanseaa ŋɔn daa piili u tuma maa la.

english_verse: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
translation_verse: Tɔ; lala dabsa maa, sahaseaa ban ni ŋɔna ŋɔn daa la'am n‑diri maa, u daa yɛli ba u kpaaŋŋu maa puuni, ni, ba di yi Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, di yi pa ni ba ti nya piiniseaa ŋɔn daa yɛli ba la, piiniseaa u Ba ŋɔn daa pun tisi noori, ni, u nin ti ba maa.

english_verse: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
translation_verse: Ni, “Gyɔni mun daa zaŋŋi koom n‑su ya, amaa dabsa ayi saha ya nin deaai Vuusim Kasi suubu.”

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Sahanseaa yaasa, Yisa nyaandɔliba zaa daa la'am u saani na, ka ba daa boosi u, ni, “Ti Dugma, pampaŋŋɔ ka i nin zaŋŋi naam suulinsi la nti labsi n‑niŋi Izraɛli dima nuu ni yaasa, bee?”

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: Amaa Sii Kasi maa yi ti kyɛ ya saani na, yiiko nin kpɛ ya sufu ni n‑kyɛ ka ya niŋi sɛhara dima m‑mooli man yɛla Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ni Gyuudii ni Samɛrii tinsi zaa, halli nti paai duniya wa lugri kam zaa.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Tɔ: ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi n‑naai, ba daa nya ka u duuri la saazugu nti kpɛ sangbana ni, ka sangbaŋŋu maa daa ligi u ka ba daa bu laa n‑nya u yaasa.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Ba daa kur gɔsiri ni, halli ka u ti kpɛ sangbana ni. Di saha maa ka niriba ayi daa gbirigi n‑zaani ba saani, niriba maa mun neema daa pɛligiya ni, nyain!

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: Ba daa yɛli nyaandɔliba maa, ni, “Yan Galilii dima wa, wula zugu ka ya kpalim n‑zɛya n‑gɔsiri sangbana ni? Ya pun nya ka Yisa da du m‑paai arizana ni; tɔ, ka ŋɔn Yisa siri nin labi na, hoo yan nya u ka u du arizana seem la.”

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Ba daa kpɛ tiŋŋa maa ni m‑paai yiri la, n‑du n‑kpɛ gyirambisa duseaa ban daa gbɛ la. Ba daa nyɛ la Piita ni Gyɔni, ni Gyeemsi, ni Anduru, ni Filipi, ni Tɔmaasi, ni Baatɔlɔmiyu, ni Matiyu, ni Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Saimoon sɔ ban daa boonni ‘Zɛlɔti’ la, ni Gyeemsi Gyuudasi.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Banŋɔ zaa nini n daa moaai n‑la'amni taaba m‑puusiri Naawunni, ban ni pɔ'aba maa, ni Mariama, Yisa ma la, ni Yisa mabiisi.

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: [Baŋŋma, m Ba Tiyɔfiluusi, ni, ŋɔn Gyuudasi daa deaai ligri u tumbeoo zugu, ka daa zaŋŋi a n‑da puu, ka u daa lu tiŋŋa u nindaa zugu n‑kpi. U puuri daa tasi, ka u puuri ni nyoori daa yi na m‑basi.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Gyɛrusalɛm niriba zaa daa wum di yɛla, ka dizugu ba daa booli puu maa, ni, ‘Akyɛlidama’ - di gbinni nyɛ la ‘Ziim Ziiya’.]

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Dama di sɔbiya Yuuma gbaŋŋu ni, ni#:- ‘U yiri nin labgi daboo, Sɔ ku laa m‑bɛ ka ni.’ ka di laa n‑sɔbi, ni#:- ‘Kyɛ ka sɔ deaai u ziiya n‑tumna!’

english_verse: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
translation_verse: Dizugu di sumni ni ninvugsɔ ti ya la'am ni ti, ni u ti niŋi sɛhara, ni, u daa siri nya ka baŋŋi, ni, ti Dugma Yisa daa yisigi kum ni. U nin nyɛ la dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ti saani sahanseaa ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn daa bɛ ni ka ti daa la'amni n‑tabinni u saani maa, n‑yi Gyɔni ŋɔn daa piili n‑suuri niriba Naawunni koom la, halli nti paai dabsiseaa Yisa ŋɔn daa du arizana ni la.”

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yiisi lala niriba ayi: Gyoosɛfi ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Baasabasi la, ka ba daa pa u yupaa, ni, ‘Gyasitisi’ la; ka ŋɔn paasi buyi mun yuuri n daa boonni Matayasi.

english_verse: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna ba daa susi Naawunni, ni, “Ti Dugma, nyini mi sɔkam zaa sufu ni teesa, ka dizugu wulisim ti niriba ayi wa puuni nyin, Naawunni, ŋɔn boori sɔ,

english_verse: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
translation_verse: ni u ti zaani Gyuudasi zaaziiya n‑tumni i tuma maa. Gyuudasi mun daa bakki nti kyaŋŋi u maŋtoori zii la ni.”

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa tɔ la tɛtɛ ni ba yiisi ba yinni, ka Matayasi daa di tɛtɛ maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi u m‑paasi tuumudima piiya ni yinni la ni.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka Pɛntikoosi Kyuu maa ŋɔn daa ti paai na, Yisa nyaandɔliba maa zaa daa la'am m‑bɛ duu yinni puuni.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Ba daa zinni, ka di daa bu yuui ka ba daa wum ka bunseelli niŋi vuuri ka di ŋmanni pɛbsigu titaari n‑yiiri saazugu n‑sigri na, ka di daa kyɛ na m‑paali duseaa ban daa bɛ ni la.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi daa kpɛ ba zaa ka ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu. Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn daa ti ba yɛttɔ'a seem, lala ka sɔkam daa tɔ'asira.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Lala niriba maa daa wum vuuri maa, ka dizugu daa kyɛ ka ba daa la'am yiri maa ni na. Ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, ban daa ti wum ka Yisa nyaandɔliba maa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ba, ba yinni yinni yɛttɔ'a puuni.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi ba zaa alahazibu pam, ka ba daa boosi taaba n‑yɛli, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni, niriba ban tɔ'asiri wa pa la Galilii niriba?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Ka di niŋi la wula ka ti zaa yinni yinni wumni di gbinni u mammantoori yɛttɔ'a puuni?

english_verse: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
translation_verse: Ti seeba bu yi Paatiya na, ni Midiya, ni Ɛlaam m‑paasi? Ti seeba mun bu yi Mɛsɔpɔteemiya na, ni Gyuudiiya, ni Kappadɔsiiya, ni Pɔntusi ni Ɛseaa?

english_verse: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
translation_verse: Yɛhudiyadima n nyɛ ti, ni seeba ban dɔli Yɛhudiyadima kaari: Kyiriti dima n nyɛ ti, ni Arabii dima m‑paasi. Ka ti sɔkam zaasa wumni ka ba tɔ'asiri Naawunni ŋɔn tum tunkara seesi n‑ti ti, ti toori yɛttɔ'a puuni!”

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Di daa gyɛri daadam maa pam, ka ba daa boosiri taaba n‑yɛtta, “Bɔ ka dinŋɔ wulisira?”

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Baseeba mun daa tuuri ba n‑yɛtta, “Ba nyuuri la daam m‑bugi!”

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Yisa tuumudima piiya ni ayi la zaa daa yisigi; ka Piita daa bɛ ba tooni, n‑tasi pampam m‑booli zama maa n‑yɛli, “Yarima n Yɛhudiyataaba wa, ni ya zaa ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm puuni, ya baŋŋma dinŋɔ gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka wum n yɛligu wa!

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: Niriba wa bu nyuuri la daam m‑bugi, hoo yan teesiri seem la; di na kur nyɛ la asuba paalli!

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Ay! Dinŋɔ ka anabi Yɔweelli daa wulisi, ni#:-

english_verse: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
translation_verse: ‘Naawunni yɛliya#:- “Di nin niŋi Dabsiri maa daari - N nin zaŋŋi n Vuusim Kasi n‑kpeesi nira kam zaa ni; N nin kyɛ ka ya biisi nin tɔ'asiri man Naawunni yɛligu - dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa. Ya na'akyimbisi nin ti nyari din nin ti niŋi, ka ya ninvugkura nin ti zaasiri zaasimaŋa.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, n nin teaai halli n daaba gba yɛla - dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa - ka n nin zaŋŋi n Sii maa n‑ti ba, Ka u nin kyɛ ka ba tɔ'asiri man Naawunni yɛligu.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: N nin wulisi n kpeeŋŋu sangbana ni, ka n nin zaŋŋi di n‑wulisi tingbaŋŋu ni gba, kaman ziim ni bugum ni nyoosi.

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: Wuntaŋŋa maa nin zibgi limm, Namboaa maa mun nin moaai hoo ziim. Ka dinŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ya Dugma Dabsiri maa nin ti paai na, dabsititaari n nyɛ ka, ni bunveelli.

english_verse: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔnkam zaa mi ti Dugma yuuri nti susiri u, nin nya tuliginsim.” ’ ”

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ka dinŋɔna, ya Izraɛli niriba, ya wumma n yɛttɔ'a wa! N tɔ'asiri Yisa ŋɔn nyɛ Nazarɛti nira la yɛla, sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi la. Ka ya gba baŋŋi u yɛla, Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka u daa tum alahazibu tuma ya sunsukkuni maa zugu.

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Ka di zaa nyaaŋŋa di daa niŋi hoo Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun teesi n‑zaali seem la; ka ba daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn Yisa n‑niŋi ya nuu ni, ka ya mun daa deaai u n‑tisi ninvugbeeri ka ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu n‑ku u.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni daa neaai u kum ni, n‑tiligi u kum wahala puuni, dama soori daa ka ni ni kum kpalim m‑mari u.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: NaaDauda daa bu tɔ'asi u yɛla? Ni#:- ‘N daa daŋŋi n‑nya ka baŋŋi, ni, n Dugma nin bɛ n tooni saha kam; Ŋɔna n zɛ n nudirigu ziiya ka n ku laa n‑damna.

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka n sufu daa maai, ka n zulimni tɔ'asiri nya'asim pam: Ka m mari la tama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n ningbiŋŋu yɛla.

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: Nyin, Naawunni, ku basi n siiya n‑ti kpiimma tiŋŋa, Bee, i kyɛ ka i bisuŋŋu maa nya poaari.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: Nyini n wulisiri ma i beesigu soori, Ka kyɛ ka n bɛ i tooni ka nya sufupeelim pam.’

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: Tɔ; dizugu, m mabiisi, ya kyɛ ka n yɛli ya paalu ni, ni, ti yaaba NaaDauda daa ka ni, ka ba sɔ'ai u, ka u gballi na kpalim m‑bɛ kpa, halli nti paai zuna dabsiri wa.

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Tirima muna zaa daa pun baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni daa yisigi ŋɔn Yisa kum ni, ka ti tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya, ni, u siri yisigiya.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Ka u naayi doaai n‑du saazugu n‑zi u zizii kan bɛ Naawunni nudirigu maa ni: n‑ziya, ka niŋi ŋɔn daa pun tisi noori ni u nin niŋi seem la, ka daa deaai Vuusim Kasi maa u Ba saani ka tum u ti saani na. Ka yan nyari ka wumni seelli wa zaa yi la ŋɔn Vuusim saani na.

english_verse: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - di daa pa la Dauda mammaŋŋa n daa du arizana ni, amaa u daa yɛli, ni#:- ‘Ti Dugma daa yɛli n‑ti n Dugma maa, Ni, “Kyɛm n nudirigu zii na n‑zinni,

english_verse: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
translation_verse: Halli ka n nin zaŋŋi i dindaandima zaa N‑niŋi i nuusi ni.” ’

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ya kyɛ ka ya Izraɛli niriba zaa sakki ka baŋŋi, ni, halli ni ya daa zaŋŋi Yisa n‑ku u maa zaa yɔri, Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u ti niŋi ti Dugma, ni Masiiya ti Tuligra.”

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban daa wum yɛttɔ'a wa n‑naai la, ba sufu daa sa'amiya pam, ka ba daa boosi Piita ni u taaba maa, ni, “Ti mabiisi, wula ka ti ya n‑niŋi?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Tuuba! Ya kyɛli ya tumbeeri tuma m‑basi, ka ya sɔkam zaa yinni yinni deaai Naawunni koom suubu, Yisa Masiiya Tuligra yuuri zugu. Ka Naawunni nin kyɛ ya taalli zaa m‑pɛŋŋi ya, ka ya nin deaai piini Naawunni saani, piini maa mun nyɛ Naawunni Sii Kasi kpeebu ya sufu ni.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni n daa pun pɔ noori maa n‑ti yarima, ni ya biisi maa; n‑ti seeba ban bɛ wa'aligu ni seeba zaa ti Dugma ŋɔn ti ya m‑booli la.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Piita daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'asuma pam m‑paasi ba, ka laa n‑kpamsi ba nimmoaa, n‑yɛli, ni, “Ya tuligma ya mansi n‑yi zuna niriba wa sunsukkuni na, dama bumpoosa n kur nyɛ ba!”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Ka ba daa zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑wumni Yisa tuumudima wulisigu maa ni nimbiri moaa, ka la'amni ni ban n‑diri diibu ka puusiri Naawunni ni taaba.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dabeem daa sigi sɔkam zaa zugu na, ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u tuumudima tumni tunkara ni alahazibu yɛla.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa daa la'am m‑bɛ ni, ka daa sakki n‑yɛtti, ni, ba zaa marigu nyɛ la yimmu.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Ba daa koosiri ban mari neenseesi la m‑putti ligri maa n‑tiri taaba, hoo sɔkam bukaata ŋɔn daa bɛ seem la.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Ba daa la'am m‑bɛ ni dabsiri kam zaa Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka laa n‑la'amni taaba n‑diri diibu ba mammantoori yiya ni. Ba daa diri nya'asim pam ka ba sufu daa maai zaa.

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: Ba daa pɛ'ari Naawunni, ka niriba zaa mun daa boori ba yɛla. Ka beoo kam zaa ti Dugma daa kyɛ ka niriba daa nya tuliginsim n‑la'amni na m‑paasiri ba zugu.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Dahinseaa ka Piita ni Gyɔni ti kyɛnni Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wuntaŋŋa kurigu ata saha, din nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima Naawunni susigu saha la.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba daa zi gbariga na - ninvugsɔ ban pun dɔ'ai u lala u biilim ni, nti dooli Gyeeŋŋu Yiri za'anɔseaa ban daa boonni ‘Za'anɔveelli’ la ziiya. Ba daa yi pun ziiri u la lala na nti doontiri bɛni dabsa zaa, ka u maanni bara zama maa saani, ban daa yi kpeeri Yiri maa ni sahanseaa la.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Tɔ; barimaanna wa n daa nya ka Piita ni Gyɔni ya n‑kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni na, ka u maanni ba bara.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Ka Piita mirigi u nini n‑kpa u, ni Gyɔni gba m‑paasi, ka yɛli u, “Gɔsim ti!”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Ka u wukki u zugu n‑gɔsiri ba, ka teesiri, ni, ba ya ti u seelli.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Ka Piita yɛli u, “N ka la'afu ni n ti i, amaa n nin ti i man mari bunseelli: Yisa Masiiya yuuri zugu, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Nazarɛti tiŋŋa nira la, doaam saazugu n‑zaani ka leebi ŋɔn kyɛnna!”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: N‑daa yɛli lala, ka daa gbaai doo maa nudirigu n‑suŋŋi u ni u doaai na. Di saha ka u napɔna ni u napɔnkpaasi daa kpaŋŋi zaa,

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: ka u doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnna, ka dɔli ba halli nti kpɛ Yiri maa puuni. Ka kur gbaai kyɛnni ka yikkri m‑puusiri ka pɛ'ari Naawunni.

english_verse: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
translation_verse: Ka sɔkam daa nya u kyɛnni maa ka pɛ'ari Naawunni,

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: ka baŋŋi, ni, ŋɔna n yi nyɛ gbariga n‑zintiri Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Za'anɔveelli ziiya m‑maanni bara la. Ka din daa niŋi doo maa daa gyɛri ba ka daa niŋi ba alahazibu pam.

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Tɔ; gbariga maa daa kur vili Piita ni Gyɔni m‑mari ba kirikiri, ka daadam zaa daa kabi n‑zɔri n‑dɔli ba, halli nti la'am Yiri maa maasim ziseaa ban daa boonni ‘NaaSulɛmaana Leeŋŋa’ la ni.

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Piita ŋɔn daa nya lala, u daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, “Yan Izraɛli dima wa, bɔ n niŋi ka yɛlli wa niŋi ya alahazibu? Bee wula ka ya ti gɔsiri ti hoo tirima mammaŋŋa yiiko zugu n kyɛ ka gbariga wa doaai n‑kyɛnni wa? - bee, ti mammaŋŋa ban nyɛ ninvugsuma zugu?”

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ay! Naawunni tuma nla - Naawunni ŋɔn daa nya pɛ'abu ti yaadima saani: Ibrahim daa gyeem u, ni Yisahaaku, ni Yaakubu - ŋɔn Naawunni n daa pun duusi u yiribii Yisa yuuri: amaa yarima daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn Yisa n‑taasi Gɔmna Pailiti saani nti kari u sariya, ka Gɔmna ŋɔn daa boori ni u basi u sahanseaa la ya daa za'asi u yɛla.

english_verse: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
translation_verse: Ii! - Ninvugsuŋŋu sɔ ŋɔn daa laa n‑nyɛ Naawunni nirimaŋni ka ya daa za'asi, ka daa laa n‑kyɛ ka Gɔmna zaŋŋi ninvugkuurisɔ m‑basi n‑ti ya,

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: ka ku Nyɔvuri Daana maa. Naawunni mun daa neaai u kum ni. Tirima n daa nya ka baŋŋi, ni, lala n nyɛ yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu da kyɛ ka gbariga wa kpaŋŋi, u yuuri yɛdda niŋbu zugu. Ya nyari doo wa ka mi u; ŋɔn niŋi Yisa yuuri yɛdda maa kyɛ ka u nya alaafɛ zaa pampaŋŋɔa, ka ya mammansi nini nya.”

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “M mabiisi, m baŋŋiya halli, ni, ziilinsi zugu ka ya daa niŋi Yisa lala, yan ni ya kpamma zaa.

english_verse: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya niŋma tuuba ka ŋmaligi n‑kyɛ ya tumbeeri tuma zaa, ka Naawunni nin deaai ya taalli zaa sindaani.

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Dama dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ti Dugma nin taasi maasim saha na n‑ti ya, ka naayi tum ŋɔn pun yiisi Masisɔ nti diisi naam fukumsi maa, ŋɔn Yisa nla, ka kyɛ ka u labi na n‑di di zaa.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Musa daa yɛliya: ‘Ti Dugma Naawunni nin ti yiisi sɔ ya mabiisi puuni n‑kyɛ ka u niŋi anabi, kaman ŋɔn daa yiisi ma seem la.’, ni, ‘U yi ti yɛli ya seelli kam, ya dɔli ka lala.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Ka sɔ kam zaa ŋɔn nin ti za'asi lala anabi yɛligu maa wumpu, ŋɔna nin ti ya m‑baasi yɔri.’ ”

english_verse: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Yɛlimaŋni, anabidima zaa - n‑yi Samuwɛli halli ni zuna - ba zaa daa tɔ'asi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti zuna saha wa yɛla.

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Ya gba nyɛ la anabidima wa ya'ansi, ka ya su nangbambasiseelli Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi n‑ti ya yaadima maa. U daa yɛli ti yaaba Ibrahim, ni, u biisi zuliya zugu ka duniya wa niriba zaa nin nya zugsuŋŋu.

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Dizugu u daa yiisi u yiribii maa n‑tum u na, ni u ti kyɛ ka yarima daŋŋi n‑nya lala zugsuŋŋu maa. Ya beesigu takkri zugu ka ya zaa nin yi ya tumbeeri ni.”

english_verse: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Piita ni Gyɔni daa na kur bɛ ni n‑tɔ'asiri ni n‑tiri zama maa, ka maalimaanniba daa kyɛ na, ni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Pirinsi toondaana, ni Saddusidima maa m‑paasi.

english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti paai ba saani na la, ba sufu daa yisigiya pam dama Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa tɔ'asiri ka wulisiri zama maa, ni, niriba nin ti yisigi kum puuni hoo Yisa ŋɔn daa pun yisigi seem la.

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu ba daa gbaai Piita ni Gyɔni n‑kpeesi sarika duu ni, ka guri ba ni beoo neaai, din daa pun zibgiri maa zugu.

english_verse: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, dama niriba pam ban daa wum Piita ni Gyɔni yɛttɔ'a la daa sakki ba teesa puuni n‑ti Yisa. Seeba ban zaa daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa daa nyɛ la dɔppa tusa anu.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ba daa mari ba Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni m‑booli zuguraandima ni ba kyam na n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a maa - ban ni tinkpamma ni karimbadima maa zaa.

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: Annasi sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana gba daa la'am m‑bɛ bɛni, ni Kayafasi ni Gyɔni ni Alɛkzanda ni ba dɔ'am kpamma zaa.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Ba daa zaali Piita ni Gyɔni kpamma maa tooni, ka boosi ba n‑yɛnni, ni, “Ya zaŋŋi la kpeeŋŋu bɔa, bee ŋɔni yuuri ka ya mara, n‑niŋi dinŋɔa?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Bɛni ka Naawunni Sii daa suŋŋi Piita ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Toondaandima wumma! Kpamma wumma!

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: Ya kur moaai la nini m‑boosiri ti zuna zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsunseaa din sa niŋi ka gbariga wa nya tippu:

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: tɔ! - ya baŋŋma, halli Izraɛli tiŋŋa niriba zaasa baŋŋma, ni, di nyɛ la Nazarɛti doo la, Yisa Masiiya, yuuri zugu n kyɛ ka gbariga wa zaani ya tooni zuna ka mari alaafɛ wa. Ŋɔn Yisa ka ya daa kyɛ ka ba gbaai u n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, amaa Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u daa yisigi u kum puuni.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: Di daa nyɛ la Yisa ka Naawunni Litaafi la daa yɛtti u yɛla, ni#:- ‘Tammeeriba, ya wumma! Yan daa za'asi kugseaa la - Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu.’

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Yisa kɔkɔ nin ti fa ya m‑basi, dama Naawunni daa bu kyɛ ka ninvugsɔ bɛ duniya wa ni ka nira yi ti booli u yuuri ka u fa u m‑basi, m‑paasi ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka di daa nyɛ la alahazibu n‑tiri ban daa la'am m‑bɛ bɛni maa, Piita ni Gyɔni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri ni sufukpeemni seem ka daa bu mari dabeem ba yɛttɔ'a maa puuni zugu. Ni, ba pa la karikyidima, amaa zugsabilli dima n kur nyɛ ba. Ka ba daa baŋŋi, ni, ba nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli Yisa.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zaa yɔri, ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑yɛli seelli, dama ba nini daa nya ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ tippu la daa bɛ ba saani n‑zɛya.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba yɛli ba, ni, ba yim duu maa ni n‑gura, ka daa naayi piili n‑tɔ'asi taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba yɛla,

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: ni, “Ti ya niŋi dɔppa wa la wula? Ninvugseeba zaa ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni mi halli, ni, ba tum tunkarigu. Ti ku toaai n‑yɛli, ni, di pa lala.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Tɔ - ya kyɛ ka ti kpaamni ba, ni, ba di laa n‑zaŋŋi Yisa yuuri n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti sɔsɔa. Lala nin kyɛ ka yɛttɔ'a wa ku laa m‑moonni n‑tiri niriba.”

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa laa m‑booli ba ni ba kpɛ na, ka yɛli ba, ni, sɔseaa laa n‑ka ni ka ba nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi Yisa yuuri n‑tɔ'asi niriba, bee, n‑wulisi niriba.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Amaa Piita ni Gyɔni daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya gaŋŋma ya mammansi din tuusi Naawunni saani: ti sakkma n‑ti ya noori la, bee, ti sakkma n‑ti Naawunni noori?

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Tirima muna - soori ka ni ka ti ku tɔ'asi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tin daa nya ka wum bunseesi la.”

english_verse: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
translation_verse: Dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa nya tippu n‑wulisi Naawunni gyirima ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa muna - ba daa dɔ'ai u ka di paai yuuma pisinaasi.

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka ban daa zaŋŋi Piita ni Gyɔni m‑basi la, ba daa labi ba taaba saani nti yɛli ba, ka daa kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpamma maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa zaa.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Ka ba zaasa ban la'amni m‑bɛ ni maa daa pɛ'ai Naawunni, ni, “Ti Daana Naawunni, nyini n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa naam saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu maa, ni teeku gba, ni seelikam zaa an bɛ a puuni.

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: I daa kyɛ ka i Vuusim Kasi suŋŋi i yiribii la, ŋɔn nyɛ ti yaaba Dauda, ka u daa yɛli#:- ‘Bɔzugu ka tinzundima nyuuri bara? Bɔzugu ka tinsi pam niriba teesiri teesiyɔya?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Duniya wa nadima la'am la taaba, - Zuguraandima la'asi m‑bɛ luginyinni, Ni ba ti tuusi ti Dugma ni u Masiiya maa tɔppu.’

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ka ba daa siri la'am taaba tiŋŋa wa puuni, ni ba ti tuusi Yisa: ŋɔn mun nyɛ la i bisuŋŋu sɔ nyin daa diisi Masiiya maa, ka Pɔntiyusi Pailiti, ni NaaHɛroodi - halli tinzundima ni Izraɛli tiŋŋa niriba zaa - daa la'asi na n‑tuusi u.

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: Ba daa tum seela zaa nyin, Naawunni, ŋɔn daa pun teesi n‑zaali seem maa.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ, ti Dugma, wumma ban yɛli ti seem, ni, ba nin namsi ti. Tim ti sufukpeemni ni ti ti tɔ'asi i yɛla,

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: ka kyɛ ka i nuu yaa bɛ ti saani, ni ti tibi baaridima ka tumni tunkara ni alahazibu tuma i maŋtoori bii Yisa yuuri ni.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Ka ban daa puusi lala n‑naai sahanseaa maa, luginseaa ban daa la'am m‑bɛ ni la daa miimni pam. Naawunni Sii Kasi daa kpɛ ba n‑ti ba yaa, ni ba ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛligu nimmoosi, ka bu mari dabeem.

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka ninvugseeba zaa ban daa sakki n‑ti Yisa la daa mari nangbanyinni ni sufu yinni. Ba sɔsɔ daa bu yɛli, ni, u marigu kam zaa nyɛ la ŋɔn kɔkɔ dinni: ba daa la'amni n‑tɔtti seelikam zaa n‑tiri taaba.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Yisa tuumudima la daa tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛla ni yaa, n‑yɛtti niriba, ni, ti Dugma Yisa yisigi kum ni ka ba mammansi nini nya. Ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba zaa mari sufumaasim pam,

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: ka fara daa laa n‑ka ba sɔa. Dama seeba zaa ban daa mari puri bee yiya la daa yi koosiri a ni m‑mari ligri maa na

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: nti ti tuumudima la, ka ba tɔri n‑ti sɔ zaa ŋɔn mari bukaata.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: ka ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a daa niŋi nangbanyinni ka u sɔ'ai ligri maa seesi n‑ti u maŋŋa, ka daa zaŋŋi an kpalim m‑mari na n‑dooli tuumudima maa tooni.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa yɛli, “Ananayasi! Wula ka Sintaani paali i sufu ni, halli ka i ma'ai ziri n‑ti Sii Kasi maa, ka zaŋŋi la'aseesi nyin daa deaai i tingbanni zugu maa n‑yiisi aseesi n‑sɔ'ai m‑mara?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Tingbanni maa daa pa la i dinni? Ka nyin daa koosi ka maa, i daa bu su ligri maa? Bɔ n kyɛ ka i niŋi lala? I bu ma'ai n‑ti la ninsaala, amaa Naawunni.”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Ananayasi ŋɔn daa wum nŋɔ maa, u daa lu tiŋŋa n‑kpi. Ka dabeem pam daa gbaai ban daa wum din daa niŋi maa zaa.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Ka na'akyimbisi maa daa doaai m‑pili u ka zaŋŋi u n‑zi n‑yi nti sɔ'ai.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di daa yuui beela ka u pɔ'a daa kpɛ na, ka daa bu mi din pun niŋi.

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Piita daa boosi u, ni, “Yɛlima! la'aseesi ban daa yɔ n‑ti nyin ni Ananayasi tingbanni maa zugu la zaa nŋɔa?” Ka u daa sakki, ni, “Ii, ka ligri maa nla.”
