english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa yɛli u, “Wula ka ya niŋi nangbanyinni ni ya zamsi ti Dugma Sii maa? Gɔsima! ninvugseeba ban da zaŋŋi i sira n‑sɔ'ai la nɔba na bɛ la za'anɔri ni wa, ka ba nin zi i gba n‑yi.”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ŋɔn gba daa lu tiŋŋa Piita tooni ka kpi. Ka na'akyimbisi maa daa labi n‑kpɛ na, n‑nya u ka u kpi, ka ba daa zaŋŋi u n‑zi n‑yi n‑sɔ'ai u sira maa lugri.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Dabeem pam daa gbaai Yisa nyaandɔliba ban daa la'amni taaba maa, ni ninvugseeba ban daa ti wum lahabaari maa zaa m‑paasi.

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka tuumudima maa daa tumni alahazibu tuma pam niriba maa saani n‑wulisiri u gyirima. Ba zaa daa kur la'amni la NaaSulɛmaana Leeŋŋa maa maasim ni.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Sɔsɔ daa ka sufukpeemni din nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka ba la'am n‑tabli ba saani, halli ni nyaandɔliba maa daa mari gyirima niriba maa saani pam.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Lala maa zugu ka niriba daa ziiri ba baaridima n‑kyɛnni sɔkpaŋŋa n‑doontiri gadu ni sɔna zugu, ni, Piita yi ti gatta, baseeba di yi ti kpɛ halli u sisii maasim ni gba.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Zama daa la'am tinkpansi maa ni, n‑kyɛnni Gyɛrusalɛm na n‑ziiri ba baaridima maa na, m‑paasi baseeba arizimbeeri ŋɔn daa daamni maa, ka ba zaa daa nya tippu.

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa ni u taaba ban daa dɔli Saddusidima soori la daa doaai n‑zabri ba nyuuri pam.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Ba daa gbaai tuumudima maa n‑kpeesi sarika duu ni.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Amaa yuŋŋu daa ti paai ka ti Dugma malaaka daa yoaai sarika maa dunɔya maa ka daa yiisi ba na,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: ni, “Ya kyɛma nti zaani Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa puuni n‑yɛli niriba maa lahabaari maa zaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi beesigu paalli wa ni.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Tɔ; beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ba daa kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka daa piili n‑wulisiri niriba hoo malaaka maa ŋɔn yɛli ba seem la. Ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ni u nyaaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti paai kootu duu ni ka daa booli kpambaya maa ni Izraɛli kpamma zaa ni ba la'am na, ba daa tum sarika maa ni m‑boori tuumudima maa.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Amaa Pirinsi maa ŋɔn daa paai sarika maa ni, ba daa bu nya ba bɛni, ka daa labi na n‑tisi lahabaari,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: ni, “Ti nya ka dunɔya maa kpari ni suŋŋa, ka ban guri maa zɛ za'anɔya maa ni, amaa tin yoaai ti bu nya sɔ duu maa puuni.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Ban daa wum lahabaari maa, di daa gyɛri Pirinsi toondaana ni gyeeŋŋu kpambaya maa pam, ka ba daa birisiri, ni, anŋɔ ya n‑naai la wula?

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka sɔ daa kpɛ na n‑yɛli, “Ya gɔsima! Yan sa zaŋŋi dɔseeba n‑niŋi dansarika duu ni la - ba zɛ la Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni n‑wulisiri niriba maa.”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Yiri Pirinsi toondaana maa daa doaai n‑zaŋŋi u nyaaŋŋa n‑kyaŋŋi nti zi tuumudima maa na; amaa ba daa bu zaŋŋi yaa n‑gbaai ba, dama ba daa zɔri la zama maa dabeem, ni, di pa seem ba nin lɔbi ba kuga.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Ban daa zi ba na sahanseaa la, ba daa kyɛ ka ba zaani la'aŋŋu maa puuni, ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa boosi ba, ni,

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Ti daa bu zaali ya zaalikpeemni, ni, yan di laa n‑wulisiri yuuri wa zugu?”, ni, “Amaa ya mun daa pun kyɛ ka yɛligu wa yɛligi m‑paali Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa zaa. Halli ya boori ni ya kyɛ ka doo wa kum taalli tabli ti gba.”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Ka Piita ni u taaba daa labsi n‑yɛli, “Ti nin sakki la Naawunni noori, ka pa ninsaala noori.

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Naawunni, sɔ ti badima ŋɔn daa gyeem maa: ŋɔna n daa neaai Yisa kum ni. Yarima muna daa pun zaŋŋi u n‑kpaasi n‑yuli doo zugu ka ku u.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn ka Naawunni mun daa duusi n‑ziili u nudirigu ya'ari ziziiya, hoo nabiiya ni tuligra. U nin ti kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima takki ba halibeoo n‑kyɛ ala'akyi, ka Naawunni naayi kyɛ ba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Tirima n nyɛ ban nya ka wum anŋɔ zaa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem la, ka ti yɛtti niriba lala; ka yɛtti zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni Vuusim Kasi yɛla, sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti seeba ban deaai u yɛligu maa.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Ban daa wum dinŋɔ maa, suuri daa ku ba, ka ba daa boori ni ba ku tuumudima maa.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Amaa Farisisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Gameeliyɛli daa doaai ni u tɔ'asi la'aŋŋu maa ni. U daa nyɛ zaaligu la karimbasɔ daadam maa zaa ŋɔn daa tisiri gyirima n‑gaari karimbadima zaa. U daa yɛli, ni, ba yiisim niriba maa beela,

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: ka u daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba n‑yɛli, ni, “Ya Izraɛli dima wa, ya teesim suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi yan ya n‑zaŋŋi dɔppa wa n‑niŋi seem maa.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Gyuudasi, Galilii nira la, gba daa yi na, niriba kaaligu la saha, ka daa zaŋŋi ninvugseeba ka ba daa bu sakki Gɔmnanti yɛligu. Ba daa ku u gba ka u nyaandɔliba zaa daa wurigi.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Dizugu, yɛttɔ'aseaa kan zɛya wa ni, man ya n‑kpaamni ya, ni, ya kyɛli dɔppa wa m‑basi, ka ba kyaŋŋi. Dama ba yɛligu, ni ba tuma maa, yi nyɛ la ninsaala dinni, a nin lu.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Amaa a yi yi la Naawunni saani na, ya ku toaai n‑goaai niriba wa. Di ya n‑kyɛ ka ya zabri ni Naawunni!” Ka u yɛttɔ'a wa daa kyɛ ka ba takki ba teesa.

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa booli tuumudima maa na n‑feebi ba, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, ba di laa n‑tɔ'asi Yisa yuuri yɛla, ka daa kyɛ ka ba bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba mun daa yi la'aŋŋu maa ni ni sufumaasim, dama ba daa sumsi ni ba diisi ba vi Yisa yuuri maa zugu.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Beoo kam ba daa bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ni niriba yiya puuni, ka daa bu kyɛ wulisigu; ba daa na kpalim m‑moonni la lahabasuŋŋu, ni, Yisa n nyɛ Masiiya - Masisɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tum na ka laa n‑diisi u naam.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Sahanseaa Yisa nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa zoaari m‑paasiri la, nangbankpeemni n daa lu ba puuni; di daa nyɛ la seeba ban tɔ'asiri Griiktiri la ni ban tɔ'asiri Hibruuli la sunsukkuni. Griiktiri dima maa daa galimni la Hibruuli dima maa, ni, “Ban yi tɔtti diibu n‑tisiri dabsa zaa la, ti pukɔya bu nyari ba tɔrli.”

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka tuumudima piiya ni ayi la daa booli nyaandɔliba zaa ni ba la'amma taaba, ka yɛli ba, ni, “Tirima kyɛli Naawunni yɛligu tuma ka gɔsiri diibu yɛla, bee? Ay! - lala ka soori!

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Dizugu, ti mabiisi, ya yiisim ya niriba ayɔpɔi, seeba ban bɛ ninvugsuma m‑mari Naawunni Sii Kasi yiiko, ni yɛm; ka ti ti kyɛ ka ba su diibu tuma maa.

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: Ka tirima mun zaŋŋi ti sufu zaa n‑niŋni Naawunni puusigu ni lahabasuŋŋu wulisigu ni.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Ka ba zaa daa sakki n‑ti yɛligu wa, ka daa yiisi Sitiivin ŋɔn daa mari yɛdda pam ni Vuusim Kasi yiiko la, ni Filipi ni Pirɔkyɔrusi, ni Nikanoori, ni Taimoon, ni Paamɛnaasi, ni Nikɔlaasi ŋɔn daa yi Antiyɔkku ni na la, ka daa pun yi Griika dima gyeeŋŋu soori la ni, ni u ti dɔli Yɛhudiya dima dinni.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Niriba wa ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisiri tuumudima maa, ka ba daa susi Naawunni n‑ti ba ka zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa ba zugri n‑diisi ba tuma maa.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa ka Naawunni wulisigu wumpu maa daa kur yɛligri ni, ka ban daa dɔli Yisa soori maa kaaligu daa nɔbi Gyɛrusalɛm puuni, halli ka Yɛhudiyadima maalimaanniba pam daa sakki n‑deaai Yisa yɛdda.

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Sitiivin muna - Naawunni daa zaŋŋi gyirima ni yiiko n‑ti u, ka u daa tum alahazibu tunkara ka Yɛhudiyadima niriba daa nya.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Amaa ninvugseeba daa doaai n‑ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni ni Sitiivin. Ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni ban daa boonni ‘Sumaŋŋa La'aŋŋu Duu’ la ni maa n daa nyɛ ba, ni Sairiini dima ni Alɛkzandiriya dima, ni seeba ban daa yi Silisii na, ni Ɛseaa.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa bu nyaŋŋi Sitiivin, dama Naawunni Siiya yɛm daa bɛ u yɛttɔ'a maa ni.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa bɔ dɔseeba ni ba yɛlima, ni, ba wumiya ka Sitiivin tɔ'asiri n‑galimni Musa yɛla, halli ni Naawunni yɛla gba.

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Ban daa tɔ'asi ba ziri yɛttɔ'a lala maa, ba daa kyɛ ka niriba zaa daa doaai n‑gbaai Sitiivin, ban ni ba kpamma ni ba karimbadima maa m‑paasi, m‑mari u n‑kyaŋŋi tiŋŋa maa kpambaya saani ni ba kari u sariya.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ba daa kyɛ ka ninvugseeba kpɛ na n‑tɔ'asi m‑ma'ai ziri, ni, “Doo wa kur tɔ'asiri ni n‑galimni Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri la, ni zaaligu maa.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Ti wumiya ka u yɛli Yisa ŋɔn daa nyɛ Nazarɛti nira la yɛla, ni, ŋɔna nin sa'am Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wa, ka takki ti kaya seesi Musa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi n‑ti ti la.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Ka seeba ban daa zi sariya karibu zii la ni zaa nini daa gɔsiri la Sitiivin la, ka ba daa ti nya u nindaa maa, ka ka ŋmanni la hoo malaaka dinni.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Ka di saha maa ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa daa boosi Sitiivin, ni, “Di zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, bee?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Ka Sitiivin daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, “M badima, ni m mabiisi, ya wumma! Ti yaaba Ibrahim ŋɔn daa bɛ Mɛsɔpɔteemiya tingbaŋŋu ni la, ka na bu yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Kyaraani tiŋŋa - di saha ka Naawunni, Gyirima Daana, daa kasigi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi u lala saha maa,

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: nti yɛli u, ni, ‘Kyɛl i tingbaŋŋu wa, ni i dɔ'am zaa, ka bakki n‑kyɛ tingbanseaa man ya wulisi i maa na.’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: Di saha u daa kyɛ u tiŋŋa maa, Kalidii dima tiŋŋa la, ka daa kyaŋŋi nti bɛ Kyaraani tiŋŋa. U ba ŋɔn daa ti kpi, Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u yi bɛni ka gaari m‑paai la tingbanseaa yan na bɛ ni la.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Ka daa bu ti u tingbanseaa zaa ni u bɛ ni n‑su ka, halli beela; amaa ka daa yɛli u, ni, u nin zaŋŋi tingbaŋŋu maa n‑ti u ni u nyaaŋŋa zaa, ka ba nin ti di ka faari n‑su, halli ni di saha maa ti yaaba Ibrahim daa ka biiya.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Naawunni daa yɛli kanŋɔ yɛttɔ'a, ni, u ya'ansi nin ti bɛ tinseelli ni, ka tiŋŋa maa niriba nin zaŋŋi ba n‑kyɛ ka ba leebi ba daaba n‑niŋi ba wahala yuuma kɔbsinaasi.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli Ibrahim, ni, ‘Amaa man nin ti kyɛ ninvugseeba ban su ba maa saani na ka darigi ba tuba pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka i niriba maa yi na m‑bɛ kpa n‑gyeemni ma.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni daa zaali dɔppa gunibu kaari, ni di ti wulisi ŋɔn ni Ibrahim ŋɔn loori la taaba nangbanyinni seem. Tɔ - ka dizugu ban daa dɔ'ai Ibrahim bii Yisahaaku sahaseaa la, ba daa guni u daba anii daari maa; Yisahaaku bii mun daa nyɛ Yaakubu, ka Yaakubu mun daa dɔ'ai u bidibsi pii ni ayi la, tin boonni seeba Izraɛli za'anɔya kpamma maa.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: Ka kpamma maa daa ti zabi nyuuri n‑niŋi ba tinzɔ Yisifu, n‑daa ti zaŋŋi u n‑koosi n‑ti Igyipti dima. Amaa Naawunni daa zaani u nyaaŋŋa Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa ni,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: n‑yiisi u wahala zaa puuni; ka laa n‑ti u bamsim, ka kyɛ ka u nya gyirima Igyipti naaba NaaFirawuna saani. Ka Naaba daa kyɛ ka u su ŋɔn NaaFirawuna yiri zaŋŋi n‑tabli Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa zaa.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: Di saha ka kɔm daa ti lu Igyipti ni Kyɛna'an tingbana zaa ni, ka daa ti niŋi ba wahala pam; ka ti yaadima maa daa boori bundirigu ka kɔŋŋi.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di saha maa ka ti yaaba Yaakubu daa ti wum, ni, kyi bɛ Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni; ka u daa daŋŋi n‑tum ti yaadima maa, ni ba kyaŋŋi Igyipti nti da a na.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa ti laa n‑doaai n‑kyaŋŋi buyi kyɛnni. Di saha maa ka Yisifu daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi u bɛya dima ka ba daa baŋŋi u, ka NaaFirawuna gba daa baŋŋi Yisifu bɛya maa ŋɔn nyɛ seeba.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Ka Yaakubu daa yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi nti zinni Igyipti, halli ka ŋɔn ni ti yaakpamma zaa daa ti ka ni.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa zi ba n‑labi Sɛkyeem tiŋŋa na, nti sɔ'ai ba ba gbala ziseaa ni, Sɛkyeem ziseaa ka ti yaaba Ibrahim ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi anzulifa n‑da Heemɔri biisi saani la.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun yɛli Ibrahim, ni, u nin suŋŋi u seem la, ka di saha daa naayi pɔtta; di saha maa ka ba zuliya maa daa nɔbi n‑zoaai pam Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa ni,

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: halli ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa zi Yisifu yɛla daa ti di Igyipti naam,

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: ka daa zaŋŋi u teesibeeri n‑namsiri ti yaadima ka mugsiri ba. U daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi ba bileesi m‑basira, ni ba ku laa n‑nya biisi ban vu yaasa.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba daa dɔ'ai Musa, ka u daa veeli pam ka Naawunni nini tigi u yɛla; ka u ba ni u ma daa mari u duu ni halli nti paai gɔya ata u ba yiri.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka sahanseaa daa ti paai ni ba zaŋŋi bii maa m‑basi, ka NaaFirawuna bipuŋŋa daa piisi u nti wubsi n‑zaŋŋi u, ni u leebi u mammaŋŋa biiya.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Ka Musa daa bamsi Igyipti dima bamsim zaa nti leebi yaa daana u yɛttɔ'a zugu ni u tuma zugu m‑paasi.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Ka u daa kyaŋŋi nti nya ka Igyipti dɔsɔ ŋɔn mugsiri ŋɔn Musa dɔ'ariba maa yinni ka di daa bu dɔli soori, ka u daa ŋmɛ Igyipti doo maa n‑ku u.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: U daa teesiya, ni, u mabiisi nin baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni n tum u na ni u ti fa ba m‑basi, amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi lala.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Ka dizugu beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai ka Musa laa n‑yi nti nya Izraɛli dima maa niriba ayi ŋɔn zabra. Ka u kyaŋŋi ni u ti goaai ba ka yɛli, ‘M mabiisi, ya baŋŋma, ni, ya nyɛ la mabiitiri yinni dima; ka wula ka ya ŋmaligi n‑zabra?’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn daa niŋni u kpeaa putoo maa daa gyɛ la suuri n‑niŋi Musa ka boosi u, ni, ‘Ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi nyini n‑leebi ti toondaana, bee, ti sarikatta?’

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: ni, ‘I boori la i ku ma, hoo nyin sa ku Igyipti doo la seem maa, bee?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Musa ŋɔn daa wum lala maa, ka u daa zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi Midiyan tingbaŋŋu ni. Bɛni ka u daa kpalim m‑bɛ ni, halli nti dɔ'ai u bidibsi ayi.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: Di yuuma pisinaasi nyaaŋŋa ka malaaka daa pusi n‑yi Musa zugu na, yoo kan yuuri boonni Sainayi la ni, n‑ŋmanni hoo bugum n‑diri tutuuri puuni.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Ka Musa ŋɔn daa nya lala ka di gyɛri u, ka u vuui m‑paai bɛni ni u ti gɔsi suŋŋa. Di saha ka u daa wum ti Dugma kukɔri, ka u tɔ'asiri n‑tiri u, ni,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Mani n nyɛ Naawunni ka i badima gyeemni ma. Halli Ibrahim Naawunni n nyɛ ma, ni Yisahaaku Naawunni, ni Yaakubu gba.’ Ka dizugu dabeem daa gbaai Musa ka u ningbiŋŋu daa gyigra; ka u daa bu laa n‑sakki n‑gɔsiri bɛni.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma daa laa n‑yɛli u, ni, ‘Pirigma i namna maa, dama nyin ŋɔn zɛ seela maa nyɛ la din bɛ kasi n‑ti ma!’

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Ni, ‘M pun nya n niriba ŋɔn diri wahala seelli Igyipti dima nuu ni, ka laa n‑wum ba fabla zaa; lala zugu ka n sigi na ni n ti fa ba m‑basi.’ Ka yɛli, ni, ‘Ka dizugu kyɛm na ka n taasi i Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni.’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna - ya bu nya? - ŋɔn Musa sɔ Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa za'asi n‑yɛli u, ni, ‘Ŋɔni n lee n‑zaŋŋi i n‑leebi toondaana bee, ti sarikatta?’, ŋɔna ka Naawunni daa tum na ni u ti leebi ba toondaana ni ŋɔn nin fa ba m‑basi. U daa niŋi lala, malaaka sɔ ŋɔn daa pusi n‑yi u zugu tutuuri maa puuni la suŋŋsim zugu.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Ka u siri daa fa ba n‑yiisi ba na, ka daa tumni alahazibu tuma ni tunkara pam Igyipti puuni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi TeekuZeoo ziiya, halli yoo ni gba, ban daa goori bɛni sahaseaa yuuma pisinaasi la.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ŋɔn Musa daa nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa yɛli Izraɛli dima, ni, ‘Naawunni nin yiisi sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ ya dɔ'ara, n‑kyɛ ka u niŋi anabi hoo mani ŋɔn bɛ seem la.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Ŋɔna n daa nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa zaani ti Izraɛli badima ni malaaka sunsukkuni. Dama ba daa la'am yoo ni, ka malaaka maa daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ŋɔna, Sainayi zoori zugu, ni u ti yɛli n‑ti ba. Bɛni maa ka u daa deaai Naawunni yɛligu din tiri nyɔvuri maa yɛla n‑wulisiri n‑tiri ti na.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: Amaa ti yaadima maa daa za'asi u noori ka waligi ba mansi ka kyɛ u, ka boori Igyipti lappu:

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba daa maali bunni hoo na'abila, ka kɔri bunkɔbri m‑maali ka, ka ba sufu pɛligi pam ban daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑maali bunseelli maa zugu.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Ka dizugu Naawunni daa yiisi u nuu ka kyɛ ba, ka ba daa susiri la saŋmariwuusi: lala zugu ka anabisɔ daa sɔbi n‑dooli Bukku maa puuni, ni#:- ‘Ya Izraɛli dima wa! Yan daa bɛ yoo ni maa - Halli nti paai yuuma pisinaasi la - Lala saha maa zaa, yan daa kɔrigri bunkɔbri maa Man Naawunni ka ya daa kɔrigri n‑ti ma, bee?

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Ay! Ya daa ziiri ya wunseesi duri - Wunseaa yan boonni “Mɔlɔkyi” la, Ni ya saŋmariwuseaa yan boonni “Rɛfan” la. Ya daa maali buna ni ya puusiri a ni: Dizugu man nin zaŋŋi ya nti basi, N‑taasi ya Babɛlɔn tiŋŋa tooni sa.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ti yaadima ŋɔn daa bɛ yoo ni la, ba daa mari la Naawunni tanti duu; kana ka ba daa maali, ka ka daa ŋmanni la hoo Naawunni ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi Musa n‑wulisi u seem maa.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ti yaadimseeba ban daa pa nyaaŋŋa la daa deaai duu maa m‑mari ka n‑zi na - ban ni Gyɔsuuwa daa niŋi lala. Di daa nyɛ la ban daa katti niriba burabura la saha, ninvugseeba Naawunni ŋɔn daa yiisi m‑basi ka kyɛ ka u niriba kpɛ na. Ka ba daa kpalim m‑bɛ lala, halli nti paai NaaDauda saha.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Dauda ka Naawunni nini daa tigi u yɛla, ka dizugu u daa susi soori Naawunni saani ni u ti bɔ gyeeŋŋu ziiya n‑ti u yaaba Yaakubu yiri dima.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Amaa u bii NaaSulɛmaana maa n daa ti mɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: Amaa dinŋɔ zaa nyɛ la yɔri, dama Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa bu kpeeri nuusi ŋɔn mɛ duseaa puuni m‑bɛ ni. Ka dizugu anabisɔ n daa yɛli, ni#:-

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: ‘Dinŋɔ ka ti Dugma yɛtta: “Arizana nyɛ la n naam ziziiya, Tingbaŋŋu maa mun nyɛ n gbaya ŋɔn tɔmni seela: Ka yiri bɔ ka ya lee n‑ya m‑mɛ n‑ti ma? Bee, luginseelli m bɛ duniya wa ni Ka n nin zinni n‑vuusira?

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Anabisɔ n daa bɛ ni ka ya yaadima daa bu namsi u, halli yinni gba, bee? Ba daa bu ku Naawunni tuumudima maa? - Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun tum tuumuseeba maa ni ba daŋŋi m‑moonni Yɛlimaŋni Daana ŋɔn ya n‑kyɛ na la yɛla maa. Ka pampaŋŋɔ yarima mun gbaai la Yɛlimaŋni Daana mammaŋŋa n‑yi u nyaaŋŋa ka ku u.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Ya daa deaai Naawunni zaaligu malaakadima saani, amaa ka lee m‑bu dɔli ka halli beela.”

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Sitiivin yɛligu wa ŋɔn daa lu ba tuba ni, ba sufu daa doaai pam, halli ka ba nyurimni ba kparima n‑niŋni u.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni Sisuŋŋu daa paali Sitiivin ni, ka u wukki u zugu n‑gɔsi saazugu n‑nya Naawunni gyirima veelim, ka nya ka Yisa zɛ Naawunni nudirigu ziiya.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Sitiivin daa yɛli, “Ya nyama! N nya ka arizana lakkri ni, ka Ninsaala Bii maa zɛ la Naawunni nudirigu ziiya!”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Ka ba zaa daa gbaai kaasigu n‑nyɛri ba tuba ka zɔ n‑la'am n‑gyiligi u na.

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: Ka daa gbaai u n‑yiisi ba tiŋŋa maa tɔriga ni nti lɔbi u kuga. Ka ban daa di sɛhara n‑galim u ka laa n‑lɔbi u maa daa yeaai ba bunyeera nti dooli na'akyimbilisɔ tooni, ka u yuuri boonni la Sɔla.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Ban daa lɔbri Sitiivin kuga sahanseaa maa, u daa susi la ti Dugma n‑tɔ'asiri, ni, “N Dugma Yisa, deaam n nyɔvuri!”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑kaasi n‑yɛli, “N Dugma, di zaŋŋi ba tumbeoo wa taalli n‑ziili ba!” Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala n‑naai, u daa ti leebi Naawunni dinni.

english_verse: Saul approved the stoning of Stephen. Some faithful followers of the Lord buried Stephen and mourned very much for him. Saul Makes Trouble for the Church At that time the church in Jerusalem suffered terribly. All of the Lord's followers, except the apostles, were scattered everywhere in Judea and Samaria.
translation_verse: Ka Sɔla gba daa bɛ ni n‑sakkri ban daa ku Sitiivin yɛla maa. Ka Sitiivin ŋɔn daa kpi la, ninvugsunseeba daa kpikki u n‑kyaŋŋi nti sɔ'ai, ka daa fabli pam u kum zugu. Tɔ: ka di saha maa ba daa piili n‑namsiri Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban daa la'amni Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni maa. Dizugu ba daa zɔ n‑yirigri zaa, n‑kyaŋŋi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu; kulla Yisa tuumudima kɔkɔ n daa kpalim timpuuni.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Sɔla mun daa kpalim n‑dɔla Yisa niriba maa ni nimmoaa, ni u ti sa'am ba yɛla. U daa dɔli la yiri yiri n‑gbaasiri ba, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa, baseeba ban dɔli Yisa soori la, m‑mari ba n‑kyɛnni n‑kpatti dansarika ni.

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka baseeba ban daa zɔ n‑yirigi lala maa daa dɔli la tinsi tinsi n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu n‑tiri niriba.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Filipi mun daa kyaŋŋi Samɛrii timpuuni. Ŋɔn daa ti paai la, u daa gbaai mooligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Masiiya maa yɛla.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Di saha ka daadam pam daa la'am na, ka daa wumni Filipi yɛttɔ'a la ni nimbiri moaa, ka nyari tunkariseesi ŋɔn daa tumna. Ba daa yoaai ba tuba n‑wumsi ŋɔn yɛli seem zaa.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Dama arizimbeeri daa kaasi ka yi ninvugseeba ban mari ba ni, ka wɔbsi ni gbarisi gba daa nya tippu;

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: ka di daa mari sufupeelim n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban daa bɛ Samɛrii timpuuni maa.

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ tiŋŋa wa ni ka u yuuri boonni Saimoon. U daa niŋni la tiim tuma, ka di daa mari alahazibu n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban bɛ tiŋŋa maa ni la. U mun daa laa n‑duusi u maŋŋa, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugtitaari.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Nira kam, bibila halli ni ninvugkurigu gba, daa maai ba mansi n‑wum Saimoon yɛttɔ'a maa, ka yɛtti, ni, “Naawunni kpeeŋŋu daansɔ ban boonni, ni, ‘Buntitaari’ la n kur nyɛ u.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Din daa kyɛ ka niriba maa daa boori u yɛla maa daa nyɛ la: u daa pun tum u tiima tuma ba saani ka di daa yuui pam.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Amaa niriba ŋɔn daa ti sakki n‑ti Filipi yɛttɔ'a maa, ni lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam soori la, ni Yisa Masiiya yuuri yɛla, ba zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu - dɔppa ni pɔ'aba zaa.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Saimoon mammaŋŋa gba daa sakki lala, ka ba daa su u gba koom. U daa zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa n‑tabli Filipi ni nimbirimoaa, ka daa nya Filipi ŋɔn daa niŋni tunkara ni alahazibu tunseesi, ka di daa gyɛri u pam.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Tɔ; di nyaaŋŋa Gyɛrusalɛm ni tuumudima maa ban daa ti wum, ni, Samɛrii dima sakki n‑ti Naawunni yɛlisuma la, ba daa tum Piita ni Gyɔni ba saani na.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Piita ni Gyɔni ŋɔn daa paai na la, ba daa susi Naawunni n‑tisi seeba ban pun sakki n‑ti Yisa, ni, Naawunni Vuusim Kasi nin sigi ba saani na.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Dama ba daa na kur deaai la koom suubu ti Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu, ka Naawunni Sii Kasi la na bu bɛ ba saani.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Piita ni Gyɔni daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa niriba maa zugri ka Naawunni Sisuŋŋu daa kyɛ ba saani na.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Ka Saimoon daa nya Naawunni tuumudima maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa niriba zugri ka Sii maa kyɛ ba saani na seem maa, ka u daa zaŋŋi ligri ni u ti ti Piita ni Gyɔni,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: ka yɛli ba, ni, “Ya tim man gba yaa kanŋɔ taabu, ka ninvugsɔ zaa man ti zaŋŋi n nuu m‑pa u ni, Naawunni Vuusim Suŋŋu maa nin kyɛ di daama saani na.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “I nin kpi n‑sa'am m‑basi, nyini ni i ligri maa zaa! - nyin teesi, ni, i nin toaai n‑zaŋŋi ligri n‑da Naawunni piini maa zugu.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: I ka yɛttɔ'aseaa dinŋɔ puuni; i nuu ka ti faari diibu yɛla ni, dama i sufu bu zɛ yimm suŋŋa Naawunni saani.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Dama n nya ka i zɛ la tumbeeri toom ziiya, ka ba pun zaŋŋi ala'akyi kutti m‑ba i!”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Saimoon daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ya bɛlimma ti Dugma Naawunni n‑ti ma, ni, nyin yɛli seem la di paai ma na!”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Ban daa yɛli Saimoon dinŋɔ maa, ba daa yi n‑tɔ'asi Yisa yɛla n‑tisi niriba maa ka wulisiri ba, ka daa labi n‑kuli Gyɛrusalɛm. Ban daa bɛ soori zugu n‑kunni maa, ba daa tɔ'asi Yisa lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑tisi Samɛrii dima tinkpansi pam puuni.

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Ka Filipi daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi. Di saha maa ka Itiɔpiiya dɔsɔ daa dɔli soori maa zugu n‑kunna. Itiɔpiiya tingbaŋŋu Naaba kpeema, namɔ'aliga, n daa nyɛ u - pɔ'aba naam n‑daa la, ka ba boonni di, ni, ‘Kandaasi’. Ŋɔn n daa su Pɔ'a-naaba maa tuma n‑gɔsiri u neema ni u ligri yɛla zaa n‑ti u. U daa pun kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm nti puusi Naawunni ka daa naai,

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: ka kpɛ u tarɔkku ni n‑zinni, ka wuri datti u n‑kyɛnna. Ŋɔn daa bɛ soori n‑kunni maa, u daa karimni la anabi Aizaaya bukku la puuni.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni Siiya daa yɛli Filipi, ni, “Kyɛm nti paai tarɔkku wa!”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Ka Filipi daa zɔ m‑paai bɛni ka wum, ka doo maa karimni la Aizaaya bukku la puuni; ka u boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i baŋŋi nyin karimni seelli wa gbinni?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Ka doo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Sɔ yi bu wulisi ma, man mammaŋŋa ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai m‑baŋŋi di gbinni?”, ka daa naayi yɛli Filipi, ni, u duum na n‑zinni u tarɔkku maa puuni.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Aizaaya gbaŋŋu maa puuni luginseaa ka u daa karimni maa n‑daa nŋɔa#:- “I yi mari peoo n‑wa ni i ti ku u, U yi bɛ la baalimm, ka bu yigra; Bee pɛbila ŋɔn ku kaasi U kɔrigri ziiya - Lala ka di daama gba daa bu yɛli seelli.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Ba daa zeem u ka di u sariya ba yuubu: Sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑yɛli u nyaaŋŋa dima yɛla, Dama u laa n‑ka duniya wa ni.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Itiɔpii doo maa daa yi boosi Filipi, ni, “Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna yɛlim ma: ŋɔni yɛla ka anabi la tɔ'asiri wa? U daa tɔ'asiri u mammaŋŋa, bee, sɔ mun sa?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka Filipi daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a maa ka piili luginseaa doo maa ŋɔn daa karimni ni la, n‑wulisi u lahabasuŋŋu maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa yɛla.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Ban daa bɛ soori zugu n‑kyɛnni la, ba daa ti paai la bɔ'aseaa ka koom daa bɛ ka ni. Ka Itiɔpii doo maa yɛli Filipi, ni, “I bu nya koom wa? Seelli bɛ ni din nin ta'ari ma, ni, n ku toaai n‑deaai Naawunni koom suubu, bee?”

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Ka yɛli sɔ ŋɔn duusiri tarɔkku maa, ni, u zaanma, ka ba daa zaani. Ka ŋɔn ni Filipi daa sigi na n‑kyaŋŋi bɔkku maa puuni, ka Filipi daa naayi su u Naawunni koom maa.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa ti yi koom maa puuni na, ti Dugma Vuusim daa fa Filipi n‑kyaŋŋi, ka Itiɔpii doo maa daa bu laa n‑nya u yaasa, amaa u daa dɔli u soori n‑kyɛnni ni sufupeelim.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka Filipi mun daa ti bɛ Azootisi tiŋŋa, ka u daa dɔli la bɛni tinsi maa m‑moonni Yisa lahabasuŋŋu n‑tiri ba, halli nti paai Sizariiya timpuuni.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Sɔla mun daa kpaŋŋi u maŋŋa ni nimmoaa n‑wulisiri, ni, yɛlimaŋni Yisa siri nyɛ la Masisɔ Naawunni ŋɔn tum na la. Dizugu Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa bɛ Damasikusi daa kɔŋŋi yɛlli, ni ba lusi u u yɛttɔ'a maa ni.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Doaam saazugu yooma n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Damasikusi. Ba nin ti yɛli i nyin ya n‑niŋi seem.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: N nin wulisi u ŋɔn nin di wahala seem n yuuri zugu.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna Sɔla daa labi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm. Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai bɛni, u daa boori ni u paasi la Yisa nyaandɔliba la saani; amaa dabeem daa mari ba. Ba daa bu basi yɛm, ni, u dɔli la Yisa ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: U pun nya zaasimni, ni, dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Ananayasi kpɛ u saani na nti siisi u, ni u nini maa ti neaai yaasa.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Dinsaha maa ka bunseesi an daa ŋmanni hoo pɛ'ari n yi Sɔla nini ni n‑lu tiŋŋa, ka Sɔla daa laa n‑neaai. Di saha ka Ananayasi daa su Sɔla Naawunni koom.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Sɔla daa boori la nimmoaa ŋɔn nin niŋi seem n‑ku Yisa nyaandɔliba. U daa kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la saani

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: nti susi gbanseesi ka ba sɔbi n‑ti la'aŋŋu duri an bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa niriba la, ni, u yi ti nya ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa soori bɛni, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba, ni, u mari la soori ni u gbaasi ba n‑lɔ m‑mari ba n‑labi Gyɛrusalɛm na.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ŋɔn daa ti wa u Damasikusi maa ka daa ti pɔtti bɛni, ka neesim nyakki n‑yi saazugu na nti gyiligi u.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: U daa lu la tiŋŋa, ka wum ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri n‑tiri u, ni, “Sɔla, Sɔla, bɔ zugu ka i namsiri ma?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Ka Sɔla daa boosi n‑yɛli, “Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?”, ka u labsi n‑yɛli. “Mani Yisa: mani ka i namsiri maa.”

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli Sɔla maa daa wum kukɔri maa, amaa ba daa bu nya sɔa; ka ba daa kɔŋŋi yɛligu di zugu.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Sɔla mun daa doaai saazugu n‑lakki u nini, amaa u daa zoomiya ka ban daa dɔli u maa daa dari u n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Damasikusi.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Sɔla daa zoomiya daba ata ka koom bee diibu daa bu gaari u noori ni.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka nyaandɔlisɔ n daa bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa, ka u yuuri boonni Ananayasi. U daa zaasi zaasimni, ni, ti Dugma tɔ'asi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ananayasi!”, ka u daa sakki, “Naa! Mani nŋɔa, n Dugma!”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma daa yɛli u, ni, “Kyɛm sɔseaa kan boonni ‘Sɔtuusiri’ la ni nti kpɛ Gyuudasi yiri m‑boori Taasusi dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Sɔla la ziiya. Pampaŋŋɔ u na puusiri ni.

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ananayasi ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, u daa yɛliya, “Mma, n Dugma! Niriba pam pun ti ma doo wa lahabaari, ni, ŋɔn daa niŋi i niriba putoo Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni,

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: halli ni u deaai soori gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa saani, ni, u mari la soori ni u kyɛ na n‑gbaasi ninvugseeba ban gyeemni i la.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Ka ti Dugma daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ni dinŋɔ zaa, kur kyɛma! Dama doo maa nyɛ la n tuntunsɔ ŋɔn ya mari n yuuri nti ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima, ni ba nadima, ni Yɛhudiyadima maa m‑paasi.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Ananayasi daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi. U daa kpɛ yiri maa ni n‑zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa Sɔla zugu ka yɛli, “M mabii Sɔla, ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi n‑ti i soori maa zugu la, ŋɔna n tum ma i saani na, ni n kyɛ na nti neaai i nini la ka kyɛ ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi bɛ i saani ni kpeeŋŋu.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: U daa di diibu ka u yaa daa labi u na. Tɔ: Sɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban daa bɛ Damasikusi timpuuni saani n‑yuui beela.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: U daa piili n‑yiisiri wa'azu tiŋŋa maa la'aŋŋu duri ni, ni, Yisa nyɛ la Naawunni biiya.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Di daa gyɛri ban wumni maa zaa, ka ba daa tɔ'asiri taaba, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni, pa dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka ba gbaasiri lala yuuri nyaandɔliba maa la nŋɔa? Ka u daa niŋi lala Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ kpa na ni u ti gbaasi ba n‑lɔ n‑taasi bɛni n‑niŋi gyeeŋŋu kpamma maa nuu ni. Pa ŋɔna nŋɔa?”

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Amaa lala lahabaari daa paai Sɔla na. Ba daa yɛli, ni, ba nin guri tiŋŋa maa za'anɔya wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu saha zaa, ni ba gbaai u n‑ku u.

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Baanabasi daa zaŋŋi Sɔla n‑kyaŋŋi nti puusi Yisa tuumudima maa. U daa wulisi ba din daa niŋi Sɔla soori maa ni seem maa, ni, ti Dugma daa kyɛ ka Sɔla nya u ka daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u. Halli ni Sɔla ŋɔn daa bɛ Damasikusi timpuuni seem n‑tɔ'asiri paalu ni, ni sufukpeemni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa yɛla ka u daa wulisi ba gba.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Sɔla daa paasi ba zugu ka ba gyinni Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ka u daa wulisi niriba zaa ni sufukpeemni, Yisa yuuri zugu.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn ni Griika dima ŋɔn daa ti di azama halli nti ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni la, ba daa boori ni ba gbaai u n‑ku u.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Ka u Yisa nyaandɔlitaaba ban daa ti baŋŋi lala maa, ba daa mari u n‑kyaŋŋi Sizarii tiŋŋa n‑zaali u soori, ni u kuli Taasusi tiŋŋa.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Tɔ; di saha ka Yisa niriba daa laa n‑ka daaŋŋu Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu zaa ni, halli Samɛrii tingbaŋŋu ni Galilii gba ba daa bu nya yɛttɔ'aseaa. Ba daa nɔbri ni, n‑tumni ti Dugma ŋɔn boori seem, ka bɛ ni Naawunni Sii Kasi sufukpeemni zugu.

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Piita daa gyinni n‑kaari la tinsi halli nti paai Naawunni niriba ban bɛ Lidda tiŋŋa ni.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: U daa ti nya dɔsɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Iniyasi; gbariga n daa nyɛ u ka kur kpalim n‑dɔya, ka di paai yuuma anii.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Piita daa yɛli u, “Iniyasi, Yisa Masiiya n tibi i, doaama n‑kpabi i bundoaa wa!” Tɔ, ka Iniyasi di va n‑doaai.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Ka niriba ban daa bɛ Lidda tiŋŋa ni, ni Seaarɔn tingbaŋŋu dima maa zaa daa nya u n‑niŋi yɛdda n‑ŋmaligi n‑dɔli ti Dugma.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka pɔ'asɔ n daa bɛ Gyɔppa tiŋŋa, Yisa nyaandɔliba sunsukkuni, ka u yuuri boonni Tabita, bee Dookasi . U pupeelim ni u tunsuma daa ka tɔriga.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Amaa saha daa ti kyɛ na ka u beeri halli nti kpi. Ba daa peaai u kɔbeoo, ka mari u n‑du n‑kpɛ gyirambisa duseaa n‑dooli.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ba tiŋŋa Gyɔppa ni Lidda tiŋŋa daa bu wɔ'a, ka dizugu nyaandɔliba maa ŋɔn daa pun wum, ni, Piita bɛ Lidda, ba daa tum niriba ayi bɛni ni ba ti booli u nimbirimoaa na. Ni, ba susim u, ni, “Kur kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ yooma na!”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Piita ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa doaai n‑dɔli ba yooma. Ŋɔn daa ti paai na, ba daa mari u n‑du saazugu n‑kpɛ duu maa ni, ka pukɔya maa zaa daa gyiligi u na, n‑kumna; ka ba daa wulisiri u neenseesi Dookasi ŋɔn daa sɛ n‑ti ba, u nyɔvuri saha la.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Piita daa kyɛ ka ba zaa yi duu maa ni. U daa gbaani tiŋŋa ka susi Naawunni pɔi, ka naayi ŋmaligi n‑kpa kum maa ka yɛli, “Tabita, doaam n‑zaani!” Ka u daa neaai u nini n‑gɔsi Piita ka doaai n‑zinni.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Piita daa suŋŋi u ka u doaai n‑zaani saazugu, ka daa booli Yisa nyaandɔliba ni pukɔya maa ni ba kpeem na, nti wulisi ba, ni, pɔ'a maa nya nyɔvuri.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Gyɔppa dima maa zaa daa wum lahabaari maa, ka ba pam daa niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Piita mun daa kpalim Gyɔppa ka di yuui beela. U daa gbeeri la Saimoon, gbanzaba sɔ, yiri ni.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Dɔsɔ n daa bɛ Sizarii tiŋŋa ka u yuuri boonni Kɔniiliyusi. U daa nyɛ la toondaana sɔ ŋɔn daa su soogya kɔbga fukumsi, ka u soogyadima daa nyɛ ban boonni ‘Italii Dima’ maa.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa dɔla Naawunni ni yɛlimaŋni. U daa suŋŋi Yɛhudiya faradima maa ka susiri Naawunni saha kam.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi dahinyinni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi hoo wuntaŋŋa kurigu ata saha, ka doo wa daa nya zii. U daa kur nya Naawunni malaaka ka u kpɛ u saani na nti yɛli u, ni, “Kɔniiliyusi!”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Ka Kɔniiliyusi daa gɔsi u suŋŋa ka dabeem daa gbaai u; ka u daa sakki, ni, “Naa, n dugma! Bɔ n nyɛ di?” Malaaka maa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “I wunsusigu yɛla ni i tunsuma n‑tiri faradima zaa naayi paai la Naawunni teaari, ka u sufu maai i zugu.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu pampaŋŋɔ tumma niriba ni ba kyaŋŋi Gyɔppa m‑bɔ Saimoon ziiya - ban pa sɔ yupaa, ni, ‘Piita’ la -

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: ka ba mari u na. U gbeeri la Saimoon sɔ, gbanzaba, saani; u yiri maa zɛ la teeku gbiriŋŋu ziiya.”

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka malaaka maa ŋɔn ti u lahabaari wa n‑naai, u daa bakki ka kyɛ u. Ka Kɔniiliyusi daa booli u yiri niriba ayi ni u soogyadima yinni ŋɔn gba daa dɔli Naawunni soori ni nimmoaa ka bɛ u yiri la ni.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: U daa yɛli ba seelli kam din niŋi maa, ka daa tum ba Gyɔppa.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Tɔ; beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai ka ban daa ti kyɛnni m‑pɔtti Gyɔppa na, Piita daa du yiri la pii maa zugu, agbaa saha, ni u ti puusi Naawunni.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Di saha ka kɔm daa ti gbaai u, ka u boori seelli ni u di; ka ban daa maanni diibu sahanseaa maa, u daa nya zii.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: U daa nya ka sangbana yoaai ka bunseelli daa sigri na, ka ka ŋmanni hoo tanti gbantitaari ban lɔ ka tuba anaasi n‑sigsiri ka tiŋŋa na.

english_verse: In it were all kinds of animals, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: N‑nya, ka bunkɔbri daa bɛ ka puuni - bunkyɛnna ni bunvuura, halli niinsi gba.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ka u wum kukɔri, ni, “Piita, doaam n‑kɔrigi n‑ŋɔbi!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Ay, n Dugma! N na zi n‑di bunseelli kam kan kyisiri, bee, kan mari da'ari.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka kukɔri maa maali n‑yɛli u, ni, “Naawunni ŋɔn tisi bunseelli, ni, ‘bunsuŋŋu’, nyin di kyisiri ka, ni, ‘da'ari’!”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di daa niŋi lala napɔmbuta, ka bunni maa naayi labi n‑du saazugu.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Piita ŋɔn daa kur teesiri yɛlli wa zugu ka di gyɛri u pam, lala saha ka dɔseeba Kɔniiliyusi ŋɔn sa tum la gba daa pun baŋŋi Saimoon yiri maa ziiya m‑paai na n‑zɛ samanni.

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Piita daa na kur teesiri ŋɔn nya seesi maa yɛla, ka Naawunni Sii maa ti yɛli u, “Dɔppa ata m boori i ziiya;

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa sigi n‑yɛli niriba maa, ni, “Mani ka ya boori maa. Dinŋɔna, bɔ zugu ka ya kyɛ kpa na?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Naawunni malaaka ŋɔn sa wulisi Kɔniiliyusi seem la zugu. Ŋɔn Kɔniiliyusi mun nyɛ la soogya toondaana, ka u dɔli la soori ka gyeemni Naawunni, ka Yɛhudiyadima zaa tiri u gyirima. Malaaka maa sa yɛli u, ni, u kyɛ ka nyin kyɛ u yiri maa na ka u ti wum yɛttɔ'aseesi nyin mari n‑yɛli u maa.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa zi ba n‑kpɛ ni ba ti gbɛ u saani. Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai u daa niŋi siri ka ŋɔn ni ban n kyaŋŋi. Ka Gyɔppa ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa gba daa dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai yaasa, ba daa paai Sizarii tiŋŋa. Kɔniiliyusi daa pun booli u dɔ'am ni u zɔdima zaa, ni, ba la'amma na, ka daa guusiri ba soori maa zugu.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Piita ŋɔn daa ya kpɛ yiri maa ni, Kɔniiliyusi daa paai u bɛni n‑gbaani u tooni.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa yɛli u, “Doaama! Man gba nyɛ la ninsaala!”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Ka ba daa gbaai sɔsiga ni taaba ka kpeera, halli nti paai daadam zaa la'aŋŋu.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Ka Piita daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya mi halli, ni, soori ka ni n‑ti Yɛhudiya ninvugsɔ ni u ti la'am ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn pa Yɛhudiya nira, bee, n‑kpɛ u yiri ni. Amaa Naawunni wulisi ma, ni, n di kyisi ninsaalisɔ yɛla, ni, ‘da'ari daana n nyɛ u’.

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Ka Kɔniiliyusi daa labsi n‑yɛli, “Di dabsa anaasi nŋɔ ka n daa bɛ n yiri wa ni, saha hoo kanŋɔa, wuntaŋŋa kurigu ata saha, n‑susiri Naawunni. Ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi suturinya'ara daa pusi ya'abi n tooni na,

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: ka yɛli, ‘Kɔniiliyusi, i wunsusigu yɛla ni i tunsuma n‑tiri faradima zaa naayi paai Naawunni teaari ka u sufu maai i zugu.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Ka dizugu pampaŋŋɔ tumma niriba ni ba kyaŋŋi Gyɔppa m‑bɔ Saimoon ziiya - ban pa sɔ yupaa, ni, ‘Piita’ la - ka ba mari u na. U gbeeri la Saimoon sɔ, gbanzaba, saani: u yiri maa zɛ la teeku gbiriŋŋu ziiya.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Dizugu ka n daa tum yooma m‑booli i maa, ka i gba niŋi suŋŋa n‑kyɛ na wa. Pampaŋŋɔ ti zaa bɛ la Naawunni tooni ni ti ti wum Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti i ni i ti yɛli ti.”

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Piita daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli, “Pampaŋŋɔ n yi baŋŋiya, ni, di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni, Naawunni bu gamsira,

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: amaa u deaari butiri kam niriba ban gyeemni u ka tumni din tuusi seem la.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Yɛttɔ'a maa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti Izraɛli dima, ni, ba nin nya sufudoaa Yisa Masiiya zugu; ni, ŋɔn Yisa siri nyɛ la sɔkam zaa Dugma maa.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Ya zaa mi halli din daa piili n‑niŋi Galilii tingbaŋŋu maa ni, sahanseaa Gyɔni ŋɔn daa mooli Naawunni koom suubu yɛla, halli nti paai Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu zaa.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Yisa, Nazarɛti doo maa yɛla nla: Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka u Vuusim Kasi ni u yiiko bɛ u ni seem la, ka lala ka Naawunni daa wulisi, ni, u diisi u naam. Ni, u daa gyili n‑tumni tunsuma ka tibri Sintaani ŋɔn daa daamni seeba zaa, Naawunni ŋɔn daa bɛ u saani zugu.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka dinŋɔna tirima n nyɛ u sɛhara dima; ti yɛtti, ni, ti siri daa bɛ ni n‑nya ŋɔn Yisa ŋɔn daa niŋi seem Yɛhudiyadima tingbana zaa ni, halli Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni gba. Ni, ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli doo zugu n‑ku u;

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: amaa Naawunni daa doaai u kum ni di daba ata daari, ka kyɛ ka niriba nya u.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Pa sɔkam n daa nya u, amaa tirima - Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun yiisi seeba ni ti niŋi u sɛhara dima maa - tin daa nya u. Tin ni ŋɔna n daa la'am n‑di ka nyu, u kum yisigri nyaaŋŋa maa.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: U daa kpamsi ti, ni, ti wulisim niriba ka laa n‑di sɛhara, ni, ŋɔna ka Naawunni yiisi ni u ti niŋi sɔ ŋɔn nin kari niriba kam zaa sariya - ban na nɛa, ni ban pun kpi zaa m‑paasi.

english_verse: Every one of the prophets has said that all who have faith in Jesus will have their sins forgiven in his name.
translation_verse: Halli ŋɔn Yisa yɛla ka anabidima zaa daa tɔ'asiri n‑diri sɛhara, ni, ŋɔn yuuri zugu ka nira kam zaa ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda nin nya u tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Ka Piita ŋɔn daa na kur tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a wa, Naawunni Sii Kasi maa daa kpɛ ban wumni u yɛla maa zaa sufu ni.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ka Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban dɔli Piita na daa nya lala: Yɛhudiya niriba n daa nyɛ ba, ka di daa gyɛri ba pam, dama halli ban pa Yɛhudiyadima gba ka Naawunni tisi u piini, ni, u Sii Kasi maa kpaai m‑paali ba.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Dama ba daa wum ka ba tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'azuna balibubalibu m‑pɛ'ari Naawunni. Di saha ka Piita daa boosi n‑yɛli,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: ni, “Sɔ nin toaai n‑ta'ai niriba wa ni ba di su Naawunni koom? Ba deaai la Naawunni Vuusim Kasi maa hoo tin gba ŋɔn daa deaai u seem la.”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Dizugu u daa ti noori ni ba suum ba Naawunni koom Yisa yuuri ni. Ka di nyaaŋŋa ba daa susi u, ni, u kpalimma m‑bɛ ba saani n‑niŋi dabsa ayi.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Ka Piita ŋɔn daa kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm sahanseaa la, baseeba ban daa dɔli Yɛhudiyadima kaari ni nimmoaa la daa ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni n‑galimni u,

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: ka yɛtti, ni, “I kpɛ la yɔkɔla dima yiya ka nyin ni ban n di.”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Dizugu Piita daa piili yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba din daa niŋi seem zaa deede,

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: ni, “Man daa bɛ Gyɔppa timpuuni, ka daa puusiri Naawunni; ka di daa niŋi ma hoo goom ka n gbisiri n‑zaasira, ka n daa nya zii, ni, n daa nya ka bunseelli daa sigri na, ka ka ŋmanni hoo tanti gbantitaari ban lɔ ka tuba anaasi n‑sigsiri ka halli nti paai man saani na.

english_verse: When I looked in it, I saw animals, wild beasts, reptiles, and birds.
translation_verse: Ka n daa gɔsi suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi din bɛ ka ni, n‑nya ka bunkɔbri daa bɛ ka puuni - bunkyɛnna ni bunvuura, halli ni niinsi gba.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum kukɔri, ni, ‘Piita, doaama n‑kɔrigi n‑ŋɔbi!’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, ‘Ay, n Dugma! N na zi n‑di bunseelikam kan kyisira, bee, m‑mari da'ari.’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ka kukɔri maa maali n‑yɛli ma, ni, ‘Naawunni ŋɔn tisi bunseelli, ni, “bunsuŋŋu”, nyin di kyisiri ka, ni, “da'ari”!’

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di daa niŋi lala napɔmbuta, ka bunni maa naayi labi n‑du saazugu.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka niriba ata daa paai na, n‑zaani yiri seaa tin daa bɛ ni maa tooni, ni, ba tum ba la Sizariiya, ni ba kyɛ man saani na.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Naawunni Sii Kasi daa yɛli ma, ni, n di guusi ka dɔli ba n‑kyaŋŋi. M mabiisi bayoobu wa gba daa dɔli ma n‑kyaŋŋi, halli nti paai n‑kpɛ doo maa yiri.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: U daa yɛli ti ŋɔn daa bɛ u yiri maa puuni ka daa nya malaaka seem, ka u yi na nti yɛli u, ni, ‘Tumma Gyɔppa m‑booli Saimoon na - ban pa sɔ yupaa, ni, “Piita” la.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: U nin tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑ti ya, an nin kyɛ ka nyin ni i yiri zaa nya tuliginsim.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: Tɔ; man daa piili n‑tɔ'asi sahanseaa maa, Naawunni Vuusim Kasi daa kyɛ ba zugu na hoo ŋɔn daa pun kyɛ ti zugu na piiligu ni seem la.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa teaai ti Dugma ŋɔn daa yɛli seem, ni, ‘Gyɔni daa zaŋŋi koom n‑su ya, amaa yarim mun nin deaai Vuusim Kasi suubu.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Dizugu Naawunni daa pun ti ba lala piini hoo piiniseaa ŋɔn daa ti ti, sahanseaa tin daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda la. Ka mani n nyɛ ŋɔni ka nin ta'ai Naawunni ŋɔn tum seem?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Ban daa ti wum dinŋɔ maa, ba daa bu laa n‑ga'ai, ka daa gbaai Naawunni pɛ'abu n‑yɛtti, ni, “Ban pa Yɛhudiyadima gba ka Naawunni ti soori ni ba takki ba teesibeeri n‑niŋi tuuba ni ba ti nya nyɔvuri!”

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Sitiivin kuubu nyaaŋŋa ka Yisa nyaandɔliba daa nya namsigu pam Yɛhudiyadima tinsi la ni, ka ba pam daa wurim n‑kyaŋŋi tinseesi, halli ka baseeba daa paai Fɔnisii tingbaŋŋu ni, ni Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu ni, ni Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa. Ba daa bu tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛligu n‑ti sɔ na'ala Yɛhudiyadima kɔkɔa.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Amaa baseeba daa bɛ ni, ban daa yi la Saapirusi ni Sairiini tingbana la ni na, ka ba daa kyaŋŋi Antiyɔkku ka daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri Griikadima maa gba, n‑yɛtti ti Dugma Yisa lahabasuŋŋu yɛla maa.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni nuu daa bɛ ba saani, ka niriba pam daa niŋi yɛdda ka takki ba beebu n‑deaai ti Dugma maa.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ lahabaari maa daa ti paai Yisa niriba la'aŋŋu dima Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ka ba daa tum Baanabasi ni u kyaŋŋi Antiyɔkku.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa paai bɛni nti nya Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun tisi piinisuma n‑tiri bɛni niriba maa, u sufu daa maaya: ka u daa kpamsi ba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑zɛ ti Dugma maa nyaaŋŋa ni nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Dama dɔsuŋŋu n daa nyɛ u, ka Sii Kasi daa bɛ u ni, ka u yɛdda gba zoaai. Di saha ka niriba pam daa la'am m‑paasi ban daa dɔli Naawunni soori maa zugu.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinzugu Baanabasi daa yisigi n‑kyaŋŋi Taasusi tiŋŋa ni u ti bɔ Sɔla ziiya.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti nya u, u daa zi u n‑labi n‑kyɛ Antiyɔkku na. Ka Baanabasi ni Sɔla daa la'am ni Naawunni niriba bɛni n‑niŋi yuummunni zaa, n‑wulisiri niriba pam. Antiyɔkku ka ba daa piili m‑boonni Yisa nyaandɔliba, ni, ‘Kirisita niriba’ la.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka anabiseeba daa yi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑kyɛ Antiyɔkku na.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Ba yinni ŋɔn yuuri boonni Agabusi la daa doaai, ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi maa daa kyɛ ka u wulisi ba, ni, kɔm nin ti lu tinsi zaa ni.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu Yisa niriba maa daa ŋmɛ saawara n‑gbaai gbaari, ni, ba ya n‑suŋŋi la ba mabiseeba ban bɛ Gyuudiiya la, sɔkam ni u yaa tɔriga.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ba daa niŋi lala, ka kyɛ ka Baanabasi ni Sɔla deaai ba piini maa n‑zi n‑kyaŋŋi kpamma maa saani.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka NaaHɛroodi daa piili n‑gbaari ninvugseeba ban daa la'amni taaba n‑dɔli Yisa soori maa n‑niŋni ba putoo.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: U daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi takoobi n‑ku Gyɔni beeri Gyeemsi la.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti nya ka lala niŋi Yɛhudiyadima la nya'asim maa, u daa gbaai Piita gba - dinŋɔ daa niŋi la Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la saha -

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
