translation_verse: n‑gbaai u n‑kpeesi sarika duu ni. Soogyadima piiya ni ayoobu n daa guri u: ba daa puri ba la banaanaasi, ni ba deaari taaba n‑guri u. Dama Naaba daa yɛnni, ni, Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu ŋɔn ti ya n‑gaari la, u nin yiisi u na n‑ti zama maa.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Dizugu Piita daa kpalim m‑bɛ sarika duu maa ni, amaa Yisa nyaandɔliba daa kur la'amni n‑susiri la Naawunni n‑tiri Piita ni nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka saha maa ŋɔn daa ti paai, kaman beoo ka Hɛroodi ya yiisi Piita sarika maa ni na n‑ti niriba maa. Ka lala saha maa, yuŋŋu, Piita daa gbisiri ni; ka ba daa zaŋŋi kyɔrimi n‑ga u n‑tabli soogyadima ayiyi u ya'a ayi zaa, ka daa guri u lala. Ka soogyaseeba daa zɛ n‑guri sarika za'anɔroo maa m‑paasi.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Di saha ka ti Dugma malaaka gbirigi n‑yi bɛni na, ka neesim daa neaai sarika duu maa ni. U daa gbaai Piita bɔkku n‑neaai u, ka yɛli u, “Doaam yooma!” Ka kyɔrimidima maa daa yi Piita nuusi ni n‑lu tiŋŋa.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Malaaka maa daa yɛli u, ni, “Loom i sɛlooriga yooma ka piri i namna!”, ka u daa niŋi lala. Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli “Zaŋŋma i kparigu n‑yɛ ka dɔli ma!”

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑yakki soogyadima zaaziisi buyi, nti paai kutti za'anɔseaa kan yiiri timpuuni maa. Ban daa ti paai sahanseaa la, kyɛburu maa daa yoaai ka toori, ka ba daa yi. Ba daa dɔli la sɔroo n‑kyɛnna, ka malaaka maa ti kyɛ Piita m‑basi ka kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka Piita daa ti baŋŋi din daa niŋi, ka yɛli, “M baŋŋiya! Di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni. Naawunni n siri tum u malaaka ni u kyɛ na n‑tiligi ma NaaHɛroodi nuu ni, ni Yɛhudiyadima ŋɔn yɛli, ni, ban nin niŋi ma seem la ni zaa.”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Ka u teesi m‑baŋŋi, ka kyaŋŋi Mariama yiri, ŋɔn bii daa nyɛ Gyɔni, sɔ ban daa boonni ‘Maaki’ la; bɛni ka niriba pam daa la'am n‑susiri Naawunni.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: U daa baŋŋi kukɔri maa, ni, Piita n nyɛ u, ka u sufu daa maai pam; halli ka u bu yoaai n‑ti u, amaa u daa zɔ n‑kpɛ nti yɛli ba, ni, Piita zɛ la samanni.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli u, “Gyɛrigu wa!”, amaa u daa kur tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa, ni, di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni. Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u “U malaaka n nyɛ u.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Piita daa kpalim n‑kpaasiri ka boonna, ka ba daa naayi yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑nya u, ka di daa niŋi ba alahazibu.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Ka u daa zaŋŋi u nuu m‑bulisi ba, ni, ba kyɛli ba vuuri, ka tɔ'asi n‑wulisi ba ti Dugma ŋɔn niŋi seem n‑yiisi u sarika ni na. Ka laa n‑yɛli, ni, ba yɛlim dinŋɔ n‑ti Gyeemsi ni ba mabiisi zaa; ka naayi bakki n‑kyaŋŋi seela.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, di daa gyɛri soogyaseeba ban daa guri Piita maa pam, ka ba daa boosiri taaba, ni, Piita bɛ la yani?

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Ka NaaHɛroodi daa yɛli, ni, ba yiisim u na, ka ba daa bɔ u zii n‑kɔŋŋi. Ka u daa boosi soogyadima maa boosa, ka tisi noori, ni, ba kuum ba. Di nyaaŋŋa NaaHɛroodi daa yi Gyuudiiya n‑kyaŋŋi Sizariiya nti niŋi dabsa ayi bɛni.

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka NaaHɛroodi sufu daa yisigi n‑niŋi Taaya ni Saidoon niriba ka ba daa la'am taaba n‑kyɛ na ni ba ti nya u. Ba daa kur tɔ'asiri n‑tiri Bilasitusi - ŋɔn daa gɔsiri Naaba duu yɛla n‑tiri u la - halli ka u ti sakki ni u nin zɔ ba sindaani Naaba maa saani. Ba tinsi maa ŋɔn daa deaari ba bundira Naa maa tingbana ni la zugu n‑daa la.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Ba daa zaali yɛttɔ'a maa dabsiri: ka dabsiri maa ŋɔn daa ti paai, NaaHɛroodi daa bɔbi u naam kpariveela ka zinni bimbinni maa zugu, ka tɔ'asi ba yɛttɔ'anya'asa.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Ka zama maa daa tasi n‑yɛli, “Haaya! Di pa la ninsaala n tɔ'asiri wa, amaa Naawunni n tɔ'asiri maa!”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Naawunni malaaka daa ŋmɛ Hɛroodi ya'abi n‑dooli, ŋɔn daa bu tiri Naawunni gyirima zugu. U daa poaai n‑yiiri zunzuya, halli nti kpi.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Baanabasi ni Sɔla daa tum ba tuma Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni n‑naai, ka daa labi Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa na. Ka Gyɔni sɔa, ban daa pa yupaa, ni, ‘Maaki’ la, daa dɔli ba n‑kyɛ na.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Ban daa gyeemni ti Dugma ka loori noori sahanseaa la, Naawunni Sii Kasi maa daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya yiisim Baanabasi ni Sɔla n‑zaali n‑ti ma, ka ba tum man yiisi ba ni ba ti tum tunseesi la.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa puusi Naawunni ni noori loobu pɔi, ka naayi zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa ba zugri ka zaali ba soori.

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Naawunni Sii Kasi ŋɔn daa tum Baanabasi ni Sɔla maa, ba daa kyaŋŋi la Sɛlusii tiŋŋa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti paai bɛni n‑sigi Salamiisi tiŋŋa, ba daa bɛ bɛni n‑kpeeri Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duri ni, nti moonni Naawunni yɛligu. Ka Gyɔni gba daa dɔli, n‑suŋŋni ba.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu muna - ka bɛ la teeku kooma sunsukkuni; ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑gyili ka tinsi zaa, nti paai Pafoosi tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka ba daa nya tiimaraansɔ, ka u yuuri boonni Baa Yisa. U daa nyɛ la Yɛhudiya nira, ka zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa n‑leebi anabi,

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: ka daa bɛ Sɛgyeoosi Pɔla yiri ni, sɔ ŋɔn daa di Gɔmna n‑su Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu zaa fukumsi la. Ŋɔn Pɔla daa mari la yɛm pam: u daa booli Baanabasi ni Sɔla ka ba kyɛ na, ŋɔn daa boori ni u wum Naawunni yɛligu zugu.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn Ɛlimasi muna - lala ka ba daa takki tiimaraana maa yuuri - Ɛlimasi daa kyiisi la yɛligu maa, ka daa kpaŋŋni u maŋŋa n‑ta'ari Gɔmna maa ni u di niŋi Yisa yɛdda.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Di saha ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi yiiko daa paali Sɔla - sɔ ban pa u yupaa, ni, ‘Pɔla’ la - ka u daa gɔsi tiimaraana maa suŋŋa

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: ka yɛli u, ni, “Nyin Sintaani bii wa! I nyɛ la yɛlisuma zaa dindaana. I mari la yɔmsibeeri, ka boori ni i zaŋŋi ti Dugma yɛlimaŋni n‑leebi ziri! Ay - nyin kyɛl lala!

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Ti Dugma nin zaŋŋi u nuu n‑ŋmɛ i pampaŋŋɔ. I nin ti leebi zooma, ka ku laa n‑nya wuntaŋŋa neesim ka di paai sahaseaa!” Ka di saha ka Ɛlimasi daa ti nya ka zibsim kpɛ u nini ni, ka u daa tuutiri n‑gyinna, m‑boori sɔ ŋɔn nin datti u n‑kyɛnna.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Ka Gɔmna maa ŋɔn daa ti nya lala, ka wum ban daa tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛla seem, di daa niŋi u alahazibu, ka dizugu u daa niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda.

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Pɔla dima daa laa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑yi Pafoosi tiŋŋa n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi Pɛriga, tinseaa kan bɛ Pamfilii tingbaŋŋu ni. Bɛni ka Gyɔni Maaki daa basi ba ka labi n‑kuli Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Pɔla dima mun daa yi Pɛriga n‑gaari tooni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Antiyɔkku tinseaa, kan bɛ Pisidii tingbaŋŋu la ni. Ka Yɛhudiyadima Vuusim Dabsiri daari ŋɔn daa ti paai, ba daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu ni n‑zinni.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa karim Musa sɔppu kariŋŋu maa n‑naai la, ni anabidima sɔppu kariŋŋu gba, la'aŋŋu kpamma maa daa tum ba saani na, ni, “Ti mabiisi, ya yi mari lahabaseaa kan nin kpamsi niriba wa, ti boori ni ya tɔ'asi n‑ti ti.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Ka dizugu Pɔla daa yisigi n‑zaani, n‑gaasi u nuu, ka daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri, ni, “M mabiisi, ya Izraɛli dima, ni ban dɔli Izraɛli dima soori n‑gyeemni Naawunni, ya wumma!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Naawunni n su Izraɛli dima yɛla: u daa yiisi ti yaadima ka kyɛ ka ba daa zoaai ka ba yuuri du, ban daa nyɛ saamma Igyipti tingbaŋŋu maa ni sahanseaa la. Tɔ, ka di nyaaŋŋa u daa zaŋŋi u yiiko ni kpeeŋŋu n‑yiisi ba na,

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ya baŋŋma suŋŋa, ka di ti nya wahala hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa wulisi seem la, ni#:-

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: m‑paai Kyɛna'an tingbaŋŋu na. Bɛni ka u daa sa'am bura ayɔpɔi ka daa zaŋŋi ba tiŋŋa maa n‑ti u niriba.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Ka di nyaaŋŋa Naawunni daa basi Sɔla ka yiisi Dauda, Gyɛsi bii la, ka u leebi ba naaba. Naawunni daa tɔ'asi Dauda yɛla nŋɔa: ‘N nya ka Gyɛsi bii Dauda n nyɛ man boori sɔa: ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nin tum man boori seem n nyɛ u!’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna di daa nyɛ ŋɔn NaaDauda za'anɔri dɔsɔa, Yisa, ka Naawunni daa yiisi, hoo ŋɔn daa pun tisi noori seem la, ni u ti leebi ŋɔn nin fa Izraɛli dima m‑basi.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn daa ti ya piili u tuma maa, Gyɔni daa pun daŋŋi tooni m‑moonni n‑tiri Izraɛli dima, ni, ba kyɛli ba tumbeeri ka takki ba beebu, ka su Naawunni koom.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Ka sahanseaa Gyɔni ŋɔn daa ya paai u tuma naari, u daa yɛli, ‘Ya teesiya, ni, n nyɛ la ŋɔni? M pa la yan guri sɔ maa. Amaa ya wumma! U bɛ la n nyaaŋŋa n‑kyɛnni na, ka man bu sumni m‑pirigi u namna n‑ti u!’ ”

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Tɔ, m mabiisi, yarima ban yi Ibrahim butiri ni na, ni seeba ban bɛ ya saani n‑gyeemni Naawunni la, tirima saani ka tuliginsim lahabasuŋŋu wa paai na.

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Gyɛrusalɛm niriba ni ba toondaandima daa bu baŋŋi anabidima sɔppu yɛla gbinni, halli ni ba karimni a ni Vuusim Dabsiri kam zaa maa zaa yɔri: ka dizugu ba daa kyɛ ka lala yɛla maa daa niŋi lala; ka daa kari ŋɔn Yisa sariya n‑yɛli, ni, u luya ni.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Ba daa tum anŋɔ zaa hoo Sɔppu maa ŋɔn daa pun wulisi u yɛla seem la; ka tum lala n‑naai. Ka di nyaaŋŋa ba daa sigsi u kum dɔpulli maa zugu na n‑zaŋŋi u n‑sɔ'ai gballi ni.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni daa neaai u kum ni.

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: Ka di daa niŋi dabsa pam ka u daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisiri n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban daa pun dɔli u n‑yi Galilii n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na. Ban mun niŋi u sɛharadima, m‑moonni ban nya ka wum yɛttɔ'aseesi n‑tiri Naawunni niriba maa, halli ni zuna.

english_verse: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
translation_verse: Dizugu tirima gba bɛ la kpa wa, ni ti yɛli ya lahabasuŋŋu maa, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa tisi noori n‑ti ya yaadima la,

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: lala ka u siri tum n‑ti ti, tin nyɛ ba ya'ansi la; ka u yisigi Yisa kum ni. Dina ka ba daa sɔbi Yuuma bukku maa ni, yuumni ayi daana puuni la, ni#:- ‘M bii n nyɛ i; Zuna dabsiri wa ka n dɔ'ai i.’

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: N‑laa m‑paasi, ni, ŋɔna nin yisigi u kum ni, ka ku laa n‑nya poosigu yaasa: u daa yɛli nŋɔa#:- ‘N nin zaŋŋi bunsunseesi Dauda ŋɔn daa deaai la, Ka a nyɛ la bunveela Ka birisigu laa n‑ka a puuni la, M‑mari na n‑ti i.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Ka di daa laa n‑wulisi luginseaa ni, n‑yɛli, ni#:- ‘I ku kyɛ ka i maŋtoori ninvugsuŋŋu nin leebi bumpoosiri.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka NaaDauda ŋɔn daa tum Naawunni yuubu u duniya wa ni beebu saha halli nti naai maa, u daa ti ka ni, m‑paai u yaadima saani. Ammi, di saha u ningbina daa bu poaai? Ay - a poaaya!

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa yisigi sɔ u kum ni la - ŋɔn mun daa bu poaai.

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: ‘Ya nyam dinŋɔa, yarima bunyaarisi wa! Di nin ti gyɛri ya pam: ya kyɛma sa! Ka baŋŋi, ni, n tumni n tuma ya dabsa wa ni - Alahazibu tunseaa ya ku sakki n‑niŋi ka yɛdda, Halli ba yi ti wulisi ya ka yɛla gba.’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Tɔ: Ka ban daa puusi n‑naai ka daa yiiri la'aŋŋu duu maa ni na, ba daa yɛli Pɔla ni Baanabasi, ni, ba labma na Vuusim Dabsiri kan kyɛnni na nti tugi yɛttɔ'a maa.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Ka niriba maa ŋɔn daa wurigi n‑naai, Yɛhudiyadima maa pam, ni seeba ban daa dɔli ba Naawunni soori la, daa dɔli Pɔla ni Baanabasi; ka ba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba, n‑kpaamni ba, ni, ba mari Naawunni piiniseaa ban deaai la suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri maa ŋɔn daa paai na la, tiŋŋa maa niriba zaa daa la'amiya, ni ba ti wum Naawunni yɛligu maa.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Ka Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn daa ti nya daadam wa, nyuuri daa wum ba pam, ka ba daa gbaai kyiisigu, ni, Pɔla ŋɔn wulisiri yɛttɔ'aseesi la pa la yɛlimaŋni, ka tuuri u tupeela.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa zaŋŋi ba sufukpeemni n‑labsi ba, n‑yɛli, “Tɔ! Ti yi da bu daŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka yarima wum Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa, di naa n‑ku mari soori. Amaa yan za'asiri maa ka yɛnni, ni, ya bu sumsi ni ya nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari maa; tɔ, ti nin labsi ti zikki la ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Dama lala ka Naawunni daa zaali ti noori, ni#:- ‘N yiisi i, ni i ti leebi neesim, N‑neaai n‑ti tinzundima ban pa ya niriba; Ka laa n‑leebi tuliginsim soori N‑tiri duniya wa lugri kam zaa dima.’ ”

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la ban daa ti wum lala, ba mari la sufupeelli pam m‑pɛ'ari ti Dugma yɛligu maa. Ka ba seeba daa nyɛ seeba ban ya n‑nya nyɔvuri kan ka naari, ka daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda di zugu.

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu daa gyili m‑paai bɛni tingbaŋŋu maa zaa.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima daa kpa'atiri la pɔ'aseeba ban daa dɔli ba Naawunni soori ka nyɛ tiŋŋa maa ninvugtibsa la, ni tinkpamma maa m‑paasi, halli ka di ti paai ni ba zabri Pɔla ni Baanabasi n‑niŋi ba putoo tuma, ka kari ba n‑yiisi ba tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka Pɔla dima daa kpaasi ba napɔntam m‑basi, n‑wulisi, ni, ba za'asiri ba ni; ka doaai n‑kyaŋŋi Aikoonyum tiŋŋa.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba ban daa deaai lahabasuŋŋu maa ka niŋi yɛdda la daa mari sufupeelli, ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa bɛ ba saani.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi ŋɔn daa paai Aikoonyum tiŋŋa, di daa kur niŋi lala yaasa. Ba daa kpɛ Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑tɔ'asi lala, halli ka Yɛhudiyadima ni tiŋŋa maa niriba pam daa niŋi yɛdda.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa za'asi yɛdda niŋbu la daa tugli ban pa Yɛhudiya niriba la n‑kyɛ ka ba suuri yisigi, ka ba daa zabi ni yɛddaniŋniba maa.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa yuui bɛni ka daa bɛ ni n‑tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa n‑wulisiri ti Dugma yɛla; ka ti Dugma Naawunni daa zɛ ba nyaaŋŋa, ka daa ti ba yiiko n‑kyɛ ka ba tumni alahazibu tuma, din wulisi, ni, ba yɛttɔ'a maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni, ŋɔn Naawunni nyɛ la Wurilim Daana.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Dizugu tiŋŋa maa niriba daa puri la buyi: baseeba daa zɛ la Yɛhudiyadima maa nyaaŋŋa, ka seeba mun daa zɛ Yisa tuumudima maa nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Ka Yɛhudiyadima maa ni buriseesi niriba daa la'am ni tiŋŋa maa toondaandima, ni ba teesi ba nin niŋi tuumudima maa putoo seem ka lɔbi ba kuga.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Tɔ; Lisitira timpuuni ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ ni n‑nyɛ gbariga: ba daa dɔ'ai u la lala, ka u daa na zi n‑kyaŋŋi halli ni di dabsiri maa.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: U daa zi ni n‑wumsiri Pɔla yɛttɔ'a maa; ka Pɔla daa nya u, ka gɔsi u suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi, ni, yɛdda daa bɛ gbariga maa ni, ka u nin toaai n‑nya alaafɛa.

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu u daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli u, “Doaam saazugu n‑zaani i gbaya zugu, kyappi!” Ka doo maa daa yisigi n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka zama maa ŋɔn daa nya Pɔla ŋɔn daa niŋi seem maa, ba daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri pampam ba maŋtoori yɛttɔ'a ni, ni, “Ti tingbaŋŋu wa buga maa labgi la ninsaala n‑sigi ti saani na!”

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Zeoosi gyeeŋŋu yiri daa zɛ la tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa; ka dizugu tindaansɔ ŋɔn daa su Zeoosi maaligu yɛla la daa zaŋŋi na'araari ni tiisi puum n‑kyɛ tiŋŋa maa za'anɔri zii na, ni ŋɔn ni niriba maa ya maali la sara hoo ba tiŋŋa kaari ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla ni Baanabasi ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi ban daa teesi, ni, ba ya n‑niŋi seem maa, ba daa toosi ba sutura sufusa'aŋŋu zugu, ka vaai n‑zɔ n‑kpɛ zama maa sunsukkuni, ka tasi

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: n‑yɛli, “Zama! Bɔ zugu ka ya lee n‑niŋni lala? Tirima gba nyɛ la ninsaalidima hoo yan nyɛ seem wa! Halli dinŋɔ zugu n daa kyɛ ka ti kyɛ na m‑moonni Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑tiri ya, ni ya ti kyɛ bunkyɔ'ama wa gyeeŋŋu ka labi na n‑tabli Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana saani. Ŋɔna n daa naam arizana ni duniya wa ni teeku zaa, m‑paasi bunseelli kam din bɛ a ni.

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Saha din gaari sa halli ni zuna, u daa kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa niŋi la u sufu yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem,

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: amaa u daa bu kyɛ ka ba kɔŋŋi yɛliseesi an wulisiri ba u yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, dama u daa zaŋŋi u mammaŋŋa tunsuma n‑ti ya. Seoo ni saha ka u tiri ya saa, ka tiri ya agazeeri saha m‑paasi ni ya ti nya ya puri antaam. U tiri ya bundirigu ka kyɛ ka ya mari sufumaasim.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Ni ba yɛttɔ'a wa zaa yɔri, di daa tɔ pam ni ba kyɛ ka daadam maa bu maali ba hoo ban maanni ba buga seem.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Yɛhudiyadimseeba daa yi Antiyɔkku Pisidii tiŋŋa na, ka seeba daa yi Aikoonyum na. Ba daa tɔ'asi niriba maa n‑kyɛ ka ba deaai ba noori, ka zaŋŋi kuga n‑lɔbi Pɔla halli nti yɛli, ni, u kpi ni; ka vuui u n‑yiisi tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa ka kyɛ u.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Amaa ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa ŋɔn daa yi na n‑zɛ n‑gyiligi u, u daa doaai n‑labi n‑kpɛ tiŋŋa maa puuni. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ŋɔna ni Baanabasi daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Dɛribee.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ba daa ti mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti tiŋŋa wa niriba, ka daa kyɛ ka niriba pam daa niŋi Yisa nyaandɔliba. Ka di nyaaŋŋa ba daa labi n‑dɔli Lisitira, ni Aikoonyum, halli nti labi m‑paai Antiyɔkku.

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ba daa kaai Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban bɛ lala tinsi maa ni n‑kpamsiri ba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi m‑bari n‑zaani ni sufukpeemni, dama din bɛ seem maa, faasee ka ti nya namsigu pam pɔi ka naayi paai Naawunni naam beebu.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ya'aseaa zaa Yisa nyaandɔliba ŋɔn daa la'am m‑bɛ ni, Pɔla dima daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa seeba zugri ni ba diisi ba Yisa tuma kpamma. Ba daa lɔ la noori n‑susi Naawunni, ni, u gɔsi ban daa niŋi u yɛdda maa ka guri ba yɛla suŋŋa.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ba daa dɔli Pisidii tingbaŋŋu nti paai Pamfiliiya.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Ka bɛni ba daa kpɛ Pɛriga timpuuni nti tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛligu pɔi, ka naayi kyaŋŋi Ataliiya, tinseaa kan zɛ teeku gbiriŋŋu la.

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka ba daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi n‑labi n‑kuli Antiyɔkku, dama bɛni niriba daa daŋŋi n‑susi Naawunni wurilim tunsuma n‑ti ba, ni ba deaai tuntumsi maa, ka pampaŋŋɔ ba daa tum n‑naai.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa paai na, ba daa la'am ni Yisa nyaandɔliba la'aŋŋu dima zaa, ka daa wulisi ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa tum seem n‑ti ba maa, ni, “Naawunni yoaai la soori, ni ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la gba ti dɔli ka niŋni Yisa yɛdda.”

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa bɛ bɛni nyaandɔliba maa saani n‑yuui beela.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ninvugseeba daa yi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyɛ Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa na. Lala niriba daa wulisiri ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa la, ni, see ka ba guni hoo Musa ŋɔn daa zaali seem la; ni, di yi pa lala ba ku toaai n‑nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla ni Baanabasi daa ŋmɛ ba nangbankpeemni pam yɛttɔ'a wa zugu. Ka dizugu ba daa bɔ niriba m‑paasi Pɔla ni Baanabasi zugu n‑tum ni ba kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm n‑nya Yisa tuumudima ni kpamma maa, lala yɛttɔ'a maa zugu.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Yisa nyaandɔliba la'aŋŋu dima ŋɔn daa tum ba maa, ba daa dɔli Fɔnisii ni Samɛrii tingbana n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ba nyaandɔlitaaba n‑wulisiri ba ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la ŋɔn niŋi tuuba seem maa. Ka di daa niŋi ba nya'asim pam.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa ti kpɛ Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni maa, ba daa paai yɛddaniŋniba ni tuumudima ni kpamma saani na. Ka ban daa gyaari ba n‑naai maa, ba daa piili ba yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka ba tum seem.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa yɛddaniŋniseeba ban daa dɔli Farisii dima soori maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑tɔ'asi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa yɛla. Ba daa yɛli, ni, di sumsi ni ba guni ba, ka kpamsi ba, ni, ba dɔli Musa zaaligu yɛla zaa.

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka din daa toaai beela, tuumudima ni kpamma maa daa la'am, ni ba ti tɔ'asi lala yɛttɔ'a maa n‑nya a soori ziiya kasi.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa ti ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni dinŋɔ zugu n‑yuui, ka Piita naayi doaai n‑yɛli ba, ni, “M mabiisi, ya mi halli, ni, mani ka Naawunni daa daŋŋi n‑yiisi, ni n kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la wum lahabasuŋŋu wa man noori ni ka niŋi yɛdda.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn mi din bɛ ninsaala sufu ni la daa wulisi, ni, ba niŋi yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni; dama u daa ti ba u Sii Kasi hoo ŋɔn daa pun ti ti gba seem la.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Naawunni bu wulisi, ni, ti bɛ ti kɔkɔ ka kyɛ ba, dama ba gba yɛdda niŋbu zugu ka u kpiisi la ba sufu ni taalli n‑ti ba.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Ka pampaŋŋɔ, bɔ n niŋi ka ya ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni ni Naawunni? - ka zaŋŋi zitibsa n‑ziili ban ya n‑dɔli u la zugu, ziseesi tirima ŋɔn bu nyaŋŋi n‑zi - halli ti yaadima gba daa bu nyaŋŋi.

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Ay! Amaa tirima niŋni yɛdda, ni, ti Dugma Yisa wurilim tuma zugu ka u kyɛ ka ti nin tuligi, ka lala n nyɛ di n‑tiri ban gba.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka daadam maa zaa daa sini vuuri ka wumni Pɔla ni Baanabasi yɛttɔ'a, ka ba daa wulisi ba Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka ba tum tunkara ni alahazibu yɛla seem maa, ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Ban daa ti tɔ'asi n‑naai maa, ka Gyeemsi daa tɔ'asi n‑yɛli, ni, “M Mabiisi, ya wumma!

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simiyɔn na pun wulisi ti Naawunni ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑yiisi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima ninvugseeba seem, ni ba ti leebi ŋɔn Naawunni niriba.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘Nyama! NaaDauda naam suulinsi pun fiilim; Kaman sugri kan wurim n‑lu. Amaa di nyaaŋŋa man Naawunni nin ti labi na Ni n ti laa m‑paasi ka yaasa, Ka maali ka suŋŋa.

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Ba nin bɔ ba Dugma ziiya dizugu - Ban kam kpalim nyaaŋŋa maa; Ni tinsi zaasa niriba ban kpeeri n yuuri maasim ni maa. Man Naawunni yɛli lala, ka mun nin niŋi:

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: Ka Gyeemsi daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Man teesa nyɛ la nŋɔa: ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la yi ti niŋi tuuba n‑dɔli Yisa, di bu sumni ni ti daamni ba yɛlimugsa.

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: See ka ti sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ba, ni, ba di ŋɔbi numda'aseesi ban kɔrigi m‑maanni ba'a ni buga la, bee, a seesi an bu kɔrigi, bee, an na mari ziim; ka ba di tum tumbeeri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔgoora ni pɔ'agoora yɛla.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Dama ba daa kur tɔ'asiri Musa yɛla ti yaadima saha halli ni zuna; ba karimni a ni tinsi kam zaa puuni, ka moonni a Vuusim Dabsiri kam, la'aŋŋu duri puuni.”

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ba zaa daa la'am taaba - tuumudima maa, ni kpamma, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa zaa - ka daa gbaai ba gbaari, ni, ba ya n‑yiisi la ba ninvugseeba n‑tum ba Antiyɔkku, ban ni Pɔla ni Baanabasi. Ka dizugu ba daa yiisi la Gyuudasi , ni Sailasi; ban mun daa bɛ la Yisa nyaandɔliba toondaandima sunsukkuni.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Ban ka ba daa tɔlim gbaŋŋu maa, ni#:- “Nnya, tirima, ya mabiseeba ban nyɛ Yisa tuumudima ni kpamma la, m puusiri ya: yaseeba ban nyɛ ti mabiisi, halli ka ya pa la ti Yɛhudiyataaba la, ka bɛ la Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Siriiya ni Silisii tingbana la zaa.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Ti wumiya, ni, seeba daa yi ti saani m‑paai ya na - tirima mun daa bu tum ba - ka daa daamni ya n‑sa'amni ya sufu ni yɛttɔ'aseesi ban yɛtti maa.

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: Dizugu ka ti la'am taaba n‑sakki n‑gbaai, ni, ti yiisi ninvugseeba n‑tum ya saani na, m‑paasi Pɔla ni Baanabasi zugu. Halli ni Pɔla ni Baanabasi gba nyɛ la ti zɔsuma,

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: ka daa zaŋŋi ba nyɔvuya m‑pa talima ti Dugma Yisa yuuri maa zugu.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Dizugu ti tumni la Gyuudasi ni Sailasi ni ba zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'a m‑paasi tin sɔbi seelli wa, n‑wulisiri di gbinni.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Tɔ: Naawunni Siiya Vuusim Kasi ŋɔn gbaai n‑zaali seem nŋɔa - ka ti gba sakki lala: ni, ti di daamni ya m‑paasi yɛttɔ'akpana ayi-ata wa#:-

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled. You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: ni, ya di ŋɔbi numda'aseesi ban kɔrigi m‑maanni ba'a ni buga la, bee, seesi an bu kɔrigi, bee, an na mari ziim; ya di tum tumbeeri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔgoora ni pɔ'agoora yɛla ni. Ya yi gbaai ya mansi ka kyɛ anŋɔ maa, ya nin niŋi suŋŋa. Naawunni paasim ya goom.”

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dɔppa maa daa kyaŋŋi m‑paai Antiyɔkku na, ka daa la'asi yɛdda dima maa zaa nti ti ba gbaŋŋu maa.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Ban daa karim ka maa, di daa niŋi ba sufumaasim ka laa n‑kpamsi ba sufu pam.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Gyuudasi ni Sailasi gba daa nyɛ la anabidima, ka ba daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'anya'asa pam n‑kpamsiri ba mabiisi maa.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Ban daa niŋi saha beela bɛni, Yisa nyaandɔliba maa daa zaali ba soori, ka taaliseaa ka ni. Ba daa labi n‑kuli ban daa tum ba maa saani.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Pɔla ni Baanabasi mun daa kpalim m‑bɛ Antiyɔkku timpuuni, ka ban ni ba taaba pam daa wulisiri Naawunni yɛligu ka moonni lahabasuŋŋu maa.

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Tɔ. Saha ŋɔn daa ti gaari beela, Pɔla daa yɛli Baanabasi, ni, “Kyɛ ka ti labi n‑kaai tinseesi tin daa pun kyaŋŋi n‑wulisi Naawunni yɛttɔ'a maa, n‑nya ti mabiisi maa ŋɔn bɛ seem.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Ka di saha ka Baanabasi daa boori ni u zaŋŋi Gyɔni, sɔ ŋɔn boonni Maaki la, ka ba kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla daa bu sakki ni ba zaŋŋi u, ŋɔn daa bakki ka kyɛ ba Pamfilii tingbaŋŋu ni maa zugu, ka daa bu laa n‑dɔli ba n‑la'amni n‑tumna.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Ka ba daa ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni dizugu, halli ka daa wɔligi ka kyɛ taaba. Ka dinzugu Baanabasi daa zaŋŋi Maaki ka ba kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi Saapirusi.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Pɔla mun daa zaŋŋi la Sailasi m‑bakki; nyaandɔliba maa daa zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi Naawunni nuu ni ka kyeoosi ba.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa dɔli la Siriiya ni Silisiiya n‑kpamsiri ban niŋi yɛdda n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni Yisa maa.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa gɔ n‑gɔ, halli nti paai Dɛribee ni Lisitira tinsi. Bɛni ka Yisa nyaandɔlisɔ daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Timɔti. U ma gba daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda, ka mun daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyapɔ'a, amaa u ba daa nyɛ la Griika doowa.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka yɛddaniŋniba ban bɛ Lisitira ni Aikoonyum la daa yɛtti, ni, Timɔti nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Pɔla daa boori ni Timɔti dɔli u ka ba kyaŋŋi. Ka lala zugu u daa guni u. Yɛhudiyadima ban daa bɛ bɛni zugu ka u daa niŋi lala, dama ba zaa baŋŋi, ni, Timɔti ba daa nyɛ la Griika nira.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ba daa dɔli tinsi maa, ka daa yɛli Yisa niriba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑sakki zaaliseesi tuumudima ni kpamma ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm maa ŋɔn daa zaali n‑ti ba la, ka dɔli a suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Dizugu ban daa la'amni n‑dɔli Yisa maa daa mari yaa ni yɛdda, ka ba daa zoaari m‑paasiri sahakam.

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ba daa gɔ n‑dɔli Firigyii ni Galɛseaa tingbana dama Naawunni Sii Suŋŋu daa bu ti ba soori ni ba tɔ'asiri lahabaari maa Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ba daa dɔli nti paai Misii tingbaŋŋu maa tɔriga. Ba daa yɛnni, ni, ba nin kpɛ Bitinii tingbaŋŋu maa ni, amaa Yisa Siiya daa bu sakki lala n‑ti ba.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ba daa lɔkki n‑gaari Misiiya nti paai Tirɔ'asi tiŋŋa.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Di daari yuŋŋu maa Pɔla daa zaasi zaasimni, n‑nya Masidoonya nira ŋɔn zɛya m‑bɛlimni u, ni, “Kyɛm Masidoonya na nti suŋŋi ti!”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa nya zaasimni kanŋɔa zugu, ti daa sɛ'asi ya'abi ni ti yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu, dama ti daa teesi, ni, Naawunni n daa booli ti ni ti mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑tisi bɛni niriba maa.

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu ti daa kpɛ la nyariŋŋu n‑yi Tirɔ'asi n‑du n‑lɔŋŋi m‑paai Samɔtreesi, ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti kaai ti daa gaari m‑paai Niyapɔlisi tiŋŋa.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: N‑daa yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Filipaai - kana n nyɛ Masidoonya ya'ari wa tinsi toondaana, ka nyɛ la Room dima suulinsi tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka ti daa yuui beela.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Ka Vuusim Dabsiri daari maa, ti daa yi tiŋŋa maa puuni n‑kyaŋŋi kuliga ya'ari; bɛni ka ti daa teesi, ni, Yɛhudiyadima maa daa la'amni n‑gyeemna. Ti daa zinni bɛni n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri pɔ'aseeba ban daa la'am bɛni maa.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ bɛni ka u yuuri boonni Liidiya. U daa yi la Tayatayira tiŋŋa ni na, ka daa koosiri kyinkyinsuma an ligri daa zoaai. Pa Yɛhudiyapɔ'a n daa nyɛ u, amaa u daa dɔli Yɛhudiyadima soori n‑gyeemni Naawunni. Naawunni daa kyɛ ka u nini neaai, ka u daa wum Pɔla yɛttɔ'a maa ka sakki.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu, ka u daa susi ti, ni, ti kyɛm na nti bɛ u yiri. Ni, “Ya yi siri baŋŋi, ni, n nyɛ la yɛdda daana n‑ti ti Dugma, yan kyɛm na.” Ka ŋɔn daa bɛlim ti lala zugu daa kyɛ ka ti sakki lala n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Dindahinseaa ka ti daa ti wa puusigu ziiya, ka daa ti tuusi pɔ'asarisɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ daabri, ka daa mari arizinni ŋɔn daa nyɛ ba'akɔligu dinni ka daa kyɛ ka pɔ'a maa bugri m‑baŋŋni yɛla. Lala tuma zugu ka baseeba ban daa su pɔ'a maa daa nyari ligri pam u bugbu maa ni.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a wa daa dɔli Pɔla nyaaŋŋa n‑kaasiri n‑yɛtta, “Niriba wa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ yiiko zaa daana la tuntumniba. Ba yɛtti ya la yan nin niŋi seem n‑nya tuliginsim!”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: U daa kpalim n‑dɔla, ka daa kur tɔ'asiri lala dabsa pam. U daa ka'ali Pɔla pam, halli ka u daa ŋmaligi n‑yɛli arizinni maa, ni, “Yisa Masiiya yuuri zugu ka n yɛtti i - yim na ka kyɛ u!” Ka arizinni maa daa siri yi na ya'abi.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ban daa su pɔ'asariga maa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, ba ku laa n‑toaai n‑nya u bugbu ligri la, ba daa gbaai Pɔla ni Sailasi n‑daai ba n‑kyaŋŋi timpuuni nti niŋi tiŋŋa zuguraandima nuu ni.

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: Ba wulisira sɔseesi an bu sumni ni ti wumna, bee, ti dɔla; dama Room niriba n nyɛ ti.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Di saha ka daadam pam daa la'am n‑galimni Pɔla ni Sailasi, ka tiŋŋa maa sariyakattiba daa zaŋŋi ba n‑toaai ba kpari n‑yeaai ka kyɛ ka ba feebi ba daari.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu ba daa bu ba pam, ka zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi sarika ni; ka yɛli sarikadaana maa, ni, u kparim ba suŋŋa.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Ka sarikadaana ŋɔn daa wum lala, u daa deaai ba n‑zaŋŋi n‑niŋi sarika dutoonni la ni, ka ba ba nɔba ni datibsiri.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka yuntasuusi ka Pɔla ni Sailasi daa puusiri Naawunni ka yuumni yuuma n‑tiri u, ka ba dansarikataaba daa wumsira.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Di saha ka tiŋŋa maa daa dam, ka dansarika yiri maa daa miimna, halli ni ka nyeeba maa gba. Ka ka duri maa za'anɔya maa daa yoosi, ka ban daa ga ba nɔba kyɔrimidima la zaa daa gaasi n‑lu.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Ka dansarikadaana maa ŋɔn daa neaai goom ni n‑nya ka duri maa za'anɔya la zaa daa yoosi, u daa teesi, ni, dansarika dima maa zaa zɔya, ka dizugu u daa foaai u takoobi, ni u ku u maŋŋa.

english_verse: But Paul shouted, “Don't harm yourself! No one has escaped.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, “Di niŋi i maŋŋa seelli! Ti zaa na bɛ kpa!”

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dansarikadaana maa daa booli, ni, ba mari bugum na, ka daa kpɛ yooma na. U daa zɔri dabeem n‑gyigra, ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑ti Pɔla ni Sailasi.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Ka u daa mari ba n‑yi na; ka boosi ba, ni, “M Badima, n ya n‑niŋi la wula n‑nya tuliginsim?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli u, ni, “Niŋma ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda, ka i nin nya tuliginsim - nyin ni i yiri dima zaa.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba daa tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛla maa n‑tisi ŋɔn ni u yiri dima maa.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Ka dirindaari maa yuŋŋu ka dansarikadaana maa daa zaŋŋi ba m‑peaai ba nɔra la n‑ti ba, ka ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa deaai Naawunni koom suubu.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Ka daa mari Pɔla ni Sailasi n‑kpɛ u yiri maa na nti ti ba bundirigu ka ba di: ka ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa daa mari sufupeelim pam ba Naawunni yɛdda niŋbu zugu.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, tiŋŋa maa zuguraandima daa tum pirinsi na ka yɛli, ni, “Ya yiisim niriba wa m‑basi!”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Dizugu dansarikadaana maa daa yɛli lala n‑ti Pɔla, ni, “Zugudaandima maa yɛliya, ni, ti yiisim nyin ni Sailasi m‑basi; dizugu ya kyɛma, ka Naawunni nin paasi ya sufumaasim.”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Halli ba sa bu tɔ'asi ti yɛligu gba amaa ba sa bu ti zama maa tooni, tirima mun nyɛ la Room suulinsi niriba, ka sa zaŋŋi ti n‑kpeesi dansarika ni. Ka zuna ba yi boori la ba sɔ'ai m‑basi ti baalimm, fa! Ti ku sakki, walla! Faasee ka ba mammansi kyɛ na n‑yiisi ti m‑basi.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka pirinsi maa daa labi nti yɛli lala n‑ti zuguraandima maa. Ka ban daa wum, ni, Pɔla ni Sailasi nyɛ la Room suulinsi niriba la, dabeem daa gbaai ba.

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ba daa kyɛ na ni ba ti zɔ ba mammantoori sindaani Pɔla dima saani na, ka daa mari ba n‑yi dansarika maa ni na, n‑susi ba, ni, ba yim ba tiŋŋa maa ni.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yi dansarika maa na n‑kyaŋŋi Liidiya yiri n‑nya yɛddaniŋniba maa, ka tɔ'asi kpamsigu yɛttɔ'a n‑ti ba pɔi, ka naayi bakki n‑kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Pɔla dima daa kyaŋŋi n‑dɔli Amfipɔlisi ni Apɔloonya tinsi n‑yakki n‑gaari nti paai Tɛsalɔnika tiŋŋa, tinseaa Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu ŋɔn daa bɛ ni la.

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ya tum tuligra sɔ na n‑diisi u Masiiya naam la. Ka u daa wulisiri ba di gbinni, ni, lala Masiiya maa soori nyɛ la wahala diibu soori, ka di nyaaŋŋa kum neaari nin bɛ ni. Ni, dizugu Masiiya maa n nyɛ ŋɔnŋɔa, ni, “Yisa sɔ man moonni u yɛla n‑tiri ya maa.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Yɛhudiyadimseeba daa deaai u yɛligu maa ka daa paasi Pɔla ni Sailasi zugu; ka Griika dimseeba ban puusiri Naawunni la, ba bɔbgu, ni pɔ'akpamma gba daa bu kɔŋŋi paasigu.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa zabi la nyuuri, ka daa booli ninvugkyɔ'ama seeba ban daa guusi n‑guusiri daa puuni, ka ti la'asi zama pam n‑daamni tiŋŋa maa niriba. Ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑zaani Gyeesɔni yiri tooni, Pɔla ni Sailasi ŋɔn daa kpɛ seelli la, ni, u yiisim ba n‑ti ba na.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Amaa ban daa bu ti nya ba maa, ba daa gbaai Gyeesɔni ni ninvugseeba ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda la, n‑darigi ba n‑kyaŋŋi tiŋŋa maa tinkpamma saani. Ba daa kur tasiri n‑yɛtta, “Ninvugseeba ban sa'amni duniya zaa yɛla maa paai ti na!

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: Ba kyɛ ti tiŋŋa wa na, halli ka Gyeesɔni deaai ba n‑kpeesi u yiri ni. Ba galimni la NaaTitaari zaaligu la, dama ba yɛliya, ni, Nasɔ m bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Yisa.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Ban daa yɛtti lala yɛttɔ'a maa, ba daa ka'ali la zama ni tinkpamma maa pam.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Ka kpamma maa daa zaali Gyeesɔni ni u taaba samni ka yɛli ba, ni, ba di laa n‑zaali yɛttɔ'aseaa, ka naayi basi ba.

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Ka baseeba ban daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda maa daa doaai di dabsiri maa yuŋŋu, n‑zaali Pɔla ni Sailasi soori, ni ba kyaŋŋi nti paai Bɛriiya. Ban daa ti paai lala tiŋŋa maa, ba daa kpɛ la Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu la ni.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Bɛni niriba maa sufu daa neaai n‑gaari Tɛsalɔnika dima dinni. Ba daa deaai Naawunni yɛligu maa ni nimmoaa, ka daa gbaai Naawunni Litaafi kariŋŋu dabsa kam, ni ba ti baŋŋi di siri bɛ lala, bee?

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Ba pam daa niŋi Yisa yɛdda, ni Griika pɔ'akpamma m‑paasi, ka ba dɔppa gba daa bu kɔŋŋi yɛdda niŋbu.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyadimseeba bam bɛ Tɛsalɔnika la ŋɔn daa ti wum, ni, Bɛriiya gba ka Pɔla moonni Naawunni yɛligu maa, ba daa kyɛ na n‑daamni daadam maa ka ka'anni ba.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Dinzugu di daa bu yuui ka yɛddaniŋniba daa zaŋŋi Pɔla n‑taasi halli nti paai teeku gbiriŋŋu; Sailasi mun daa kpalim m‑bɛ Bɛrii timpuuni, ŋɔn ni Timɔti gba.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban daa zaali Pɔla soori maa daa mari u n‑kyaŋŋi halli nti paai Ateensi tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka Pɔla daa tɔlim ba Sailasi ni Timɔti saani, ni, ba yɛlim ba, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑kyɛ u saani na yooma. Ka ba naayi labi n‑kuli Bɛriiya.

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Pɔla ŋɔn daa bɛ Ateensi n‑guri Sailasi ni Timɔti maa, u sufu daa bu dɔya, ŋɔn daa kur gɔsiri n‑nyari ba'a ni buga ŋɔn daa zoaai bɛni seem maa zugu.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu u daa kpɛ la'aŋŋu duu maa ni, nti tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a maa n‑tiri Yɛhudiyadima maa ni baseeba ban daa dɔli ba soori la, ka laa n‑kpeeri daa puuni dabsa kam nti tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ban daa gatti bɛni zaa.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Karimbadima n daa bɛ bɛni, baseeba ban daa dɔli Ɛpikuruusi soori, ni seeba mun daa dɔli Sitoowa dima soori. Lala karimbadima ni Pɔla n daa mari nangbankpeemni, ka ninvugseeba daa boosiri n‑yɛli, “Yɛttɔ'ayɔya daana wa yɛtti la wula?”; baseeba mun daa yɛtti, ni, “Asee u tɔ'asiri wumpaala yɛla.” Pɔla ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri ‘Yisa’ yɛla, ni ‘kumyisigri’ yɛla maa zugu ka ba daa yɛtti lala maa.

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Ba daa la'amni la Ariyɔpagusi kunkunni zugu nti tɔ'asi ba yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu ba daa taasi Pɔla n‑du bɛni ka daa yɛli u, “I ku kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi wulisigu paaliseaa nyin wulisiri wa?

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Ti na zi n‑wum yɛttɔ'aseesi nyin tɔ'asiri wa taabu, ka ti boori ni ti baŋŋi a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Ba daa yɛli lala, dama Ateensi tiŋŋa niriba ni ba saamma ban bɛ bɛni daa ka sufupeelli tuma di yi pa la ba gyinni n‑tɔ'asiri ka wumni lahabaseaa kan nyɛ bumpaalli.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Pɔla daa doaai n‑zaani kunkunni zugu la'aŋŋu dima maa tooni ka yɛli, “Ya Ateensi dima, wumma! M pun gɔsi m‑baŋŋi, ni, ya dɔli la ya ba'a ni buga yɛla zaa, halli ni nimmoosi.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Dama man daa goori n‑gɔsiri ya bungyeemna, ka nya wumpuusigu bimbinseaa ka ba daa boaai n‑sɔbi ka yɛla ka saazugu, ni, ‘Kanŋɔ nyɛ la wunsɔ dinni, amaa ti na zi u.’ Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ya puusiri u ni ka na zi u, man mun moonni la u yɛla n‑tiri ya.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: Naawunni n nyɛ u: u daa naam duniya wa, ni seelli kam zaa an bɛ ka ni. Ŋɔna n su saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu zaa. Ammi, lala Naawunni nin kpeeri la ninsaala ŋɔn mɛ gyeeŋŋu duri seesi n‑gbɛa?

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: Bee, u kɔŋŋni bunseelli ka ninsaala nin mari ka na n‑ti u? Ay! di pa lala. Dama ŋɔn mammaŋŋa mari nyɔvuri ni alaafɛ ni bunseelli kam zaa n‑tisira.

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: U daa kyɛ ka ninsaalidima butiri kam zaa daa yi ninvugyinni ni na, ni ba ti yɛligri m‑bɛ duniya wa luga zaa puuni; ka daa zaali ba yinni yinni ba saha yɛla, ka laa n‑zaali ba tinsi tɔrisi ŋɔn bɛ seela.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: U daa niŋi lala ni ba ti boori u yɛla ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka kyɛnni tooni beelabeela ni ba ti baŋŋi u - halli ni anŋɔ zaa, u siri bu wɔ'a ti sɔkam saani.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn zugu ka ti bɛ ni, ka kyɛnna, ka mari nyɔvuri. Ya timbiseeba daa bu yuumni lala? Ba daa yɛliya, ni#:- ‘Ti gba nyɛ la u biisi.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ti yi siri nyɛ u biisi maa, ti di teesiri, ni, wummaŋni bɛ la hoo salima, bee anzulifa, bee kugveela, bee bunseelli kam tuma baŋŋniba ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑tumni la.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Sahanseaa din gaari la Naawunni daa pun di sugru zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninsaalidima tuma la ni, dama ba daa na zi u yɛla. Amaa pampaŋŋɔ u kpaamni la nira kam zaa, ban bɛ seelikam zaa maa, ni ba takkma ba teesibeeri ka kyɛ ba ala'akyi tuma.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Dama u na pun zaali dabsiri, dabsiseaa ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi u yɛlimaŋni maa n‑kari duniya zaa niriba sariya. Ka di nin dɔli dɔsɔ yɛligu - dɔsɔ ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn pun yiisi di zugu la. U daa pun wulisi ti zaa, ni, di siri nin niŋi, ka daa zaŋŋi di daama n‑kyɛ ka u neaai u kum nyaaŋŋa.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti wum Pɔla ŋɔn tɔ'asiri kum neaari yɛliseesi la, baseeba daa maali u la ansarisi, amaa baseeba mun daa yɛliya, “Ti boori la i laa n‑tɔ'asi lahabaari wa yɛla n‑ti ti yaasa.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa bakki n‑yi la'aŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka ninvugseeba daa paasi u zugu ka niŋi yɛligu maa yɛdda. Diyɔnisusi n daa nyɛ ba yinni, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ ba tinkpamma maa ni yinni, ni pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Damɛrisa, ka ninvugseeba daa paasi ba zugu yaasa.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa yi Ateensi n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Kɔrinti tiŋŋa.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: U daa nya Yɛhudiyadɔsɔ bɛni ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Akwila la, ka u nyɛ la Pɔntusi tingbaŋŋu nira, ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a Prisila. Ba daa pun yi la Italii tingbaŋŋu ni na, NaaKlɔdiyusi ŋɔn daa kari Yɛhudiyadima zaa ni ba yi ni Room tiŋŋa la zugu. Pɔla daa kyaŋŋi nti kaai ba,

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: ka naayi kpɛ ba yiri nti gbɛa; ka ŋɔn ni ban la'am n‑tumna, dama ba zaa daa seeri la gbana ni tantidima.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Vuusim Dabsiri zaa u daa kpeeri la Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu nti diri azama n‑tiri Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima zaa, m‑boori ni ba sakki n‑deaai yɛligu maa.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Sailasi ni Timɔti ŋɔn daa yi Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu na, Pɔla nini daa moaai la Naawunni yɛligu yɛla ni kɔkɔa, n‑wulisiri Yɛhudiyadima maa, ni, Yisa n siri nyɛ Naawunni ŋɔn tum sɔ na ni u ti di Masiiya la.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn daa kyiisi u, ka tuuri u maa, u daa pisigi u sutura n‑wulisi ba, ni, u za'asiri ba yɛla, ka yɛli “Tɔ, ka dizugu ya yi ti kpi, ya yɛla kpalim m‑bɛ ya mammaŋŋa zugu ni. Man yiisi n nuu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi din sumsi ni n tum seem n‑ti yarima maa ni. Pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna n ya n‑kyaŋŋi la ban pa Yɛhudiyadima saani.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Ka u daa gaari n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ yiri seaa kan daa pɔri la'aŋŋu duu maa ziiya; yiri maa daana yuuri n daa boonni Titiyusi Gyasitisi, u mun daa nyɛ la ŋɔn dɔli Naawunni soori hoo Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn dɔli seem la.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Di saha maa Krispusi - la'aŋŋu duu maa kpeema n‑daa la - daa ti niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda, ŋɔn ni u yiri dima zaa. Ka Kɔrinti dima maa pam ban daa wum yɛligu maa daa niŋi Naawunni yɛdda ka ba daa su ba Naawunni koom.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka Pɔla zaasi zaasimni u yuŋŋu goom puuni, ka daa yɛli u, “Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i. Kpalimma n‑tɔ'asira, di kyɛ yɛttɔ'a maa!

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: Dama m bɛ i saani ka sɔ ku toaai n‑zabi n‑sa'am i yɛla. Nyama! - m mari n niriba pam tiŋŋa wa puuni.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Dizugu ka Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ ni, n‑niŋi yuumni ni purigri, n‑wulisiri ba Naawunni yɛttɔ'a maa.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka di daa niŋi lala ka Galeoo daa di Akaaya tingbaŋŋu Gɔmna. Di saha maa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa la'am n‑zabi Pɔla ka daa zi u n‑kyaŋŋi kootu ni.

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: Ka yɛtti, ni, “Doo wa bɛlimni niriba n‑nyaŋŋna, ni, ba dɔli sɔseaa kan pa ti zaaligu soori n‑gyeemni Naawunni.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa ya n‑tɔ'asi sahanseaa la, Galeoo mun daa yɛli Yɛhudiyadima maa, ni, “Ya yi da galimni u la zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi taalli, bee, tumbeeri niŋbu zugu, di naa m‑mari soori ni n wum n‑ti ya.

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: Amaa di nyɛ la boosa zaŋŋi n‑kpa noori yɛttɔ'a ni, ni yuya, ni ya zaaligu wa yɛla - ay, ya mammansi maalim a! N ku sakki n‑leebi sarikatta anŋɔ yɛla zugu.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Ka daa kyɛ ka ba kari ba n‑yiisi kootu duu maa ni.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Ka ba zaa daa tuligi n‑zaŋŋi Sɔsitɛniisi - sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ la'aŋŋu duu la kpeema maa - m‑bu u kootu maa tooni. Amaa ka Galeoo ba'a daa ka lala maa ni.

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ Kɔrinti n‑niŋi dabsa ayi, ka naayi kyeoosi u mabiisi maa ka daa kyaŋŋi Kyɛnkyiriiya - tinseaa kan zɛ teeku gbiriŋŋu ni nla - nti kpɛ nyariŋŋu ni, n‑du ni u ti kyaŋŋi Siriiya tingbaŋŋu; ka Prisila ni Akwila daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi. Kyɛnkyirii maa ni ka u daa puni u zugu, ŋɔn daa pun pɔ noori la zugu.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑gaari nti paai Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa, ka u daa kyɛ ba bɛni. U mammaŋŋa daa kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ Yɛhudiyadima la'aŋŋu duu maa, ni u ti di azama n‑ti ba.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Ba daa susi u, ni, u kpalimma ba saani dabsa beela m‑paasi; amaa u daa bu sakki lala.

english_verse: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
translation_verse: U daa susi soori n‑kyeoosi ba ka yɛli, “Naawunni yi ti ti ma soori, n nin labi na.”, ka naayi bakki ka kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: N‑niŋi dabsa ayi bɛni, ka laa n‑yi tiŋŋa wa ni n‑gaari n‑dɔli tinseesi an bɛ Galɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni, ni Firigyii tingbaŋŋu, n‑kpamsiri Yisa nyaandɔliba maa zaa.

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dɔsɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Apɔloosi. Yɛhudiyadoo n daa nyɛ u, ka u daa yi Alɛkzandiriya tiŋŋa ni na, ka daa mari yɛm pam, halli nti mi Naawunni Bukku yɛla zaa. Ŋɔna daa ti paai Ɛfɛsusi di saha maa.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Ba daa pun wulisi u Naawunni soori, ka u daa tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa n‑wulisiri Yisa yɛla ŋɔn nyɛ seem maa deede, amaa u daa mi la Gyɔni kɔsuubu kɔkɔa.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Ka u daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri ni nimmoaa la'aŋŋu duu maa puuni. Ka Akwila ni Prisila ŋɔn daa ti wum u yɛttɔ'a maa, ba daa booli u ni, ni, u kyam ba saani na, ka daa wulisi u, m‑paasi u Naawunni soori maa yɛla suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka Apɔloosi daa boori ni u bakki n‑kyaŋŋi Akaaya tingbaŋŋu maa ni. Ka ti mabiisi daa kpamsi u, ka daa sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ba mabiseeba ban bɛ bɛni maa, ni, ba kpaŋŋma ba mansi n‑deaai u. Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai bɛni, u daa la'am ni ninvugseeba Naawunni ŋɔn pun kyɛ ka ba niŋi u yɛdda maa, ka daa suŋŋi ba pam.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Dama Yɛhudiyadima ŋɔn daa ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni sahanseaa la, u daa zaŋŋi Naawunni Bukku yɛla n‑tɔ'asi n‑nyaŋŋi ba niriba tooni, n‑wulisiri, ni, Naawunni Masiiya maa nyɛ la Yisa.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Apɔloosi ŋɔn daa bɛ Kɔrinti tiŋŋa la, Pɔla daa dɔli sɔseaa kan daa du tingbaŋŋu puuni la, nti paai Ɛfɛsusi na. Bɛni ka u daa paai Yisa nyaandɔliseeba,

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: ka daa boosi ba, ni, “Ammi, ya daa deaai Sii Kasi maa, yan daa niŋi yɛdda sahaseaa la?” Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ay! - ti na zi n‑wum Sii Kasi yɛla.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa boosi ba n‑yɛli, “Ka Naawunni kɔsuubu bɔ ka ya daa deaai?” Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Gyɔni kɔsuubu.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Pɔla daa yɛliya, ni, “Gyɔni kɔsuubu gbinni n daa nyɛ la: ni, ninvugsɔ yi takki u beebu n‑kyɛ tumbeeri tuma zaa ka ŋmaligi n‑teesi teesisuma; amaa Gyɔni mammaŋŋa daa yɛli niriba maa, ni, ba niŋma yɛdda nti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nin dɔli u nyaaŋŋa la, ka ŋɔna n nyɛ Yisa.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa ti wum dinŋɔa, ba daa su Naawunni koom maa, ti Dugma Yisa yuuri ni.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa ba zugri, ka Naawunni Sii maa daa paali ba na, ka ba daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'azuna balibubalibu, ni anabitiri yɛla.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Ba zaa daa nyɛ la kaman dɔppa piiya ni ayi.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ bɛni halli gɔya ata saha, ka daa kpeeri la'aŋŋu duu maa ni n‑tɔ'asiri ni sufukpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam beebu, ni u ti kyɛ ka ba sakki u yɛla maa.
