english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Amaa baseeba mun daa mari la zugkpeeŋŋu, ka daa za'asiri yɛdda niŋbu ka sa'amni Yisa soori maa yɛla paalu ni. Dizugu ka Pɔla daa kyɛ ba, ka daa zaŋŋi baseeba ban daa dɔli Yisa nyaaŋŋa maa n‑wɔligi n‑yi ba saani. Ka beoo kam ba daa kpeeri Tiranusi kariŋŋu duu la ni, n‑tɔ'asiri ka diri azama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa yɛla.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Ba daa niŋni nŋɔa, halli yuuma ayi, ka Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu dima zaa, Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima gba, daa ti wum ti Dugma yɛligu maa.

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Lala saha maa ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka Pɔla daa tumni alahazibu tunkara pam.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Ba daa zaŋŋi kpakyeesa ni antikyidima an daa siisi u, n‑zi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑ti baaridima ka ba nya alaafɛa, ka arizimbeeri daa zɔ ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa gyinni n‑katti arizimbeeri n‑yiisiri niriba ni maa daa mɔya ni ba zaŋŋi Yisa yuuri n‑katti ba. Ba daa yi yɛnni, ni, “Yisa sɔ Pɔla ŋɔn moonni u yɛla la - ŋɔna yuuri zugu ka n yɛli, ni, i yim na!”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Ba sunsukkuni seeba n daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyabiisi ayɔpɔi, seeba ban daa nyɛ Sikyiiva - gyeeŋŋu toondaansɔ - biisi.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Amaa arizimbeoo maa daa labsi n‑yɛli, “M mi Yisa, ka mi Pɔla gba, amaa bandima n nyɛ yarima?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Ka dɔsɔ arizinni ŋɔn daa bɛ u ni maa daa gbaai ba ni n‑nyaŋŋi ba zaa ka pa ba dansi, ka ba naayi zɔ n‑yi yiri maa ni n‑kyaŋŋi yɔri hoo ban daa dɔ'ai ba seem.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima ban daa bɛ Ɛfɛsusi la zaa ŋɔn daa ti wum lahabaari kanŋɔa, dabeem daa gbaai ba, ka ba daa tisi ti Dugma Yisa yuuri gyirima pam.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Ka di saha ka niriba pam ban daa niŋi yɛdda daa kyɛ na n‑yɛli ba taalli ni ba tumbeeri yɛla paalu ni.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Lala daa kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛligu maa daa kyɛnni tooni m‑paari niriba pam, ka mari yiiko.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ka Pɔla daa gbaai kyɛnni, ni u ti dɔli Masidoonya ni Akaaya tingbana, ka gaari halli nti paai Gyɛrusalɛm. Ni, “N yi kyaŋŋi n kyɛnni wa n‑naai, see ka n kyaŋŋi Room tiŋŋa gba.”

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: U daa kpalim Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu n‑yuui beela m‑paasi, ka daa tum u suŋŋniba ayi - Timɔti ni Ɛrasitusi - ni ba daŋŋi u tooni n‑kyaŋŋi Masidoonya.

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi lala saha maa ka daaŋŋu pam daa doaai niriba maa ni, Yisa soori maa zugu.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Anzulifa mankyeerisɔ n daa bɛ ni, ka u yuuri boonni Dimitiriyusi la. U daa maanni buga gyeeŋŋu neema n‑tiri ba bugseaa kan yuuri boonni Atɛmisa la, ka daa ŋmanni la pɔ'a. Ka lala tuma zugu ka anzulifa tuma baŋŋniba daa ti nya ligri pam.

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ŋɔna Dimitiriyusi daa booli baseeba zaa ban daa tumni lala tuma taabu maa, ni, ba la'amma na, ka yɛli ba, “N zɔdima! ya mi halli, ni, ti nyari arizikki pam ti tuma wa zugu.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Ka ya nyari ka wumni doo wa, Pɔla, yɛla: u tɔ'asiri n‑nyaŋŋi niriba pam Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa wa puuni, ni Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu zaa. U yɛtti, ni, ninsaala ŋɔn maali wunseesi pa la Naawunni ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ba sakkri lala n‑ti u.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Dizugu di pa seem ni ti tuma wa nin ti kɔŋŋi ka yusuŋŋu; ka din paasi lala zugu, ti gyeeŋŋu duu maa, ni ti bugri tibsigu daana Atɛmisa sɔ ŋɔn bɛ ka puuni la gba nin ti kɔŋŋi gyirima. Pampaŋŋɔ ka Ɛseaa dima zaa - halli duniya wa dima zaa gba - gyeemni u ni. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ba nin ti tam u yɛla, Pɔla yɛttɔ'a zugu!”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Ban daa wum dinŋɔa, ba sufu daa yisigiya, ka ba daa piili n‑tasiri pampam, ni, “Ɛfɛsusi dima bugri! Atɛmisa, yiiko daana!”

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Ka di daa bu yuui ka tiŋŋa maa zaa daa damni n‑vuura. Niriba maa daa gbaai Gayusi ni Arisitaakusi, baseeba ban daa dɔli Pɔla n‑yi Masidoonya ni na la, ka daa vaai n‑guui hoo ninvugyinni nti paai deema ziiya n‑kpɛ bɛni.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Pɔla daa boori la u yi daadam maa tooni na, amaa u karimbiisi daa bu ti u soori.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Halli bɛni tinkpamma maa seeba gba ban daa di Pɔla zoori daa tum u saani na n‑susiri u, ni, u di kpɛ deema zii maa ni.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: La'aŋŋu maa daa mari la nangbanwala. Seeba n daa tasiri la yɛlli yinni, ka seeba mun tasiri yɛliseaa. Ka niriba maa pam daa bu mi la'aŋŋu wa gbinni ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Yɛhudiyadima maa mun daa kyɛ ka Alɛkzanda kyaŋŋi tooni, ka zama maa puuni ninvugseeba daa tasiri n‑yɛtti u ŋɔn nin niŋi seem. U daa zaŋŋi u nuusi n‑wulisi ba, ni, ba kyɛli ba vuuri ka u tɔ'asi n‑wulisi ba ŋɔn ni u taaba ŋɔn ka taalli seem.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Amaa ban daa ti baŋŋi, ni, u nyɛ la Yɛhudiya nira, ba zaa daa la'am n‑tasiri, ni, “Ɛfɛsusi dima bugri! Atɛmisa, yiiko daana!”; ka kpalim n‑tasiri lala halli nti yuui hoo hawa ayi saha.

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka tiŋŋa maa Sakatiri daa naayi kyɛ ka ba sini ba vuuri, ka yɛli, ni, “Ya Ɛfɛsusi dima, wumma! Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa wa puuni ka ti guri la Atɛmisa yiikodaana, ni u gyeeŋŋu duu maa, ni u ba'akugri kan daa yi arizana n‑sigi na la. Duniya wa zaa puuni, ninvugsɔ m bɛ ni ŋɔn bu baŋŋi lala? Ay! - sɔ ka ni.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔ zugu, yɛla anŋɔ zɛya ka sɔ ku toaai n‑kyiisi a wa; di bu sum ni ya fɔ surimm? - ka ku gyigi n‑niŋi seelli.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Ya zi niriba wa n‑kyɛ kpa na, amaa ba bu zuuri gyeeŋŋu duri neema, ba mun bu tuuri ka galimni ti bugri maa.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dizugu Dimitiriyusi ni u tuntuntaaba yi nya la taaliseaa sɔ zaa saani, kootudima m bɛ ni, ni gɔmnadima gba bɛ ni: ba nin toaai n‑galim lala nira ba saani.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Di yi ti nyɛ la yɛttɔ'aseaa m‑paasi ka ya boori ya yiisi paalu, di sumsi ni ya mari ka na tiŋŋa maa la'aŋŋu dabsisuŋŋu maa daari.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Din nyɛ seem maa, di bu ya'ai ni ba zaŋŋi ti yɛttɔ'a n‑kyaŋŋi n‑galim ti, zuna daaŋŋu yɛla maa zugu. Di yi ti bɛ lala, ti ku toaai n‑wulisi din taasi zuna yɛttɔ'a maa na, dama daliiri suŋŋu din taasi ka na ka ni.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa yɛli nŋɔ n‑naai, u daa kyɛ ka zama maa doaai n‑wurigi n‑kuli.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka niriba daaŋŋu maa ŋɔn daa naai la, Pɔla daa booli Yisa nyaandɔliba maa na, ni u ti kpamsi ba. Ka ŋɔn daa kpamsi ba n‑naai, u daa kyeoosi ba ka daa kpa n‑wa Masidoonya.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: U daa gɔ n‑dɔli bɛni tinsi maa zaa n‑tɔ'asiri pam ka kpamsiri niriba, halli nti paai Griika tingbaŋŋu,

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: n‑daa bɛ bɛni gɔya ata. U daa ti ya kyaŋŋi Siriiya, amaa lala saha maa ka Yɛhudiyadima daa gbaai gbaari, ni, ba ti daam u, ka dizugu u daa ŋmaligi n‑labi n‑dɔli Masidoonya.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli u n‑kyaŋŋi la daa yi la u tuma tingbana zaa ni na; ba mun yuya nŋɔa: Pirusi bii Soopata la, sɔ ŋɔn daa yi Bɛriiya ni na; Arisitaakusi ni Sɛkundusi mun yi Tɛsalɔnika ni na; Gayusi mun yi Dɛribee ni - m‑paasi Timɔti zugu; Tikyikusi ni Tirɔfimusi mun yi Ɛseaa ni na.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Banŋɔ zaa daa daŋŋi tooni n‑guri ti, Tirɔ'asi tiŋŋa maa ni.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Tirima mun daa yuui beela, halli ka Boroboropaalli Kyuu daa ti gaari pɔi, ka ti naayi kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑yi Filipaai tiŋŋa; ka daba anu nyaaŋŋa ti daa ti paai ba la Tirɔ'asi na. Ti daa bɛ bɛni dabsa ayɔpɔi.

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Ka Alahadaari ŋɔn daa ti paai, ti daa la'am taaba ni ti puri taaba paanu. Pɔla daa tɔ'asi n‑ti niriba maa n‑yuui, halli ni yuntasuusi, dama u daa boori ni, beoo yi ti neaai, u nin bakki.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Ti daa la'am la gyirambisa duseaa ni, ka firiladima pam n daa bɛ bɛni;

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: ka dizugu goom daa mari na'akyimbilisɔ ŋɔn yuuri daa boonni Yutikusi. U daa zinni n‑tabli la takoro ni. Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri n‑yuui maa, Yutikusi daa gbisi n‑zɔkki, n‑yi gyirambisa maa, kan tɔm buta zugu, n‑lu tiŋŋa na. Ba daa sigi na n‑kpikki u, ni, u kpiya.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla mun daa sigi na n‑dooni bii maa zugu n‑gɔbi u, ka yɛli ba, “Ya di kyɛ ka dabeem mari ya. U nɛ ni maa!”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Ka ti daa laa n‑du saazugu, nti ŋmaai paanu m‑puri n‑ti taaba n‑di; Ka Pɔla daa laa n‑gbaai u yɛttɔ'a maa, halli ka beoo ti neaai, ka naayi bakki.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Na'akyimbila la muna - ba daa mari u n‑kuli ka u mari alaafɛa; ka di daa niŋi ba sufumaasim pampam.

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Tɔ; Pɔla daa pun yɛli, ni, u ya kyaŋŋi la nɔba m‑paai Asɔsi tiŋŋa, ka dizugu tirima mun daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni ni ti ti tuusi u.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai ti Asɔsi na maa, u daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa na ka ti daa du n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Mitiliini tiŋŋa.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa paai Kyiyɔsi tingbaŋŋu zii sa. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti laa n‑neaai ti daa lɔŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Samɔsi ya'ari: n‑neaai beoo yaasa ka ti daa paai Miliitusi tiŋŋa.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Pɔla daa boori ni u ti gaari yooma m‑paai Gyɛrusalɛm, ka Pɛntikoosi Kyuu dabsa ku ti gaari; dizugu u daa bu boori ni u ti yuui Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni, see ka u kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni n‑yakki n‑gaari Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa.

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu ŋɔn daa bɛ Miliitusi maa, u daa tum Ɛfɛsusi m‑booli Yisa nyaandɔliba kpamma maa na.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Ban daa ti paai u na, u daa yɛli ba, ni, “Ya mi halli m beesigu ŋɔn daa bɛ seem, man daa bɛ ya saani sahanseaa la; man daa piili m‑bɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu maa ni halli nti yuui.

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: N daa gyeemni Naawunni ni mansigsigu, ni kunkuma; halli ni Yɛhudiyadima ban daa boori ni ba tum ma tumbeeri zaa.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Ya mi, ni, n daa bu kɔŋŋi wa'azu yiisigu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseelli kam din nin suŋŋi ya, ka wulisiri a n‑tiri ya paalu ni, ka laa n‑gyinni yiri yiri n‑tɔ'asiri maa.

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: N yɛli Yɛhudiyadima ni Griika dima zaa, ni, ba ŋmaligma n‑kyɛ Naawunni saani na, ka niŋi ti Dugma Yisa yɛdda.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ ka Sii Kasi m mari ma n‑wa Gyɛrusalɛm: m mun bu mi din ya n‑niŋi ma bɛni.

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Man mi seaa nyɛ la, ni, tiŋŋa kam zaa puuni ka Naawunni Vuusim Kasi maa yɛtti, ni, m baŋŋma, ni, n nin nya sarika duu yɛla ni wahala.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Amaa m ba'a ka m maŋtoori nyɔvuri maa ni, see ka n dɔli n beebu soori nti paai ka naari; ka ti tum tunseaa ti Dugma Yisa ŋɔn zaali ma zaa, halli nti wulisi Naawunni piinisuma lahabaari ŋɔn nyɛ seem zaa.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ m mi, ni, ya sɔ ku ti laa n‑nya ma yaasa; ya saani ka n daa bɛ ni n‑gyinni m‑moonni Naawunni naam yɛla n‑ti ya zaa.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Di zugu n yɛli ya zuna, ni, ninsaala kam kum taalli bu dɔli ma.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Halli m bu kɔŋŋi ni m mooli Naawunni ŋɔn teesi, ni, u nin niŋi seem zaa n‑ti ya.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Dizugu, m Badima, n susiri ya, ni, ya gɔsim ya mansi, ka laa n‑guri ninvugseeba Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑niŋi ya nuusi ni ni ya gɔsiri ba maa, hoo ban guri peesi seem mooni la. Naawunni niriba n nyɛ ba, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u maŋtoori ziim n‑yɔ seeba samni, ni ba ti nya nyɔvuri.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: M mi halli, ni, n yi ti bakki, di nin ti bɛ kaman kunduna putoo dima nin ti kpɛ na, ka ba ku ti zɔ bunkɔbri maa namboo halli beela.

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Di pa seem, halli yarima puuni gba seeba nin ti doaai n‑ŋmaligi yɛlimaŋni maa ka kyɛ ka nyaandɔliba maa ti dɔli ba m‑burigi n‑yi sɔsuŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya guusima sahakam! Ya teaama n yɛla, ni, yuuma ata an gaari wa n daa bu kyɛ ya kpamsigu ni nintam, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka pampaŋŋɔ n nin zaŋŋi ya n‑niŋi Naawunni nuusi ni, ni u piinisuma yɛla an nin toaai m‑maali ya yɛla suŋŋa, ka kyɛ ka ya di ya faari tɔrli Naawunni mammantoori niriba zaa sunsukkuni.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: N kɔrisi daa ka sɔsɔ ligri ni, bee, u sutura ni.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Ya zaa mi, ni, n daa tumni n nuusi tuma wa, ni n ti ku m bukaata zaa, ni n taaba dinni gba.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa niŋi lala, ni n ti wulisi ya, ni, di sumni ni ya gba tumni lala ka di nin kyɛ ka ya nin toaai n‑suŋŋni ban ka yaa la. Yan di tam ti Dugma Yisa yɛligu la, ni, ‘piini tisibu mari anfaani n‑gaari piini deaari.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ŋɔn daa yɛli nŋɔ n‑naai, u daa gbaani tiŋŋa n‑susi Naawunni, ŋɔna ni ban zaa.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Ka ba zaa daa kumna, ka gɔbri u m‑puusiri u suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Din daa niŋi ba sufusa'aŋŋu pam daa nyɛ la ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa, ni, ba ku laa n‑nya u nindaa yaasa. Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa beeli u m‑paai nyariŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka tin daa ti basi ba maa, ti daa kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi kyippi m‑paai Koosi tiŋŋa. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa kyaŋŋi la Roodisi tiŋŋa, n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Patara tiŋŋa.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka ti daa nya nyarinseaa kan wa Fɔnisiiya, ka dinzugu ti daa kpɛ ka ni.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Ti daa nya Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu n‑kyɛ ka ti nuzaa ya'ari ka gaari n‑kyaŋŋi Siriiya nti sigi Taaya tiŋŋa maa ni. Bɛni ka ba daa ti ya yiisi nyariŋŋu maa neema.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Ka ti dabsa ŋɔn daa ti naai maa, ba daa yi na ni ba zaali ti soori. Nyaandɔliba maa zaa ni ba pɔ'aba ni biisi daa beeli ti, halli nti yi tiŋŋa maa kpaŋŋa, ka ti daa gbaani teeku duuri n‑susi wunsusigu,

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: ka daa kyeoosi taaba bɛni. Ka tirima naayi kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ka ba mun daa labi n‑kuli.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Ti daa yi Taaya n‑du m‑paai Tɔlimeesi tiŋŋa, n‑sigi m‑puusi ti mabiisi la, ka daa bɛ ba saani dabsiri yinni.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa laa n‑du m‑paai Sizarii tiŋŋa nti kpɛ Filipi yiri, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ tuntumniba ayɔpɔi la ni yinni, ka daa yiisiri lahabasuŋŋu maa wa'azu.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Ti daa bɛ ba saani n‑yuui beela pɔi, ka anabisɔ ŋɔn yuuri boonni Agabusi daa yi Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu ni n‑kyɛ na.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa kyɛ ti saani na n‑zaŋŋi Pɔla sɛlooriga n‑lɔ u mammaŋŋa gbaya ni u nuusi; ka yɛli, ni, “Naawunni Sii Kasi yɛliya: ‘Lala ka Yɛhudiyaseeba ban bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm maa nin ti lɔ sɛlooriga wa daana, n‑zaŋŋi u n‑niŋi u ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa nuusi ni.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tin daa wum nŋɔa, tin ni ti niriba ban daa bɛ bɛni maa zaa daa bɛlim Pɔla, ni, u di kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla daa yɛli ti, di saha maa, ni, “Wula zugu ka ya niŋni lala? Ya kumni n‑sa'amni la n sufu. Man mun niŋi siri ni n kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm ka ba ti zaŋŋi ma n‑lɔ - halli ba yi ku ma gba - n Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ka tin daa kpamsiri u n‑gyɛ maa, ti daa sini ka yɛli, “Naawunni yuubu niŋma!”

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka lala dabsa ŋɔn daa ti naai, ti daa sɛ'asi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Ka nyaandɔliseeba ban bɛ Sizariiya la daa dɔli ti n‑zi ti n‑kyaŋŋi Mineesɔn yiri, ni ti kpɛ u saani - u nyɛ la Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu nira ka daa pun yuui Yisa dɔligu ni.

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ti daa paai Gyɛrusalɛm na, ka ti mabiisi maa daa deaai ti ni sufumaasim.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Pɔla daa zi ti n‑kpɛ Gyeemsi saani ka kpamma maa zaa gba daa la'am m‑bɛ bɛni.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ban daa wum lala, ba daa pɛ'ai Naawunni, ka laa n‑yɛli Pɔla, ni, “Ti mabiiya, i bu nya? Niriba tusatusa bɛ la ti saani ban nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima ka niŋi Yisa yɛdda. Ka banŋɔ zaa nyɛ la ban boori ni ba na kpalim n‑dɔli ba zaaligu kaari maa.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ba wum i yɛla maa, ni, i wulisiri Yɛhudiyaseeba ban bɛ tinzuna tinsi zaa ni, ni, ba kyɛli Musa zaaligu yɛla; yaasa, ni, i yɛtti ba, ni, ba di laa n‑guni ba biisi n‑dɔli kaari maa.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Dizugu ti ya niŋi la wula? Ba nin baasi n‑wum, ni, i kyɛ kpa na.

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Tɔ; wumma! Ti nin wulisi i, nyin nin niŋi seem. Ti mari la ti dɔppa anaasi ban pun dɔli ti kaari m‑pɔ noori.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna zaŋŋma lala niriba maa ka sɛ'asi i maŋŋa kasikasi n‑dɔli ba n‑yɔ ba nangbampɔri sama zaa n‑ti ba, ka ba nin toaai m‑puni ba zugri ka naayi naai ba yɛla zaa. Ka dina nin kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa baŋŋi, ni, ban yɛtti yɛttɔ'aseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i yɛla maa, ni, lala yɛttɔ'a maa ka gbinni, amaa i mammaŋŋa gba tumni la kaari ni ta'ara ŋɔn zaali seem maa.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima ka niŋni Yisa yɛdda muna - ti daa pun sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ba n‑yɛli tin daa gbaai seem, ni, ba mari ba mansi suŋŋa ka di ŋɔbi numda'aseesi ban kɔrigi m‑maanni ba'a ni buga la, ni seesi an bu kɔrigi, bee, an na mari ziim: ka di tum tumbeeri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔgoora ni pɔ'agoora yɛla.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, Pɔla siri daa zaŋŋi niriba maa ka daa sɛ'asi u maŋŋa; ka ŋɔn ni ban zaa daa kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ni u yɛli n‑ti maalimaanniba n‑zaali ba mansɛ'asigu naari dabsiri maa, di nyɛ la dabsiseaa ban ya maali maaligu maa ba zaasa zugu n‑naai ba nangbampɔri yɛla.

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dabsa ayɔpɔi maa daari ŋɔn daa ti paari na, Yɛhudiyaseeba ban yi Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni na daa ti nya Pɔla, ka u bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni. Ka ba daa dam zama maa ka daa gbaai u.

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Ka tasiri n‑yɛtta, “Yarim Izraɛli dima, ya ku suŋŋi ti? Doo wa n nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn wulisiri niriba ya'ari kam, ka tuuri ti butiri, ni ti zaaligu, ni ti Yiri wa! Halli u zi la Griika dima gba n‑kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri wa na, n‑kyɛ ka ka da'ai zaa.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Ba daa pun nya Tirɔfimusi - Ɛfɛsusi nira la - Pɔla saani, ka ba bɛ la timpuuni; lala zugu ka ba daa ti teesi, ni, ŋɔna ka Pɔla zi n‑kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa na.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Ka tiŋŋa maa zaa daa doaai, ka niriba maa daa zɔ n‑la'am n‑gbaai Pɔla n‑darigi u n‑yiisi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ka daa yɔ Yiri maa za'anɔya yoomyooma.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa moori ni ba ku u sahanseaa la, lala lahabaari daa paai langyima kpeema maa saani, ni, Gyɛrusalɛm zaa bɛ la zaba ni.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Di saha u daa doaai ya'abi n‑zaŋŋi langyima ni ba toondaanseeba n‑zɔ n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai bɛni. Ka zama maa ŋɔn daa ti nya kpeema maa ni langyimadima maa, ba daa kyɛ Pɔla buubu.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Ka kpeema maa daa kyɛ ka ba gbaai u, n‑lɔ u kutti ayi. Ka daa boosi, ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nyɛ sɔa, ni ŋɔn niŋi tumbɛseelli.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Ka zama maa seeba daa tasiri n‑yɛtti kookooba ka daa bu yɛtti yɛlli yinni, ka u daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑baŋŋi di gbinni ni yɛlimaŋni, ba vuuri maa zugu. Ka dizugu u daa yɛli, ni, ba zaŋŋma u m‑mari n‑kyɛ Baarikyi yiri maa na.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti paai dɔliga maa ni, langyima maa daa kpikki u n‑zi, niriba ŋɔn kpa'ari n‑daamni seem la zugu.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Ka zama maa daa kur dɔli ba n‑tasiri pampam, ni, “Ya kyɛ ka u baŋŋi!”

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban daa ti ya zaŋŋi Pɔla n‑kpeesi Baarikyi yiri maa ni, u daa boosi kpeema maa n‑yɛli, ni, “Soori bɛ ni, ni, n yɛli seelli n‑ti i?”; ka u daa labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “I wum la Griiktiri, fa?

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti u soori maa, Pɔla daa zaani dɔliga maa saazugu ka wukki u nuu n‑gaasi n‑wulisi zama maa; ka din daa fɔ surimm, u daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a ba maŋtoori yɛttɔ'a, Hibruuli, ni; n‑yɛtti, ni,

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “M Badima, ya wumma! M mabiisi, ya wumma! N ya tɔ'asi n‑wulisi n yɛla yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ban daa ti wum ka u tɔ'asiri ba maŋtoori yɛttɔ'a n‑tiri ba maa, ba daa kyɛ ba vuuri ka fɔ surimm. Ka Pɔla daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli ba, ni,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “N nyɛ la Yɛhudiyadoowa, ka ba daa dɔ'ai ma Taasusi, Silisii tingbaŋŋu ni; amaa Gyɛrusalɛm wa ka ba daa mari ma ka n zoaai na. Kpa wa ka n karimba Gameeliyɛli daa wulisi ma; n daa bamsi ti yaadima yɛla zaa ni nimbirimoaa, ka n daa zaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni hoo yarim ban la'am la kpa zuna wa ŋɔn zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑tiri u seem la.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Man daa namsiri la ninvugseeba ban dɔli Yisa soori wa, halli nti ku baseeba. N daa gbaasi ba, dɔppa ni pɔ'aba, n‑zaŋŋi ba n‑niŋi dansarika duu ni.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: Ya yi ti boosi ya Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa, ni kpamma la'aŋŋu dima zaa, ba nin sakki, ni, n daa niŋi lala. Halli ka ba mammansi daa sɔbi gbana n‑ti ma, ni, m mari n‑kyaŋŋi n‑tisi ti mabiseeba ban daa bɛ Damasikusi tiŋŋa la, ni, n gbaam Yisa niriba maa n‑zi n‑kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na, ka ba darigi ba tubri.”

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: Ni, “Man ŋɔn daa ti wa bɛni maa ka daa ti pɔtti tiŋŋa maa ni, wuntaŋŋa agbaa saha, neesim pam daa nyakki n‑yi saazugu na n‑gyiligi ma.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: N daa lu la tiŋŋa, ka wum ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ma, ni, ‘Sɔla, Sɔla, bɔ zugu ka i namsiri ma?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa boosi n‑yɛli, ‘Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?’, ka u labsi n‑yɛli ma, ni, ‘Mani Yisa, Nazarɛti nira la: mani ka i namsiri maa.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli ma maa daa nya neesim maa, amaa ba daa bu wum kukɔri maa ŋɔn tɔ'asi ma seem.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, ‘Ka dinŋɔna, n Dugma, n niŋma wula?’; ka u yɛli, ‘Doaam saazugu n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Damasikusi. Ba nin ti yɛli i man pun gbaai, ni, i niŋi seem.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: N daa zoomiya, neesim ŋɔn daa neaai pampam la zugu, ka n nyaandɔliba maa daa dari ma n‑kyaŋŋi nti paai Damasikusi.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Ananayasi daa kyɛ n saani na - bɛni dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa dɔli Naawunni zaaligu ni nimmoaa nla, ka tiŋŋa maa Yɛhudiyadima zaa daa tiri u gyirima.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: U daa kpɛ na n‑zaani n‑tabli ma, ka yɛli, ‘M mabii Sɔla, neaam i nini n‑nyara!’ Di saha maa n daa neaai n nini n‑wukki n‑gɔsi u.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u daa yɛli ma, ni, ‘Naawunni, sɔ ti yaadima maa ŋɔn daa gyeemni la - ŋɔn Naawunni n yiisi i, ni i ti baŋŋi ŋɔn boori seem, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi u Yɛlimaŋni Daana n‑wulisi i, ka kyɛ ka i wum yɛttɔ'a u mammaŋŋa noori ni.

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna bɔ zugu ka i laa n‑guusira? Doaam saazugu nti su Naawunni koom: boolim u yuuri ka u peaai i tumbeeri da'ari zaa m‑basi.’ ”

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: ka n daa nya, ka Naawunni daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ma, ni, ‘Doaam yooma n‑yi Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa wa ni, dama kpa wa niriba ku sakki n‑niŋi i yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n yɛla maa yɛdda.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: Ka n daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ‘N Dugma, ba mi halli, ni, n daa kpeeri la'aŋŋu duri ni n‑gbaasiri ninvugseeba ban niŋi i yɛdda maa, ka kpatti ba duu ni ka buuri ba.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: M‑paasi, ba mi, ni, ban daa ku i sɛharadaana Sitiivin sahanseaa la, ni, n daa bɛ bɛni n‑kpamsiri ba ka guri ba neema.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Amaa u daa yɛli ma, ni, ‘Kur kyɛma! Dama n nin tum i, halli ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa saani.’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka zama maa daa kur wumsiri la Pɔla noori, halli ka u ti yɛli lala maa; amaa di saha ka ba daa gbaai tasibu n‑yɛtti, ni, “Ya mari u n‑kyaŋŋi! N‑ku u gba! Lala nira bu sumsi ni duniya wa beebu!”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Ba daa damni la ba mansi, n‑gba'atiri ba neema, ka yeenni tanni.

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu langyima kpeema maa daa yɛli u niriba, ni, ba mari Pɔla n‑kpɛ ba Baarikyi yiri nti boosi u boosa ka feebi u, ni ba ti baŋŋi daliiriseaa din kyɛ ka Yɛhudiyadima maa zeesiri ba mansi n‑niŋi u lala.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Amaa ban daa loori u feebgu zii maa ni sahanseaa la, u daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ba toondaansɔ ŋɔn daa zɛ bɛni maa, ni, “Ammi, ya nin zaŋŋi Room suulinsi nira n‑feebi? Halli ka bu kari u yɛttɔ'a maa sariya m‑baŋŋi u taalli - di mari soori?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Ka toondaana maa ŋɔn daa ti wum lala maa, u daa kyaŋŋi langyima kpeema maa saani nti boosi u, ni, “Bɔ ka i ya n‑niŋi? Doo maa nyɛ la Room suulinsi nira!”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Ka kpeema maa daa kyaŋŋi Pɔla saani nti boosi u n‑yɛli, “Room suulinsi nira n siri nyɛ i?”; ka u labsi n‑yɛli u ni, “Ii!”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Ka kpeema maa yɛli u, “Man daa yɔ la ligri pam ka naayi deaai lala suulinsi beebu maa.”; ka Pɔla labsi n‑yɛli u, ni “Man muna - ba daa dɔ'ai ma la lala suulinsi ni.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Dinzugu langyinseeba ban ya boosi u boosa la daa labi ba nyaaŋŋa, ka dabeem daa gbaai ba kpeema maa, dama u daa pun lɔ doo maa, ka naayi baŋŋi, ni, Room suulinsi nira n nyɛ u.

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka langyima kpeema maa daa kpalim m‑boori ni u baŋŋi yɛttɔ'a maa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni wula zugu ka Yɛhudiyadima daa ya galimni u. Ka dizugu beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai u daa yiisi Pɔla na, ka zaali noori, ni, gyeeŋŋu toondaandima la'asima ni kpamma zaa, ka kyɛ ka Pɔla kpɛ na n‑zaani ba tooni.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Pɔla ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u nini n‑kpa kpamma la'aŋŋu dima maa, u daa gbaai u yɛttɔ'a n‑yɛli, “M mabiisi, n nyɔvuri puuni halli ni zuna, m beesigu nyɛ la bunkasi Naawunni tooni.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana Ananayasi daa yɛli ban zɛya la, ni, ba ŋmeem u noori u yɛlisim maa zugu.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli “Naawunni nin ti ŋmɛ i gba! I ŋmani la hoo goonkuriseaa ban zaŋŋi titaari n‑ta, ka ka gɔsibu veela, amaa ka puuni bu zaani suŋŋa. I zinni n‑katti ma sariya Naawunni zaaligu zugu, ka i mammaŋŋa sa'amni zaaligu maa ka yɛtti, ni, ba ŋmeem ma!”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa zɛya la daa yɛli u, ni, “Ammi, i tuuri la Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa lala?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Ka u labsi n‑yɛli ba, “M mabiisi, ya gaafara! N da bu mi, ni, Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana n nyɛ u. Ba daa bu sɔbi lala, Naawunni yɛligu maa ni? Ni, ‘I di yɛli yɛttɔ'abeoo n‑tiri ŋɔn su i butiri fukumsi.’ ”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Pɔla daa baŋŋiya, ni, kpamma maa purigri daa dɔli la Saddusii soori, ka seeba mun dɔli Farisii dinni. Ka dizugu u daa tasi pampam la'aŋŋu maa puuni n‑yɛli, “M mabiisi, ya wumma! N nyɛ la Farisii doo, ka m badima gba daa dɔli Farisii soori maa. Yɛttɔ'aseaa tin la'amni na ni ti tɔ'asi nyɛ la yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man mari tama seelli kum yisigri ni.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala maa, Farisii dima ni Saddusidima maa nɔya daa wɔliya, ka la'aŋŋu maa purigi buyi.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Nangbankpeemni wa gbinni nyɛ la nŋɔa: Saddusidima daa yɛtti, ni, ninvugseeba ban kpi la ku laa n‑yisigi, bee, n‑neaai kum ni; ni siisi ni malaakadima yɛla pa la yɛlimaŋni: Farisii dima mun daa sakkri n‑tiri kum yisigri, ni siisi ni malaakadima yɛla zaa.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Ka dinzugu vuuri pam daa doaaya, ka seeba ban daa nyɛ zaaligu karimbadima ka bɛ la Farisii dima ya'ari la daa yi paalu na n‑yɛtti, ni, “Ti bu nya doo wa taaliseaa; di bu ya'ai malaaka bee siiya n tɔ'asi n‑ti u maa.”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Sugyeaa maa daa paasiri ni, ka dabeem daa gbaai langyima kpeema maa, ni, ba nin bu Pɔla n‑toosi u; ka dizugu u daa yɛli u langyimadima maa, ni, ba kpeem na n‑gbaai Pɔla n‑yiisi n‑yi daadam maa sunsukkuni, ka mari u n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi Baarikyi yiri ni.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dirindaari maa, yuŋŋu, ka ti Dugma daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi Pɔla, ka yɛli, “Mari i dablim maa! Nyin pun tɔ'asi n yɛla suŋŋa Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni seem la, lala ka i nin laa n‑tɔ'asi Room timpuuni gba.”

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dizugu ba daa kyaŋŋi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ni kpamma maa saani nti yɛli, “Tirima la'am taaba m‑pɔ noori, ni, ti ku laa n‑lam diibu, na'ala ti ku la Pɔla.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna see ka yarima ni kpamma maa zaa ti susi langyima kpeema maa, ni, ya ya maali n‑viisi doo maa yɛttɔ'a maa suŋŋa, ka dizugu ba mari u n‑kyɛ ya saani na. Tirima muna nin maali siri ni ti ku u pɔi ka u ti paai kpa na.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla tinzɔpɔ'a bisɔ daa wum ban gbaari seem maa, ka daa kyaŋŋi Baarikyi yiri maa ni, nti kpɛ n‑yɛli Pɔla.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Ka di saha Pɔla daa booli langyima toondaansɔ na nti yɛli u, ni, u zaŋŋma bii wa n‑kpɛ kpeema maa saani, ni, u mari la lahabaseaa ni u ti u.

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa mari u n‑kpɛ kpeema maa saani n‑yɛli, “Pɔla sɔ ban kpari dansarika duu ni la m booli ma n‑yɛli, ni, m mari na'akyimbila wa n‑kyɛ i saani na, ni, u mari la bunseelli ni u yɛli i.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Ka langyima kpeema maa daa gbaai bii maa nuu n‑kyɛ ka ba labi kpaŋŋa sa, ka boosi u, ni, “Bumbɔ ka i mari ni i yɛli ma?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yɛli, “Yɛhudiyadima maa gbaari la gbaari, ni, ba yɛlim i, ni, i sa nin zaŋŋi Pɔla beoo asuba m‑mari u n‑kyaŋŋi ba kpamma la'aŋŋu maa ni, ni, ba ya maali n‑viisi la u yɛttɔ'a maa suŋŋa.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Ka kpeema maa daa kpamsi u, ni, u di kyɛ ka sɔ baŋŋi, ni, u pun yɛli u lala lahabaari maa, ka basi u ka u bakki.

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Ka bɔ bumbaarigu n‑ti Pɔla gba, ka u ba, ka ti taasi u Gɔmna Filiksi saani ni alaafɛa.”

english_verse: The commander wrote a letter that said:
translation_verse: Ka u daa sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti Gɔmna maa, ni:

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Doo wa ka Yɛhudiyadima daa gbaai ka daa boori ni ba ku u; amaa n daa baŋŋiya, ni, Room suulinsi nira n nyɛ u, ka daa zaŋŋi n langyinseeba n‑fa u ba saani.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: N daa boori ni m baŋŋi ban gbaai u seelli zugu, ka n daa zaŋŋi u n‑kyaŋŋi ba kpamma la'aŋŋu ni;

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: ka n ti baŋŋi, ni, galimni maa nyɛ la seelli din yiiri ba zaaligu yɛla ni na, amaa ba daa bu wulisi u kum taalli ŋɔn nyɛ seem, halli dansarika taalli gba.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka n daa wum lahabaari, ni, ba ya sɔ'ai n‑niŋi la doo maa yɛttɔ'abeoo, ka dizugu n kyɛ ka ba taasi u i saani na. Ban galimni u maa muna - n yɛli ba, ni, ba mari ba yɛttɔ'a maa m‑paai i saani na.”

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka langyimadima maa daa tum hoo ŋɔn yɛli ba seem maa, ka mari Pɔla n‑kyaŋŋi yuŋŋu maa, halli nti paai Antipatirisi.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa kaai maa, ban daa kyɛnni nɔba la daa ŋmaligi n‑labi ba Baarikyi ni, ka kyɛ ka wuribaariba maa mari Pɔla, halli nti paai ba kyɛnni maa tɔriga.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Ba daa paai la Sizariiya n‑zaŋŋi gbaŋŋu maa n‑ti Gɔmna maa, ka daa zaŋŋi Pɔla gba n‑niŋi u nuu ni.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka Gɔmna maa daa karim gbaŋŋu maa, ka boosi Pɔla ŋɔn yi tingbanseaa na, ka baŋŋi, ni, Silisiiya;

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: ka u yɛli u, ni, “Ban n galimni i maa yi ti paai na, n nin wum i yɛttɔ'a maa.” Ka u daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi u n‑kpeesi NaaHɛroodi naam yiri kurigu ni duseaa n‑guri u.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Dabsa anu nyaaŋŋa ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana Ananayasi daa paai na, ni kpamseeba m‑paasi u zugu, n‑taasi Tɛritulusi ba yɛttɔ'atɔ'asira na; ka ba daa wulisi Gɔmna n‑yɛli ban galimni Pɔla seem.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa booli Pɔla na, ka Tɛritulusi daa gbaai yɛttɔ'a ni u ti galimni u, ni, “Ti Ba gyirimadaana, Filiksi, wumma! Di nyɛ la i yiiko ni ka ti mari sufudoaa; ka di laa n‑nyɛ la i yuubu ni ka ti tingbaŋŋu wa maali, ka takki n‑nyɛ bunsuŋŋu.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Seelikam zaa, ni ya'ari kam zaa, ti deaari i lala suŋŋsim maa; ka dizugu ti puusiri i la wurilim puusigu.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ti bu boori ni ti sa'am i saha yaasa, amaa ti susiri i ni, i sufubaalim zugu, ni, i wum ti yɛlli beela.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: Dama ti nya ninvugkyɔ'aŋŋu wa, ka u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn daamni Yɛhudiyadima maa duniya wa ya'ari kam zaa, ka ba zabra; ka u nyɛ la toondaana n‑ti Nazarɛti wɔligri soori dima wa.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ŋɔn Lisiyasi daa yɛli, ni, baseeba ban ya galim u, see ka ba kyɛ i saani na.]] Tɔ, ka i yi ti viisi m‑boosi u lala boosa, i mammaŋŋa nin toaai n‑wum u saani bunseesi zaa tin galimni u a zugu maa.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Ka Yɛhudiyadima maa zaa daa la'am n‑galimni u m‑paasi, n‑yɛtti, ni, anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna maa ŋɔn daa wulisi u, ni, u tɔ'asima maa, Pɔla daa yɛliya, “M mi halli, ni, i pun katti sariya tingbaŋŋu wa puuni n‑yuui; ka dizugu n sufu maaya pam dinŋɔ zugu, ni, di nyɛ la nyini tooni ka n ya tɔ'asi n yɛttɔ'a wa.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: I nin toaai m‑boosi m‑baŋŋi i mammaŋŋa, ni, di bu gaari dapiiya ni ayi man daa kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na ni n ti gyeem Naawunni.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Ba daa bu nya ka man ni sɔ ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa ni, ba mun daa bu laa n‑nya ka n damni daadam maa, la'aŋŋu yiya ni bee timpuuni.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Ba mun ku toaai n‑wulisi i, ni, yɛlimaŋni bɛ ban yɛtti n‑galimni ma bunseesi wa ni, halli beela.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Kulla dinŋɔ ka n sakki n‑ti i, ni, di nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni: n gyeemni la ti yaadima Naawunni n‑dɔli ban boonni sɔseaa, ni, ‘wɔligri soori’ la. Amaa lala soori dɔligu puuni ka n laa n‑niŋni yɛdda n‑niŋi din bɛ Naawunni zaaligu puuni zaa, ni anabidima ŋɔn daa sɔbi seem zaa m‑paasi;

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: ka laa m‑mari tama Naawunni zugu, ni, ninvugsuma ni ninvugbeeri zaa nin ti doaai kum ni, ka lala tama ka ninvugseeba ban galimni ma la gba mara.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka n kur moori saha kam ni nimbirimoaa, ni n sufu ni teesa ku diisiri ma galinseelli zaa vi, zaŋŋi n‑kpa Naawunni, ni ninsaalidima ni.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka di nyaaŋŋa ka yuunseesi daa gaari, ka pampaŋŋɔ n daa kyɛ na ni n ti tisi piini n‑tiri faradima m butiri taaba sunsukkuni, ni n Naawunni piini m‑paasi.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Man daa niŋni lala maa zugu, n daa sɛ'asi m maŋŋa kasikasi n‑kpɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni, ka ba daa nya ma bɛni. Di saha ka zama daa bu dɔli ma, man mun daa bu daamni sɔa.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyaseeba ban daa yi Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni na n daa bɛ ni - halli ba yi daa nya taaliseaa n saani, di sumni ni ban da bɛ la kpa wa n‑galimni ma.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ninvugseeba ban zɛya wa, see ka ba yɛli ban daa nya n taaliseaa, man daa zɛ ba la'aŋŋu maa ni sahanseaa la.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Ay! Taalli ka ni, di yi pa la yɛttɔ'a yinni man daa zɛ ba tooni n‑kaasi n‑yɛli seaa la, ni, ‘Yɛttɔ'aseaa tin tɔ'asiri kpa wa zuna, nyɛ la yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi kum yisigri yɛla.’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna Filiksi daa nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn mi Yisa soori yɛla an bɛ seem maa deede; ka dizugu di saha maa u daa zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'a maa n‑zaali dabsiri. Ni, “Langyima kpeema Lisiyasi yi ti paai na sahanseaa, n nin kari ya sariya maa.”

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: U daa yɛli langyima toondaana maa, ni, ba zaŋŋma Pɔla n‑kpari, amaa ba kyɛ ka u nya paalu beela, ka ba di za'asi u zɔdima kpeebu na ni ba ti suŋŋi u.

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi dabsa beela ka Gɔmna Filiksi daa kyɛ na, ŋɔn ni u pɔ'a Diruusila, sɔ ŋɔn daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyapɔ'a la; ka u daa tum m‑booli Pɔla na, ni ba ti wum u yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa Masiiya yɛdda niŋbu.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn daa paai u kpaamni yɛla zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ninvugmaŋni tunsuma ŋɔn nyɛ seem la, ni mansɛ'asigu yɛla, ni Naawunni ŋɔn ya kari niriba sariya la, di saha ka dabeem daa gbaai Filiksi ka u daa yɛli, “Kyɛla ka kyaŋŋi pampaŋŋɔ! N yi ti nya dahama n nin booli i na yaasa.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: U daa laa n‑teesi m‑paasi, ni, Pɔla ya n‑ti u la ligri, ka dizugu u daa kur boonni u na sahakam, ni ba diri azama.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Amaa yuuma ayi ŋɔn daa gaari, Pɔrikyuusi Fɛsitusi daa deaai Filiksi; ka Filiksi mun daa boori ni u maai Yɛhudiyadima maa sufu, ka dizugu u daa kyɛ ka ba kpalim n‑kpatti Pɔla.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Fɛsitusi ŋɔn daa ti di Gɔmna fukumsi maa sahanseaa la, u daa niŋi dabsa ata Sizariiya timpuuni pɔi, ka naayi yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm.

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: Ka Yɛhudiyadima gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni ba kpamma maa daa paai u na nti galimni Pɔla

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: ka susiri Gɔmna maa, ni, u kpaŋŋma u maŋŋa n‑suŋŋi ba yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ka kyɛ ka ba taasi Pɔla Gyɛrusalɛm na. Ba daa susi u lala dama ba daa boori ni ba sɔ'ai n‑guri soori ka yi ya'abi na n‑ku u.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Amaa Gɔmna Fɛsitusi daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, ni, Sizarii tiŋŋa ka ba daa pun guri Pɔla, halli ni u mammaŋŋa ya n‑labi n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni ka u kyɛnni saha bu ya yuui.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Ka dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ya zuguraanseeba doaai n‑dɔli ma n‑kyaŋŋi bɛni, ka taaliseaa yi bɛ doo maa saani, ya nin toaai n‑galim u n‑wulisi lala.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Gɔmna maa daa kpalim m‑bɛ Gyɛrusalɛm n‑niŋi daba anii-awai ka naayi bakki n‑kyaŋŋi Sizariiya. Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, u daa zi kootu ni ka kyɛ ka ba mari Pɔla na.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa ti kpɛ na maa, Gyɛrusalɛm dima daa kyɛ na n‑zaani n‑gyiligi u n‑galimni u yɛttɔ'abeeri ni taalitibsa yɛla; amaa ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑wulisi anŋɔ zaa yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla mun daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli, ni, “M bu tum taaliseaa n‑galim Yɛhudiyadima zaaligu, bee ba Gyeeŋŋu Yiri, yɛttɔ'aseelli ni; halli n‑galim Room NaaTitaari yɛla gba.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Amaa Gɔmna Fɛsitusi daa boori ni u suŋŋi Yɛhudiyadima maa m‑maai ba sufu; ka dizugu u daa yɛli Pɔla, ni, “Ammi, i nin sakki, ni, i kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm, ka n nin tɔ'asi i yɛttɔ'a wa bɛni?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “N zɛ la NaaTitaari kootu ni, ka bɛni ka di sumni ni ba kari n sariya. I mi halli, ni, n na zi n‑tum taaliseaa n‑galim Yɛhudiyadima maa.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Halli n yi da tum tumbɛseaa kan sumsi ni kum, n ku za'asi, ni, ba ku ma dizugu. Amaa ba yi galimni ma yɔriyɔri n ku sakki, ni, sɔ zaŋŋi ma n‑niŋi ba nuu ni. Dizugu n susi, ni, i zaŋŋi n yɛttɔ'a wa n‑zaali n‑ti NaaTitaari saani.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Di saha ka Fɛsitusi ni u kpamma maa daa ŋmɛ saawara; ka naayi sakki, ni, “I susiya, ni, ti zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'a wa n‑zaali n‑ti NaaTitaari. Tɔ; NaaTitaari saani ka i siri nin kyaŋŋi.”

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi dahinseesi ka NaaAgrippa ni u pɔ'a Bɛriniisi daa ti paai Sizarii na, ni ba ti puusi Gɔmna Fɛsitusi.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Ba daa yuui bɛni beela, ka Fɛsitusi daa zaŋŋi Pɔla yɛla n‑wulisi n‑ti Naaba maa, ni, “Gɔmna Filiksi daa kyɛ ka dɔsɔ kpalim la dansarika duu ni;

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: ka man daa kyaŋŋi Gyɛrusalɛm la, gyeeŋŋu toondaandima ni kpamma maa daa kyɛ n saani na ni ba ti tɔ'asi lala doo maa yɛla, ni, n kpaŋŋma m maŋŋa n‑tɔ'asi u yɛttɔ'a n‑lusi u n‑ti ba.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: Man mun daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli ba, ni, di pa la ti Room dima kaari ni ti lusi sɔa, see ka ti daŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka ŋɔn ni ban galimni u la ti kpɛ na n‑tɔ'asi taaba yɛttɔ'a maa yɛla, ka u mari kukɔri n‑kyiisiri ba galiŋŋu maa.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ba daa dɔli ma n‑kyɛ kpa na; man mun n daa bu zaali yɛttɔ'a maa dabsiri, amaa beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, n daa zini kootu ni, n‑kyɛ ka ba mari doo maa na.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Ka baseeba ban daa galimni u yɛla maa ŋɔn daa ti zaani ni ba ti tɔ'asi, ba daa bu wulisi tumbɛseesi man daa teesi, ni, ba naa m‑mari n‑zaali u.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Amaa di daa nyɛ la nangbankpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba maŋtoori wungyeeŋŋu yɛla, ni dɔsɔ ŋɔn boonni Yisa la, ni, u kpiya; Pɔla mun yɛli, ni, u na bɛ la u nyɔvuri ni.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Dizugu n daa bu baŋŋi man ya n‑gbaai yɛttɔ'a maa seem, ka daa boosi u, ni, u nin sakki, ni, ti kyɛm Gyɛrusalɛm n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a maa, bee?

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Amaa u mun daa susiya, ni, ti zaali yɛttɔ'a maa, halli ni u ti kyaŋŋi ti Ba NaaTitaari saani n‑kari sariya maa. Ka dizugu n daa kyɛ ka ba na guri u dansarika duu ni, see ka n nya soori ni ti zaŋŋi u n‑taasi ti Dugma Naaba saani.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Ka NaaAgrippa daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “M boori ni n wum doo maa yɛttɔ'a m mammaŋŋa.” Ka Fɛsitusi daa yɛli u, ni, “Beoo i sa nin wum u yɛla.”

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, NaaAgrippa ni Bɛriniisi daa yi na ni ba naam gyirima pam, ka kyɛ na nti kpɛ kootu dutitaari maa ni, ban ni soogyadima kpamma maa ni tiŋŋa maa ninvugtibsa zaa. Ka Gɔmna Fɛsitusi daa kyɛ ka ba zaŋŋi Pɔla m‑mari u n‑kpɛ na.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Gɔmna maa daa yɛli, “M Ba NaaAgrippa, wumma! Zama, ya wumma! Ya gɔsim doo wa! Ŋɔnŋɔ yɛla zugu ka Yɛhudiya sɔkam zaa daa kyɛ n saani na Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni, ka laa n‑kyɛ kpa na yaasa, ka tasiri pampam, ni, di bu laa n‑sumsi ni doo wa kpalim u nyɔvuri ni.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Man mun daa viisi u yɛla, ka daa bu nya tumbɛseaa u saani kan sumsi kum. Amaa u daa susi yɛttɔ'a zaaligu n‑ti ti Ba NaaTitaari, ka dizugu n daa gbaai u yɛla lala, ni, n nin tum u Naaba saani.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Amaa n na ka bunseelli ka n nin toaai n‑sɔbi u yɛttɔ'a maa yɛla yɛlimaŋni maa deede n‑tɔlim n‑ti Naaba. Ka dizugu, NaaAgrippa, n kyɛ ka u kyɛ i tooni na, ni yarima zaa tooni, ni ti ti viisi u yɛla; nti nya yɛttɔ'aseaa man nin ti sɔbi n‑tɔlim.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Dama di bu dɔli la soori ni n tum yɛttɔ'adaansɔ Naaba saani ka na ka yɛttɔ'aseaa, ni n wulisi Naaba din kyɛ ka ba galimni u.”

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: Ni, “M Ba NaaAgrippa, n yɛtti, ni, m mari zugsuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi n zuna yɛttɔ'a wa yɛla. Dama di nyɛ la nyini tooni ka m mari la kukɔri ni n gyɛrigri n‑kyiisiri ban galimni ma yɛla zaa.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: M mi halli, ni, i baŋŋi ti Yɛhudiyadima kaya ni ti nangbankpeemni yɛla zaa. Dizugu n susiri i, ni, i kur di sugru n‑wum n yɛttɔ'a wa.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Ba daa mi n yɛla ka di yuui: ba yi sakki n‑yɛli yɛlimaŋni, ba nin yɛli, ni, n daa dɔli la ti zaaligu nimmoosi soori maa, di nyɛ la Farisii dima dinni.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Ka pampaŋŋɔ ba zaŋŋi ma n‑zaali kpa wa la ti yaadima tama maa yɛla zugu, Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti noori, ni, u nin ti ba maa; ka dinŋɔ zugu ka ba galimni ma, walla!

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Lala yɛla maa ka ti za'anɔya pii ni ayi niriba zaa guri ni tama ni nimmoaa, ni ba ti nya lala yɛla maa. Ka dizugu ba kur gyeemni la Naawunni, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa. Naaba wumma - di nyɛ la lala Naawunni noori yɛligu maa zugu ka ba galimni ma, halli ni ban nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima gba!

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Bɔzugu ka ya ku nyaŋŋi n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni siri neaari la niriba kum ni?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dizugu man gba daa teesi, ni, di sumni ni n kpaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑zabi ban dɔli Yisa, Nazarɛti doo la, yɛdda soori maa.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Ka n daa niŋi lala n‑namsiri ba Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni: n daa gbaasi Naawunni niriba pam n‑kpari duu ni, dama gyeeŋŋu toondaandima daa ti ma soori, ni, n tum lala. Halli ban daa ku ba sahanseaa la, n daa bu yiisi n nuu ba yɛla maa ni.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Yinyaari daa kpɛ ma lala, halli ka la'aŋŋu duri zaa puuni ka n daa darigri ba tuba ka mugsiri ba, dama n daa boori ni ba ti za'asi Yisa yɛla; halli n daa kyaŋŋi tinseesi gba m‑paasi, ni n ti namsiri ba.

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: Ka lala zugu n daa deaai gyeeŋŋu toondaandima yiiko ka yi soori ni n kyaŋŋi Damasikusi tiŋŋa.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Ka di daa paai wuntaŋŋa agbaa saha, ka neesim pam daa nyakki n‑yi saazugu ni na nti gyiligi man ni ninvugseeba ban daa dɔli ma la zaa. Lala neesim maa daa gaari wuntaŋŋa neesim ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Ti zaa daa lu la tiŋŋa, ka n wum ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ma, Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ni, ‘Sɔla, Sɔla, bɔzugu ka i namsiri ma? I ŋmani la na'asɔ ŋɔn koori ka za'asira, ka ba feebri u.’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Ka n daa boosi n‑yɛli, ‘Ŋɔni nla, n dugma?’, ka u labsi n‑yɛli ma, ni, ‘Mani Yisa: mani ka i namsiri maa.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Amaa doaam n‑zaani saazugu! Man wulisiri la m mammaŋŋa n‑tiri i, ni n zaŋŋi i n‑leebi n tuntumna. I nin ti yɛli niriba nyin nya yɛttɔ'aseesi zuna wa, ka laa n‑yɛli ba lala bunseesi nyin nin nya yaasa.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: N nin fa i m‑basi Yɛhudiyadima nuu ni, ni ban pa Yɛhudiyadima nuu ni gba, dama ban saani ka n ya tum i.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: I nin kyɛ ka ba nini neaai; i nin kyɛ ka ba ŋmaligi ka kyɛ zibsim maa, n‑kyɛ neesim maa ni na; i nin kyɛ ka ba ŋmaligi n‑yi Sintaani fukumsi ni m‑paai Naawunni saani na, ka ba nin ti niŋi ma yɛdda halli nti nya ba taalli zaa kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi, ka la'am Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni.’

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Ay! - n daa daŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai Damasikusi m‑mooli n‑ti ba, ni, ba kyɛli ba teesibeeri ni ba tumbeeri yɛla m‑basi ka ŋmaligi n‑kyɛ Naawunni saani na ka tumni lala tunsuma maa. Ka di nyaaŋŋa n daa kyɛ Gyɛrusalɛm na m‑moonni lala, ni Gyuudii tingbaŋŋu maa ni m‑paasi, ka naayi kyaŋŋi ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa tinsi gba n‑wulisiri ba yɛttɔ'a wa yɛla.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Ka anŋɔ zaa zugu ka Yɛhudiyadima daa gbaai ma Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa puuni n‑yɛli, ni, ba ku ma.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti suŋŋi ma zugu, n na bɛ ni halli ni zuna; ka n zɛya n‑wulisiri niriba wa zaasa, birikyindima ni tarimma zaa; m bu yɛtti sɛseela na'ala anabidima ŋɔn daa pun yɛli, ni, di nin niŋi seem, ni Musa gba daa yɛli lala.

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Di nyɛ la: ni, Naawunni ŋɔn diisi sɔ Masiiya la nin ti di wahala; ni din paasi lala, ni, u nin leebi ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni n‑yisigi kum ni, ka dizugu u nin ti kyɛ ka neesim neaai n‑tiri u maŋtoori niriba, ni ninvugseeba ban paasi ba zugu maa zaa.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi u yɛla lala n‑naai, Fɛsitusi daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, ni, “N zɔ Pɔla, i zugu sa'amiya! I kariŋŋu pam ni i bamsim zugu n kyɛ ka yinyaari kpɛ i.”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Ay, n zugu bu sa'am. I bu nya, m Ba Fɛsitusi, ni, n tɔ'asiri hoo ninvugmaŋni ŋɔn tɔ'asiri seem, n‑wulisiri yɛlimaŋni yɛla?

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: N tɔ'asiri yɛla maa zaa ka bu sɔ'ari seelli, man tɔ'asiri Naa maa tooni zugu; ka di pa la u na zi n‑wum anŋɔ zaa lahabaari. U mi man tɔ'asiri yɛliseesi maa gbinni suŋŋa, dama ba daa bu sɔ'ai n‑tum anŋɔ zaa.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: M Ba NaaAgrippa, i niŋi anabidima yɛla maa yɛdda? - Ii! M mi halli, ni, i niŋiya!”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Ka Naa maa daa gyɛrigi n‑yɛli u, ni, “Dinŋɔna, i teesiya, ni, di ku yuui ka i kyɛ ka n leebi Kirisitadoowa, bee?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa labsi n‑yɛli, ni, “Di yi yuui, bee, di yi bu yuui, n siri boori ni nyini, ni seeba zaa ban wumni n yɛttɔ'a zuna wa m‑paasi i zugu, nin ti leebi hoo man ŋɔn bɛ seem la - amaa pa la ban lɔ ma kyɔrimi wa taabu!”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
