translation_verse: Tɔ; ka di saha Naaba daa yisigi n‑zaani saazugu, ŋɔn ni Gɔmna ni Bɛriniisi ni ba taaba ban zintiri bimbinni zugu la zaa.

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa doaai n‑kyɛnni maa, ba daa sɔsiri n‑yɛtti taaba, ni, “Doo wa bu tum sɛseelli din sumni kum, halli din sum ni dansarika duu kparibu gba.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Ka NaaAgrippa yɛli Fɛsitusi, ni, “U yi da bu zaali u yɛttɔ'a NaaTitaari saani, u naa n‑toaai n‑yi n‑kyaŋŋi yɔri.”

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Ka ti daa nya nyarintitaseaa kan yi Addiramitiyum tiŋŋa ni na, ka ka ya kyaŋŋi Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ya'ari sa. Ti daa kpɛ ka ni, tirim ni Arisitaakusi, ŋɔn daa yi Tɛsalɔnika ni na, kan nyɛ Masidoonya tingbaŋŋu ni tinseaa.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa paai Saidoon tiŋŋa. Bɛni ka Gyuliyusi daa ti Pɔla soori ni u sigi n‑kyaŋŋi nti kaai u zɔdima, ni ba gɔsi u yɛla; dama Gyuliyusi daa nyɛ la nirilim daana.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Tin daa yi bɛni sahanseaa maa, ti daa du m‑burigi n‑gaari Saapirusi tingbaŋŋu nyaaŋŋa sa, dama pɛsiŋŋu daa bu zeeri suŋŋa n‑ti ti.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Ka ti daa kyaŋŋi tooni n‑yakki teeku ziseaa kan bɛ Silisii ni Pamfilii tingbana ya'ari maa, halli nti paai Maayira tiŋŋa kan zɛ Lisii tingbaŋŋu ni.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka toondaana maa daa nya nyarinseaa kan yi Alɛkzandiriya tiŋŋa ni na, ni ka ti kyaŋŋi Italii tingbaŋŋu ni, ka daa kyɛ ka ti kpɛ kana.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Ti daa du n‑kyaŋŋi; ka di daa niŋi lala, ka ti kyɛnni daa mukki ti pam, ka ti kyɛnni beelabeela ka naayi paai Kunidoosi tiŋŋa. Di saha ka pɛsiŋŋu daa tuusi ti, ka dizugu ti daa ŋmaligi n‑yakki nti dɔli Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu ni. N‑kyɛnni n‑kyɛ ka tiŋŋa wa bɛ ti nudirigu ya'ari sa, n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gaari Salimooni tiŋŋa;

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: n‑dɔli beelabeela ka naayi paai ya'aseaa ban boonni Nyarima Ya'aveelli la - bɛni ni Lasaya tiŋŋa daa bu wɔ'a.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Ti daa yuui beela bɛni, ka saha daa bu laa n‑veeli ni ba kyɛnni teeku maa ni, dama Yɛhudiyadima nɔlɔri saha daa pun gaari. Dizugu ka Pɔla daa kpamsi ba

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: n‑yɛli, ni, “N zɔdima, m mi halli, ni, ti yi ti kyaŋŋi tooni yaasa di nin kyɛ ka ti yɛla sa'am zaa - pa nyariŋŋu ni neema kɔkɔa, amaa ni ti nyɔvuya gba.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Di zaa yɔri, dama langyima toondaana maa daa niŋi nyariŋŋu daana maa ni u nyarinduusira yɛla yɛdda, n‑deaai ba yɛligu ka za'asi Pɔla dinni.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Ban daa bɛ seelli maa pa la zaazisuŋŋu, dama waari saha ka pɛsiŋŋu nin zeeri n‑kpeeri bɛni n‑daamni ba. Dizugu ba niriba pam daa ŋmɛ saawara n‑gbaai, ni, ba bakkma bɛni n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi; ni, di bu ya'ai ni ba ti paai Kyiriti tinseaa ban boonni Fiiniksi la, dama bɛni ba nin toaai n‑kpɛ ya'aseaa kan veeli n‑ti nyarima, waari saha maa, halli ni kan bɛ seem maa ku kyɛ ka lala pɛsima ti kpɛ na.

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ban daa ti nya ka pɛsisuŋŋu daa zeeri n‑yiiri ba nyaaŋŋa na, ba daa teesi, ni, ba nin toaai n‑kyaŋŋi ba kyɛnni ban daa gbaai seem la, ka daa foaai buntibsiri kan ta'ari kyɛnni la, n‑du n‑dɔli Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu gbiriŋŋu ziiya.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Di daa niŋi dabsiri yinni yaasa ka ti daa diri wahala pɛsiŋŋu maa zugu, ka dizugu ba daa gbaai la neema maa basibu.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: M‑paai dabsa ata daari ka ba zaa daa zaŋŋi ba nuusi n‑yiisi nyariŋŋu maa neema zaa m‑basira.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Di daa yuui lala ka ti daa bu nya wuntaŋŋa neesim, bee saŋmariwuusi; ka pɛsibeoo daa kpalim n‑zeeri ti, ka ti daa laa n‑ka tama, ni, ti nin tuligi halli beela.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka tin daa bu di diibu ka di yuui la, Pɔla daa zɛ saazugu ba zaa sunsukkuni n‑yɛli, “N zɔdima, ya yi daa wum n noori la ka daa bu yisigi Kyiriti tin daa bɛ seela maa, di naa n‑sɔa. Lala yisigri zugu ka ti yɛla sa'am maa.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ n kpamsiri ya, ni, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu dɔya; ya sɔ ku basi yɔri, kulla nyariŋŋu maa kɔkɔa.

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: ka yɛli ma, ni, ‘Pɔla, di zɔri dabeem! See ka i nin ti zɛ NaaTitaari tooni: ka dizugu Naawunni zaŋŋi la ninvugseeba ban la'am m‑bɛ i saani nyariŋŋu wa puuni zaa n‑ti i.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Dizugu, n zɔdima, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu dɔya. N niŋi Naawunni yɛdda, ni, u nin tum hoo ŋɔn sa yɛli ma seem la.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Ti nin baasi m‑paai tingbanseaa kan bɛ teeku kooma sunsukkuni maa ni.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Dizugu ba daa lɔrigi buntibsa maa m‑basi koom maa ni, ka lɔrigi nyarimpaari la ni ba ti laa m‑piili n‑duusiri; ka zaŋŋi tantibila n‑duusi n‑tabli nyariŋŋu tooni, ni ka ti gbaai pɛsiŋŋu n‑daai ba ka ba kyaŋŋi.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Di saha ka nyariŋŋu dima daa yɛtti, ni, ba ya zɔ ni ka kyɛ ti; dizugu ba daa lɔrigi nyarimbila maa n‑sigsiri ka n‑niŋni teeku koom maa ni; ka ma'ari ziri, ni, ba ya sigsi buntibsa tooni sa m‑paasi an bɛ nyaaŋŋa zugu.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Di saha ka dabeem daa gbaai ba, ni, di pa seem ba nin ŋmɛ n‑firi luginseelli din bu veeli puuni, ka dizugu ba daa zaŋŋi buntibsa anaasi n‑lɔ miisi n‑lɔ nyariŋŋu nyaaŋŋa n‑sigsi koom maa ni, ni a ti ta'ai nyariŋŋu kyɛnni; ka naayi kpalim n‑susiri wunsusigu n‑guri bɛkyikyeoo saha.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Amaa Pɔla daa yɛli toondaana maa ni u niriba maa, ni, “Bana yi bu kpalim m‑bɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni, ya sɔ ku ti tuligi.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Ka langyimadima daa ŋmaai nyarimbila miisi maa ka kyɛ ka ka lu m‑basi.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Ya bu ya di diibu pampaŋŋɔa? Lala nin suŋŋi ya, ka ya ti yi ya wahala wa puuni ni alaafɛa. Ya sɔsɔ ku basi yɔri - halli ni ya ninvugsɔ zɔbgu yinni gba.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa yɛli lala n‑naai maa, u daa zaŋŋi diibu m‑puusi Naawunni di zugu ba zaa tooni, ka ŋmaai n‑ŋɔbi.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba zaa sufu daa kpaŋŋi, ka ba gba daa deaai diibu n‑di.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Ka ban daa di diibu n‑naai, ba daa zaŋŋi kyi an daa bɛ nyariŋŋu maa puuni n‑yiisi m‑basi koom maa ni, ni di kyɛ ka nyariŋŋu maa ku laa n‑tibsa pam.

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Ka beoo ŋɔn daa ti neaai, ti daa nya ka tingbaŋŋu bɛ ni, amaa ba daa bu baŋŋi ka. Amaa ba nya ya'aseaa ban teesi, ni, mɔ'abisigu m bɛ ni ka tampikara ka ni; ka daa boori ni ba ti kpɛ bɛni n‑kyɛ ka nyariŋŋu maa firi tanni maa ni n‑zaani.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa paai ya'aseaa ka kooma daa kyɛnni n‑tuusiri taaba ka labra, ka ba daa burigi n‑firi, halli ka nyariŋŋu maa toonni ku sakki n‑kyaŋŋi bee n‑labi, sahanseaa ka kooma maa daa ŋmeeri ka nyaaŋŋa n‑kabri ka.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Di saha ka langyimadima daa boori ni ba ku dansarika niriba maa, ni, di bu ya'ai ba sɔ nin ti dugi koom m‑paai tingbaŋŋu n‑zɔa.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Amaa ba toondaana daa bu sakki lala, ŋɔn daa boori ni u tuligi Pɔla zugu. U daa yɛli, ni, ban mi koom dugbu la, ni, ba kpeem koom puuni n‑dugi m‑paai tingbaŋŋu maa.

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: Ni, ban kpalim muna - ba gbaam taabudaari, bee, nyariŋŋu dakyɛra maa, m‑mari n‑kyaŋŋi: ka lala zugu ba zaa naayi paai teeku duuri maa ni alaafɛa.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka tin daa ti paai tiŋŋa maa n‑tuligi maa, ti daa ti baŋŋi, ni, tingbaŋŋu maa yuuri boonni la Malita.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Tiŋŋa maa niriba daa suŋŋi ti ni nirilim pam. Ba daa nyoaai bugum n‑kyɛ ka ti zaa kyɛ na n‑wuusi, saa maa ŋɔn buuri ti zugu, ni waari maa ŋɔn daa niŋi ti seem.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa boori daari na n‑niŋni bugum maa ni, ka lala saha ka waafu daa bɛ daari maa sunsukkuni n‑gbisira, ka bugum maa tuulim daa neaai u, ka u yi na nti dum Pɔla nuu maa.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Tiŋŋa maa biisi ŋɔn daa ti nya ka waafu maa kpalim n‑kyɛ'ai u nuu maa, ba daa yɛli taaba, ni, “Mma! Ninvugkuura n nyɛ u. U tuligi n‑yi teeku koom maa ni na, amaa di bu sumni ni u kpalim m‑bɛ ni.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Pɔla mun daa kpaasi waafu maa m‑basi bugum maa ni, ka kur kpalim m‑mari alaafɛa.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Niriba maa daa teesi, ni, di ku yuui ka u fuusi, bee, ka u lu tiŋŋa ya'abi ka kpi. Ka ba guusiri lala n‑yuui, ka sɛseela bu niŋi u, ka ba naayi takki ba teesa n‑yɛli, ni, wunsɔ n nyɛ u.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Ka dɔsɔ daa bɛ ni ŋɔn daa su tingbaŋŋu fukumsi maa, ka u yuuri daa boonni Publiyusi. Ŋɔn daa su puseesi an daa bɛ tin daa bɛ luginseaa la ni. Ka u daa booli ti ni ti kpɛ u yiri m‑bɛ bɛni n‑niŋi daba ata, ka gɔsi ti yɛla suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Publiyusi ba maa daa ka alaafɛa n‑dɔya - u puu n daa beera ka u niŋŋa zaa bii. Ka Pɔla daa kpɛ u saani n‑susi Naawunni ka zaŋŋi u nuusi m‑pa u zugu ka tibi u.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Ka din daa niŋi lala maa, tingbaŋŋu maa ninvugseeba ban kpalim ka kɔŋŋi alaafɛ daa kyɛ na, ni ba ti nya tippu.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Ba daa ti ti piini pam; ka tin daa nya soori ni ti yi bɛni maa, ba daa ti ti la bunseelli kam kan daa pɔsigi ti saani.

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ti daa niŋi gɔya ata bɛni, ka naayi nya nyarinseaa kan daa pun zɛ tingbaŋŋu maa lugri n‑guri waari saha baasigu. Alɛkzandiriya nyariŋŋu n daa nyɛ ka, ka ba daa puri ka yuseaa kan nyɛ ba Wungyaasi yuuri.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Ti daa du n‑lɔŋŋi m‑paai Sairakuusi tiŋŋa n‑niŋi daba ata bɛni;

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi n‑gyili halli nti paai Rɛgyeoo tiŋŋa. N‑neaai beoo ka pɛsisuŋŋu daa piili n‑zeeri n‑yi ti nyaaŋŋa, ka ti daa gaari yooma n‑niŋi daba ayi kyɛnni ka naayi paai Putiyooli tiŋŋa.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka ti daa nya ti nyaandɔlitaaba, ka ba daa mukki ti ni ti kpalim m‑bɛ ba saani n‑niŋi daba ayɔpɔi. Dizugu ti daa niŋi lala pɔi, ka naayi gbaai soori ni ti kyaŋŋi ti Room maa.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Ka Yisa nyaandɔliseeba ban daa bɛ Room maa daa wum ti kyɛnni na lahabaari, ka daa yi na ni ba ti tuusi ti soori; ka paai ti Apiiya Daa ni na, ka baseeba mun daa paai ti Yirikpeera Ata tiŋŋa na. Ka Pɔla ŋɔn daa ti nya ba maa, u daa puusi Naawunni taari, ka u sufu daa maai.

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka tin daa ti kpɛ Room timpuuni maa, ba daa ti Pɔla soori ni u bɔ yiri seaa u mammaŋŋa n‑kpɛa; see ka ba kyɛ ka langyinsɔ daa kpalim u saani n‑guri u.

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa niŋi daba ata bɛni, ka naayi booli Yɛhudiyadimseeba ban daa bɛ bɛni toondaandima maa, ni ba kyɛ u saani na. Ka ban daa ti paai na maa, u daa yɛli ba, ni, “M mabiisi, man bu tum taaliseaa n‑ti ti butiri, bee, ti yaadima kaya maa; amaa di zaa yɔri, ba daa gbaai ma Gyɛrusalɛm tiŋŋa maa ni, n‑lɔ ma n‑niŋi Room dima nuusi ni.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Room dima mun daa viisi n yɛla ka daa boori ni ba kyɛ ma m‑basi, dama ba daa bu nya taaliseaa n saani kan sumsi ni kum.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Amaa Yɛhudiyadima daa bu sakki lala, ka dizugu n daa bu nya soori see ka n ti susi, ni, ba zaŋŋi n yɛttɔ'a maa n‑zaali NaaTitaari saani na. Di pa la man mari la yɛttɔ'aseaa ni n ti galimni n Yɛhudiyataaba.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu m boonni ya, ni ya kyɛm na ka ti ti di azama; ka ya nin ti nya ka baŋŋi din m bɛ seem, ni, Izraɛli dima ban mari tama seaa zugu ka n kpalim n‑lɔ kyɔrimi wa.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa labsi n‑yɛli u, “Ti na bu deaai gbana zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi i yɛla, Gyuudiiya dima ŋɔn sɔbi seesi n‑ti ti na, ka ti mabisɔ na bu paai ti na ni u ti tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑galim i.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Amaa ti boori ni ti ti wum din bɛ i saani, dama ti mi, ni, niriba zaa galimni la wɔligri soori wa yɛla tinsi zaa puuni.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa zaali dabsiri, ka daa kyɛ u yirikpeetiri na ni niriba pampam. Ka Pɔla daa wulisi ba ni nimmoosi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni naam yɛla. U daa tɔ'asiri Musa sɔppu yɛla, ni u ti kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi Yisa yɛla a puuni n‑niŋi u yɛdda - halli anabidima sɔppu gba ka u daa wulisi ba lala - wuntaŋŋa saha halli nti paai yuŋŋu.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ba mun seeba daa niŋi u yɛligu yɛdda, baseeba mun daa za'asi yɛdda niŋbu.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Ka ban daa ka nangbanyinni ni taaba maa, ba daa doaai ni ba bakki n‑kuli; ka Pɔla daa yɛli ba yɛligu yinni m‑paasi, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa tɔ'asi ya yɛla; u daa kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya yɛli ya yaadima,

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: ni, Naawunni yɛliya#:- ‘Kyɛma butiri wa niriba saani n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Ya nin wum, m‑maali n‑wum, Amaa ya ku baŋŋi di gbinni. Ya nin gɔsi m‑maali n‑gɔsi, Amaa ya ku nya di.”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: Dama n niriba wa muna - Ba sufu ni teesa bu zulima, Ba ligi la ba tuba, Ba mumni ba nini: Dama di yi pa lala Ba nini naa n‑nyara, Ba tuba naa n‑wumna, Ba sufu ni teesa naa n‑neaara, Ka ba ti labi n saani na Nti nya tippu n saani.’

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ya baŋŋma! Dizugu ba tɔlim tuliginsim wa lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa; ban nin ti wum ka.”

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Ka Pɔla daa kpalim m‑bɛ lala halli n‑niŋi yuuma ayi bɛni. U daa yoori u maŋtoori ligri n‑kuuri u bukaata, ka daa deaari sɔkam zaa ban daa kyɛ u saani na.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Ka daa moonni Naawunni naam lahabaari ka wulisiri ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya yɛla ni nimbiri moaa, ka sɔsɔ daa bu za'asi u.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Man Pɔla n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya. Mani n nyɛ sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn booli ma ni n ti leebi u tuumu la, ka laa n‑gaŋŋi ma n‑zaali u lahabasuŋŋu tuntumna.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u Naawunni Siiya beebu muna - Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u yiiko n‑kyɛ ka u neaai kum ni, ka lala ka u daa gaŋŋi u ka ti baŋŋi, ni, u siri nyɛ la Naawunni Biiya. Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya nla.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn zugu n kyɛ ka Naawunni niŋi bunsuma n‑niŋi ti ni, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka tirima leebi u tuumu. Ka u tum ti, ni ti ti kyɛ ka tingbana zaa niriba yi niŋi yɛdda u yuuri zugu ka deaai u noori.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Tɔ: yarima ka n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya, ya zaa ban bɛ Room tiŋŋa ka Naawunni wuri ya ka booli ya nti kyɛ ka ya leebi u niriba maa. Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, tim ya bunsuma ni alaafɛ zaa.

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Tɔ: din daŋŋi tooni nyɛ la: ni, n teaari ya yɛla m‑puusiri Naawunni, Yisa Masiiya zugu, dama ya yɛdda niŋbu yɛla moonni n‑gyinni duniya zaa.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: N kur gyeemni Naawunni ni n sufu zaa, ka tumni u Bii maa lahabasuŋŋu tuma ni nimbirimoaa. Ka ŋɔn Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ya baŋŋi man ŋɔn kpalim n‑susiri u n‑teaari ya yɛla seem la saha kam.

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: N yi ti bɛ wumpuusigu ni saha kam zaa maa ka n niŋni lala. N susiri Naawunni, ni, u sakkma, ni, n naayi nya soori ni n ti kyɛ ya saani na.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Dama di kur mugsiri ma, ni n ti nya ya; ni n mari piiniseelli kan yi Naawunni Sii maa saani n‑ti ya, ka ya nin kpaŋŋi di zugu.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Di nyɛ la: ti kpamsiri taaba sufu, man ni yarima zaa, ti zaa ŋɔn niŋni yɛdda seem zugu.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, m boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, n daa pun gbaai ya saani kyɛnni na sahaseesi, amaa n kɔŋŋi la soori halli ni zuna. M boori m mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa ya saani n‑nya seeba ban leebi Naawunni niriba, hoo man daa pun nya seem tinseesi ni la - dama n tuma maa antaam nla.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: Lala zugu n kyɛ ka m boori nimmoaa ni n kyɛ ya saani na m‑mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑ti ya Room timpuuni dima gba.

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Vi bu mari ma lahabasuŋŋu wa zugu: dama lahabasuŋŋu maa nyɛ la Naawunni yiiko din kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda la ti nya tuliginsim; Izraɛli dima daŋŋni la tooni, ka ban pa Izraɛli dima naayi pa.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Lahabaari maa wulisi la Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori ka di bɛ la yɛdda zugu ka laa n‑taasiri la yɛdda paasigu ni, ka ba naayi nya taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi. Hoo din sɔbi seem Naawunni Bukku maa ni, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni daana nin nya nyɔvuri u yɛdda niŋbu zugu.”

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni bɛ la arizana ni, ka u nin ti wulisi u sufu ŋɔn yisigi seem n‑tiri seeba ban mi ŋɔn Naawunni yɛlimaŋni maa ka bu boori ni di yi paalu. Ba za'asiri Naawunni ka tumni ba putoo tuma, ni ba tumbeeri tuma.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Naawunni yɛla bamsim siri bɛ la ba saani, dama ŋɔn Naawunni mammaŋŋa daa kyɛ ka di yi paalu n‑ti ba.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Dama sɔsɔ nini ku toaai n‑nya Naawunni yiiko ni u beebu an ka naari maa: amaa di zaa yɔri, ŋɔn daa naam duniya maa zugu n kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi anŋɔ zaa yɛla: a bamsim maa daa yi paalu lala saha maa, halli ni zuna. Dizugu niriba wa ka noori ni ba ti yiisi ba mansi ba taalli ni.

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Halli ban daa mi Naawunni yɛla maa zaa yɔri, ba daa za'asi ni, ni, ba ku gyeem u ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni zugu, bee, ba puusi u barika di zugu. Ba teesi, ni, ba mari la yɛm, amaa ba ka; ka lala maa kyɛ ka ba yɛm maa baligi zaa.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Ban daa boonni ba mansi baŋŋniba seem la, ba daa kur leebri la gyɛra,

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: halli ka ba daa ti kyɛ Naawunni gyirima din ka naari maa, n‑takki n‑gyeemni bunseesi an ŋmanni la ninsaala, bee, bunyigra, bee, bunkɔbri, bee, bunvuura.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi ba m‑basi ba halibeoo tunfiisa maa puuni, ka ba naayi tumni din bu tu n‑sa'amni taaba ningbina.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Dama ba za'asi la Naawunni yɛlimaŋni maa ka boori ziri, ka naayi gyeemni bunnaamna ka kyɛ buna Naamna maa mammaŋŋa; ŋɔn Naawunni Naamna sumni la pɛ'abu saha din ka baasigu; ammi.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ba m‑basi ba vi kɔrisi tuma ni; halli ka ba pɔ'aba kyɛ doo ni pɔ'a la'aŋŋu ka takki m‑boori ba pɔ'ataaba.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Ka ba dappa gba kyɛ doo ni pɔ'a la'ansuŋŋu ka dɔli ba sufu ni kɔribeeri ka kpɛ zugfa'asim tuma ni ba dabtaaba: ba boori bunseesi an ka soori, ka naayi deaari yɛlibeeri an sum ni ba lala tuma.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Ban daa bu sakki ni ba baŋŋi Naawunni maa zugu n kyɛ ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi ba m‑basi ba toori teesiyɔya ni, nti tumni tunseesi an bu tuusi.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Lala maa zugu ba yi bɛ la tumpoosa ni, ni tumbeeri ni beem tuma, ni ala'akyi tuma ni; ba yɛla zaa nyɛ la nyuuri zappu, ni ninvugkuubu; ba boori la zaba, n‑yɔmsiri niriba, m‑mari halibeoo, ka zuuri la niriba nyaaŋŋa;

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: ba tuuri la niriba ka yaai Naawunni gba; ka nyari ba mansi, m‑mari karimbaani, n‑nyuuri bara, ba kur boori la ba maali n‑nya yɛttɔ'abeeri sɔpaala; ba za'asiri ba ba ni ba ma noori;

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: ba ka yɛm, bee yɛdda, bee wurilim, bee namboo.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Ba mi halli, Naawunni ŋɔn zaali seem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi lala tuma maa, ni, di sum ni ban tumni lala maa kpi. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ba sini ka tumni a ni, ka din gaari zaa nyɛ la ba suŋŋni la seeba ni ba gba tumni lala.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna, n zɔa, i yi zaali zaaligu m‑bugsiri niriba yɛla, i ka sɔseaa nyin nin zaŋŋi n‑wulisi i yɛlimaŋni. Dama yɛttɔ'aseaa zaa ka i ya tɔ'asi n‑galim i taraana, lala yɛttɔ'a maa zugu ka i galimni i mammaŋŋa, nyin ŋɔn bugsiri ba ka na tumni lala tuma maa zugu.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Ti mun mi, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn katti sariya ka galimni ban tumni lala tuma maa n‑lusiri ba, ni, u sariya karibu wa dɔli la yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Bee, i bu baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni zɔri i la namboo ni u ti zi i n‑taasiri ziseaa ka i ŋmaligi ka kyɛ i tumbeeri ni i teesibeeri yɛla? Lala zugu ka i zeemni ŋɔn Naawunni nambɔzoaa, ni u veelim, ni u sugru, an galisi n‑tiri i: bee, m ma'ai ni?

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Ay! I tuppuwumni zugu ka i ku sakki n‑ŋmaligi ka kyɛ i yɛlibeeri maa, ka lala ka i la'asiri toom yɛla n‑zaantiri n‑tiri i maŋŋa, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni suuri daari; sahanseaa ka u laa n‑nyɛ la ŋɔn katti sariya ni yɛlimaŋni ka sɔkam zaa nya.

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka Naawunni nin ti kyɛ ka sɔkam deaai bunseesi an sum ni ŋɔn daa pun tum seem la.

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Baseeba ban kpa n‑tum suŋŋa m‑boori veelim, ni gyirima, ni nyɔvuri din bu sa'amni la ziiya, ban ka Naawunni nin tisi nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Ban mun mari halibeoo n‑kur boori ba mansi ka za'asiri yɛlimaŋni ka dɔli yɛlibeeri, ban ka Naawunni sugyeaa nin kpeesi namsigu ni toom ni.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nira ŋɔn tumni tumbeeri zaa nin nya wahala ni sufusa'aŋŋu: Izraɛli dima nin daŋŋi tooni n‑nya, ka ban pa Izraɛli dima naayi pa.

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Amaa veelim, ni gyirima, ni sufumaasim nin bɛ ni n‑tiri ban niŋni din tuusi: Izraɛli dima nin daŋŋi tooni n‑deaai, ka ban pa Izraɛli dima naayi pa.

english_verse: God doesn't have any favorites!
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni bu wulisiri ninvuggaŋŋsim.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Bankam ka zaaligu maa, ka tumni ala'akyi, ba nin ti baasi yɔri ka zaaligu maa nuu ka di ni; ban mun mari zaaligu maa, ka tumni ala'akyi, ban ka ba nin zaŋŋi zaaligu maa n‑kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: Dama ba wulisiri, ni, zaaligu maa tunsuma bɛ la ba sufu ni, ka ba sufu yɛm kyɛ ka lala zaaligu yi paalu, ba mammaŋŋa teesa yi diri sɛhara n‑galimni ba tunseesi ka laa n‑sakkri ni aseesi yaasa, ni, ana n tuusi.

english_verse: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
translation_verse: Tɔ: anŋɔ zaa nin niŋi la dabsiri seaa Naawunni ŋɔn ya kari saalidima yɛlisɔ'ara sariya la, ka di yi Kirisita Yisa saani na. Lala ka man kur wulisiri niriba, n lahabasuŋŋu mooligu maa ni.

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛtti, ni, i mi Naawunni yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka sakki n‑ti din veela halli nti yakki zaa, ka pun bamsi zaaligu kariŋŋu n‑naai.

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: Ka i laa n‑teesi, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔn datti zoomma, n‑nyɛ neesim n‑ti ban bɛ zibsim ni la;

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: ni, i wulisiri ban kɔŋŋni yɛm; ni, biisi karimba n nyɛ i. I niŋni i mammaŋŋa yɛdda anŋɔ zaa zugu, dama i mari la zaaligu maa, ka yɛm ni yɛlimaŋni zaa gbinni bɛ di ni.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: Tɔ, nyin ŋɔn wulisiri niriba lala maa, i ku wulisi i maŋŋa? Nyin ŋɔn moonni n‑tiri ba, ni, ba di zuura la - i mammaŋŋa zuuri, bee?

english_verse: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
translation_verse: Nyin yɛtti, ni, niriba di niŋni zina la, i niŋni zina i mammaŋŋa? Ka yɛtti, ni, i bu boori buga yɛla, i zuuri buga duri buna?

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Halli i mari la zaaligu maa ka nyuuri bara dizugu, i burigri zaaligu maa ni, m‑boaari Naawunni gyirima?

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli la, ni, “Yarima zugu ka Naawunni yuuri sa'amni ban pa Izraɛli dima saani.”

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gunibu yɛla muna - i yi dɔli zaaligu maa tuma suŋŋa, dinŋɔna ka i gunibu mari la anfaani; amaa i yi burigi zaaligu maa ni, i labgi la hoo ban bu guni sɔ la.

english_verse: In fact, if they obey the Law, they are as good as anyone who is circumcised.
translation_verse: Ban bu guni la mun yi dɔli zaaligu maa deede, ba ku nya ba la hoo ban guni maa? Ay! - ba nin nya ba lala.

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn bu guni ka dɔli zaaligu maa nin galim nyin ŋɔn mari zaaligu gbaŋŋu ka guni ka mun burigi zaaligu maa ni.

english_verse: Just because you live like a Jew and are circumcised doesn't make you a real Jew.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn nyɛ Izraɛli nira la paalu ni kɔkɔa pa la Izraɛli nira halaali; ka gunibu maŋni mun pa la din niŋi ningbina paalu ni kɔkɔa.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: Ay! U sufu ni ka nira n nyɛ Izraɛli nira halaali, ka gunibu maŋni gba nyɛ la sufu ni yɛttɔ'a. Di yi la Naawunni Sii maa ni na, pa gbana sɔppu saani. Lala nira pɛ'abu bu yiiri la ninsaala saani, amaa Naawunni saani.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna bɔ anfaani m bɛ ni n‑ti nira ŋɔn nyɛ Izraɛli nira zugu? Bee, bɔ ka gunibu paasira?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ay! Anfaani pam m bɛ ni, walla! Tuuri, Naawunni daa zaŋŋi u yɛligu maa n‑niŋi la Izraɛli dima nuu ni, ni ba ti gura.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ, ba seeba ka yɛdda: tɔ, ka ba yɛdda kaalinsi maa wulisi, ni, Naawunni gba ka yɛdda, bee?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Ay! di pa lala. Naawunni kur kpalim n‑zɛ u yɛlimaŋni ni, halli ni ninsaala zaa ŋɔn nyɛ ziri daana. Kaman di pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Lala ka i yɛlimaŋni yi paalu ni, Ni, i yɛla zaa mari yɛdda; Ba yi ya kari i sariya I yɛttɔ'a nin zɛ suŋŋa n‑nyaŋŋi ba.”

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Ni, “N yi ti ma'ai ziri ka n ziri kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛlimaŋni yiiri paalu, tɔ, m paasiri Naawunni pɛ'abu di zugu. Ka dinŋɔna bɔ zugu ka ba galim ma hoo tumbeeri daana?”

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Ni, “Dinzugu ya kyɛ ka ti tum tumbeeri, ka lala ka bunsuŋŋu nin yi di ni na.” Halli ninvugseeba zuuri n nyaaŋŋa n‑yɛtti, ni, man tɔ'asiri n‑yɛtti lala maa zugu. Ninvugseeba ban tuuri ma n‑yɛtti lala maa siri sumni ni tubdarigri.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ti yɛtti la wula? Ti Izraɛli dima maa muna - ti yɛttɔ'a sɔa, bee, a bu sɔa? Ay! ti yɛla bu sɔa; dama ti pun wulisiya, ni, sɔkam zaa bɛ la tumbeeri ni - ban nyɛ Izraɛli niriba ni ban pa Izraɛli dima zaa.

english_verse: The Scriptures tell us, “No one is acceptable to God!
translation_verse: Kaman hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Sɔsɔ ka yɛlimaŋni, Pa yinni kɔkɔ gba.

english_verse: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
translation_verse: Sɔ mun ka ni m‑mari yɛm, Sɔsɔ bu boori Naawunni ziiya.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Sɔkam zaa burigri soori ni, Sɔkam zaa leebi la bunkyɔ'aŋŋu. Sɔ n ka ni n‑tumni din veela, Pa yinni kɔkɔ gba.”

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: Ni#:- “Ba lɔŋŋu ŋmanni la gballi noori - Gbaliseaa kan na bu pi: Ba yɛttɔ'a zaa nyɛ la yɔmsigu.” Ni#:- “Ba mari la gyɛriŋŋa toom n‑sɔ'ari ba nangbana ni.”

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: Ni#:- “Ba zulimbeeri toom yiiri ba nɔya ni na.”

english_verse: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
translation_verse: Ba sa'amni la buna n‑gaari, N‑taasi la sufusa'aŋŋu beerim m‑paasi.

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: Ba mun bu mi sugru soori.”

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: Ni#:- “Ba mun bu tiri Naawunni gyirima.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ti mi halli, ni, zaaligu sɔppu ŋɔn yɛli seem la, ka yɛtti n‑tiri la zaaligu niriba la - di nyɛ la, Izraɛli dima maa. Lala zugu ka ba ku laa n‑nya kukɔri, ka baŋŋi, ni, duniya niriba zaa mari la yɛttɔ'a ni ba ti tɔ'asi Naawunni tooni.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: Din laa m‑paasi, ninsaala zaa ku toaai n‑dɔli zaaligu soori ka naayi yi Naawunni sariya karibu ni na, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana n nyɛ u. Dama zaaligu maa tuma nyɛ la lala: di kur kyɛ ka taalli yɛla yi paalu.

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ muna ka Naawunni wulisi u yɛlimaŋni soori kan bu dɔli la zaaligu sɔppu la, halli ka zaaligu gbaŋŋu ni anabidima yɛla daa pun mooli, ni, di nin niŋi lala.

english_verse: God treats everyone alike. He accepts people only because they have faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Naawunni ŋɔn tiri yɛlimaŋni seem wa dɔli la Yisa Masiiya yɛdda niŋbu, ka dinŋɔ nyɛ la lala n‑ti sɔkam zaa ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda gba. Wɔligri ka ni,

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: dama sɔkam tum la taalli ka kɔŋŋi soori ni ba paai Naawunni gyirima ziiya.

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn tisiri ba piini yɔri, samni ka ni, la zugu, u yiisi ba taalli ni, ka zaali ba yɛlimaŋni, Kirisita Yisa ŋɔn fa ba m‑basi zugu.

english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: U boori la u niŋi lala n‑wulisi u yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn bɛ seem saha kanŋɔa; dizugu u mammaŋŋa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka laa n‑nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn deaari sɔkam ŋɔn niŋi Yisa yɛdda la gba, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ti nin nyu bara dizugu, bee? Ay! Barinyuubu ka ni. Wula zugu? Dama di zaa bu dɔli la zaaligu maa dɔligu, amaa di dɔli la sɔseaa - di nyɛ la yɛdda niŋbu soori.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna ti mari teesa, ni, niriba bɛ la yɛlimaŋni dima ba yɛdda niŋbu zugu; di pa la zaaligu la tuma zugu ka ba nya yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Ammi, Naawunni nyɛ la Izraɛli dima kɔkɔ Naawunni? - ka u ba'a ka ban kpalim la ni? Ay! di pa lala - ban pa Izraɛli dima la gba Naawunni n nyɛ u.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu, ti kyiisiri, ni, anfaani ka zaaligu la ni, halli beela? - dama ti boori yɛdda niŋbu yɛla. Ay! pa lala: ti ya zaŋŋi zaaligu maa n‑zaali la suŋŋa di zugu.

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Ka di yi daa nyɛ la tunsuma zugu ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, dinŋɔna u da mari seelli ni u kuuli di zugu, amaa pa Naawunni saani ka u daa kuuli.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Halli NaaDauda gba daa pun yɛli lala nira yɛla - di nyɛ la: ninvugsɔ Naawunni ŋɔn yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana, ka di pa la u tuma zugu. U daa tɔ'asi di daama zugsuŋŋu yɛla, ni#:-

english_verse: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu gba n‑ti seeba Naawunni sariya ŋɔn bu lusi ba n‑yɛli, ni, ba galim n‑tum la taalli.”

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka NaaDauda ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi zugsunseaa wa nyɛ la ban guni la kɔkɔ dinni? Bee, ban bu guni su ka gba? Ay! - ban bu guni la gba nin mara, dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni, “U yɛdda zugu ka Naawunni daa yɛli Ibrahim, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.”

english_verse: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
translation_verse: Ka wula saha ka Naawunni daa yɛli lala? Di saha maa ka Ibrahim guniya, bee, u daa na bu guni? - Ay! di saha ka u na bu guni.

english_verse: Abraham let himself be circumcised to show he had been accepted because of his faith even before he was circumcised. This makes Abraham the father of all who are acceptable to God because of their faith, even though they are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka ba daa naayi guni u, ka u gunibu wa daa wulisi la, ni, Naawunni pun yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana u yɛdda zugu; yɛdda wa mun ka u daa pun niŋi ka ba daa na bu guni u. Dinzugu ŋɔn Ibrahim nyɛ la yɛdda niŋniba zaa ba - baseeba ban niŋi Naawunni yɛdda ka na bu guni la - ka di nin kyɛ ka Naawunni yɛtti, ni, ba nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni dima.

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: Ka u laa n‑nyɛ la seeba ban guni la zaa ba m‑paasi - baseeba ban pa la ban guni kɔkɔa, amaa ba yɛdda niŋbu gba dɔli la ti zaa ba, Ibrahim, soori maa, dama ŋɔn daa niŋi Naawunni yɛdda pɔi, ka naayi guni.

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti u nangbambasiri n‑ti ‘Ibrahim ni u nyaaŋŋa’ maa, u daa yɛli, ni, u nin kyɛ ka u di duniya wa hoo u faari; u daa niŋi lala maa, pa la Ibrahim ŋɔn daa dɔli zaaligu la zugu, amaa ŋɔn daa niŋi ŋɔn Naawunni yɛdda la zugu.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Ka di yi nyɛ la zaaligu zugu ka Naawunni nangbambasiri paari ninvugseeba ban dɔli ka, dinŋɔna yɛdda nyɛ la yɔri, ka Naawunni nangbambasiri gba nyɛ yɔri.

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: Zaaligu la tumni la sufuyisigri zugu, amaa zaaligu yi ka ni, zaaligu galiŋŋu gba ku bɛ ni.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Nangbambasiri maa daa kyɛ na la yɛdda zugu, ka ka zɛ la deede, ni ka ti leebi piini n‑tiri Ibrahim nyaaŋŋa zaa, pa la seeba ban dɔli zaaligu la kɔkɔa, amaa ni seeba ban niŋni yɛdda hoo Ibrahim gba. Ka ŋɔn Ibrahim nyɛ la ti zaa ba.

english_verse: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
translation_verse: Litaafi yɛliya, ni, “N nin kyɛ ka i leebi bura pam yaaba.” - ŋɔn daa niŋi Naawunni yɛdda maa zugu nla. U daa niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni tisiri la nyɔvuri, ka u yɛligu daa kyɛ ka din daa ka ni la yi ti bɛ ni.

english_verse: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
translation_verse: Ibrahim daa niŋi yɛdda ka mari tama, halli din daa ti paai ni u ti koaai tama maa zaa u daa kpalim m‑mari tama, ka di zugu u daa leebi ‘bura pam yaaba’, dama Litaafi daa pun yɛli lala, ni, “Lala ka i nyaaŋŋa nin ti bɛ ni.”

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Ii - anŋɔ zaa yɔri u yɛdda daa bu basi u, ka u daa bu birisi Naawunni nangbambasiri la; u yɛdda wa daa ti u la yiiko, ka u daa pɛ'ai Naawunni.

english_verse: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
translation_verse: U daa maali n‑kpamsi u sufu ni yɛdda, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn basi u nangbambasiri maa seem la, di siri nin niŋi lala.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Ni, u ‘yɛtti, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana’ - ba daa bu sɔbi lala ŋɔn Ibrahim zugu kɔkɔa.

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
translation_verse: Ay! - ba daa sɔbi di lala maa n‑ti la tirima, dama ba nin yɛli ti gba lala, ti seeba ban niŋi yɛdda n‑tiri Naawunni, ni, u daa yisigi ti Dugma Yisa kum ni la.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Ti tumbeeri zugu ka ba daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn Yisa n‑tisi ni u kpi; ka u mun yisigi kum ni, ni ti ti yi na hoo yɛlimaŋni dima.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dizugu tin niŋi yɛdda la zugu kyɛ ka Naawunni deaai ti, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima. Ka lala kyɛ ka nangbanyinni bɛ ni tin ni Naawunni sunsukkuni, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn Yisa zugu daa kyɛ ka ti nya yɛdda niŋbu soori nti kpɛ n‑zɛ tin bɛ zaazisunseaa wa ni. Di nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn daa tum lala tunsuma n‑ti ti maa. Dizugu ka ti mari sufupeelli, dama ti mari tama, ni, ti nin nya Naawunni gyirima veelim.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Din paasi maa, ti mari sufupeelli halli ti wahala diibu puuni gba, dama ti mi, ni, wahala diibu paasiri ti sugru ni sufukpeemni.

english_verse: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
translation_verse: Sugru mun paasiri ti beesisuŋŋu, ka beesisuŋŋu mun paasiri ti tama.

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: Ka tama maa paasigu ku kyɛ ka ti di vi, dama Naawunni zaŋŋi u Siiya n‑ti ti ka ti baŋŋi u wurilim ŋɔn zoaai seem di zugu.

english_verse: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
translation_verse: Kirisita muna - sahanseaa ti daa na ka yaa la, lala saha maa deede ka u daa kpi n‑ti tirim tumbeeri dima maa.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Di tɔ pam ni sɔ sakki n‑kpi n‑ti ninvugsɔa - halli n‑ti yɛlimaŋni daansɔ gba. Amaa di bu ya'ai ka nira yi mari sufukpeemni n‑sakki n‑kpi ninvugsuŋŋu zugu.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔna - ŋɔn daa niŋi seem n‑wulisi ti, ni, u wuri ti pam nŋɔa#:- tin daa na bɛ tumbeeri ni sahanseaa la, Kirisita daa kpi n‑ti ti.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di yi bɛ lala maa, ka Naawunni pun deaai ti, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima, Yisa Masiiya ziim maa zugu, dinŋɔna ti mi halli, ni, ŋɔn Yisa zugu ka ti nin laa n‑yi Naawunni sugyeaa ni gba.

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Dama tin daa nyɛ Naawunni dindaandima sahanseaa la, u Bii Yisa kum maa zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni deaai ti taalli sindaani maa. Di zugu ti mi halli, ni, Yisa nyɔvuri zugu nin kyɛ ka ti nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Amaa pa dinŋɔ kɔkɔ gba. See ka ti mari sufupeelli pam Naawunni saani, ka gyeemni u ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu dama ŋɔn Yisa n daa zɔ sindaani n‑ti ti ka u Ba deaai.

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: Tumbeoo siri daa pun bɛ la duniya wa puuni pɔi, ka Musa na bu deaai zaaligu maa yɛla Naawunni saani. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ba daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri tumbeoo galinsi yɛla, zaaligu la ŋɔn daa na ka ni la zugu.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Amaa kum daa pun su ba zaa fukumsi n‑yi Adam saha halli nti paai Musa. Sɔkam daa kpi, halli ban daa bu tum tumbɛseelli kan ŋmanni Adam dinni la nti za'asi Naawunni noori zaaligu. Ŋɔn Adam muna - u nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni n‑wulisiri sɔ ŋɔn nin naayi kyɛ na la yɛla ŋɔn ti ya bɛ seem.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni piini yiiko bu ŋmanni tumbeoo dinni. Ay! - di gaari lala zugu pam. Ti mi halli, ni, ninvugyinni taalli zugu ka niriba pam daa kpi; amaa Naawunni piini zoaari m‑paasiri n‑suŋŋni niriba pam, ka lala gba yiiri la ninvugyinni zugu ni na - di nyɛ la: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Din laa m‑paasi: Naawunni piini siri bu ŋmanni la ninvugyinni maa tumbeoo la yɛla: dama tumbeoo yinni kɔkɔ zugu ka Naawunni daa kari sɔkam sariya n‑lusi ba, amaa Naawunni piini zugu daa kyɛ ka tumbeeri pam yɛla daa kpiisi, ka ba yiisi niriba maa, ni, ba mari yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Tumbeoo yinni taalli zugu daa kyɛ ka kum su sɔkam fukumsi, ka di daa yi lala tumbɛdaana yinni maa saani na. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ti mi halli, ni, seeba ban deaai Naawunni yɛlimaŋni u piinisuŋŋu pam zugu la nin ti su nyɔvuri naam fukumsi, ka di mun nyɛ la tunsuŋŋu daana yinni zugu, ŋɔn nyɛ Yisa Kirisita.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zaa kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi, ni, nira yinni tumbeoo zugu kyɛ ka niriba zaa yɛttɔ'a lu Naawunni saani, ka ba zaa nya taalli u saani. Lala yaasa ka nira yinni maa tunsuŋŋu zugu kyɛ ka niriba zaa yi taalli ni, ka ba mari yɛlimaŋni Naawunni saani nti nya nyɔvuri.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Ninvugyinni maa daa za'asi Naawunni noori, ka di zugu niriba pam daa nya taalli, ni, ala'akyi dima n nyɛ ba. Lala yaasa ka ninvugyinni ŋɔnla daa sakki Naawunni noori, ka di zugu niriba pam daa yi taalli ni, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima n nyɛ ba.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Zaaligu muna - ba daa mari ka na ni ba ti kyɛ ka taalli tumpu maali m‑paasira. Amaa taalli ŋɔn maali m‑paasiri seem la, Naawunni piini gba daa paasi n‑gaari lala zugu.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Tumbeoo zugu n‑daa la, dama tumbeoo daa mari kum yiiko n‑su niriba fukumsi, amaa lala ka Naawunni piinisuŋŋu mari yɛlimaŋni yiiko n‑su ba fukumsi, halli ka ba ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Tɔ; dinŋɔna ti ya yɛli la wula? Ni, ti kpalimma n‑tumni tumbeeri ni ti ti kyɛ ka Naawunni piinisuma zoaari m‑paasira?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Ay! - di pa lala. Ti nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban pun kpi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Tumbeoo yiiko yɛla ni. Ka wula zugu ka ti nin laa m‑bɛ tumbeeri tumpu ni?

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Din laa m‑paasi, ban daa su ti koom ni ti ti paasi Yisa zugu n‑kpi maa, ba daa zaŋŋi ti m‑paasi u zugu n‑sɔ'ai. Ka di gbinni nyɛ la nŋɔa: ni, ti Ba Naawunni gyirima yiiko daa kyɛ ka Yisa doaai kum ni; ka di zugu lala kyɛ ka ti gba doaai nti bɛ beesipaalli.

english_verse: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
translation_verse: Dama ti yi ŋmanni la ti Dugma Yisa n‑kpi u kum wa taabu, di kpa, ni, ti nin ŋmanni hoo ŋɔna u kum doaari ni gba.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Ka ti mi, ni, ti beesikura la ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑la'am m‑paasi Masiiya zugu n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu, ni lala kyɛ ka ti beesikura ningbina nin sa'am m‑basi, Tumbeoo ŋɔn su a yɛla zaa maa zugu. Dinŋɔna ti ningbina ku laa n‑nyɛ seesi Ala'akyi ŋɔn su hoo ban su daaba seem maa.

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn kpi zaa pun yi ala'akyi ni.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka tirim ŋɔn pun paasi Kirisita zugu n‑kpi maa, dinŋɔna ti mari la yɛdda, ni, ti nin paasi u zugu m‑mari nyɔvuri gba.

english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Ti mi, ni, Kirisita daa pun yisigi kum ni, ka di zugu u ku laa n‑kpi, dama kum laa n‑ka yiiko u saani.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa kpi maa, u daa kpi yummu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Tumbeoo yiiko saani: ka ŋɔn mari nyɔvuri maa muna, u mari n‑ti la Naawunni saani.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya gba teesima ya mansi yɛla lala. Ni, ya gba pun kpi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Tumbeoo yiiko ni, ka mari nyɔvuri n‑ti Naawunni, yan la'am m‑bɛ Kirisita Yisa saani zugu.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Ya ningbina nyɛ la seesi an kpiira, ka di zugu yan di kyɛ ka Tumbeoo su a fukumsi nti kyɛ ka a dɔli Tumbeoo kɔribeeri yɛla.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Ya di laa n‑zaŋŋi ya ningbina luga zaa n‑tiri Tumbeoo, ka a ti leebi Tumbeoo tuma neema. Amaa ya zaŋŋma ya ningbina luga zaa n‑tiri Naawunni ka a ti leebi u yɛlimaŋni tuma neema, dama ya nyɛ la seeba ban pun neaai kum ni.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna Ala'akyi ku laa n‑nyɛ ya dugma, dama zaaligu maa bu su ya yɛla, amaa ka Naawunni piinisuma tuma n su ya.

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Bɔ yaasa? Zaaligu bu su ti, na'ala Naawunni piini n su ti: ammi, ti tumma tumbeeri lala zugu? - Ay! pa lala.

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Amaa ti puusiri Naawunni barika! Dama ni ya daa bɛ Ala'akyi suulinsi ni n‑wumni Tumbeoo yɛligu hoo daaba maa zaa, ya daa wum yɛlimaŋni mooligu la ka doaai n‑takki ya sufu ni teesa ka sakki n‑deaai wulisigu maa.

english_verse: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
translation_verse: Ka ya daa laa n‑yi Ala'akyi suulinsi ni na, ka leebi Yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn su seeba.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Tɔ, n tɔ'asiri la ninsaala duniya wa puuni beesigu yɛla n‑tiri ya, dama a gbinni bamsim bu tɔa; ka yan bɛ la duniya wa puuni la zugu n kyɛ ka ya na ka yaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛlizulima yɛla. Ya daa kyɛ ka ya ningbina luga zaa leebi hoo daaba n‑ti da'ari tuma ni zaaligu za'asigu. Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ya zaŋŋi ya ningbina n‑leebi la daaba n‑ti Naawunni yɛlimaŋni la, n‑tumni tunsuma kasikasi.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Yan daa bɛ Ala'akyi suulinsi ni hoo daaba sahanseaa la, Yɛlimaŋni maa daa bu su ya fukumsi.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Ka anfaani bɔ ka ya daa nya di zugu? Ay, zaalim! Lala tumbeeri maa zugu daa kur kyɛ ka ya di vi, ka yɛla wa zaa naari nyɛ la kum.

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba fa ya m‑basi n‑yiisi Ala'akyi fukumsi ni na, ni ya leebi Naawunni suulinsi niriba. Dinŋɔ zugu ka ya nyari anfaani pam ka leebi la Naawunni dinni, din mun naari nyɛ la nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka di daa nyɛ la lala n‑ti ya, m mabiisi. Ba daa kyɛ ka ya paasi Kirisita ningbina zugu ka ku ya, di zugu ya laa n‑ka ni n‑ti zaaligu yɛla. Ka dizugu ya nya soori ni ya ti leebi sɔ dinni, ŋɔn yisigi kum ni - di nyɛ la Yisa; ni di niŋi anfaani n‑ti Naawunni.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Sahanseaa la, ti beesigu daa dɔli ti ningbinkɔrisi yɛla. Di saha ka tumbeeri tumpu boorimma daa dɔli zaaligu maa n‑nya yaa n‑tumni ti ningbina luga zaa ni; ka ti daa bu nya anfaani di zugu - ay! kum kɔkɔ ka ti daa nya.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ ti kpiya ni n‑ti zaaligu maa, ka di zugu zaaligu wa bu laa n‑su ti yɛla. Lala yi kyɛ ka ti bu laa n‑dɔli sɔkurigu kan nyɛ zaaligu sɔppu maa dinni la, amaa sɔpaalli kan nyɛ Sii maa dinni.

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Dinzugu, ti ya yɛli la wula? - ni, Naawunni zaaligu maa nyɛ la ala'akyi, bee? Ay! pa lala. Amaa di yi da pa la zaaligu la zugu, n naa n‑ku baŋŋi ala'akyi ŋɔn nyɛ seelli. Dama zaaligu maa yi da bu yɛli, ni, “Di kyɛ ka i sufu kɔrisi kpɛ i kpeaa buna zugu.”, n naa n‑ku baŋŋi niriba neema boobu yɛla.

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Ala'akyi daa dɔli zaaligu la n‑nya zaazisuŋŋu pɔi, ka naayi kyɛ ka neema boobu teesibeeri balibu pam kpɛ n sufu ni. Amaa zaaligu yi ka ni, Ala'akyi mun gba kpi ni.

english_verse: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
translation_verse: Sahanseaa la, n daa mari nyɔvuri, ka na zi zaaligu yɛla maa. Amaa zaaligu la ŋɔn daa ti paai ma na, ka daa kyɛ ka Ala'akyi neaai ni,

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: ka man kpi. Zaaligu la gbinni nyɛ la nyɔvuri, amaa man saani ka daa zi la kum na.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Zaaligu maa zugu ka Ala'akyi daa nya soori n‑yɔmsi ma, n‑zaŋŋi zaaligu maa yɛla n‑kyɛ ka n kpi.

english_verse: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
translation_verse: Ka bunsuŋŋu dinŋɔ n kyɛ ka kum paai ma la, bee wula? - Ay, pa lala! Ala'akyi n nyɛ din ziiri kum na, ka ka daa dɔlisi bunsuŋŋu wa n‑tum lala maa; ka di zugu ka ti baŋŋi ala'akyi beeri ŋɔn nyɛ seem la. Lala zugu ka zaaligu la kyɛ ka tumbeeri zaa yiiri paalu.

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Ti mi halli, ni, zaaligu la nyɛ la seaa kan dɔlisi ni siiya; amaa man mun nyɛ duniya wa ni ninsaala, ka ba koosiri ma ni hoo daabri n‑ti Ala'akyi.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Halli n ku toaai m‑baŋŋi man tumni seelli. Dama m bu tumni man boori tunseesi. Ay! - tunseesi man bu boori la zaa ka n tumna.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Lala zugu, n yi tum taaliseaa ka yɛli, ni, m bu boori lala, n yɛttɔ'a wa wulisiya, ni, zaaligu la veela, ka n sakki lala.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔ pa la mani n tumni taalli maa, amaa Ala'akyi m bɛ n saani n‑tumna.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: M mi, ni, bunsunseelli ka m beesigu ni, halli beela - di nyɛ la n duniya wa ni ningbina la ni beesigu ni. M boori tunsuma tumpu, amaa n kɔŋŋi la a tumpu.

english_verse: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
translation_verse: M boori la tunsuŋŋu tumpu n‑kɔŋŋi, ka laa n‑tumni tumbɛseelli man bu boori maa.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: N yi tum taaliseaa ka yɛli, ni, m bu boori lala, dinŋɔ pa la mani n tumni taalli maa, amaa Ala'akyi m bɛ n saani n‑tumna.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: N yi baŋŋi yɛlimaŋni nŋɔa: ni, n yi kur boori ni n ti tumni din veela, lala saha maa ka tumbeoo tumpu bɛ n saani.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Dama n sufu ni teesa zaa kur sakkri ni n‑tiri Naawunni zaaligu tuma,

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: amaa m baŋŋiya, ni, zaaliseaa n bɛ n ningbiŋŋu luga ni. Ka lala zaalibeoo tuusiri tɔppu n‑niŋni n sufu ni teesa zaalisuŋŋu la, ka nyaŋŋi ma n‑gbaai ma nti kpari n ningbiŋŋu luga ni teesibeoo ziiya.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Waay, waay, waay! Man wahala daana wa! Ŋɔni n lee n‑ya fa ma m‑basi, n‑yiisi ma duniya wa ni ningbina beesigu wa ni, din taasiri ma kum ni?

english_verse: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_verse: Ay! M pɛ'ari Naawunni ka puusiri u barika, dama ŋɔn pun fa ma m‑basi, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya ti Dugma zugu. Ah, tɔ! M mammaŋŋa sufu ni teesa puuni ka n dɔli la Naawunni zaaligu sɔsuŋŋu, amaa n duniya wa ni ningbina beesigu puuni ka n na dɔli la Ala'akyi sɔbeoo.

english_verse: If you belong to Christ Jesus, you won't be punished.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna ba ku laa n‑toaai n‑kari Kirisita Yisa niriba sariya n‑lusi ba, halli beela.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: Dama zaalipaalli bɛ la Yisa Kirisita saani; Naawunni Sii maa zaaligu n nyɛ ka, ka ka tiri nyɔvuri. Zaaligu wa n kyɛ ka i yi zaalikurigu la suulinsi ni na - Ala'akyi ŋɔn su zaaliseaa maa nla, kan tiri kum.

english_verse: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
translation_verse: Ban daa sɔbi zaaligu seaa n‑dooli la muna, di daa kɔŋŋi yiiko, duniya wa ni ninsaalidima ningbina ŋɔn boori tumbeoo maa zugu. Amaa Naawunni mammaŋŋa daa zaŋŋi u yiiko n‑tumna. U daa kyɛ ka u toori Biiya kyɛ ti saani na nti leebi ŋɔn mari duniya wa ni ningbina, hoo tumbeeri dima ŋɔn mari seem la - halli tumbeeri zugu ka u daa kyɛ na gba. Lala ka Naawunni daa nyaŋŋi n‑kari sariya n‑lusi Ala'akyi; dama duniya wa ni ningbina maa ni ka tumbeeri bɛ ni n‑tumna.

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: Di zaa gbinni nyɛ la nŋɔa: ni, Naawunni zaaligu ŋɔn ya zaali ti yɛlimaŋni maa, lala yɛlimaŋni naayi bɛ ti beesigu ni zaa - tiseeba ban bu dɔli ningbina kɔribeeri sɔkura la, amaa sii maa sɔsuŋŋu, bumpaalli, ka ti dɔla.

english_verse: People who are ruled by their desires think only of themselves. Everyone who is ruled by the Holy Spirit thinks about spiritual things.
translation_verse: Dama seeba ban bɛ duniya wa ningbinkɔrisi ni la, ba zikki zaa bɛ ningbinkɔrisi yɛla ni; amaa seeba ban bɛ sii maa beesisuŋŋu ni la, ban mun zikki kur bɛ sisuŋŋu yɛla ni.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Ningbinkɔrisi teesa tiri la kum, amaa sii maa beesigu teesa tisiri la nyɔvuri ni sufudoaa.

english_verse: Our desires fight against God, because they do not and cannot obey God's laws.
translation_verse: Duniya wa ni ningbinkɔrisi teesa kur zaŋŋi la Naawunni dimni; a bu sakkri n‑tiri Naawunni zaaligu - ka mun pun ku toaai n‑sakki lala.

english_verse: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka ninvugseeba ban beesigu dɔli ningbinkɔrisi soori la ku toaai n‑tigi Naawunni nini.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Yarima muna - duniya wa ningbinkɔrisi bu su ya, amaa Sii maa beesisuŋŋu ni ka ya bɛ ni. Halli sɔ yi ka Kirisita Siiya, u pa la Yisa dinni gba.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Ya duniya wa ni ningbina muna - kum su a, tumbeeri zugu. Amaa di zaa yɔri, Kirisita yi bɛ ya ni, ya siisi mari la nyɔvuri, Naawunni ŋɔn deaai ya hoo yɛlimaŋni dima maa zugu.

english_verse: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
translation_verse: dama ya yi ti dɔli ningbina beesibeoo soori ya nin kpi. Amaa ya beesigu yi dɔli la Sii maa soori, lala yi kyɛ ka ningbinkɔrisi kpi, ka yarima mun nin neaai.

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: Dama sɔkam ŋɔn dɔli Sii maa beesigu la nyɛ la Naawunni biiya.

english_verse: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
translation_verse: Halli Sii maa mammaŋŋa paasiri la ti toori siisi zugu n‑wulisiri, ni, ti siri nyɛ la Naawunni biisi.

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ti yi nyɛ la Naawunni biisi, ti laa n‑nyɛ la u faari dittiba. Ti nin di faari Naawunni saani, halli tin ni Kirisita nin la'am n‑di ka. Tin ni ŋɔn yi siri ti la'am n‑di u wahala, dinŋɔna ti ya la'amni n‑nya u gyirima gba.

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
translation_verse: Man ŋɔn nya seem maa nŋɔa#:- pampaŋŋɔ namsigu wa ku toaai m‑ma'asi ni gyirima veelim seaa ban nin kasigi n‑ti ti la.

english_verse: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni daa naam duniya zaa, ka ka na bɛ ni n‑guusiri sahanseaa ŋɔn ya yiisi u biisi paalu ni na maa.

english_verse: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
translation_verse: Dama duniya wa zaa ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑kpeesi yɛttɔ'ayɔŋŋu ni, ka di pa la duniya yuubu zugu, amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa zaali lala maa zugu. Dama u daa mari tama,

english_verse: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
translation_verse: ni, duniya wa zaa nin nya fa-m‑basi ka ku laa m‑bɛ sa'aŋŋu suulinsi ni; di saha ka ka nin paai mansuulinsi ni gyirima ziiya, hoo Naawunni biisi zaa ŋɔn nin ti mari seem la.

english_verse: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
translation_verse: Dama ti mi halli, ni, duniya wa zaa nyuusiri ni ka diri la wahala hoo pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn bɛ dɔ'am wahala maa ni ŋɔn nyuusiri seem la. Ka di bɛ la lala halli ni zuna.

english_verse: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
translation_verse: Ka di pa la duniya wa kɔkɔa, amaa ti mammansi gba, tirim ban daŋŋi n‑deaai Sii maa, ti gba nyuusiri ni ti sufu ni n‑guusiri sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn nin deaai ti hoo u biisi, ka fa ti ningbina m‑basi la.

english_verse: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
