translation_verse: Dama ti pun deaai tuliginsim, ka di nyɛ la tin mari lala tama maa ziiya. Tɔ, ka ti nini yi pun nya bunseelli, lala pa la tama marigu; dama sɔsɔ ku laa n‑yɛli, ni, u mari tama, ka mun pun nya.

english_verse: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
translation_verse: Amaa ti yi mari tama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti nini ŋɔn bu nyari bunseaa, ti nin guri di la ni sugru.

english_verse: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
translation_verse: Lala ka Sii maa suŋŋni ti, tin ka yaa maa zugu. Dama ti bu mi din da sum ni ti susi Naawunni seela, amaa Sii maa zɔri ti sindaani n‑tiri ti ni nimmoaa, halli ka sɔ ku toaai n‑yɛli u nyuusigu gbinni zaa ŋɔn nyɛ seem gba.

english_verse: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
translation_verse: Naawunni mun viisiri m‑baŋŋi din bɛ ninsaalidima sufu ni, ka laa m‑baŋŋi din bɛ Sii maa teesa ni, dama Sii maa zɔri la Naawunni niriba sindaani n‑tiri ba, ka ŋɔn niŋni seem maa dɔli la Naawunni yuubu soori.

english_verse: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
translation_verse: Ti mi halli, ni, seelli kam zaa ni, Naawunni suŋŋni la ninvugseeba ban wuri u maa - di nyɛ la: baseeba ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa daŋŋi m‑pii maa, u yuubu la zugu.

english_verse: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
translation_verse: Dama u siri daa daŋŋi m‑mi u niriba, ka laa n‑zaali ba, ni ba ti ŋmanni u Bii la. Ka lala kyɛ ka u Bii maa nin nyɛ Zuuwa u babiisi pam saani.

english_verse: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑zaali seeba lala maa, ban ka u daa yiisi; ka daa yiisi ba, n‑deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima. Ka ŋɔn daa deaai ba lala maa, u daa laa n‑ti ba gyirima.

english_verse: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna wula ka ti ya yɛli di zugu? Naawunni yi bɛ ti saani, ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑zabri ti? Ay! sɔ ka ni.

english_verse: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
translation_verse: Ŋɔna ŋɔn daa bu za'asi, ni, u toori Bii ku kpi, amaa u daa zaŋŋi u n‑tisi ti zaa zugu; ka dinŋɔna, wula zugu ka u ku ti ti bunsuma kam zaa m‑paasi u zugu? - U ku toaai m‑mɔŋŋi.

english_verse: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
translation_verse: Ŋɔni n lee n‑nya galinsi ni u ti tɔ'asi n‑galim Naawunni ninvuggamsa maa? - ay! sɔ ka ni. Dama Naawunni pun deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima.

english_verse: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
translation_verse: Bee, ŋɔni n nin lee n‑kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba? Dama di nyɛ la Kirisita ŋɔn daa kpi, ka din gaari lala, u nyɛ la ŋɔn yisigi kum ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ziya Naawunni nudirigu ya'ari la, ka laa n‑zɔri ti sindaani Naawunni saani.

english_verse: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑wɔligi tin ni Kirisita wurilim? Daaŋŋu bee? - bee mugsigu? Ban tumni ti putoo nin niŋi? Kɔm ŋɔn ti lu, bee, ti ti kɔŋŋi kyinkyina? Dabeem yɛla nin niŋi, bee tɔppu?

english_verse: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
translation_verse: - Hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Nyin, Naawunni, zugu ka ba kuuri ti, Beoo kam zaa ka ba kuuri ti. Dizugu ti ŋmanni la peesi - Peesi ban ya kɔri seeba.”

english_verse: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
translation_verse: - Ay! Anŋɔ zaa puuni ti nin nya nyaŋŋni, Yisa ŋɔn wuri ti maa zugu.

english_verse: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
translation_verse: Man ŋɔn ya tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ya wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, man ŋɔn bɛ Kirisita saani maa zugu; m bu ma'ari ziri, halli Naawunni Sii Kasi kur kyɛ ka n sufu ni teesa wulisiri lala gba.

english_verse: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
translation_verse: Ni, m mari la sufusa'aŋŋu pam, ni beerim din bu naara; ka di zaa nyɛ la m mabiisi, Yɛhudiyadima maa, zugu. Dama di yi naa n‑suŋŋi bana, man naa n‑sakki, ni, Naawunni ti za'asi ma, ka kari ma Kirisita saani.

english_verse: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
translation_verse: Ba nyɛ la Izraɛli dima: Naawunni daa deaai ba hoo u biisi ka wulisi ba u gyirima veelim; u daa pɔ noori n‑ti ba nangbanyinni zugu, ka daa zaali ba u zaaligu, ni ba wungyeeŋŋu soori yɛla; ka u daa ti ba noori zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsuma tisibu.

english_verse: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Ban su kurimni sa yaadima yɛla; halli ba butiri maa ni, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u ninsaala ningbina yɛla maa, ka Kirisita Masiiya daa yi na. Ka ŋɔn mun nyɛ la Naawunni, ka u gaari bunseelli kam zaa, ka ba pɛ'ari u ka gyeemni u saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi.

english_verse: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
translation_verse: Amaa di pa la kaman Naawunni yɛttɔ'a luya. Dama di pa la ban nya ba dɔ'am Izraɛli ni la zaa n nyɛ Izraɛli niriba ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ n wulisi, ni, di pa la ban dɔ'ai seeba zaa n nyɛ Naawunni biisi maa, amaa ban dɔli la Naawunni nangbambasiri soori n‑dɔ'ai seeba la n nyɛ biisi ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: Don't forget what happened to the twin sons of Isaac and Rebekah.
translation_verse: Ka di pa la lala kɔkɔa, amaa Rɛbɛkka mun daa mari doo yinni puuwa - di nyɛ la: ti yaaba Yisahaaku dinni.

english_verse: Even before they were born or had done anything good or bad, the Lord told Rebekah that her older son would serve the younger one. The Lord said this to show he makes his own choices and it wasn't because of anything either of them had done.
translation_verse: Amaa u daa na bu dɔ'ai u gyeesi maa, ka di zugu u biisi wa na bu tum tunseelli zaa - bunsuŋŋu, bee, bumbeoo - ka lala saha ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, “Bikpeema la nin ti gyeemni u tunzɔ.” U daa niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka ŋɔn Naawunni yuubu ti zɛ suŋŋa, dama u boori ni u ninvuggamsa yiiri na, pa ba tuma zugu, amaa ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn boonni ba la zugu.

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
translation_verse: Hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Yaakubu ka n daa wura, Iisɔ mun ka n daa yaai.”

english_verse: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Dama u daa yɛli Musa, ni#:- “Mani n mari namboo n‑tiri man ya zɔri seeba namboo la; Ka niŋi sufubaalli n‑tiri n sufu ŋɔn baali n‑tiri seeba la.”

english_verse: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
translation_verse: Dizugu di zaa pa la ninsaala yuubu, bee, ninsaala ŋɔn moori n‑tumni seem la zugu, amaa di bɛ la Naawunni nambɔzoaa maa zugu.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
translation_verse: Dama Litaafi maa yɛli n‑ti NaaFirawuna, ni, “Mani n kyɛ ka i doaai na ni n ti zaŋŋi i n‑wulisi n yiiko ŋɔn nyɛ seelli maa, ka kyɛ ka n yuuri du duniya wa zaa ni.”

english_verse: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
translation_verse: Dinzugu u siri mari namboo n‑tiri ninvugseeba, hoo u yuubu ŋɔn bɛ seem la; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka seeba mari tuppuwumni, ka di gba dɔli la u yuubu.

english_verse: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
translation_verse: Tɔ: di pa seem ya ninvugsɔ nin ti yɛli ma, ni, “Ka dinŋɔna, di niŋi la wula ka Naawunni kpalim n‑nyari ti galinsi? Dama sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑ta'ai ŋɔn Naawunni yuubu ŋɔn boori seem.”

english_verse: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka n ya labsi u, ni, “Eeh, n zɔa! Ka nyini n nyɛ ŋɔni? Nyin ninsaala nin boosiri Naawunni boosa, bee wula? Dukku ku toaai n‑doaai m‑boosi ŋɔn mɛ ka la, ni, ‘Bɔ zugu ka i mɛ ma lala?’: bee m ma'ai ni?”

english_verse: Doesn't a potter have the right to make a fancy bowl and a plain bowl out of the same lump of clay?”
translation_verse: Ammi, dugmeera ka soori ni u zaŋŋi ya'ari m‑mɛ ŋɔn boori a seem, bee? Ay! - u nin toaai n‑gbeaai ya'ari maa m‑mɛ laveelli, ka laa n‑gbeaai bɛni m‑mɛ laseaa ban zaŋŋi n‑tumni da'ari tuma.

english_verse: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
translation_verse: Di bu ya'ai ka Naawunni yi boori u ti wulisi u yiiko, ni u sugyeaa, ŋɔn bɛ seem. Ka dizugu u daa niŋi sugru pam n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban ŋmanni la da'ari tuma laasi, seesi ban daa maali ni a ti sa'am m‑basi, ŋɔn Naawunni sugyeaa zugu.

english_verse: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Ka di bu laa n‑ya'ai ka u boori u ti wulisi u gyirima ŋɔn veeli pam seem. Ka dizugu u daa niŋi lala n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban ŋmanni la veelim tuma laasi la, ka u zɔ ba namboo, dama u daa daŋŋi m‑maali ba ni ba ti nya gyirima.

english_verse: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
translation_verse: Hoo din sɔbi Hɔseaa bukku maa ni, ni#:- “Ban daa pa n niriba la, N nin booli ba n niriba. Man daa bu wuri sɔ la, N nin booli u, ni, n wurilim nira.

english_verse: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
translation_verse: Ka ya'aseaa ban daa yɛli ba, Ni, ‘Ya pa la n niriba.’ la, Bɛni ka ba nin boonni ba, Ni, ‘Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana biisi’. ”

english_verse: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
translation_verse: Ka Aizaaya mun daa kaasi n‑yɛli zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Izraɛli dima yɛla, ni, “Halli Izraɛli niriba yi ti zoaai hoo teekuduuri mɔ'abisigu gba, di zaa yɔri, ba beela nin ti kpalim n‑nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
translation_verse: Dama ti Dugma maa nin kari u sariya m‑basi duniya ni na, ka din ya naai la yooma.”

english_verse: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
translation_verse: Hoo Aizaaya ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑yɛli seem la, ni#:- “Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa yi daa bu kyɛ biisi wa Ni ba kpalim n‑ti ti, Di daa naa n‑niŋi ti hoo Sɔdoom ni Gɔmɔra - Tinseesi Naawunni ŋɔn daa sa'am maa.”

english_verse: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
translation_verse: Ka ti ya yɛli la wula yaasa? Ni, ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa daa bu boori Naawunni yɛlimaŋni ziiya, amaa ba daa nya ka. Yɛdda zugu yɛlimaŋni beebu ziiya ka ba daa paai.

english_verse: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
translation_verse: Wula zugu n‑daa la? - Dama ba daa bu dɔli yɛdda soori, amaa zaaligu tuma tumpu dinni ka ba daa dɔla. Ba daa tuui n‑lu kugri maa zugu,

english_verse: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
translation_verse: hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni#:- “Ya nyama! N doonni la kugri, Buntitaari, n‑niŋni Zayɔn tiŋŋa ni. Kugseaa kan nin kyɛ ka niriba tuui n nyɛ ka, Ni tampiiya kan kyɛ ka ba lu tiŋŋa. Amaa ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda muna - Ŋɔna n ku di vi, halli beela.”

english_verse: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, di nyɛ la n sufu yuubu ni n Yɛhudiyataaba ti nya tuliginsim; lala zugu ka m puusiri Naawunni n‑tiri ba sahakam.

english_verse: what makes people acceptable to him. So they refuse to trust God, and they try to be acceptable by obeying the Law.
translation_verse: Dama ba bu mi Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori, ka di zugu ba boori la ba zaali ba mammaŋŋa yɛlimaŋni soori. Lala zugu ka ba daa za'asi Naawunni soori ka bu paai ya'aseaa Naawunni ŋɔn naa n‑deaai ba, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima.

english_verse: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
translation_verse: Kirisita Masiiya nyɛ la zaaligu la gbinni, ni kan wa saala gba. U kyɛ ka seeba ban niŋi u yɛdda maa zaa yi paalu ni, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima.

english_verse: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
translation_verse: Musa daa sɔbi zaaligu la yɛlimaŋni soori yɛla n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔnkam tumni zaaligu tuma maa zaasa nin nya nyɔvuri di zugu.

english_verse: But people whose faith makes them acceptable to God will never ask, “Who will go up to heaven to bring Christ down?”
translation_verse: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛlimaŋni din yi yɛdda niŋbu ni na, ni, “Di birisiri i sufu ni, ni, ‘Ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑du saazugu m‑paai arizana ni?’, ” - di nyɛ la: ni, u du m‑paai bɛni nti zaŋŋi Kirisita n‑sigi na -

english_verse: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
translation_verse: “bee, ‘Ŋɔni n nin toaai n‑sigi n‑kpɛ bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa ni?’;” - di nyɛ la: n‑zaŋŋi Kirisita n‑duusi n‑yi kum ni na.

english_verse: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
translation_verse: Ka di yɛli la wula? - ni#:- “Yɛligu maa bu wɔ'a ya saani, Halli ka bɛ la ya nɔya ni, Ka bɛ la ya sufu ni gba.” - di nyɛ la yɛligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛdda niŋbu, yɛliseaa tirima ŋɔn moonni maa.

english_verse: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
translation_verse: Ni, ‘ya nɔya’, dama i yaari la i noori n‑sakki, ni, Yisa nyɛ la i Dugma; ni, ‘ya sufu’, dama i niŋni i sufu ni yɛdda, ni, Naawunni daa neaai Yisa kum ni. Ka anŋɔ abayi zaa zugu ka ya nin nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
translation_verse: Dama nira niŋni yɛdda u sufu ni, ka di zugu Naawunni deaai u, ni, yɛlimaŋni daana. Ka u laa n‑yaai u noori n‑sakki n‑wulisi paalu ni, ka nya tuliginsim di zugu.

english_verse: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
translation_verse: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, “Ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda la muna - ŋɔna n ku di vi halli beela.”

english_verse: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
translation_verse: Kpa wa ka wɔligri ka ni - Yɛhudiyadima ku bɛ ba kɔkɔa ka kyɛ Griika dima. Naawunni nyɛ la ba zaa Dugma, ka ŋɔn mun zaŋŋi bunsunseelli kam n‑tiri baseeba ban mi u yuuri n‑susiri u maa.

english_verse: All who call out to the Lord will be saved.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ŋɔnkam mi ti Dugma yuuri ka susiri u la nin nya tuliginsim.”

english_verse: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ba ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai n‑susiri u, ka bu daŋŋi n‑niŋi u yɛdda? Bee, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai n‑niŋi u yɛdda, ka na bu wum u yɛla? Ka ba ya niŋi la wula n‑toaai n‑wum u yɛla, ka di pa la sɔ pun mooli u lahabaari maa n‑ti ba?

english_verse: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
translation_verse: Ban moonni maa muna - ba ku toaai m‑mooli, see ka sɔ yi tum ba ni ba ti niŋi lala. Hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni, “Di kur niŋni i la nya'asim ni i ti nya seeba ban mari lahabasuŋŋu maa n‑kyɛnni na.”

english_verse: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
translation_verse: Amaa pa sɔkam zaa n daa sakki n‑ti lahabasuŋŋu maa; hoo Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la, ni, “Ti Dugma, ŋɔni n niŋi ti kukɔri la yɛdda? - Ay, sɔ zaa bu niŋi!”

english_verse: No one can have faith without hearing the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna yɛdda niŋbu yiiri la mooligu maa wumpu ni, ka mooligu wumpu mun yiiri la Kirisita yɛligu ni.

english_verse: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
translation_verse: Amaa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yɛhudiyadima maa yɛla ka m boosiya, ni, “Ba siri daa bu wum, bee bɔa?” - Ay! Dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni#:- “Ba kukɔya gyili duniya lugri kam zaa, Ka ba yɛligu paai tingbaŋŋu zaa tɔriga.”

english_verse: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
translation_verse: Dizugu n laa n‑ya m‑boosi, ni, Izraɛli dima siri daa bu baŋŋi? Ay! - Halli Musa daa daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni, ti Dugma yɛliya, ni#:- “N nin zaŋŋi ban pa butiri ni yɛlimaŋni n‑kyɛ ka ya zabi ba nyuuri: Ka laa n‑zaŋŋi ninvugseeba ban kɔŋŋi yɛm n‑kyɛ ka ya suuri yisigi.”

english_verse: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
translation_verse: Ka Aizaaya mun daa niŋi sufukpeemni n‑gaari lala, halli ka u daa ti yɛli, ni, ti Dugma yɛliya, ni#:- “Seeba ban daa bu boori n zii la - Ban ka n daa kyɛ ka ba nya ma: Ka daa zaŋŋi m maŋŋa n‑wulisi n‑ti seeba ban bu boosi n yɛla la.”

english_verse: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
translation_verse: Ka tin na kpalim n‑viisiri anabi Aizaaya yɛla maa, u daa laa n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti Izraɛli dima, ni, “Beoo kam ka n daa teaai n nuusi n‑ti n niriba, amaa ba daa nyɛ tuppuwumni dima ni zabbooriba.”

english_verse: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna m boosiri ya, ni, ammi Naawunni za'asi u niriba m‑basi, bee? - Ay! di pa lala. M mammaŋŋa nyɛ la Izraɛli nira, Ibrahim tuŋŋu ni ka n yi na, halli Bɛngyamin za'anɔri bii n nyɛ ma.

english_verse: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
translation_verse: Naawunni bu za'asi u niriba, seeba ŋɔn daa daŋŋi m‑baŋŋi la. Ammi, ya bu mi Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli Ilaygya yɛla seem la? - Ni, ŋɔn Ilaygya daa susiri Naawunni n‑tuusiri Izraɛli dima seem maa,

english_verse: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
translation_verse: ni, “N Dugma, ba daa ku i anabidima la, ba mun daa sa'am i gyeeŋŋu ziisi la, ka man kɔkɔ n kpalim, halli ka ba boori ni ba ku ma gba!”

english_verse: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
translation_verse: Bɔ yaasa? Izraɛli dima daa bu nya ban daa boori bunseelli nimmoaa la, amaa ninvugyiisa maa daa nya di. Ban kpalim maa mun daa mari tuppuwumni,

english_verse: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
translation_verse: Tɔ - ka lala gba zugu ka pampaŋŋɔ ninvugyiisa kpalimiya - kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi piini maa ŋɔn yiisi ninvugseeba la nla.

english_verse: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Ka di yi nyɛ la piini maa zugu ŋɔna, di bu laa n‑dɔli la tunsuma tumpu. Di yi pa lala, kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi piini maa naa n‑ku nyɛ piini.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
translation_verse: hoo din daa sɔbi seem la, ni#:- “Naawunni daa ti ba sibugsiga - U kyɛ ka ba nini ku nya, U kyɛ ka ba tuba ku wum, Halli nti paai zuna.”

english_verse: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
translation_verse: Ka NaaDauda gba daa yɛli, ni#:- “Ba la'amni n‑diri diibu pam - Kyɛ ka di leebi la bɛriga n‑gbaasi ba! Di leebi la kyɛ'ariga n‑gba ba, Ni bɔ'azuliŋŋu ka ba lu n‑sigi ka puuni. - Lala nin yɔ ba samni n‑ti ba.

english_verse: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka ba nini zibgi Ka ba ku toaai n‑nyari, halli beela! Kyɛ ka ba kuntiri ni n‑ziiri zitibsa halli saha kan ka tɔriga!”

english_verse: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
translation_verse: Dizugu m boosiri ya, ni: ammi, ba tuya ni ba ti lu tiŋŋa? - Ay! di pa lala. Amaa di nyɛ la ban burigri la zugu ka fa-m‑basi paai ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani na, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ban Yɛhudiyadima maa ti zabri nyuuri.

english_verse: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
translation_verse: Dizugu, ba burigri maa kyɛ ka bunsuma paari la duniya wa dima maa zaa, halli ba pɔsigri zugu ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima leebi hoo bundaandima. Tɔ, ka di yi bɛ lala, Yɛhudiyadima zaa ŋɔn ti la'am m‑paasi maa, di ya niŋi la wula? - di siri nin veela n‑gaari zaa!

english_verse: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka pampaŋŋɔ n ya tɔ'asiri n‑tiri yaseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la. Ka man ŋɔn nyɛ la tuumu n‑ti yan ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa, n nin tum n tuma maa zaa ni nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
translation_verse: N teesiri, ni, di pa seem n Yɛhudiyataaba nin ti zabi nyuuri, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ba ninvugseeba ti nya fa-m‑basi.

english_verse: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
translation_verse: Dama sahanseaa ka Naawunni daa za'asi ba la, lala zugu daa kyɛ ka u ti la'asi duniya dima zaa na. Ka di yi bɛ lala, u yi laa n‑deaari ba na, di ya bɛ la wula? - Pa nyɔvuri n‑yi kum ni na nla?

english_verse: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
translation_verse: Ba yi daŋŋi n‑gbeaai maasibeeri beela nti zaŋŋi n‑tisi Naawunni, lala zugu yi kyɛ ka maasa maa zaa nyɛ la Naawunni dinni. Ba yi zaŋŋi tinya'ari n‑tisi Naawunni, lala zugu yi kyɛ ka timunni maa nyɛ la u dinni.

english_verse: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
translation_verse: Di ŋmanni la hoo ban ŋmaai tiseaa kan wanni wula m‑basi ka zaŋŋi moo ni tiwulli n‑tugi n‑tabli. Lala ka ba zaŋŋi i n‑kpeesi n‑tabli Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni. Ka tiwulipaalli wa deaari la sum ni bunsuma zaa an yiiri tii maa nya'a ni na.

english_verse: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
translation_verse: Ka di yi bɛ lala, nyin bumpaalli ku toaai n‑nyu bara n‑zeemni bunkura la. I yi boori i nyu bara, kur teaama, ni, di pa la nyini m‑mari tii la nya'a maa, amaa nya'a maa n mari i.

english_verse: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
translation_verse: Di pa seem i ya yɛli, ni, ba daa ŋmaai wulikura maa m‑basi ni ba ti tugi i n‑tabli.

english_verse: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
translation_verse: Tɔ, yɛlimaŋni nla. Lala wula maa ka ba daa ŋmaai m‑basi a yɛdda kaalinsi maa zugu; ka nyini ka ba daa tugi n‑tabli, i yɛdda niŋbu zugu. Dizugu nyin di mari karimbaani, see ka i zɔri dabeem,

english_verse: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
translation_verse: dama Naawunni daa bu niŋi sugru n‑ti wulimaŋa la, ka u ku ti di sugru n‑ti nyini gba.

english_verse: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
translation_verse: I bu nya Naawunni ŋɔn zɔri namboo seem? - ka laa n‑darigri tuba gba. U darigri ban lu la tuba, amaa ka zɔ nyini namboo, i yi ti kpalim m‑bɛ u nambɔzoaa maa ni. Di yi pa lala, u nin ŋmaai i gba m‑basi.

english_verse: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
translation_verse: Ka banla gba yi ti kyɛ ba yɛdda za'asigu, ba nin laa n‑tugi n‑kpeesi yaasa, dama Naawunni mari la yiiko ni u ti niŋi lala.

english_verse: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
translation_verse: I bu nya? U daa pun zaŋŋi nyini, sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ moo ni tii maa wulli, ni u ti takki i n‑tugi n‑kpɛ tisuŋŋu ni n‑tabli. Tɔ: lala n nyɛ di; ka dinŋɔna labsim ma: ammi, i teesiri, ni, u ku toaai n‑zaŋŋi tisuŋŋu wulimaŋa la ka laa n‑tugi n‑tabli? U nin toaai, walla!

english_verse: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, yɛlisɔ'ara nŋɔa, amaa m bu boori ni ya kɔŋŋi a bamsim, ka naayi zaŋŋi ya toori yɛm m‑boori a gbinni. Kulla Izraɛli dimseeba daa mari zugkpeeŋŋu ni tuppuwumni, see ka ban pa Izraɛli dima ti kpɛ na ka ba kaalli maa ti paai.

english_verse: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
translation_verse: Ka di naayi kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima zaa ti nya tuliginsim hoo din daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Tuligra maa nin yi Zayɔn na; U nin yiisi din bu tuusi zaa m‑basi, Ka kyɛ ka Yaakubu dima ti leebi kasi zaa.

english_verse: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
translation_verse: Lala ka n ti ba noori, n‑zaali nangbanyinni zaaligu n‑ti ba: Di saha ka n nin kyɛ ba tumbeeri m‑pɛŋŋi ba.”

english_verse: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
translation_verse: Ban saani ka di kur bɛ la lala: zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla, ban ni Naawunni mari la dimni, ka di nyɛ la yarima zugu. Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni yiisigu piini zugu muna, ba mari la wurilim, ka di nyɛ la ba yaadima zugu.

english_verse: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni yi ti ya zaŋŋi u fa-m‑basi piini n‑tisi seeba, bee, u yi ti booli seeba u saani na, u bu ya takki u lala teesa maa.

english_verse: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
translation_verse: Yarima daa bu wum n‑ti Naawunni, amaa ka yi nya u namboo pampaŋŋɔa, ka di nyɛ la ban ŋɔn bu sakkri Naawunni wumpu zugu.

english_verse: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni kpeesi sɔkam tuppuwumni zii ni, hoo ban kpatti la tumbeeri dima dansarika duu ni seem la. Ka u niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa naayi nya u nambɔzoaa.

english_verse: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
translation_verse: Naawunni bunsuma n zulima, yee! U katti la sariya ka di gbinni sɔ'aya; u sɔya zaa mun ka baŋŋni! Ni#:-

english_verse: “Has anyone known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?
translation_verse: “Ka ŋɔni n na mi m‑baŋŋi Naawunni teesa gbinni? Bee, ŋɔni n na mi n‑kpaam u?”

english_verse: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
translation_verse: Ni#:- “Halli ŋɔni n‑daŋŋi n‑ti Naawunni piini, Ni Naawunni ti labsi n‑ti u yaasa?”

english_verse: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Dama bunseelikam zaa yi la u saani na, ka ŋɔn zugu ka a bɛ ni, ka a laa m‑paari u saani. Ŋɔna n sum ni pɛ'abu saha din ka tɔriga. Ammi.

english_verse: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
translation_verse: Dizugu n susiri ya ni, m mabiisi, Naawunni nambɔzoaa zugu, ni, ya zaŋŋma ya mansi m‑maali sara n‑ti Naawunni ka u nini tigi. Di nyɛ la: yan na nɛ wa, ya nin leebi Naawunni dinni, dama dina n nyɛ gyeeŋŋu maŋni n‑tiri u.

english_verse: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka duniya wa dima beesigu mugsi ya, ni ya gba bɛ la lala; amaa ya kyɛ ka ya beesigu takki n‑dɔli ya nini ŋɔn neaai n‑nya sɔpaalli la. Ya tumma Naawunni yuubu tuma nti baŋŋi a gbinni, ni, a veela ka laa n‑nyɛ bunsuma zaa ka Naawunni nini n tigri a.

english_verse: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
translation_verse: Tɔ; Naawunni ŋɔn pun zaŋŋi u suŋŋsim piini n‑ti ma la zugu, n kpaamni ya zaa yinniyinni nŋɔa: ni, yan di gɔsi ya mammansi yɛla n‑teesi, ni, a veela, halli ka ya lala teesa gaari din siri nyɛ yɛlimaŋni maa. Ay! - Ya kyɛ ka ya teesa dɔli la yam maŋni, hoo Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi yɛdda n‑ti ya sɔkam yinniyinni seem.

english_verse: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
translation_verse: Ya nyama - ningbiŋŋu maa nyɛ la bummunni yinni, amaa tuga pam bɛ ka ni, ka a seelikam zaa mari la ka tumni, ka tumni.

english_verse: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
translation_verse: Di nyɛ la lala n‑ti ti zaa: ti zoaaya halli, amaa ti nyɛ la hoo ningbimmunni, tin ŋɔn bɛ Yisa Kirisita ni la zugu. Ka ti zaa ŋmanni la u ningbiŋŋu tuga n‑suŋŋni taaba.

english_verse: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? Naawunni pun zaŋŋi u piini n‑ti ti zaa, ka a nyɛ la kookooba. Sɔ yi nyɛ anabi, Naawunni piini maa zugu, ŋɔn tɔ'asima u yɛdda ŋɔn yi ti ti u, ni u ti yɛli seem la.

english_verse: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
translation_verse: Sɔ mun yi gyeemni la niriba, Naawunni piini zugu, ŋɔn zɛ yummu u lala tuma maa: ka sɔ mun yi tumni karimba tuma, ŋɔn gba tumma ni nimmoaa.

english_verse: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
translation_verse: Yaasa, sɔ yi kpamsiri niriba, di sum ni u kpamsiri ba suŋŋa; sɔ mun yi tɔtti tɔra, ŋɔn tumma ni u sufu zaa; sɔ mun yi su toondaana fukumsi, nimmoosi ka u tumni u tuma; halli sɔ yi ya suŋŋi faradima, ŋɔn mari sufupeelli n‑suŋŋi ba.

english_verse: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka ya siri wuri la niriba yɛlimaŋni ni, ka di yɔmsira. Ya yaama Sintaani yɛla, ka gyi din nyɛ bunsuŋŋu.

english_verse: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
translation_verse: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi mabitirigu wurilim, see ka ya wuri taaba suŋŋa. Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gyirima mun tisibu, see ka ya gyeemni taaba nimmoosi.

english_verse: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
translation_verse: Yan mari tama maa zugu, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu maai; yan mun nya namsigu sahanseaa maa, ya kur mari sugru di puuni; ka yan susiri Naawunni maa, ya susima nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
translation_verse: Ya suŋŋma ya nyaandɔlitaaba ban bɛ wahala ni la, ka sɛ'asi n‑deaari saamma n‑kpeesiri ya yiya ni.

english_verse: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
translation_verse: Ya susim bunsuma Naawunni saani n‑tiri seeba ban mugsiri ya: ii! - ya susim bunsuma, ka pa la bumbeoo n‑tiri ba.

english_verse: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
translation_verse: Baseeba ban mari sufupeelli maa, ya paasima ba zugu m‑mari sufupeelli; ban mun sufu sa'am, ya paasima ba zugu sufusa'aŋŋu ni.

english_verse: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
translation_verse: Ya la'amma m‑bɛ ni taaba ni sugru. Yan di duusi ya mansi amaa ka sakki n‑tabli tarimma saani. Yan di boori ya mansi, bee, n‑yɛtti ni, yɛm daandima n nyɛ ya.

english_verse: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
translation_verse: Yan di tum tumbeeri n‑labsi sɔa, ŋɔn tum tumbeoo n‑niŋi ya la zugu. Ay! - ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑tumni tunseesi niriba zaa ŋɔn gɔsiri n‑yɛtti, ni, a veela.

english_verse: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka yan ni sɔkam mari la nangbanyinni, halli ya yi ti nyaŋŋi lala.

english_verse: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
translation_verse: M mabiwutta, yan di yɔ sɔ tumbeeri samni, amaa ya kyɛ u yoobu yɛla n‑ti la Naawunni, dama ŋɔna n su tubdarigri yɛla zaa. Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, ti Dugma yɛliya, ni, labsigu n nyɛ u dinni, ni, ŋɔna nin labsi n‑yɔa.

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
translation_verse: N‑laa m‑paasi, ni, “Kɔm yi mari i dindaana, nyin tim u diibu ka u di; kɔnyuuri mun yi mari u, nyin tim u koom: i yi niŋi nŋɔ di nin ŋmanni la i moaari la bugunsaala n‑ziinni u zugu.”

english_verse: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
translation_verse: Di kyɛ ka tumbeoo nyaŋŋi i; see ka i mari tunsuŋŋu n‑nyaŋŋi tumbeoo.

english_verse: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa sakki n‑ti tinkpamma, ni ban su fukumsi la zaa. Dama kpamni ni fukumsi n ka ni m‑bu yi Naawunni saani na. Ŋɔn Naawunni pun zaali naam fukumsi dima an bɛ ni zaa.

english_verse: People who oppose the authorities are opposing what God has done, and they will be punished.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔnkam za'asi fukumsi dima, di daama za'asi la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali seela. Ka seeba ban za'asi ba la muna - ba sumsi la tubdarigri.

english_verse: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, ŋɔni n zɔri fukumsi dima dabeem? - Di pa la seeba ban tumni tunsuma la, amaa ban tumni tumbeeri la. I yi boori ni i bɛ ni ka ku zɔri fukumsi dima dabeem, nyin tumma tunsuma ka ba nin pɛ'ai i.

english_verse: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn su naam fukumsi la nyɛ la Naawunni tuntumna, i suŋŋsim zugu. Amaa i yi ti tum din bu tuusi, nyin zɔma u, dama u bu mari naam yiiko maa yɔri; dama u nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn zaali sɔ ni u ti zaŋŋi ŋɔn Naawunni sufuyisigri n‑darigri tumbeeri dima tuba.

english_verse: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
translation_verse: Dizugu di sum ni ti sakkri n‑tiri naam fukumsi dima, pa dabeem zugu kɔkɔa, amaa ti sufu ni teesa ŋɔn mi, ni, lala veela maa zugu.

english_verse: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
translation_verse: Lala zugu gba ka ya yoori lampoo ni faarigu, dama ban deaari maa tumni n‑tiri Naawunni ni nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
translation_verse: Yan di di sɔ zaa samni. Sanseaa bu dɔli ya, di yi pa la, ni, ya wuri taaba; dama ŋɔnkam wuri u taraana pun dɔli la zaaligu zaa.

english_verse: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Ya mi zaaligudima la yɛla: ni, “Di tum zina.”, ni, “Di ku ninsaala.”, “Di zu.”, ni, “Di kyɛ ka i sufu kɔrisi kpɛ i kpeaa buna zugu.” - halli ni an kpalim zaa m‑paasi: tɔ, a zaa bɛ la yɛlli yinni wa puuni, ni, “Wuri i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la.”

english_verse: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
translation_verse: Wurilim daana ku toaai n‑sa'am u taraana yɛla, lala zugu ka wurilim lu n‑ti zaaligudima la zaa.

english_verse: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ya tumma anŋɔa, dama ya mi saha maa ŋɔn paai seem la. Saha paaya: yan neaam goom ni, dama ti tuliginsim saha maa ku laa n‑yuui hoo din daa yuui seem tin daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi Naawunni yɛdda sahanseaa la.

english_verse: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
translation_verse: Yuŋŋu kyɛnni n‑gatta, ka beoo ya neaai. Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti za'asi tunseesi an tumni zibsim ni la zaa, ka zaŋŋi la neesim ni tuma hoo tɔbbiisi ŋɔn zaŋŋi ba neema ni ba ti tuusi tɔppu seem la.

english_verse: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
translation_verse: Wuntaŋŋa ni ka ti bɛ ni, dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti beebu dɔli la soori, ka pa la dimaŋŋa ni daam nyuubu, bee, pɔ'agoorilim ni dɔgoorilim ni tumbeeri zaa, bee, zaba ni nyuuri zappu. Ay, ya kyɛl anŋɔ zaa m‑basi,

english_verse: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
translation_verse: ka zaŋŋi ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya n‑niŋi ya beesigu ni, hoo nira ŋɔn yi yɛ u sutura seem la. Ka yan di dɔli ya ningbinkɔrisi yuubu tumbeeri maa, halli beela.

english_verse: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ni ka u yɛdda bu kpeem Naawunni dɔligu ni, yan deaam u ya saani, amaa yan di ŋmɛ u nangbankpeemni zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u kyisigu yɛla.

english_verse: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
translation_verse: Dama seeba mari la yɛdda din kyɛ ka ba diri bunseelikam zaa; seeba mun yɛdda bu kpeem, ka ba diri la zɛvaari kɔkɔ di zugu.

english_verse: But you should not criticize others for eating or for not eating. After all, God welcomes everyone.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑di bunseelikam zaa la di galim ŋɔn bu diri la, ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn diri zɛvaari kɔkɔ la gba di galim ŋɔnla, dama Naawunni deaai di daama gba.

english_verse: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
translation_verse: I nyɛ la ŋɔni n‑katti sɔ daabri sariya? Di ti nyɛ la ŋɔn su u maa n nin baŋŋi u nyaŋŋiya, bee, u luya. Halli u nin nyaŋŋi n‑zɛ yummu, dama u dugma nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka u nyaŋŋi.

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
translation_verse: M‑paasi yaasa, seeba saani dabsiseesi veela n‑gaari taaba, ka seeba mun saani ka a zaa nyɛ la yimmu. Sɔkam zaa zaanma suŋŋa u toori teesa puuni.

english_verse: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
translation_verse: Sɔ yi gamsiri dabsa, Naawunni yuuri zugu ka u niŋni lala; sɔ mun yi diri seelli zaa, Naawunni yuuri zugu ka u dira, dama u puusiri la Naawunni bundirigu zugu; halli sɔ yi bu sakki n‑diri bundiriseesi, Naawunni yuuri ka ŋɔn gba niŋni lala, ka ŋɔn gba puusiri la Naawunni u bundirigu zugu.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
translation_verse: Dama ti sɔ ka ni m‑bɛ u toori zugu, sɔ mun ka ni n‑kpiiri u kɔkɔ zugu.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
translation_verse: Ti yi bɛ ni, ti Dugma zugu ka ti bɛ ni; ti mun yi kpi, ti kpi la ti Dugma zugu. Dinzugu ti yi bɛ ni, bee, ti kpi ni, ti Dugma n su ti yɛla zaa.

english_verse: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Kirisita daa kpi ka laa n‑yisigi kum ni: ni, u leebi ban pun kpi, ni ban na bɛ ni maa zaa, Dugma.

english_verse: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
translation_verse: I nyɛ la ŋɔni? - nyin ŋɔn galimni i mabiiya n‑zeemni u lala? Ti zaa nin ti zaani Naawunni maa tooni ka u kari ti sariya.

english_verse: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
translation_verse: Dizugu ti sɔkam zaa nin tɔ'asi u toori beebu yɛttɔ'a n‑ti Naawunni.

english_verse: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
translation_verse: Dinzugu ti di laa n‑galimni taaba, amaa ya kyɛ ka ti gɔsi tin nin niŋi seem ka ti mabiisi di burigi, bee, n‑lu tumbeeri ni.

english_verse: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
translation_verse: Man ŋɔn bɛ Kirisita ni maa kyɛ ka man bu nya ka bundiriseaa kyisiri ka toori; amaa sɔ yi teesi u sufu ni, ni, ka kyisira, dinŋɔna ka siri kyisiri n‑ti di daama.

english_verse: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: I yi daamni i mabiiya nyin diri seelli zugu, dinŋɔna i bu tumni la wurilim zugu. Kirisita daa kpi u zugu, ka nyin di kyɛ ka i bundirigu diibu zugu ti sa'am u yɛla.

english_verse: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka bunsunseaa yan mari maa ti nya yubeoo.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni naam maa pa la diibu, ni nyuubu; amaa naam maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni, ni sufudoaa, ni nya'asim piiniseaa Naawunni Sii maa ŋɔn tisira.

english_verse: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
translation_verse: Ka sɔ yi dɔli la soori wa n‑gyeemni Kirisita, Naawunni nini tigi di daama, ka u yuuri du ninsaalidima saani.

english_verse: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
translation_verse: Lala zugu, ya kyɛ ka ti gɔsiri la bunseesi an nin mari sufudoaa ka kyɛ ka ti suŋŋni taaba ka kyɛnni tooni.

english_verse: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka bundirigu zugu ti sa'am Naawunni ŋɔn maali seelli. Bundiriseelli bu kyisira, amaa di bu veeli ni i di seelli ka di nin kyɛ ka i mabii maa lu tumbeoo ni.

english_verse: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
translation_verse: Ti yi ŋɔbi numni, bee, n‑nyu daam, bee, anŋɔ taabu zaa, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka ti mabii lu tumbeeri ni, dinŋɔna ti di ŋɔbi, bee, n‑nyu a, halli fiiŋ. Lala n sɔa.

english_verse: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
translation_verse: Kur mari i yɛdda wa yɛla suŋŋa, nyin ni Naawunni saani. Zugsuŋŋu nin bɛ ni n‑ti ŋɔn baŋŋi u sufu ni, ni, ŋɔn niŋni seem wa ka galinsi.

english_verse: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn birisiri ŋɔn di seelli maa zugu, ka naayi di, Naawunni nin galim di daama di zugu, dama u bu dɔli la u yɛdda soori. Ka seelli zaa din bu dɔli yɛdda nyɛ la tumbeoo.

english_verse: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dizugu di sumsi ni tirima ban mari yaa ti yɛdda zugu la ti mari sugru n‑tiri ban ka yaa la: see ka ti maari ba sufu, ka ti di boori ti maari ti mammansi sufu.

english_verse: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
translation_verse: Sɔkam niŋma din nin kyɛ ka u taraana sufu maai ka u tumni tunsuma, ka kyɛnni tooni Naawunni saani.

english_verse: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
translation_verse: Halli Kirisita gba daa bu maai la u mammaŋŋa sufu, amaa di daa bɛ la hoo din daa sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni, “Ban n daa boori la Naawunni tuubu la daa tu la mani.”

english_verse: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni siri su sugru ni kpamsigu yɛla, ka u nin kyɛ ka ya dɔli Yisa Kirisita ka kpalim m‑mari nangbanyinni ni taaba.

english_verse: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
translation_verse: Ka ya nin la'am n‑zaŋŋi nangbanyinni m‑pɛ'ai Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la.

english_verse: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
translation_verse: Dinzugu ya deaama taaba hoo Kirisita ŋɔn pun deaai ya seem la, Naawunni pɛ'abu zugu.

english_verse: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
translation_verse: Dama n yɛtti ya, ni, Kirisita daa leebi la Yɛhudiyadima maa daabri ni u ti wulisi Naawunni yɛlimaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni u ti zaali nangbambasiri maa deede - nangbambasiseesi ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun basi n‑ti ti yaadima la.

english_verse: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la mun pɛ'ari Naawunni u nambɔzoaa zugu, hoo din sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni#:- “Dinzugu n nin pɛ'ai nyin Naawunni, N nin pɛ'ai i ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la saani. N nin yuumni n‑tiri i yuuri.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
translation_verse: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi yaasa, ni#:- “Ya mari sufupeelli, yan pa Yɛhudiyadima wa, Ya paasim u niriba zugu m‑mara.”

english_verse: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
translation_verse: Yaasa, ni#:- “Ya pɛ'ama ti Dugma, yan pa Yɛhudiyadima wa, Ka kyɛ ka sɔkam zaa tiri u gyirima.”

english_verse: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
translation_verse: Ka Aizaaya gba daa yɛli, ni#:- “Gyɛsi tuŋŋu ni dɔsɔ nin ti bɛ ni n‑doaai na n‑su ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la naam; Ŋɔna zugu ka ba nin ti nya tama.”

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
translation_verse: Naawunni siri su tama yɛla, ka n susiri u, ni, u kyɛ ka sufumaasim ni sufudoaa ti paali ya sufu, yan niŋni yɛdda maa zugu. Ka Sii Kasi la yiiko nin kyɛ ka ya mari tama pam halli nti yakki.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, m mi halli, ni, ya mari yɛlisuma zaa, ka bamsim kur paali ya ni, ka ya nyaŋŋi n‑kpaamni taaba.

english_verse: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
translation_verse: Amaa yɛttɔ'aseesi ziiya ka n niŋi sufukpeemni n‑sɔbi n‑ti ya na beela n‑teaari ya. Di yi la n yɛlimaŋni piini maa ni na, piiniseaa Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la.

english_verse: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
translation_verse: U ti ma, ni, n niŋi Yisa Kirisita tuntumna ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa saani. N tuma mun nyɛ la Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu tuma, nti kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima la ti la'asi na ka Naawunni Sii Kasi kyɛ ka Naawunni deaai ba la kasikasi. Ka tuma wa ŋmanni la ŋɔn maanni sara tuma, hoo ban la'asi la puri ni antaam na, m‑maali n‑ti Naawunni seem la.

english_verse: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
translation_verse: Dizugu Yisa Kirisita zugu ka m mari soori ni n nyu bara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni yɛla.

english_verse: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
translation_verse: Dama n ku ma'asi ni n tɔ'asi seelli di yi pa la Kirisita ŋɔn zaŋŋi ma n‑tum tunseesi la; ka laa n‑yɛli yɛttɔ'aseesi la, ni di kyɛ ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa ti sakki n‑ti Naawunni.

english_verse: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
translation_verse: U zaŋŋi lala tunkara ni alahazibu tuma yiiko n‑tum tuma wa n nuu ni, ni Sii Kasi yiiko. Ka di nyɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm zaŋŋi n‑gyiligi wa'aligu sa, halli nti paai Illirikum tingbaŋŋu ni, ka n mooli Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla zaa.

english_verse: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - M ba'a kur bɛ la timpaala ni, ni n ti mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa luginseaa niriba ban na bu wum Kirisita yuuri la ni. Lala ka n ku meeri n‑tɔmni sɔ nyeebri zugu.

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
translation_verse: Amaa di bɛ la hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:- “Ban na zi n‑wum u lahabaari la, Ban nin nya ka baŋŋi: Ban na zi n‑wum u yɛla maa, Ban nin baŋŋi di gbinni.”

english_verse: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka di yuui ka m boori soori ni n ti kyɛ ya saani na n‑guui.

english_verse: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
translation_verse: Amaa pampaŋŋɔ n laa n‑ka tunseelli luga wa puuni, ka n yi boori ni n kyɛ ya saani na yuuma seem nŋɔa,

english_verse: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
translation_verse: m mari tama, ni, n yi ya gaari n‑kyaŋŋi Sipeen tingbaŋŋu ni, n nin nya ya. Ka n yi ti kaai ya n‑niŋi daba ayi pɔi, ka ti la'am m‑maai taaba sufu, ya nin naayi toaai n‑zaali ma soori ni ka n kyaŋŋi n kyɛnni la.

english_verse: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
translation_verse: Dama Masidoonya dima ni Akaaya dima daa gbaai toosigu ni sufupeelli, ni, ba ti toosi n‑suŋŋi fara dimseeba ban bɛ Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni, Gyɛrusalɛm timpuuni la.

english_verse: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ba daa gbaai lala ni sufupeelli, halli Gyɛrusalɛm dima samni n dɔli ba gba. Dama Gyɛrusalɛm dima daa zaŋŋi ba Naawunni bunsuma n‑tɔri n‑ti ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa nti suŋŋi ba siisi, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka di sumsi ni ban ti zaŋŋi ba duniya wa bunsuma n‑tɔri n‑ti Yɛhudiyadima wa n‑suŋŋi ba ningbina.

english_verse: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
translation_verse: Dizugu n yi tum dinŋɔ maa, n‑zaŋŋi ban bɔ seelli maa nti ti Gyɛrusalɛm dima maa n‑naai, n nin dɔli ya saani n‑gaari n‑kyaŋŋi n Sipeen la.

english_verse: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
translation_verse: M mi halli, ni, n yi kyɛ ya saani na, n ya mari Kirisita bunsuma zaa n‑kyɛ na.

english_verse: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
translation_verse: N susiri ya ni, m mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu, ni Sii Kasi wurilim zugu gba, ni, ya suŋŋi ma ka ti susi Naawunni nimmoosi man yɛla zugu.

english_verse: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
translation_verse: Ya susima, ni, n nin yi Gyuudii dimseeba ban bu niŋi yɛdda la nuusi ni na, ka Gyɛrusalɛm puuni Naawunni niriba la nini nin tigi n suŋŋsim maa.

english_verse: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
translation_verse: Ka lala zugu Naawunni nin ti ma soori ni n kyɛ ya saani na ni sufupeelli, ka man ni yarima nin la'am taaba m‑maai taaba sufu.

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
translation_verse: Naawunni siri su sufudoaa yɛla; ŋɔn Naawunni bɛ ya zaa saani. Ammi.

english_verse: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka m boori ni ya ti deaai ti tunzɔpɔ'a wa ŋɔn yuuri boonni Fiibi. U nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn tumni Naawunni tuma n‑tiri u niriba ban la'amni Kyɛnkyiriiya tiŋŋa puuni.

english_verse: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
translation_verse: Ya deaam u hoo din sumsi ni ya deaai ya mabii seem la, ti Dugma yuuri zugu. U yi ti boori seelli ya saani, ya suŋŋma u yan nin toaai seem zaa, dama ŋɔn gba suŋŋi niriba pam halli ni man gba.

english_verse: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
translation_verse: Ya puusima Prisila ni Akwila n‑ti ma, dama ba n nyɛ n tuntuntaaba la, Kirisita Yisa saani.

english_verse: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
translation_verse: Ba daa zaŋŋi ba nyɔvuya m‑pa talima man zugu, ka di pa la man kɔkɔ m pɛ'ari ba, see ninvugseeba ban pa Yɛhudiyadima ka la'amni ti Dugma yuuri zugu la gba - ban zaa paasi man zugu m‑pɛ'ara.

english_verse: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Ya puusima ban mun la'amni ban Akwila ni Prisila yiri maa puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni maa. Ka puusi n zɔsuŋŋu Ɛpeenɛtusi; ŋɔna n nyɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi ba zaa n‑leebi Yisa nyaandɔla la.

english_verse: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
translation_verse: Ka puusi Mariama, dama u tum pam n‑ti ya.

english_verse: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
translation_verse: Ni Andirɔnikusi ni Gyuniyasi: n dɔ'ariba n nyɛ ba, ka man ni ban n daa la'am m‑bɛ dansarika ni. Yisa tuumudima maa tiri ba gyirima, halli ni ba daa daŋŋi ma n‑leebi Yisa nyaandɔliba gba.

english_verse: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka laa m‑puusi Ampiliyatusi n‑ti ma, dama n wuri u hoo Naawunni biiya:

english_verse: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
translation_verse: ni Yuribanusi ti tuntunkpeaa Kirisita Masiiya zugu, ni Siteekyusi n zɔsuŋŋu.

english_verse: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
translation_verse: Apɛliisi gba n nyɛ gyirima daana Kirisita yuuri zugu, ya puusima u n‑ti ma; ŋɔn ni Arisitɔbulusi yiri dima la zaa.

english_verse: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ka puusi n dɔ'ara Hɛroodiyɔni n‑ti ma, ni Naasisusi yiri dima, ban nyɛ ti Dugma niriba la, m‑paasi.

english_verse: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
translation_verse: Tirifeena ni Tirifoosa n nyɛ pɔ'aseeba ban tum tunkara n‑ti ti Dugma, ya puusima ba n‑ti ma; ka puusi Pɛrisisi man wuri sɔ la - ŋɔn gba tum tunkara n‑ti ti Dugma.

english_verse: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
translation_verse: Ka puusi Ruufusi dama ti Dugma pun yiisi u: ka laa m‑puusi u ma la, ŋɔn nyɛ man gba ma.

english_verse: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
translation_verse: Ka puusi Asinkyiratusi, ni Filɛgɔni, ni Hɛrimiisi, ni Patirɔbaasi, ni Hɛrimaasi, ni ti mabiisi ban bɛ ba saani maa zaa.

english_verse: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
translation_verse: Ka laa m‑puusi Filɔlɔgusi, ni Gyuliya, ni Neeriyusi ni u tunzɔpɔ'a la, ni Ɔlimpaasi ni Yisa nyaandɔliba ban bɛ ba saani zaa.

english_verse: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
translation_verse: Ya puusima taaba suŋŋasuŋŋa ti wurilim puusigu ni. Kirisita Masiiya nyaandɔliba la zaa puusiri ya na.

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
translation_verse: N kpaamni ya ni, ni, ya baŋŋma seeba ban wulisiri wulisiseesi an sa'amni yan pun bamsi wulisigu la; ba kur taasiri la nangbankpeemni na, ni ba ti lusi ya. Ya yim lala niriba maa yɛla ni.

english_verse: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
translation_verse: Dama lala niriba bu gyeemni ti Dugma Kirisita, amaa ba maŋtoori kɔrisi yɛla ka ba gyeemna. Ka ba zaŋŋi ba yɛttɔ'anya'asa ni nangbammalisim la n‑yɔmsiri ninvugseeba ban teesa bu zulima.

english_verse: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
translation_verse: Yarim muna - sɔkam mi, ni, ya deaari Naawunni noori, ka dizugu n sufu maasi ya yɛla zugu. M boori ni ya teesa ti zulima zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunsuma, amaa ka laa m‑boori ni a ti kɔŋŋi bumbeeri bamsim, halli fiiŋ.

english_verse: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Naawunni n su sufudoaa yɛla zaa, ka di ku yuui ka u nin kyɛ ka ya nɔ n‑tɔm Sintaani zugu n‑ŋmirigi u. Ti Dugma Kirisita Masiiya wurilim kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni.

english_verse: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
translation_verse: Timɔti, n tuntunkpeaa la, puusiri ya suŋŋa, ni n dɔ'ariba m‑paasi - Luusiyusi, ni Gyeesɔni ni Soosipata nla.

english_verse: Gaius welcomes me and the whole church into his home, and he sends his greetings. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our dear friend Quartus send their greetings too. Paul's Closing Prayer
translation_verse: Gayusi gba puusiri ya ni; n yirikpɛdaana n nyɛ u, ka laa n‑kyɛ ka Yisa niriba la'amni u yiri maa ni. Ɛrasitusi gba puusira - ŋɔn su ti tiŋŋa wa ligri yɛla fukumsi la - ni ti mabii Kuwaatusi m‑paasi.

english_verse: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ari Naawunni! Ŋɔna nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka ya nya yaa, ka di yi la man wulisiri lahabasuŋŋu maa ni na, ni Yisa Kirisita yɛla mooligu ni na. Di nyɛ la din wulisi yɛlisɔ'ara gbinni n‑tiri ya la. Ba daa sɔ'ai yɛla wa, piiligu ni halli ni zuna,

english_verse: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: amaa pampaŋŋɔ ba kasigi a gbinni n‑wulisi ti. Hoo anabidima ŋɔn daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, lala lahabaari maa ka Naawunni, saha din ka naari la Daana, yɛtti, ni, ba yiisima na n‑wulisiri ya'ari kam, ni duniya zaa niriba ti deaai u noori n‑niŋi Yisa yɛdda.

english_verse: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Naawunni siri bɛ la u kɔkɔ m‑mari yɛm; gyirima zaa nyɛ la u dinni halli ni saha din ka tɔriga. Ti pɛ'ama u, Yisa Kirisita Masiiya zugu! Ammi.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
translation_verse: Man Pɔla n‑sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑ti ya, man ni ti mabii Sɔsitɛniisi. Man nyɛ sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn booli ma ni n ti leebi u tuumu, dama lala ka u sufu yuubu daa wulisi.

english_verse: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Ti Ba Naawunni, ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita, zaŋŋma u piinisuma ni alaafɛ zaa n‑tisi ya.

english_verse: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: M puusiri Naawunni barika ya zugu sahakam, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u piinisuma n‑ti ya la zugu, ka di nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa saani.

english_verse: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka ya beebu kur wulisiri, ni, lahabasuŋŋu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Kirisita yɛla maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni deede.

english_verse: You are not missing out on any blessings, as you wait for him to return.
translation_verse: Dinzugu ya bu kɔŋŋi Naawunni piinisuma, halli yinni gba, ka kpalim n‑guusiri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yiibu na saha.

english_verse: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
translation_verse: U siri nin mari ya suŋŋa halli nti paai di tɔriga, ka ya nin zɛ yummu ti Dugma Yisa dabsiri daari maa ni, galinseelli ka ni.

english_verse: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
translation_verse: Naawunni nyɛ la yɛdda daana, ka ŋɔna zugu ka ba daa booli ya, ni ya yi na n‑la'amni m‑bɛ u Bii Yisa Kirisita ti Dugma saani.

english_verse: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka n susiri ya, m mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita zugu, ni, ya zaa mari nangbanyinni ni taaba; yan di ŋmeeri nangbankpeemni n‑wɔligri ka kyɛri taaba. See ka ya kpamsiri taaba ka mari sufu yinni ni teesiyinni.

english_verse: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
translation_verse: M mabiisi wumma! N wumni ya yɛla Kloowi yiri dima maa saani, ni, zaba m bɛ ya saani.

english_verse: Has Christ been divided up? Was I nailed to a cross for you? Were you baptized in my name?
translation_verse: Ammi, ba purigi Kirisita zii bunaasi? Ba daa kpaasi Pɔla n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu ni ya ti nya tuliginsim, bee? - Ay! pa lala. Bee, ba daa su ya Pɔla koom? - Ay, halli fiiŋ gba!

english_verse: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
translation_verse: Di niŋi ma nya'asim Naawunni zugu, ni, n daa bu su ya ninvugsɔ koom, na'ala Krispusi ni Gayusi kɔkɔa.

english_verse: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
translation_verse: Dama lala zugu ya ku toaai n‑yɛli, ni, n daa su ya koom m mammantoori yuuri zugu.

english_verse: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - di daa pa la koom suubu yɛla zugu ka Kirisita daa tum ma ya saani na, amaa lahabasuŋŋu mooligu zugu; ka m bu zaŋŋi ninsaala yɛm m‑moonna, dama lala kyɛ ka Kirisita dɔpulli zugu kum yɛla ti basi yɔri.

english_verse: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
translation_verse: Dama dɔpulli maa lahabaari muna - ninvugseeba ban bɛ baasiyɔri soori ni maa yi ti wum di, ba yɛtti, ni, gyɛrilim; amaa tirima mun ban bɛ tuliginsim soori maa ni la saani ka di mari la Naawunni yiiko.

english_verse: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
translation_verse: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “N nin sa'am yɛm dima bamsim maa; N nin gbarigi ba miilinsi.”

english_verse: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka yɛm daana la lee? Bamsim daana la lee? Ŋɔn viisiri m‑baŋŋni pampaŋŋɔ duniya beesigu yɛla la bɛ la yani? Naawunni n zaŋŋi duniya wa yɛm n‑leebi gyɛrilim, bee, m ma'ari ni?

english_verse: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
translation_verse: Naawunni mammaŋŋa yɛm zugu daa kyɛ ka duniya wa dima daa zaŋŋi ba toori yɛm ni ba ti baŋŋi u yɛla n‑kɔŋŋi. Ka u sufu yuubu zugu ka baseeba ban wum lahabaari mooligu din ŋmani hoo gyɛrilim la n‑niŋi yɛdda la muna - ban nyari fa-m‑basi.

english_verse: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
translation_verse: Tɔ: Yɛhudiya dima maa boori la alahazibu tuma nyaabu, ka Griika dima maa mun boori yɛm yɛla wumpu;

english_verse: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
translation_verse: amaa tirima ŋɔn moonni lahabaseaa maa nyɛ la Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yɛla: ni, ba daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu. Ka Yɛhudiya dima maa yɛtti, ni, din kyisiri nla; ka Griika dima maa mun yɛtti, ni, gyɛrilim n nyɛ di.

english_verse: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn booli seeba la ŋɔna, u siri nyɛ la Masiiya n‑ti ba, ka laa n‑nyɛ Naawunni yɛm ni u yiiko gba - halli ba yi nyɛ Yɛhudiya dima, ni Griika dima zaa.

english_verse: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni gyɛrilim mari yɛm n‑gaari saalidima yɛm, ka Naawunni bugsim mari yaa n‑gaari saalidima yaa.

english_verse: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, ya teesima yan daa bɛ seem ka ba daa booli ya. Ya sunsukkuni ya ka niriba pam ban mari duniya wa yɛm; ya ka niriba pam ban mari yiiko, bee, n‑yi naam zuliya ni.

english_verse: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni yiisi la bunseesi an mari gyɛrilim duniya wa dima saani, ka mari a n‑diisiri yɛm dima maa vi: Naawunni yiisi la an nyɛ bumbugsa duniya wa dima saani, ka mari a n‑diisiri yaa dima maa vi.

english_verse: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
translation_verse: U yiisi la tarimma, ni niriba ŋɔn zeemni bunseesi, halli bunseesi an ka ni gba, ka mari a n‑kyɛ ka bunseesi an bɛ ni maa ti basi yɔri.

english_verse: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
translation_verse: Ka lala kyɛ ka sɔ ku toaai n‑nyu bara ŋɔn Naawunni saani.

english_verse: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Naawunni zugu ka ya bɛ Yisa Kirisita ni; ŋɔna ka Naawunni kyɛ ka u leebi yɛm n‑ti ti, ni yɛlimaŋni, ka u laa n‑kyɛ ka ti leebi Naawunni mammaŋŋa niriba kasi kasi, ka laa n‑nya fa-m‑basi.

english_verse: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
translation_verse: Dizugu - hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli - “Ŋɔn ya n‑nyu bara zaa, kyɛ ka u nyuuri a ni Naawunni yuuri zugu kɔkɔa.”

english_verse: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
translation_verse: Tɔ: m mabiisi, man daa kyɛ ya saani na maa, ni n ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni yɛligu n‑tiri ya, ni ya ti baŋŋi Naawunni yɛlisɔ'ara - lala saha n daa bu kyɛ na ni n ti zaŋŋi yɛlinya'asa an mari bamsim n‑tɔ'asira.

english_verse: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Ay! - n daa yɛli, ni, man ŋɔn bɛ ya saani la m bu ya sakki m‑baŋŋi seelli na'ala Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yɛla kɔkɔ ka n nin baŋŋi - halli ban daa kpaasi u n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu gba.

english_verse: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
translation_verse: N yɛligu maa, ni man daa mooli lahabaari la gba, daa pa la yɛm yɛttɔ'anya'asa, amaa a daa wulisiri la Naawunni Sii maa yiiko yɛla,

english_verse: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
translation_verse: ni lala ti kyɛ ka ya yɛdda niŋbu ku bɛ saalidima yɛm zugu, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu.

english_verse: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Yɛm yɛla ka ti siri yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ban gbaai kpamni la saani, amaa di pa la pampaŋŋɔ saha yɛm maa, pa din yi ban su pampaŋŋɔ duniya fukumsi la saani na - ban kur naari yɔri.

english_verse: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
translation_verse: Ay! Naawunni yɛm ka ti tɔ'asira - bunsɔ'arigu din daa bu yi paalu na. Lala yɛm ka Naawunni daa zaali pɔi ka naayi naam pampaŋŋɔ duniya wa; ka niŋi lala ni u ti kyɛ ka tirima nin nya gyirima.

english_verse: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
translation_verse: Yɛm wa muna - ban su pampaŋŋɔ duniya fukumsi la sɔsɔ daa bu baŋŋi di gbinni; halli ba yi daa baŋŋi, ba naa n‑ku kpaasi ti Dugma gyirima daana n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu.

english_verse: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
translation_verse: Ba siri daa bu baŋŋi, hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Bunseesi sɔ nimbiri ŋɔn na bu nya, Bunseesi sɔ tubri ŋɔn na bu wum, Bunseesi ninsaalisɔ sufu ŋɔn na bu teesi a yɛla - Anŋɔ ka Naawunni pun maali siri, U nin zaŋŋi a n‑ti ban wuri u maa.”

english_verse: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
translation_verse: Tirim muna - ŋɔn Naawunni zaŋŋi anŋɔ taabu n‑wulisi ti; u Sii maa zugu ka u wulisiri a ni, dama Sii maa viisiri la seelikam, halli Naawunni yɛlizulima gba.

english_verse: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
translation_verse: Ka saalidima saani, ŋɔni m mi u saalitaraana yɛla deede? - Ay, sɔsɔ bu mi, na'ala di daama maŋtoori sufu ni sii maa m mi u yɛla. Lala ka sɔ na zi Naawunni yɛla deede, na'ala Naawunni Siiya.

english_verse: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
translation_verse: Pa duniya wa siiya ka ti daa deaai; amaa Sisɔ ŋɔn yi Naawunni saani na. Ka lala n kyɛ ka ti baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ti ti piiniseesi la zaa.

english_verse: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
translation_verse: Tɔ: lala ka ti wulisira. Di bu yi la duniya wa yɛm wulisigu ni na, amaa hoo Sii maa ŋɔn wulisiri ti seem; ka ti mari n‑suŋŋni Sii maa niriba, ni ba ti baŋŋi u yɛla.

english_verse: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba ban ka Sii maa bu deaari yɛliseesi an yiiri Naawunni Sii maa ni na, dama a nyɛ la gyɛrilim ba saani. Ba bu baŋŋi a gbinni, dama sɔ ku toaai m‑baŋŋi, di yi pa la u dɔli Sii maa soori n‑viisiri a.

english_verse: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
translation_verse: Nira ŋɔn mari Sii maa viisiri la seelikam, amaa sɔ mun bu viisiri ŋɔna.

english_verse: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
translation_verse: Ni#:- “Dama ti Dugma sufu ni teesa muna - Ŋɔni n na mi m‑baŋŋi a? Ŋɔni mun ŋmeeri u saawara?” Tirima mun mari la Kirisita Masiiya sufu ni teesa.

english_verse: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, n ku toaai n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ya hoo ban mi Sii maa yɛla la, amaa hoo ban mi ninsaala yɛla la kɔkɔa, dama ya na nyɛ la bileesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Kirisita yɛla.

english_verse: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
translation_verse: Dizugu n daa mɔ'asiri ya la bisim, pa dikpeema; dama ya daa bu sa'ai ditibsa diibu. Halli ni zuna ya na ku toaai n‑deaai ninvugkura diibu,

english_verse: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
translation_verse: dama ya na zi Sii maa yɛla. Nyuuri zappu ni suuri yisigri ŋɔn na bɛ ya saani, pa duniya wa saalidima yɛla ka ya dɔla?

english_verse: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - sɔ yɛtti, ni, Pɔla ka u dɔla; ka sɔ mun yɛtti, ni, Apɔloosi ka u boora: pa duniya wa ninsaalidima yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa?

english_verse: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
