translation_verse: Apɔloosi nyɛ la ŋɔni? Bee, Pɔla nyɛ la ŋɔni? Ti zaa nyɛ la Naawunni tuntumniba, ka di kyɛ ka yarima niŋni yɛdda dima, amaa bunseelikam yi la Naawunni saani na n‑ti ti.

english_verse: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
translation_verse: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya yɛdda yɛla, mani n daa buri bumbura la, ka Apɔloosi mun daa yɔ'ai bumbula la koom, amaa Naawunni yiiko zugu ka a zoaai na n‑niŋna.

english_verse: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn butti ni ŋɔn yɔ'ari koom la zaa tumni la tumni yinni, ka ti Dugma nin yɔ ba zaa hoo ban tumni seem la.

english_verse: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
translation_verse: Tirima zaa la'am n‑tumni Naawunni tuma: yan mun nyɛ la Naawunni puu ka ti koora, ka laa n‑nyɛ u yiri ka ti meera.

english_verse: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ tammeera ni yɛlimaŋni, hoo man deaai piiniseesi Naawunni saani la, ka n daa nyeebi nyeebri maa; ka sɔ mun deaai m‑meeri n‑tɔmna: tɔ! see ka sɔkam mari u maŋŋa ka baŋŋi ŋɔn tumni u meebu tuma seem.

english_verse: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
translation_verse: Amaa sariya karibu daari yi paai na, di nin yiisi sɔkam tuma paalu ni na, n‑wulisi an bɛ seem, dama bugum n ya zamsi sɔkam zaa tuma.

english_verse: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka bugum yi bu ti nyaŋŋi sɔ tuma, di daama nin deaai u laara sanyoaa.

english_verse: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
translation_verse: Amaa bugum yi di sɔ tuma zaa, di daama nin kɔŋŋi marigu; u mammaŋŋa ku baasi yɔri, amaa u nin ŋmanni la hoo ŋɔn kpeeri bugum ni n‑gatta.

english_verse: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
translation_verse: Ya bu baŋŋi, ni, yarima n nyɛ Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu, bee? Halli Naawunni Siiya bɛ ya ni gba.

english_verse: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ yi sa'am Naawunni gyeeŋŋu ziiya, Naawunni gba nin sa'am di daama. Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Duu nyɛ la u dinni, kan nyɛ bunkasikasi: ka yarim n nyɛ u Gyeeŋŋu Duu wa.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
translation_verse: Tɔ: yan di kyɛ ka ya sɔ yɔmsi u maŋŋa; ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ya saani ka teesi, ni, u nyɛ la yɛm daana duniya wa saani, kyɛ ka di daama leebi gyɛrigu; ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka u leebi yɛm daana ni yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
translation_verse: Dama duniya wa yɛm nyɛ la gyɛrilim Naawunni saani. Lala zugu ka di pun sɔbi n‑dooli, ni, “Yɛm daandima maa muna - Naawunni zaŋŋi ba toori yɛmgɔliŋŋa m‑bɛtti n‑gbaari ba.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
translation_verse: Ka di laa n‑sɔbi, ni, “Ti Dugma m mi yɛm dima teesa zaa, ka a nyɛ la yɔri.”

english_verse: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
translation_verse: Ii! - Pɔla nyɛ la ya dinni, ni Apɔloosi, ni Siifasi Piita; yarima n su beesigu ni kum fukumsi; din bɛ ni pampaŋŋɔ ni din nin niŋi zaa - anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la ya dinni.

english_verse: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
translation_verse: Ka ya mun nyɛ la Kirisita Masiiya ŋɔn su ninvugseeba, ka ŋɔn Kirisita nyɛ la Naawunni dinni.

english_verse: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna see ka ba nyari ti, ni, Kirisita tuntumniba, ni seeba Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi u yɛlisɔ'ara fukumsi n‑niŋi ba nuu ni.

english_verse: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
translation_verse: Din kpalim ka ba boori tuntumniba saani nyɛ la, ni, ba zɛ yummu n‑tumna.

english_verse: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
translation_verse: Man mun ba'a ka ni, ni, yarima yi katti n sariya, bee ninsaalidima kootu sɛseelli yi katta; halli m mammaŋŋa gba - man bu katti n yɛla sariya.

english_verse: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
translation_verse: M bu nya teesibeeri n sufu ni, amaa lala ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka n niŋi yɛlimaŋni daana; ti Dugma kɔkɔ nin ti kari ma sariya.

english_verse: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
translation_verse: Lala zugu yan di daŋŋi n‑kari sariya ka saha maa na bu paai na; halli ni ti Dugma kyɛnni na. Ŋɔna nin kyɛ ka din sɔ'ai zibsim ni zaa nin ti yi paalu na, ka wulisi saalidima sufu ni teesa an bɛ seem. Di saha ka sɔkam zaa nin ti nya pɛ'abu din sumsi u, Naawunni saani.

english_verse: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, yarima zugu ka n zaŋŋi man ni Apɔloosi yɛla wa n‑wulisiri ya, ni ya baŋŋi yɛligu wa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni#:- “See ka ya ti dɔli din sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ka ku yakki Naawunni ŋɔn zaali seem.” Ni, yan di gyeemni sɔ ka laa n‑zeemni u taraana.

english_verse: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
translation_verse: Bɔ n wulisi, ni, ya wɔligiya ni ka kyɛ ya taaba? Bɔ ka ya mara, di yi pa la ya daa deaai ka la piini? Ka ya yi deaai piini maa, bɔ n kyɛ ka ya nyuuri bara, hoo yan bu deaai di zaa?

english_verse: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
translation_verse: Ni, bundaandima n nyɛ ya, fa! Ni, ya tigiya ka bu kɔŋŋi seela. Ni, ya pun di naam ka kyɛ ti. Tɔ: ya yi siri di naam, di veela! - dama ti gba naa n‑la'am n‑di naam maa.

english_verse: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
translation_verse: N teesi, ni, Naawunni zaŋŋi tirima, u tuumudima maa, ka labsi ti yɛla nyaaŋŋa. Ti ŋmanni la hoo ninvugseeba ban kari ti sariya n‑lusi ti, ni, ti sum la kum; ka ti diri lala wahala ni sɔkam ti gɔsira - halli malaakadima ni ninsaalidima zaa gba.

english_verse: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
translation_verse: Tirima siri nyɛ la gyɛra, Kirisita zugu; ka yarima mun wulisiri ninneesim Kirisita saani, fa! Bumbugsa n nyɛ ti, ka ya mun mari kpeeŋŋu. Ya nyari gyirima, ka ti mun nyari fiiliŋŋu.

english_verse: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
translation_verse: Halli ni zuna ti kpiiri la kɔm ni kɔnyuuri, n‑ka bunyeera, ka laa n‑nyari mugsigu ti gyiliŋŋu la puuni.

english_verse: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
translation_verse: Ti namsiri n‑tumni tuma ni ti nuusi. Ka niriba tuuri ti, ka ti mun kaari kasuma n‑tiri ba; ba namsiri ti ni, ka ti mun bu za'asiri ba yuubu.

english_verse: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
translation_verse: Ba tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑niŋni ti, amaa ti kpaŋŋi ti maŋŋa n‑sakki n‑ti ba baalimm. Ti ŋmanni la duniya zaa sa'ari, hoo ninsaalidima maa sa'atampuuri, halli ni zuna.

english_verse: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
translation_verse: M bu sɔbri wa ni n ti diisi ya la vi, amaa n kpaamni ya ni hoo m biwutta.

english_verse: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Dama halli ya yi mari ya karimbadima tusa tusa zaŋŋi n‑kpa Kirisita ziiya gba, ya ka badima pam, see yinni kɔkɔa. Yɛlimaŋni, mani n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai ya zaŋŋi n‑kpa lahabasuŋŋu beesigu ziiya ka di nyɛ la Kirisita Yisa saani.

english_verse: and I want you to be like me.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka n susiri ya, ni, ya dɔli n beesigu soori.

english_verse: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka n tum Timɔti ya saani na; u nyɛ la m biwuttigu ni yɛlimaŋni, ka laa n‑nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn zɛ yummu ti Dugma yɛla zugu. Ŋɔna nin toaai n‑teaari ya man Kirisita beesigu sɔya yɛla, hoo man ŋɔn wulisi seem lugri kam man la'amni ni Naawunni niriba zaa.

english_verse: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
translation_verse: Man na bu kyɛ ya saani na zugu ka ya seeba mari karimbaani.

english_verse: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
translation_verse: Amaa di siri ku yuui ka n nin kaai ya na, Naawunni yi ti ti ma lala soori. Di saha n nin baŋŋi karimbaani dima maa gbinni, pa ba yɛttɔ'a yɛla kɔkɔa, amaa ni ba yiiko ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni naam pa la yɛttɔ'a kɔkɔa, amaa yiiko ni gba.

english_verse: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
translation_verse: Bɔ ka ya boora? - N kyɛm na nti darigi ya tuba? - bee, n zaŋŋi Sii maa wurilim ni nirilim n‑kyɛ ya saani na?

english_verse: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
translation_verse: Lahabaari siri yi na, ni, zina m bɛ ya saani - ka ka taabu ka ni, halli ban bu dɔli Naawunni soori la saani gba: ni, dɔsɔ n di u ba pɔ'a.

english_verse: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
translation_verse: Ka ya mun nyuuri la bara, fa! Di da bu sum ni ya sufu sa'am di zugu? - ka ya yi da zaŋŋi ŋɔn tumni lala maa n‑yiisi ya la'aŋŋu ni, di da sɔa.

english_verse: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
translation_verse: Ya yi ti la'am taaba ti Dugma Yisa yuuri zugu, ka n gba sii bɛ la ya saani, ti Dugma Yisa yiiko kur bɛ la bɛni.

english_verse: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
translation_verse: Di saha ya zaŋŋma lala doo maa n‑ti Sintaani ka u sa'am u ningbina, ka di yi pa seem lala nin kyɛ ka u sii maa nin nya tuliginsim, ti Dugma dabsiri daari.

english_verse: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
translation_verse: Ay! ya bara nyuubu wa bu veela! Ya bu mi paanu dabina yɛla - ni, ba zaŋŋi a beela n‑niŋi boroboro zum puuni, ka a nin nyaŋŋi n‑tum m‑pitti paanu n‑fuusiri a zaa gba.

english_verse: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna ya yiisim dabinkura la m‑basi, ka ya leebi boroboropaalli, an ka dabina. Halli, ya siri pun bɛ la lala, dama ba pun kɔrigi Kirisita, hoo ti Yakki-n‑Gaari pɛbila maaligu la - pa paanu kan ka dabina n nyɛ Yakki-n‑Gaari dinni?

english_verse: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti maali Kyuu wa suŋŋa: ti di mari dabinkura paanu an kyisira, bee, tumbeeri ni putoo buna; amaa bumpaala, an ka dabina, an nyɛ la yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni dina.

english_verse: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
translation_verse: N daa pun sɔbi n‑ti ya n gbaŋŋu la ni, ni, yan di la'am m‑bɛ ni ni zina dima.

english_verse: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
translation_verse: Tɔ - pa la duniya wa niriba ban tumni zina la yɛla ka n daa tɔ'asi, bee, beem dima, bee, ban yɔmsiri n‑faari niriba, halli baseeba ban gyeemni buga la gba: di yi da nyɛ la lala, ya naa n‑yi la duniya wa ni zaa.

english_verse: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
translation_verse: Seeba ban pa ti niriba muna - Naawunni nin kari ba sariya. Ni, “Ya karima putoo daana n‑yiisi ya puuni.”

english_verse: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
translation_verse: Ya sɔ yi mari yɛttɔ'a ni u taraana, ka u zaŋŋi lala yɛttɔ'a maa n‑kyaŋŋi ban bu gyeemni Naawunni la saani ka kyɛ Naawunni niriba maa - lala mari soori? - Ay! lala ka soori.

english_verse: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
translation_verse: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, Naawunni niriba maa nin ti kari duniya wa zaa sariya? Ka ya yi ya kari duniya wa sariya, ya ku toaai n‑kari sarabisi?

english_verse: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
translation_verse: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ti nin kari halli malaakadima sariya gba? Ka ku toaai n‑kari saalidima maa yɛla, fa?

english_verse: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
translation_verse: Amaa yarima yi mari yɛttɔ'a anŋɔ taabu, ya kyɛnni seeba saani ni ba kari n‑ti ya, ka ba pa la ninvugseeba Naawunni niriba ŋɔn tiri gyirima.

english_verse: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
translation_verse: N yɛtti dinŋɔ la, ni n ti diisi ya vi. Ammi, sɔ ka ya saani ŋɔn mari yɛm n nin toaai m‑maali ya yɛla maa? - halli ninvugu yinni gba?

english_verse: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
translation_verse: Ka ya ziiri taaba n‑kyɛnni kootu ni, halli seeba ban ka yɛdda saani.

english_verse: But instead, you cheat and rob other followers.
translation_verse: Amaa yarima n tumni taalli ka faari buna - halli ya taaba saani la gba!

english_verse: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
translation_verse: Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, ban tumni taalli la ku nya Naawunni naam maa? Yan di yɔmsi ya mansi: see ka ya baŋŋi, ni, zina dima ku nya Naawunni naam, ka ba'amaanniba gba ku nya, bee gyɛ'amiru dima ni dɔppa ban tumni lala, bee ban zaŋŋni ba dɔtaaba n‑doontira;

english_verse: will share in God's kingdom. Neither will any thief or greedy person or drunkard or anyone who curses and cheats others.
translation_verse: ni na'ayigsi, ni beem dima, ni danyuuriba, ni minaafigsi, ni ban yɔmsiri niriba - banŋɔ sɔsɔ ku nya Naawunni naam; ba nuu ka lala faari maa yɛla ni, halli beela.

english_verse: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
translation_verse: Ya seeba mun daa bɛ la lala. Amaa ya daa nya koom suubu; ba daa kyɛ ka ya leebi Naawunni niriba kasikasi; ba daa deaai ya, ni, yɛlimaŋni dima, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya yuuri zugu, ni ti Ba Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la saani.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
translation_verse: Ni, ‘M mari la seelikam yiiko.’ - tɔ, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri suŋŋsim. Ni, ‘M mari la seelikam yiiko’ - tɔ, amaa di kyɛ ka seelikam zaa nyaŋŋi ma.

english_verse: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
translation_verse: Ni, ‘Diibu nyɛ la puuri dinni, ka puuri mun su diibu yɛla.’ - tɔ, amaa Naawunni nin kyɛ ka abayi wa zaa ti baasi yɔri. Pa zina n su ningbina fukumsi, amaa ti Dugma n su a; ningbina maa Dugma n nyɛ u.

english_verse: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
translation_verse: Naawunni yiiko ka u daa zaŋŋi n‑yisigi ti Dugma kum ni, ka u nin yisigi ti gba lala.

english_verse: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
translation_verse: Ya bu mi, ni, ya ningbina nyɛ la Kirisita ningbinluga? Ka n ya niŋi la wula n‑zaŋŋi Kirisita ningbinlugri n‑la'am n‑leebi gyɛ'amiru ningbiŋŋu lugri? Ay! - lala ka soori.

english_verse: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
translation_verse: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ŋɔn la'am n‑tabli ni gyɛ'amiru, ni, babayi maa zaa leebi la yummu? Dama di yɛliya, ni, “Babayi maa zaa nin la'am m‑bɛ ni kaman bunni yinni.”

english_verse: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mun la'am u maŋŋa ni ti Dugma, ba leebi la sii yinni.

english_verse: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
translation_verse: Ya zɔma n‑yi zina tuma ni; dama tumbeeri zaa pa la nira ningbiŋŋu puuni yɛttɔ'a, na'ala zina tuma nyɛ lala; amaa ŋɔn niŋi zina tuma zaa niŋni lala tumbeeri u toori ningbiŋŋu ni.

english_verse: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
translation_verse: Bee, ya bu mi, ni, ya ningbina maa nyɛ la Sii Kasi gbeebu ziiya? - u Gyeeŋŋu Duu. Dama ya daa deaai Sii Kasi maa Naawunni nuu ni, ka u bɛ la ya saani. Ya bu su ya maŋtoori yɛla;

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
translation_verse: ay! - ba daa da ya ni. Dizugu ya zaŋŋma ya ningbina n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima.

english_verse: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseesi yan daa sɔbi a yɛla la: di veela ni doo bɛ ni ka bu di pɔ'a.

english_verse: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
translation_verse: Amaa zina kɔrisi zugu, ya kyɛ ka doo zaa mari u pɔ'a, ka pɔ'a zaa mari u sira.

english_verse: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka doo maa tiri u pɔ'a din sumsi u, ka pɔ'a maa gba tiri u sira, hoo din tuusi seem.

english_verse: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
translation_verse: Dama pɔ'a maa ningbiŋŋu pa la u toori dinni kɔkɔa, amaa u sira gba dinni; ka lala doo maa ningbiŋŋu pa la u toori dinni kɔkɔa, amaa u pɔ'a gba dinni.

english_verse: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
translation_verse: Yan di za'asi taaba, ka di yi pa la ya niŋi la nangbanyinni, ni, ya zaŋŋi lala saha maa n‑susiri Naawunni; ka laa n‑la'am taaba yaasa. Ka di kyɛ ka Sintaani zamsi ya n‑nyaŋŋi, yan ku toaai m‑mari ya mansi zugu.

english_verse: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
translation_verse: Man yɛtti la nŋɔa, di pa la n zaali ya lala zaaligu, amaa ya yi tumni lala, n nin sakki.

english_verse: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
translation_verse: Man saani, n da boori la sɔkam dɔli la n toori soori. Amaa sɔkam mari la u toori soori Naawunni piini zugu: yinni mari la piini kanŋɔa, ka sɔ mari la seaa.

english_verse: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka n yɛtti n‑tiri seeba ban na bu di pɔ'a, bee, n‑kuli doowa, ni pukɔya gba, ni, ba yi kpalim m‑bɛ hoo man bɛ seem wa, di sɔa.

english_verse: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
translation_verse: Amaa ba yi ku toaai n‑gbaai ba mansi, ba dim pɔ'aba, bee, n‑kuli dɔppa. Ya yi niŋi lala di veela n‑gaari ya kyɛ ka pɔ'aba bee dɔppa yɛla ti mugsi ya.

english_verse: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
translation_verse: Ban mun mari pɔ'aba, bee, siridima ka n yɛtti wa - amaa pa mani n‑yɛtta, amaa ti Dugma zaaligu nŋɔa - ni, pɔ'a di yi u sira yiri:

english_verse: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
translation_verse: amaa u yi yi, ŋɔn di laa n‑kuli dɔsɔa, bee, ŋɔn labma u sira maa yiri. Ka doo mun di yiisi u pɔ'a.

english_verse: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
translation_verse: Ban kpalim muna ka n yɛtti wa - mani n‑yɛtta, pa ti Dugma zaaligu nŋɔa - ni, Kirisita bisɔ yi mari u pɔ'a ka pɔ'a maa pa la yɛdda nira amaa u sakki ni u kpalim m‑bɛ u saani, tɔ, ŋɔn di yiisi u pɔ'a maa.

english_verse: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a mun yi mari u sira ka sira maa pa la yɛdda nira amaa u sakki ni u kpalim m‑bɛ u saani, tɔ, ŋɔn di yi.

english_verse: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
translation_verse: Dama doo ŋɔn ka yɛdda maa leebi la Naawunni dinni u pɔ'a la zugu, ka pɔ'a sɔ ŋɔn ka yɛdda la gba leebi la Naawunni dinni u sira la zugu: di yi pa lala, ya biisi naa m‑pa la Naawunni dinni, amaa ka ba siri nyɛ la u dinni.

english_verse: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
translation_verse: Amaa ŋɔn ka yɛdda la yi ya bakki, kyɛ ka u bakki. Yɛdda daana la ka taalli dinŋɔ ni: Naawunni m booli ti ni ti bɛ sufudoaa ni.

english_verse: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
translation_verse: I bu nya, pɔ'a? - di bu ta'ai ni i fa i sira m‑basi. Bee nyini bu nya, doowa? - di bu ta'ai ni i fa i pɔ'a m‑basi.

english_verse: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
translation_verse: Di zaa yɔri, ya kyɛ ka sɔkam kpalim m‑bɛ beesiseaa ti Dugma ŋɔn daa zaali u, sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli u maa. N zaaligu n‑kur la, man yi bɛ Naawunni niriba la'aŋŋu kam zaa ni.

english_verse: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
translation_verse: Gunibu bee bu guni zaa nyɛ la yɔri: din kpa talasi nyɛ la Naawunni zaaligu zaa dɔligu.

english_verse: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka sɔkam kpalim m‑bɛ ŋɔn daa bɛ seem sahanseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli u.

english_verse: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
translation_verse: I yi daa nyɛ la daabri i booligu saha maa, nyin di kyɛ ka di mugsi i - amaa i yi ti nya soori ni i ti yi daabtiri maa na, kur dɔla ka n‑yi.

english_verse: When the Lord chooses slaves, they become his free people. And when he chooses free people, they become slaves of Christ.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, ŋɔn daa nyɛ daabri u booligu maa saha zaa mari la yɔlisim ti Dugma saani, ka ŋɔn mun daa su u maŋŋa u booligu maa saha leebi la daabri n‑ti Kirisita.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
translation_verse: Ba daa da ya ni n‑yɔa; dizugu yan di leebi daaba n‑ti saalidima.

english_verse: Stay what you were when God chose you. Unmarried People
translation_verse: Dizugu sɔkam ŋɔn daa bɛ seem zaa ka Naawunni daa booli u, u kpalimma m‑bɛ lala, Naawunni zugu.

english_verse: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ban na bu di pɔ'aba muna, ni pɔ'aseeba ban na bu kuli dɔppa gba - n ka ti Dugma zaaligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba yɛla; amaa man ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ Naawunni ŋɔn zɔ namboo n‑ti ma la, ka u kyɛ ka n niŋi yɛdda daana di zugu, m mari la saawara n‑ti ba.

english_verse: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
translation_verse: Ni, n teesiya, ni, zuna mugsigu ŋɔn bɛ seem zugu, ba yi kpalim m‑bɛ lala, di sɔa.

english_verse: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
translation_verse: Ni, i pun di pɔ'a? - tɔ, di yiisi u. I na ka pɔ'a - tɔ, di bɔ pɔ'a.

english_verse: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
translation_verse: Amaa i yi di pɔ'a, i bu tum taalli; ka pɔ'asariga yi kuli doowa, u gba bu tum taalli. Amaa ban mari pɔ'aba, bee siridima, nin di wahala duniya wa puuni, ka m boori ni n kyɛ ka ya ku di lala wahala.

english_verse: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
translation_verse: Man wulisiri seelli maa nŋɔa, m mabiisi: saha maa ku yuui. Pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑wa, see ka ban mari pɔ'aba gba bɛ hoo ban ka.

english_verse: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
translation_verse: Lala yaasa, kyɛ ka ban kumni la bɛ hoo ban bu kumna, ka ban mari sufumaasim bɛ hoo ban mari sufusa'aŋŋu, halli ban daari neema gba bɛ hoo ban bu su marigu.

english_verse: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
translation_verse: M‑paasi lala, ban mari duniya buna n‑tumna, ba di kyɛ ka ba ba'a zaa bɛ a ni. Dama duniya kanŋɔ gatti ni maa.

english_verse: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
translation_verse: M bu boori ni seelli ti mugsi ya. Dakoori ba'a zaa bɛ la ti Dugma yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka ti Dugma sufu maai.

english_verse: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
translation_verse: Pɔ'adaana mun ba'a bɛ la duniya yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka u pɔ'a sufu maai;

english_verse: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
translation_verse: ka u ba'a puri buyi. Lala ka pukoori, bee, pɔ'asariga ba'a zaa bɛ la ti Dugma yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka bɛ kasi n‑ti ti Dugma, u ningbiŋŋu ni, ni u sii ni gba. Pɔ'a sɔ ŋɔn mari u sira muna - u ba'a bɛ la duniya yɛla ni; ni u baŋŋi ŋɔn nin niŋi seem ka u sira sufu maai.

english_verse: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
translation_verse: N yɛtti dinŋɔ la ya suŋŋsim zugu; pa la man ya ta'ari ya yɛla. N ya wulisiya ya ya nin niŋi seem ka ya teesa ku puri, amaa ka ya tumni din tuusi, ka kur gyeemni ti Dugma la suŋŋasuŋŋa.

english_verse: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi dɔsɔ ŋɔn su pɔ'asariga yɛla maa: pɔ'a maa yuuma yi paai doo kulibu ka dɔsɔ ŋɔn su u yɛla maa teesi, ni, u bu tumni din tu seem n‑tiri u, kyɛ ka u niŋi u sufu ŋɔn boori seem: ba yi ti niŋi amriya di ka taalli.

english_verse: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna pɔ'a maa yi ti kuli doowa, lala dɔli la soori amaa u yi bu kuli doowa, di sɔa.

english_verse: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a sɔ yi kuli doowa, di sum ni u bɛ la u sira maa saani, halli ni doo maa nyɔvuri tɔriga. Amaa u sira maa yi ti kpi, u mari soori ni u kuli dɔsɔ ŋɔn boora; amaa see ka doo maa nyɛ la ti Dugma nira.

english_verse: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
translation_verse: Di zaa yɔri, u yi kpalim la pukoori u nin mari sufumaasim n‑gaari lala - man teesi seem nŋɔa, ka m mi, ni, n gba mari Naawunni Siiya.

english_verse: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi ban zaŋŋi m‑maali ba'a la yɛla: ni, ‘Ti zaa mari la bamsim.’ - tɔ, ti siri mi lala halli, amaa bamsim lala taabu zugu ka niriba kur duusiri ba mansi; wurilim mun nyɛ la din paasiri niriba beesisuŋŋu.

english_verse: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
translation_verse: Ka nira ŋɔn teesi, ni, ŋɔn mi la seelli la - di daama na ka miilinsi din dɔli soori.

english_verse: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
translation_verse: Amaa nira ŋɔn wuri Naawunni, Naawunni mi u yɛla.

english_verse: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
translation_verse: Tɔ: zaŋŋi n‑kpa ba'a bundira diibu ziiya, ti mi, ni, ba'ari pa la bunseelli kam duniya wa ni, ka wunni ka ni di yi pa la Naawunni kɔkɔa.

english_verse: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
translation_verse: Ba yi tɔ'asi ba'a ni wuna yɛla duniya wa puuni, saazugu bee tingbaŋŋu ni - halli ka ba siri tɔ'asiri la wuna ni dugmadima pam yɛla -

english_verse: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
translation_verse: di zaa yɔri, dama tirima saani ka Naawunni yinni bɛ ni. Ti Ba Naawunni n nyɛ u, ka bunseelli kam yi la u saani na, ka ti gba bɛ ni n‑tiri u. Ka ti Dugma yinni gba bɛ ni: Yisa Kirisita n nyɛ u, ka bunseelli kam bɛ ni ŋɔn Yisa zugu, halli ŋɔn zugu ka ti gba bɛ ni.

english_verse: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni nla: amaa pa sɔkam zaa m mari bamsim wa. Seeba na kpalim m‑miili la ba'a yɛla, halli ka ba yi ti di lala diibu ba teesiri, ni, ba diri a ni n‑tiri ba'ari maa. Lala ka ban bu mi taalli ni din tu la yɛla galimiya ni.

english_verse: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
translation_verse: Pa diibu zugu ka ti pɔtti Naawunni saani na: ti yi bu di, ti ku pɔsigi seelli; ti mun yi di, lala ku paasiri ti seelli.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
translation_verse: Amaa ya baŋŋma ka ya yuubu niŋbu di kyɛ ka ban yɛdda bu zɛ deede la ti burigi n‑lu.

english_verse: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - ŋɔn bu mi taalli ni din tu la yi ti nya ka nyini bamsim daana wa bɛ la ba'a duri ni n‑diri diibu, ammi, lala ku kyɛ ka u di ba'a diibu? - Ii, u nin di.

english_verse: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
translation_verse: Ya yi tum taalli wa taabu n‑niŋi ya mabiisi, ka kyɛ ka ba burigi ban na zi soori deede zugu, ya tumni la taalli n‑niŋi Kirisita.

english_verse: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
translation_verse: Dizugu n numni wa ŋɔppu yi nin kyɛ ka n taraana burigi, dinŋɔna n ku laa n‑ŋɔbi numni yaasa, dama di nin kyɛ ka n taraana burigi n‑lu.

english_verse: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Man bu su m mammaŋŋa? Pa tuumu n nyɛ ma? Bee, m pun bu nya ti Dugma Yisa? Ay! - n daa nya u ka leebi u tuumu, ka ŋɔna n ti ma yɔlisimma: halli n tuma zugu ka yarim gba leebi Naawunni niriba.

english_verse: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
translation_verse: Dizugu n yi pa la tuumu n‑ti seeba, n nyɛ la tuumu n‑ti yarima - man zugu ka ya nyɛ la Naawunni niriba, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka ba baŋŋi, ni, tuumu n nyɛ ma.

english_verse: When people question me, I tell them
translation_verse: N labsigu nŋɔa, n‑tiri ban ya boosi ma boosa.

english_verse: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
translation_verse: Ammi, tirima ka soori ni ti nya ti bundira ni bunnyuura?

english_verse: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
translation_verse: Halli tirima kɔkɔ n ka soori ni ti di pɔ'a ŋɔn nyɛ yɛddaniŋna, ka kyɛ ka u dɔli ti ti gyiliŋŋu ni, bee? - hoo tuumuseeba la ŋɔn tumni seem, ni ti Dugma tunzɔppa la, ni Siifasi gba.

english_verse: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
translation_verse: Bee, kulla man ni Baanabasi kɔkɔ n ka soori ni ti ku ti bukaata, see ka ti tumni ti toori nuusi tuma?

english_verse: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
translation_verse: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli Musa zaaligu sɔppu ni, ni, “Nigi yi tumni kyi buubu tuma, yan di lɔ ba nɔya.” - Tɔ, nigi yɛla m mugsi Naawunni, bee?

english_verse: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
translation_verse: Ay! - U yɛtti dinŋɔ zaa n‑ti la tirima, bee wula? Ii! - tirima zugu ka ba daa sɔbi a zaa, ni lala kyɛ ka ŋɔn koora ni ŋɔn buuri kyi zaa nin la'am m‑mari tama, ni, ba nin deaai ba tɔra.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka tin daa tum ya saani seem la ŋmanni la hoo kyi koobu tuma ka di bɛ la ya siisi ziiya; ka di yi bɛ lala, ammi, ti ka soori ni ti nya anfaani ya saani duniya wa ziiya?

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Soori mun yi bɛ ni ka baseeba nyari lala anfaani ya saani ka di bu sum ni tirima gba nya? Amaa di zaa yɔri, ti daa bu zaŋŋi lala soori maa. Ti daa yɔ ti toori samni zaa, dama ti daa bu boori ni Kirisita lahabasuŋŋu tuma labi nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
translation_verse: M‑paasi lala, ya bu mi, ni, ban tumni Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri ni maa nyari ba diibu la lala tuma zugu? Ii! - ban deaari bunkɔbri niriba saani m‑maanni n‑tiri ba kur mari ba tɔra.

english_verse: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
translation_verse: Lala taabu ka ti Dugma zaali, ni, ban tumni u lahabasuŋŋu tuma maa nin nya lala tuma maa yoori.

english_verse: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
translation_verse: Soori bɛ ni, amaa man mun daa bu deaai lala anfaani. M bu sɔbri yɛla wa ni ya ti niŋi lala n‑ti ma. Ay! - n yi da kpi di naa n‑sɔa! M bu boori ni ba ta'ari m bara nyuubu.

english_verse: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - n yi mooli lahabasuŋŋu maa yɛla, lala bu ti ma soori ni n ti nyuuri bara. Yɛlimaŋni, di nyɛ ma la talasi - halli n yi bu moonna, n nin di wahala.

english_verse: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
translation_verse: N yi tumni lala tuma maa m mammaŋŋa sufu yuubu zugu, n nyari anfaani di ni; amaa di yi pa la n yuubu zugu, n kur kpalim n‑tumni tunseesi ban zaali ma la.

english_verse: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
translation_verse: Ka n yoori anfaani nyɛ la bɔa? Dinŋɔ kɔkɔa: ni, n yi moonni lahabasuŋŋu maa ka ba deaari yɔri, man ŋɔn bu boori ligri zugu, anfaani nla n‑ti ma.

english_verse: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ni man su n toori maa zaa, n sakki, ni, n leebi la daabri n‑ti sɔkam, ni lala ti kyɛ ka n nya niriba pam ka ba ti tuligi.

english_verse: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
translation_verse: Yɛhudiya dima maa saani ka n daa leebi hoo Yɛhudiya doowa, ni lala nin kyɛ ka n nya ba ka ba ti tuligi; n siri pa la zaaligu ŋɔn su sɔ fukumsi, amaa n leebi la hoo lala nira n‑tiri zaaligu ŋɔn su seeba fukumsi la, ka lala ti kyɛ ka zaaligu niriba wa ti nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
translation_verse: Ban mun ka zaaligu la saani ka n yi leebi ba taraana n‑gba kɔŋŋi zaaligu: n siri pa la sɔ ŋɔn bu dɔli Naawunni ŋɔn zaali soori la, dama Kirisita zaaligu su n yɛla zaa, amaa lala kyɛ ka n nya ban ka zaaligu la ka ba ti tuligi.

english_verse: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
translation_verse: Halli ban ka yaa la saani n daa sakki n‑leebi ŋɔn gba ka yaa; yɛlimaŋni, saalidima zaa ŋɔn bɛ seem man gba daa sakki, ni, n ti leebi lala; ka lala ti kyɛ ka n nyaŋŋi n‑fa seeba m‑basi.

english_verse: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
translation_verse: Anŋɔ zaa ka n niŋna, Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu zugu, ni man gba nin toaai n‑la'am n‑di ka bunsuma.

english_verse: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
translation_verse: M‑paasi, ya bu mi zoaa yɛla? - ni, ban zɔri zoaa ni la saani, zɔzɔttiba maa zaa zɔra, amaa yinni kɔkɔ n yi zɔ n‑di. Dizugu ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑zɔ suŋŋa din nin kyɛ ka ya zɔ n‑di.

english_verse: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
translation_verse: Dizugu man mun bu zɔri zoaa la ni hoo ŋɔn zɔri yɔriyɔri; m bu zabri hoo ŋɔn buuri pɛsigu.

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: Ay! - n mari n toori ningbiŋŋu ka gbaari ka ni nimmoaa, ni lala zugu kyɛ ka m pa la sɔ ŋɔn yiisi wa'azu n‑ti seeba, ka m mammaŋŋa kɔŋŋi nyaabu.

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, m bu boori ya kpalim ziilinsi ni, see ka ya baŋŋi, ni, ti badima zaa daa la'am m‑bɛ sangbaŋŋu la lɔŋni, ka ba zaa daa la'am n‑kpɛ teeku maa puuni n‑lɔŋŋi.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Ba zaa daa la'am n‑di diseebu din daa pa duniya wa diibu,

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Di zaa yɔri, ba pam n daa nyɛ seeba Naawunni sufu ŋɔn daa bu maai ba zugu; ka ba daa kpi n‑kpalim n‑dɔya yoo maa ni.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka anŋɔ n nyɛ an kpaamni tirima n‑wulisiri ti, ni, ti di dɔli kɔribeeri hoo ban daa dɔli seem.

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: Halli yan di leebi ban maanni ba'a, hoo ban seeba daa maanni la - hoo din pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem, ni, “Niriba maa daa zinni ni ba ti di ka nyu, ka naayi doaai na nti deemna.”

english_verse: Some of those people did shameful things, and in a single day about
translation_verse: Ti di laa n‑niŋi zina tuma, hoo ban seeba ŋɔn daa niŋi seem, ka ba niriba tusa pisi ni ata daa kpi dahinyinni la.

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: Ni, ‘M mari la seelikam yiiko.’ - tɔ, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri suŋŋsim. Ni, ‘M mari la seelikam yiiko.’ - tɔ, amaa pa seelikam zaa m paasiri niriba beesisuŋŋu.

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Ka ti di zamsiri ti Dugma, hoo ban seeba ŋɔn daa zamsira ka wigi daa ku ba.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Yaasa, ti di nyurimna, hoo ban seeba ŋɔn daa nyurimni maa ka malaaka kum daana daa kyɛ ka ba baasi yɔri.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Tɔ: anŋɔ zaa daa paai ba na, ka a wulisiri ti yɛla, ka ba daa sɔbi a n‑dooli ni ba ti kpaamni ti a zugu - tirima ban kpalim nti nya duniya naari saha paari na.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Dizugu, i yi teesi, ni, i zɛ la deede, nyin baŋŋma ka di lu!

english_verse: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
translation_verse: Yan nya zamsigu seem, di bu yakki n‑gaari ninsaalidima ŋɔn nyari seaa. Yɛdda daana n nyɛ Naawunni, u ku kyɛ ka zamsigu seaa kan gaari ya paai ya. Halli zamsigu yi ti paai ya na, u nin bɔ sɔseaa kan nin kyɛ ka ya nyaŋŋi.

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna, m mabiwutta, ya zɔm ka yi ba'a ni buga gyeeŋŋu ni.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Ya nini n neaai, ka di zugu ka n tɔ'asiri lala n‑tiri ya. Ya mammansi kur baŋŋma n yɛttɔ'a maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
translation_verse: Ti yi mari bunnyuura kɔbeaa la m‑puusi Naawunni barika, pa la tin la'amni n‑tɔtti Kirisita ziim la zugu? Ti mun yi zaŋŋi diibu la n‑ŋmaari n‑tiri taaba, pa la tin la'amni n‑tɔtti Kirisita ningbina zugu?

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka diibu yinni m bɛ ni, ka tirim ban zoaai la'amni n‑ŋɔbra, ka ti ti leebi ninvugyinni ningbina, dama ti zaa la'am n‑diri diibu yinni.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Ya bu ya teesi Izraɛli dima maa yɛla? Ban nyɛ Izraɛli dima duniya wa soori zugu la, niriba zaa ban la'amni m‑maanni sara la, ba laa n‑la'amni n‑tɔtti numni maa n‑ŋɔbra.

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Bɔ ka n wulisiri wa? - Ni, ba'ari numni nyɛ seelli? bee, ni, ba'ari nyɛ seelli?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Ay! - amaa ba'amaanniba bu maanni Naawunni, arizimbeeri ka ba maanna. Ka m bu boori ni ya la'amni n‑deaari arizimbeeri yɛla tɔra.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, ya ku toaai n‑nyu ti Dugma bunnyuura kɔbeaa ni, ka laa n‑nyu arizimbeeri dinni m‑paasi - lala ka soori. Ya ku toaai n‑di ti Dugma laa ni, ka laa n‑di arizimbeeri dinni ni - lala gba ka soori.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Bee, ti ya kyɛ ka ti Dugma nyuuri yisigi? Ammi, ti mari yaa n‑gaari u? Ay - ti ka, walla!

english_verse: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka sɔkam bɔ u mammaŋŋa yɛla, amaa u taraana yɛla ka u boora.

english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Ya ŋɔbma nakoosidima numkoosa zaa, amaa di boosiri numseesi yɛla ni ya ti nya galinsi a zugu.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Dama di sɔbiya, ni, “Duniya wa, ni din bɛ ka ni maa - a zaa nyɛ la ti Dugma dinni.”

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Sɔ ŋɔn bu niŋi yɛdda yi ti zuusi ya u bundirigu ka ya boori kyɛnni, yan dima ban zaali seelli na zaa; yan di boosiri a yɛla ni ya ti nya galinsi a zugu.

english_verse: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
translation_verse: - Tɔ, lala nira maa sufu ni teesa yɛla ka n tɔ'asiri maa, pa ya mammaŋŋa dina. Mani n su m mammaŋŋa yɛla, ka dizugu n ku kyɛ ka sɔ zaŋŋi u sufu ni teesa n‑galim ma ka di mugsi ma.

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: N yi puusi Naawunni ka di diibu maa, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑galim ma, diibu wa zugu? - dama m pun puusi Naawunni di zugu.

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya yi diri diibu, bee, n‑nyuuri bunnyuura, bee, ya yi niŋni seelli zaa, ya kyɛ ka ya tumni lala n‑tiri Naawunni gyirima.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ burigi n‑lu ya yɛla zugu - pa Izraɛli dima, bee ban pa Izraɛli niriba, halli Naawunni niriba gba.

english_verse: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
translation_verse: Lala ka man moora, ni n kyɛ ka sɔkam sufu maai n yɛla zugu. M bu boori m mammaŋŋa anfaani yɛla, see din mari anfaani n‑tiri sɔkam - di nyɛ la: ni, ba nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Ya dɔli n soori maa, hoo man gba dɔli Kirisita Masiiya soori seem la.

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka m pɛ'ari ya, dama ya teaari n yɛla sahakam ka kpalim n‑dɔli wulisigu maa zaa, hoo man daa zaali ya seem la.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Amaa m boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, dɔppa saani sɔkam zaa zuguraana n nyɛ Kirisita Masiiya, pɔ'a mun zuguraana nyɛ la u sira, hoo Kirisita zuguraana ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Dɔsɔ ŋɔn pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni, bee, n‑tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a, di daama diisiri u maŋŋa vi.

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: Amaa pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn mun bu pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni, bee, n‑tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a, lala pɔ'a diisiri u maŋŋa vi. U yi ti niŋi lala, di ŋmanni la hoo ŋɔn laa m‑puni u zugu ka doaai ni zugkoaa n‑tɔ'asira.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
translation_verse: Dama pɔ'a yi ku sakki, ni, u bɔbi u zugu, dinŋɔna ŋɔn kyeema, bee, m‑puni ka doaai n‑zaani zugkoaa. Ka lala mun yi nyɛ la vi, dinŋɔna see ka u bɔbi u zugu.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Doo bu sum ni u pili u zugu, dama Naawunni ŋmaari n nyɛ u, n‑tisiri Naawunni gyirima; pɔ'a mun tisiri doo maa gyirima.

english_verse: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
translation_verse: Dama ba daa bu zaŋŋi pɔ'a m‑maali doowa, amaa ba daa naam doowa pɔi ka naayi yiisi pɔ'a u saani na.

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: Dizugu pa pɔ'a zugu ka doo daa bɛ ni, amaa doo maa zugu ka ba daa maali pɔ'a maa.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna see ka pɔ'a maa bɔbi u zugu suŋŋa, ban su u fukumsi zugu, ni malaakadima la zugu.

english_verse: As far as the Lord is concerned, men and women need each other.
translation_verse: Ni dinŋɔ zaa, ti Dugma saani pɔ'a pa u kɔkɔa ka kyɛ doowa, ka doo gba pa u kɔkɔa ka kyɛ pɔ'a.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Ba daa naam doo ka yiisi pɔ'a u saani na, ka pɔ'a mun yi laa n‑dɔ'ari doowa. Ka seelikam yi la Naawunni saani.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Ya mammansi teesim din sumsi ni: pɔ'a yi bu pili u zugu ka susiri Naawunni - di mari soori?

english_verse: But long hair is a beautiful way for a woman to cover her head.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a mun yi mari u zɔbwɔ'ala, di mari la gyirima n‑ti u. Ba ti u lala zɔbri ni a ti leebi bɔbsuŋŋu n‑ti u.

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Sɔ yi ya laa n‑ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni dinŋɔ zugu, kyɛ ka n yɛli ya, ni, tirima bu dɔli lala soori, halli ya'aseelikam Naawunni niriba ŋɔn la'amni n‑gyeemna.

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Dama, daŋŋni, n wum ya lahabaari, ni, ya yi la'am taaba ni ya gyeem Naawunni, ya mari la nangbanwala - ka n sakki n sufu ni, ni, di bu ta'ari ni ya bɛ lala.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Dama see ka nangbanwala bɛ ya ni, ka lala nin kyɛ ka Naawunni nini ŋɔn tigi seeba la yɛla ti yi paalu ni.

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: Dama ya yi diri ni, sɔkam diri la u mammaŋŋa diibu n‑daŋŋni u taraana; ka kɔm mari seeba, halli ka seeba mun nyuuri nti bugra.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka sahanseaa kam ya yi ti di bundira wa ka nyu kɔbeaa wa bunnyuura, ya moonni la ti Dugma kum yɛla, halli ni u lappu na.

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔnkam di ka nyu ka bu baŋŋi di gbinni, ni, ti Dugma ningbina ni u ziim maa nŋɔa, di daama bɔ la sariya karibu m‑basi u maŋŋa zugu.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Ka ban daa di n‑naai maa, u daa zaŋŋi kɔbeaa gba lala, ni, “Naawunni nangbanyinni zaalipaalli maa nŋɔa; n ziim nin maali lala zaaligu maa n‑ti ya. Ya yi kur ti nyu kanŋɔ sahanseaa zaa, yan teaama n yɛla.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Dama man daa deaai seelli ti Dugma saani la ka n daa zaŋŋi n‑wulisi ya: ni, yunseaa ban daa figsi ti Dugma Yisa maa, u daa daŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi diibu la

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: m‑puusi Naawunni pɛ'abu n‑yɛli, ni, “N ningbina nŋɔa n‑ti yarima; ya niŋma lala ka teaari n yɛla.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Dizugu ŋɔn mi, ni, u bu dɔli soori, ka laa n‑di bundira wa, bee, n‑nyu bunnyuura wa, u galimni la ti Dugma ningbina ni u ziim maa.

english_verse: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: See ka nira viisi u maŋŋa suŋŋa pɔi ka naayi di bundira maa ka nyu kɔbeaa maa bunnyuura.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Lala zugu kyɛ ka ya niriba pam kɔŋŋi yaa ka beera; halli ya seeba mun pun ka ni.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Amaa ti Dugma yi viisiri ti yɛla ka kari ti sariya, u wubsiri ti n‑zaanni ti soori ni, ni lala ti kyɛ ka ba ku la'am ti ni duniya wa dima n‑kari ti sariya n‑lusi ti.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna, m mabiisi, ya yi la'am taaba ni ya ti di, see ka ya guri taaba.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Kɔm yi mari sɔa, ŋɔn dima u yiri ni pɔi, ka lala nin kyɛ ka ya yi la'am taaba ya ku nya galinsi di zugu. Tɔ: n yi ti kyɛ ya saani na, n nin laa n‑wulisi ya din kpalim la zaa.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Ya mi halli, ni, yan daa na bu niŋi tuuba la, ba daa yɔmsi ya ka ya dɔli ba'a an bu nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asira.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Dizugu n yɛtti ya, ni, Naawunni Sii ŋɔn bɛ sɔ saani zaa, di daama ku toaai n‑yɛli “Yisa baasima yɔri!” Ka di yi pa Sii Kasi maa zugu, sɔ mun ku toaai n‑yɛli, ni, “Yisa nyɛ la ti Dugma!”

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka piini balibubalibu m bɛ ni, amaa Sii yinni maa bɛ ni n‑tisira.

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: Gyeeŋŋu sɔya balibubalibu m bɛ ni, amaa ti Dugma yinni ka ti gyeemna.

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: Tuma balibubalibu gba bɛ ni, amaa Naawunni yinni maa n tumni a zaa sɔkam puuni.

english_verse: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
translation_verse: Sii maa wulisiri u maŋŋa n‑tiri sɔkam zaa, ni di zaa ti maali ka kyaŋŋi tooni.

english_verse: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Sii maa zugu ka ninvugsɔ mari yɛm yɛttɔ'a; ka sɔ mun mari bamsim, lala Sii yinni maa zugu.

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: Sɔ yaasa mari la yɛdda lala Sii maa zugu, ka sɔ mun bɛ ni ka u deaai baaridima tippu, Sii yinni maa zugu.

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Seeba yaasa deaari la alahazibu tuma yiiko, bee, anabitiri yɛttɔ'a, bee, m‑baŋŋi siisi yɛla viisigu, bee, n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, bee, m‑baŋŋi lala yɛttɔ'a maa gbinni n‑wulisira.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: Anŋɔ zaa nyɛ la Sii yinni maa tuma, ka u mari n‑tisiri u sufu ŋɔn boori seem.

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Sii yinni maa zugu ka ti zaa daa su Naawunni koom, ka di daa kyɛ ka ti la'am n‑niŋi ningbiŋŋu yinni; halli ni ti seeba daa nyɛ Yɛhudiyadima ka seeba mun daa nyɛ Griikadima gba; ka seeba daa nyɛ daaba, seeba mun daa su ba mansi; ti zaa daa la'am n‑nyu Sii yinni maa.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ningbiŋŋu muna - pa lugri yinni kɔkɔ nla, amaa luga pam.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Sɔ nɔbri yi ti yɛli, ni, “Tɔ, m pa la nuu, dizugu m pa ningbiŋŋu maa lugri, halli fiiŋ gba!”, lala maa ku kyɛ ka ka siri pa ningbiŋŋu maa luginseaa.

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Bee, sɔ tubri yi ti yɛli, ni, “Tɔ, m pa la nimbiri, dizugu m pa ningbiŋŋu lugri kɔtaa!”, lala maa ku kyɛ ka ka siri pa ningbiŋŋu maa luginseaa.

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - ningbiŋŋu zaa yi da nyɛ la nimbiri, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑wumna? Bee, ka zaa yi da nyɛ la tubri, ba ya niŋi la wula n‑wumni nyuu?

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Amaa din nyɛ seem maa, Naawunni daa niŋi luga maa zaa la a lugri a lugri, hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, a zaa yi da nya lugri yinni kɔkɔa, yani ka ningbiŋŋu da ya bɛ ni?

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Din nyɛ seem maa, luga maa zoaai ni, amaa a la'amni n‑niŋi ningbimmunni.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Nimbiri ku toaai n‑yɛli nuu, ni, “I ka bukaata man saani!”, bee, zugu yɛli nɔba, ni, “Ya ka ma bukaata!”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Ay! - halli luginseesi an ka yaa mari la bukaata gba.

english_verse: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
translation_verse: Ka luginseesi tin yɛtti, ni, an ka gyirima la n nyɛ a, ana ka ti gɔsiri suŋŋa; ka a seesi an bu veela la, ana ka ti mari suŋŋa vi zugu

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: - halli ka a bunveela la bu boori lala suŋŋsim. Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn maali ningbiŋŋu seem maa, u zaŋŋi la gyirima n‑ti luginseesi an yɛla bu mugsi pam la.

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: Naawunni tum lala ni u ti kyɛ ka nangbankpeemni ni wɔligri ka ningbiŋŋu maa ni, amaa ka luga maa zaa ti suŋŋni m‑mari taaba.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Lugri yinni yi ti namsi, a zaa yi la'am n‑namsi ni; lugri yinni mun yi nya pɛ'abu, sufu maari n nyɛ di n‑tiri a zaa.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Tɔ: yarima mun n la'am n‑nyɛ Kirisita ningbiŋŋu, ka ya zaa yinni yinni n nyɛ u ningbinluga.

english_verse: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni daa yiisi u niriba ka ba tumni tuma balibubalibu n‑tiri taaba: u daa daŋŋi n‑zaali seeba ni ba ti niŋi u tuumudima maa; ka laa n‑zaali seeba m‑pa anabidima; nta karimbadima, m‑paasi ban tumni alahazibu tuma, ban tibri baaridima, ban suŋŋni niriba, ni Naawunni niriba toondaandima, ni ban tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: Sɔkam zaa nyɛ la tuumu? - Ay! Sɔkam nyɛ la anabi, bee, karimba? Sɔkam n toaai n‑tumni alahazibu tuma? - Ay! di pa lala.

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: M‑paasi yaasa: sɔkam tibri la baaridima? Sɔkam n toaai n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu? Bee, m‑baŋŋi lala yɛttɔ'a gbinni? - Ay!

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔna ya booma piiniseesi an tibsa la. Halli ya kyɛ ka n wulisi ya sɔseaa kan veeli n‑gaari zaa#:-

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: N yi nyaŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri ninsaalidima yɛttɔ'a kam zaa, halli ni malaakadima yɛttɔ'a gba, ka mun ka wurilim, dinŋɔna n ŋmanni la gbiliŋŋa bee feeŋŋa n‑kumni yɔri.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: N yi niŋi anabitiri, ka laa m‑mari bunsɔ'ara zaa bamsim, m‑mari yɛdda halli nti kyɛ ka zɔya vuui m‑paai seela, ka mun ka wurilim, dinŋɔna m pa seelli.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Halli n yi zaŋŋi n ligri ni n neema zaa n‑tɔri fara dima, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi n ningbiŋŋu n‑kyɛ ka ba nyɔ ka bugum, ka mun ka wurilim, n ka anfaani anŋɔ zaa zugu.

english_verse: Love is patient and kind, never jealous, boastful, proud, or
translation_verse: Wurilim mari la sugru ni nambɔzoaa; wurilim bu zabri nyuuri; ka mun bu nyuuri bara, bee, m‑mari karimbaani.

english_verse: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
translation_verse: Wurilim bu tumni gyɛrilim; di bu boori di mammaŋŋa yuubu, bee, n‑gyɛ suuri yooma: wurilim bu teaari taalli n‑guri ni di ti boosi.

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
translation_verse: Wurilim sufu bu maara ninvugseeba tumbeeri zugu; amaa ka mari la sufumaasim yɛlimaŋni zaa zugu.

english_verse: Love is always supportive, loyal, hopeful, and trusting.
translation_verse: Wurilim ta'ari ni, m‑mari yɛdda seelikam puuni, ka laa m‑mari tama ka diri sugru seelikam puuni.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Wurilim bu naara: amaa anabitiri yɛla yi bɛ ni, a nin paai a tɔriga; ka ba yi ti tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, ba nin naa n‑sini ba noori; bamsim gba nin paai di naari.

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
translation_verse: Dama ti bamsim maa pa la bamsimunni, ka ti anabitiri gba pa la bummunni.

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: Ka din nyɛ bummunni deede la yi ti paai na, lala saha ka an pa bummuna la nin gaari n‑naai.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? man daa nyɛ bibila sahanseaa la, n daa mari la biilim n yɛttɔ'a puuni, ni n teesa ni n yɛm yɛla zaa ni. Amaa man daa leebi bunkurigu maa, n daa zaŋŋi biilim yɛla maa zaa m‑basi.

english_verse: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
translation_verse: Dama pampaŋŋɔ ti nyari liisaliisa hoo mansɔ'asi ni, amaa ka ti nin nya kasi hoo nini ŋɔn kpa taaba, din daari maa. Pampaŋŋɔ n na bu mi yɛla zaa suŋŋa, a seesi kɔkɔ ka m mi; amaa din daari maa n nin ti baŋŋi di zaa kasi, hoo ban baŋŋi ma seem la.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Dizugu an kpalim m‑bɛ ni n nyɛ yɛdda, ni tama, ni wurilim, abata wa; amaa din gaari a zaa nyɛ la wurilim.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna ya dɔli wurilim sɔya, ka bɔ Sii maa piini; ka kan daŋŋi tooni la nyɛ la anabitiri yɛla piini.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu maa bu tɔ'asiri n‑tiri saalidima, amaa Naawunni ka u tɔ'asira. Halli sɔsɔ bu baŋŋni a gbinni, ka u kpalim n‑yɛtti yɛlisɔ'ara u siiya puuni.

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mun tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛttɔ'a maa yi tɔ'asiri n‑tiri niriba, di paasiri ba beesigu ka kpamsiri ba.

english_verse: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
translation_verse: Ii - ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu maa paasiri u maŋtoori beesigu, ŋɔn mun yɛtti anabitiri yɛla la paasiri Naawunni niriba zaa beesigu.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Tɔ, n da boori la ni ya zaa toaai n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, ka laa m‑boori ni ya yɛtti anabitiri yɛla, ka lala yi sɔa. Anabi gaari ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu, di yi pa la sɔ bɛ ni n‑wulisiri yɛttɔ'a balibu maa gbinni ka lala nin paasi Naawunni niriba beesigu.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Tɔ, m mabiisi: n yi kyɛ ya saani na n‑tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, n suŋŋni ya la wula? Ay, zaalim! - ka di yi pa la n laa m‑mari yɛlisɔ'ara kasigri, bee, bamsim yɛla, bee, anabitiri yɛla, bee, wulisigu n‑ti ya na.

english_verse: If all musical instruments sounded alike, how would you know the difference between a flute and a harp?
translation_verse: Halli bunseesi an ka nyɔvuri saani di kur bɛ la lala: ya bu nya? - bunŋmeera hoo yiiya bee goongyi yi bu ŋmɛ n‑gɔligi suŋŋa, wula ka nira ya baŋŋi ŋmeebu maa gbinni?

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Ka bunseaa ban ŋmeeri m‑moonni tɔppu yɛla la muna - ka yi bu ŋmɛ n‑gɔligi, wula ka ba ya baŋŋi n‑niŋi tɔppu siri?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Ya mammansi yɛttɔ'a ni gba, ya yi bu kyɛ ka ya yɛla yiiri suŋŋa, wula ka sɔ ya niŋi m‑baŋŋi yan yɛtti seelli maa gbinni? Ay! - lala kur ŋmanni la yan tɔ'asiri n‑tiri pɛsigu.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Amaa sɔ yi ti tɔ'asi n‑ti ma ka m bu wum u yɛttɔ'a maa, u nin gɔsi ma, ni, tinzunnira, ka man mun gɔsiri u gba lala.

english_verse: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
translation_verse: Tɔ: dizugu ya mansi ŋɔn boori Sii maa piini la, ya kpaŋŋma n‑nya an paasiri la Naawunni niriba beesigu, ka ya zaa ti kyaŋŋi tooni.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna, kyɛ ka ŋɔn tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu zaa la ti susiri Naawunni ni u kyɛ ka u toaai n‑wulisiri di gbinni gba.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Dama n yi tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu m‑puusi Naawunni, n sii maa puusiri ni, amaa n yɛm ka anfaani di puuni.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna - n ya niŋi la wula? N nin zaŋŋi n sii maa m‑puusi Naawunni, ka laa n‑zaŋŋi n yɛm m‑puusi u; n nin zaŋŋi n sii maa n‑yuum yuuma n‑ti Naawunni, ka zaŋŋi n yɛm n‑yuumni m‑paasi.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Bee, i yi puusiri Naawunni i sii puuni, wula ka ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ bɛni nin niŋi m‑baŋŋi n‑niŋi “Ammi!” n‑ti i puusigu maa? - dama ŋɔn bu wumni nyin yɛtti seelli wa.

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Nyin ŋɔn puusiri suŋŋa maa zaa yɔri, di ku toaai m‑paasi i taraana beesigu.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Halli man tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu n‑gaari ya zaa, ka m puusi Naawunni di zugu.

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Ni di zaa yɔri, n yi bɛ la Naawunni niriba sunsukkuni, m boori ni n zaŋŋi n yɛm n‑tɔ'asi beela ni n ti wulisi n taaba, ka di gaari n yi tɔ'asi pam yɛttɔ'azuna ni ka ba bu wumna.

english_verse: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
translation_verse: Tɔ, m mabiisi: yan di teesiri hoo bibisi: yɛlibeeri ziiya ka ya siri nin bɛ ni hoo bileesi, amaa yɛm ziiya see ka ya nin bɛ ni hoo ninvugkura.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Di sɔbiya, Naawunni zaaligu maa ni, ni#:- “N nin zaŋŋi yɛttɔ'azuna Ni yan bu mi seeba maa yɛligu N‑tɔ'asi n‑ti butiri wa niriba, Ni di zaa ba ku wum n‑ti ma. - Lala ka ti Dugma yɛli.”

english_verse: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna yɛttɔ'azuna bulisiri yɛla n‑tiri ban ka yɛdda la, pa ban mara; anabitiri yɛla mun bulisiri yɛla n‑tiri ban mari yɛdda, pa ban ka.

english_verse: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Naawunni niriba yi la'am n‑gyeemna, ka ba zaa tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibubalibu, ka saamma bee ban ka yɛdda dima kpɛ na, ba ku yɛli, ni, ya kpɛ la yinyaari?

english_verse: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
translation_verse: Amaa ya zaa yi tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛla ka lala nira ti kpɛ na, ya zaa zugu ka u nin ti baŋŋi u taalli ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni, ba kari u sariya ka lusi u.

english_verse: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
translation_verse: Di saha ka u nin yɛli u sufu yɛlisɔ'ara an bɛ seem zaa, ka gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeem Naawunni ka yɛli, ni, “Naawunni siri bɛ ya saani.”

english_verse: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
translation_verse: Bɔ n yi kpalim, m mabiisi? - Ya yi la'am taaba, sɔkam mari u yuumni, bee, u yɛliwulisira, bee, u bunsɔ'ara kasigri yɛla, bee, u tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'a balibu bee n‑wulisiri lala yɛttɔ'a gbinni. Tɔ - ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑kyɛ ka anŋɔ zaa nin paasi Naawunni niriba beesigu.

english_verse: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
translation_verse: Seeba yi ya tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a balibu yɛla, kyɛ ka di nyɛ la babayi-ata kɔkɔa, ka ba tɔ'asi yinniyinni n‑deaai taaba; ka laa n‑kyɛ ka sɔ bɛ ni n‑wulisiri a gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
translation_verse: Amaa sɔ yi ka ni n‑wulisiri a gbinni, kyɛ ka yɛttɔ'a balibu daana sini Naawunni niriba la'aŋŋu ni, ka naayi tɔ'asi n‑tiri u maŋŋa ni Naawunni.

english_verse: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
translation_verse: Anabidima maa muna - ya kyɛ ka ba gba ti tɔ'asi ba niriba ayi-ata, ka ban kpalim la kpasi ba yɛttɔ'a maa.

english_verse: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
translation_verse: Ka yɛttɔ'a yi kyɛ sɔ ŋɔn ziya saani na, kyɛ ka ŋɔn pun tɔ'asiri la ti tɔ'asi n‑naai pɔi, ka ŋɔnla naayi deaai yɛttɔ'a.

english_verse: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
translation_verse: Dama ya zaa nin toaai n‑tɔ'asi ya anabitiri yɛla n‑deaari taaba yinniyinni, ka sɔkam bamsi ka di kpamsi ba.

english_verse: A prophet should be willing to stop and let someone else speak.
translation_verse: Anabidima maa n su ba mammaŋŋa anabitiri yɛla fukumsi,

english_verse: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
translation_verse: dama Naawunni pa la ŋɔn burimna, amaa ŋɔn maanna. See ka ya dɔli ban la'amni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la zaa kaari,

english_verse: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
translation_verse: ka kyɛ ka pɔ'aba kpalim m‑bɛ surimm ya la'aŋŋu maa ni. Ba ka soori ni ba tɔ'asira, see ka ba dɔli soori hoo zaaligu maa ŋɔn yɛli seem la.

english_verse: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a yi boori ni u baŋŋi yɛttɔ'aseaa gbinni, see ka ba naayi kuli yiri ni ka u ti boosi u sira. Pɔ'a yi ti tɔ'asiri Naawunni niriba la'aŋŋu ni, di mari la vi.
