translation_verse: Ba nin ti yi taaba nyaaŋŋa; ba nin luuri n‑tumni basabasa; ba nin tibgri ba mansi ba yɛttɔ'a ni. Halli ka sɔkam boori la u sufuyuubu bunnya'asa ka za'asi Naawunni yɛla wumpu.

english_verse: Even though they will make a show of being religious, their religion won't be real. Don't have anything to do with such people.
translation_verse: Niriba ti ya n‑dɔli la addiini soori ka niriba ti nya, amaa ka bu dɔli Naawunni yiiko soori ni yɛlimaŋni. Zɔma lala niriba.

english_verse: Some men fool whole families, just to get power over those women who are slaves of sin and are controlled by all sorts of desires.
translation_verse: Niriba wa taabu yi kur yɔmsiri n‑kpeeri yiya ni, ni ba ti la'ari pɔ'aseeba ban ka yaa n‑gbaasiri ba ni ba ti leebi ba nyaandɔliba. Lala pɔ'aba mun dɔli ba, ba maŋtoori tumbeeri yɛla zugu, ni ba kɔribeeri yɛla zugu m‑paasi.

english_verse: These women always want to learn something new, but they never can discover the truth.
translation_verse: Lala pɔ'aba maa yi kur wumni wulisigu, amaa ba bu ya nyaŋŋi m‑paai yɛlimaŋni yɛla bamsim.

english_verse: Just as Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, these people are enemies of the truth. Their minds are sick, and their faith isn't real.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka niriba wa ŋmanni la Gyansi ni Gyambirisi, ban daa nyɛ Igyipti dɔseeba ban daa kyiisi Musa yɛla la; lala ka niriba wa yi kyiisiri yɛlimaŋni maa. Lala niriba zaa mari la teesibeeri, ka kɔŋŋi yɛdda soori dɔligu.

english_verse: But they won't get very far with their foolishness. Soon everyone will know the truth about them, just as Jannes and Jambres were found out. Paul's Last Instructions to Timothy
translation_verse: Amaa ba yɛla ku toaai n‑kyaŋŋi tooni. Lala niriba daa kyiisi Musa, ka ba gyɛrilim daa yi paalu ka sɔkam nya, lala ka sɔkam nin ti nya niriba wa dinni paalu ni.

english_verse: Timothy, you know what I teach and how I live. You know what I want to do and what I believe. You have seen how patient and loving I am, and how in the past I put up with
translation_verse: Nyin mun daa pun dɔli man ŋɔn daa wulisi sɔseelli. I daa pun bamsi n beebu soori, ka mi man ŋɔn daa gbaai gbaari seem la. I pun mi n yɛdda yɛla, ni n sugru diibu yɛla. I dɔli la n wurilim soori, ka laa n‑zɛ yummu hoo man daa zɛ seem la.

english_verse: trouble and suffering in the cities of Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. Yet the Lord rescued me from all those terrible troubles.
translation_verse: Halli i laa m‑mi mugsigu ni wahala seesi an daa paai ma na: i mi n dindaandima ban daa niŋi ma seem Antiyɔkku tiŋŋa, ni Aikoonyum ni Lisitira tinsi ni gba. Amaa ka ti Dugma daa yiisi ma a zaa ni.

english_verse: In fact, anyone who belongs to Christ Jesus and wants to live right will have trouble from others.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, ninsaala kam ŋɔn boori ni u dɔli sɔsuŋŋu ka gyeemni Kirisita Yisa nin nya namsigu.

english_verse: But evil people who pretend to be what they are not will become worse than ever, as they fool others and are fooled themselves.
translation_verse: Halli ka teesibeeri dima ni ziri dima zaa nin maali n‑gbarigi ba tumbeeri zugu. Ba yɔmsiri niriba, ka niriba mun yɔmsiri ba gba.

english_verse: Keep on being faithful to what you were taught and to what you believed. After all, you know who taught you these things.
translation_verse: Amaa nyin muna - see ka i kur kpalim n‑dɔli ban daa pun wulisi i bunseesi la, seesi nyin daa pun niŋi a yɛdda; dama i mi halli ban saani ka i daa bamsi a.

english_verse: Since childhood, you have known the Holy Scriptures that are able to make you wise enough to have faith in Christ Jesus and be saved.
translation_verse: Ka laa m‑mi, ni, i daa baŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu, nyin daa pun nyɛ bibila sahanseaa la. Lala yɛligu maa teesa zugu ka i nin ti nya bamsim, ka di zugu i nin niŋi Kirisita Yisa yɛdda nti nya tuliginsim u zugu.

english_verse: Everything in the Scriptures is God's Word. All of it is useful for teaching and helping people and for correcting them and showing them how to live.
translation_verse: Litaafi yɛligu maa sɔppu zaa ka Naawunni vuusim daa ti ti na; a zaa mari yiiko nti wulisi ti, ka kyiisi ziri, ni ti ti baŋŋi tumbeeri ni din bu dɔlisi yɛla; ka a laa n‑wulisiri ti ŋɔn Naawunni yɛlimaŋni soori ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: The Scriptures train God's servants to do all kinds of good deeds.
translation_verse: Dizugu Naawunni nira nin toaai n‑tumni ŋɔn Naawunni sufuyuubu ka laa m‑mari yɛm ni yiiko ni u ti tum tunsuma zaa.

english_verse: When Christ Jesus comes as king, he will be the judge of everyone, whether they are living or dead. So with God and Christ as witnesses, I command you
translation_verse: Naawunni zugu ka n ya n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'a wa n‑kpaam i, Kirisita Yisa zugu; dama ŋɔn Yisa nin ti kari ban nɛ ni ban kpi zaa sariya. Ŋɔn Yisa Masiiya lappu na zugu, ni u naam diibu la zugu, ka n ya n‑yɛli i ni, ni, i niŋma nŋɔa#:-

english_verse: to preach God's message. Do it willingly, even if it isn't the popular thing to do. You must correct people and point out their sins. But also cheer them up, and when you instruct them, always be patient.
translation_verse: Moolima Naawunni yɛligu; kur yirigma di yɛla sahasuŋŋu, halli ni sahaseaa din pa sahasuŋŋu gba. Sɛ'asim niriba, kpaasim ba zugri ni; kpaamni niriba suŋŋa; kur dim sugru n‑wulisiri n‑wulisiri ba.

english_verse: The time is coming when people won't listen to good teaching. Instead, they will look for teachers who will please them by telling them only what they are itching to hear.
translation_verse: Dama saha kyɛnni na ka niriba ku ti laa n‑deaari yɛlimaŋni wulisigu, amaa ka nin dɔli la ba mammaŋŋa kɔrisi soori. Ba nin bɔ m‑bɔ karimbadima bɔbgu, seeba ban nin wulisiri wulisiseesi an niŋni ba nya'asim m‑paasira.

english_verse: They will turn from the truth and eagerly listen to senseless stories.
translation_verse: Ba nin za'asi yɛlimaŋni wumpu, ka zaŋŋi ba ba'aba zaa n‑niŋi kurimbu ni sa lahabaya an ka yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: But you must stay calm and be willing to suffer. You must work hard, telling the good news and to do your job well.
translation_verse: Amaa nyin muna - kur gbaama i maŋŋa yɛlli kam zaa puuni, m‑mari sugru wahala diibu ni; tumni lahabasuŋŋu mooligu tuma, ka laa n‑tum Naawunni ŋɔn yiisi i, ni i tum tunseaa la.

english_verse: Now the time has come for me to die. My life is like a drink offering being poured out on the altar.
translation_verse: Man muna - saha maa paaya ni n nyɔvuri leebi hoo sara maaligu: n duniya wa ni yiibu saha pɔtta.

english_verse: I have fought well. I have finished the race, and I have been faithful.
translation_verse: N zabi zabsuŋŋu n‑naai; n pun zɔ nti paai zoaa maa tɔriga; n daa mari n yɛdda niŋbu suŋŋa.

english_verse: So a crown will be given to me for pleasing the Lord. He judges fairly, and on the day of judgment he will give a crown to me and to everyone else who wants him to appear with power. Personal Instructions
translation_verse: Seela bu laa n‑kpalim n‑ti ma, di yi pa la n yɛlimaŋni tuma wa laara deaari. Ti Dugma Yɛlimaŋni Daana nin ti kari sariya Zaadaari maa, ka ŋɔn saani ka n nin deaai ka. Laara n dɔya n‑guri ma, ka di pa la man kɔkɔ ka u ti ya n‑zaŋŋi ka n‑tisi, amaa u nin ti zaŋŋi n‑ti bankam zaa sufu boori u lappu na.

english_verse: Come to see me as soon as you can.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ n saani na yoomyooma.

english_verse: Demas loves the things of this world so much that he left me and went to Thessalonica. Crescens has gone to Galatia, and Titus has gone to Dalmatia.
translation_verse: Dama ba zaa bakki ka kyɛ ma: Diimasi daa kyaŋŋi Tɛsalɔnika, ŋɔn wuri duniya wa buna zugu; Karɛseensi mun daa kyaŋŋi Galɛseaa, ka Taituusi kyaŋŋi Dalimɛseaa.

english_verse: Only Luke has stayed with me. Mark can be very helpful to me, so please find him and bring him with you.
translation_verse: Luuki kɔkɔ n kur kpalim m‑bɛ n saani. Zaŋŋma Maaki m‑paasi i maŋŋa zugu n‑kyɛ na, dama u nyɛ la ŋɔn nin toaai n‑suŋŋni ma suŋŋa.

english_verse: I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
translation_verse: Man mun daa tum la Tikyikusi, ni u kyaŋŋi Ɛfɛsusi.

english_verse: Alexander, the metalworker, has hurt me in many ways. But the Lord will pay him back for what he has done.
translation_verse: Alɛkzanda ŋɔn nyɛ mankyeeri la daa tum ma taalli pam: ti Dugma nin boosi u beeri, n‑labsi ŋɔn tum seem maa zugu.

english_verse: Alexander opposes what we preach, so you had better watch out for him.
translation_verse: See ka nyini gba mari i maŋŋa u saani, dama u daa zabi n‑kyiisi ti yɛligu la nimbirimoaa.

english_verse: When I was first put on trial, no one helped me. In fact, everyone deserted me. I hope it won't be held against them.
translation_verse: Man daa pun daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n yɛttɔ'a la kootu ni, amaa sɔ daa bu zaani n nyaaŋŋa; sɔkam zaa daa zɔ ni ka kyɛ ma. Naawunni di zaŋŋi di taalli n‑ziili ba!

english_verse: But the Lord stood beside me. He gave me the strength to tell his full message, so that all Gentiles would hear it. And I was kept safe from hungry lions.
translation_verse: Amaa ti Dugma daa zaani n nyaaŋŋa, ka ti ma yaa, nti kyɛ ka lahabasuŋŋu maa mooligu ti yi paalu, ka ban pa Yɛhudiyadima maa zaa wum di. Ti Dugma daa yiisi ma gbugimni noori ni na ni alaafɛa.

english_verse: The Lord will always keep me from being harmed by evil, and he will bring me safely into his heavenly kingdom. Praise him forever and ever! Amen. Final Greetings
translation_verse: Ti Dugma nin yiisi ma yɛlibeoo kam zaa ni na, ka nin mari ma halli nti paai u arizana naam ni. Ŋɔna n sum ni tibgri halli ni saha kan ka tɔriga! Ammi!

english_verse: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila and to the family of Onesiphorus.
translation_verse: Puusima Prisila ni Akwila ni Ɔneesifɔru yiri dima n‑ti ma.

english_verse: Erastus stayed at Corinth. Trophimus was sick when I left him at Miletus.
translation_verse: Ɛrasitusi daa kpalim m‑bɛ Kɔrinti. Ka n daa kyɛ Tirɔfimusi mun Miliitusi timpuuni, ŋɔn daa ka alaafɛ zugu.

english_verse: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus, Pudens, Linus, and Claudia send you their greetings, and so do the rest of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ na ka waari saha na bu piili. Yubulusi puusiri i ni, ŋɔn ni Pudɛnsi ni Linusi ni Klɔdiya, zaŋŋi n‑tabli ti mabiisi ban kpalim la zaa.

english_verse: I pray that the Lord will bless your life and will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Ti Dugma kpamsim i sufu! Naawunni piinisuma nin kpalim m‑bɛ ya ni!

english_verse: Then they will have the hope of eternal life God promised long ago. And God never tells a lie!
translation_verse: Naawunni n daa daŋŋi m‑basi ti nangbambasiri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi nyɔvuri kan ka baasigu la pɔi ka duniya naayi piili. Ka lala nyɔvuri tama zugu ka ti mari ti yɛdda ni bamsim maa n‑zɛ yummu.

english_verse: Titus, because of our faith, you are like a son to me. I pray that God our Father and Christ Jesus our Savior will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! What Titus Was To Do in Crete
translation_verse: N sɔbri n‑tiri la nyini, Taituusi, dama i nyɛ la m bimaŋni, ti zaa ŋɔn mari yɛdda yinni zugu. Ti Ba Naawunni ni Kirisita Yisa nin niŋi bunsuma zaa n‑niŋi i, ka ti i alaafɛa.

english_verse: I left you in Crete to do what had been left undone and to appoint leaders for the churches in each town. As I told you,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka n daa kyɛ i, ni i kpalim m‑bɛ Kyiriti tingbaŋŋu ni, ni i dɔlisi tin daa bu naai tunseesi la ka maali n‑zaanni a. N daa laa m‑boori la i yiisi niriba n‑diisi toondaandima tinkam zaa ni, kaman man ŋɔn daa wulisi i seem maa.

english_verse: they must have a good reputation and be faithful in marriage. Their children must be followers of the Lord and not have a reputation for being wild and disobedient.
translation_verse: I yi diisi sɔ toondaana, see ka di daama mari la beesisuŋŋu ka ka galiŋŋu. See ka u mari la pɔ'a yinni, ka u biisi n nyɛ seeba ban niŋni yɛdda, ka niriba ku nya taalli ba saani, ni, ba bu gbaari ba mansi suŋŋa, bee, ba bu deaari ba badima maa noori.

english_verse: Church officials are in charge of God's work, and so they must also have a good reputation. They must not be bossy, quick-tempered, heavy drinkers, bullies, or dishonest in business.
translation_verse: Toondaana ŋɔn su Naawunni yiri dima tuma fukumsi zugu, di niŋi la kamaata ka u mari la beesisuŋŋu ka ka galiŋŋu. Di bu sumni ni u nyuuri bara, bee, u sufu yisigi yoomyooma. Di mun bu laa n‑sumni ni u nyɛ danyuura, bee, zabboora bee, ŋɔn ba'a zaa bɛ la ligri boobu ni.

english_verse: Instead, they must be friendly to strangers and enjoy doing good things. They must also be sensible, fair, pure, and self-controlled.
translation_verse: See ka u deaari la saamma u yiri ni, ka wuri din veela. Toondaana sumni la u nyɛ yɛm daana ni yɛlimaŋni daana, ni ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ kasi ka mari u maŋŋa suŋŋa.

english_verse: There are many who don't respect authority, and they fool others by talking nonsense. This is especially true of some Jewish followers.
translation_verse: Dama niriba pam siri bɛ ni ban mari tuppuwumni ka kur tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama ni ba ti yɔmsiri niriba. Seeba ban na kur dɔli la Yɛhudiyadima addiini kaya soori maa bɛ la lala halli.

english_verse: But you must make them be quiet. They are after money, and they upset whole families by teaching what they should not.
translation_verse: Ba yɛttɔ'a ka soori - see ka i kyɛ ka ba sina, dama ba wulisiri la wulisiseelli din da bu sumni ni ba wulisi. Lala wulisigu maa ŋmaari la yiya. Nyoori zugu ka ba niŋni lala maa.

english_verse: It is like one of their own prophets once said, “The people of Crete always tell lies. They are greedy and lazy like wild animals.”
translation_verse: Kyiriti dima maa muna - halli ba maŋtoori anabisɔ gba daa yɛli ba yɛla, ni#:- “Kyiriti dima kur nyɛ la ziri dima saha zaa; Ka nyɛ ninvugbeeri, ka nyɛ gbinnya'adima: Beem dima ban wuri bundirigu pam yɔri.”

english_verse: This surely is a true saying. And you should be hard on such people, so you can help them grow stronger in their faith.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni n‑kur la. Dinzugu kpaasim ba zugri ni suŋŋa. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ba yɛdda maali n‑kpaŋŋi suŋŋa.

english_verse: Don't pay any attention to any of those senseless Jewish stories and human commands. These are made up by people who won't obey the truth.
translation_verse: I yi niŋi lala, ba ku laa n‑wumni sɔlinseesi Yɛhudiyadima maa ŋɔn sɔlimni n‑tisiri ba maa; bee, ninsaalidima ban za'asi yɛlimaŋni soori la ŋɔn zaali zaaliseesi maa.

english_verse: Everything is pure for someone whose heart is pure. But nothing is pure for an unbeliever with a dirty mind. That person's mind and conscience are destroyed.
translation_verse: Bunseelli kam zaa nyɛ la kasi n‑ti ninvugseeba ban nyɛ kasi; amaa ban tumni da'ari tuma ka bu niŋni yɛdda la, bunseelli n ka ni n‑nyɛ kasi n‑ti ba. Ba sufu, ni ba sufu ni teesa zaa nyɛ la da'ari.

english_verse: Such people claim to know God, but their actions prove they really don't. They are disgusting. They won't obey God, and they are too worthless to do anything good.
translation_verse: Ba yɛliya, ni, ba mi Naawunni, amaa ka ba tuma zaa kyiisiri u ni. Ba tuma zaa nyɛ la din kyisira, ni noori za'asigu. Dinzugu ba ku nyaŋŋi n‑tum tunsuŋŋu zaa.

english_verse: Titus, you must teach only what is correct.
translation_verse: Amaa nyin mun kur wulisima wulisigu seelli din dɔlisi kan nyɛ yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: Tell the older men to have self-control and to be serious and sensible. Their faith, love, and patience must never fail.
translation_verse: Wulisima ninvugkura la, ni, ba bɛ baalimm, ka ba beebu maa nin sum ni gyirima tisibu. Wulisima ba ka ba mari yɛddamaŋni ni wurilimmaŋni, ka kur kpalim n‑zɛ yummu, dama anŋɔ zaa tisiri alaafɛa.

english_verse: Tell the older women to behave as those who love the Lord should. They must not gossip about others or be slaves of wine. They must teach what is proper,
translation_verse: Wulisima pɔ'akura la gba ka ba beebu mari gyirima; amaa ka pa ni ba leebi ban zuuri niriba nyaaŋŋa, bee, daam ŋɔn su seeba. Kyɛ ka ba wulisiri la din veela.

english_verse: Each of the younger women must be sensible and kind, as well as a good homemaker, who puts her own husband first. Then no one can say insulting things about God's message.
translation_verse: Ni, m‑paasi lala, ni ba mari mangbaari ka bɛ kasi, n‑tumni ba yiya tuma suŋŋa, ka sigsiri ba mansi n‑tiri ba siridima. Dinŋɔ zaa nin kyɛ ka sɔ ku maali Naawunni yɛligu maa ansarisi.

english_verse: Tell the young men to have self-control in everything.
translation_verse: Di sumni, ni, i kpamsi na'akyimbisi la gba ka ba mari mangbaari.

english_verse: Use clean language that no one can criticize. Do this, and your enemies will be too ashamed to say anything against you.
translation_verse: ni i yɛttɔ'a zaa bɛ suŋŋa ka sɔ ku toaai n‑galim i. Dinŋɔ nin kyɛ ka ban bu sakki i yɛligu maa di vi, dama ba ku nya yɛttɔ'abeoo ni ba ti zaŋŋi n‑galim ti beebu soori.

english_verse: Tell slaves always to please their owners by obeying them in everything. Slaves must not talk back to their owners
translation_verse: Wulisim daaba la gba ka ba sigsiri ba mansi n‑tiri ba dugmadima bunseelikam zaa ni. Wulisim ba ka ba tumni din nin kyɛ ka ba dugmadima nini tigra, ka bu ŋmeeri ba nangbankpeemni.

english_verse: or steal from them. They must be completely honest and trustworthy. Then everyone will show great respect for what is taught about God our Savior. God's Kindness and the New Life
translation_verse: Ka ba di zuuri ba dugmadima neema, amaa ba kyɛ ka ba beebu kasigi n‑wulisi, ni, ba nyɛ la yɛdda dima ka zɛ yummu ni yɛlimaŋni. Dina nin kyɛ ka Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ ti tuligra maa yɛla wulisigu la ti nya gyirima, ban n niŋni bunseelikam zaa zugu.

english_verse: God has shown us undeserved grace by coming to save all people.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni n siri wulisi u wurilim paalu ni, ni, u piinisuma zugu ka sɔkam nin toaai n‑nya tuliginsim.

english_verse: He taught us to give up our wicked ways and our worldly desires and to live decent and honest lives in this world.
translation_verse: U wurilim piini wa lahabaari n wulisiri ti, ni, ti za'asima din bu dɔli Naawunni soori, ni din nyɛ duniya wa kɔribeeri, amaa ka kur mari mangbaari, ka zɛ deede, ka laa m‑mari beebu seelli din dɔli la Naawunni soori, saha kanŋɔna.

english_verse: We are filled with hope, as we wait for the glorious return of our great God and Savior Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Ti niŋma dinŋɔ ka guusiri ti tama paalu yiibu na - Yisa Masiiya ŋɔn ti ya zaŋŋi u gyirima n‑yi paalu ni na nla, ka u nyɛ la ti Naawuntitaari, ni ti Tuligra.

english_verse: He gave himself to rescue us from everything evil and to make our hearts pure. He wanted us to be his own people and to be eager to do right.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Yisa n daa zaŋŋi u nyɔvuri n‑tisi, ni u yɔlisi tirima ni ti ti leebi u toori niriba maa. Ka u laa n‑yiisi ti tumbeeri kam zaa ni, ka nyeesi ti ka ti leebi kasi, ka ti sufu kur nin boori la ti tumni din veela.

english_verse: Teach these things, as you use your full authority to encourage and correct people. Make sure you earn everyone's respect.
translation_verse: Anŋɔ n nyɛ bunseesi din sumni ni i wulisiri n‑tiri ba. Mari yiiko zaa n‑kpamsiri ba, ka kpaasiri ba zugri ni. Nyin mun di kyɛ ka sɔ gɔsi i yɔri.

english_verse: Remind your people to obey the rulers and authorities and not to be rebellious. They must always be ready to do something helpful
translation_verse: Teaama i niriba n‑yɛtti ba, ni, ba kyɛ ka ba sufu bɛ nadima ni, ni ba tinkpamma ni, ka wumni ba noori yɛligu ka tumni tunsuma saha kam zaa.

english_verse: and not say cruel things or argue. They should be gentle and kind to everyone.
translation_verse: Ka i laa n‑yɛli ba, ni, ba di sa'amni ba kpeaa yuuri, bee, m‑boori zaba; amaa ba mari nangbanyinni ni sɔkam zaa ka sigsiri ba mansi niriba zaa saani.

english_verse: We used to be stupid, disobedient, and foolish, as well as slaves of all sorts of desires and pleasures. We were evil and jealous. Everyone hated us, and we hated everyone.
translation_verse: Tirima mun gba daa zi Naawunni yɛla. Ka ti daa niŋni gyɛrilim, ka daa dɔli ti mammaŋŋa sufu yuubu ni kɔribeeri, ka burigri n‑yi sɔsuŋŋu ni. Ti daa za'asi ŋɔn Naawunni yɛligu ka daa sa'amni ti taaba yuya ka laa n‑zabri ba nyuuri. Ka niriba daa yaai ti, ka ti mun gba daa bu boori ba yɛla.

english_verse: God our Savior showed us how good and kind he is.
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni ti tuligra ŋɔn daa ti wulisi u sufupeelli maa soori, ni u wurilim,

english_verse: He saved us because of his mercy, and not because of any good things we have done. God washed us by the power of the Holy Spirit. He gave us new birth and a fresh beginning.
translation_verse: ti daa nya tuliginsim. Di daa pa la ti tunsuma zugu, amaa ŋɔn Naawunni daa zɔ ti namboo zugu. U daa zaŋŋi u Sii Kasi maa n‑niŋi ti ni, ka zaŋŋi u kɔsuŋŋu n‑su ti, nti peaai ti tumbeeri m‑basi, ni ti ti maali n‑dɔ'ai n‑nya nyɔvuri paalli.

english_verse: Jesus treated us much better than we deserve. He made us acceptable to God and gave us the hope of eternal life.
translation_verse: U daa niŋi lala ni u ti deaai ti hoo yɛlimaŋni dima u lala wurilim piini la zugu, ka kyɛ ka ti nin mari tama, ni, ti siri nin di nyɔvuri kan ka tɔriga hoo ti faari tɔrli.

english_verse: This message is certainly true. These teachings are useful and helpful for everyone. I want you to insist that the people follow them, so that all who have faith in God will be sure to do good deeds.
translation_verse: Yɛligu wa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni ka i nin toaai n‑niŋi ka yɛdda. M boori la i wulisiri yɛla wa zaa ni nimbirimoaa. Ka lala zugu nin kyɛ ka ninvugseeba ban pun niŋi Naawunni yɛdda la kur nin teesiri tunsuma tumpu yɛla saha kam. Dama lala nin suŋŋi niriba ka niŋi sɔkam zaa anfaani.

english_verse: But don't have anything to do with stupid arguments about ancestors. And stay away from disagreements and quarrels about the Law of Moses. Such arguments are useless and senseless.
translation_verse: Amaa di kyɛ ka i ba'a bɛ yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama nangbankpeemni wa ni, bee, yaadima yuya dɔlisigu ni, bee zaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi zaaligu kaari sɔya. Anŋɔ zaa ka gbinni, ka bɛ la yɔri.

english_verse: Warn troublemakers once or twice. Then don't have anything else to do with them.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn boori wɔligri muna - kur kpamsim u napɔmbuyi pɔi, ka u yi na za'asira, nyin naayi kyɛli u ka di laa m‑puusiri u.

english_verse: You know their minds are twisted, and their own sins show how guilty they are. Personal Instructions and Greetings
translation_verse: Lala nira maa nyɛ la bungɔliŋŋu ka kur tumni la tumbeeri: u katti la u mammaŋŋa sariya ka lusi u maŋŋa.

english_verse: I plan to send Artemas or Tychicus to you. After he arrives, please try your best to meet me at Nicopolis. I have decided to spend the winter there.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka n gbaai la gbaari, ni, n nin kpalim m‑bɛ Nikɔpɔlisi tiŋŋa see ka waari saha ti naai: dizugu n yi ti tum Aritimasi bee Tikyikusi i saani na, nyin kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa ka kyaŋŋi ni i ti paai ma bɛni.

english_verse: When Zenas the lawyer and Apollos get ready to leave, help them as much as you can, so they won't have need of anything.
translation_verse: Kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑suŋŋi Looya Ziinasi ni Apɔloosi ka zaali ba soori, ka di kyɛ ka ba bunseelli ti pɔsigi.

english_verse: Our people should learn to spend their time doing something useful and worthwhile.
translation_verse: Kur kyɛ ka ti niriba maa ti bamsi tunsuma nimmoaa tumpu, ni ba ti ku ba dabsa zaa bukaata ka ku kpalim m‑bɛ yɔri.

english_verse: Greetings to you from everyone here. Greet all of our friends who share in our faith. I pray that the Lord will be kind to all of you!
translation_verse: Baseeba ban bɛ kpa maa puusiri i ni. Ka nyin mun puusima ti zɔdima ban niŋi yɛdda maa zaa n‑ti ma. Naawunni piinisuma bɛ ya zaa saani.

english_verse: From Paul, who is in jail for serving Christ Jesus, and from Timothy, who is like a brother because of our faith. Philemon, you work with us and are very dear to us. This letter is to you
translation_verse: Man Pɔla, ŋɔn daa kpɛ sarika Kirisita Yisa Masiiya yɛla zugu, ni ti mabii Timɔti - ti puusiri i ni. Mani Pɔla n‑sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri nyini Failiimɔn, tin la'am n‑tumni Kirisita Yisa tuma la zugu.

english_verse: and to the church that meets in your home. It is also to our dear friend Apphia and to Archippus, who serves the Lord as we do.
translation_verse: Ti laa m‑puusiri i pɔ'a Siisita Afiiya, ni i bii Akyipusi, ni Yisa nyaandɔliba ban la'amni i yiri puuni la. Akyipusi gba nyɛ la ŋɔn moori n‑dɔla Yisa suŋŋa.

english_verse: I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Philemon's Love and Faith
translation_verse: Ti Dugma Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa suŋŋma i ka ti i sufumaasim.

english_verse: Philemon, each time I mention you in my prayers, I thank God.
translation_verse: Tɔ: m mabii Failiimɔn, man ŋɔn susiri wunsusigu sahanseaa zaa, n yi puusiri Naawunni pam i zugu.

english_verse: I hear about your faith in our Lord Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
translation_verse: Dama m mabiisi yɛtti ma, ni, i wuri ti Dugma Yisa pam, ka laa n‑niŋni u yɛdda, ka boori ban nyɛ Yisa nyaandɔliba zaa.

english_verse: As you share your faith with others, I pray they may come to know all the blessings Christ has given us.
translation_verse: N laa n‑susiri Naawunni, ni, u kyɛ ka nyin ni ban niŋi yɛdda ka la'am n‑gyeemni Naawunni maa zaa ti mari nangbanyinni. Lala maa nin kyɛ ka i baŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi bunsunseesi zaa n‑ti ti Kirisita zugu.

english_verse: My friend, your love has made me happy and has greatly encouraged me. It has also cheered the hearts of God's people. Paul Speaks to Philemon about Onesimus
translation_verse: Nyini Failiimɔn n nyɛ m mabisuŋŋu, ka i boori la Naawunni nyaandɔliba zaa yɛla ka kpamsiri ba sufu n‑tiri ba. Lala zugu ka man gba nya sufupeelli ni sufudoaa.

english_verse: But I would rather ask you to do it simply because of love. Yes, as someone in jail for Christ,
translation_verse: Amaa man ŋɔn wuri i la zugu ka n susiri i ni, ni, i suŋŋi ma. Ii: man Pɔla, ŋɔn nyɛ Kirisita Yisa kpambari la - halli ka pampaŋŋɔ ba gbaai ma n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpari Yisa yɛla zugu -

english_verse: Before this, he was useless to you, but now he is useful both to you and to me.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Ɔneesima yuuri maa gbinni nyɛ la ‘anfaani’, amaa u daa ka anfaani n‑ti i; ŋɔn mun yi niŋi tuuba wa, u mari la anfaani n‑tiri mani ni nyini zaa.

english_verse: I would like to keep him here with me, where he could take your place in helping me while I am here in prison for preaching the good news.
translation_verse: Halli m boori u kpalim n saani n‑tumni Naawunni tuma maa; lala ka u laa n‑zɛ i zaazii ni n‑suŋŋni ma, ban daa kpari ma lahabasuŋŋu maa zugu la.

english_verse: But I won't do anything unless you agree to it first. I want your act of kindness to come from your heart, and not be something you feel forced to do.
translation_verse: Amaa m bu boori ni n niŋi seelli, see ka nyin mammaŋŋa baŋŋi di gbinni. I yi ti baŋŋi, i nin tum din tuusi i toori sufu yuubu zugu, pa la man zaali i lala maa zugu.

english_verse: Perhaps Onesimus was taken from you for a little while so you could have him back for good,
translation_verse: Di yi pa seem ka lala zugu ka ŋɔn daa zɔ ka kyɛ i beela maa: ni, u yi ti labi na, i nin mari u halli saha din ka tɔriga.

english_verse: but not as a slave. Onesimus is much more than a slave. To me he is a dear friend, but to you he is even more, both as a person and as a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ u yi leebi la ti mabiwuttigu, u laa m‑pa daabri, ka lala veela n‑gaari zaa. U nyɛ la m mabiiya, amaa see ka nyin ni ŋɔn lɔ la mabiitiri. N‑gaari lala, ti wuri u hoo dɔsuŋŋu, ka laa n‑wuri u hoo ti Dugma biiya m‑paasi.

english_verse: If you consider me a friend because of Christ, then welcome Onesimus as you would welcome me.
translation_verse: Man ni nyin la'am n‑tumni Naawunni tuma. Di zugu deaama u hoo nyin deaai ma seem maa.

english_verse: If he has cheated you or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
translation_verse: U yi sa'am la i bunseelli kam, bee, u di la i samni, nyin yɛlima ma ka n yɔa.

english_verse: My dear friend and follower of Christ our Lord, please cheer me up by doing this for me.
translation_verse: Di zugu kpaŋŋma i maŋŋa n‑kyɛ ka n nya anfaani dinŋɔ zugu: kur kyɛ ka n sufu maai, dama ti zaa nyɛ la Yisa nyaandɔliba.

english_verse: I am sure you will do all I have asked, and even more.
translation_verse: N sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri i, dama m baŋŋiya, ni, i nin wum n noori wa, halli ka tum n‑gaari man yɛtti seem wa.

english_verse: Please get a room ready for me. I hope your prayers will be answered, and I can visit you.
translation_verse: Din paasi, n susiri i ni, ni, i maalima duu siri n‑zaali ma. Dama m mari tama, ni, Naawunni nin kyɛ ka ba yiisi ma n‑ti ya na, dama ya susiri Naawunni n‑tiri ma.

english_verse: Epaphras is also here in jail for being a follower of Christ Jesus. He sends his greetings,
translation_verse: Ɛpafraasi gba puusiri i ni. Ŋɔn gba ka ba daa kpari duu, Kirisita Yisa zugu.

english_verse: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, who work together with me.
translation_verse: Maaki, Arisitaakusi, Diimasi, ni Luuki dima zaa puusiri i ni. Man ni ban n la'amni n‑tumni Yisa tuma maa.

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you!
translation_verse: Ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya piinisuma bɛ ya sufu zaa ni.

english_verse: Long ago in many ways and at many times God's prophets spoke his message to our ancestors.
translation_verse: Sahanseaa din gaari la, Naawunni daa tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ti yaadima la; u daa niŋi lala anabidima nɔya ni, dama ba niriba pam daa tɔ'asiri ba saha ba saha, ni zii baligubaligu.

english_verse: But now at last, God sent his Son to bring his message to us. God created the universe by his Son, and everything will someday belong to the Son.
translation_verse: Amaa zuna sahanseaa din baasi nyaaŋŋa wa ka u tɔ'asiri n‑tiri ti la u Bii maa noori ni. U Bii maa ka u daa gaŋŋi, ni duniya wa naari saha yi ti paai na, u nin ti leebi bunseelli kam faditta, dama ŋɔn kɔbri ni gba ka Naawunni daa naam duniya zaa.

english_verse: God's Son has all the brightness of God's own glory and is like him in every way. By his own mighty word, he holds the universe together. After the Son had washed away our sins, he sat down at the right side of the glorious God in heaven.
translation_verse: Bii maa n wulisiri Naawunni gyirima ŋɔn ŋmanni seem la; ka na mari Naawunni mammaŋŋa beebu, halli ni Naawunni ŋɔn bɛ seem la, ŋɔn gba bɛ la lala. U yɛligu kan mari yiiko pam maa ka u zaŋŋni m‑mari bunseelli kam. Ŋɔn daa ti maali soori ni daadam nya ala'akyi kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi n‑naai, u daa zinni la Naawunni Ŋɔn Galisi maa nudirigu zizii maa arizana ni.

english_verse: He had become much greater than the angels, and the name he was given is far greater than any of theirs. God's Son Is Greater than Angels
translation_verse: Dinzugu Naawunni daa zaali u la zaazititaari ni, n‑gaari malaakadima; kaman ŋɔn daa pun ti u yuuri kan gaari ba yuya seem la.

english_verse: God has never said to any of the angels, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!” Neither has God said to any of them, “I will be his Father, and he will be my Son!”
translation_verse: Naawunni daa yɛli Yisa, ni#:- “Nyini n nyɛ m biiya; Zuna n leebi i ba.” m‑paasi yaasa: Naawunni daa yɛli Yisa, ni#:- “N nin leebi i ba, Ka i mun leebi m biiya.” - U daa na mi n‑yɛli malaakasɔ lala? - Ay, halli beela!

english_verse: When God brings his first-born Son into the world, he commands all of his angels to worship him.
translation_verse: Din paasi yaasa, Naawunni ŋɔn daa ti yɛ n‑tum u bikpeema maa ni u kyɛ duniya wa ni na, u daa yɛliya, ni#:- “Ya kyɛ ka Naawunni malaakadima zaa ti gyeem u!”

english_verse: And when God speaks about the angels, he says, “I change my angels into wind and my servants into flaming fire.”
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn daa tɔ'asiri malaakadima maa yɛla maa, u daa yɛli la nŋɔa#:- “U maali u malaakadima maa la hoo pɛsiŋŋu, Ka kyɛ ka u tuumudima bɛ hoo bugum peelim.”

english_verse: But God says about his Son, “You are God, and you will rule as King forever! Your royal power brings about justice.
translation_verse: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u Bii maa yɛla muna ka u daa yɛli, ni#:- “Naawunni, i naam zizii la kpalimna, Ka nin bɛ ni halli ni saha kam. I naam zangbeaa bɛ la i nuu ni - Sɔsuŋŋu ni yɛlimaŋni naam zangbeaa!

english_verse: You loved justice and hated evil, and so I, your God, have chosen you. I appointed you and made you happier than any of your friends.”
translation_verse: Nyin wuri din tuusi Ka yaai din bu tuusi. Di zugu ka Naawunni pun diisi i naam - Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ i Dugma maa - Ka i sufupeelim gaari i taaba dinni zaa.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “In the beginning, Lord, you were the one who laid the foundation of the earth and created the heavens.
translation_verse: U naa n‑yɛli yaasa#:- “Piiligu maa ni, n Dugma, Ka nyini n daa nyeebi duniya wa; Sangbana la gba nyin, Naawunni, daa maali.

english_verse: They will all disappear and wear out like clothes, but you will last forever.
translation_verse: Anŋɔ zaa nin ti gaari, Amaa nyin kur ya kpalim m‑bɛ ni; A zaa nin ti kpi Hoo sutura ŋɔn kpiiri seem la.

english_verse: You will roll them up like a robe and change them like a garment. But you are always the same, and you will live forever.”
translation_verse: Ka i nin ti kpabi a hoo kparikurigu la, Ka a takki hoo kparigu ŋɔn takkri seem maa. Amaa nyin kur nin kpalim m‑bɛ lala ka ka takkri, I mun kur ka naari.”

english_verse: We must give our full attention to what we were told, so we won't drift away.
translation_verse: Dinzugu di kpa la talasi ka ti sufu kpalim m‑bɛ tin daa pun wum yɛlimanseelli la suŋŋa; din nin kyɛ ka bunseelli ku zaŋŋi ti m‑miligi ka kyɛ di.

english_verse: The message spoken by angels proved to be true, and all who disobeyed or rejected it were punished as they deserved.
translation_verse: Dama yɛttɔ'aseesi malaakadima ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi la daa zɛ la deede ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ŋɔnkam daa bu dɔli a, bee, n‑deaai zaaligu la, di daama siri daa nya tubdarigri din sumni u.

english_verse: So if we refuse this great way of being saved, how can we hope to escape? The Lord himself was the first to tell about it, and people who heard the message proved to us that it was true.
translation_verse: Ka dinŋɔna wula ka tirima nin toaai n‑zɔ n‑yi tubdarigri maa ni, ti ba'a yi ka tiliginsim din galisi wa ni? Ti Dugma mammaŋŋa n daa daŋŋi m‑mooli tiliginsim wa yɛla, ka baseeba ban daa wum lala daa wulisi ti, ni, di siri nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: God himself showed that his message was true by working all kinds of powerful miracles and wonders. He also gave his Holy Spirit to anyone he chose to. The One Who Leads Us To Be Saved
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni gba daa zaŋŋi makkri tuma ni alahazibu tuntumsa n‑di lala sɛhara, ni alahazibu tuma baligu pam, ka daa tɔri Sii Kasi piini la, hoo u yuubu ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: We know that God did not put the future world under the power of angels.
translation_verse: Pa malaakadima ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi duniyaseaa kan ti ya kyɛ na suubu fukumsi n‑niŋi ba nuusi ni: lala duniya la n nyɛ tin tɔ'asiri seaa yɛla wa.

english_verse: Somewhere in the Scriptures someone says to God, “What makes you care about us humans? Why are you concerned for weaklings such as we?
translation_verse: Amaa sɔ n daa sɔbi Naawunni yɛligu luginseaa ni, ka yɛli, ni#:- “Ti Dugma Naawunni, ninsaala nyɛ la bɔa? - Halli ka i teesiri u yɛla? Bee, daadam bii dee n‑nyɛ la bɔa? - Ka i ba'a bɛ u ni?

english_verse: You made us lower than the angels for a while. Yet you have crowned us with glory and honor.
translation_verse: I daa naam u ka u gyirima poaai malaakadima gyirima, Ka di niŋi lala saha beela: Amaa ka daa kyɛ ka u mari pɛ'abu ni gyirima pam,

english_verse: And you have put everything under our power!” God has put everything under our power and has not left anything out of our power. But we still don't see it all under our control.
translation_verse: Ka zaŋŋi bunseelli kam zaa fukumsi n‑niŋi u nuusi ni.” Ni, Naawunni ‘zaŋŋni bunseelli kam zaa’ n‑niŋni ninsaala ‘fukumsi’ ni maa; tɔ - u daa bu kyɛ halli bunni yinni din wulisi, ni, ŋɔn ninsaala bu su di. Amaa di zaa yɔri, ti bu nya ka ninsaala su bunseelli kam zaa fukumsi pampaŋŋɔa.

english_verse: What we do see is Jesus, who for a little while was made lower than the angels. Because of God's gift of undeserved grace, Jesus died for everyone. And now that Jesus has suffered and died, he is crowned with glory and honor!
translation_verse: Kulla Yisa ka ti nya, ka u siri daa ‘poaai malaakadima’ saha beela, ni u ti di wahala n‑kpi. Ka ti laa n‑nya u, ka u mari gyirima pampaŋŋɔa. Dama Naawunni wurilim zugu ka u daa sakki, ni, u ti baŋŋi kum toom ŋɔn nyɛ seem, n‑niŋi lala daadam kam zugu.

english_verse: Everything belongs to God, and all things were created by his power. So God did the right thing when he made Jesus perfect by suffering, as Jesus led many of God's children to be saved and to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa naam bunseelli kam, ka u mari bunseelli kam: ka mun daa yɛnni, ni, u nin zaŋŋi biisi pam ni ba ti la'am n‑nya gyirima. Di zugu di daa tuusiya ni u kyɛ ka Yisa bɛ beesikasi zaa ka ka galiŋŋu, wahala diibu puuni. Dama ŋɔn Yisa nyɛ la biisi wa toondaana ŋɔn ziiri ba n‑taasiri tiliginsim ziiya.

english_verse: Jesus and the people he makes holy all belong to the same family. This is why he isn't ashamed to call them his brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn kyɛ ka niriba leebi la Naawunni niriba m‑bɛ kasi, ŋɔn ni ŋɔn suŋŋni seeba lala maa - ba zaa la'am m‑mari ba dɔ'am yinni. Di zugu ka Yisa bu zɔri vi ni u booli ba, ni, u mabiisi.

english_verse: He even said to God, “I will tell them your name and sing your praises when they come together to worship.”
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn daa yɛli n‑ti Naawunni seem nŋɔa#: “N nin mooli i yuuri maa n‑ti m mabiisi maa; Ka yuum yuuma m‑pɛ'ai i, ti la'aŋŋu puuni.”

english_verse: He also said, “I will trust God.” Then he said, “Here I am with the children God has given me.”
translation_verse: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli, “N nin zaŋŋi n yɛdda zaa n‑niŋi u ni.” Ka laa n‑yɛli yaasa, ni, “Nnya ma! - Man ni biseeba nyin zaŋŋi n‑ti ma la.”

english_verse: We are people of flesh and blood. This is why Jesus became one of us. He died to destroy the devil, who had power over death.
translation_verse: Ka biisi maa ŋɔn nyɛ saalidima m‑mari ningbiŋŋu ni ziim la zugu, ŋɔn Yisa gba daa leebi ba taabu, ka ŋɔn ni ban la'am m‑mari ningbiŋŋu yinni. U daa niŋi la lala ni u kum zugu ti kyɛ ka u sa'am Sintaani ŋɔn mari kum yiiko la.

english_verse: But he also died to rescue all of us who live each day in fear of dying.
translation_verse: Ka lala ka u daa yiisi daaba maa m‑basi - baseeba ban daa kpalim m‑bɛ daabtiri ni ba beebu zaa, kum dabeem zugu la.

english_verse: Jesus clearly did not come to help angels, but he did come to help Abraham's descendants.
translation_verse: Di pa la malaakadima maa zugu ka u daa niŋi lala, ni u ti suŋŋi bana, amaa Ibrahim ya'ansi ka u daa suŋŋi.

english_verse: And now that Jesus has suffered and was tempted, he can help anyone else who is tempted.
translation_verse: Ka ŋɔn mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa di wahala u zamsigu nyaabu puuni zugu, u nin toaai n‑suŋŋi ninvugseeba ban nyari zamsigu pampaŋŋɔa.

english_verse: Jesus was faithful to God, who appointed him, just as Moses was faithful in serving all God's people.
translation_verse: U daa zaani yummu n‑ti Ŋɔn yiisi u n‑zaali maa, kaman Musa gba ŋɔn daa zaani yummu u Naawunni gyeeŋŋu duu puuni tuma ni seem la.

english_verse: But Jesus deserves more honor than Moses, just as the builder of a house deserves more honor than the house.
translation_verse: Amaa Masiiya nyɛ la ŋɔn sumsi ni u mari gyirima n‑gaari Musa, kaman tammeera ŋɔn mari gyirima n‑gaari yiri seaa ŋɔn mɛ seem la.

english_verse: Of course, every house is built by someone, and God is really the one who built everything.
translation_verse: Yiri kam mari la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mɛ ka, amaa Naawunni n nyɛ ŋɔn mɛ bunseelli kam zaa.

english_verse: Moses was a faithful servant and told God's people what would be said in the future.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Musa daa zaani yummu hoo kpambari Naawunni yiri maa zaa ni, bee, hoo wuddaana, ni u ti niŋi sɛhara zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑tɔ'asi yɛliseesi la.

english_verse: But Christ is the Son in charge of God's people. And we are those people, if we keep on being brave and don't lose hope. A Rest for God's People
translation_verse: Yisa Masiiya mun bɛ la Naawunni yiri ni hoo yiddaana biiya, ka tirima muna - ti yi ti mari sufukpeemni ni nimbirimoaa - tirima mun nyɛ la hoo yiri dima maa, see ka ti laa n‑niŋi tin mari tamaseaa maa yɛdda, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi di baasigu.

english_verse: It is just as the Holy Spirit says, “If you hear God's voice today,
translation_verse: Dizugu ka Naawunni Sii Vuusim Kasi teaari n‑yɛtta#:- “Ya yi wum Naawunni kukɔri zuna dabsiri wa,

english_verse: don't be stubborn! Don't rebel like those people who were tested in the desert. *
translation_verse: Ya miri ka ya mari tuppuwumni, Kaman ya Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa mari seem, n‑nya galinsi yoo maa ni sahanseaa la, Yan daa zamsi ma la zugu.

english_verse: For forty years your ancestors tested God and saw the things he did.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka ya yaadima daa zamsi ma, Halli ni ba daa kur nya man Naawunni tuma maa zaa yɔri -

english_verse: “Then God got tired of them and said, ‘You people never show good sense, and you don't understand what I want you to do.’
translation_verse: N tuma ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem yuumpisinaasi puuni la. Di zugu n daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi ba zamaani maa ni Halli nti yɛli ba yɛla, ni, ‘Ba sufu teesa ni ka ba kur burigri ni, Ka za'asiri n sɔya dɔligu!’

english_verse: God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ”
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka n sufu daa yisigi, Ka n daa pɔ noori n‑yɛli#: ‘Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya man saani!’ ”

english_verse: My friends, watch out! Don't let evil thoughts or doubts make any of you turn from the living God.
translation_verse: Tɔ: m mabiisi, yan di kyɛ ka ya sɔ mari teesibeeri u sufu ni an birisira ka za'asiri yɛdda niŋbu; lala nin kyɛ ka u yi Naawunni nyaaŋŋa - Naawunni sɔ ŋɔn nyɛ Nyɔvuri Daana.

english_verse: You must encourage one another each day. And you must keep on while there is still a time that can be called “today.” If you don't, then sin may fool some of you and make you stubborn.
translation_verse: Amaa sahanseaa Naawunni Sii Kasi ŋɔn boonni, ni, ‘zuna dabsiri wa’ la, ya kpamsima taaba sufu dabsiri kam, ka di kyɛ ka ya sɔsɔ mari tuppuwumni ka dɔli tumbeeri yɔmsigu yɛla. -

english_verse: We were sure about Christ when we first became his people. So let's hold tightly to our faith until the end.
translation_verse: Dama ti yi ti kpalim m‑mari tin daa daŋŋi m‑mari tamaseaa la halli nti paai seelikam baasigu maa, lala nin kyɛ ka ti nyɛ la baseeba ban la'am m‑bɛ Masiiya saani, ka ti zaa teesa nyɛ yummu. -

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn like those who rebelled.”
translation_verse: Hoo tin na pun yɛli seem la, ni#:- “Ya yi wum Naawunni kukɔri zuna dabsiri wa, Ya miri ka ya mari tuppuwumni, Kaman ya Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa mari seem, ”

english_verse: Who were those people that heard God's voice and rebelled? Weren't they the same ones that came out of Egypt with Moses?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka bandima n‑lee n‑nyɛ ban ‘n daa wum Naawunni kukɔri’ maa ka daa ‘mari tuppuwumni’? - Pa Musa ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi ninvugseeba n‑yi Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni na zaa la nla?

english_verse: And who did God say would never enter his place of rest? Weren't they the ones that disobeyed him?
translation_verse: Ka bandima ka Naawunni daa pɔ noori n‑yɛli ba, ni, ‘Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya man saani.’? - Pa ninvugseeba ban daa mari tuppuwumni maa nla?

english_verse: We see that those people did not enter the place of rest because they did not have faith.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dizugu ti nya ka di nyɛ la ba yɛdda za'asigu zugu ka ba daa bu kpɛ lala vuusim zii maa ni.

english_verse: The promise to enter the place of rest is still good, and we must take care that none of you miss out.
translation_verse: Dizugu vuusim ziseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi ka nangbambasiri maa, lala nangbambasiri maa na kpalim n‑zɛya: dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ti mari ti mansi ka ti sɔ ku kɔŋŋi vuusim zii maa paari na.

english_verse: We have heard the message, just as they did. But they failed to believe what they heard, and the message did not do them any good.
translation_verse: Naawunni lahabasuŋŋu maa daa paai tirim gba saani na, kaman di daa paai ban saani seem la. Tɔ, ban daa wum lahabaseaa daa bu ku ba bukyi, dama ban ŋɔn daa wum maa, ba daa bu niŋi di yɛdda.

english_verse: Only people who have faith will enter the place of rest. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God became angry and told the people, ‘You will never enter my place of rest!’ ” God said this, even though everything has been ready from the time of creation.
translation_verse: Amaa tirima ban pun niŋi yɛdda la kpɛ vuusim zii maa Naawunni saani. Lala zugu ka u yɛliya, ni#:- “Lala zugu ka n sufu daa yisigi, Ka n daa pɔ noori n‑yɛli#: ‘Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya n saani.’ ” Naawunni n daa yɛli lala, halli ŋɔn daa pun naam duniya wa, ka naai u tuma maa.

english_verse: In fact, somewhere the Scriptures say that by the seventh day, God had finished his work, and so he rested.
translation_verse: Dama dabsaayɔpɔi daari yɛla ka Naawunni pun yɛli, Litaafi puuni ya'aseaa, ni, Naawunni daa vuusi u tuma zaa puuni dabsaayɔpɔi daari.

english_verse: We also read that he later said, “You people will never enter my place of rest!”
translation_verse: Ka daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, ‘Ba ku ti nya vuusim ziiya n saani.’

english_verse: This means that the promise to enter is still good, because those who first heard about it disobeyed and did not enter.
translation_verse: Di zugu vuusim zii maa nyaabu na kpalim n‑guri ninvugseeba ni ba ti vuusi, amaa ban daa tuui n‑wum lala lahabasuŋŋu maa daa bu paai vuusim ziiya Naawunni saani, ban daa bu niŋi yɛdda zugu.

english_verse: Much later God told David to make the promise again, just as I have already said, “If you hear his voice today, don't be stubborn!”
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni daa laa n‑zaali dabsiseaa kan boonni, ni, ‘zuna dabsiri wa’; ka di daa yuui pɔi, ka u daa kyɛ ka NaaDauda daa yuum n‑yɛli - hoo tin na pun yɛli seem la - ni#:- “Ya yi wum Naawunni kukɔri zuna dabsiri wa, Ya miri ka ya mari tuppuwumni.”

english_verse: If Joshua had really given the people rest, there would not be any need for God to talk about another day of rest.
translation_verse: Gyɔsuuwa yi siri daa kyɛ ka Naawunni niriba nya vuusimaŋni, Naawunni naa n‑ku laa n‑tɔ'asi ‘vuusim dabsiri’ yɛla yaasa.

english_verse: But God has promised us a Sabbath when we will rest, even though it has not yet come.
translation_verse: Lala zugu, vuusim dabsiri na kur zɛ ni n‑guri Naawunni niriba ni ba vuusi.

english_verse: On that day God's people will rest from their work, just as God rested from his work.
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam kpɛ vuusim ni Naawunni saani pun kyɛ u tuma ka vuusira - hoo Naawunni mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa tum u tuma n‑naai ka vuusi seem la.

english_verse: We should do our best to enter the place of rest, so none of us will disobey and miss going there, as they did.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti kpaŋŋi ti maŋŋa m‑paai vuusim ziiya Naawunni saani, ka di kyɛ ka sɔsɔ dɔli la banŋɔ tuppuwumni soori nti kɔŋŋi lala vuusim maa, u yɛdda kɔŋŋsim zugu.

english_verse: God's word is alive and powerful! It is sharper than any double-edged sword. His word can cut through our spirits and souls and through our joints and marrow, until it discovers the desires and thoughts of our hearts.
translation_verse: Naawunni yɛligu mari la nyɔvuri, ka tumni ka tuma pam. Di ŋmani la suuwa, ka diri n‑gaari takoobi kan mari nɔya ayi; ka ŋmaari ni n‑wɔligri ninsaala sii ni u ningbiŋŋu ziiya, ka laa m‑paari u kɔbtuga ni kɔba kpaam. Lala ka Naawunni yɛligu siri viisiri m‑baŋŋni ninsaala sufu boorim ni u sufu ni teesa zaa ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: Nothing is hidden from God! He sees through everything, and we will have to tell him the truth. Jesus Is the Great High Priest
translation_verse: Bunseelli ka ni n‑nin toaai n‑sɔ'ai Naawunni saani, dama duniya yɛla ni buna kam zaa dɔ la u tooni, ka ti zaa nin ti vii ti beesigu m‑bulisi u.

english_verse: We have a great high priest, who has gone into heaven, and he is Jesus the Son of God. This is why we must hold on to what we have said about him.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka Naawunni Bii Yisa ŋɔn nyɛ ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana la n ya kyɛ ka ti gyi ti yɛdda tin sakki seaa maa nuusi ayi. Ti Toondaana wa n galisi, ka du arizana n‑gaari n‑kpɛa.

english_verse: So whenever we are in need, we should come bravely before the throne of our merciful God. There we will be treated with undeserved grace, and we will find help.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti niŋi sufukpeemni n‑vuui m‑pɔri Naawunni naam ziziiya, dama ŋɔna n nyɛ alibarika daana ka tisiri u piinisuma yɔri. Lala nin kyɛ ka u zɔ ti namboo. Ka laa n‑niŋi ti alibarika din nin toaai n‑suŋŋi ti sahaseaa tin boori suŋŋsim zaa.

english_verse: Every high priest is appointed to help others by offering gifts and sacrifices to God because of their sins.
translation_verse: Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana zaa nyɛ la ban yiisi sɔ u ninsaalidima ni n‑diisi, ni u ti gyeemni Naawunni ka zɔri sindaani ba tumbeeri zugu, ka maanni sara n‑kɔrigri bunkɔbri di zugu.

english_verse: A high priest has weaknesses of his own, and he feels sorry for foolish and sinful people.
translation_verse: U nin toaai m‑mari ban bu mi soori, ni ban bɔrigri, la ni sugru, dama u gba nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn ka kpeeŋŋu.

english_verse: This is why he must offer sacrifices for his own sins and for the sins of others.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka di mugsi ni u maanni la sara tumbeeri zugu n‑tiri u niriba maa, ka laa m‑maanni u maŋtoori tumbeeri zugu.

english_verse: But no one can have the honor of being a high priest simply by wanting to be one. Only God can choose a priest, and God is the one who chose Aaron.
translation_verse: Sɔsɔ bu diisiri u maŋŋa lala tuma maa: Naawunni booligu kɔkɔ n nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka nira leebi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana - hoo ŋɔn Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli Aruuna n‑diisi u seem la.

english_verse: This is how it was with Christ. He became a high priest, but not just because he wanted the honor of being one. It was God who told him, “You are my Son, because today I have become your Father!”
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka Yisa daa bu wukki u maŋŋa ni u leebi Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana. Amaa di daa nyɛ la Naawunni ŋɔn daa yɛli u, ni#:- “I nyɛ la m biiya: Zuna ka n leebi i ba.”

english_verse: In another place, God says, “You are a priest forever just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑yɛli ya'aseaa yaasa, ni#:- “I nin di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana nti bɛ ni saha kam, Ka di dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”

english_verse: God had the power to save Jesus from death. And while Jesus was on earth, he begged God with loud crying and tears to save him. He truly worshiped God, and God listened to his prayers.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Yisa daa puusiri Naawunni: u duniya wa beebu saha u daa susiri wunsusigu ni kunkuma ni nintam. U daa yɛtti, ni, Naawunni n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nin toaai n‑tuligi u kum ni. Ka Naawunni daa wum n‑ti u, dama u daa sigsi u maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni.

english_verse: Jesus is God's own Son, but still he had to suffer before he could learn what it really means to obey God.
translation_verse: U daa nyɛ la Naawunni biiya, amaa di zaa yɔri, u daa di wahala ka lala daa kyɛ ka u bamsi u Ba noori deaari.

english_verse: Suffering made Jesus perfect, and now he can save forever all who obey him.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ti naai seelikam, u daa ti leebi la tiliginsim kan ka naari daana n‑ti seeba ban sakki u yɛligu la.

english_verse: This is because God chose him to be a high priest like Melchizedek. Warning against Turning Away
translation_verse: Naawunni mun daa yiisi u, ni u ti ‘di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana’ n‑‘dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem’ la.

english_verse: Much more could be said about this subject. But it is hard to explain, and all of you are slow to understand.
translation_verse: Yɛla pam ka ti mara ni ti ti tɔ'asi n‑ti ya, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa yɛla; amaa di tɔ pam ni ti ti tɔ'asi di gbinni dama ya bu deaari yɛligu yooma.

english_verse: By now you should have been teachers, but once again you need to be taught the simplest things about what God has said. You need milk instead of solid food.
translation_verse: Di yuuya ni: di da sum ka ya nyɛ la karimbadima pampaŋŋɔa, amaa ya na boori ka sɔ laa n‑wulisi ya Naawunni yuubu yɛla piiligu, ni daŋŋni yɛttɔ'aseesi ban na pun niŋni tuuba la ŋɔn bamsiri maa. Ya boori la bisim, pa la diibu din tibsa.

english_verse: People who live on milk are like babies who don't really know what is right.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ bisim zugu na bu mi din tuusi, dama u nyɛ la bileaa.

english_verse: Solid food is for mature people who have been trained to know right from wrong.
translation_verse: Amaa diibu din tibsa nyɛ la bunkura diibu, ka ban yam baŋŋi yɛlisuma ni yɛlibeeri n‑gamsiri la dinni.

english_verse: We must try to become mature and start thinking about more than just the basic things we were taught about Christ. We shouldn't need to keep talking about why we ought to turn from deeds that bring death and why we ought to have faith in God.
translation_verse: Dizugu ti kyɛm tooni ka kyɛ ban daa piili n‑wulisi ti yɛliseesi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Yisa Masiiya yɛla maa, yɛliseesi an bu tɔ la. Ya kyɛ ka ti beebu ŋmanni la bunkura dinni ka bu laa n‑dɔli ti yɛdda piiligu yɛla, hoo ti beebu takkri, tin daa yi ti yɛliyirima sahanseaa la, bee, tin daa daŋŋi n‑niŋi Naawunni yɛdda seem la.

english_verse: And we shouldn't need to keep teaching about baptisms or about the laying on of hands or about people being raised from death and the future judgment.
translation_verse: Lala piiligu yɛla sunsukkuni bɛ la kpaaŋŋu din wulisiri tin nin peaai Naawunni koom seem, bee, ban zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑pa ninvugseeba ban niŋi tuuba la zugu seem, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi niriba ban nin ti yisigi kum ni, ka Naawunni nin ti kari ba sariyaseaa kan ku naai halli saha kan ka naari.

english_verse: Let's grow up, if God is willing.
translation_verse: Tɔ; Naawunni yi ti sakki, ti nin niŋi lala n‑kyɛ anŋɔ zaa.

english_verse: But what about people who turn away after they have already seen the light and have received the gift from heaven and have shared in the Holy Spirit? What about those who turn away after they have received the good message of God and the powers of the future world? There is no way to bring them back. What they are doing is the same as nailing the Son of God to a cross and insulting him in public!
translation_verse: Dama ya teesima ninvugseeba ban pun wum lahabaari maa yɛla ka ba pun lam m‑baŋŋi Naawunni yiri nya'asim din nyɛ seem, ka yi leebi la seeba ban la'am n‑deaai Vuusim Kasi maa Naawunni saani. Ba laa m‑baŋŋi Naawunni yɛligu maa nya'asim suŋŋa, ni yiikoseesi an yi la sahaseaa kan kyɛnni na ni gba. Lala niriba yi ti mi neesim yɛla wa zaa pɔi ka naayi za'asi Naawunni yɛdda m‑basi, soori ka ni ni ba nin toaai m‑maali n‑niŋi tuuba yaasa. Ban za'asiri lala maa, ba laa m‑maali n‑kpaasiri Naawunni Bii n‑tabinni dɔpulli zugu yaasa, ka diisiri u vi paalu ni.

english_verse: A field is useful to farmers, if there is enough rain to make good crops grow. In fact, God will bless such a field.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - saa yi miiri tingbanseaa zugu saha kam, ka ba koori ka ka ba bumbula niŋni suŋŋa n‑tiri kukpaariba maa, lala tingbanni nyari la kasuma Naawunni saani.

english_verse: But land that produces only thornbushes is worthless. It is likely to fall under God's curse, and in the end it will be set on fire.
translation_verse: Amaa puseaa kan wali goosi bee wubligu an ka fa'ari, lala puu ka Naawunni kabeeri bɛ ka ni, ka ka baasigu nyɛ la: bugum di ka.

english_verse: My friends, we are talking this way. But we are sure that you are doing those really good things people do when they are being saved.
translation_verse: Ti tɔ'asiri lala zaa yɔri, ti mabiisi, dama ti mi suŋŋa, ni, di pa lala ya saani; amaa yarima n niŋni yɛdda ni yɛliseesi an mari tiliginsim.

english_verse: God is always fair. He will remember how you helped his people in the past and how you are still helping them. You belong to God, and he won't forget the love you have shown his people.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni ka putoo din nin kyɛ ka u nini yɔ'ali ya tuma ni ya wurilim ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ni, ya daa gyeemni Naawunni niriba ka suŋŋni ba, halli ka ya na kpalim n‑suŋŋni ba yaasa.

english_verse: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
translation_verse: Ti boori ni ya zaa yinniyinni nin kpalim n‑tumni pupeelim tuma lala ni nimbirimoaa, ka laa m‑baŋŋi tamaseaa tin mara, ka niŋi yɛdda zaŋŋi n‑wa saha naari.

english_verse: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
translation_verse: Ya yi ti niŋi lala, ya ku tumni gbinnya'ari. Ay! - ti bu boori lala. Amaa ya nin dɔli ninvugsuma soori, ninvugseeba ban daa niŋi yɛdda ni sugru, halli nti di faariseaa Naawunni ŋɔn pun pɔ noori, ni, u nin zaŋŋi n‑ti ti la.

english_verse: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
translation_verse: Ka Ibrahim ŋɔn daa niŋi sugru seem maa n daa kyɛ ka u naayi di nangbampɔri yɛla maa.

english_verse: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ninsaalidima yi ti ŋmɛ nangbankpeemni, ba ninvugsɔ yi ti ya pɔ noori, bunseelli kan gaari u ka u nin ti pɔ n‑wulisi u yɛdda, ka lala ka yɛttɔ'a maa naaya.

english_verse: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna yɛla ayi wa m bɛ ni, seesi an ku toaai n‑takki, ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi a n‑wulisi ti, yɛliseesi Naawunni ku ma'ai ziri a zugu; lala nin kyɛ ka ti mari sufukpeemni ti tama puuni. Dama ti pun zɔ n‑kyɛ duniya yɛlibeeri maa nti deaai tama zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi bunseesi Naawunni ŋɔn zaŋŋi n‑zaali ti tooni la.

english_verse: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
translation_verse: Lala Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka wa daa nyɛ la Salɛm tiŋŋa naaba, ka laa n‑gyeemni Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa, ka maanni u maaligu m‑paasi. Ka Ibrahim ŋɔn daa tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi nadima maa n‑naai ka kunna, ŋɔn Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa yi na n‑tuusi u soori ka daa susi Naawunni bunsuma n‑ti Ibrahim.

english_verse: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
translation_verse: Din saha ka Ibrahim daa yiisi bunni pii kam zaa puuni bunyinni zakka n‑ti u. Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka mun yuuri gbinni nyɛ la buyi: u mammaŋŋa yuuri gbinni nyɛ la ‘Yɛlimaŋni Naaba’, amaa ba yɛnni m‑paasi, ni, u nyɛ la Salɛm Naaba, ka di gbinni nyɛ la ‘Sufudoaa Naaba’.

english_verse: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
translation_verse: Luginseaa ka ni n‑wulisi, ni, ŋɔn Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa mari u ba, bee u ma, bee u yaadima. Bunseelli mun ka ni n‑wulisi u dɔ'am bee u kum yɛla. Dinzugu u ŋmani la Naawunni Bii maa. U kur ya kpalim n‑gyeemni Naawunni ka maanni sara halli ni saha din ka tariga.

english_verse: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
translation_verse: Ya kur gɔsim n‑nya ŋɔn daa nyɛ ninvugtitaari seem! Halli ti yaaba Ibrahim daa zaŋŋi ŋɔn daa deaai bunseelli kam zaa tɔppu la ni n‑yiisi zakka n‑ti Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka.

english_verse: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
translation_verse: Tɔ; zaaligu wulisiya, ni, Liivai za'anɔri biisi kɔkɔ nin toaai n‑leebi maaligu maanniba. Ban kɔkɔ ka zaaligu wulisi, ni, ba deaari zakka Izraɛli dima saani. Izraɛli dima mun nyɛ ba mabiisi, amaa ba yiisiri zakka n‑ti ba, halli ni ba zaa yaaba n nyɛ Ibrahim maa yɔri.

english_verse: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
translation_verse: Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa bu yi Liivai za'anɔri ni na, amaa u daa deaai zakka Ibrahim saani. Ibrahim mun nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn daa deaai Naawunni nangbambasiri, amaa ŋɔn Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka daa susi Naawunni bunsuma n‑ti u.

english_verse: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
translation_verse: Di ka birisigu, ni, ŋɔn galisi n susiri Naawunni bunsuma n‑tiri ninvugsɔ ŋɔn poora.

english_verse: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
translation_verse: Liivai za'anɔri ni maalimaanniba n nyɛ seeba ban kpiira, ka ba deaari zakka. Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka mun kum lahabaari ka ni, dinŋɔna u vu. Litaafi ŋɔn yɛli seem la nla.

english_verse: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
translation_verse: Tɔ; Ibrahim ŋɔn daa yɔ zakka n‑ti Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka la, di nyɛ la kaman hoo u bii Liivai gba daa yɔa, halli ni ŋɔn ni u biisi n nyɛ ban deaari zakka.

english_verse: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
translation_verse: Dama Ibrahim ŋɔn daa tuusi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka soori ni sahanseaa la, ba daa na bu dɔ'ai u ya'aŋŋa Liivai.

english_verse: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Liivai maalimaantiri soori maa zugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi zaaligu n‑tisi u niriba Izraɛli dima. Amaa ba tuma maa yi daa toaai n‑kyɛ ka niriba zaani Naawunni tooni ka ka galimni, dinŋɔna di daa bu kpa talasi ni maalimaannisɔ kyɛ na yaasa. Maalimaanna wa nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn yi la Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya ni na, pa Aruuna za'anɔri nira.

english_verse: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
translation_verse: Dama maalimaantiri soori yi ti takki, di kpa la talasi ni zaaligu gba takki.

english_verse: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
translation_verse: Ka maalimaannisɔ hoo Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka yi daa yi paalu na, dinŋɔna tin tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'aseelli maa nin yi kasi ka sɔkam baŋŋi.

english_verse: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
translation_verse: Pa ninsaala zaaligu zugu ka lala maalimaanna daa di u maalimaantiri, bee, u yaadima za'anɔri tuŋŋu zugu, amaa nyɔvuri seaa kan ka naari la yiiko zugu.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Dama Litaafi yɛliya, ni#:- “I nin di Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana nti bɛ ni saha kam, Ka di dɔlisi Mɛlikyizɛdɛkka zuliya soori ŋɔn nyɛ seem.”

english_verse: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
translation_verse: Zaalikurigu la daa ka yaa, ka ku toaai n‑ku ninsaala bukyi. Lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka m‑basi.

english_verse: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
translation_verse: Bɔ zugu? - Musa zaaligu maa ŋɔn daa na ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka bunseelli leebi kasi ka ka galimni maa zugu. Dinzugu ka Naawunni daa zaŋŋi tamapaalli n‑zaali zaalikurigu zaani maa ni. Lala tama maa n ya kyɛ ka ti paari Naawunni saani.

english_verse: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
translation_verse: Amaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa ya diisi Yisa maalimaanna, u daa daŋŋi m‑pɔ pɔri. Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Ti Dugma n pɔ pɔri wa, Ni, ‘I ya nyɛ la maalimaanna Sahaseaa din ka tɔriga.’ U ku laa n‑takki u sufu yaasa.”

english_verse: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
translation_verse: Naawunni ŋɔn daa pɔ pɔseaa wa zugu ka ti baŋŋi, ni, Yisa ŋɔn zaali nangbambasipaaliseaa la gaari zaalikurigu la.

english_verse: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
translation_verse: Din paasi nŋɔa: maalimaanniba wa pam n daa bɛ ni, dama ba daa kpiiri ni. Sɔ daa yi naai la u tuma ka sɔ daa deaai u.

english_verse: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
translation_verse: Yisa mun bɛ la saha din ka tɔriga. Dizugu u pa la sɔ ŋɔn bakki ka sɔ zaani u zaanni ni m‑maali sara.

english_verse: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka u mari yiiko ka di nin toaai n‑tuligi bankam paai u ka u zi ba n‑kyaŋŋi Naawunni saani la, pampaŋŋɔ ni saha zaa. U vu la saha din ka naari n‑zɔri Naawunni sindaani ba zugu.

english_verse: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
translation_verse: Yisa ŋɔn yi leebi ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana wa - tɔ! lala kɔkɔ ka ti nin ku ti bukaata zaa. U nyɛ la kasi. U ka taaliseaa bee ala'akyi. U beebu ni ka Naawunni wɔligi u n‑zaali u kɔkɔ n‑kyɛ tumbeeri dima. Halli gba u daa duusi u saazugu n‑yakki sangbana zugu.

english_verse: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
translation_verse: U bu ŋmani la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima wa. Dama dabsiri kam ba yi daŋŋni m‑maanni la sara ba mammaŋŋa ala'akyi zugu, ka naayi maali niriba zugu. Amaa Yisa daa maali sara yinni, u daa maali yimmu n‑ti ninsaalikam, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑tisi la.

english_verse: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_verse: Musa zaaligu puuni ba daa gaŋŋni la ninsaala dima, baseeba gbarigi ka mari galimni, n‑diisiri ba Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima. Amaa di nyaaŋŋa Naawunni daa pɔ la pɔri ka diisi u Bii maa. Ŋɔna n ka galimni saha din ka baasigu.

english_verse: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
translation_verse: Tin tɔ'asiri seem maa yɛlikpanni ka n ya n‑yɛli ya wa: ti mari la ti lala Gyeeŋŋu Toondaansɔa, ka u pun la'am n‑zini Naam ziiya, arizana ni.

english_verse: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
translation_verse: Ka u tumni ti Dugma ziziiya Gyeeŋŋu Duu ni, Sugri maa puuni - sugseaa ti Dugma mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa ti, ka di pa la saalisɔ ŋɔn daa ti la.

english_verse: Since all priests must offer gifts and sacrifices, Christ also needed to have something to offer.
translation_verse: Dama Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana zaa tumni nyɛ la: u zaŋŋni piini m‑maali, ka lala zugu ka di sum ka ti Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana wa gba mari u bunseelli din nin tisi.

english_verse: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: U yi da bɛ duniya wa ni, u da naa n‑ku nyɛ halli maalimaanna gba, dama maalimaanniba pun bɛ ni m‑maanni maaligu hoo zaaligu maa ŋɔn wulisi seem la.

english_verse: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
translation_verse: Ban daa mari gyeeŋŋu ziseaa kan daa kur ŋmani hoo bummaŋseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa wulisi ba la. Lala gyeeŋŋu Sugri mun bɛ la saazugu, Naawunni saani. Dama Musa ŋɔn daa ya n‑ti Sugri maa, u daa nya kpaaŋŋu, ni, “Gɔsim suŋŋa ka Sugri maa zaa ti hoo nyin daa nya seem zoori zugu la.”

english_verse: Now Christ has been appointed to serve as a priest in a much better way, and he has given us much assurance of a better agreement.
translation_verse: Yisa yi deaai la gyeeŋŋu tunseesi an gaari banŋɔ dina. Ka lala maa zugu n kyɛ ka ŋɔna n zɔri sindaani Naawunni saani ni u ti zaali nangbanyinni zaalipaalli, ka u lala zaaligu maa gba gaari ba dinni, ni ka nangbampɔri la zaa.

english_verse: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
translation_verse: Dama zaalikurigu la yi daa ka taalli, di naa n‑ku mugsi ni seaa takki.

english_verse: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
translation_verse: Naawunni mun daa nya ba taalli, ŋɔn daa yɛli ba, ni#:- “ ‘Dabsa maa kyɛnni na’ - Ti Dugma n yɛtti lala - ‘Ka n nin ti zaali nangbanyinni zaalipaalli, Ka man ni Izraɛli dima dɔli ka, Ni Gyuuda yiri dima gba.

english_verse: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
translation_verse: Ka ku ti ŋmani nangbanyinni zaaliseaa Man daa mari ni ba yaadima maa. Dabsiri seaa ka n daa gbaai ba nuu n‑yiisi ba Igyipti tingbaŋŋu na la, Lala dabsiri maa ka n daa zaali ka. Amaa ba na bu kpalim n‑niŋi ka yɛdda Ka dizugu’ - ti Dugma daa yɛliya - ‘n sufu daa ka ba ni.’ ”

english_verse: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Ni#:- “ ‘Dinŋɔ ka n nin basi noori n‑ti ba, ni, n nin suŋŋi ba dabsa an kyɛnni na:’ - lala ka ti Dugma daa yɛli - ‘Ni, “N nin zaŋŋi n zaaligu yɛla n‑sɔ'ai ba sufu ni; Di ya n‑niŋi hoo man zaŋŋi la bunni n‑sɔbi n‑niŋi a zaa ba sufu ni seem la. N nin leebi ba Naawunni, Ka ba gba leebi n niriba.”

english_verse: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Ba ku laa n‑wulisiri taaba, bee, ba yɛtti taaba, ni, “Baŋŋma ti Dugma la.”, Dama ba zaa nin ti mi ma, Ba tarimma zaŋŋi m‑paai zuguraandima.

english_verse: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
translation_verse: Dama n nin kyɛ ba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi, Ka ku teaai ba tumbeeri maa yaasa.’ ”

english_verse: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn tɔ'asiri nangbanyinni seaa, ni, ‘bumpaalli’ maa - tɔ, di wulisiya, ni, daŋdaŋ nangbanyinni la kurigiya ni, ka ku laa n‑yuui.

english_verse: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
translation_verse: Daŋdaŋ nangbanyinni zaaligu la muna - halli kan gba daa mari la kyisa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi gyeeŋŋu soori, ka laa m‑mari duniya wa ni wumpuusigu ziiya.

english_verse: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
translation_verse: Sugri wa daa mari salima teebuli, ban daa nyɔ tikubsim turaali seaa zugu; ni nangbanyinni yɛla adakka gba, ka ba zaŋŋi salima n‑tabli ka nyaaŋŋa zaa. Adakka wa puuni ka salima dukku kan mari ‘Manna’ diibu la daa bɛ ni, ni Aruuna zangbɛseaa kan daa pusi vaari la; m‑paasi kuglama, nangbanyinni yɛla ŋɔn daa sɔbi m‑pa seesi ni la - anŋɔ zaa daa bɛ la adakka maa puuni.

english_verse: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
translation_verse: Ban daa sɛ'asi lala n‑naai, di daa kyɛ ka maalimaanniba kpeeri daŋdaŋ sugri la ni, n‑tumni ba sahakam tuma.

english_verse: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
translation_verse: Amaa sugseaa kan pa abayi la puuni ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana kɔkɔ n daa naa n‑toaai n‑kpɛa; n‑kpɛ bɛni yimmu yuumni ni. Ka daa ka soori ni u kpɛ ka bu mari ziim na; ka u yiisi lala ziim n‑teaari tumbeeri yɛla - u mammaŋŋa ni niriba ŋɔn bu mi ka tum tumbɛseesi la.

english_verse: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka Vuusim Kasi maa daa wulisiya, ni, soori daa ka ni ka ba daa naa n‑dɔli n‑kpeeri ti Dugma maa saani, sahanseaa sugseesi ban daa daŋŋi n‑ti la ŋɔn daa na kpalim n‑zɛya maa.

english_verse: This also has a meaning for today. It shows we cannot make our consciences clear by offering gifts and sacrifices.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ wulisiri zuna yɛla ŋɔn bɛ seem la, ni, ba lala maaligu ni ba piini tisibu zaa ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka gyeengyeemniba maa sufu ni teesa di galimni ba.

english_verse: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
translation_verse: Amaa di wulisiri la bundira, ni bunnyuura, ni koom suubu deaari: ningbiŋŋu bunkyisira anŋɔ taabu daa zaani halli ka saha ti paai ka yɛlli kam niŋi suŋŋa n‑naai.

english_verse: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa daa kyɛ na hoo Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana, ka di nyɛ la yɛlisuma la zugu - yɛliseesi an pun bɛ ni la. Dinzugu u daa dɔlisi Sugri kan gaari suga zaa; Sugri wa pa la ban zaŋŋi nuusi n‑ti seaa - halli ka pa la duniya wa dinni, halli beela.

english_verse: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
translation_verse: Sugri wa ka u daa dɔli n‑kpɛ ti Dugma Beebu Ziiya. U mun daa bu mari la buusi bee na'abisi ziim n‑kpɛa, amaa u daa mari la u toori ziim, dama u daa pun nya mansuulinsi n‑ti niriba, ka ba ku laa n‑leebi daaba yaasa.

english_verse: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
translation_verse: Dama ba zaŋŋi buusi ziim ni nigi ziim m‑miisi n‑niŋi ninvugseeba ban sa'am kyisigu la, ka lala zugu kyɛ ka ba ningbina niŋi kasi.

english_verse: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna Yisa Kirisita ŋɔn daa ka galimni ka zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑ti Naawunni hoo ban maanni bunkɔbri seem maa, u lala maaligu gaari ban dinni. Naawunni Siiya daa sakki, ni, u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑tisi; ka u tuma wa kyɛ ka ti sufu ni teesa bu laa n‑galimni ti ti tumbeeri zugu, tunseesi an daa kuuri ti la. Ka ti naayi baŋŋi n‑gyeemni Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana.

english_verse: Christ died to rescue those who had sinned and broken the old agreement. Now he brings his chosen ones a new agreement with its guarantee of God's eternal blessings!
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu ka u leebi la nangbanyinni zaalipaalli la sindaanzɔtta, ni u ti kyɛ ka ban booli seeba la nya nangbampɔri seaa kan faari m‑basira, ka bu naara. Dama kum daa kuya, ka u daa da ba n‑ti ba mansuulinsi, ni ba ku laa n‑niŋi daaba n‑ti tumbeeri an daa bɛ daŋdaŋ nangbanyinni zaaligu la ni.

english_verse: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
translation_verse: Kum nyaaŋŋa ka ba diri faari; nira yi na bu kpi, faari diibu ku toaai m‑bɛ ni.

english_verse: Blood was also used to put the first agreement into effect.
translation_verse: Nangbanyinni zaalikurigu la gba daa bu zaani deede, di yi pa la ba daa yiisi ziim.

english_verse: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
translation_verse: Dama Musa ŋɔn daa ti yɛli niriba maa nangbanyinni zaaligu maa yɛla, hoo Litaafi maa ŋɔn wulisi seem la, u daa zaŋŋi la nigi ni buusi ziim, ni koom, ka zaŋŋi hisɔppu tivaari soo ni gaarizeaa, m‑miisi gbanseaa kan m mari zaaligu dima la, ka miisi niriba maa gba.

english_verse: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: Ka yɛtta, “Ziim wa nyɛ la ban yiisi seebu n‑kyɛ ka nangbanyinni zaalipaalli zaani - zaaliseaa Naawunni ŋɔn ti ya, ni, ya dɔla.”

english_verse: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
translation_verse: U daa laa n‑zaŋŋi ziim maa m‑miisi n‑niŋi Sugri la, ni bunseesi ka ba daa mari n‑tumni ka tuma la gba.

english_verse: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, zaaligu maa zugu yɛla pam leebi yɛlisuma ziim maa zugu, ka ziim maa yi daa bu yiisi, taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi daa ka ni Naawunni saani.

english_verse: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
translation_verse: Dama buna wa ka ba daa maali ni a ti ŋmani arizana ni buna la. Ka di daa tu ni ba mari a m‑maanni a zugu, ni ba ti kyɛ ka a nyɛ kasi. Ka dinŋɔna arizana buna mammansi boori la maaligu kan gaari anŋɔa.

english_verse: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
translation_verse: Dama Yisa ŋɔn daa kpɛ gyeeŋŋu ziiya halli n‑ti paai gyeeŋŋu nimbirimoaa zii maa, ka daa pa la ninsaalidima ŋɔn maali ya'aseaa, ka laa m‑pa ban gɔsi bunseesi an nyɛ la bummaŋa n‑zaŋŋi ba nuusi m‑maali buna an nin ti ŋmani lala. Ninsaala ŋɔn maali bunseaa la n kur wulisiri bummaŋni ŋɔn nyɛ seem. Ay! - u daa kpɛ la arizana ni, ni u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa n‑wulisi Naawunni ti zugu.
