english_verse: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
translation_verse: Di mun pa hoo Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana ŋɔn tumni seem la. Dama u kpeeri n‑kpeeri Naawunni Beebu Ziiya yuumni kam, ka kur mari ziim bun pa u toori ziim. Ay! ŋɔn Yisa bu kpeeri arizana ni n‑zaŋŋni u maŋŋa n‑yiisiri sara sahakam.

english_verse: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
translation_verse: Dama di yi daa nyɛ lala, u daa naa m‑bɛ ni n‑kpiiri n‑kpiira, duniya piiligu ni zaŋŋi m‑paai zuna. Amaa din nyɛ seem pampaŋŋɔa, u kyɛ na yimmu, sahanseaa duniya wa naari ŋɔn pɔtti na la: u kyɛ na ni u zaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑maali maaliseaa kan nin kyɛ ka Naawunni di sugru n‑ti ti, ti tumbeeri zugu.

english_verse: We die only once, and then we are judged.
translation_verse: Ninsaala ŋɔn kpiiri yummu ka sariya karibu dɔlisiri seem la,

english_verse: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_verse: lala ka Yisa zaŋŋi u maŋŋa m‑maali yummu maa. Ka u nin ti labi na yaasa: ka di pa la u kari tumbeeri sariya zugu, amaa ni u tiligi seeba ban ziya n‑guusiri u ni nimbirimoaa la.

english_verse: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
translation_verse: Izraɛli dima zaaligu maa daa bɛ la hoo ŋmansigu, ka teaari ti yɛlisuma an kyɛnni na maa. Ka pa la bunsuma la mammansi. Dama yuumni kam ka ba maanni sara m‑maanni sara, amaa ba ya n‑niŋi la wula n‑suŋŋi ninvugseeba ban kyɛnni Naawunni saani na maa? Ay! - lala ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka ba leebi hoo din sumsi ni ba bɛ seem deede.

english_verse: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - di yi pa lala, ba naa n‑ku laa m‑maanni a, dama ba sufu da naa n‑ku laa n‑galim ba tumbeeri zugu. Ba ala'akyi taalli zaa yi siri daa kpiisi m‑basi, maaligu balim kam zaa naa n‑ku bɛ ni.

english_verse: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
translation_verse: Dama nigi ni buusi ziim ku toaai n‑ti ti tumbeeri taalli kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

english_verse: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu, Yisa ŋɔn daa ya n‑kyɛ duniya wa ni na maa, u daa yɛli, ni#:- “Nyin, Naawunni, bu boori sara maaligu, I bu laa m‑boori koom tisibu: See ninsaala ningbina ka i maali n‑ti ma:

english_verse: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
translation_verse: Pa bunkɔbri ka ba maali ka bugum di a, Halli sara maaligu ala'akyi zugu gba: - Anŋɔ zaasa zugu i nini bu tigra.

english_verse: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka n yɛli#:- ‘Mani nŋɔa - ni n niŋni nyin boori seem: Ii, mani nŋɔ, m Ba Naawunni - Hoo n yɛla pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, Litaafi gbaŋŋu maa puuni ka a sɔbi.’ ”

english_verse: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
translation_verse: Tɔ: u daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni, ‘pa bunkɔbri ka ba maali ka bugum di a’, ni, ‘halli sara maaligu ala'akyi zugu gba’, ni, ‘anŋɔ zaasa zugu’ ka Naawunni ‘nini bu tigra’. Amaa anŋɔ taabu zaa dɔli la zaalikurigu soori.

english_verse: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ka u naayi yɛli, ni, ‘Mani nŋɔa - ni n niŋni nyin boori seem, m Ba Naawunni.’ Ka di gbinni nyɛ la: ni, u basi an daŋŋi tooni la ka zaŋŋi bumpaala an pa nyaaŋŋa n‑zaali a zaaziiya.

english_verse: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka dinŋɔna beoo kam zaa ka maalimaanniba kur zɛ la ba tuma tumpu ni, m‑maanni ba sahakam bunkɔbmaaligu, amaa lala maaligu tuma zaa ku toaai n‑taasi tumbeeri kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi na.

english_verse: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
translation_verse: Amaa Yisa Kirisita ŋɔna, u daa maali maaligu yinni kɔkɔ tumbeeri zaa taalli zugu: lala maaligu maa mari yiiko saha kam. U daa maali n‑naai pɔi, ka naayi labi n‑zinni Naawunni nudirigu ziziiya.

english_verse: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka u kpalim n‑ziya, halli ni Naawunni ti zaŋŋi u dindaandima zaa n‑dooli tiŋŋa ka u noora.

english_verse: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, u daa maali maaligu yinni ka tiligi seeba ban bɛ sɔsuŋŋu ni, ni ba ti paai Naawunni saani.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
translation_verse: Naawunni Vuusim Kasi la gba wulisiri ti lala. U daŋŋi n‑yɛli, ni#:-

english_verse: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
translation_verse: Ni#:- “N ku laa n‑teaai ba tumbeeri yɛla, Bee, ba ala'akyi taalli yaasa.”

english_verse: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
translation_verse: Tɔ! dinŋɔna anŋɔ zaa ka Naawunni tam m‑basi, ka dizugu sɔsɔ bu laa n‑sum ni ba maali bunkɔbmaaligu u tumbeeri taalli zugu.

english_verse: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu, m mabiisi, ti mari soori ni ti kpɛ Dukasi maa, ya'aseela din kyisiri da'ari zaasa; n‑kpɛ ka vi ka ti ni, Yisa kum wa zugu.

english_verse: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
translation_verse: Yaasa m‑paasi, ti mari la Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana, ka u su Naawunni Yiri maa yɛla.

english_verse: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
translation_verse: Dizugu, ya kyɛ ka ti pɔri na n‑kpɛ Naawunni saani. Ti yi ya kpɛa, see ka ti sufu zɛ yummu, ka ti niŋi yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni. Ya kyɛ ka ti sufu nyɛ sufuveela an bu galimni ti, dama ba pun zaŋŋi kɔsuŋŋu n‑su ti ningbina zaa, ka di ŋmani la hoo ban zaŋŋi ziim n‑niŋi ti, kaman zaalikurigu da'ari peaari soori ŋɔn daa nyɛ seem la.

english_verse: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ti mari tama wa ni nimbirimoaa, dama ti nin toaai n‑niŋi Naawunni ni u nangbampɔri la yɛdda.

english_verse: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ti sufu bɛ taaba yɛla ni, n‑suŋŋni taaba ni ti zaa kyaŋŋi tooni n‑wuri niriba ka tumni tunsuma.

english_verse: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
translation_verse: Yan di za'asi taaba la'aŋŋu, hoo seeba ŋɔn za'asiri seem la. Amaa ya kpamsiri taaba sufu. Ka niŋni anŋɔ zaa ni nimbirimoaa, dama ya nya ka ti Dugma dabsiri kyɛnni na saha bu ya n‑yuui.

english_verse: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dizugu ya bu nya? Ti yi ti mi yɛlimaŋni seaa ban pun wulisi ti pɔi, ka laa n‑kpalim n‑tumni tumbeeri, dinŋɔna bunkɔbmaaligu zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ala'akyi laa n‑ka ni n‑ti ti.

english_verse: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
translation_verse: Ay! - Din kpalim nyɛ la dabeem zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi sariya karibu din kyɛnni na, ni bugum seelli bun nin di baseeba ban kyiisiri Naawunni yɛla.

english_verse: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
translation_verse: Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa za'asi Musa zaaligu yɛla, ka niriba ayi- ata di sɛhara, ni, ba wum lala - tɔ, lala nira daa yi kpi ni di zugu, ka ba daa bu zɔ u namboo halli beela.

english_verse: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka ninvugsɔ yi ti za'asi Naawunni Biiya, bɔ n ya n‑niŋi ŋɔna? U tu ŋɔn daa kpi u zugu, ka yɛnni ni, u Tiligra ziim pa seela - halli ni lala ziim maa kyɛ ka Naawunni basi nangbanyinni nangbambasiri n‑ti u gba: ka laa n‑zeem Naawunni Sisuŋŋu.

english_verse: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
translation_verse: Dama ti mi Naawunni yɛla, ni, u yɛliya#:- “Mani n su labsigu, N nin labsi n‑yɔa.” Ka yɛli m‑paasi, ni#:- “Ti Dugma nin ti kari u niriba sariya.”

english_verse: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
translation_verse: Di siri mari dabeem pam ni Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana la ti kari i sariya n‑lusi i!

english_verse: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
translation_verse: Lala saha maa ka ba daa yi namsiri ya ka zeemni ya niriba tooni; bee, ya daa yi ti sakki n‑zaŋŋi ya mansi n‑zɛ la ya taaba nyaaŋŋa, ban pun di namsi anŋɔ taabu sahanseaa la.

english_verse: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
translation_verse: Ya daa teesi dansarika dima yɛla n‑suŋŋi ba; halli niriba ban daa fa ya marigu zaa gba, di daa bu niŋi ya sufusa'aŋŋu; dama ya daa mi, ni, ya mari seelli din gaari lala ka mun ka naari.

english_verse: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
translation_verse: Dizugu yan di kyɛ ya sufukpeemni m‑basi, dama sufukpeemni nin ti ti ya yoori pam.

english_verse: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
translation_verse: See ka ya mari sugru. Lala nin kyɛ ka ya nin toaai n‑tum Naawunni ŋɔn boori seem ka naayi deaai bunsunseesi Ŋɔn daa basi noori, ni, u nin ti ti seem maa.

english_verse: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
translation_verse: Dama di sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Di kpalim la beela, Ni ŋɔn kyɛnni na maa ti kyɛ na; U bu ya n‑yuui.”

english_verse: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ka galiŋŋu n saani, U niŋni ma yɛdda, Ka dizugu u nin nya nyɔvuri. Amaa, u yi ti labi nyaaŋŋa, N sufu ku maai di daama zugu.”

english_verse: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_verse: Tirima mun pa la ninvugseeba ban labri nyaaŋŋa nti baasi yɔri, amaa ban niŋni yɛdda nti nya tiliginsim.

english_verse: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
translation_verse: Yɛdda wa taabu zugu ka ti yaadima yɛlisuma yi paalu#:-

english_verse: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka ti baŋŋi, ni, Naawunni yɛligu n daa kyɛ ka duniya naam, ka tin nya seesi wa zaa an daa ti bɛ ni maa, bunseesi an bu nyara saani ka a daa yi na.

english_verse: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka Eebli daa yiisi sara n‑ti Naawunni, ka ka anfaani gaari u beeri Kyeen sara. Ka lala daa wulisi, ni, u daa nyɛ yɛlimaŋni daana, dama Naawunni daa deaai u piini. Eebli daa kpiya, amaa yɛdda zugu ka u kpalim n‑tɔ'asiri ni.

english_verse: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka kum daa bu paai Inɔkyi, dama “Naawunni n daa zaŋŋi u, ka ba daa bu laa n‑nya u, duniya wa ni.” Niriba daa pun daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi u yɛlisuma, ni, ŋɔn daa “mi Naawunni suŋŋasuŋŋa, ka dɔli u yuubu zaa”, ka Naawunni na bu zaŋŋi u.

english_verse: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
translation_verse: Tɔ: yɛdda yi ka ni, sɔ ku toaai m‑mi Naawunni n‑dɔli u yuubu; di yi pa la nira ŋɔn kyɛ Naawunni saani na niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni bɛ ni, ka laa n‑niŋi yɛdda, ni, ŋɔn Naawunni wumni n‑tiri ban boori u ziiya.

english_verse: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka Nuhu daa sakki Naawunni kpaaŋŋu halli u nini daa na bu nya koom maa gba, ka daa kpaasi nyariŋŋu maa ka ka daa ti tiligi ŋɔn ni u yiri dima maa. Ka di zugu ba daa kari ninvugseeba ban daa bu niŋi yɛdda lala maa sariya yɛttɔ'a n‑lusi ba, ba yɛdda kaalinsi zugu. Ka ŋɔn Nuhu daa naayi di faari kan nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni yuuri kan yi yɛdda niŋbu ni na.

english_verse: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka Ibrahim daa sakki, sahaseaa Naawunni ŋɔn daa booli u n‑wulisi tingbanseaa ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑ti u, ka ka ti leebi u faari la. U daa sakki, ka daa doaai n‑kyaŋŋi, ka dinŋɔ zaa daa niŋni maa ka Ibrahim na bu mi ŋɔn wa luginseaa maa.

english_verse: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu yaasa ka u daa gɔ n‑gɔ u faari tingbaŋŋu maa puuni, hoo saana bee sɔkyɛnna: u daa gbeeri la suga ni, ka Yisahaaku ni Yaakubu gba daa goori n‑gbeeri lala, halli ni Naawunni daa la'am ba Ibrahim saani m‑basi nangbambasiri, ni, u nin ti ba faari maa.

english_verse: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Ibrahim ba'a zaa daa bɛ la tinseaa kan mari nyeebsuma ni, ka Naawunni su ka fukumsi ka laa n‑nyɛ ŋɔn meeri ka.

english_verse: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka doo yinni wa, ŋɔn daa yɛtti, ni, u kpiiri ni, ka u dɔ'am daa zoaai hoo saŋmariwuusi ŋɔn zoaai saazugu seem maa, bee, hoo teekuduuri mɔ'abisigu ŋɔn zoaari seem, nti kɔŋŋi kaaligu.

english_verse: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
translation_verse: Tɔ: banŋɔ zaa daa niŋi la yɛdda, halli ni ba kum. Ba daa bu deaai Naawunni ŋɔn daa basi noori, ni, u nin ti ba seelli la; ba daa kur nyɛ a ni ka a bɛ la wa'aligu, amaa ba sufu daa gyaari a. Ba daa sakkiya, ni, ba na bu su tingbaŋŋu maa, amaa ba nyɛ la saamma ni sɔkyɛnniba duniya wa puuni.

english_verse: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
translation_verse: Niriba ban tɔ'asiri lala maa wulisiya, ni, ba boori la ba toori tiŋŋa.

english_verse: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - ba yi daa kpalim n‑tɔ'asiri ban daa yi tingbanseaa ni na la yɛla, ba daa naa n‑nya soori n‑labi bɛni.

english_verse: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
translation_verse: Ay! - ba daa boori la tinseaa kan sɔa: saazugu tiŋŋa nla. Dizugu ka Naawunni bu nya vi ni ba ti boonni u, ni, ba Naawunni; dama u daa maanni tiŋŋa siri n‑ti ba.

english_verse: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
translation_verse: halli ni ŋɔn Yisahaaku saani ka Naawunni daa pɔ noori ni, u nyaaŋŋa ni u butiri zaa nin ti yi na.

english_verse: because he was sure that God could raise people to life. This was just like getting Isaac back from death.
translation_verse: Ibrahim mun daa yɛnni, ni, Naawunni nin toaai n‑neaai kpiima kum ni ka ba vu, ka di siri daa ŋmanni la hoo Yisahaaku daa neaai kum ni n‑labi vuya ni, ka Ibrahim daa deaai u lala.

english_verse: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka ŋɔn Yaakubu gba daa ka n‑niŋi Yisifu biisi la, ŋɔn daa ya n‑kpi sahanseaa la, ka daa danni u zangbeaa zugu ka gyeemni Naawunni.

english_verse: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka Yisifu kum saha ŋɔn daa ti pɔtta, u daa tɔ'asi Izraɛli dima maa ŋɔn nin ti yi Igyipti tingbaŋŋu ni seem, ka daa laa n‑wulisi ba ban nin ti zaŋŋi u kɔba n‑niŋi a seem.

english_verse: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka Musa ba ni u ma ŋɔn daa dɔ'ai u maa, ba daa nya ka u veela pam ka daa sɔ'ai u ka di paai gɔya ata, ba mun daa bu zɔri Naaba zaaligu maa.

english_verse: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu ka ŋɔn Musa, ŋɔn daa ti sa'ai nira maa, daa bu sakki, ni, ba boonni u, ni, NaaFirawuna bipuŋŋa biiya.

english_verse: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
translation_verse: U daa sakkiya, ni, u ti la'am n‑di namsi ni Naawunni niriba la, ka za'asi ala'akyi ŋɔn kyɛ ka beebu nya'asa seem la, halli saha beela.

english_verse: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
translation_verse: Masiiya zugu ka u daa sakki, ni, u yi di vi, di sɔa: u daa kyɛ Igyipti bunsuma, dama u daa gɔsiri la u dahinseelli yoori.

english_verse: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
translation_verse: Yɛdda daa laa n‑kyɛ ka u daa bu zɔ NaaFirawuna sufuyisigri ka daa bakki ka kyɛ Igyipti tingbaŋŋu. Dama u daa zaŋŋi u nini n‑kpa n‑gɔsi Naawunni, halli ni Naawunni ŋɔn bu nyara gba.

english_verse: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Yɛdda zugu muna ka u daa kyɛ ka ba di Yakki-n‑Gaari Kyuu la ka daa miisi ziim n‑niŋi maa, ni ŋɔn kuuri Igyipti bikpamma la di siisi Izraɛli dima maa biisi.

english_verse: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
translation_verse: U yɛdda niŋbu zugu ka TeekuZeoo koom daa nyuusi, ka Izraɛli dima maa daa sigi n‑nɔ tankuumni n‑gaari; amaa Igyipti niriba la ŋɔn daa ma'asi n‑sigi teeku maa ni, koom daa di ba.

english_verse: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
translation_verse: Niriba ŋɔn daa niŋi yɛdda zugu ka Gyɛrikoo gooma zaa daa lu: ba daa kyaŋŋi n‑gyiligi dabsa ayɔpɔi, ka a daa lu.

english_verse: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
translation_verse: Halli Reehabu, gyɛ'amiru la, daa niŋi yɛdda; ka di zugu ba daa bu ku u ban daa ka yɛdda la saani, dama ninvugseeba ban daa kyɛ na ni ba ti gɔsi tiŋŋa la daa gbɛ pɔ'a maa saani ka u daa deaai ba suŋŋa.

english_verse: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka n nin laa n‑yɛli la wula? N ka saha n nin tɔ'asi Gyidiyɔn yɛla, ni Barakku, ni Samsɔn, ni Gyɛfta, ni NaaDauda, ni Samuwɛli, ni anabidima yɛla maa.

english_verse: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
translation_verse: Ba zaa daa niŋi la Naawunni yɛdda, n‑tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi nti di naam, ba daa kari sariya ni yɛlimaŋni, ka deaai Naawunni ŋɔn daa pɔ noori, ni, u ti tisi seem la; halli n daa ti kpari gbigma nɔya,

english_verse: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
translation_verse: n‑kpiisi bugum zilima yaa; niriba ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi takoobi ni ba ti kyɛ ba, ba daa nya fa-m‑basi. Ba daa nyɛ la bumbugsa ka laa n‑leebi yaa daandima; ba daa nya kpeeŋŋu tɔppu ni, ka daa ta'ai tinzundima tɔbbiisi n‑labsi ba nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
translation_verse: Ba daa kyɛ ka seeba neaai kum ni na, ka labsi ba n‑ti ba madima, bee, ba pɔ'aba. Ba mun daa namsi lala niriba seeba ni ba ti za'asi Naawunni, amaa ka ba daa bu sakki n‑za'asi ni ba ti yi namsigu maa ni. Ay! - Ba daa boori ni ba ti yisigi kum ni n‑nya bunsuma an gaari lala.

english_verse: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
translation_verse: Ba daa diisi baseeba vi, ka feebi ba; ba daa ba baseeba gba bansi n‑kpari dansarika duu ni.

english_verse: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
translation_verse: Ba daa lɔbi seeba kuga n‑ku, ka zaŋŋi sooyadima ni takoobidima n‑ŋmaai seeba n‑ku ba. Lala niriba seeba mun daa yɛ la peesi ni buusi gbana n‑gyinna; fara daa mari ba, ba daa ka seela; niriba daa namsiri ba ka ba daa di wahala zaa.

english_verse: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
translation_verse: Halli ni duniya wa daa bu sum ni lala niriba bɛ ka ni. Ba daa kyɛnni n‑gyinni yoo ni, ni zɔya ziisi ni, n‑gbeeri tampifeeri ni, ni vɔya an bɛ tiŋŋa.

english_verse: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
translation_verse: Banŋɔ zaa - yɛdda zugu ka ba tɔ'asi ba yɛlisuma yɛla, amaa ba sɔ daa bu nya nangbampɔri maa seela.

english_verse: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_verse: Di gbinni nyɛ la: ni, Naawunni daa pun daŋŋi n‑gbaai gbaari din nyɛ bunsuŋŋu n‑ti tirima: ni, ban ku toaai n‑nya ba bunsuma m‑maali m‑bɛ deede ba kɔkɔa, see ka tirima gba la'am ba saani n‑nya.

english_verse: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna, sɛharadima wa n zoaai, ka ba zɛya n‑gyiligi ti. Ka di zugu ya kyɛ ka ti yiisi yɛlitibsa an vili n‑tabli ti la zaasa, ni ala'akyi din kpɛ la yooma n‑goaai ti kyɛnni maa. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka ti nya sufukpeemni n‑zɔri n‑kyɛnni tooni n‑dɔli sɔseaa ban zaali n‑ti ti la.

english_verse: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
translation_verse: Ka di zugu ti wukkma ti zugu n‑kyɛ ka ti nini kpa la Yisa. Ŋɔna n nyɛ ti yɛdda yɛla piiligu, ka laa n‑nyɛ a naari gba. Ŋɔna n daa di dɔpulli kum wahala ka bu zaŋŋi di ka di nyɛ vi, dama u daa gɔsiri la sufupeelim seaa din dɔ lala wahala maa tooni la. Ka di zugu pampaŋŋɔ ka u paai la u Naawunni nudirigu zizii maa n‑ziya.

english_verse: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
translation_verse: Ya siri teaama u yɛla, ŋɔn daa mɔrigi n‑di wahalaseaa ala'akyi dima ŋɔn daa yaai u yɛla seem la. Lala nin kyɛ ka ya yɛdda ku gbarigi, bee, ya sufu n‑kyirim.

english_verse: None of you have yet been hurt in your battle against sin.
translation_verse: Yarima ŋɔn moori situusi moobu ni ya ti za'asiri ala'akyi yɛla la, ya na bu mɔ halli nti paai dansi ni kum nyaabu.

english_verse: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
translation_verse: Dama ŋɔn wuri ninvugseeba la ka ti Dugma sa'asiri maa, Ka u feebri ŋɔn zaŋŋi ninvugsɔ n‑leebi u biiya.”

english_verse: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
translation_verse: Ya deaama lala sa'asigu maa kaman di yi la ya Ba saani na: lala nin bugsi, ni, ya nyɛ la Naawunni biisi. Ka bii ŋɔni n lee m‑bɛ ni ka u ba bu sa'asiri u? Ay! - sɔ ka ni!

english_verse: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
translation_verse: Ya yi bu nya Naawunni sa'asigu kaman u biisi zaa ŋɔn nyari seem maa, dinŋɔna di bugsiya, ni, ya pa la u biisi ni yɛlimaŋni, amaa seegyɛbisi n kur nyɛ ya.

english_verse: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
translation_verse: Zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ti duniya wa beebu puuni ti badima n sa'asiri ti, ka ti tiri ba gyirima ka gyeemni ba. Amaa din gaari lala ka laa n‑kpa talasi nyɛ la: ni, ti deaai ti Ba Naawunni sa'asigu; dama u su ti siisi yɛla fukumsi, ka u nin kyɛ ka ti nya nyɔvuri.

english_verse: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
translation_verse: Ti duniya ni badima sa'asiri ti saha beela, ka di dɔli la ba sufu yuubu ŋɔn wulisiri ba seem. Naawunni mun sa'asiri ti la hoo din sumsi ti, ka di yuui m‑paai saha din ka naari; ni tin ni ŋɔn ti la'am m‑bɛ u beebu kasikasi maa ni.

english_verse: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
translation_verse: Tubdarigri saha na mari la beerim, ka ba bu nya di anfaani ŋɔn nyɛ seem; amaa ka di nyaaŋŋa ban deaai lala sa'asigu maa nin nya di nyoori, ka di nyɛ la sufudoaa ni yɛlimaŋni beesigu.

english_verse: Now stand up straight! Stop your knees from shaking
translation_verse: Dizugu ya kpamsima ya nuusi ni ya duna, dama a ka yaa.

english_verse: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
translation_verse: Ka kyɛ ka sɔgɔlima maa yi teaai m‑maali suŋŋa. Ka lala nin kyɛ ka ban nyɛ wɔbsi maa ku burigri soori ni, amaa ka laa n‑nya tippu.

english_verse: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ya kpaŋŋma ya maŋŋa ka nangbanyinni bɛ yan ni sɔkam zaa saani, ka galinsi ku bɛ ni; amaa ya la'amni m‑bɛ beebu kasi n‑tiri taaba, dama di yi pa lala sɔ ka ni n nin nya ti Dugma maa.

english_verse: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
translation_verse: Ya mari ya mansi ka di kyɛ ka sɔ kɔŋŋi Naawunni bunsuma, miri ka putoo yɛla kpɛ na ka taasi galinsi na n‑sa'amni niriba pam yɛla.

english_verse: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
translation_verse: Halli yan di kyɛ ka sɔ tumni zina, bee, u bɛ kaman Iisɔ ŋɔn ba'a daa ka Naawunni yɛttɔ'a ni, ka daa zaŋŋi u dɔ'am kpamni n‑takki sa'aŋmanni yinni la.

english_verse: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
translation_verse: Ka ya mi, ni, di nyaaŋŋa Iisɔ daa ti boori ni u ba ka kasuma n‑ti u, ka daa bu nya sɔseelli ni u ti dɔli n‑takki ŋɔn pun tum seem la, halli u daa yoolim n‑kum nintam la yɔri, soori daa ka ni.

english_verse: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka yarima bu paai la luginseelli ban nin toaai n‑siisi na, hoo Izraɛli dima ŋɔn daa paai zoori maa seem la, zɔseaa bugum ŋɔn daa dira, ka zibsim ni sangbansabla ni sanzeoo zeebu m bɛ ni,

english_verse: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
translation_verse: Ba daa bu nyaŋŋi ban daa zaali ba zaaligu seaa maa, ni, “Halli bunkɔbgu gba yi siisi zoori maa, yan lɔbma u kuga n‑ku.”

english_verse: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
translation_verse: Lala gɔsibu daa mari dabeem pam, halli ka Musa u mammaŋŋa daa ti fabli, ni, dabeem mari u halli ka u daa gyigra.

english_verse: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
translation_verse: Amaa yarima ŋɔn paai zɔseelli na nyɛ la Zayɔn zoori; ya paai la Naawunni ŋɔn nyɛ Nyɔvuri Daana la tiŋŋa na - di nyɛ la arizana ni Gyɛrusalɛm la; ya paai la malaakadima maa saani ka ba tusatusa la'amni la taaba ni sufupeelim pam;

english_verse: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
translation_verse: halli ya paai la bikpamma la'aŋŋu maa na gba, baseeba yuya sɔbi m‑bɛ arizana ni bukku maa ni. Dinŋɔna ya paai la Naawunni saani na, ka u nyɛ la sɔkam zaa sarikatta: ka naayi paai Naawunni niriba ziiya, ya'aseaa ba nin ti kpalimni m‑bɛ ni, dama ba daa dɔli u soori deede n‑naai.

english_verse: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
translation_verse: Ya paai la Yisa saani na gba, ŋɔn nyɛ sindaanzɔtta zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi nangbanyinni sɔpaalli yɛla, ka laa m‑paai u ziim zii na, dama ba zaŋŋi u ziim m‑miisi; ka di wulisiri yɛliseesi an mari anfaani n‑gaari Eebli yɛla maa.

english_verse: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka dizugu yan di za'asi ŋɔn tɔ'asiri ya yɛttɔ'a wa wumpu. Dama ban Izraɛli dima maa daa za'asi ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɛ duniya wa puuni la noori, ka daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑tiligi; ka tirima ya niŋi la wula n‑tiligi, ti yi ti za'asi ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni maa noori wumpu?

english_verse: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
translation_verse: Lala saha maa ka Naawunni kukɔri daa kyɛ ka tingbaŋŋu dam, amaa pampaŋŋɔ u basi la noori, ni, “N ku laa n‑kyɛ ka tingbaŋŋu kɔkɔ n dam, amaa n nin laa m‑paasi yummu n‑damni saazugu ni sangbana gba.”

english_verse: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ari Naawunni, dama u zaŋŋi la naam n‑ti ti, ka di ku toaai n‑dam. Ka ya laa n‑kyɛ ka ti zaŋŋi pɛ'abu din mari dabeem ni gyirima n‑gyeemni u, ka lala nin kyɛ ka u nini tigi,

english_verse: Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_verse: dama ti Naawunni siri nyɛ la ‘bugum din dira’.

english_verse: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ya kpalim n‑wuri taaba hoo mabiisi.

english_verse: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Ya deaama saamma ya yiya ni, dama lala maa taabu zugu ka seeba daa ti deaai malaakadima ka bu mi.

english_verse: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
translation_verse: Ya teaama ban kpari dansarika duu ni la yɛla, ka kyɛ ka di ŋmanni hoo yarima ni ban ka ba la'am n‑kpari; ka laa n‑teaai ban bɛ namsi ni la yɛla dama ya gba nyɛ la ninsaalidima, ka di pa seem ya gba nin di wahala.

english_verse: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka doo ni u pɔ'a beebu mari gyirima, ka ba la'am m‑bɛ ni ni yɛdda, ka di veela; di kyɛ ka ba goori hoo dɔgoora bee pɔ'agoora. Naawunni nin kari lala niriba sariya, ni zina zaa tumniba dinni, n‑lusi ba.

english_verse: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka ya sufu zaa ti bɛ ligri ni, amaa ya kyɛ ka ya nini nani yan mari bunseesi la; dama Naawunni yɛliya, ni, “N ku bakki ka kyɛ ya, n mun ku za'asi ya.”

english_verse: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
translation_verse: Dinzugu ti mari sufukpeemni ka yɛtti, ni#:- “Ti Dugma n nyɛ n suŋŋna - Dabeem ku mari ma: Bɔ ka ninsaala nin toaai n‑niŋi ma?”

english_verse: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
translation_verse: Ya teaama ya tooni dima yɛla, ban daa tɔ'asi Naawunni yɛligu n‑ti ya la. Ya teesima n‑nya ba beebu ŋɔn daa naayi niŋi seem; ka ma'asiri ba, ya yɛdda niŋbu puuni.

english_verse: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
translation_verse: Yisa Kirisita Masiiya siri kpalim n‑nyɛ ŋɔn sa nyɛ seem soosila, n‑kpalim n‑nyɛ lala zuna, ka kur nin kpalim n‑nyɛ lala sahakam.

english_verse: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
translation_verse: Yan di kyɛ ka wulisipaala balibubalibu yɔmsi ya ka ya bɔrigi. Di tuya ni Naawunni piini kpamsi ti sufu, ka pa la bundira kyisigu yɛla, an ka bukaata n‑ti ban dɔli a la.

english_verse: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
translation_verse: Dama ti mari maaligu ni ti deaari, ka ban kpalim n‑tumni Gyeeŋŋu Sugri tuma la ka soori ni ba la'am n‑di diibu wa.

english_verse: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
translation_verse: Banŋɔ kaari maa ni ka Gyeeŋŋu Toondaana maa n yi mari bunkɔbri ziim n‑kpɛ Naawunni Beebu Ziiya; ka di wulisi, ni, tumbeeri zugu maaligu nla. Amaa bunkɔbri wa muna - ba daa zaŋŋi a n‑yiisi ba gbeebu ziiya kpaŋŋa sa n‑nyoaai bugum n‑di a.

english_verse: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
translation_verse: Lala zugu ka Yisa gba daa di namsi tiŋŋa maa za'anɔri kpaŋŋa sa, ni u toori ziim kyɛ ka daadam ti leebi Naawunni niriba.

english_verse: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna ya kyɛ ka ti doaai tin gbɛ seela, n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa m‑paai u saani, ka tirima ni ŋɔna la'am n‑di vi seesi ŋɔn daa di maa.

english_verse: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
translation_verse: Dama ti ka tiŋŋa kan zɛ deede n‑yuui duniya wa ni, na'ala ti boori tinseaa kan kyɛnni na la.

english_verse: Our sacrifice is to keep offering praise to God in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ti maanni pɛ'abu sara n‑tiri Naawunni sahakam, ŋɔn Yisa yuuri zugu - ti nangbana ŋɔn pɛ'ari u yuuri seem nla.

english_verse: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
translation_verse: Yan di tam tunsuma tumpu yɛla, ka tɔtti ya marigu ni taaba, dama lala sara maaligu maari Naawunni sufu.

english_verse: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
translation_verse: Ya sakkma n‑tiri ya tooni dima, ka wumni ba noori. Ban gɔsiri ya zugu ni nimmoosi hoo ninvugseeba ban gali gɔgala, dama ba zuguraandima nin boosi ba boosa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ba tuma yɛla. Ya wumma n‑tiri ba; ka ba sufu maasa ba tuma ni, ka yɛlimugsa ka ni, dama di yi pa lala di ku suŋŋi ya.

english_verse: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
translation_verse: Ya susima Naawunni n‑ti ti. Ti niŋi yɛdda ka zilisigu ka ni, ka mari tama, ni, ti beebu zaa nin sum ni gyirima tisibu.

english_verse: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
translation_verse: Din gaari zaa, man kpamsiri ya ni, ni, ya susima Naawunni, ni, di ku yuui ka n nya soori n‑labi ya saani na.

english_verse: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
translation_verse: Naawunni nyɛ la Sufudoaa Daana: nangbanyinni zaaligu ziim zugu ka u daa yisigi ŋɔn nyɛ PɛguriZuguraana la kum ni - ŋɔn nyɛ la ti Dugma Yisa.

english_verse: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn Naawunni ti ya bunni kam zaa kan nin kyɛ ka ya niŋni u sufu yuubu la, n‑tumni tunsuma zaa. U nin bɛ ya puuni n‑tumni bunseelli kam din nin kyɛ ka u nini tigi, Yisa Kirisita yuuri zugu. Ŋɔna n su pɛ'abu saha kam. Ammi.

english_verse: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, n kpamsiri ya, ni, ya sakki n‑wumni n kpaaŋŋu wa, dama man ŋɔn sɔbi gbanseaa n‑ti ya wa kur nyɛ la bumbila fiiŋ.

english_verse: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
translation_verse: M boori ni ya baŋŋi, ni, ba basi ti mabii Timɔti la. U yi paai na pampaŋŋɔa, man ni ŋɔn nin kyɛ na nti nya ya.

english_verse: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
translation_verse: Ya puusima ya tooni dima maa, ni Naawunni niriba zaa. Italii dima maa puusiri ya na.

english_verse: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
translation_verse: Naawunni tim ya zaa u piinisuma ni alaafɛa.

english_verse: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
translation_verse: Mani, Gyeemsi, n sɔbri wa m‑puusiri ya. N sɔbri n‑tiri ya Izraɛli za'anɔya pii-ni-ayi niriba, yaseeba ban wurim m‑bɛ duniya tingbana la zaa. Man Gyeemsi mun nyɛ Naawunni ni ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita daabri la.

english_verse: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, sahaseaa zaa ya yi ti bɛ zamsigu zaa puuni, ya kyɛ ka ya kpalim m‑mari sufupeelli;

english_verse: You know you learn to endure by having your faith tested.
translation_verse: ka baŋŋi, ni, ya yɛdda zamsigu maa kyɛ ka ya nyari sufukpeemni.

english_verse: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
translation_verse: Ka ya zɛ yummu n‑kyɛ ka ya sufukpeemni maa kpaŋŋi zaa n‑naai, ka di zugu ka ya nin doaai na suŋŋa ka ku kɔŋŋi bunseelli kam zaa.

english_verse: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
translation_verse: Amaa ya sɔ yi ti kɔŋŋi yam, ŋɔn susim Naawunni ka u nin deaai. Dama Ŋɔna n tiri sɔkam ka bu za'asira.

english_verse: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
translation_verse: Amaa see ka di daama mari yɛdda n‑susira, ka di zulisira, dama ŋɔnkam zulisiri ŋmanni la mɔ'ari, pɛbsigu ŋɔn zeeri ka sahanseaa la, ka ka kɔmiisi laanni n‑kyɛnni ka labri na.

english_verse: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka ya mabitarima mari sufupeelli, dama Naawunni kyɛ ka u yuuri du.

english_verse: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
translation_verse: Ka bundaana gba mari sufupeelli, ba yi ti sigsi u n‑labsi u nyaaŋŋa, dama u nin ti kuui m‑basi kaman mɔpuum.

english_verse: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
translation_verse: Wuntaŋŋa yi pusi nti bii pam, moori maa kuuri ni ka a puum suui, ka a veelim sa'am; lala ka bundaana gba nin ti suui n‑lu u beesigu puuni.

english_verse: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ninvugsɔ ŋɔn mari sufukpeemni n‑zɛ yummu zamsigu puuni la, dama zamsigu nyaaŋŋa u nin deaai nyɔvuri piiniseaa ka ti Dugma basi nangbambasiri, ni, u nin zaŋŋi n‑ti seeba ban wuri u la.

english_verse: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
translation_verse: Sɔ yi ti nya zamsigu, ŋɔn di yɛli, ni, Naawunni n zamsiri u, dama bumbeoo ku toaai n‑zamsi Naawunni, u gba mun ku zaŋŋi bumbeoo n‑zamsi sɔ.

english_verse: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
translation_verse: Ti mammansi ningbinkɔrisi n kyɛ ka ti nyari zamsigu, sahaseaa ti ningbinkɔrisi yi ti yɔmsi ti n‑kpeesi zamsigu ni maa.

english_verse: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
translation_verse: Ningbinkɔrisi mun yi gbaai puuwa, ka dɔ'ari la ala'akyi; ka ala'akyi yi ti zoaai, ka mun dɔ'ari la kum.

english_verse: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna yan di kyɛ ka ba yɔmsi ya, m mabisuma!

english_verse: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
translation_verse: Piinisuŋŋu kan veela zaa yi la saazugu na, n‑yiiri ti Ba saani na, Sɔ ŋɔn su bunseesi an neaari saazugu zaa maa. Ŋɔn mun bu takkra, u bu ŋmanni la hoo sisiisi an ta'asira.

english_verse: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
translation_verse: Ŋɔna n daa yɛli, ni, u dɔ'ai ti ni, ni ti leebi u yɛlimaŋni yɛligu biisi, ka ti niŋi hoo u puu antaam bundira piiligu.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should be quick to listen and slow to speak or to get angry.
translation_verse: M mabisuma, ya kpasim dinŋɔa: sɔkam gbaam wumpu yoomyooma, amaa u di gbaai yɛttɔ'a yooma, bee, u di kyɛ ka u sufu yisigi yooma!

english_verse: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
translation_verse: Dama ninsaala sufuyisigri bu tiri yɛlimaŋni beesiseaa Naawunni ŋɔn boori maa.

english_verse: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya basima dɔgoorilim ni pɔ'agoorilim, ni yɛlibeeri an zoaari pam zuna wa, ka deaai yɛttɔ'aseaa ban pun buri i sufu ni la. Kana nin fa ya m‑basi.

english_verse: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
translation_verse: Ŋɔnkam wumni yɛligu maa ka bu tumni din yɛli seem maa ŋmanni la hoo dɔsɔ ŋɔn gɔsi mansɔ'asi puuni, ni u nya u nindaa,

english_verse: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
translation_verse: ka yi gɔsi ka bakki, u yi tam la yooma u nindaa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
translation_verse: Amaa dɔsɔ ŋɔn gɔsi zaalisuŋŋu bunsuma soori maa, ni sɔseaa kan tisiri mansuulinsi, ka kpalimni n‑niŋni lala - halli u bu tamni ŋɔn wum seelli maa amaa ka niŋi lala - di daama nin nya anfaani u tuma ni.

english_verse: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
translation_verse: Sɔ yi teesi, ni, u nyɛ la zaaligu sɔdɔla amaa ka bu gbaai u zulimni garigɛ suŋŋa, u yɔmsiri la u maŋŋa, ka sɔseaa ŋɔn dɔla maa ka anfaani.

english_verse: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
translation_verse: Ti Ba Naawunni ŋɔn sakki sɔseaa, ni, kana n nyɛ bunkasi ka ka lasim nŋɔa#:- ni, ti gɔsim kpibisi ni pukɔya ba wahala ni n‑suŋŋi ba, ka kyɛ ka ti duniya wa yɛla di sa'am ti.

english_verse: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya nyɛ la pɛ'abu zaa Daana, ka ti mun niŋi u yɛdda maa; dizugu yan di tibgri niriba n‑gamsiri hoo ya nini ŋɔn nya ba seem maa.

english_verse: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
translation_verse: Di pa seem bundaana yi ti piri salima nuntuuwa m‑bɔbi suŋŋa n‑kyɛ ya la'aŋŋu ni na, ka nɔndaana gba bɔbi u neenkyɛra n‑kyɛ na.

english_verse: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
translation_verse: Ya yi ti tisi ŋɔn bɔbi suŋŋa la gyirima, n‑yɛli u, “M Ba, i ku zinni zizisuŋŋu wa ni?”, ka yɛli nɔndaana la, “Zaanma kpa sa!”, bee, “Zinma tiŋŋa, n nɔba tooni!”

english_verse: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna, ya tum taalli ya ninvuggaŋŋni la zugu. Halli ya zaŋŋni ya teesibeeri n‑katti la sariya ka di ka la yɛlimaŋni. Di pa lala?

english_verse: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: N zɔsuma, ya wumma! Naawunni n daa yiisi duniya wa nɔndaandima ni ba ti niŋi u yɛdda, ka dizugu ba nyɛ la bundaandima u saani. Ba nin di faari nti su Naawunni naam beebu, hoo ŋɔn daa basi nangbambasiri n‑zaali ban wuri u la seem maa.

english_verse: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
translation_verse: Di zaa yɔri, ya na kur zeemni la nɔndaandima! Yaasa - bandima n namsiri ya ka mari ya n‑yiiri sariya kattiba tooni la? Pa bundaandima nla?

english_verse: Aren't they the ones who make fun of your Lord?
translation_verse: Ban n tɔ'asiri yɛttɔ'ayɔya an sa'amni ya Dugma yusuŋŋu wa!

english_verse: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
translation_verse: Naawunni Litaafi maa mari la Naaba zaaligu yɛla, ka di yɛliya, ni, “Wura i taraana hoo nyin wuri i mammaŋŋa seem la!” Tɔ, ya yi niŋni lala maa, di veela.

english_verse: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
translation_verse: Amaa ya yi tibgri niriba ba bɔbri zugu ya tumni la taalli, ka zaaligu maa wulisi, ni, ya galimiya.

english_verse: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
translation_verse: Dama ninvugsɔ ŋɔn dɔli zaaligudima zaa, amaa ka galim zaaligu yinni kɔkɔa, lala nira galim la zaaligu zaa.

english_verse: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
translation_verse: Dama Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, “Di tum zina!”, ka daa laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Di ku ninsaala!” Dizugu i yi bu tum zina, amaa ka i ku nira, i bu tum la taalli ka galim zaaligu?

english_verse: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
translation_verse: Sariya karibu daari, Naawunni ku ti zɔ namboo n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn daa bu zɔri u kpeaa namboo; amaa ka nambɔzɔtta nin mari sufupeelli, lala dabsiri maa daari.

english_verse: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
translation_verse: Tɔ, m mabiisi, ninvugsɔ yi ti yɛli, ni, u mari la yɛdda, ka mun bu tumni tunsuma, bɔ yɛdda nla? Lala taabu yɛdda nin toaai n‑tiligi sɔa? - Ay!

english_verse: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
translation_verse: Ya mabisɔa, doowa bee pɔ'a, yi ti yɛli, ni, u ka kparigu bee, bundirigu,

english_verse: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
translation_verse: ka ya ninvugsɔ yi ti yɛli u, ni, “Bɛ ni suŋŋa ka diri n‑tigra; waari mun di wum i!” ka bu ti u ŋɔn boori seelli maa, suŋŋsim bɔ nla?

english_verse: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
translation_verse: Tɔ: yɛdda nyɛ la lala: tunsuma yi ka yɛdda ni, ka kpi ni.

english_verse: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
translation_verse: Di yi pa seem ka ninvugsɔ ya n‑yɛli, ni, “Tɔ; nyin mari la yɛdda, ka man mun mari tunsuma. Di zaa nla!” Tɔ! - man nin kyɛ ka n tunsuma wulisi i n yɛdda maa ŋɔn nyɛ seem; ka nyin mun ya niŋi la wula n‑wulisi ma i yɛdda ŋɔn nyɛ seem, halli i yi ka tunsuma?

english_verse: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
translation_verse: Ni, i niŋi yɛdda, ni, Naawunni yinni m bɛ ni? Di veela! Amaa baŋŋma, ni, arizimbeeri gba mari lala yɛdda maa - ka di zugu ba gyigri ni, dabeem zugu.

english_verse: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
translation_verse: Nyin yam kaalinsi daana wa! Baŋŋma, ni, yɛdda, ka tunsuma ka ni, nyɛ la yɔri!

english_verse: Well, our ancestor Abraham pleased God by putting his son Isaac on the altar to sacrifice him.
translation_verse: I baŋŋiya, ni, ti Ba Ibrahim tunsuma zugu daa kyɛ ka u leebi yɛlimaŋni daana, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi u bii Yisahaaku ni u maali n‑tisi Naawunni zugu.

english_verse: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
translation_verse: Bɛni ka i nin nya yɛdda ni tunsuma ŋɔn tumni n‑dɔla taaba seem. U tunsuma zugu daa kyɛ ka u yɛdda maa daa paai di tariga.

english_verse: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
translation_verse: Litaafi maa ni ka di yɛliya, ni, “Ibrahim daa sakki n‑niŋi ti Dugma yɛdda, ka u yɛdda maa zugu daa kyɛ ka Naawunni daa yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni daana.” Lala n kyɛ ka ba booli u, ni, ‘Naawunni zɔa’.

english_verse: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? See ka tunsuma paasi ninsaala yɛdda ni, u ku toaai n‑niŋi yɛlimaŋni nira.

english_verse: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
translation_verse: Ningbiŋŋu maa yi ti wɔligi ka kyɛ siiya, ka kpi ni; ka di bɛ la lala - yɛdda yi wɔligi ka kyɛ tunsuma maa ka gba kpi ni.

english_verse: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, yan di kyɛ ka ya niriba pam ti leebi karimbadima; dama ya mi, ni, tirima ban tumni lala tuma maa - ti sariya karibu nin ti kpeem pam.

english_verse: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni ti ninsaadima zaa burigri la sɔsuŋŋu ni ka galimna: amaa ka ninvugsɔ yi bɛ ni m‑bu galim u yɛligu maa ni, lala nira nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu kasi, ka nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn nyaŋŋi m‑mari u maŋŋa.

english_verse: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? Ti yi zaŋŋi garigɛ n‑niŋi yoofu noori, ti nin nyaŋŋi u ka u sakki ti yuubu; ti nin mari u n‑tumni hoo tin boori seem.

english_verse: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, nyarinkara an bɛ teeku ni maa, a yi kur karigi pampam seem, pɛbsigu m mari a n‑kyɛnni ni kpeeŋŋu. Amaa ba mari la bumbila fiiŋ kan ŋmaligra, ka nyarintitaari wa dɔli la ninvugsɔ ŋɔn duusiri maa ŋɔn gbaari soori seem maa.

english_verse: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
translation_verse: Zulimni gba nyɛ la dinŋɔa: ka n poora pam ningbina ni, ka mari yɛla pam. M‑paasi yaasa - daari yi zoaai pam n‑dɔya, bugunsaalli yinni kɔkɔ nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka a zaa di.

english_verse: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, ninsaala zulimni ŋmanni la bugum; ka bɛ la u ningbina maa ni kaman duniya yɛlibeoo n nyɛ ka - bumbeoo halli nti yakki. Ka sa'amni la u ningbina zaa, ka kyɛ ka u bɛ wahala ni sahakam zaa, hoo bugum puuni beesigu ŋɔn nyɛ seem; halli ka Sintaani bugum ziiya yiiko ni ka zulimni moaari ka dinni.

english_verse: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
translation_verse: Bunkɔbri balibu zaa, ni niinsi balibu zaa, ni bunvuura ni koom ni bunni zaa, saala nyaŋŋi ba zaa, ka ba zaa yiiko bɛ la u nuusi ni.

english_verse: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
translation_verse: Amaa zulimni ŋɔna, saala ka ka yiiko; ka nyɛ la bumbeoo ka bu vuusira; kan mari lɔkku seebu n kuura.

english_verse: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
translation_verse: Nangbanni yinni maa ni ka yɛttɔ'asuma yiiri na, ni yɛttɔ'abeeri m‑paasi. M mabiisi, lala ka soori halli beela.

english_verse: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
translation_verse: Kɔbuliga nimbiri yinni ka kɔsuŋŋu yiiri na, ka kɔbeoo nin laa n‑yi ka ni na m‑paasi, bee? - Ay!

english_verse: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
translation_verse: M mabiisi, kinkaŋŋa nin toaai n‑wali taama? - bee, gaa nin toaai n‑wali kinkama? Ay, a ku nyaŋŋi! Ka dinŋɔna kɔbuliga ku toaai n‑yiisi kɔsuŋŋu ni kɔbeoo m‑paasi saha yinni.

english_verse: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka ŋɔni m bɛ ya sunsukkuni n nyɛ yamdaana ni baŋŋna? - Kyɛ ka di daama ti tumni tunsuma n‑sigsiri u maŋŋa, lala nin kyɛ ka ti nya u yam maa ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
translation_verse: Amaa ya yi mari nyuuri bee putoo ni zinyuu din yiiri ya sufu ni na, yan di nyuuri bara di zugu ka leebi ban kyiisiri yɛlimaŋni maa.

english_verse: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
translation_verse: Dama dinŋɔ taabu pa la yam seelli din yiiri la arizana ni na, amaa di nyɛ la duniya wa yam, din yi la daadam beesigu ni na; halli Sintaani n su di.

english_verse: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
translation_verse: Yɛlimaŋni, nyuuri zappu ni zinyuu ŋɔn bɛ seela, bɛni ka kyiisigu ni tumbeeri kam tuma gba bɛ ni.

english_verse: Why do you fight and argue with each other? Isn't it because you are full of selfish desires that fight to control your body?
translation_verse: Tɔ; Bɔzugu ka zaba ni nangbankpeemni ŋmeebu bɛ ya sunsukkuni? Pa la ya kɔribeeri zugu, ni teesibɛseesi an zabri taaba ya sufu ni?

english_verse: You want something you don't have, and you will do anything to get it. You will even kill! But you still cannot get what you want, and you won't get it by fighting and arguing. You should pray for it.
translation_verse: Ya boori m‑boori ya sufu yuubu, n‑gyɛa, ka dizugu ya kuuri niriba. Ya zabri niriba nyuuri, ka dizugu ya ŋmeeri taaba nangbankpeemni n‑tuusiri tɔppu. Amaa ya bu susiri Naawunni ka lala zugu ka ya kɔŋŋna.

english_verse: Yet even when you do pray, your prayers are not answered, because you pray just for selfish reasons.
translation_verse: Halli ya yi susiri gba, ya bu deaara, dama ya susigu bu dɔli soori; ya boori bunseesi yan nin zaŋŋi n‑di ya maŋtoori kɔrisi nya'asim.

english_verse: You people aren't faithful to God! Don't you know if you love the world, you are God's enemies? And if you decide to be a friend of the world, you make yourself an enemy of God.
translation_verse: Ya za'asiri Naawunni yɛdda niŋbu hoo pɔ'agoorli ŋɔn za'asiri u sira seem. Ammi, ya bu mi, ni, duniya wa zoori loobu nyɛ la Naawunni dimni? N yɛliya, ni, sɔkam zaa ŋɔn boori duniya wa zoori, ŋɔna n leebi Naawunni dindaana.

english_verse: Do you doubt the Scriptures that say, “God truly cares about the Spirit he has put in us”?
translation_verse: Bee, ya teesiya, ni, Naawunni Sɔppu la tɔ'asi la yɔri? Ni, Naawunni kyɛ ka u Siiya bɛ ti saani; ka u ku sakki, ni, ti za'asi u ka wuri sɔa.

english_verse: In fact, God treats us with even greater kindness, just as the Scriptures say, “God opposes everyone who is proud, but he blesses all who are humble with undeserved grace.”
translation_verse: Halli u tumni wurilim tuma n‑tiri ti n‑gaari lala gba, ka dizugu ba daa sɔbi n‑dooli, ni#:- “Naawunni za'asiri karimbaani dima, Ka u tumni la wurilim n‑suŋŋni ban sigsi ba mansi.”

english_verse: Surrender to God! Resist the devil, and he will run from you.
translation_verse: Dizugu, ya sakkma n‑ti Naawunni. Ya za'asim Sintaani ka u nin zɔ ka kyɛ ya.

english_verse: Come near to God, and he will come near to you. Clean up your lives, you sinners. Purify your hearts, you people who can't make up your mind.
translation_verse: Ya vuum m‑pɔri Naawunni saani, ka u gba nin ti pɔri ya saani na. Yaseeba ban nyɛ tumbeeri dima, ya peaam ya nuusi da'ari m‑basi; yaseeba ban nyɛ birikɔ'anu dima, ya kyɛ ka ya sufu zɛ yimmu n‑niŋi kasi.

english_verse: Be sad and sorry and weep. Stop laughing and start crying. Be gloomy instead of glad.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am. Ya kumma ka kaasira: ya takkma ya laari maa ka di leebi kunkuma, ka ya sufumaasim wa leebi sufusa'aŋŋu!

english_verse: Be humble in the Lord's presence, and he will honor you. Saying Cruel Things about Others
translation_verse: Ya sigsim ya mansi ti Dugma saani, ka u nin wukki ya saazugu.

english_verse: My friends, don't say cruel things about others! If you do, or if you condemn others, you are condemning God's Law. And if you condemn the Law, you put yourself above the Law and refuse to obey either it
translation_verse: M mabiisi, yan di tɛrisiri taaba. Nira yi tɛrisiri u taraansɔ ŋɔn nyɛ Kirisita biiya, bee, u yi kari u sariya, u tɛrisiri Naawunni zaaligu ka katti zaaligu sariya. Tɔ, ka dinŋɔna i yi ti katti zaaligu sariya, i laa m‑pa ninvugsɔ ŋɔn deaai zaaligu maa n‑tumna, amaa i leebi la di sarikatta.

english_verse: or God who gave it. God is our judge, and he can save or destroy us. What right do you have to condemn anyone? Warning against Bragging
translation_verse: Naawunni kɔkɔ n tisiri zaaligu, ka laa n‑nyɛ sariya katta. Ŋɔn kɔkɔ n nin toaai n‑tuligi nira, bee, u sa'am u yɛla. Nyin mun teesiya, ni, i nyɛ la ŋɔni, n nin toaai n‑kari i ninsaalikpeaa sariya?

english_verse: You should know better than to say, “Today or tomorrow we will go to the city. We will do business there for a year and make a lot of money!”
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ, ya wumma man ya n‑yɛli ya seelli wa: yaseeba ban yɛtti, ni, “Zuna bee beoo ti ya gɔ ni n‑kyaŋŋi tinyaakaza ni, m‑bɛ bɛni ka di paai yuumni, n‑tumni dabiiligu tuma n‑nya ligri pam.”

english_verse: What do you know about tomorrow? How can you be so sure about your life? It is nothing more than mist that appears for only a little while before it disappears.
translation_verse: Halli ya bu mi yan sa ya nya yɛttɔ'aseaa beoo gba! Ya ŋmanni la pa'ari din bɛ asuba saha; ka di ku yuui, ka di nin wurimni yooma ka sɔ ku laa n‑nya di.

english_verse: You should say, “If the Lord lets us live, we will do these things.”
translation_verse: Ay! See ka ya yɛli nŋɔa: ni, “Di yi siri nyɛ ti Dugma sufuyuubu, ti nin kpalim m‑bɛ ni n‑tumni nŋɔ, bee, nŋɔa.”

english_verse: Yet you are stupid enough to brag, and it is wrong to be so proud.
translation_verse: Amaa din bɛ seem maa, ya mari la karimbaani, ka kuunna; kuula anŋɔ zaa bu veela.

english_verse: If you don't do what you know is right, you have sinned.
translation_verse: Dizugu, ninvugsɔ yi mi din tuusi ni u tum, ka bu niŋi di, di daama tumni la tumbeoo.

english_verse: You rich people should cry and weep! Terrible things are going to happen to you.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ, ya wumma man ya n‑yɛli ya seelli wa, yaseeba ban nyɛ bundaandima maa: ya kumma ka kaasira, dama wahala kyɛnni ya zugu na.

english_verse: Your treasures have already rotted, and moths have eaten your clothes.
translation_verse: Ya marigu maa zaa poaai, ka leelisi ŋɔbi ya neema maa zaa n‑yoosi.

english_verse: Your money has rusted, and the rust will be evidence against you, as it burns your body like fire. Yet you keep on storing up wealth in these last days.
translation_verse: Ya salima ni ya anzulifa maa zaa nyamiya, ka a nyaŋŋu maa nin ti wulisi ya galiŋŋu ŋɔn bɛ seem. Di nin kpɛ ya ningbina ni n‑vuunni n‑dira hoo bugum. Ya boori la buna ni nimmoaa n‑zaanni a ni, amaa naari dabsa maa pun paai ti na.

english_verse: You refused to pay the people who worked in your fields, and now their unpaid wages are shouting out against you. The Lord All-Powerful has surely heard the cries of the workers who harvested your crops.
translation_verse: Tuntumniba daa tum ya tuma, amaa ya daa nyɛri ba; ya wumma! - ya lala tumbeeri damni ŋɔn paai saazugu seem! Ba daa la'asi ya puri antaam n‑ti ya na, ka bu deaai sɛseela: ban kumni seem n‑niŋni ya, ya baŋŋma ka ba kunkuma maa lu la ti Dugma Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa tuba ni.

english_verse: While here on earth, you have thought only of filling your own stomachs and having a good time. But now you are like fat cattle on their way to be butchered.
translation_verse: Duniya wa puuni ya daa bɛ la beesisuŋŋu ka daa diri ya sufu yuubu bundira. Ya diri n‑karigri zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ya kɔribu daari.

english_verse: You have condemned and murdered innocent people, who couldn't even fight back. Be Patient and Kind
translation_verse: Halli ya daa kari ban ka galiŋŋu sariya n‑lusi ba, ka ku ba, ka ba daa bu ta'ai ya sufu ni teesa.

english_verse: Be patient like those farmers and don't give up. The Lord will soon be here!
translation_verse: Lala zugu ya gba mari sugru ka di koaai tama, dama ti Dugma nin kyɛ na, u bu ya yuui.

english_verse: Don't grumble about each other or you will be judged, and the judge is right outside the door.
translation_verse: Dizugu yan di fabinni ni taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛttɔ'abisibisi. Ya baŋŋma, ni, ba nin kari ya sariya di zugu. Sariya Katta ni yɛlimaŋni la nin paai ti na - halli u pun bɛ la za'anɔri ziiya!

english_verse: My friends, follow the example of the prophets who spoke for the Lord. They were patient, even when they had to suffer.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, ya baŋŋma anabidima ban daa tɔ'asi ti Dugma yuuri zugu la yɛla. Ba daa zɛ yummu wahala pam puuni. Lala nin ti wulisi ya sugru ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: In fact, we praise the ones who endured the most. You remember how patient Job was and how the Lord finally helped him. The Lord did this because he is so merciful and kind.
translation_verse: Ya teaama, ni, di nyɛ la seeba ban mari ba maŋŋa maa ka ti yɛtti, ni, ba nya zugsuŋŋu Naawunni saani. Ya pun wum Ayuuba ŋɔn daa gbaai u maŋŋa n‑di sugru seem maa; ka ya mi halli, ti Dugma Naawunni ŋɔn daa naayi tum seem n‑ti u. Dama ti Dugma Naawunni maa nyɛ la wurilim zaa daana ka zɔri niriba namboo.

english_verse: My friends, above all else, don't take an oath. You must not swear by heaven or by earth or by anything else. “Yes” or “No” is all you need to say. If you say anything more, you will be condemned.
translation_verse: Di zaa nyaaŋŋa, m mabiisi, yan di poori noori: di pɔ arizana, bee, tingbaŋŋu, bee, seelli kam zaa. Ya kyɛ ka ya ‘Ii’ nyɛ la ‘Ii!’ ka ya ‘Ay!’ nyɛ la ‘Ay!’: di yi pa lala, ba nin kari ya sariya n‑lusi ya.

english_verse: If you are having trouble, you should pray. And if you are feeling good, you should sing praises.
translation_verse: Ya sɔ bɛ mugsima ni, bee? U susim Naawunni! Ya sɔ mari sufumaasim, bee? U yuumma pɛ'abu yuuma n‑ti Naawunni!

english_verse: If you are sick, ask the church leaders to come and pray for you. Ask them to put olive oil on you in the name of the Lord.
translation_verse: Ya sɔ beera? U boolim Naawunni niriba kpamma ka ba susi n‑ti u, ka niŋi kpaam n‑soosi u zugu, ti Dugma yuuri maa ni.

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray for sick people, they will get well. The Lord will heal them, and if they have sinned, he will forgive them.
translation_verse: Ka wunsusigu yi susi la ni yɛdda, di nin kyɛ ka dooru daana maa nya alaafɛa; ti Dugma nin doaai u. Halli u yi tum la tumbeeri, u nin nya kyɛ-m‑pɛŋŋi.

english_verse: If you have sinned, you should tell each other what you have done. Then you can pray for one another and be healed. The prayer of an innocent person is powerful, and it can help a lot.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya yɛlim yan tum taalli seem yɛla n‑ti taaba, ka susi Naawunni n‑ti taaba, ka di nin kyɛ ka ya nya alaafɛa. Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn ka taalli ka susi ni nimmoaa, u susigu mari yaa pam ka u nin nya.

english_verse: Elijah was just as human as we are, and for three and a half years his prayers kept the rain from falling.
translation_verse: Ilaygya daa kur nyɛ la ninsaala hoo tirima. U daa susi ni sufu yinni, ni, saa di mi, ka saa siri daa bu mi tingbaŋŋu maa ni, ka di daa niŋi yuuma ata ni purigri.

english_verse: But when he did pray for rain, it fell from the skies and made the crops grow.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑susi ka sangbana daa ti tisi saa na, ka tingbaŋŋu maa daa yiisi diibu na.

english_verse: My friends, if any followers have wandered away from the truth, you should try to lead them back.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, di yi pa seem ya sɔ nin ti bɔrigi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi yɛlimaŋni ziiya, ka u taraansɔ zi u n‑labsi soori ni na.

english_verse: If you turn sinners from the wrong way, you will save them from death, and many of their sins will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka lala nira baŋŋi dinŋɔa: ni, ŋɔnkam nyaŋŋi n‑kyɛ ka ala'akyi daana kyɛ u tumbeeri maa m‑basi, u yiisi tumbeeri daana wa kum yɛla puuni, ka sɔ'ai la tumbeeri pam taalli.

english_verse: From Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To God's people who are scattered like foreigners in Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia.
translation_verse: Gbaŋŋu wa yi la man Piita saani n‑tiri ninvugseeba ban yi ba toori tiŋŋa n‑wurigi m‑bɛ Pɔntusi tingbaŋŋu, ni Galɛseaa, ni Kappadɔsii, ni Ɛseaa, ni Bitinii tingbana m‑paasi la.

english_verse: God the Father decided to choose you as his people, and his Spirit has made you holy. You have obeyed Jesus Christ and are sprinkled with his blood. I pray that God will be kind to you and will keep on giving you peace! A Real Reason for Hope
translation_verse: Yarima ka Naawunni ti Ba daa pun baŋŋi ya yɛla, ka yiisi ya, n‑kyɛ ka ya leebi u dinni, m‑bɛ kasi u Sii Kasi zugu la, ni ya sakki n‑ti Yisa Kirisita, ka u ziim zugu ka ya nin niŋi kasi. M puusiri ya zaa suŋŋa, ni, Naawunni ti ya alaafɛ ni sufudoaa zaa.

english_verse: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. God is so good, and by raising Jesus from death, he has given us new life and a hope that lives on.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka ti pɛ'ai Naawunni, ŋɔn nyɛ ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Ba la, u namboo la zugu. Ŋɔn daa yisigi Yisa Kirisita kum ni la zugu, u daa yiisi nyɔvuri paalli na n‑ti ti. Ka lala zugu ti mari tama, ni, ti ti nya beesigu paalli ti kum nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: God has something stored up for you in heaven, where it will never decay or be ruined or disappear.
translation_verse: Lala ka ti nin di faari. Faari wa mun nyɛ la ban zaali seaa arizana ni n‑guri ti la. Ka ku toaai n‑sa'am, bee, m‑baasi, bee, n‑suui hoo moori ŋɔn suuri seem.

english_verse: You have faith in God, whose power will protect you until the last day. Then he will save you, just as he has always planned to do.
translation_verse: Dama ya yɛdda niŋbu nin kyɛ ka Naawunni yiiko mari ya, halli ka tiliginsim din pun maali siri la ti paai ya na, duniya wa naari saha.

english_verse: On that day you will be glad, even if you have to go through many hard trials for a while.
translation_verse: Di sumni ni ya mari sufupeelli di zugu, halli ni ya yi na kpalim n‑nyari namsigu ni wahala beela - ay! di bu ya yuui.

english_verse: Your faith will be like gold that has been tested in a fire. And these trials will prove that your faith is worth much more than gold that can be destroyed. They will show that you will be given praise and honor and glory when Jesus Christ returns.
translation_verse: Wahala balibubalibu wa nin kyɛ ka ya yɛdda nin nya zamsigu kaman ban yi zamsiri salima bugum ni seem la. Amaa salima ŋɔn nin ti naai, ya yɛdda mun nin zamsi n‑yi bummaŋni, ka mari anfaani pam. Di nin ti ti ya pɛ'abu, ni gyirima, ni tibsim, Yisa Kirisita yi ti yi paalu sahanseaa la.

english_verse: You have never seen Jesus, and you don't see him now. But still you love him and have faith in him, and no words can tell how glad and happy
translation_verse: Ya na bu nya u, ka wuri u; halli ni yan bu nyari u zaa yɔri ya niŋi u yɛdda, ka nyari sufupeelli pam ni sufumaasim zaa.

english_verse: you are to be saved. This is why you have faith.
translation_verse: Dama ya yi nyari la ya yɛdda nyoori, di nyɛ la ya siisi tiliginsim.

english_verse: Some prophets told how God would treat you with undeserved grace, and they searched hard to find out more about the way you would be saved.
translation_verse: Tiliginsim dinŋɔ ka anabidima daa guri la, saha an gaari la ni, ban daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asiri Naawunni ŋɔn ya n‑zaŋŋi u wurilim piini n‑ti yarima la yɛla; dizugu ba daa viisi m‑boori di gbinni suŋŋa.

english_verse: The Spirit of Christ was in them and was telling them how Christ would suffer and would then be given great honor. So they searched to find out exactly who Christ would be and when this would happen.
translation_verse: Yisa Kirisita Sii Kasi daa bɛ ba ni n‑daŋŋi n‑wulisiri ba ŋɔn Yisa Kirisita wahala yɛla - ni u gyirima din nin dɔli nyaaŋŋa na yɛla gba. Ka ba daa boori nimmoosi ni ba ti baŋŋi dinŋɔ saha, ni din ti ya n‑nyɛ seem.

english_verse: But they were told that they were serving you and not themselves. They preached to you by the power of the Holy Spirit, who was sent from heaven. And their message was only for you, even though angels would like to know more about it. Chosen To Live a Holy Life
translation_verse: Amaa di daa kasigi n‑ti ba, ni, ba bu tumni n‑tiri la ba mansi, amaa ba tumni n‑tiri la yarima. Lala zugu, pampaŋŋɔ saha wa ka ba tum Sii Kasi ka u yi arizana n‑sigi na ni u ti wulisi lahabasuŋŋu n‑ti ya - alahazibu seesi malaakadima ŋɔn guusiri ni ba nya maa.

english_verse: Behave like obedient children. Don't let your lives be controlled by your desires, as they used to be.
translation_verse: Ya n nyɛ ban deaai Naawunni noori la hoo u bisuma, ka di zugu yan di kyɛ ka kɔrisi su ya sufu yɛla, hoo din daa niŋi yan daa na zi sɛseela sahanseaa la seem maa.

english_verse: Always live as God's holy people should, because God is the one who chose you, and he is holy.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya beebu zaa puuni, see ka ya bɛ kasi hoo Naawunni ŋɔn booli ya maa ŋɔn bɛ kasi seem la.

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say, “I am the holy God, and you must be holy too.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; di sɔbi n‑dooli Naawunni Bukku ni, ni, Naawunni yɛliya, “Ya bɛ kasi dama m bɛ la kasi.”

english_verse: You say that God is your Father, but God doesn't have favorites! He judges all people by what they do. So you must honor God while you live as strangers here on earth.
translation_verse: Ya puusiri Naawunni, ni, ya Ba: ka ŋɔn Naawunni mun bɛ la sɔ ŋɔn katti sɔkam sariya hoo nira maa tuma ŋɔn nyɛ seem, ka bu deaari sɔ nini. Di yi ti bɛ lala, see ka ya zɔ dabeem, yan na bɛ duniya wa ni sahanseaa la ka na bu kuli Naawunni saani maa.

english_verse: You were rescued from the useless way of life you learned from your ancestors. But you know you were not rescued by such things as silver or gold that don't last forever.
translation_verse: Dama ya mi halli, ni, Naawunni ŋɔn daa tiligi ya n‑yiisi ya ya badima yɛttɔ'akyɔ'ama tuma maa ni, pa anzulifa ka u daa zaŋŋi m‑pa talima n‑yiisi ya, bee, salima, bee lala bunseelli zaa kan sa'amna.

english_verse: Christ was chosen even before the world was created, but because of you, he did not come until these last days.
translation_verse: Naawunni daa pun daŋŋi m‑pii u pɔi, ka naayi naam duniya wa. Halli ka ŋɔn Yisa daa ti yi paalu zɛmaana baasigu saha wa. Yarima zugu n‑daa la.

english_verse: And when he did come, it was to lead you to have faith in God, who raised him from death and honored him in a glorious way. This is why you have put your faith and hope in God.
translation_verse: Naawunni daa neaai u kum ni, ka ti u gyirima. Ŋɔnŋɔ zugu ka ya niŋni Naawunni yɛdda, ka ya kur kyɛ ka ya yɛdda niŋbu ni ya tama zaa bɛ la Naawunni saani.

english_verse: You obeyed the truth, and your souls were made pure. Now you sincerely love each other. But you must keep on loving with all your heart.
translation_verse: Ya pun dɔli la yɛlimaŋni soori, ka deaai Naawunni noori n‑leebi u dinni. Ka lala yi kyɛ ka ya siri wuri ya mabiisi ka yɔmsa ka ni. Dizugu ya na wuri ba, ya sufu ni, ni nimmoaa.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “Humans wither like grass, and their glory fades like wild flowers. Grass dries up, and flowers fall to the ground.
translation_verse: Di nyɛ la hoo din daa pun sɔbi n‑dooli seem la, ni#:- “Ninsaala ŋmanni la moori, Ba mun gyirima zaa ŋmanni la mɔpuum. Moori kpiiri ni, Ka a puum maa suui m‑basi,

english_verse: But what the Lord has said will stand forever.” Our good news to you is what the Lord has said.
translation_verse: Amaa ya Dugma yɛligu ku ti basi, Amaa din nin bɛ ni Halli ni saha din ka naari.” Lala ‘yɛligu’ maa nyɛ la lahabasunseaa ban daa moonni na n‑ti ya la.

english_verse: Stop being hateful! Quit trying to fool people, and start being sincere. Don't be jealous or say cruel things about others.
translation_verse: Ya yiisim din bu veela zaa m‑basi - yɔmsa, ni ziri, ni nyuuri, ni nangbammalisim.

english_verse: Be like newborn babies who are thirsty for the pure spiritual milk that will help you grow and be saved.
translation_verse: Ya nyɛ la hoo bileesi ka ba dɔ'ai ya Naawunni niriba maa ni. Dizugu ya kyɛ ka Naawunni Sii bisim kɔkɔ kɔm mari ya, dama buna wa n nin suŋŋi ya ka ya zoaai na n‑nya tiliginsim.

english_verse: You have already found out how good the Lord really is.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ya pun nyu bu, ka baŋŋi ti Dugma ŋɔn veela seem.

english_verse: Come to Jesus Christ. He is the living stone people have rejected, but which God has chosen and highly honored.
translation_verse: Ya vuum ya mansi m‑pɔri u. Ŋɔna n nyɛ hoo kugri, halli ni u nɛ ni maa - kugseaa niriba ŋɔn za'asi, amaa kana ka Naawunni yiisi ka u nya ka ka veela.

english_verse: And now you are living stones being used to build a spiritual house. You are also a group of holy priests, and with the help of Jesus Christ you will offer sacrifices that please God.
translation_verse: Ka yarima gba ŋmani la u kuga, ka ya gba n nyɛ seeba ban nɛa. Ba zaŋŋni ya m‑meeri Sii Kasi Gyeeŋŋu Yiri. Ka ya laa n‑nyɛ u gyeeŋŋu toondaandima la, ka mari maaligu na - bunkasiseesi ban maali n‑tiri Naawunni ka u sakki n‑deaari a la, Yisa Kirisita zugu.

english_verse: It is just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a choice and precious cornerstone. No one who has faith in this one will be disappointed.”
translation_verse: Litaafi yɛliya#:- “Gɔsim man ŋɔn yiisi sunsukka kugri n‑ti Zayɔn, Bunveelli ka n gamsi n‑tisi: Ka sɔkam ŋɔn niŋi u yɛdda muna - Ŋɔna n ku di vi, halli beela.”

english_verse: You are followers of the Lord, and this stone is precious to you. But it isn't precious to those who refuse to follow him. They are the builders who tossed aside the stone that turned out to be the most important one of all.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔ wulisiya, ni, ya yɛdda dima maa saani, di nin veeli n‑ti ya; amaa ban ka yɛdda dima maa ŋɔna, u yɛ n‑ŋmani la hoo din sɔbi seem, ni#:- “Kugseaa tammeeriba ŋɔn daa za'asi la - Kana daa leebi kan gbaai nyeebri maa, N‑gbaari ka zaa ni kpeeŋŋu.”

english_verse: But you are God's chosen and special people. You are a group of royal priests and a holy nation. God has brought you out of darkness into his marvelous light. Now you must tell all the wonderful things he has done. The Scriptures say,
