english_verse: So if I come, I will remind him of how he has been attacking us with gossip. Not only has he been doing this, but he refuses to welcome any of the Lord's followers who come by. And when other church members want to welcome them, he puts them out of the church.
translation_verse: N yi ti kyɛ ya saani na, n nin kyɛ ka u baŋŋi ma zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn niŋi seem maa zaa - halli yɛlibɛseesi ni ziriseesi ŋɔn ma'ari n‑tiri ma la. Ni lala maa zaa di na bu sakki u: u ku sakki ni u deaai ti Kirisita mabitaaba ban yi kyɛnni ya saani na. Halli seeba yi boori ni ba deaai ba, u bu sakkra, ka katti lala niriba n‑yiisiri gyeemniba la'aŋŋu maa ni.

english_verse: Dear friend, don't imitate the evil deeds of others, but follow the example of people who do kind deeds. They are God's children, but those who are always doing evil have never seen God.
translation_verse: N zɔsuŋŋu, di dɔli ban tumni din bu dɔli soori nyaaŋŋa. Ay! - see ka i dɔli ban tumni din tuusi soori maa. Dama ŋɔnkam tumni din tuusi yi la Naawunni saani na, ŋɔn mun tumni din bu tuusi maa na zi n‑nya Naawunni.

english_verse: Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the true message that he teaches. I also speak well of him, and you know what I say is true. Final Greetings
translation_verse: Sɔkam zaa tɔ'asiri Dimitiriyusi yɛla, ni, u nyɛ la ninvugsuŋŋu; yɛlimaŋni maa gba tɔ'asiri u yɛla la suŋŋa. Tirima gba zaŋŋi tin mi seem m‑paasi n‑wulisi u veelim ŋɔn bɛ seem, ka i mi, ni, tin yɛtti seem maa nyɛ la yɛlimaŋni.

english_verse: I have much more to say to you, but I don't want to write it with pen and ink.
translation_verse: Tɔ. N na mari yɛla pam ni n ya n‑yɛli n‑ti i, amaa di zaa yɔri, n bu boori n sɔbi a n‑niŋi gbaŋŋu ni n‑ti i na.

english_verse: I pray that God will bless you with peace! Your friends send their greetings. Please give a personal greeting to each of our friends.
translation_verse: Dama m mari tama, ni, di bu ya n‑yuui ka n nin kyɛ i saani na n‑kaai i, ka ti nini nya taaba ka ti di taaba azama. Alaafɛ ni sufudoaa bɛ i saani. I zɔdima m puusiri i ni. Puusima ti zɔdima zaa yinniyinni.

english_verse: From Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and the brother of James. To all who are chosen and loved by God the Father and are kept safe by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Man Gyuuda, Gyeemsi sunzɔ la, n sɔbri gbaŋŋu wa n‑tiri ya; man ŋɔn nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn tumni n‑tiri Yisa Kirisita Masiiya hoo u daabri la. Yarima ka n sɔbri gbaŋŋu maa n‑tiri ya, dama n wuri ya ti Ba Naawunni yɛla zugu, ka ba pun booli ya Naawunni saani na ka Yisa Kirisita mari ya suŋŋa.

english_verse: I pray that God will greatly bless you with mercy, peace, and love! False Teachers
translation_verse: Ya nin nya namboo Naawunni saani, ni alaafɛ ni wurilim zaa.

english_verse: My dear friends, I really wanted to write you about God's saving power at work in our lives. But instead, I must write and ask you to defend the faith that God has once for all given to his people.
translation_verse: M mabiisi, n wuri ya pam ka di zugu n da yi boori la n sɔbi gbaŋŋu n‑ti ya zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi man ni yan zaa ŋɔn la'am n‑nya tiliginsim seem maa; amaa di kpa la talasi ni n daŋŋi n‑sɔbi yɛttɔ'a n‑susiri ya, ni, ya kpaŋŋma ya mansi n‑zaŋŋi ya kpeeŋŋu zaa m‑moora, ni ya ti zɛ yɛdda yɛla maa nyaaŋŋa - yɛdda yɛliseesi Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun zaali n‑ti u niriba la.

english_verse: Some godless people have sneaked in among us and are saying, “God treats us much better than we deserve, and so it is all right to be immoral.” They even deny we must obey Jesus Christ as our only Master and Lord. But long ago the Scriptures warned that these godless people were doomed.
translation_verse: Dama ninvugseeba pun sɔ'ai n‑kpɛ ya saani na, ka za'asiri Naawunni gyeeŋŋu yɛla. Halli ba daa pun sɔbi lala niriba yɛla, ni, ba nin kari ba sariya n‑lusi ba. Ninvugbeeri wa n kur zeemni ti Dugma Naawunni piinisuma, halli nti takki Naawunni yɛla ka a leebi la da'ari tuma an bu dɔli soori; ka ba za'asiri ti Dugma Yisa Masiiya yɛla, halli ni ŋɔn kɔkɔ ŋɔn su ti yɛla zaa gba.

english_verse: You also know about the angels who didn't do their work and left their proper places. God chained them with everlasting chains and is now keeping them in dark pits until the great day of judgment.
translation_verse: Halli malaakadima maa gba - baseeba ban daa bu sakki, ni, ba ti kpalim ba maŋtoori naam fukumsi yɛla ni, ka daa kyɛ ba beebu ziiya m‑bakki la - ban ka Naawunni daa lɔ la kyɔrimseesi an ku toaai n‑lɔrigi sahakam, n‑kpari zibsim zii maa ni, halli n‑guri sariya karibu daari maa.

english_verse: We should also be warned by what happened to the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah and the nearby towns. Their people became immoral and committed all sorts of sexual sins. Then God made an example of them and punished them with eternal fire.
translation_verse: Lala gba ka Sɔdoom dima, ni Gɔmɔra dima, ni tinseesi an daa gyiligi bɛni niriba m‑paasi daa bɛ ni. Ba daa tumni la zina tuma ka dɔli la kɔrisi yɛliseesi an bu dɔli soori; ka lala zugu ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka ba nya tubdarigri saha din ka naari, bugum maa ni; halli ka ba yɛla ti leebi la wulisigu n‑kpamsiri sɔkam zaa.

english_verse: The people I am talking about are behaving just like those dreamers who destroyed their own bodies. They reject all authority and insult angels.
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka lala deede ka niriba wa zaasiri zaasibeeri, ka di zugu ba sa'amni la ba maŋtoori ningbina, ka za'asiri la fukumsi ka tuuri la gyirima daandima.

english_verse: Even Michael, the chief angel, didn't dare to insult the devil, when the two of them were arguing about the body of Moses. All Michael said was, “The Lord will punish you!”
translation_verse: Ya bu nya, halli malaakadima zuguraana maa, Maikyɛli la, daa bu tu Sintaani gba, Musa ŋɔn daa kpi ka ba daa mari nangbankpeemni n‑ti taaba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi u ningbiŋŋu yɛla. Ay! - u daa kur yɛli u, ni, “Ti Dugma nin boosi i!”

english_verse: But these people insult powers they don't know anything about. They are like senseless animals that end up getting destroyed, because they live only by their feelings.
translation_verse: Amaa niriba wa muna - ba yi bu baŋŋi bunseelli gbinni, kana ka ba tuura. Halli ba yi ti baŋŋi bunseesi, ba mari la yam hoo bunkɔbri ŋɔn mari seem m‑baŋŋi a, ka bamsim din bu tuusi wa mun kyɛ ka ba basi la yɔri.

english_verse: Now they are in for real trouble. They have followed Cain's example and have made the same mistake that Balaam did by caring only for money. They have also rebelled against God, just as Korah did. Because of all this, they will be destroyed.
translation_verse: Waai, waai, waai! Ba yɛla basi la yɔri! Dama ba dɔli la Kyeen sɔbeoo la. Ba mun sufu kpɛ Beelɛm yɔmsigu yɛla ni, ka ba nin nya tubdarigri hoo ŋɔn Beelɛm ŋɔn daa nya seem la. Halli ba za'asiri yɛlimaŋni soori hoo Koora ŋɔn daa za'asi seem, ka ba nin nya baasiyɔri hoo ŋɔna.

english_verse: These people are filthy minded, and by their shameful and selfish actions they spoil the meals you eat together. They are like clouds blown along by the wind, but never bringing any rain. They are like leafless trees, uprooted and dead, and unable to produce fruit.
translation_verse: Ya yi ti la'amni n‑diri bundirigu wurilim zugu, lala niriba kpeeri na n‑sa'amni ya yɛla, ka vi bu kpɛ ba di zugu. Ba ŋmanni la pɛguriseeba ban bu guri peesi maa, amaa ba maŋtoori yɛla ka ba gɔsira; bee, sangbana an ka saa ka pɛsiŋŋu kur zeeri a ka a kyɛnni kpa, n‑labri kpa; bee, tiseesi an bu wanni wala halli ni waligu saha ŋɔn paai maa zaa yɔri - a pun kpi napɔmbuyi, ka ba nin foaai a m‑basi.

english_verse: Their shameful deeds show up like foam on wild ocean waves. They are like wandering stars forever doomed to the darkest pits of hell.
translation_verse: Ba laa n‑ŋmanni la teeku kooma; a damni la kɔmiisi n‑tumni la toom n‑yiisiri la fufukka - ba vi tuma nla; bee, saŋmariwuusi n nyɛ ba, seesi an goori n‑goora, halli ka zibsim din ka naari ti deaai ba m‑mara.

english_verse: Enoch was the seventh person after Adam, and he was talking about these people when he said: Look! The Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of holy angels
translation_verse: Inɔkyi daa nyɛ la Adam ya'aŋŋa, sɔ ŋɔn daa yi badima ayɔpɔi Adam nyaaŋŋa ni na: ŋɔna n daa tɔ'asi anabitiri yɛttɔ'a zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi niriba wa yɛla, ni, “Ya nyama! Naawunni kyɛ na, ŋɔn ni u nyaaŋŋa - u toori niriba tusatusa la;

english_verse: to judge everyone. He will punish all those ungodly people for all the evil things they have done. The Lord will surely punish those ungodly sinners for every evil thing they have ever said about him.
translation_verse: u kyɛ na ni u ti kari sɔkam sariya. U nin darigi ba zaa tubri ba tumbeeri zugu, ni ban daa tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'abeeri n‑za'asi ŋɔn Naawunni maa zugu - ala'akyi dima n nyɛ ba ka ba za'asiri Naawunni noori.”

english_verse: These people grumble and complain and live by their own selfish desires. They brag about themselves and flatter others to get what they want. More Warnings
translation_verse: Niriba wa nyɛ la yɛttɔ'adɔ'asa daandima, ba kur fabinni la yɔriyɔri; ba dɔli la ba kɔribeeri sɔya, n‑tɔ'asiri n‑nyuuri bara; ba bu tiri niriba gyirima, amaa ba ya n‑yɔmsi ba n‑kyɛ ka ba nini tigi ba yɛla.

english_verse: My dear friends, remember the warning you were given by the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Yarima muna, m mabiseeba man wuri suŋŋa wa, ya teaama yɛttɔ'aseesi yan daa pun wum la, aseesi ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita tuumudima ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑tɔ'asi n‑ti ya la.

english_verse: They told you that near the end of time, selfish and godless people would start making fun of God.
translation_verse: Ni, ba daa yɛli, “Saha nin ti kyɛ na din nyɛ naari saha ka ninvugseeba nin ti bɛ ni n‑niŋni ansarisi, n‑dɔli la ba kɔribeeri, ka za'asiri Naawunni yɛla.”

english_verse: And now these people are already making you turn against each other. They think only about this life, and they don't have God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Lala niriba n nyɛ baseeba ban purigi ya ka kyɛ ka ya wɔligi taaba. Ba dɔli la ba duniya wa yam yɛla, ka kɔŋŋi Naawunni Sii maa zaa.

english_verse: Dear friends, keep building on the foundation of your most holy faith, as the Holy Spirit helps you to pray.
translation_verse: Yarima muna, m mabiisi, ya kpalimma n‑zɛ yummu ya yɛdda maa ni, dama di zaa nyɛ la Naawunni dinni. Ya puusima Sii Kasi maa yiiko ni,

english_verse: And keep in step with God's love, as you wait for our Lord Jesus Christ to show how kind he is by giving you eternal life.
translation_verse: ka kur kpalimma Naawunni wurilim la puuni. Ya dim sugru n‑guusiri ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita nambɔzoaa saha la, dama lala saha maa ka ya nin ti nya nyɔvuri kan ka tariga.

english_verse: Be helpful to all who may have doubts.
translation_verse: Ya zɔm namboo n‑ti ninvugseeba ban birisira.

english_verse: Rescue any who need to be saved, as you would rescue someone from a fire. Then with fear in your own hearts, have mercy on everyone who needs it. But hate even the clothes of those who have been made dirty by their filthy deeds. Final Prayer
translation_verse: Ninvugseeba muna - ya faama ba m‑basi bugum ni na. Seeba yaasa ya zɔm ba namboo, amaa ka zɔ ba dabeem gba, halli nti kyisi ba bunyeera da'ari siisigu gba, dama ba ningbinkɔrisi yɛla sa'amni a ni.

english_verse: Offer praise to God our Savior because of our Lord Jesus Christ! Only God can keep you from falling and make you pure and joyful in his glorious presence. Before time began and now and forever, God is worthy of glory, honor, power, and authority. Amen.
translation_verse: Naawunni kɔkɔ n nyɛ ti tiligra, ka ŋɔn nin toaai n‑guri ya n‑kyɛ ka ya kpalim m‑bɛ kasi. U nin zaali ya u gyirima zii tooni, ka da'ari ka ni, ka kyɛ ka ya sufu pɛligi zaa di zugu. Ya kyɛ ka ŋɔn Naawunni nya gyirima ti Dugma Yisa Kirisita Masiiya maa zugu, ka nya duusigu, ni kpeeŋŋu, ni yaa. Ka di daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ la lala, ka na bɛ la lala zuna, ka di nin kpalim m‑bɛ lala saha din ka naari. Ammi.

english_verse: This is what God showed to Jesus Christ, so that he could tell his servants what must happen soon. Christ then sent his angel with the message to his servant John.
translation_verse: Gbaŋŋu wa lahabaari wulisiri la Naawunni ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka Yisa Kirisita Masiiya ti kasigi n‑wulisi n‑ti u nyaandɔliba la. Di nyɛ la lahabaari zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi duniya yɛliseesi an ya n‑niŋi pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑kyɛnna. Yɛla wa zugu ka Masiiya maa daa tum u malaakasɔ man Gyɔni saani, man ŋɔn nyɛ u daabri maa.

english_verse: And John told everything he had seen about God's message and about what Jesus Christ had said and done.
translation_verse: Dizugu man Gyɔni nini ŋɔn daa nya yɛliseesi maa zaa m‑bɛ gbaŋŋu wa puuni. A zaa nyɛ la Naawunni yɛlimaŋni yɛla ka Yisa Masiiya daa kasigi a n‑dooli paalu ni.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who reads this prophecy to others, and he will bless everyone who hears and obeys it. The time is almost here.
translation_verse: Dizugu zugsuŋŋu pam bɛ nira zaa ŋɔn karim gbaŋŋu wa n‑ti u taaba. Ninvugseeba ban nin wum gbaŋŋu wa yɛligu ka sakki n‑ti ka yɛla gba mari zugsuŋŋu pam. Dama saha bu ya yuui ka gbaŋŋu wa puuni yɛla wa zaa nin yi paalu ka niriba nini nya.

english_verse: From John to the seven churches in Asia. I pray that you will be blessed with kindness and peace from God, who is and was and is coming. May you receive kindness and peace from the seven spirits before the throne of God.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ man Gyɔni m puusiri yaseeba ban dɔli Yisa ka la'amni taaba gyeeŋŋu ziisi ayɔpɔi seesi an bɛ Ɛseaa tingbaŋŋu ni la. Yɛttɔ'a wa ka n ya n‑yɛli ya: Naawunni daa bɛ ni, ka na bɛ ni, halli u nin bɛ ni sahakam: u nin zɔ ya zaa namboo ka ti ya sufudoaa, yaseeba ban dɔli Yisa. Ka u siiya buyɔpɔi bɛ u naam zizii tooni maa, ka ŋɔn gba nin niŋi lala n‑ti ya.

english_verse: May kindness and peace be yours from Jesus Christ, the faithful witness. Jesus was the first to conquer death, and he is the ruler of all earthly kings. Christ loves us, and by his blood he set us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Yisa Kirisita n daa tuui n‑neaai kum ni, ka nyɛ yɛdda daana ni sɛharaditta maa; ŋɔn gba nin zɔ ya namboo ka ti ya sufudoaa. Dama ŋɔna n nyɛ duniya zaa nadima Naaba. Ŋɔn Yisa wuri ti pam, ka lala zugu ka u daa kpi n‑yiisi ti ti tumbeeri taalli ni.

english_verse: He lets us rule as kings and serve God his Father as priests. To him be glory and power forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: Ka u laa n‑kyɛ ka ti leebi nabiisi ka gyeemni u Ba Naawunni hoo gyeeŋŋu toondaandima. Gyirima ni yiiko pam bɛ ŋɔn Yisa Kirisita Masiiya saani, halli saha din ka tariga! Ammi.

english_verse: Look! He is coming with the clouds. Everyone will see him, even the ones who stuck a sword through him. All people on earth will weep because of him. Yes, it will happen! Amen.
translation_verse: Ya gɔsima n‑nya! U bɛ sangbana ni n‑kyɛnni na; Di saha ya zaa nini nin nya u - Halli ninvugseeba ban daa kusi u maa gba: Dizugu ninsaala bura zaa niriba Nin kum ka fabli u zugu. Lala n nyɛ di. Ammi.

english_verse: The Lord God says, “I am Alpha and Omega, the one who is and was and is coming. I am God All-Powerful!” A Vision of the Risen Lord
translation_verse: Ti Dugma Naawunni yɛliya: “Mani n nyɛ piiligu, ka n mun nyɛ naari. Mani n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn na bɛ ni, ka daa pun bɛ ni, halli n nin ti kyɛ na: Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa.”

english_verse: I am John, a follower together with all of you. We suffer because Jesus is our king, but he gives us the strength to endure. I was sent to Patmos Island, because I had preached God's message and had told about Jesus.
translation_verse: Tɔ: mani n nyɛ ya mabii Gyɔni n‑sɔbri wa; ka man ni yarima zaa la'amni n‑diri wahala ni sugru, Yisa yuuri zugu, ni ti Dugma Naawunni naam la zugu. Tɔ, ka n daa bɛ la Patimɔsi tingbaŋŋu ni, tingbanseaa kan bɛ teeku kooma sunsukkuni. N daa bɛ bɛni Naawunni yɛligu zugu, man daa moonni u yɛla n‑wulisiri niriba maa zugu.

english_verse: On the Lord's day the Spirit took control of me, and behind me I heard a loud voice that sounded like a trumpet.
translation_verse: Di daari maa daa nyɛ la ti Dugma dabsiri maa, Alahari; ka n Dugma Sii maa daa paali ma ni, halli ka n ti wum kukɔri n nyaaŋŋa, ka ka kumni pampam hoo kikaa ŋɔn kumni seem.

english_verse: The voice said, “Write in a book what you see. Then send it to the seven churches in Ephesus, Smyrna, Pergamum, Thyatira, Sardis, Philadelphia, and Laodicea.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma i nini ŋɔn nya bunseesi ka i tuba gba wum yɛttɔ'aseesi maa zaa. Sɔbma a n‑tɔlim n‑ti Yisa niriba maa seeba ban la'amni taaba n‑gyeemni Naawunni gyeeŋŋu ziisi ayɔpɔi, tinsi ayɔpɔi puuni: ka lala tinsi yuya nŋɔa#:- Ɛfɛsusi, Simeena, Peegamɔn, Tayatayira, Saadisi, Filadɛlifiya ni Leoodisiiya.”

english_verse: When I turned to see who was speaking to me, I saw seven gold lampstands.
translation_verse: Di saha maa n daa gyeaai, ni n ti nya ninvugsɔ ŋɔn tɔ'asiri maa; ka daa nya ka salima buna ayɔpɔi n zɛya, ka firiladima tɔm n‑tɔm a zugu.

english_verse: There with the lampstands was someone who seemed to be the Son of Man. He was wearing a robe that reached down to his feet, and a gold cloth was wrapped around his chest.
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya firiladima maa sunsukkuni, ka sɔ n daa zɛya n‑ŋmanni hoo ninsaala, ka yɛ zalabia ka u sɛlooriga nyɛ salima bunni.

english_verse: His head and his hair were white as wool or snow, and his eyes looked like flames of fire.
translation_verse: U zugu ni u zɔbri daa pɛligi la hoo gumni, bee, hoo la'apeeliga, ka u nini nya'asiri ni hoo bugunsaala.

english_verse: His feet were glowing like bronze being heated in a furnace, and his voice sounded like the roar of a waterfall.
translation_verse: U nɔba nya'ari hoo wuntaŋŋa, ka u kukɔri daa ŋmanni la hoo kɔpɔbra pam damni.

english_verse: He held seven stars in his right hand, and a sharp double-edged sword was coming from his mouth. His face was shining as bright as the sun at noon.
translation_verse: Di saha maa u daa gyi la saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi u nudirigu ni, ka takoobi dittigu yiiri u noori ni na. Ka u nindaa mun kur nya'ari hoo wuntaŋŋa ŋɔn yi bii n‑nya'ari seem la.

english_verse: When I saw him, I fell at his feet like a dead person. But he put his right hand on me and said: Don't be afraid! I am the first, the last,
translation_verse: Man daa nya u sahanseaa la, n daa lu la tiŋŋa m‑pɔbri u toonni hoo man kpi ni la; ka u zaŋŋi u nudirigu m‑pa n zugu ka yɛli ma, ni, “Di kyɛ ka dabeem mari i, dama mani n nyɛ ŋɔn nyɛ piiligu ni naari!”

english_verse: and the living one. I died, but now I am alive forever, and I have the keys to death and the world of the dead.
translation_verse: Di saha ka u laa n‑yɛli, “Mani n nyɛ ŋɔn nɛ maa! N daa kpi ni, ka neaai nti kpalim saha din ka tariga; di zugu kum ni kpiimma tiŋŋa fukumsi zaa bɛ la n saani.

english_verse: Write what you have seen and what is and what will happen after these things.
translation_verse: Dinzugu sɔbma yɛliseesi i nini ŋɔn nyari wa zaasa, dama a nyɛ la yɛliseesi an niŋni pampaŋŋɔ ni yɛliseesi an nin kyɛ na di nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: I will explain the mystery of the seven stars you saw at my right side and the seven gold lampstands. The seven stars are the angels of the seven churches, and the lampstands are the seven churches.
translation_verse: Nyin ŋɔn nyari saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi ni firila tɔmpu ziisi wa - tɔ, a zaa nyɛ la yɛlisɔ'ara ka laa m‑mari a gbinni, a gbinni: saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi maa nyɛ la malaakaseeba ban guri Naawunni niriba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni u, tinsi ayɔpɔi maa puuni la. Ka firiladima ayɔpɔi la gba nyɛ la lala Naawunni niriba maa.”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Ephesus: I am the one who holds the seven stars in my right hand, and I walk among the seven gold lampstands. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Ɛfɛsusi tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Ŋɔn gyi la saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi u nudirigu ni la yɛligu nŋɔa; ŋɔn kyɛnni salima firilatɔmna sunsukkuni maa. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:-

english_verse: I know everything you have done, including your hard work and how you have endured. I know you won't put up with anyone who is evil. When some people pretended to be apostles, you tested them and found out they were liars.
translation_verse: “M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni, ya tumni tunkara ka diri sugru; ka ya bu boori tumbeeri tumniba, halli ninvugseeba ban ma'ari ziri n‑yɛtti, ni, ba nyɛ la Yisa tuumudima: ban ka ya viisiri suŋŋa m‑baŋŋi, ni, ba pa la lala.

english_verse: You have endured and gone through hard times because of me, yet you have not given up.
translation_verse: Ka ya diri sugru, ka zaŋŋni sufukpeemni n‑tumni n‑tiri ma, ka bu gyeera.

english_verse: But I do have something against you! And it is this: You don't have as much love as you used to.
translation_verse: Amaa m mari yɛttɔ'aseaa n nin yɛli n‑galim ya, ni, ya daa daŋŋi n‑wuri ma suŋŋa, amaa ya bu laa n‑wuri ma lala.

english_verse: Think about where you have fallen from, and then turn back and do as you did at first. If you don't turn back, I will come and take away your lampstand.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya teaama yan daa du saazugu seem ka baŋŋi yan lu n‑sigi seem; ka takki ya teesa ni ya beesigu, ka laa m‑maali n‑tumni hoo yan daa daŋŋi n‑tumni seem maa. Ya yi bu niŋi lala, n nin kyɛ na n‑yiisi ya firilatɔmnigu la ka zaazii maa ni m‑basi.

english_verse: But there is one thing you are doing right. You hate what the Nicolaitans are doing, and so do I.
translation_verse: Halli bunsuŋŋu na kpalim m‑bɛ ya saani. Dama ya ku sakki Nikɔlaati dima yɛla, hoo man gba ŋɔn ku sakki ba yɛla seem.”

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. I will let everyone who wins the victory eat from the life-giving tree in God's wonderful garden. The Letter to Smyrna
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la: “Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin kyɛ ka u di nyɔvuri tii maa wala, tiseaa kan zɛ Naawunni zisuŋŋu la puuni maa.” ’ ”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Smyrna: I am the first and the last. I died, but now I am alive! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Simeena tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Ŋɔn n nyɛ piiligu ka laa n‑nyɛ naari yɛligu nŋɔa: u daa kpiya, ka laa n‑yisigi n‑nɛa. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:-

english_verse: I know how much you suffer and how poor you are, but you are rich. I also know the cruel things being said about you by people who claim to be God's people. But they are not really God's people. They are a group that belongs to Satan.
translation_verse: “M mi ya wahala ni ya fara beesigu ŋɔn bɛ seem: amaa - ya bu nya? - ya nyɛ la bundaandima. M mi niriba wa ŋɔn tuuri ya seem maa - ninvugseeba ban yɛtti, ni, Izraɛli dima n nyɛ ba, halli ni ba pa la lala, amaa Sintaani la'aŋŋu duu niriba n nyɛ ba.

english_verse: Don't worry about what you will suffer. The devil will throw some of you into jail, and you will be tested and made to suffer for ten days. But if you are faithful until you die, I will reward you with a glorious life.
translation_verse: Yan di zɔ dabeem yan yɛ n‑nya namsigu seem zugu. Ya bu nya? - Sintaani nin kyɛ ka ba gbaasi yaseeba n‑kpari sarika duu ni n‑namsi ya, ka ya nin nya wahala dabsipiiya. Ya kur zɛ yummu m‑mari ya yɛdda niŋbu kirikiri, halli nti paai kum, ka mani nin zaŋŋi piinisuma n‑ti ya - di nyɛ la nyɔvuri.”

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. Whoever wins the victory will not be hurt by the second death. The Letter to Pergamum
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la: “Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, kum din paasiri buyi maa ku toaai n‑niŋi u seelli.” ’ ”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Pergamum: I am the one who has the sharp double-edged sword! Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Peegamɔn tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Ŋɔn mari takoobi maa yɛligu nŋɔa. Ŋɔna n‑yɛtti ya, ni#:-

english_verse: I know you live where Satan has his throne. But you have kept true to my name. Right there where Satan lives, my faithful witness Antipas was taken from you and put to death. Even then you did not give up your faith in me.
translation_verse: “M mi yan bɛ seela maa, ni, Sintaani naam zizii bɛ la bɛni. Ya bu nya? - ka halli ni dinŋɔ zaa, ya na mari n yuuri yɛla suŋŋa, ka bu za'asi yan niŋi ma yɛdda seem, halli Antipasi saha gba - Antipasi sɔ ŋɔn daa mooli n yɛla ka ba daa ku u ya tiŋŋa maa puuni, kan nyɛ Sintaani ŋɔn bɛ seela.

english_verse: I do have a few things against you. Some of you are following the teaching of Balaam. Long ago he told Balak to teach the people of Israel to eat food that had been offered to idols and to be immoral.
translation_verse: Amaa m mari yɛttɔ'aseesi ni n nin yɛli n‑galim ya, ni, ninvugseeba bɛ ya saani ban dɔli la Beelɛm wulisigu yɛla. Dama ŋɔn Beelɛm daa nyɛ dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa wulisi NaaBalakku ŋɔn nin niŋi seem n‑kyɛ ka Izraɛli dima tum tumbeeri. Ka lala daa kyɛ ka ba daa burigi soori, n‑tɔtti maaligu numni n‑dira, ka a daa yi la ziri wuna la sara ni na; ka u daa laa n‑kyɛ ka ba tumni zina m‑paasi.

english_verse: Now some of you are following the teaching of the Nicolaitans.
translation_verse: M‑paasi yaasa, ya mari ninvugseeba ban dɔli Nikɔlaati dima wulisigu la.

english_verse: Turn back! If you don't, I will come quickly and fight against these people. And my words will cut like a sword.
translation_verse: Dizugu ya takkma ya teesa ni ya beesigu la! Di yi pa lala, n nin kyɛ ya saani yooma na, n‑zaŋŋi n noori ni takoobi maa n‑tuusi ya tɔppu.”

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. To everyone who wins the victory, I will give some of the hidden food. I will also give each one a white stone with a new name written on it. No one will know that name except the one who is given the stone. The Letter to Thyatira
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la: “Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin zaŋŋi ‘manna’ disɔ'ara n‑ti u, ka laa n‑ti u kugpeelli, ka ba sɔbi yupaalli n‑niŋi ka, ka sɔ ku mi lala yuuri maa na'ala ŋɔn deaai kugri maa.” ’ ”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Thyatira: I am the Son of God! My eyes are like flames of fire, and my feet are like bronze. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Ni, “Ka dinŋɔ ka i sɔbi n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Tayatayira tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Naawunni Bii yɛligu nŋɔa; sɔ ŋɔn mari u nini hoo bugunsaala maa, ka u nɔba nya'ari hoo wuntaŋŋa. Ŋɔna n‑yɛtti ya, ni#:-

english_verse: I know everything about you, including your love, your faith, your service, and how you have endured. I know you are doing more now than you have ever done before.
translation_verse: “M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem, ni ya wurilim, ni ya yɛdda maa; m mi yan tumni n‑suŋŋni niriba seem, ka ya sufu ŋɔn laa n‑kpeem seem maa. Halli ya naayi tumni tunkara ka a gaari yan daa daŋŋi n‑tum seesi la.

english_verse: But I still have something against you because of that woman Jezebel. She calls herself a prophet, and you let her teach and mislead my servants to do immoral things and to eat food offered to idols.
translation_verse: Amaa m mari yɛttɔ'aseaa n nin yɛli n‑galim ya, ni, ya kyɛ ka pɔ'a wa, Gyɛzibɛla, wulisiri n‑yɔmsiri n niriba la. U ma'ai la ziri n‑yɛtti, ni, pɔ'a-anabi n nyɛ u, ka yɔmsiri ba ni ba ti tumni zina, ka diri ziri wuna sara.

english_verse: I gave her a chance to turn from her sins, but she did not want to stop doing these immoral things.
translation_verse: N pun ti u saha ni u takki u teesibeeri ni u beesigu la, amaa u bu sakki, ni, u kyɛ u zina tuma la.

english_verse: I am going to strike down Jezebel. Everyone who does these immoral things with her will also be punished, if they don't stop.
translation_verse: Dizugu n nin zaŋŋi u m‑basi namsigu gadu zugu, ŋɔn ni ban tumni zina u saani la m‑paasi, ka ba yi bu takki ba teesibeeri ni tumbɛseesi pɔ'a wa ŋɔn wulisi ba la zaa, ba nin di wahala pam.

english_verse: I will even kill her followers. Then all the churches will see that I know everyone's thoughts and feelings. I will treat each of you as you deserve.
translation_verse: N nin kyɛ ka u biisi maa ti kpi zaa. Lala saha maa ka ninvugseeba ban la'amni n‑gyeemni ma tinsi tinsi puuni maa nin ti baŋŋi, ni, man nyɛ la sɔ ŋɔn viisiri niriba sufu ni teesa, ka nin ti tisi ya sɔkam hoo u maŋtoori tuma ŋɔn sumsi ni u deaai seem maa.

english_verse: Some of you in Thyatira don't follow Jezebel's teaching. You don't know anything about what her followers call the “deep secrets of Satan.” So I won't burden you down with any other commands.
translation_verse: Amaa kyɛ ka n tɔ'asi n‑ti ya Tayatayira dimseeba ban kpalim la, yan bu deaai wulisigu wa zaa. Ya bu bamsi ban boonni ‘yɛlisɔ'ara, bunzulima’ la - Sintaani yɛlisɔ'ara n kur nyɛ a! Tɔ, yarima muna - n ku ziili ya ziseaa m‑paasi,

english_verse: But until I come, you must hold firmly to the teaching you have.
translation_verse: di yi pa la ya kpalim n‑gyi yan na mari bunseesi maa, halli ka n ti ya kyɛ ya saani na.

english_verse: I will give power over the nations to everyone who wins the victory and keeps on obeying me until the end.
translation_verse: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, sɔkam ŋɔn mari n tuma tumpu suŋŋa halli nti paai yɛla zaa naari maa#:- ‘N nin zaŋŋi fukumsi n‑ti u Ka u nin ti su niriba kam zaa.

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la.’ ”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Sardis: I have the seven spirits of God and the seven stars. Listen to what I say. I know what you are doing. Everyone may think you are alive, but you are dead.
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Saadisi tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Ŋɔn su Naawunni siiya buyɔpɔi yɛttɔ'a, ka u su la saŋmariwuusi ayɔpɔi gba. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:- “M mi ya tuma an bɛ seem, ni, ba yɛtti ya yɛla, ni, ya neaai ni maa, amaa ya kpi ni.

english_verse: Wake up! You have only a little strength left, and it is almost gone. So try to become stronger. I have found that you are not completely obeying God.
translation_verse: Ya neaama! ka kpamsi din kpalim ya saani, halli an ya n‑kpi gba, dama m bu nya ya tuma maa ka a nya tummuna Naawunni saani.

english_verse: Remember the teaching you were given and heard. Hold firmly to it and turn from your sins. If you don't wake up, I will come when you least expect it, just as a thief does.
translation_verse: Di zugu ya teaama yan daa deaai seelli, ni yan daa wum seelli maa; ya wumsima anŋɔ zaa ka kyɛ ka ya sufu sa'am ya tumbeeri taalli zugu, ka takki ya beebu di zugu. Dama ya yi ku neaai, n nin kyɛ na hoo na'ayiga ŋɔn kyɛ na seem, ka ya ku baŋŋi sahaseaa man nin kyɛ ya saani na.

english_verse: A few of you in Sardis have not dirtied your clothes with sin. You will walk with me in white clothes, because you are worthy.
translation_verse: Amaa ya Saadisi dima maa seeba bu sa'am ya situra ni da'ari tuma. Ban nin ti yɛ situripeela ka man ni ban nin la'am n‑kyɛnna, dama ba sum la lala.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will wear white clothes. Their names will not be erased from the book of life, and I will tell my Father and his angels that they are my followers.
translation_verse: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, ŋɔn gba nin ti yɛ situripeela ka man ku kyɛ ka ba nyeesi u yuuri n‑yiisi nyɔvuri bukku maa ni m‑basi. N nin di u sɛhara n‑zɛ u nyaaŋŋa m Ba Naawunni saani ni u malaakadima saani.”

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Philadelphia
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la.’ ”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Philadelphia: I am the one who is holy and true, and I have the keys that belonged to David. When I open a door, no one can close it. And when I close a door, no one can open it. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Filadɛlifiya tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Ŋɔn n nyɛ Naawunni dinni, yɛlimaŋni daana maa yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa. Ŋɔna m mari la NaaDauda saafibii maa: ka u yi ti yoaai seelli, sɔ n ku toaai n‑yɔa; ka u mun yi ti yɔ seelli, sɔ n ku toaai n‑yoaai. Ŋɔna n yɛtti ya, ni#:-

english_verse: I know everything you have done. And I have placed before you an open door no one can close. You were not very strong, but you obeyed my message and did not deny you are my followers.
translation_verse: “M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem: ya bu nya? - za'anɔri ka n yoaai m‑basi ya la, ka sɔ ku toaai n‑yɔ ka. Dama m mi halli, ni, ya yaa bu zoaai, amaa ya deaari n noori maa ka bu yi n nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: Now you will see what I will do with those people who belong to Satan's group. They claim to be God's people, but they are liars. I will make them come and kneel down at your feet. Then they will know that I love you.
translation_verse: Sintaani la'aŋŋu duu dima maa muna - baseeba ban yɛtti, ni, ba nyɛ la Izraɛli dima maa, amaa ba ma'ari ni, ba pa lala - n nin kyɛ ka ban nin kyɛ ya saani na n‑gbaani ya tooni ka baŋŋi, ni, n wuri ya.

english_verse: You obeyed my message and endured. So I will protect you from the time of testing everyone in all the world must go through.
translation_verse: Ya daa deaai n noori ka di sugru n‑dɔla, di zugu n nin deaai ya n‑guri ya suŋŋa, ka wahalaseaa kan ti ya kyɛ na la ku niŋi ya seela - ka nin ti kyɛ na sahaseaa n‑zamsi duniya wa ni niriba zaa.

english_verse: I am coming soon. So hold firmly to what you have, and no one will take away the crown you will be given as your reward.
translation_verse: Pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni na. Ya gbaama yan mari seelli la kpeeŋŋa, ka di kyɛ ka sɔ ti fa ya naam zupiliga ya saani.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will be made into a pillar in the temple of my God, and they will stay there forever. I will write on each of them the name of my God and the name of his city. It is the new Jerusalem my God will send down from heaven. I will also write on them my own new name.
translation_verse: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin zaŋŋi u n‑kyɛ ka u leebi hoo zaantɔliga ka ba sa m Ba Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri maa puuni, ka u ku laa n‑yi bɛni na yaasa. N nin sɔbi m Ba Naawunni yuuri m‑pa n‑niŋi u, ni ŋɔn Naawunni timpaalli la yuuri, di nyɛ la Gyɛrusalɛm bumpaalli la. Tiŋŋa wa ya n‑yi saazugu, m Ba saani, n‑sigi na. Ka n nin sɔbi m mammantoori yupaalli m‑pa di daama ni m‑paasi.”

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches. The Letter to Laodicea
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la.’ ”

english_verse: This is what you must write to the angel of the church in Laodicea: I am the one called Amen! I am the faithful and true witness and the source of God's creation. Listen to what I say.
translation_verse: Ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑ti malaaka sɔ ŋɔn su fukumsi zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi ban bɛ Leoodisiiya tiŋŋa n‑la'amni n‑gyeemni ma#:- ‘Ŋɔn yuuri boonni, ni, ‘Ammi’ yɛttɔ'a nŋɔa: ŋɔna n nyɛ yɛdda ni yɛlimaŋni daana, sɔ ŋɔn diri sɛhara suŋŋa. Ŋɔna n daa piili Naawunni ŋɔn daa naam bunseelli kam, ka su di zaa fukumsi. Ŋɔna n‑yɛtti ya, ni#:-

english_verse: I know everything you have done, and you are not cold or hot. I wish you were either one or the other.
translation_verse: “M mi ya tuma ŋɔn bɛ seem: ni, ya bu maasa, ya mun bu tuula - man da boori ka ya nyɛ la a yinni.

english_verse: But since you are lukewarm and neither cold nor hot, I will spit you out of my mouth.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna yan bu maasa ka mun bu tuula zugu, ka kur kpalim n‑nyɛ bunŋmamm, n ya n‑ti ya ni n‑yiisi n noori ni m‑basi.

english_verse: You claim to be rich and successful and to have everything you need. But you don't know how bad off you really are. You are pitiful, poor, blind, and naked.
translation_verse: Halli ya yɛtti, ni, ‘Bundaandima n nyɛ ti - ti mari la marigu pam ka bu kɔŋŋi seelli!’ - amaa ya bu baŋŋi, ni, ya siri nyɛ la faradima, ka wahala mari ya, ka ya kɔŋŋi bunseelli kam; ya laa n‑zoom zaa ka kyɛnni beelli, situra ka ni.

english_verse: Buy your gold from me. It has been refined in a fire, and it will make you rich. Buy white clothes from me. Wear them and you can cover up your shameful nakedness. Buy medicine for your eyes, so you will be able to see.
translation_verse: Mani n kpaamni ya, ni, ya kyɛm n saani na n‑da salima - ban zaŋŋi bugum n‑kuri seesi ka a ti leebi kasikasi maa - ka ya siri nin leebi bundaandima: ya daama situripeela n‑yɛa, ka lala nin ligi ya beelli kyɛnni vi maa: halli ka da nini mirigri gba, ka di nin kyɛ ka ya nini neaara.

english_verse: I correct and punish everyone I love. So make up your minds to turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Ya bu nya? - Man ŋɔn wuri seeba zaa, ban ka n sa'asiri ka kpamsiri, ni ba ti bamsi yam. Dinzugu ya kyɛ ka ya nini moaai ka takki ya teesibeeri ni ya tumbeeri soori.

english_verse: Listen! I am standing and knocking at your door. If you hear my voice and open the door, I will come in and we will eat together.
translation_verse: Nnya ma! N zɛ la za'anɔri maa ni n‑kpaasiri m‑boonna; ka ŋɔnkam yi wum n kukɔri maa ka yoaai n‑ti ma, n nin kpɛ na n‑di diibu u saani, halli man ni ŋɔna nin ti la'am n‑di.

english_verse: Everyone who wins the victory will sit with me on my throne, just as I won the victory and sat with my Father on his throne.
translation_verse: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi, n nin ti u soori ni u zinni man saani n naam gbana zugu, hoo man gba daa pun nyaŋŋi ka zinni m Ba naam gbana zugu seem la.”

english_verse: If you have ears, listen to what the Spirit says to the churches.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, kyɛ ka u wum Sii Kasi maa ŋɔn yɛtti seela n‑tiri ban la'amni tinsi tinsi puuni n‑gyeemni Naawunni la.’ ”

english_verse: After this, I looked and saw a door that opened into heaven. Then the voice that had spoken to me at first and that sounded like a trumpet said, “Come up here! I will show you what must happen next.”
translation_verse: Tɔ: anŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka n daa nya, ka arizana ziiya ka ba yoaai hoo kyɛburu: ka n daa wum man ŋɔn daa pun wum kukɔseaa la, ka ka bɛ hoo kikaa kunkuma maa. Kukɔri maa yɛtti nŋɔa#:- “Duuma kpa na, ka n nin wulisi i din ti ya niŋi dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa.”

english_verse: Right then the Spirit took control of me, and there in heaven I saw a throne and someone sitting on it.
translation_verse: Ka Naawunni Sii Kasi daa paali ma, ka n nya naam ziziiya arizana puuni ka sɔ n ziya.

english_verse: The one who was sitting there sparkled like precious stones of jasper and carnelian. A rainbow that looked like an emerald surrounded the throne.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn daa ziya maa gɔsibu daa veela hoo mansɔ'asi kugveela ban boonni ‘gyasipa’ ni ‘Sadiisi kugri’ la ŋɔn veela seem. Ka sasuu veelim daa gyiligi naam zizii maa hoo mansɔ'asi veelim ŋɔn bɛ seem.

english_verse: Twenty-four other thrones were in a circle around that throne. And on each of these thrones there was an elder dressed in white clothes and wearing a gold crown.
translation_verse: Ka ziziisi pisi ni anaasi gba daa bɛ ni n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa, ka kpambaya pisi ni anaasi zi a ni: ba daa yɛ bunyeeripeela n‑ziya, ka pili salima naam zupilisi ba zugri ni.

english_verse: Flashes of lightning and roars of thunder came out from the throne in the center of the circle. Seven torches, which are the seven spirits of God, were burning in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Ka saa daa nya'asiri ka tasiri m‑mari vuuri pam naam zizii maa ni na, ka bugum mɔkpala ayɔpɔi neaari zizii maa tooni - Naawunni siiya buyɔpɔi n kur nyɛ ana.

english_verse: Also in front of the throne was something that looked like a glass sea, clear as crystal. Around the throne in the center were four living creatures covered front and back with eyes.
translation_verse: Ka bunseelli daa dooni naam zizii maa tooni hoo mɔ'ari, ka ka gɔsibu ŋmanni la hoo mansɔ'asi. Ka bunvuya anaasi n daa zɛ n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa sunsukkuni, ka ba lugri kam mari la nini n‑gɔsira.

english_verse: The first creature was like a lion, the second one was like a bull, the third one had the face of a human, and the fourth was like a flying eagle.
translation_verse: Ka bunvusɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni maa daa ŋmanni la gbigimni, ka ŋɔn pa nyaaŋŋa ŋmanni la na'araa, ka ŋɔn paasi buta mari nindaa hoo ninsaala dinni, ka ŋɔn paasi bunaasi mun ŋmanni la wubga n‑yikkra.

english_verse: Each of the four living creatures had six wings, and their bodies were covered with eyes. Day and night they never stopped singing, “Holy, holy, holy is the Lord, the all-powerful God, who was and is and is coming!”
translation_verse: Ka babanaasi maa zaa daa mari kpunkpama ayooyoobu, ka ba lugri kam mari la nini n‑gɔsira. Ba daa kur yuumni yuuma sahakam, wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu, ni#:- “Kasi! kasi! kasi! Ti Dugma Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa Nyɛ la ŋɔn bɛ kasi maa! Ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni, Ka u kpalim m‑bɛ ni pampaŋŋɔa, Halli ŋɔn ya kyɛ na.”

english_verse: The living creatures kept praising, honoring, and thanking the one who sits on the throne and who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Ka bunvuya maa daa yi yuumni lala, n‑tibgri Ŋɔn zi naam zizii maa ni, ka tiri u gyirima, ni, u nin bɛ saha din ka naari. Ka lala saha maa

english_verse: “Our Lord and God, you are worthy to receive glory, honor, and power. You created all things, and by your decision they are and were created.”
translation_verse: “Nyini n nyɛ ti Dugma, Nyini n nyɛ Naawunni, I sumni tibgri ni gyirima, I sumni yiiko marigu! Dama nyini n daa naam bunseelli kam, I yuubu zugu ka a zaa daa naam, I yuubu zugu ka a zaa daa ti bɛ ni.”

english_verse: In the right hand of the one sitting on the throne I saw a scroll that had writing on the inside and on the outside. And it was sealed in seven places.
translation_verse: Ka n daa gɔsi nti nya bukkubila, ka Sɔ ŋɔn daa zi naam zizii maa ni la daa mari ka u nudirigu ni. Ka sɔppu daa bɛ ka puuni, ni ka nyaaŋŋa m‑paasi; ka ba zaŋŋi ka n‑kpabi, ka tabli buntabinna ayɔpɔi.

english_verse: I saw a mighty angel ask with a loud voice, “Who is worthy to open the scroll and break its seals?”
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya malaaka kpeeŋŋu daansɔa, ka u tasiri m‑boonni pampam, ni, “Ŋɔni n sum ni u ti lakki buntabinna maa n‑yoaai bukku wa?”

english_verse: No one in heaven or on earth or under the earth was able to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Amaa sɔsɔ daa ka ni n‑nin nyaŋŋi n‑yoaai bukku maa, halli n‑gɔsi ka gba; saazugu sɔ ka ni, tingbaŋŋu gba sɔ ka ni, halli tingbanlɔŋŋu ni gba sɔ ka ni.

english_verse: I cried hard because no one was found worthy to open the scroll or look inside it.
translation_verse: Man mun daa kum pam, dama sɔ daa ka ni n‑sumsi ni u yoaai bukku maa, halli n‑gɔsi ka gba.

english_verse: Then one of the elders said to me, “Stop crying and look! The one who is called both the ‘Lion from the Tribe of Judah’ and ‘King David's Great Descendant’ has won the victory. He will open the scroll and its seven seals.”
translation_verse: Amaa kpambaya pisi ni anaasi sɔ daa yɛli ma, ni, “Di kumna! Dama Gbigimni maa nyaŋŋiya, Gyuuda za'anɔri Gbigimni la; NaaDauda dɔ'am tuŋŋu maa Nya'ari gba n nyɛ u. Ŋɔna n nin toaai n‑lakki buntabinna ayɔpɔi n‑yoaai bukkubila maa.”

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a Lamb standing in the center of the throne surrounded by the four living creatures and the elders. The Lamb looked as if it had once been killed. It had seven horns and seven eyes, which are the seven spirits of God, sent out to all the earth.
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa nya pɛbilisɔa. U daa zɛya naam zizii la puuni, ka bunvuya anaasi ni kpambaya maa daa gyiligi u, ka di daa bɛ la hoo ban pun kɔrigi u seem la. U daa mari yiila ayɔpɔi ni nini ayɔpɔi, an wulisi Naawunni siiya buyɔpɔi la, ka u mari a n‑tumni ni a kyaŋŋi duniya wa zaa puuni la.

english_verse: The Lamb went over and took the scroll from the right hand of the one who sat on the throne.
translation_verse: Ka Pɛbila wa daa kyɛ na n‑deaai bukku maa, Ŋɔn daa zi naam zizii maa ni saani.

english_verse: Then they sang a new song, “You are worthy to receive the scroll and open its seals, because you were killed. And with your own blood you bought for God people from every tribe, language, nation, and race.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yuumni n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Nyin n sumsi ni i deaai bukku maa, I mun sumsi ni i lakki ka buntabinna! Dama nyini ka ba daa pun kɔrigi, Ka i lala kum zugu daa yɔ sɔkam samni N‑da ba n‑ti Naawunni na: Zuliya pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, Ni tinsi pam niriba, ni bura pam.

english_verse: You let them become kings and serve God as priests, and they will rule on earth.”
translation_verse: I daa kyɛ ka ba di naam Naawunni saani, Ka diisi ba Gyeeŋŋu Toondaandima n‑ti u - Ban mun nin ti di duniya wa ni naam.”

english_verse: As I looked, I heard the voices of a large number of angels around the throne and the voices of the living creatures and of the elders. There were millions and millions of them,
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa nya, ka daa wum malaakadima pam damni - malaaka tusatusa, bee miiliyɔnmiiliyɔn. Ba daa zɛ n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa, ni bunvuya ni kpambaya maa ziiya.

english_verse: and they were saying in a loud voice, “The Lamb who was killed is worthy to receive power, riches, wisdom, strength, honor, glory, and praise.”
translation_verse: M‑boonni pampam n‑yɛtta#:- “Pɛbila maa sumni seelikam deaari! Ba daa kɔrigi u, ka u sumni anŋɔ zaa#: U sum ni u deaai kpeeŋŋu ni arizikki, U sum ni u deaai yam ni yaa, U sum ni u deaai pɛ'abu, ni gyirima, ni yɛlisuma!”

english_verse: Then I heard all beings in heaven and on the earth and under the earth and in the sea offer praise. Together, all of them were saying, “Praise, honor, glory, and strength forever and ever to the one who sits on the throne and to the Lamb!”
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa wum, ka ban daa naam bunseelli kam zaa gba daa tɔ'asira - an bɛ saazugu, ni an bɛ tingbaŋŋu ni, ni an bɛ tingbanlɔŋŋu ni, halli an bɛ teeku ni zaa - n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Pɛ'abu ka ba zaŋŋi n‑ti u: Ba tisi Ŋɔn zi naam zizii maa ni, Pɛbila maa gba ka ba tisira - Pɛ'abu, ni gyirima, ni fukumsi Halli ni saha din ka naari.”

english_verse: The four living creatures said “Amen,” while the elders knelt down and worshiped.
translation_verse: Ka bunvuya anaasi maa daa sakki, ni, “Ammi!”; ka kpambaya maa daa gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeemna.

english_verse: At the same time I saw the Lamb open the first of the seven seals, I heard one of the four living creatures shout with a voice like thunder. It said, “Come out!”
translation_verse: N daa nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinna ayɔpɔi la yinni; ka n daa ti wum bunvuya anaasi maa yinni ŋɔn tasiri kaman satasiŋŋa n‑yɛtti, “Kyɛm na!”

english_verse: Then I saw a white horse. Its rider carried a bow and was given a crown. He had already won some victories, and he went out to win more.
translation_verse: Ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya wuripeelli, ka ŋɔn daa ba u maa mari taroo, ka ba zaŋŋi naam zupiliga n‑ti u, ka u daa yi na ni u ti tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the second seal, I heard the second living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buyi la; ka n daa wum bunvusɔ ŋɔn paasi buyi daana la ŋɔn gba n yɛtti, “Kyɛm na!”

english_verse: Then another horse came out. It was fiery red. And its rider was given the power to take away all peace from the earth, so people would slaughter one another. He was also given a big sword.
translation_verse: Ka wurizeaa daa yi na, ka sɔ ŋɔn daa ba u maa, ka ba zaŋŋi yiiko n‑ti u ni u ti fa duniya zaa sugru ni nangbanyinni ba saani, din nin kyɛ ka daadam kuuri taaba. Ka ba zaŋŋi takoobi titaari n‑niŋi u nuu ni.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the third seal, I heard the third living creature say, “Come out!” Then I saw a black horse, and its rider had a balance scale in one hand.
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buta la; ka n daa wum bunvusɔ ŋɔn paasi buta la ŋɔn gba n yɛtti, “Kyɛm na!” Ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya wurisabilli, ka ŋɔn daa ba u maa daa mari gbarigbinni kan ma'asiri kyi u nuu ni.

english_verse: I heard what sounded like a voice from somewhere among the four living creatures. It said, “A liter of wheat will cost you a whole day's wages! Three liters of barley will cost you a day's wages too. But don't ruin the olive oil or the wine.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum hoo kukɔri n‑tasiri pampam bunvuya anaasi la sunsukkuni, ni, “Kyɛ ka ba tumni dabsimunni n‑kur nyari kasuma gbarigbinni yinni, bee, kabeeri gbarigbina ata - a ligri nla! - amaa di sa'am kpaam maa, bee, daam maa!”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fourth seal, I heard the voice of the fourth living creature say, “Come out!”
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri bunaasi la; ka n daa wum bunvusɔ ŋɔn paasi bunaasi la ŋɔn gba n yɛtti, “Kyɛm na!”

english_verse: Then I saw a pale green horse. Its rider was named Death, and Death's Kingdom followed close behind. They were given power over one fourth of the earth, and they could kill its people with swords, famines, diseases, and wild animals.
translation_verse: Ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya wuripirisiri, ka ŋɔn daa ba u maa yuuri boonni la ‘Kum’, ka u taraana ‘Kpiimma Tiŋŋa’ daa dɔla. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi yiiko n‑ti ba zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi duniya purigri bunaasi zugu yinni niriba, ni ba zaŋŋi takoobi fukumsi n‑kuuri ba, ni kɔm fukumsi, ni dooru ni mooni bungbaara dinni m‑paasi.

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the fifth seal, I saw under the altar the souls of everyone who had been killed for speaking God's message and telling about their faith.
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri bunu la; ka n daa gɔsi nti nya ninvugseeba ka ba daa pun ku ba, ban daa mooli Naawunni yɛligu la zugu, ni ban daa wulisi yɛliseesi ban daa nya ka wum Naawunni saani la zugu; banŋɔ zaa siisi daa bɛ la wungyeeŋŋu teebuli maa lɔŋni.

english_verse: They shouted, “Master, you are holy and faithful! How long will it be before you judge and punish the people of this earth who killed us?”
translation_verse: Ka ban zaa n daa tasiri kpeeŋŋakpeeŋŋa n‑yɛtti, ni “Ti Dugma, nyin kur nyɛ la Yɛlimaŋni Daana kasikasi! - ka bɔ saha ka i nin ti kari ti sariya n‑ti ti, ka zaali duniya dima samni, ti kum zugu?”

english_verse: Then each of those who had been killed was given a white robe and told to rest for a little while. They had to wait until the complete number of the Lord's other servants and followers would be killed.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ba daa zaŋŋi bunyeeripeela n‑ti ba zaa, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, ba vuusima beela, halli ka ba taaba zaa saha gba ti paai, ba mabiseeba ban gba nyɛ Naawunni daaba maa - seeba duniya niriba ya n‑ku m‑paasi ba zugu la.

english_verse: When I saw the Lamb open the sixth seal, I looked and saw a great earthquake. The sun turned as dark as sackcloth, and the moon became red as blood.
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya, ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buyoobu la; ka n daa gɔsi nti nya ka tingbaŋŋu daa dam pampam. Ka wuntaŋŋa maa daa sɔbgi libilibi, ka namboaa daa moaai zimizimi zaa,

english_verse: The stars in the sky fell to earth, just like figs shaken loose by a windstorm.
translation_verse: halli saŋmariwuusi gba daa luri tingbaŋŋu ni na, hoo sahanseaa sunzɔpiliŋŋa ŋɔn zeeri n‑zɛ'asiri kinkaŋŋa ka ka wala luri tiŋŋa ni seem maa.

english_verse: Then the sky was rolled up like a scroll, and all mountains and islands were moved from their places.
translation_verse: Ka sangbaŋŋu maa daa kpabi hoo sɔŋŋu m‑bakki, ka tingbaŋŋu ni zɔya la daa yigsi a beesigu ziiya, ni tingbanseesi an bɛ teeku ni la gba.

english_verse: The kings of the earth, its famous people, and its military leaders hid in caves or behind rocks on the mountains. They hid there together with the rich and the powerful and with all the slaves and free people.
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa nya duniya nadima ni ninvugkara ni kpambaya, m‑paasi bundaandima ni ban mari kpeeŋŋu la, ni sɔkam zaa, daaba ni sumaŋdima zaa: ka ba zaa daa zaŋŋi ba mansi n‑sɔ'ari la zɔya ni feeri ni tampiisi lɔŋni.

english_verse: Then they shouted to the mountains and the rocks, “Fall on us! Hide us from the one who sits on the throne and from the anger of the Lamb.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa boonni n‑yɛtti zɔya ni tampiisi maa, ni, “Ya luma ti zugri na n‑sɔ'ai ti, ka Sɔ ŋɔn zi naam zizii la di nya ti, halli Pɛbila wa suuri di paai ti na!

english_verse: That terrible day has come! God and the Lamb will show their anger, and who can face it?”
translation_verse: Dama ba suuri yisigri dabsiri maa paai ti na, ka sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑kpalim n‑zɛ ba tooni.”

english_verse: After this I saw four angels. Each one was standing on one of the earth's four corners. The angels held back the four winds, so that no wind would blow on the earth or on the sea or on any tree. These angels had also been given the power to harm the earth and the sea. Then I saw another angel come up from where the sun rises in the east, and he was ready to put the mark of the living God on people. He shouted to the four angels,
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa nya, ka malaakasɔ daa doaai wuntampusiri zii ni na, ka u daa mari bunseelli kan nyɛ Naawunni Nyɔvuri Daana dinni, ka ka daalimni ŋɔn Naawunni maŋtoori buna. Malaaka wa daa tasi pampam n‑tiri u taaba babanaasi seeba ban daa mari yiiko ni ba sa'amni tingbaŋŋu ni teeku maa,

english_verse: “Don't harm the earth or the sea or any tree! Wait until I have marked the foreheads of the servants of our God.”
translation_verse: n‑yɛtti ba, ni, “Ya di sa'am tingbaŋŋu maa, bee teeku, bee tiisi la, see ka man daalim Naawunni niriba n‑niŋi ba gbeeri maa n‑naai, ni, Naawunni n su ba.”

english_verse: and said, “Amen! Praise, glory, wisdom, thanks, honor, power, and strength belong to our God forever and ever! Amen!”
translation_verse: n‑yɛtta#:- “Naawunni ti Dugma su la yɛlisuma, ni gyirima, ni yam, ni puusigu, ni gyeeŋŋu, ni yiiko, ni yaa; Ka di bɛ la lala halli ni saha din ka tariga. - Ammi, ammi!”

english_verse: After this, I saw a large crowd with more people than could be counted. They were from every race, tribe, nation, and language, and they stood before the throne and before the Lamb. They wore white robes and held palm branches in their hands,
translation_verse: Anŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka ninvugbɔbgu daa bɛ naam zizii maa tooni n‑zɛya, ni Pɛbila maa tooni. Niriba wa daa nyɛ la bura pam niriba, ni zuliya pam, ni tinsi pam niriba, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ka ba daa yɛ situripeela n‑zɛya, ka daa mari abe tiisi wula ba nuusi ni.

english_verse: as they shouted, “Our God, who sits upon the throne, has the power to save his people, and so does the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa kaasiri pampam n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Naawunni ti Dugma kpalim n‑ziya, ka ŋɔn ni Pɛbila maa n su tiliginsim yɛla zaa.”

english_verse: The angels who stood around the throne knelt in front of it with their faces to the ground. The elders and the four living creatures knelt there with them. Then they all worshiped God
translation_verse: Di saha ka malaakadima zaa ban daa zɛ n‑gyiligi naam zizii maa daa gbaani tiŋŋa, ban ni kpambaya maa ni bunvuya anaasi la m‑paasi, ka ba zaa daa gyeemni Naawunni

english_verse: One of the elders asked me, “Do you know who these people are that are dressed in white robes? Do you know where they come from?”
translation_verse: Ka kpamma maa ninvugsɔ daa boosi ma n‑yɛli, ni, “Ninvugseeba ban yeeri situripeela wa - bandima n nyɛ ba? Ni, yani ka ba yi na?”

english_verse: “Sir,” I answered, “you must know.” Then he told me: “These are the ones who have gone through the great suffering. They have washed their robes in the blood of the Lamb and have made them white.
translation_verse: Ka n daa labsi n‑yɛli u, ni, “M Ba, nyini m mi di.” Ka u daa yɛli ma, ni, “Tɔ; ba nyɛ la ninvugseeba ban yi wahala diibu toom ni na; Pɛbila maa ziim ka ba daa zaŋŋi m‑peaai ba situra, ni a ti pɛligi nyain lala.

english_verse: And so they stand before the throne of God and worship him in his temple day and night. The one who sits on the throne will spread his tent over them.
translation_verse: Lala zugu kyɛ ka ba bɛ la Naawunni naam zizii maa tooni, ka ba gyeemni u wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa, Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni: Ka Naawunni kpalim m‑bɛ ba saani n‑ziya; U zi la naam zizii maa ni saha kam.

english_verse: They will never hunger or thirst again, and they won't be troubled by the sun or any scorching heat.
translation_verse: Kɔm ku laa m‑mari ba, kɔnyuuri mun ku laa m‑mari ba gba: Halli wuntaŋŋa ku laa m‑bii n‑niŋi ba, bee, n‑kyɛ ka wɔligu wumni ba.

english_verse: “The Lamb in the center of the throne will be their shepherd. He will lead them to streams of life-giving water, and God will wipe all tears from their eyes.”
translation_verse: Dama Pɛbila maa bɛ la naam zizii puuni, ka u nin guri ba suŋŋa hoo pɛgura ŋɔn guri u peesi seem; U nin kyɛ ka ba dɔli kɔsuŋŋu bulisi n‑nya nyɔvuri, ka u laa n‑nyeesiri ba nini nintam n‑tiri ba sahakam.”

english_verse: When the Lamb opened the seventh seal, there was silence in heaven for about half an hour.
translation_verse: Ka sahanseaa ka Pɛbila maa daa lakki buntabinni kan paasiri buyɔpɔi la, arizana puuni zaa ka di daa fɔ surimm n‑yuui beela.

english_verse: I noticed that the seven angels who stood before God were each given a trumpet.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka n daa nya malaakadima ayɔpɔi seeba ban zɛ Naawunni tooni maa, ka ba daa zaŋŋi kikaasi ayɔpɔi n‑ti ba.

english_verse: Another angel, who had a gold container for incense, came and stood at the altar. This one was given a lot of incense to offer with the prayers of God's people on the gold altar in front of the throne.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ daa yi n‑kyɛ na nti zaani gyeeŋŋu teebuli maa tooni, m‑mari salima turaalinyooriga. Ka ba daa zaŋŋi turaali zum pam n‑ti u ni u ti nyɔa, ni, lala ka u nin ti maali Naawunni niriba wunsusigu zaa n‑tisi, salima gyeeŋŋu teebuli maa zugu - kan zɛ naam zizii maa tooni la.

english_verse: Then the smoke of the incense, together with the prayers of God's people, went up to God from the hand of the angel.
translation_verse: Ka turaali la nyoobu nyoosi daa yi malaaka maa nuu ni n‑du Naawunni tooni, ni Naawunni niriba maa wunsusigu zaa m‑paasi.

english_verse: After this, the angel filled the incense container with fire from the altar and threw it on the earth. Thunder roared, lightning flashed, and the earth shook. The Trumpets
translation_verse: Ka di nyaaŋŋa ka malaaka maa daa gbeaai bugum teebuli maa zugu m‑paali u bunni, ka zaŋŋi n‑lɔbi tingbaŋŋu ni na; ka saa daa nya'asi ka tasi m‑mari vuuri pam, ka tingbaŋŋu mun daa dam.

english_verse: The seven angels now got ready to blow their trumpets.
translation_verse: Di saha ka malaakadima ayɔpɔi, kikaasi daandima maa, daa niŋi siri ni ba peebi#:

english_verse: When the first angel blew his trumpet, hail and fire mixed with blood were thrown down on the earth. A third of the earth, a third of the trees, and a third of all green plants were burned.
translation_verse: Malaaka tooni daana maa ŋɔn daa peebi, ka sakuga ni bugum daa gyɛri ni ziim n‑kpaari n‑luri tingbaŋŋu ni na. Ka duniya wa daa puri buta, ka purigri yinni daa di bugum, ni ka tiisi, ka duniya mɔmaasa zaa gba daa di bugum.

english_verse: When the second angel blew his trumpet, something like a great fiery mountain was thrown into the sea. A third of the sea turned to blood,
translation_verse: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buyi zugu daa peebi, ka bunseelli hoo zɔtitaari kan diri bugum daa lɔbi n‑lu teeku koom ni na. Ka teeku maa yi puri buta, ka ka purigri yinni daa leebi ziim;

english_verse: a third of the living creatures in the sea died, and a third of the ships were destroyed.
translation_verse: ka teeku koom ni bunvuya purigri buta gba daa kpi, ka nyarima purigri buta daa sa'am.

english_verse: When the fourth angel blew his trumpet, a third of the sun, a third of the moon, and a third of the stars were struck. They each lost a third of their light. So during a third of the day there was no light, and a third of the night was also without light.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa bunaasi daa peebi, ka wuntaŋŋa purigri buta daa ŋmɛa, ni namboaa gba dinni, ka saŋmariwuusi gba daa puri buta ka a purigri yimmu daa ŋmɛa. Ka wuntaŋŋa saha yi puri buta, ni yuŋŋu gba, ka a purigri buta yinni yinni daa kɔŋŋi neesim di zugu.

english_verse: Then I looked and saw a lone eagle flying across the sky. It was shouting, “Trouble, trouble, trouble to everyone who lives on earth! The other three angels are now going to blow their trumpets.”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka di saha maa ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka wubga daa yigri saazugu agbaa ziiya; ka n daa wum ka u tasiri pampam n‑yɛtti, “Yɛla! Yɛla! Yɛla! Dabeem yɛla nin paai ban bɛ duniya puuni, sahanseaa malaakadima ata ban kpalim la yi ti peebi ba kikaasi!”

english_verse: When the fifth angel blew his trumpet, I saw a star fall from the sky to earth. It was given the key to the tunnel that leads down to the deep pit.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa bunu zugu la daa peebi. Ka n daa nya saŋmariwuseaa: ka ka daa lu n‑yi saazugu m‑paai tingbaŋŋu ni na, ka daa deaai saafibii kan kparigri bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa noori la.

english_verse: As it opened the tunnel, smoke poured out like the smoke of a great furnace. The sun and the air turned dark because of the smoke.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yoaai bɔ'azuliŋŋu noori maa, ka nyoosi pam daa yi na, halli n‑ligi wuntaŋŋa ni saazugu zaa neesim, hoo bugum ŋɔn diri moo puuni n‑yiisiri nyoosi seem.

english_verse: Locusts came out of the smoke and covered the earth. They were given the same power that scorpions have.
translation_verse: Ka bunyigra hoo toori daa yi nyoosi maa ni n‑sigi na, ka ba daa kyɛ ka toori maa mari yiiko hoo naŋŋa dumpu taabu.

english_verse: The locusts were told not to harm the grass on the earth or any plant or any tree. They were to punish only those people who did not have God's mark on their foreheads.
translation_verse: Ba daa yɛli ba, ni, ba di niŋi moori, bee bumbula, bee tiisi seelli, amaa ninsaaliseeba ban ka Naawunni daalimni ba gbeeri ni la ka ba daamna.

english_verse: The locusts were allowed to make them suffer for five months, but not to kill them. The suffering they caused was like the sting of a scorpion.
translation_verse: Toori maa daa ka soori ni ba ku lala niriba maa, amaa ni ba namsi ba gɔya anu. Ba namsigu maa beerim ŋmanni la naŋŋa dumpu beerim.

english_verse: In those days people will want to die, but they will not be able to. They will hope for death, but it will escape from them.
translation_verse: Lala gɔya anu maa puuni ka ba nin bɔ kum ziiya n‑kɔŋŋi: ba nin boori ni ba kpi, amaa kum nin zɔ ka kyɛ ba.

english_verse: These locusts looked like horses ready for battle. On their heads they wore something like gold crowns, and they had human faces.
translation_verse: Toori wa muna - ba daa ŋmanni la wuriseeba ban maanni siri ni ba kyaŋŋi tɔppu ni; ba zugri pili la bunseesi hoo salima naam zupilisi, ka ba nindaasi ŋmanni ninsaala nindaa.

english_verse: Their hair was like a woman's long hair, and their teeth were like those of a lion.
translation_verse: Ba zɔbri daa ŋmanni la pɔ'a zɔbwɔ'ala la, ka ba nyina ŋmanni gbigma nyina.

english_verse: On their chests they wore armor made of iron. Their wings roared like an army of horse-drawn chariots rushing into battle.
translation_verse: Ba nyaari daa ligi la bunseesi an ŋmanni kutti dawɔ'ari, ka ba mari n‑ta'ari piima ni kuga toobu ni; ka ba kpunkpama vuuri daa ŋmanni la wuri pam ŋɔn datti tarɔkudima n‑zɔri n‑wa tɔppu ni ka a mari vuuri seem la.

english_verse: Their tails were like a scorpion's tail with a stinger that had the power to hurt someone for five months.
translation_verse: Ba zuya daa ŋmanni la nansi zuya, ka ba dumpu daa ŋmanni la nansi dumpu, halli ba zuya puuni ka ba yiiko daa bɛ ni, ni ba ti daamni niriba maa gɔya anu maa.

english_verse: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa mari la naaba ŋɔn gɔsiri ba: ŋɔna n nyɛ malaakasɔ ŋɔn su bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa fukumsi la. U yuuri Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a ni boonni, ni, ‘Abaddɔn’, ka Griiktiri yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ni, ‘Apɔliyɔn’ .

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka daŋŋni dabeem yɛlli maa gaariya; amaa ka nyaaŋŋa ka dabeem yɛla ayi n yɛ n‑dɔla.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buyoobu daa peebi, ka n daa wum kukɔri ka ka tɔ'asiri n‑yi gyeeŋŋu teebuli maa luga anaasi maa zaa ni na - salima bunseaa kan zɛ Naawunni tooni maa nla.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Ka kukɔri maa daa booli malaaka maa ŋɔn pa buyoobu zugu ka mari u kikaa maa; n‑yɛli u, ni, “Basim malaakadima anaasi ban daa ba seeba n‑dooli Yufreetisi mɔ'atitaari la ni la!”

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Bugum, ni nyoosi, ni farinwuta an daa yiiri wuri maa nɔya ni na maa - anŋɔ abata zaa n daa nyɛ an daa ku niriba purigri buta la yimmu.

english_verse: The horses had powerful mouths, and their tails were like poisonous snakes that bite and hurt.
translation_verse: Dama wuri maa yiiko daa bɛ la ba nɔya ni ba zuya ni. Ba zuya maa ŋmanni la wugi, m‑mari zugri: anŋɔ ka ba mari n‑daamni niriba maa.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Di saha maa ka ninsaaliseeba daa kpalim, baseeba yɛlibeeri wa daa bu ku ba. Di zaa yɔri ba daa bu takki ba teesibeeri ni ba tumbeeri sɔya beebu, amaa ba daa kpalim n‑gyeemni wunseesi ba mammansi ŋɔn maali la. Ba daa bu kyɛ arizimbeeri gyeeŋŋu, bee bugseesi ban zaŋŋi salima m‑maali la, bee anzulifa, daanya, kuga, bee daari m‑maali, halli ni anŋɔ zaa ku toaai n‑nyara, bee n‑wumna, bee n‑kyɛnna.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka di saha maa ka n daa nya malaaka kpeeŋŋu daansɔa, ka u yi arizana ni n‑sigri na. Ka u gɔbi la sangbaŋŋu hoo kyinkyingɔbrigu, ka sasuu daa pili u zugu; u nindaa daa ŋmanni la wuntaŋŋa ka u gbaya ŋmanni langbindaari bugum.

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: U daa zali litaafibila fiiŋ, ka ka yoaai ni m‑pa'ai u nuu zugu. Ka u zaŋŋi u nɔba maa n‑tɔm duniya wa ni: u nudirigu dinni mun noori teeku zugu, ka seaa la noori tingbankuumni zugu.

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: Ka u tasi m‑booli kukɔzuliŋŋu, kan zulisim ŋmanni gbigimni nyuriŋŋu. Ka ŋɔn daa booli lala maa, satasinsi buyɔpɔi daa zaŋŋi ba gba kunkuma n‑tasi n‑sakki.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Ban daa tasi n‑naai, n daa ya sɔbi la ban yɛli seem maa, amaa n daa wum kukɔri ŋɔn tɔ'asiri n‑yiiri arizana ni na n‑yɛtti, ni, “Satasinsi buyɔpɔi maa ŋɔn yɛli seesi maa - a nyɛ la yɛlisɔ'ara. Di sɔbi a n‑dooli!”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka sɔ man daa pun nya, sɔ ŋɔn daa zɛ n‑tɔm teeku ni tingbaŋŋu maa zugu maa - ŋɔna daa ‘zaŋŋi u nudirigu n‑wukki saazugu

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: m‑pɔ Naawunni, Naawunni ŋɔn nɛ m‑bɛ ni saha din ka tariga la’, ka daa naam saazugu, ni tingbaŋŋu, ni teeku, ni bunseelli kam din bɛ a ni zaa. U daa pɔ lala n‑yɛli, ni, “Guusigu laa n‑ka ni!

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: See ka malaaka ŋɔn pa buyɔpɔi la yi peebi u kikaa maa: lala saha ka Naawunni nin tum u tunsɔ'arigu la n‑naai hoo ŋɔn daa pun yɛli n‑ti anabidima maa seem, ban daa nyɛ u tuntumniba maa.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Ka n daa laa n‑wum kukɔseaa kan daa tɔ'asi n‑yi arizana ni na la, ka ka yɛtti n‑tiri ma yaasa, ni, “Kyɛma n‑zaŋŋi litaafiseaa kan yoaai m‑bɛ malaaka maa nuu ni - malaakasɔ ŋɔn tɔm teeku ni tingbaŋŋu zugu la.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka n daa kyaŋŋi nti yɛli malaaka maa, ni, u tim ma litaafibila maa. Ka u daa yɛli ma, ni, “Deaam n‑ŋɔbi! Ka nin leebi bunseaa kan tɔ i puuri ni, halli ni i noori ni ka ka ya m‑malisa hoo siiri.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔna n daa deaai litaafibila maa u nuu ni n‑ŋɔbi, ka ka siri daa malisa n noori ni hoo siiri, amaa man daa vɔli n‑naai maa, ka daa leebi buntoori n puuri ni.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yɛli ma, ni, “See ka i laa n‑tɔ'asiri anabitiri yɛla zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi tinsi pam niriba, ni bura pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni nadima pam.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Amaa di ma'asi dundɔŋŋu kan bɛ Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa nyaaŋŋa la; kyɛli ka kpaŋŋa sa! Dama ba zaŋŋi ka n‑ti tinzuna dima, ka ba nin kpɛ tinkasi maa puuni na n‑kyɛnni n‑noori ka n‑niŋi gɔya pisinaasi ni ayi.

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Ban n nyɛ ɔlivii kpaam tiisi ayi maa, ni firilatɔmna ayi an zaantiri ti Dugma Ŋɔn Su Duniya Wa Zaa tooni la.

english_verse: Any enemy who tries to harm them will be destroyed by the fire that comes out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Ka sɔ yi ti boori u namsi ba, bugum nin yi ba noori ni na n‑di ba dindaandima wa; sɔ yi boori ni u namsi ba, di sumni ni di daama kpi lala.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Niriba ayi wa mari yiiko ni ba kpatti sangbana, ka saa ku mi dabsiseesi ban ya tɔ'asiri ti Dugma yɛla maa. Ba laa m‑mari yiiko kulisi zugu, n‑nin toaai n‑kyɛ ka a koom leebi ziim. Ka ba laa n‑toaai n‑kyɛ ka dabeem yɛla balibu kam ti niŋi duniya maa, ka a maali n‑niŋi sahaseaa sɛharadima wa sufu yi boora.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Ka ba yi ti mooli ba anabitiri yɛla n‑naai, bunkɔbsɔ nin yi bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa ni n‑du na nti tuusi ba tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi ba n‑ku ba.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Ba nin kpi n‑kpalim n‑dɔ tintitaari maa sɔroo la puuni, tinseaa ban daa zaŋŋi sɛharadima wa dugma maa n‑kpaasi n‑tabli dɔpulli zugu ka puuni maa. Tiŋŋa wa niriba tumbeeri zugu ka ba pa ka yupaasi, ni, ‘Sɔdoom’, bee, ‘Igyipti’.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Ka tinsi pam niriba ti gɔsi, ni zuliya pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni bura pam. Ba gɔsiri ban kpalim n‑dɔya seem n‑niŋi dabsa ata ni purigri, ka lala saha maa ba ku sakki ni ba sɔ'ai ba.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Ka duniya wa niriba sufu yi maai pam ba kum maa zugu. Ba diri la kyuu ka tiri taaba piini, dama anabidima ayi wa daa nyɛ seeba ban daa diisi duniya dima wahala pam.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Amaa daba ata ni purigri nyaaŋŋa, Naawunni nyɔvuri vuusim daa kpɛ ba na, ka ba daa ti doaai n‑zaani ba gbaya zugu ka dabeem pam gbaai seeba ban nya ba maa.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Di saha ka anabidima ayi maa daa wum kukɔri ka ka yi arizana ni na n‑yɛli ba, “Ya duuma kpa na!” ka ba siri daa kpɛ sangbaŋŋu ni n‑du n‑kyaŋŋi arizana ni; ka ba dindaandima nini daa nya.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Ka di nyaaŋŋa ka tingbaŋŋu daa dam, ka tintitaari maa purigri yinni piiya zugu daa lu. Niriba tusa ayɔpɔi daa kpi tingbaŋŋu dampu maa ni, ka dabeem gbaasi ban kpalim la, ka ba pɛ'ai Naawunni Arizana Daana maa.

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Dabeem yɛlli buyi zugu gaariya, ka din pa buta zugu kyɛnni na ka bu ya n‑yuui.

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Ka malaaka ŋɔn paasiri buyɔpɔi daa peebi u kikaa, ka kukɔya daa tasiri n‑yiiri arizana ni na n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Duniya zaa naam leebi ti Dugma naam, Ni ŋɔn daa diisi sɔ Masiiya, Yisa, dinni. U naam ku ti mari baasigu Halli saha din ka tariga.”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Ti puusiri i ni, ti Dugma Naawunni, Nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa - Nyin ŋɔn bɛ ni, ka daa pun bɛ ni. I deaari la i yiiko din galisi; Pampaŋŋɔ ka i di naam.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Tinsi niriba suuri yisigiya Amaa nyin gba suuri saha paaya - Sahaseaa nyin zaŋŋi ban kpi la n‑kari ba zaa sariya: Sahaseaa nyin zaŋŋi laara ni piinisuma n‑tiri anabidima ban daa tum n‑ti i la, n‑tiri i niriba, ni ban zɔri i yuuri la, n‑tiri ba zaa, birikyindima ni tarimma zaa. Saha paaya ni i ti zaŋŋi ban daa sa'am duniya maa n‑sa'am ba gba yɛla zaa.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Naawunni arizana puuni Gyeeŋŋu Duu daa yoaai, ka adakka maa bɛ bɛni, Naawunni nangbanyinni yɛla bunni la. Ka saa daa nya'asi ka tasi m‑mari vuuri pam, ka tingbaŋŋu mun daa dam, ka sakuga daa lu na, bunkara ni buntibsa pampam.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a maa daa mari la puuwa, ka u bɛ la dɔ'am wahala ni - dɔ'am beerim n daa mari u, ka u kaasiri di zugu.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Ka alahazibu yɛliseaa yaasa daa bɛ arizana puuni n‑yi na, ka bunseelli kan ŋmanni Nyɛbga m bɛ ni, ka u daa galisi pam; bunzeoo n daa nyɛ u, ka u mari zugri ayɔpɔi ni yiila piiya; ka ba daa zaŋŋi bunveela ayɔpɔi n‑vili u zugu hoo kubibɔbsi la.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Ka u zuuri daa daari saŋmariwuusi purigri yimmu buta zugu m‑basiri tingbaŋŋu maa na. Ka Nyɛbga wa zɛ pɔ'a maa tooni u dɔ'am maa saha, ni, u yi ti dɔ'ai bii maa sahanseaa, ŋɔn nin ŋɔbi u.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Pɔ'a maa daa dɔ'aya: u dɔ'ai la doowa, ka u bii maa n nyɛ sɔ ŋɔn ti ya n‑su duniya tinsi zaa naam, ka zaŋŋi u kutti damni n‑kari ba sariya. Ka ba daa fa u ya'abi n‑taasi Naawunni saani, ŋɔn Naawunni naam zizii maa ya'ari sa.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa mun daa zɔ n‑kpɛ yoo ni, seela Naawunni ŋɔn daa pun maali u beebu ziiya siri n‑ti u. Bɛni ka ba nin guri u ka diri dabsa tusiri ni kɔbsiyi ni pisiyoobu la.

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Di saha ka tɔppu daa doaai arizana ni, ka Maikyɛli ni u malaakadima tuusiri Nyɛbkarigu wa, ka ŋɔna ni u malaakadima gba daa tuusi ba.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Amaa Nyɛbga dima maa daa bu nyaŋŋi, halli ka ba daa bu laa n‑nya beebu ziiya arizana ni.
