english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Ba daa tɔ Nyɛbkarigu wa m‑basi tiŋŋa; ŋɔna n nyɛ kurilim ni sa Waafu la, ban boonni sɔ ‘Yibrisi’, bee ‘Sintaani’ la, ka u yɔmsiri la duniya wa zaa. Ba daa tɔ u m‑basi na, ŋɔna ni u malaakadima zaa.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum, ka kukɔri tɔ'asiri arizana puuni, n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Pampaŋŋɔ tiliginsim nyɛ la Naawunni dinni - Yiiko ni naam nyɛ la u dinni, Fukumsi nyɛ la u Masiiya gba dinni! Dama sɔ ŋɔn daa galim ti mabiisi la luya, U daa galimni ba Naawunni saani, Wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu zaa ka u daa galimni ba!

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Pɛbila ziim la zugu ka ba nyaŋŋi u - Ba nyaŋŋi u Naawunni yɛla la zugu; Dama ba daa moonni Naawunni yɛla - ban daa nya ka wum u saani yɛttɔ'aseesi la. Ba pa la seeba ban boori ba nyɔvuya Halli ni ba kum ni.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Yan Arizana maa, ya mari sufupeelli, Ya mari sufupeelli, yaseeba ban bɛ bɛni! Ya mun tingbaŋŋu, ni teeku, ya nya zugbeoo, Dama Sintaani sigi ya saani na; U suuri yi yisigi pampam; Dama u mi, ni, u saha ku laa n‑yuui!”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Nyɛbga wa ŋɔn daa ti baŋŋi, ni, ba zaŋŋi u m‑basi la duniya ni na, u daa kari pɔ'a maa nɔba, pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn dɔ'ai bidibga maa.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa zaŋŋi wubga kpunkpanyigra ayi n‑ti pɔ'a maa, ni u ti yikki saazugu n‑kpɛ yoo ni m‑paai u maŋtoori ziiya; ka ba ti guri u bɛni n‑niŋi saha yinni, saha buyi ni purigri, ka Wa'abeoo maa ku ti paai u na.

english_verse: The snake then spewed out water like a river to sweep the woman away.
translation_verse: Ka Nyɛbga wa daa tusiri koom pam n‑yiiri u noori ni na hoo mɔ'ari, ka koom maa daa pɔbri n‑dɔli pɔ'a maa nyaaŋŋa ni bu ti di u.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Amaa tingbaŋŋu daa suŋŋi pɔ'a maa, ka daa yaai ka noori n‑nyu koom maa, koom bun pɔbri n‑yiiri Nyɛbga la noori ni na maa.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Nyɛbga maa daa gyɛ suuri n‑niŋi pɔ'a maa ka daa kyaŋŋi ni u ti lɔ tɔppu n‑tuusi u biseeba ban kpalim la zaa, ban dɔli Naawunni zaaligu zaa deede, ka zɛ yummu m‑moonni Yisa yɛla maa.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Ka u daa zɛ teekuduuri mɔ'abisigu zugu.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya bunkɔbsɔ yaasa, ka u daa yiiri teeku koom maa ni na. U daa mari yiila piiya ni zugri ayɔpɔi, ka bunveela daa vili u yiila maa yinni yinni zaa. Ka yubeeri m bɛ u zugri ni n‑tuuri Naawunni.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Man daa nya bunkɔbsɔ wa daa ŋmanni la kaliiya, amaa u nɔba ŋmanni la bunkɔbsɔ nɔbkara an nunyeesi wa'a pam; u noori mun ŋmanni la gbigimni dinni. Nyɛbkarigu maa daa zaŋŋi u maŋtoori yiiko ni u naam zizii maa n‑ti bunkɔbbeoo wa, ka zaŋŋi fukumsi pam n‑ti u.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Tɔ, ka bunkɔbgu wa zugri maa yinni daa bɛ la hoo ban daa pun kɔrigi ka; amaa u kum daŋŋa maa daa gaari ni, ka lala daa gyɛri duniya niriba zaa pam, ka ba daa dɔli bunkɔbbeoo maa nyaaŋŋa.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Saalidima daa gyeemni Nyɛbga maa, ŋɔn daa zaŋŋi lala yiiko n‑ti bunkɔbbeoo maa zugu, ka ba daa laa n‑gyeem bunkɔbgu maa, ni, “Ŋɔni n bɛ ni n‑ŋmani hoo bunkɔbgu wa? Sɔsɔ ku toaai n‑zabi n‑nyaŋŋi u!”

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Ka u daa yaai u noori n‑tɔ'asiri yɛlibeeri n‑tuuri Naawunni, ni u ti sa'am Naawunni yuuri, ka tuuri u beebu ziiya ni seeba ban bɛ arizana ni maa.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Ba daa laa n‑ti u soori ni u ti tuusi tɔppu n‑nyaŋŋi Naawunni niriba, ka u su fukumsi m‑mari zuliya pam, ni tinsi pam niriba, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni bura pam.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Ka sɔkam ŋɔn bɛ duniya tingbaŋŋu zugu zaa nin gyeemni u, ŋɔnkam yuuri bu sɔbi n‑dooli nyɔvuri bukku maa ni; lala bukku ka Pɛbila maa mara, Pɛbilisɔ ban daa kɔrigi duniya piiligu ni halli ni zuna.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Ŋɔn mari tuba n‑wumna, ŋɔn wumma!

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Ba yi ya n‑gbaai sɔ daabri, Ban gbaam di daama! Ba yi ya n‑zaŋŋi takoobi n‑ku sɔ, Ban kuum di daama! Dizugu see ka Naawunni niriba mari sufukpeemni, See ka ba zɛ yummu n‑niŋi yɛdda.

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka n daa laa n‑nya bunkɔbsɔ yaasa, ka u yi tingbantiŋni n‑du na. U daa mari la yiila ayi, kaman pɛbila, amaa u daa tɔ'asiri kaman Nyɛbkarigu la.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Ka u daa deaai daŋŋni bunkɔbbeoo maa yiiko, halli nti su u fukumsi tuma zaa n‑tumni n‑tiri u. Ka ŋɔn daa deaai u yiiko maa, u daa kyɛ ka duniya ni niriba zaa gyeemni daŋŋni bunkɔbgu maa, sɔ ŋɔn daa daŋŋi n‑yi na ka u kum daŋŋa nya tippu maa.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: U daa tum makkri tunnyalima pam, halli nti kyɛ ka bugum yi saazugu n‑sigi tingbaŋŋu ni na, ka daadam nya.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Ka makkri seesi ŋɔn nya yiiko n‑tumni n‑tiri daŋŋni bunkɔbgu la, ana n daa yɔmsiri duniya dima. Ka u yɛli ba, ni, ba maalim bunni ka ka ŋmanni bunkɔbgu la, bunkɔbsɔ ŋɔn daa nya takoobi daŋŋa ka laa n‑neaai maa.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa ti u yiiko ni u kyɛ ka ban daa maali bunseaa la ti nɛ ka vuusira, ka laa n‑toaai n‑tɔ'asira. Ka kyɛ ka ba ku ninvugseeba ban ku sakki n‑gyeem bummaannigu kan ŋmanni bunkɔbgu la zaa.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Ka laa n‑kyɛ ka sɔkam deaai la daalimni u nudirigu ni, bee, u gbeoo ni; ni, ba zaa deaama - tarimma ni birikyindima, bundaandima ni faradima, ban su ba mansi ni daaba zaa.

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: Ni, sɔ ku toaai n‑daari neema, bee, n‑koosira, na'ala u mari la daalimni maa, di nyɛ la bunkɔbbeoo yuuri la, bee, u yuuri maa kaaligu namba la.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: [Kpa wa n nyɛ yam yɛlli; kyɛ ka ŋɔn mari hankali baŋŋi bunkɔbgu kaaligu namba gbinni: u kaaligu kɔbsiyoobu ni pisiyoobu ni ayoobu - din wulisiri la ninsaalisɔa yɛla.]

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum kukɔri ŋɔn yi arizana ni na, din ŋmanni kɔpɔbrim pam damni, bee saa ŋɔn tasi pampam; halli man daa wum kukɔseaa la daa ŋmanni la hoo goongyidima ban ŋmeeri ba bunŋmeera.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yuumni yuumpaalli naam zizii maa tooni, ni bunvuya anaasi ni kpambaya maa tooni. Sɔ daa bu nyaŋŋi m‑bamsi lala yuumni maa, na'ala niriba tusa kɔbga ni pisinaasi ni anaasi la, ban daa pun yɔ seeba samni n‑tiligi ba duniya wa ni la.

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Ban n daa nyɛ seeba ban mari ba mansi suŋŋa ka bu boori pɔ'aba, ka ba daa bu di pɔ'aba. Ba daa dɔli Pɛbila maa n‑kyaŋŋi ŋɔn daa yi wa seelikam zaa. Ninsaalidima zaa sunsukkuni, ban ka ba daa daŋŋi n‑yɔ ba samni n‑tiligi n‑ti Naawunni ni Pɛbila maa.

english_verse: They never tell lies, and they are innocent. The Messages of the Three Angels
translation_verse: Ka ziri ka ba nɔya ni, dama galinsi ka ba saani halli beela.

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Ka n daa laa n‑nya malaakasɔ yaasa, ka u yigri sangbana zii ni, m‑mari lahabasuŋŋu ni u mooli n‑ti ninvugseeba ban bɛ duniya wa ni, halli n‑tiri bura pam, ni zuliya pam, ni yɛttɔ'azuna pam dima, ni tinsi pam niriba.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Ka u daa tasi pampam n‑yɛli, ni, “Ya zɔma Naawunni! Ka ti u gyirima, dama u sariya karibu saha la paaya! Ya gyeemma u, dama u daa naam saazugu ni tingbaŋŋu, ni teeku, ni kɔbulisi la zaa!”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka malaakasɔ, ŋɔn paasi buyi la, daa dɔli na n‑yɛtta, “Luuwa! Babɛlɔn tintitaari wa luya! Dama duniya wa zaa daa nyu u daam maa - u pɔ'agootiri daam bun mari yaa la.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Di saha ka malaakasɔ, ŋɔn paasi buta la, daa dɔli na n‑tasiri n‑yɛtta, “Sɔ yi gyeem bunkɔbbeoo bummaannigu la, u nin nyu Naawunni sugyeaa daam maa; sɔ yi deaai bunkɔbgu daalimni u gbeoo ni, bee, u nuu ni,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: ŋɔn gba nin nyu. Dama Naawunni maanni la u daam bun kpeem pam la, m‑boaari n‑niŋni u suyisigri ŋmanni maa puuni n‑tiri ba ni ba nyu. Ba nin laa n‑zaŋŋi ba n‑lɔsi farinwuta bugum ni, n‑diisi ba wahala pam ka Naawunni malaakasuma nin nya, ni Pɛbila maa.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Ka ba wahala bugum la nyoosi duuri la saazugu, zuna ni saha din ka tariga; ba ku mari vuusim, yuŋŋu bee wuntaŋŋa. Ban n nyɛ ban gyeemni bunkɔbbeoo maa ni u bummaannigu la, ni ban deaai u yuuri daalimni la.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Kpa wa ka ti nya Naawunni niriba ban zɛ yummu ka di sugru seem maa: ba kpalim n‑dɔli Naawunni zaaligu yɛla suŋŋa, ka niŋi yɛdda ni Yisa.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum ka kukɔri yiiri arizana ni na n‑yɛtta “Sɔbma dinŋɔa: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti kpiimma ban kpi ti Dugma zugu pampaŋŋɔ zaŋŋi n‑wa!” Ka Sii Kasi maa yɛli, “Ii! Zugsuŋŋu siri bɛ ni n‑tiri ba; dama ba vuusiri ba tunkpeema zugu, ka ba tunsuma laara dɔli ba!”

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka sangbampeelli daa bɛ ni, ka ninvugsɔ zi ka zugu, ka u ŋmanni la ninsaalimaŋni; ka u pili la salima naam zupiliga, ka gɔriga kan diri pam bɛ u nuu ni.

english_verse: An angel came out of the temple and shouted, “Start cutting with your sickle! Harvest season is here, and all crops on earth are ripe.”
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ daa yi Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa ni na, m‑boonni pampam n‑yɛtti n‑tiri ŋɔn zi sangbaŋŋu ni la, ni “Zaŋŋma i gɔriga maa m‑piili n‑ŋma duniya wa kyi, dama a ŋmaabu saha la yi paaya, ka duniya bunŋmaara bi suŋŋa.”

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ laa n‑yi arizana Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa ni na, ka ŋɔna gba mari la gɔriga kan diri pam.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Ka malaaka sɔ yaasa daa yi Gyeeŋŋu Duu teebuli maa ni na, ka u su bugum fukumsi. Ka u daa tasi m‑booli pampam n‑yɛli ŋɔn mari gɔriga maa, ni, “Zaŋŋma i gɔriga maa m‑piili tiwala la'asigu, tiwaliseesi an bɛ duniya zaa ni, ka a gba saha paaya!”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Dizugu malaaka maa daa zaŋŋi u gɔriga n‑gbaai duniya ni tiwala la'asigu. U daa zaŋŋi tiwala maa n‑lɔbi bɔ'aseaa ban niŋni a ka puuni n‑ŋma'asiri n‑yiisiri a koom la na ni ba ti maali daam - di nyɛ la Naawunni sugyeaa ziiya.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Ka ba daa nɔ a n‑ŋma'asi tintitaari maa nyaaŋŋa, ka ziim daa yi daam ŋma'asigu bɔ'ari maa na m‑pɔbra; halli ka i yi ti dɔli n‑kyaŋŋi maalisi kɔbsiyi, di na zulim pam; ka yoofu yi ti ya n‑kpɛ, ziim maa nin paai u noori la ziiya.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya alahazibu yɛlikarigu yaasa, ka di bɛ la arizana ni: n‑nya ka malaakadima ayɔpɔi daa bɛ ni, ka ba mari dabeem yɛla ayɔpɔi la, seesi an nyɛ baasigu dina, dama Naawunni ya n‑zaŋŋi a m‑baasi u sugyeaa tuma zaa.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: N daa laa n‑nya bunseelli ka di ŋmanni mɔ'ari ka ka koom ni bugum la'amni m‑bɛ ni. Ka niriba n zɛya n‑tabi bɛni: ba nyɛ la baseeba ban nyaŋŋi bunkɔbbeoo la, ni u bummaannigu la, ni u yuuri kaaligu namba. Ba nyaŋŋiya ka daa zɛ bɛni, m‑mari goongyidima ba nuusi ni, ni ba ti gyeemni Naawunni.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa yuumni Musa yuumni la, dɔsɔ ŋɔn daa tum Naawunni tuma la yuumni maa, ka laa n‑yuumni Pɛbila maa yuumni la, ni#:- “Alahazibu yɛlikara n nyɛ i tuma maa, Ti Dugma Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa! Yɛlimaŋni n nyɛ i sɔya, An tuusi n nyɛ a, Bura bura zaa niriba Naaba!

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: Ti Dugma, sɔ ka ni n ku zɔ dabeem Ka pɛ'ai i yuuri maa. Nyin kɔkɔ n nyɛ kasi. Bura kam dima nin kyɛ na Ni ba ti gyeemni i tooni. Dama i sariya karibu mari yɛlimaŋni Ka sɔkam zaa nya ka baŋŋi.”

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa ka n daa gɔsi n‑nya, ka ba daa yoaai Gyeeŋŋu Duu kan bɛ arizana ni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni la, Sugri yiri kan daa wulisiri Naawunni yɛla la nla.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Ka n nya malaakadima ayɔpɔi maa, ban mari baasigu dabeem yɛla ayɔpɔi la. Ba daa yɛ neenpeela faratiti, ka ba zaŋŋi la salima sɛloorisi n‑lɔ ba seesi.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Di saha ka bunvuya anaasi maa yinni daa zaŋŋi salima laasi ayɔpɔi n‑ti malaakadima wa, ka a paali ni Naawunni sugyeaa - ŋɔn Naawunni sɔ ŋɔn bɛ ni saha din ka tariga.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Ka Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa zaa daa paali ni nyoosi, an bɛ ni Naawunni gyirima ni u yiiko la zugu. Ka sɔ n daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa ni, halli ka dabeem yɛla ayɔpɔi ti niŋi n‑naai, seesi malaakadima maa ŋɔn mara.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa wum kukɔri kan tasiri n‑yiiri Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa ni na n‑tɔ'asiri n‑tiri malaakadima ayɔpɔi maa, n‑yɛtta, “Ya kyɛma n‑zaŋŋi ya laasi n‑kpaai Naawunni sugyeaa m‑basi duniya maa ni!”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Ka dizugu malaakasɔ ŋɔn daŋŋi tooni maa daa kyaŋŋi n‑zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi duniya wa ni na. Ka nɔra daa niŋi niriba, m‑beera, n‑wum ba pam; ninvugseeba nɔra maa ŋɔn daa niŋi maa daa nyɛ ban daa deaai bunkɔbbeoo la daalimni ka gyeemni ban daa maali bunseelli n‑ŋmanni u la maa.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ ŋɔn pa buyi zugu maa daa zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi teeku maa ni, ka teeku kooma zaa leebi la ziim hoo kpiima ziim, ka bunvurikam ŋɔn bɛ teeku koom maa ni daa kpi.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ ŋɔn pa buta zugu maa daa zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi kulisi ni kɔbulisi zaa ni, ka a gba kooma leebi ziim.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa wum malaakasɔ ŋɔn su kooma maa fukumsi ka u yɛtta#:- “Nyini n nyɛ ŋɔn bɛ ni, Ni ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni, Ka nyɛ kasi zaa: Nyin ŋɔn kari sariya seem wa, Di siri nyɛ la din tuusi.

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: Dama ba daa ku i ninvugsuma maa, Ba daa ku anabidima maa, Ka daa kyɛ ka ba ziim yi na. Dizugu i kyɛ ka ba nyuuri la ziim - Ba siri sum ni lala.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ ŋɔn pa bunaasi zugu maa daa zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi wuntaŋŋa la ni, ka kyɛ ka ka biisim diri niriba hoo bugum.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Halli ni wuntaŋŋa maa ŋɔn daa bii n‑diri niriba seem la zaa yɔri, ba daa bu takki ba teesibeeri ni ba ti ti Naawunni gyirima: ay! - ba daa tuuri u yuuri, ŋɔn daa su dabeem yɛla wa fukumsi zugu.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ ŋɔn pa bunu zugu maa daa zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi bunkɔbbeoo la zizii maa ni. Ka bunkɔbgu maa naam tiŋŋa daa zibgi, zimm! Ka u niriba daa ŋɔbri ba zulima ba beerim la zugu.

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa tuuri Naawunni sɔ ŋɔn bɛ arizana ni, n‑tɔ'asiri u yɛttɔ'abeeri ba wahala zugu, ni ba nɔra zugu. Ba daa bu takki ba sufu ni teesa ka kyɛ ba tumbeeri tuma.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ ŋɔn pa buyoobu zugu maa daa zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi mɔ'atitaari koom maa ni, di nyɛ la Yufreetisi mɔ'ari. Ka koom la zaa daa nyuusi m‑basi, m‑maali soori siri n‑ti naseeba ban ya yi wuntampusiri luga ni na.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa nya siisi ata ban nyɛ bumbeeri pam, ka ba daa ŋmanni lɔna. Ba daa yiiri la Nyɛbga la noori ni na, ni bunkɔbbeoo la noori, ni ziri anabi la noori ni na.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Arizimbeeri siisi n nyɛ ba, ka ba tumni tunnyalima n‑gyɛtti niriba. Ba yiiri n‑gyinni duniya wa zaa, ni ba ti la'asi duniya nadima maa taaba. Ba la'asi ba, ni ba ti tuusi tɔppu di Dabsiri maa - dindahinseaa ka Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa zaali n‑ti ba la.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: Ni, “Ya bu nya? N nin kyɛ na hoo na'ayiga ŋɔn kpeeri na seem. Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bu gbisiri goom, ka kpalim n‑yɛ u kyinkyina la - lala ka u ku kyɛnni yɔriyɔri ka ba gɔsiri u n‑diisi u vi.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Ka ba daa la'asi nadima maa taaba n‑kyaŋŋi ya'aseaa ban boonni Hibruuli yɛttɔ'a maa ni, ni, ‘Arimagyɛddɔn’ la.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Ka malaakasɔ ŋɔn pa buyɔpɔi zugu maa daa zaŋŋi u laa la n‑kpaai m‑basi pɛsigu maa ni. Di saha ka kukɔri daa tasi pampam n‑yiiri Gyeeŋŋu Duu maa ni na, naam zizii la saani, n‑yɛli, ni, “Di naaya!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Ka tintitaari maa daa bɔkki m‑puri buta, ka tinzundima tinsi lu la zaa. Di saha maa ka Babɛlɔn, tintitaari wa, yɛla daa nya teaari Naawunni saani, ka Naawunni daa kyɛ ka Babɛlɔn ti nyu daam u kɔbeaa puuni, bun nyɛ u sugyeaa ni u sufu yisigri maa.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Di saha ka malaakadima ayɔpɔi yinni - malaakaseeba ban daa mari laasi ayɔpɔi maa - malaaka yinni maa daa kyɛ n saani na nti yɛli ma, ni, n kyɛm na ka u nin wulisi ma pɔ'agoorli ŋɔn galisi la yɛla, pɔ'asɔ ŋɔn daa du n‑zi n‑tɔm kooma pam zugu la. Ni, u ti wulisi ma ban ya kari u sariya n‑lusi u seem.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Ni, ŋɔna n nyɛ sɔ duniya nadima zaa ŋɔn daa doontiri u saani la, ka duniya zaa daa bugi u pɔ'agootiri maa zugu hoo ban nyuuri daam m‑bugi seem maa.

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka Naawunni Sii daa na mari ma, ka malaaka maa daa zi ma n‑kyaŋŋi yoo ni. Ka n ti nya pɔ'asɔ ka u zi bunkɔbzeaa zugu, ka Naawunni tuubu yuya daa sɔbi n‑niŋi bunkɔbgu wa lugri kam, ka u mari u zugri ayɔpɔi ni yiila piiya.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Ka pɔ'a maa daa yɛ situriveela an nyɛ bunzeesi wuyiŋ ni bunzeesi zimizimi, ka ba paasi la salima ni mansɔ'asi kugveeliseesi an ligri zoaai pam, halli bumpeela gba. U daa zali salima kɔbeaa u nuu ni, ka u tumbeeri daa paali ka, ni u pɔ'agootiri da'ari yɛla maa zaa.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Ka yuseaa daa sɔbi m‑pa u gbeoo zugu - ka din daa sɔbi seem maa nŋɔa: ‘Babɛlɔn titaari’, ni, ‘Ŋɔn dɔ'ari la duniya zaa pɔ'agootiri ni da'ari yɛla zaa.’ - ka lala yuuri mari la ka gbinni, amaa ka sɔ'aya.

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya, ka pɔ'a maa nyuuri Naawunni niriba ziim m‑bugra, ni ninvugseeba ban daa mooli Yisa yɛla ban daa nya ka wum seesi maa - ba gba ziim ka u nyuura. Ka man daa nya lala maa ka di niŋi ma alahazibu pam.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa boosi ma, ni, “Bɔ zugu ka i kyɛ ka di niŋi i alahazibu? N nin wulisi i yɛlisɔ'ara maa gbinni ŋɔn nyɛ seem, zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi pɔ'a maa ni bunkɔbsɔ ŋɔn baara, ka u mari u zugri ayɔpɔi ni yiila piiya maa.”

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Ni, “Nyin nya bunkɔbsɔ maa nyɛ la ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni, ka laa n‑ka ni pampaŋŋɔa, ka u ti ya n‑du n‑yi bɔ'azuliŋŋu maa ni na. Amaa u kur wa la baasiyɔri ziiya. Ka duniya dimseeba ban yuya pa la an daa pun sɔbi nyɔvuri bukku maa puuni duniya piiligu saha maa - di nin niŋi ba alahazibu pam, ban gɔsi n‑nya bunkɔbbeoo maa yɛla, ni, ŋɔn daa bɛ ni, ka laa n‑ka ni, ka mun ya n‑kyɛ na.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: Tɔ! [Kpa wa ka nira yi zaŋŋi yam m‑baŋŋi di gbinni suŋŋasuŋŋa] bunkɔbgu zugri ayɔpɔi la, anŋɔ mun nyɛ kunkuna ayɔpɔi seesi zugu pɔ'a maa ŋɔn ziya maa. Ni nadima ayɔpɔi n nyɛ a gba.

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: Nadima maa mun sunsukkuni, ba niriba anu pun lu, ka ninvugyinni na bɛ ni, yinni mun na bu paai na, amaa u yi ti paai na sahanseaa u nin yuui beela.

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Ka bunkɔbgu maa muna - sɔ ŋɔn daa pun bɛ ni ka laa n‑ka ni la - ŋɔna n nyɛ ŋɔn paasi anii daana, halli ka u nyɛ la nadima ayɔpɔi maa sunsukkuni. Ka u wa la baasiyɔri ziiya.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: Nyin nya yiila piiya maa muna - tɔ, a gba nyɛ la nadima piiya. Ba na bu di naam, amaa ba nin ti ti ba yiiko, ka ba di naam m‑paasi bunkɔbgu zugu n‑niŋi saha beela.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Banŋɔ nin ti niŋi nangbanyinni ni taaba, n‑zaŋŋi ba fukumsi ni ba yiiko n‑niŋi bunkɔbbeoo maa nuu ni.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Ka banŋɔ zaa nin ti la'am taaba n‑tuusi Pɛbila maa tɔppu, amaa ka Pɛbila maa nin ti nyaŋŋi ba, dama u nyɛ la dugmadima zaa Dugma ni nadima zaa Naaba. Ka ban bɛ u saani maa nyɛ la ŋɔn booli ninvugseeba maa, ka u yiisi ba ka ba zɛ yummu u nyaaŋŋa.”

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Ka nyin nya bunkɔbsɔ ni u yiila piiya seem maa, ban nin laa n‑yaai pɔ'agoorli maa halli nti sa'am u yɛla zaa ka kyɛ u yɔriyɔri, ka ba nin toosi u niŋŋa n‑ŋɔbi ka naayi zaŋŋi din kpalim n‑nyɔ bugum.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Naawunni n daa zaŋŋi lala teesa n‑niŋi ba zaa sufu ni, ni ba ti tum ŋɔn Naawunni yuubu - ni ba ti niŋi nangbanyinni n‑zaŋŋi ba yiiko zaa n‑ti bunkɔbbeoo maa, halli nti paai sahanseaa Naawunni tuma ŋɔn ti tum n‑naai hoo ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: Halli ka nyin ŋɔn nya pɔ'asɔ la nyɛ la tintitaari maa - tinseaa kan su duniya nadima zaa fukumsi ni yiiko la.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa ka n daa nya malaaka sɔa, ka u yi arizana ni n‑sigri na. U daa mari yiiko pam, ka u gyirima veelim daa neaai, halli ka u neesim daa paali duniya wa zaa.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: U daa kaasiya pam, ni#:- “Luuwa! Babɛlɔn titaari luya! Arizimbeeri n yi mari ka; Siisi bunda'arisi yi bɛ la bɛni, Niinsi gba, bunda'arisi, yi bɛ bɛni, Ni ban yaai bunseesi zaa.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Dama duniya wa zaa daa nyu u daam maa - U pɔ'agootiri daam bun mari yaa la. Duniya nadima daa doontiri u ni, Ka duniya daa biisi daa nya arizikki U pɔ'agootiri ligri zugu.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Ka n daa laa n‑wum kukɔseaa kan tɔ'asiri n‑yi arizana ni na, ni#:- “Ya yim na, n niriba! Ya yim u saani na! Yan di la'am n‑tumni u tuma - U ala'akyi tumbeeri maa! Yan di kyɛ ka u tubdarigri paai ya na!

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: Dama u zaŋŋi u tumbeeri tuma n‑tɔm a taaba, Halli ka a ti paai arizana ni. Ka Naawunni teaai u ala'akyi maa yɛla.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Ya niŋma u hoo ŋɔn daa niŋi ya seem la! Ya yooma u ŋɔn daa namsi ya seem la taabu buyi! Ya zaŋŋma u maŋtoori dadukku M‑paali u kɔbeaa; paalima ka daam bun kpeem pam - Boaama bu taabu buyi n‑ti u ka u nyu!

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: U daa gɔsi u maŋŋa pam, U daa dɔli la u ningbina kɔrisi ni nimmoaa, Dizugu ya tim u wahala pam, Tim u sufusa'aŋŋu ni nimmoaa gba. Dama u yɛtti la u maŋŋa saha kam, ni#:- ‘Kpa ka n ziya, pɔ'a-naaba! M pa la pukoori, n sira bu kpi, N ku baŋŋi wahala ŋɔn bɛ seem!’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu dooribeoo nin ti mari u - Dahinyinni ka dooribeeri bɔbgu nin mari u gba#:- U niriba nin kpi, U sufu nin sa'am, U nin kɔŋŋi bundira, Ka di nyaaŋŋa ka bugum nin di u. Dama ti Dugma Naawunni kari u sariya - Ti Dugma Naawunni ŋɔn galisiya ni.”

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: Di zugu ka duniya wa nadima nin ti kum u zugu pampam. Ba daa la'am n‑tum u zina tuma ka dɔli u ningbinkɔrisi yɛla, ka pampaŋŋɔ bugum n diri u. Ba nin ti nya di nyoosi ŋɔn duuri saazugu seem ka kumni u zugu.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Ba nin zaani wa'aligu, dama u wahala maa gɔsibu nin ti kpeesi ba dabeem. Ba nin yɛli, ni#:- “Waay, waay, waay! Yɛlibeeri! Yɛlitoosi! Babɛlɔn tintitaari wa, Tinkpeeŋŋu wa! Saha beela puuni Ka ba kari i sariya n‑lusi i.”

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: Duniya wa daa biisi gba nin kum ka fabli n‑ti u, dama sɔ bu laa n‑daari ba neema yaasa.

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: Sɔ bu laa n‑daari ba salima, anzulifa, mansɔ'asi kugnya'ara ni a bumpeela; ba ku daari ba situra - kyinkyina an nyɛ faalafaala, agoo, ni taaru; bee, ba daari ba taabu daseesi an nyuuŋŋu nya'asa, bee, ba bunveela ban zaŋŋi wɔbnyina ni taabu veela m‑maali seesi la; bee, ba kutti neema, ba daanya neema, bee ba dangalikuga.

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: Ni#:- “Tiwaliseesi zaa nyin daa boora, Ka teesi, ni, a sum la nyin kɔkɔa - Anŋɔ zaa bɔrigiya. Arizikki ni bɔbri maa zaa - A zaa bɔrigi ka kyɛ i, I ku laa n‑nya a yaasa!”

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Daa biisi wa ban daa di Babɛlɔn daa maa n‑nya arizikki dizugu, ba siri nin zaani wa'aligu, dama ba zɔri la u tubdarigri maa. Ba nin kum ka fabli,

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: ka yɛli, ni#:- “Waay, waay, waay! Yɛlibeeri! Yɛlitoosi! - Zaŋŋi n‑ti tintitaari wa! U daa yi bɔbi la bɔbsuma - Situriveela zaa. U daa zaŋŋi salima nangɔkɔrisi n‑yɛa, Ka kpa tukparinya'ara gba.

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Saha beela puuni Ka u kɔŋŋi arizikki ana zaa!” Duniya wa nyarima dima zaa - ban su a, ni sɔkam ŋɔn kpeeri a maa, ni ŋɔn duusiri maa, bee, n‑tumni nyarima tuma teeku maa ni - ban gba daa zɛ wa'aligu

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Ba daa gbeaai tankpoo n‑niŋi ba zugri, sufusa'aŋŋu zugu, ka ba kumni ka kaasiri ka fabinna; ni#:- “Waay, waay, waay! Yɛlibeeri! Yɛlitoosi! - m‑paai tintitaari wa! Nyariŋŋu daandima zaa zugu - Dama ba daa nya arizikki i saani: Ban tumni teeku ni tuma la zaa, Ba daa leebi bundaandima I arizikki maa zugu. Saha beela puuni Ka i kɔŋŋi bunseelli kam zaa!

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka kpeeŋŋu daansɔ daa zaŋŋi kugtitaari hoo neeri taabu n‑lɔbi teeku ni, ka yɛli, ni#:- “Nŋɔ taabu ka ba nin ti lɔbi u - Ba nin ti lɔbi tintitaari wa, Babɛlɔn. Sɔ ku laa n‑nya u yaasa.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Yuuma ni bunŋmeera zaa - Ba ku laa n‑wum anŋɔ i saani; Kikaasi ni yiisi peebgu - Ba ku laa n‑wum anŋɔ i saani; Ban mi tuma zaa suŋŋa - Ba ku laa n‑nya ba i saani; Ban neemni zum damni - Ba ku laa n‑wum dinŋɔ i saani;

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Babɛlɔn wa nyari la wahala Dama bɛni ka ba daa yiisi ninvugsuma ziim; Bɛni ka ba daa ku anabidima, Ni Naawunni niriba, Ni ban daa ku seeba zaa duniya wa puuni maa.”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ nyaaŋŋa n daa wum vuuri pampam; di daa ŋmanni niriba pam ŋɔn tasiri n‑kaasiri seem, ka daa bɛ arizana ni n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Allɛluuya, ya pɛ'ama Naawunni! Tiliginsim nyɛ la Naawunni dinni, Naam gba nyɛ la u dinni Ni yiiko yaasa m‑paasi!

english_verse: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
translation_verse: U katti la sariya ni yɛlimaŋni, Ka lasim ka ni. Dama u lusi pɔ'agoorli wa, Pɔ'agoora toondaana wa, Sɔ ŋɔn daa sa'amni duniya wa zaa m‑basi zaa, u zina tuma zugu. Ka Naawunni lusi u n‑darigi u tubri Ŋɔn daa ku u niriba la zugu.”

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Ka ba daa laa n‑tasi yaasa, ni#:- “Allɛluuya, ya pɛ'ama Naawunni! Bugum diri n‑diri Babɛlɔn, Ka bu nyoosi duuri saazugu Zuna ni saha din ka tariga!”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka kukɔseaa yi naam zizii maa ni na n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Ya pɛ'ama Naawunni, Tin gyeemni sɔ maa! Yaseeba ban gyeemni u la, pɛ'ama! Yaseeba ban zɔri u la, pɛ'ama! - Halli yan poora ni yan galisi maa zaa.”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum vuuri din ŋmanni niriba pam kukɔya, bee kɔpɔbrim pam damni, bee saa ŋɔn tasi pampam, n‑yɛtti, ni#:- “Allɛluuya, ya pɛ'ama Naawunni! Dama ti Dugma Naawunni di la naam - Tin gyeemni Naawunni maa, Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Ya kyɛ ka ti deem ni sufumaasim, Ya kyɛ ka ti tiri u naam gyirima! Dama Pɛbila maa amriya saha paai na, Yisa Kirisita ya n‑di u pɔ'a maa. U pɔ'a mun maali u siri.

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa yɛli ma, ni, “Sɔbma: ‘Zugsuŋŋu daandima n nyɛ ban booli seeba n‑zuusi ni ba ti di Pɛbila amriya kyuu diibu maa!’ ” Ka u laa n‑yɛli m‑paasi, ni, “Naawunni yɛttɔ'amaŋni nŋɔa.”

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Dinŋɔ zugu n daa gbaani tiŋŋa u nɔba tooni, ni n gyeem u; amaa u daa yɛli ma, “Gɔsima! Di niŋi lala! N gba nyɛ la i taraana. Man ni nyini ni i taaba ban moonni ban nya ka wum bunseesi Yisa saani maa - ti zaa tumni Naawunni tuma. Gyeemni ŋɔn Naawunni!” Ban moonni Yisa yɛliseesi maa, ni anabidima yɛla, a nyɛ la yummu.

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka di saha maa n daa nya arizana ka a yoaai n‑zɛya. Ka wuripeelli daa bɛ ni, ka ŋɔn baari u maa yuuri boonni la ‘Yɛdda Daana’ ni ‘Yɛlimaŋni Daana’; ka u zaŋŋni yɛlimaŋni n‑katti sariya ka tuusiri tɔppu.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: U nini ŋmanni la bugum bunnya'ara, ka u zugu vili la bunveela pam an ŋmanni kubibɔbsi la. U mari yuuri ka ba sɔbi n‑niŋi u ni, ka sɔsɔ na zi u yuuri wa, na'ala u mammaŋŋa m mi.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: Ŋɔn yɛ kpariseaa maa tari la ziim. Ka u yuuri boonni ‘Naawunni Yɛligu’ maa.

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: Ban tuusiri tɔppu daa dɔli u nyaaŋŋa, baseeba ban bɛ arizana ni maa. Ba gba daa ba wuripeela ka yɛ situriveela bumpeeliseesi an nyɛ kasi.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Ka takoobi kan dira yiiri u noori ni na: kana ka u ya n‑zaŋŋi n‑kyɛ tinsi pam niriba; ni, “u nin zaŋŋi kutti damni n‑guri ba”, ni, u nin “tumni Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa daam dugbu ziiya u kɔkɔa, daam maa mun wulisiri la Naawunni suuri yɛla.”

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Ba sɔbi yuseaa n‑niŋi u kparigu, ni u kpiiligu ziiya, ni ‘Nadima Naaba, ni Dugmadima Dugma’.

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: Di saha ka n daa nya malaaka ka u zɛ wuntaŋŋa puuni ka kaasi m‑booli niinsi ban yigri m‑bɛ saazugu zaa, ni “Ya kyɛm na! Ya la'amma na n‑di Naawunni bundirigu!

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya ka bunkɔbbeoo maa, ni duniya wa nadima maa, daa la'am taaba, ban ni ba tɔbbiisi la, ni ba ti tuusi tɔppu n‑zabi ŋɔn ba yoofu maa ni u tɔbbiisi maa.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Amaa ba daa gbaai bunkɔbbeoo maa, ni ziri anabi ŋɔn daa tum alahazibu tuma bunkɔbgu wa yuuri ni maa. Lala alahazibu tuma maa ka u daa zaŋŋi n‑yɔmsi ninvugseeba ban daa deaai bunkɔbgu maa daalimni la, ni ban daa gyeemni bunseelli ban daa maali n‑ŋmanni u la maa. Ba daa zaŋŋi babayi zaa, ka ba na bɛ la ba nyɔvuri ni, n‑lɔsi farinwuta bugum mɔ'ari maa ni.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: Ka ban daa kpalim la muna - ba daa zaŋŋi takoobi maa n‑ku ba, ŋɔn baari yoofu maa takoobi maa, kan yi u noori ni na maa. Ka niinsi zaa daa di ba kpiima numni maa n‑tigi.

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: Di nyaaŋŋa n daa nya naam ziziisi, ka ba daa zi a zugu n‑deaai yiiko ni ba ti kari sariya. Ka n daa laa n‑nya niriba siisi - ban daa pun fikki seeba zugri la n‑ku ba, ban daa mooli Yisa tuma ni Naawunni yɛla lahabaari la zugu. Ban daa bu gyeem bunkɔbbeoo maa, bee u bummaannigu maa, ka daa bu deaai u daalimni ba gbeeri bee ba nuusi ni. Banŋɔ zaa daa doaai kum ni, ka ban ni Yisa daa di naam n‑yuui n‑niŋi yuuma tusiri.

english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Zugsuŋŋu daana n nyɛ ninvugsɔ ŋɔn la'am m‑bɛ bɛni maa, Naawunni ninvugkasi n‑nyɛ u. U bɛ la ban daŋŋi n‑doaai kum ni la sunsukkuni. Ka kum din paasiri buyi la ka yiiko lala niriba saani, amaa ba bɛ la niriba tooni n‑gyeemni Naawunni ni Kirisita, ka ban ni Yisa nin di naam yuuntusiri.

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: Ka u nin kyaŋŋi n‑gyinni duniya wa luga anaasi la zaa n‑yɔmsiri niriba ni u ti la'asi ba zaa na - halli Gɔgyi ni Magɔgyi dima gba - ka ba la'am na n‑kyaŋŋi tɔppu ni. Ka ba zoaai pam, halli ka ba kaalli ŋmanni la mɔ'abisigu kaalli maa ŋɔn nyɛ seem.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Ka ba kyɛnni n‑kyɛnni na m‑paanni tingbaŋŋu maa, halli nti gyiligi Naawunni niriba ŋɔn daa gbɛ seela maa - ni ba tiŋŋa maa gba, Naawunni ŋɔn wuri tinseaa la. Amaa di saha maa ka bugum yi arizana ni n‑sigi na n‑di ba zaa.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Ka Yɔmsira maa, Yibrisi Sintaani la, sɔ ŋɔn daa kyɛ ka niriba maa bɔrigi la, ŋɔna ka ba daa gbaai n‑zaŋŋi n‑lɔsi farinwuta bugum mɔ'ari maa ni, ya'aseaa ba daa pun lɔsi bunkɔbbeoo maa, ni u ziri anabi maa. Ka ba zaa bɛ bɛni n‑diri wahala wuntaŋŋa ni yuŋŋu, saha din ka tariga.

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka dinŋɔ zaa nyaaŋŋa ka n daa laa n‑nya naam ziziiya, bumpeelli, ka ka daa galisi pam. Ka Naaba zi bɛni. Tingbaŋŋu ni saazugu daa zɔ m‑bakki u saani, ka a daa bu laa n‑nya beebu ziiya yaasa.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya kpiimma, birikyindima ni tarimma zaa, ka ba zaa daa bɛ la naam zizii maa tooni n‑zɛya. Di saha ka ba daa lakki buksi maa; ka daa laa n‑lakki bukseaa - kana n nyɛ nyɔvuri bukku la. Niriba tuma yɛla daa sɔbi n‑dooli buksi maa puuni, kpiimma wa ban daa tum tunseesi zaa la. Ka ba daa kari kpiimma maa sariya zaŋŋi m‑pa ba tuma ŋɔn sɔbi seem la.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: Teeku ni mɔ'a maa daa yiisi niriba na - seeba an daa di maa. Ka kum ni kpiimma tiŋŋa gba daa yiisi niriba na - seeba ban daa kpi m‑bɛ a fukumsi ni maa zaa. Ka ba daa kari sɔkam sariya n‑dɔli ban daa tum seem.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Halli kum ni kpiimma tiŋŋa gba ka ba daa zaŋŋi n‑lɔsi farinwuta bugum mɔ'ari maa ni. Lala bugum mɔ'ari maa nyɛ la kum din paasiri buyi maa.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Ŋɔn kam yuuri daa bu sɔbi n‑dooli nyɔvuri bukku maa ni, ba daa zaŋŋi di daama n‑lɔsi lala bugum mɔ'ari maa ni.

english_verse: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
translation_verse: Ka n daa nya arizampaala, ni tingbampaalli; dama arizana ni tingbaŋŋu seesi an daa pun bɛ ni la daa gaariya. Ka teeku mɔ'ari daa laa n‑ka ni.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Ka n daa wum kukɔri arizana ni, ka ka tasi n‑yɛli pampam, ni, “Pampaŋŋɔ Naawunni beebu zii paai niriba saani na, ka u nin ti bɛ ba saani. Ba nin ti nyɛ u niriba, ka Naawunni mammaŋŋa nin bɛ ba saani ka u nin nyɛ Naawunni n‑tiri ba.

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: U nin nyeesi ba nintam zaa; kum ku laa m‑bɛ ni, bee sufusa'aŋŋu bee kunkuma, bee toom; dama bunseesi kam an daa daŋŋi m‑bɛ ni la gaariya.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Di saha ka ŋɔn daa zi naam zizii maa ni la daa yɛli, ni, “Gɔsima! M maanni la bunseela zaa bumpaala.” Ka laa n‑yɛli, ni, “Sɔbma dinŋɔ n‑dooli! Dama yɛttɔ'a anŋɔ mari yɛdda ka nyɛ yɛlimaŋni.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ma, ni, “Di niŋiya. Mani n nyɛ piiligu, ka man laa n‑nyɛ naari. Kɔnyuuri ŋɔn mari sɔkam - mani nin zaŋŋi kɔseebu bun yi nyɔvuri kɔbuliga la ni n‑ti u yɔri, ka samni ka ni.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Sɔkam ŋɔn nyaŋŋi maa - anŋɔ zaa ka u nin ti di faari, ka man nin nyɛ Naawunni n‑tiri u, ka ŋɔn mun nin nyɛ m biiya.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Amaa dabeem dima, ban ka yɛdda, ni putoo dima, ni ninvugkuuriba ni ban tumni zina, ni ban tumni tiima ka gyeemni buga ni ziri kam zaa dima - ban zaa ziiya nyɛ la farinwuta bugum mɔ'ari maa ni. Lala bugum mɔ'ari maa nyɛ la kum din paasiri buyi maa.”

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka malaakasɔ daa paai ma na, ka u daa nyɛ la babayɔpɔi ban daa mari laasi maa yinni, laseesi an mari duniya naari wahala buyɔpɔi maa m‑paali zaa la. U daa kyɛ n saani na nti yɛli ma, ni, “Kyɛm na! N nin wulisi i pɔ'apaalli maa, Pɛbila maa pɔ'a la.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: Di saha ka Naawunni Sii daa na mari ma, ka malaaka maa daa zi ma m‑bakki n‑kyaŋŋi m‑paai zɔtitaari kan wɔ'a n‑du pam zugu; ka n daa nya tinkasi maa, Gyɛrusalɛm, ka ka daa yi la arizana ni, Naawunni saani, n‑sigri na.

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: Tiŋŋa maa daa nya'ari Naawunni gyirima veelim zugu. Ka daa nyalisiri hoo gyasipa - mansɔ'asi kugveeliseaa kan ligri zoaai halli nti yakki nla.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Ka daa mari la goonwa'alli kan daa zɛ n‑gyiligi ka zaa, ka goomni maa za'anɔya daa paai za'anɔya pii ni ayi. N daa nya ka malaakadima pii ni ayi daa zɛ n‑guri za'anɔya maa zaa yinniyinni. Izraɛli dima dɔ'am za'anɔya pii ni ayi yuya daa sɔbi n‑niŋi tiŋŋa maa za'anɔya ni.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Za'anɔya ata n daa bɛ ka wuntampusiri lugri ni, abata daa bɛ nudirigu lugri ni, abata daa bɛ wuntanluri lugri ni, ka abata anla daa bɛ tiŋŋa nuzaa lugri ni.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Tiŋŋa maa goomni maa daa mari la ka nyeeba pii ni ayi. Ka ba daa sɔbi Pɛbila tuumudima pii ni ayi yuya m‑pa a ni.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: Malaakasɔ ŋɔn daa tɔ'asi n‑ti ma la daa mari la salima kika'ari kan ma'asiri wa'alim. U daa zaŋŋi ka, ni u ti ma'asi tiŋŋa maa, ni ka za'anɔya ni ka gooma.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: Ba daa boaai tiŋŋa maa bummɔkku - ka wa'alim ni ka yɛlisim daa sa'ai taaba. U daa ma'asi n‑nya, ka ka wa'alim ni ka yɛlisim zaa daa paai maalsi tusiri ni kɔbsinu, ka ka saazugu wa'alim gba daa sa'ai lala.

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Gyasipa mansɔ'asi kugveela ka ba daa zaŋŋi m‑mɛ goomni la, ka tiŋŋa maa zaa mun nyɛ la salima an veela hoo mansɔ'asi taabu.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Ba daa zaŋŋi mansɔ'asi kugveela an ligri zoaai pam m‑maali gooma nyeeba maa. A zaa daa nyɛ kookooba. Kan piili maa nyɛ la gyasipapeelli, buyi daana nyɛ la lala kugseaa kan nyɛ bulu, buta nyɛ la bumpirisiri, bunaasi nyɛ la bɛŋkoom,

english_verse: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
translation_verse: bunu nyɛ la bunzɛpeelli, buyoobu nyɛ la bunzeoo zimizimi, buyɔpɔi nyɛ la dɔzum, bunii nyɛ la bunfaasiri, buwai nyɛ la bumpupɔriga, kan pa piiya nyɛ la vamaasa, pii ni yinni nyɛ la zɛkamvoo, ka kan mun pa pii ni ayi nyɛ la bunzeoo wulawula.

english_verse: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Za'anɔya pii ni ayi maa, seelli kam daa nyɛ kugkpilli yinni, bummunni, kugseesi an pɛligi wain ka a ligri zoaai. Ka tiŋŋa maa puuni sɔroo la daa nyɛ salima, ka ŋmanni mansɔ'asi kan gɔsiri m‑punna.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
translation_verse: Tɔ; ka n daa bu nya gyeeŋŋu yiri tiŋŋa maa puuni, dama Naawunni Ŋɔn Gaari Zaa nyɛ la ka gyeeŋŋu yiri, ni Pɛbila maa gba.

english_verse: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
translation_verse: Tiŋŋa maa bu boori wuntaŋŋa neesim, bee namboaa dinni, dama Naawunni gyirima neesim neaari bɛni, ka Pɛbila maa nyɛ la ka firila.

english_verse: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
translation_verse: Tinsi zaa niriba bɛ la ka neesim la ni, ka duniya wa nadima zaŋŋi la ba naam gyirima ni veelim m‑mari na.

english_verse: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
translation_verse: Beoo kam tiŋŋa maa za'anɔya kpalim n‑yoaai suŋŋa wuntaŋŋa ni, halli ka yuŋŋu laa n‑ka bɛni.

english_verse: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
translation_verse: Ka tinsi zaa nya'asim, ni gyirima, ni veelim ka ba nin mari na n‑zaali ka puuni.

english_verse: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_verse: Da'ari halli fiiŋ ku kpɛ tiŋŋa maa puuni, bee ŋɔn tumni vi bee ziri tuma, na'ala ban yuya bɛ Pɛbila ŋɔn su nyɔvuri bukku maa ni la.

english_verse: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
translation_verse: Ka malaaka maa daa laa n‑wulisi ma nyɔvuri koom kuliga, ka bu neaai kasikasi m‑pɔbri n‑yiiri naam zizii maa ni na, kan nyɛ Naawunni ni Pɛbila maa dinni.

english_verse: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
translation_verse: Kuliga koom maa daa pɔbri n‑dɔla tiŋŋa maa sɔyɛliŋŋu sunsukkuni. Ka nyɔvuri tiisi daa zɛ kɔsoori maa ya'a ayi zaa, n‑wanni wala balim pii ni ayi, n‑wanni gɔri gɔri. Ka tiisi maa vaari daa nyɛ la an tibri tinsi zaa niriba.

english_verse: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
translation_verse: Naawunni ŋɔn yɛli yɛlibeoo n‑niŋi bunseelli kam ku laa m‑bɛ bɛni. Naam zizii maa kan nyɛ Naawunni ni Pɛbila maa dinni nin bɛ tiŋŋa maa puuni: ka ban gyeemni u maa nin pɛ'ari u ka tiri u gyirima.

english_verse: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
translation_verse: Ka ba nin nya u nindaa, ka u mun yuuri nin pa ba gbeeri ni.

english_verse: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
translation_verse: Yuŋŋu ku laa m‑bɛ ni. Ba ku laa m‑boori firila, halli wuntaŋŋa neesim gba ka ba ku laa m‑boora; dama ti Dugma Naawunni nin neaari n‑tiri ba. Ka ba nin di naam, saha din ka tariga.

english_verse: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
translation_verse: Di saha ka malaaka maa daa yɛli ma, ni, “Yɛttɔ'a anŋɔ mari la yɛdda ka nyɛ yɛlimaŋni. Ti Dugma Naawunni daa su anabidima siisi maa, ka ŋɔn Naawunni tum u malaaka na, ni u ti wulisi u tuntumniba maa bunseesi an nin niŋi ka bu ya n‑yuui la.”

english_verse: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
translation_verse: Ni, “Nyama! Pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni na.” Ni, “Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti sɔkam zaa ŋɔn dɔli yɛttɔ'a wa, anabitiri yɛttɔ'aseesi an sɔbi bukku wa ni la.”

english_verse: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
translation_verse: Tɔ. Ka man, Gyɔni, muna - mani n daa wum ka nya anŋɔ zaa. Ka man ŋɔn daa wum ka nya a maa, n daa gbaani tiŋni, malaaka maa tooni - malaaka sɔ ŋɔn daa wulisiri yɛla wa maa - ni n ti gyeem u.

english_verse: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
translation_verse: Amaa u daa yɛli ma, ni, “Di niŋi lala! Man gba nyɛ la i taraana, sɔ ŋɔn gyeemni Naawunni, n‑la'amni ni nyini ni i mabiisi, anabidima maa, ni baseeba ban dɔli bukku wa yɛttɔ'a maa. Naawunni ka i gyeemna!”

english_verse: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Ka u laa n‑yɛli ma, “Di kpabi bukku wa n‑tabli, ni ka anabitiri yɛttɔ'a la! Dama a saha maa bu ya yuui.

english_verse: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
translation_verse: Kyɛ ka tumbeeri daana kpalim n‑tumni u tumbeeri, kyɛ ka da'ari daana kpalim n‑tumni u tunda'arisi, ka kyɛ ka yɛlimaŋni daana mun kpalim n‑tumni din tu ni yɛlimaŋni, ka ŋɔn nyɛ Naawunni nira kpalim n‑nyɛ bunkasi n‑tiri u.”

english_verse: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
translation_verse: Ni, “Nyama, pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni na! Man ŋɔn ya yɔ ba yoori maa muna, m mari ka n‑kyɛnni na. Sɔkam nin deaai u tuma ŋɔn sumsi seem.

english_verse: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
translation_verse: Mani n nyɛ piiligu ka mun laa n‑nyɛ naari, man daŋŋi tooni ka laa n‑dɔli nyaaŋŋa.”

english_verse: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
translation_verse: Ni, “Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti baseeba ban peaai ba situra nyain; lala ka ba nin su soori ni ba ti paai nyɔvuri tii maa ni na, ka laa n‑nyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ za'anɔya maa ni m‑paai tiŋŋa maa puuni.

english_verse: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
translation_verse: Baasi mun kpalimni m‑bɛ kpaŋŋa sa. Ban niŋni tiima, ni zina, ka kuuri niriba nla; ba gyeemni la bunseesi an pa Naawunni.”

english_verse: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
translation_verse: Ti Dugma Yisa yɛliya, “Mani, Yisa, tum n malaaka ni u ti ti ya lahabaari wa, ka ban la'amni tinsi tinsi n‑gyeemni ma la nin ti wum. Mani n nyɛ NaaDauda dɔ'am tuŋŋu maa Nya'ari: u bii n nyɛ ma gba. Mani n nyɛ bɛŋmariga ka neaari nyain.”

english_verse: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
translation_verse: Naawunni Sii maa gba, ni Pɛbila pɔ'apaalli maa m‑paasi n yɛtti, ni, “Kyɛm na!” Kyɛ ka ŋɔn wumni gba yɛli, ni, “Kyɛm na!” Kɔnyuuri yi mari sɔ zaa, kyɛ ka u kyɛ na, ka sɔ zaa ŋɔn boora: ba nin ti nyu nyɔvuri koom maa yɔri, ka samni ka ni.

english_verse: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
translation_verse: Tɔ: ka ya wumiya! N yɛtti sɔkam zaa ŋɔn wumni bukku wa anabitiri yɛttɔ'a maa, ni, yan di zaŋŋi sɛseelli m‑paasi yɛttɔ'a wa zugu; sɔ yi ti paasi, Naawunni nin paasi u wahala seesi bukku wa ŋɔn yɛli wa.

english_verse: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
translation_verse: Ka sɔ mun yi yiisi bukku wa yɛttɔ'aseesi m‑basi, Naawunni nin yiisi u tɔrli m‑basi, ka u ku toaai n‑nya nyɔvuri tii maa, ni tiŋŋa kasi la, ban sɔbi seesi yɛla bukku wa ni la.

english_verse: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
translation_verse: Sɔ ŋɔn niŋi anŋɔ zaa sɛhara la yɛtti, ni, “Ii pampaŋŋɔ ka n kyɛnni na.” Ammi: n Dugma Yisa kyɛm na!

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_verse: Tɔ: Ti Dugma Yisa piinisuma bɛ sɔkam saani. Ammi.
